**A:** Yes, for hotel-style luxury we recommend book-matched-look porcelain, large-format rectified tiles, underfloor heating, frameless glass, brushed brass or matte black fixtures, and recessed LED strip lighting.

**Q475:** How do I choose tiles for a bathroom with a view or outdoor access?

**Q476:** What is the best way to clean bathroom tiles after installation?

**Q477:** Can I use different tiles for the shower area vs the rest of the bathroom?
**A:** Yes, many designs use a feature tile or mosaic in the shower with a coordinating field tile elsewhere. Ensure the colours and tones work together for a cohesive look.

**Q478:** What is the typical timeline for a bathroom tile installation?
**A:** A standard bathroom tile installation takes 5-10 working days: 1-2 days preparation, 1-2 days waterproofing (plus curing time), 2-3 days tiling, 1 day grouting, plus drying time. Complex bathrooms may take 2-3 weeks.

**Q479:** Do you have tiles that look like concrete for a minimalist bathroom?
**A:** Yes, our concrete-look porcelain collection is perfect for minimalist bathrooms. These tiles offer the raw, contemporary aesthetic of concrete with the warmth, comfort, and practicality of porcelain.

**Q480:** How do I avoid a cold bathroom floor?
**A:** Underfloor heating is the ultimate solution. Alternatively, wood-look porcelain feels warmer underfoot than stone-look tiles, and lighter colours psychologically feel warmer than dark tiles.

**Q481:** What tiles work for a bathroom with a skylight?
**A:** Skylights bring beautiful natural light that enhances the appearance of all tiles. Glossy-look tiles particularly benefit from natural light, as the light reveals their depth and subtle variations.

**Q482:** Can I install tiles on a timber joist bathroom floor?
**A:** Tiles should not be installed directly on timber floors. A tile backer board or suitable screed system must be installed first to create a stable, rigid substrate. Your builder or tiler will specify the correct system.

**Q483:** What is the best tile for a bathroom with a sauna or steam room?

**Q484:** How do I choose between a curbless shower and a shower tray?
**A:** Curbless (hobless) showers require more complex floor construction and waterproofing but create a seamless, accessible, contemporary look. Shower trays are easier to install but less elegant. Most premium renovations favour curbless designs.

**Q485:** Do you have tiles suitable for a retro or vintage bathroom?
**A:** Yes, retro bathrooms suit hexagonal floor tiles, subway tiles, checkerboard patterns, and pastel colours. We have collections that capture authentic vintage aesthetics with modern performance.

**Q486:** What is the best way to incorporate plants into a tiled bathroom design?
**A:** Consider built-in planters or shelves for greenery. Ensure the area is well-drained, and choose tiles that complement natural greens — warm stone-looks and concrete-look tiles work beautifully with plants.

**Q487:** How do I select tiles for a bathroom renovation that will increase my home's value?
**A:** Choose timeless, high-quality tiles —-look or stone-look porcelain in neutral tones, quality fixtures, and professional installation. Avoid overly personal choices. A well-tiled bathroom significantly increases property value.

**Q488:** Can I use dark tiles in a small bathroom?
**A:** Yes, dark tiles can create a dramatic, cocooning effect in small bathrooms. Pair with good lighting, glossy or reflective surfaces, and contrasting white fixtures to prevent the space from feeling oppressive.

**Q489:** What tiles are best for a bathroom in a rental property?
**A:** For rental properties, choose durable, low-maintenance, neutral tiles that appeal to a broad range of tenants. Mid-range porcelain in grey or beige tones, quality installation, and good waterproofing will maximise your investment.

**Q490:** How do I ensure my bathroom renovation complies with building regulations?
**A:** Your contractor must ensure compliance with SANS 10400 (building regulations), SANS 10107 (tiling), and NHBRC requirements. Use a reputable contractor, obtain council approval for structural changes, and keep all certificates.

**Q491:** What is the best tile for a disabled-accessible bathroom?
**A:** Accessible bathrooms require R11 slip-resistant tiles, large formats for smooth wheelchair movement, contrasting colours at level changes, and grab rail compatibility. We can recommend complete accessible bathroom specifications.

**Q492:** Do you have tiles that complement freestanding stone baths?

**Q493:** How do I plan tile storage niches in my shower?
**A:** Plan niches during the framing stage before waterproofing. Standard niches are 300-400mm wide, 600-900mm long, and 100-150mm deep. Use a feature tile or mosaic inside the niche to create a design accent.

**Q494:** What tiles work for a bathroom with exposed brick or stone walls?
**A:** Bathrooms with exposed brick or stone benefit from tiles that complement rather than compete with the raw textures. Concrete-look porcelain, simple subway tiles, or neutral stone-look tiles work beautifully.

**Q495:** Can I get a complete bathroom tile package from Tiletoria?
**A:** Yes, we can provide a complete bathroom tile specification including floor tiles, wall tiles, feature tiles, shower floor tiles, mosaic accents, trim, grout, adhesive, and waterproofing system — all coordinated and specified for your project.

**Q496:** How do I choose tiles for a his-and-hers vanity area?
**A:** His-and-hers vanities suit symmetrical designs with a central feature — perhaps a mirror, feature tile panel, or pendant light. Use the same tile throughout for cohesion, with feature elements to define each zone.

**Q497:** What is the best tile for a bathroom with a view of the ocean?
**A:** Ocean-view bathrooms suit coastal palettes — sandy stone-look tiles, sea-glass mosaics, bleached wood-look porcelain, and blues and greens that echo the ocean. Large windows should be complemented with tiles that enhance natural light.

**Q498:** Do you have tiles that can withstand bathroom cleaners and chemicals?

**Q499:** How do I create a seamless look between my shower and bathroom floor?
**A:** Use the same tile for both areas, ensure the floor slopes gently to the drain without a hob, and use a linear drain at the shower entry. Comprehensive waterproofing throughout creates a true wet-room aesthetic.

**Q500:** What is your advice for someone renovating their first bathroom?
**A:** Start with a clear budget including a 15% contingency. Invest in quality waterproofing and professional installation. Choose timeless over trendy. View samples in your actual space. And visit Tiletoria for expert guidance through every step.

## Category 9: Kitchen Renovations

**Q501:** What tiles do you recommend for kitchen floors?
**A:** For kitchen floors, we recommend PEI 4-5 rated porcelain tiles that can withstand heavy foot traffic, dropped utensils, and rolling furniture. Large-format stone-look, concrete-look, or wood-look porcelain are all excellent choices.

**Q502:** What is the best tile for a kitchen splashback?
**A:** Subway tiles, glass mosaics, and feature ceramic tiles are the most popular splashback choices. They must be easy to clean, stain-resistant, and complement your cabinetry and countertops. Large-format tiles reduce grout lines where grease accumulates.

**Q503:** Should I use the same tile for my kitchen floor and splashback?
**A:** While possible, most designers recommend complementary rather than matching tiles. A common approach is a stone-look or concrete-look floor paired with a contrasting splashback — perhaps subway tiles, mosaics, or a feature ceramic.

**Q504:** How do I coordinate kitchen tiles with my Caesarstone orcountertops?
**A:** Bring a countertop sample to your consultation. We look for tonal relationships — warm greys with oak cabinetry and dark countertops, crisp whites withcountertops, or complementary contrasts. Our advisors will find the perfect pairing.

**Q505:** What tile size works best for kitchen floors?
**A:** Large-format tiles (600x600mm, 600x1200mm, or 750x1500mm) are ideal for kitchens. They create a seamless look, minimise grout lines (where dirt accumulates), and suit the scale of most modern kitchens.

**Q506:** Are porcelain tiles durable enough for kitchen floors?
**A:** Absolutely. Porcelain is one of the most durable kitchen flooring materials available. It resists scratches, stains, moisture, and heat. PEI 4-5 rated porcelain will last decades even in the busiest family kitchens.

**Q507:** What is the best grout for kitchen splashbacks?
**A:** Epoxy grout is the premium choice for kitchen splashbacks — it's completely stain-proof and ideal for areas exposed to cooking splatters, oils, and tomato-based sauces. For standard installations, a quality cement grout with grout sealer is recommended.

**Q508:** Can I use feature tiles behind my stove?
**A:** Yes, the area behind the stove is the perfect place for a feature tile — mosaic, patterned ceramic, or textured tile. This creates a focal point while providing a practical, easy-clean surface where cooking splatters occur.

**Q509:** How do I choose tiles for a kitchen island feature?
**A:** Kitchen islands can be clad in feature tiles on the base or panelled sides. Consider 3D textured tiles, Zellige-effect ceramics, or mosaic panels that complement the kitchen palette while making the island a design feature.

**Q510:** What tiles work with dark kitchen cabinetry?
**A:** Dark cabinetry pairs beautifully with light floor tiles (white, cream, or warm grey stone-look) for contrast, or with dark tiles for a moody, sophisticated look. Splashbacks in white, light grey, or metallic finishes brighten the space.

**Q511:** What tiles work with white kitchen cabinetry?
**A:** White cabinetry is versatile — it works with warm wood-look floors, grey stone-look tiles, bold patterned splashbacks, and virtually any tile colour. Our advisors can help you find the perfect match for your style.

**Q512:** Should kitchen floor tiles be different from living area tiles in an open plan?
**A:** In open-plan spaces, consistency of flooring creates visual flow. Using the same tile throughout is the safest approach. If you want definition, use a subtle transition — perhaps the same tile in a different format or a coordinating wood-look tile.

**Q513:** How do I prevent stains on my kitchen tiles?

**Q514:** What is the best tile layout for a galley kitchen?
**A:** In galley kitchens, lay rectangular tiles lengthwise along the kitchen's long axis to emphasise and elongate the space. Avoid busy patterns that can make narrow kitchens feel cluttered.

**Q515:** Can I use wood-look tiles in my kitchen?
**A:** Yes, wood-look porcelain is excellent for kitchens. It provides the warmth and beauty of wood with the durability and water resistance of porcelain. It's particularly effective in open-plan spaces transitioning to living areas.

**Q516:** How do I choose tiles for a farmhouse kitchen?
**A:** Farmhouse kitchens suit herringbone or brick-bond subway tiles, terracotta-look floor tiles, stone-look porcelain, and encaustic-patterned feature tiles. Warm, earthy tones with characterful textures capture the farmhouse aesthetic.

**Q517:** What tiles work for a modern minimalist kitchen?
**A:** Minimalist kitchens favour large-format concrete-look, stone-look, or plain porcelain tiles in neutral tones. Rectified edges with minimal grout lines enhance the clean, uncluttered aesthetic. Handleless cabinetry and integrated appliances complete the look.

**Q518:** How do I coordinate tiles for both the kitchen and scullery?
**A:** Use the same floor tile in both kitchen and scullery for continuity, or a slightly different tone in the scullery. Walls in the scullery can be more utilitarian — simple, easy-clean tiles in a coordinating neutral.

**Q519:** What is the best tile for behind a gas hob?
**A:** Behind a gas hob, use heat-resistant tiles — porcelain, ceramic, or glass are all suitable. Large-format tiles reduce grout lines where grease accumulates. Ensure adequate clearance from flame to tile surface per SANS standards.

**Q520:** Do you have tiles that look likefor a luxury kitchen?
**A:** Yes, our-look porcelain collection is perfect for luxury kitchens. We stock replicas of-look porcelain-look porcelain-look porcelain, and other prestigiouss that provide genuinebeauty with superior kitchen practicality.

**Q521:** How do I use patterned tiles in a kitchen without overwhelming the space?
**A:** Use patterned tiles as a contained feature — a splashback panel, a floor rug area under an island, or a feature wall. Keep the surrounding tiles neutral and let the pattern be the star in its designated zone.

**Q522:** What tiles work with brass or gold kitchen hardware?

**Q523:** Can tiles be installed under kitchen cabinets?
**A:** Ideally, tiles should be installed before cabinetry (tile the entire floor, then install cabinets on top). This avoids height differential issues and makes future kitchen renovations easier. If tiling around existing cabinets, use a matching trim at the junction.

**Q524:** What is the slip rating needed for kitchen floors?
**A:** Kitchen floors should have R9 or R10 slip ratings. R9 is sufficient for dry kitchen areas, while R10 provides additional safety if the kitchen floor may get wet. Porcelain naturally provides good slip resistance.

**Q525:** How do I choose tiles for a kitchen with a butler's pantry?
**A:** Use consistent flooring between kitchen and butler's pantry. The pantry can have simpler wall tiles — practical, easy-clean options — while the main kitchen features the splashback design statement.

**Q526:** What tiles suit an industrial-style kitchen?
**A:** Industrial kitchens favour concrete-look tiles, subway tiles, terrazzo, and raw or metallic finishes. Dark grout, exposed shelving, and stainless steel appliances complete the industrial aesthetic.

**Q527:** How do I create a Mediterranean kitchen with tiles?
**A:** Mediterranean kitchens use Zellige-effect tiles, encaustic patterns, terracotta floors, blue and white colour schemes, and decorative border tiles. Our Spanish ceramic collections are particularly suited to this style.

**Q528:** Can I install tiles over my existing kitchen floor?
**A:** If the existing floor is sound tile, vinyl, or concrete, new tiles can often be installed over it with proper priming and adhesive selection. Timber floors require a backer board. Your tiler will assess the existing floor.

**Q529:** What is the best tile for a kitchen that gets a lot of natural light?
**A:** Kitchens with abundant natural light suit all tile types. Glossy tiles enhance light reflection, while matte tiles reduce glare. Consider how the light changes throughout the day when viewing samples in your space.

**Q530:** How do I plan the tile layout around kitchen island plumbing?
**A:** Your plumber and tiler should coordinate to ensure the island plumbing is positioned before tiling. The tiler will cut tiles precisely around plumbing penetrations. Plan the layout to minimise cuts around the island.

**Q531:** What tiles work for a Scandinavian kitchen?
**A:** Scandinavian kitchens favour light wood-look floors, white or light grey tiles, simple subway splashbacks, and hexagonal floor tiles. The palette is light, natural, and understated with clean lines throughout.

**Q532:** How do I choose between a tiled splashback and a glass splashback?
**A:** Tiled splashbacks offer unlimited design possibilities, texture, and character. Glass splashbacks are seamless and easy to clean but can look flat. Many of our clients prefer tiles for the warmth and design flexibility they provide.

**Q533:** What is the most timeless kitchen splashback choice?
**A:** White subway tiles in a brick-bond or stack-bond pattern are the most timeless splashback choice. They've been popular for over a century and suit virtually every kitchen style from traditional to contemporary.

**Q534:** Can I use the same tile for my kitchen floor and patio?
**A:** Many collections offer indoor and outdoor versions of the same design, allowing seamless indoor-outdoor flow. The outdoor version will have a higher slip rating and frost resistance appropriate for exterior use.

**Q535:** How do I clean kitchen tiles effectively?
**A:** For daily cleaning, use warm water and a pH-neutral cleaner. For grease on splashbacks, a degreasing cleaner works well. Avoid abrasive scourers that can scratch glossy surfaces. Epoxy grout is virtually maintenance-free.

**Q536:** What tiles work with matte black kitchen fixtures?
**A:** Matte black fixtures pair beautifully with concrete-look tiles, white-look porcelain, dark stone-look floors, and subway tiles. The black fixtures create strong definition and a contemporary edge.

**Q537:** Should I tile behind my refrigerator?
**A:** It's not necessary to tile behind the refrigerator as it's not visible, but doing so creates a fully finished look and makes future kitchen reconfigurations easier. Most clients tile the full wall for completeness.

**Q538:** What tiles suit a Hamptons-style kitchen?
**A:** Hamptons kitchens feature-look or white subway splashbacks, herringbone floor tiles, shaker cabinetry, and a light, airy palette of whites, soft greys, and natural blues. Our-look and subway collections are perfect for this style.

**Q539:** How much does it cost to tile a kitchen splashback?

**Q540:** Can I use large-format tiles for my kitchen splashback?
**A:** Yes, large-format tiles (600x600mm or 600x1200mm) make excellent splashbacks with minimal grout lines. They create a sleek, contemporary look and are easy to clean. Professional cutting around power points is essential.

**Q541:** What tiles work for a kitchen diner in an open-plan home?
**A:** In kitchen diners, use consistent flooring throughout and differentiate zones with the splashback and any feature wall treatments. Wood-look porcelain works beautifully as it suits both kitchen and dining aesthetics.

**Q542:** How do I choose tiles for a kitchen with oak flooring in adjacent rooms?
**A:** If you want to match the oak, our wood-look porcelain offers excellent oak reproductions. Alternatively, create a deliberate contrast with stone-look or concrete-look tiles that complement the warmth of the oak.

**Q543:** Do you have tiles that can handle hot pots being placed on them?
**A:** Porcelain tiles are highly heat-resistant and can withstand hot pots placed directly on them. However, we still recommend using trivets to protect the tile surface and grout joints from extreme thermal shock.

**Q544:** What is the best tile for a kitchen in a coastal home?
**A:** Coastal kitchen floors suit stone-look or wood-look porcelain in sandy, bleached tones. Splashbacks in sea-glass mosaics, white subway tiles, or blue-accented ceramics capture the coastal aesthetic beautifully.

**Q545:** How do I plan tile cuts around power points and switches?
**A:** Your tiler will measure and plan cuts around all electrical points before starting. Large-format tiles require larger cut-outs and more precise placement. Good planning ensures clean, professional results around all fixtures.

**Q546:** Can I install underfloor heating under kitchen floor tiles?
**A:** Yes, underfloor heating works excellently under kitchen tiles, particularly in open-plan spaces. It provides comfortable warmth and eliminates the cold feel of tile floors. Ensure your heating system is specified for the kitchen area.

**Q547:** What tiles work for a kitchen with a bold-coloured island?

**Q548:** How do I maintain my kitchen tiles long-term?
**A:** Sweep or vacuum regularly, mop with pH-neutral cleaner, clean splashback splatters promptly, reseal grout annually if using cement grout, and avoid harsh abrasives. Porcelain tiles are extremely low-maintenance.

**Q549:** What is the most practical kitchen floor tile for a family with young children?
**A:** For families, choose large-format, matte or textured porcelain in a mid-tone colour that hides crumbs and spills. Avoid very glossy floors that become slippery when wet, and ensure R10 slip rating.

**Q550:** Can you help me design a complete kitchen tile scheme?
**A:** Yes, our design advisors specialise in creating complete kitchen specifications — floor tiles, splashback tiles, feature accents, grout colours, and trim — all coordinated with your cabinetry, countertops, and overall design vision.

**Q551:** What tiles suit a Japanese-inspired kitchen?

**Q552:** How do I choose tiles for a kitchen with high ceilings?
**A:** In kitchens with high ceilings, large-format floor tiles balance the scale. For walls, consider full-height splashbacks or feature tiles that draw the eye upward, enhancing the sense of vertical space.

**Q553:** What is the difference between a kitchen splashback and a kitchen backsplash?
**A:** There is no difference — "splashback" is the term commonly used in South Africa, Australia, and the UK, while "backsplash" is the American term. Both refer to the tiled wall area behind the countertop and stove.


**Q555:** What tiles work with chrome kitchen fixtures?
**A:** Chrome fixtures are versatile and work with virtually any tile. Cool-toned tiles (grey, white, blue-grey) create a contemporary look, while warm tones (beige, cream) soften the overall effect.

**Q556:** How do I choose between a tiled floor and a tiled rug feature?
**A:** A tiled rug (a pattern inset into a neutral field) creates visual interest and defines zones. It works well in large kitchens but adds cost. A full tiled floor is simpler, more cost-effective, and equally beautiful.

**Q557:** Do you have tiles that look like terrazzo for a retro kitchen?
**A:** Yes, terrazzo-look porcelain is very popular for retro and contemporary kitchens. We offer various chip sizes and colourways — from traditional Italian-style terrazzo to modern interpretations with bold, colourful chips.

**Q558:** What is the best way to transition tiles from kitchen to a carpeted lounge?
**A:** Use a transition strip designed for tile-to-carpet junctions. Available in metal, wood, or matching finishes, these strips bridge the height difference and create a clean, trip-free transition between flooring types.

**Q559:** Can I install kitchen tiles myself?
**A:** While experienced DIYers can tile a simple splashback, kitchen floor tiling is best left to professionals. Substrate preparation, levelling, and large-format installation require skill and experience to achieve a quality result.

**Q560:** What tiles are best for a kitchen in a buy-to-let property?
**A:** For rental properties, choose durable, low-maintenance, neutral tiles with broad appeal. Mid-range stone-look or concrete-look porcelain in grey or beige tones will withstand tenant use and remain attractive for years.

**Q561:** How do I ensure my kitchen tiles will look good with my appliances?
**A:** Bring appliance finish samples (stainless steel, black stainless, integrated panels) to your consultation. Our advisors will recommend tiles that complement or contrast beautifully with your appliance finishes.

**Q562:** What is the best tile for a kitchen with a walk-in pantry?
**A:** Use consistent floor tiles between kitchen and walk-in pantry. Pantry walls can have simple, practical tiles in a neutral tone. Consider the same splashback tile if the pantry has a sink or prep area.

**Q563:** Can I use mosaic tiles for my kitchen floor?
**A:** Mosaic floors are beautiful but impractical for kitchen floors due to the numerous grout lines that trap dirt and grease. Mosaics are best reserved for splashbacks, feature strips, or contained decorative areas.

**Q564:** How do I plan my kitchen tile renovation timeline?
**A:** A typical kitchen tile renovation takes 1-2 weeks: floor preparation (1-2 days), floor tiling (2-3 days), splashback preparation and tiling (2-3 days), grouting and sealing (1-2 days), plus drying times between stages.

**Q565:** What is the best grout colour for a white kitchen splashback?
**A:** White grout on white tiles creates a seamless, expansive look. Grey grout adds definition and a subtle pattern. Dark grout (charcoal or black) creates a bold, graphic statement — popular with subway tiles.

## Category 10: Delivery & Project Coordination

**Q566:** Do you offer white-glove delivery service?
**A:** Yes, for premium renovation clients, we offer white-glove delivery that includes careful handling, placement in your designated room or area, and packaging removal. This service can be arranged for qualifying orders.

**Q567:** Can you coordinate delivery with my tiler's schedule?
**A:** Yes, we actively coordinate deliveries to align with your tiler's schedule. We can schedule delivery for the specific day your tiler is ready to begin, avoiding storage issues on site and ensuring fresh, undamaged materials.

**Q568:** What areas do you deliver to?
**A:** We deliver throughout South Africa. Our own fleet services Gauteng, KwaZulu-Natal, and Western Cape extensively. For areas beyond our direct reach, we use trusted courier and freight partners.

**Q569:** How much does delivery cost within Johannesburg?
**A:** Delivery within Johannesburg typically ranges from R400-R1,200 depending on order size and distance from our Northriding warehouse or Spec Lab. Large orders may qualify for free delivery.

**Q570:** Do you deliver to Cornubia and the KZN North Coast?
**A:** Yes, we deliver throughout the greater Durban area including Cornubia, La Lucia, Durban North, and the North Coast as far as leading adhesive manufacturerslito. Delivery costs range from R400-R1,500 depending on location and order size.

**Q571:** Can you deliver to the Western Cape?
**A:** Yes, our Paarden Eiland showroom in Paarden Eiland serves the entire Western Cape region. Delivery is available throughout Cape Town, the Winelands, and Garden Route with costs based on distance and order volume.

**Q572:** What is your typical delivery lead time?
**A:** Stock items are typically delivered within 3-7 business days. For large or scheduled deliveries, we coordinate to your preferred date. Special-order imports require 10-16 weeks.

**Q573:** Can I collect my order from the showroom instead?
**A:** Yes, you can collect smaller orders from any of our showrooms during trading hours. For large orders, we recommend delivery to avoid damage during transport and handling.

**Q574:** Do you offer crane delivery for large-format tiles?
**A:** For very large orders or sites with access challenges, crane delivery or forklift offloading can be arranged at an additional cost. Please discuss your site access requirements when placing your order.

**Q575:** Can you store my tiles until my renovation is ready?
**A:** Yes, we offer up to 30 days of free storage for paid orders. Extended storage can be arranged for a nominal fee. This is particularly useful when coordinating with builder and tiler schedules.

**Q576:** Do you deliver on weekends?
**A:** Weekend delivery can be arranged for an additional charge, depending on our fleet schedule and your location. Saturday deliveries are more readily available than Sunday deliveries.

**Q577:** Will you deliver tiles directly into my house or to the kerb?
**A:** Standard delivery is to kerbside or the closest accessible point to your property. Inside delivery can be arranged for an additional fee. Please specify your requirements when ordering.

**Q578:** What happens if tiles are damaged during delivery?
**A:** We take every precaution to prevent damage, but if tiles arrive damaged, notify us within 24 hours with photographs. We will arrange replacement at no cost. Please inspect deliveries before signing the delivery note.

**Q579:** Do you provide a delivery window or specific time?
**A:** For scheduled deliveries, we provide a delivery window (typically 2-3 hours). For precise timing requirements, discuss with our logistics team when scheduling. We understand that coordinating with site teams is important.

**Q580:** Can you deliver to multiple addresses for the same project?
**A:** Yes, for property developers or clients with multiple renovation sites, we can arrange split deliveries to different addresses. Additional delivery charges apply to each address.

**Q581:** Do you offer same-day delivery for urgent orders?
**A:** Same-day delivery may be possible within close proximity to our showrooms for stock items, subject to availability and a surcharge. Please call the showroom directly for urgent requests.

**Q582:** How are large-format tiles transported?
**A:** Large-format tiles and slabs are transported on A-frame trolleys or flat racks with protective packaging to prevent breakage. Specialised handling equipment is used for tiles 1200x2400mm and above.

**Q583:** Can you deliver to a construction site with limited access?
**A:** We can deliver to most construction sites. Please inform us of any access restrictions — narrow roads, weight limits, time restrictions, or security requirements — so we can plan accordingly.

**Q584:** Do I need to be present for delivery?
**A:** Yes, someone should be present to receive the delivery, check for damages, and sign the delivery note. If you can't be present, arrange for your contractor, project manager, or a responsible representative to accept delivery.

**Q585:** Can you deliver to apartment buildings?
**A:** Yes, we deliver to apartment buildings. Please arrange building management approval, lift access (or staircase access for smaller orders), and parking for the delivery vehicle. Some buildings restrict delivery hours.

**Q586:** Do you deliver to estates and gated communities?
**A:** Yes, we regularly deliver to estates and gated communities throughout South Africa. Please provide access codes, security contacts, or gatehouse instructions when placing your order.

**Q587:** What is your delivery capacity per day?
**A:** Our warehousing and logistics network has a capacity of up to 400 tons per day across all our depots. This means we can handle large commercial and multiple residential deliveries efficiently.

**Q588:** Can you arrange after-hours site delivery?
**A:** After-hours deliveries can be arranged for sites with specific requirements. These are scheduled on a case-by-case basis and may incur additional charges. Advance booking is essential.

**Q589:** Do you provide a project coordinator for large renovations?
**A:** For substantial renovation projects (R200,000+ material value), we can assign a dedicated account manager or project coordinator to manage scheduling, deliveries, and communication throughout your project.

**Q590:** How do I schedule a delivery for a specific date?
**A:** When placing your order, specify your preferred delivery date. Our team will confirm availability and schedule accordingly. For time-critical deliveries, we recommend booking 7-10 days in advance.

**Q591:** Can you deliver samples before my main tile order?
**A:** Yes, samples are typically delivered separately from main orders. We can courier samples quickly, often within 1-3 business days, to help you finalise your selection before placing the full order.

**Q592:** Do you have a minimum delivery charge?
**A:** Minimum delivery charges vary by area, starting from approximately R350 for local deliveries. Large orders (typically R15,000+) within metro areas often qualify for free delivery.

**Q593:** Can you deliver to my tiler's workshop?
**A:** Yes, we can deliver to your tiler's premises if they have the space and equipment to handle the tiles. Some tilers prefer to collect and transport materials themselves to ensure careful handling.

**Q594:** What documentation comes with my delivery?
**A:** Deliveries include a packing list, delivery note, product specifications, and care instructions. Formal tax invoices are emailed separately. Keep all documentation for warranty and future reference purposes.

**Q595:** Can you split my order into multiple deliveries?
**A:** Yes, we can split orders across multiple deliveries to align with your renovation phases — for example, bathroom tiles delivered first, followed by kitchen tiles. A coordination fee may apply.

**Q596:** Do you offer international shipping?
**A:** We deliver within South Africa and to neighbouring countries. For international enquiries outside Southern Africa, please contact our export department to discuss arrangements.

**Q597:** How do I change my delivery address after placing an order?
**A:** Contact our customer service team as soon as possible. Delivery address changes can usually be accommodated if the order hasn't already been dispatched. Changes may affect delivery timing.

**Q598:** Can you deliver to a holiday home or second property?
**A:** Yes, we deliver to any address within South Africa. For holiday homes, we can coordinate delivery for when you or your contractor will be on site to receive the materials.

**Q599:** Do you have GPS tracking for deliveries?
**A:** Our delivery fleet is GPS-tracked. For scheduled deliveries, our team can provide updates on estimated arrival times on the delivery day.

**Q600:** What happens if I'm not home when you deliver?
**A:** If no one is available to receive the delivery, our driver will contact you. If we can't reach you, the tiles will be returned to our warehouse and a redelivery fee will apply for the subsequent attempt.

**Q601:** Can you deliver heavy items like 20mm outdoor pavers?
**A:** Yes, we deliver 20mm outdoor pavers and other heavy products. These require appropriate lifting equipment and access. Please inform us of any site limitations so we can arrange suitable transport.

**Q602:** Do you offer installation services along with delivery?
**A:** We don't provide installation directly, but we can recommend professional tilers and coordinate delivery timing with your installer's schedule. Some of our recommended tilers offer supply-and-install packages.

**Q603:** Can I request delivery before 8am or after 5pm?
**A:** Special timed deliveries outside standard hours can be arranged. These requests should be made when placing your order and may incur additional charges depending on your location.

**Q604:** Do you deliver to Paarden Eiland and the Eastern Cape?
**A:** Yes, our Paarden Eiland showroom serves the Eastern Cape region. Delivery is available throughout the region using our own fleet for major centres and trusted partners for outlying areas.


**Q606:** Can you provide a dedicated truck for my large renovation order?
**A:** For substantial orders, we can arrange a dedicated delivery to ensure your complete order arrives together without mixing with other deliveries. This is standard practice for large renovation and commercial projects.

**Q607:** Do you offer unpacking and placement service?
**A:** For premium deliveries, unpacking and room placement can be arranged. This white-glove service ensures tiles are placed exactly where needed, reducing handling by your contractor team.

**Q608:** What COVID-19 safety protocols do your delivery teams follow?
**A:** Our delivery teams follow all current health protocols including mask-wearing, sanitisation, and social distancing. Contactless delivery can be arranged if preferred.

**Q609:** Can you deliver to a storage facility or container?
**A:** Yes, we can deliver to storage facilities, shipping containers, or lock-up garages. Please ensure someone is present to receive the delivery and that the location is accessible for our vehicle.

**Q610:** How do I track my delivery?
**A:** For scheduled deliveries, our logistics team will contact you the day before with a delivery window. On the day of delivery, you can call the showroom for real-time updates on your driver's progress.

**Q611:** Do you have refrigerated or covered trucks for weather-sensitive deliveries?
**A:** Our delivery fleet includes covered trucks that protect tiles from weather during transport. Porcelain and ceramic tiles are not temperature-sensitive, but covering ensures packaging integrity in wet weather.

**Q612:** Can you deliver to farms or rural properties?
**A:** We deliver to rural properties throughout South Africa using our freight partner network. Rural deliveries typically take longer and cost more than metro deliveries. Please contact us for a specific quote.

**Q613:** What is your policy on delivery delays due to stock availability?
**A:** If an item is out of stock, we'll notify you immediately with an estimated availability date. We can deliver available items first and follow up with the outstanding items, or hold the complete order for single delivery.

**Q614:** Can my architect or project manager coordinate delivery on my behalf?
**A:** Yes, your appointed representative can manage all delivery coordination with your authorisation. Simply provide their contact details when placing your order.

**Q615:** Do you offer express delivery for urgent replacement tiles?
**A:** Yes, if you need replacement tiles urgently (for example, to complete a project on time), we can arrange express delivery from our nearest warehouse stock. Additional charges apply for express service.

**Q616:** Can you deliver to a high-rise building with a goods lift?
**A:** Yes, we deliver to high-rise buildings with goods lift access. Please confirm lift dimensions and weight capacity to ensure large-format tiles or heavy stone can be accommodated.

**Q617:** How far in advance should I book delivery for a large renovation?
**A:** For large renovations, book your delivery at least 2 weeks in advance to secure your preferred date and allow time for any adjustments. This also gives us time to consolidate your complete order.

**Q618:** Do you have weekend or after-hours customer service for delivery queries?
**A:** Our showrooms are open Saturday mornings for delivery queries and scheduling. For urgent after-hours issues, you can leave a message and our team will respond first thing Monday morning.

**Q619:** Can you deliver to my builder's yard?
**A:** Yes, we can deliver to builder's yards or construction sites. Ensure the yard has secure storage for tiles and that someone will be present to receive and sign for the delivery.

**Q620:** What is your redelivery policy if I miss the first attempt?
**A:** If a redelivery is required because no one was available to receive the order, a redelivery fee will apply. We always attempt to contact you before redelivery to confirm someone will be present.

**Q621:** Can I add items to my order after delivery is scheduled?
**A:** Additional items can usually be added if the order hasn't been loaded for dispatch. Late additions may be delivered separately or may delay the original delivery, depending on timing.

**Q622:** Do you provide pallet jacks or trolleys for on-site movement?
**A:** Our delivery vehicles carry equipment to move pallets to your designated drop point. For complex sites, please discuss your specific requirements so we can send appropriate equipment and crew.

**Q623:** Can you deliver to a site with no road access?
**A:** For sites with limited access, we can arrange delivery to the nearest accessible point. You or your contractor will need to arrange onward transport from there. Please discuss access limitations when ordering.

**Q624:** Do you have insurance for goods in transit?
**A:** Yes, all goods in transit are insured against damage or loss. If any damage occurs during delivery, report it immediately with photographs and we will arrange replacement.

**Q625:** Can you deliver to multiple units in a block of flats?
**A:** Yes, for property developers or landlords renovating multiple units, we can arrange sequential or simultaneous deliveries to each unit as required.

**Q626:** How do I prepare my home for tile delivery?
**A:** Ensure clear access from the street to your property, allocate a dry, secure storage area, and have someone available to receive and inspect the delivery. Protect existing floors if tiles will be carried through finished areas.

**Q627:** Do you notify me before delivery arrives?
**A:** Yes, our delivery team will call you 30-60 minutes before arrival to confirm someone is available to receive the order. We also confirm the delivery window the day before.

**Q628:** Can you deliver tiles in bad weather?
**A:** Our covered trucks protect tiles in all weather conditions. However, extremely severe weather may cause delays for safety reasons. We'll contact you if weather affects your scheduled delivery.

**Q629:** Do you offer a loyalty discount on delivery for repeat customers?
**A:** Collector's Club members and repeat clients enjoy preferential delivery rates, priority scheduling, and occasional free delivery promotions as part of their loyalty benefits.

**Q630:** Can you coordinate delivery with other building material suppliers?
**A:** While we don't coordinate with other suppliers directly, we can align our delivery timing with your overall project schedule. Provide us with your builder's programme and we'll fit into it seamlessly.

## Category 11: Quality Assurance & Warranties

**Q631:** What warranty do your tiles come with?
**A:** Our tiles carry manufacturer warranties that vary by product. Standard porcelain and ceramic tiles typically have a 10-year warranty against manufacturing defects. Premium imported collections may have extended warranties. Warranty details are provided with your purchase.

**Q632:** What does the tile warranty cover?
**A:** The warranty covers manufacturing defects including dimensional inaccuracy, glaze defects, colour variation beyond stated tolerances, and structural failures under normal use. It does not cover installation defects, accidental damage, or normal wear.

**Q633:** How do you ensure batch consistency for my order?
**A:** We source tiles from batch-controlled production runs and verify shade and calibre consistency before dispatch. For large orders, we confirm batch numbers and can hold additional stock from the same batch for future requirements.

**Q634:** What happens if I receive tiles from different batches?
**A:** We take every precaution to supply tiles from the same batch. If different batches are unavoidable, we notify you in advance and can arrange for you to view samples from each batch before delivery.

**Q635:** Do you inspect tiles before delivery?
**A:** Yes, our warehouse team conducts quality checks on all outgoing orders, verifying shade, calibre, quantities, and packaging integrity. We also recommend that clients inspect deliveries upon receipt and before installation begins.

**Q636:** What should I check when my tile delivery arrives?
**A:** Check: correct product codes and quantities, shade batch numbers are consistent, no visible damage to packaging or tiles, correct sizes, and that the colour matches your approved sample. Report any discrepancies within 24 hours.

**Q637:** How do I report a defect in my tiles?
**A:** Contact our customer service team immediately with photographs, your order number, and a description of the issue. For visible defects, do not install the tiles — installation constitutes acceptance. For latent defects, report as soon as discovered.

**Q638:** What is your replacement policy for defective tiles?
**A:** If tiles are confirmed as defective, we will replace them at no cost, including delivery. If the exact batch is no longer available, we will offer the closest match from current stock or a full refund.

**Q639:** Do you offer a satisfaction guarantee?
**A:** We want every client to be completely satisfied with their selection. If you haven't installed the tiles and they're in original condition, full boxes of standard stock can be returned within 30 days for a refund or exchange.

**Q640:** What is the rectification quality of your premium tiles?
**A:** Our premium imported tiles have industry-leading rectification quality with dimensional tolerances of ±0.5mm or better. This ensures narrow grout joints and perfectly aligned installations.

**Q641:** How do I know my tiles are genuine imported products?
**A:** All our imported tiles come with manufacturer documentation indicating country of origin, production facility, and batch numbers. We source directly from manufacturers in Italy, Spain, France, Turkey, Brazil, India, and China.

**Q642:** What quality standards do your tiles meet?
**A:** All our tiles comply with SANS 1449 (South African National Standard for ceramic tiles), SANS 10107 (tiling installation), and relevant EN (European Norm) standards for imported products. Compliance certificates are available on request.

**Q643:** Do you test tiles for shade variation before delivery?
**A:** Yes, our warehouse verifies shade consistency within each batch. We also encourage clients to lay out several boxes of tiles before installation to check for any natural variation and blend tiles across the installation for a harmonious look.

**Q644:** What is the "shade variation" rating and why does it matter?

**Q645:** How do I prevent shade variation issues in my installation?
**A:** Blend tiles from multiple boxes during installation rather than installing box by box. This distributes any natural variation evenly. Your tiler should be instructed to blend as they work, particularly with V3-V4 rated tiles.

**Q646:** What happens if tiles are damaged during transit?
**A:** All deliveries are insured against transit damage. If you discover damage, photograph it immediately, note it on the delivery note, and contact us within 24 hours. We will arrange replacement at no cost.

**Q647:** Do you have quality control certificates for your tiles?
**A:** Yes, quality control certificates and test reports are available for all our tile ranges. These include test results for water absorption, breaking strength, chemical resistance, and slip resistance from independent laboratories.

**Q648:** Can I request a specific production batch?
**A:** If you require a specific batch (for example, to match existing tiles), we will check availability. We can hold stock from a specific batch for up to 30 days to ensure consistency with your existing installation.

**Q649:** What is your policy on hairline cracks in tiles?
**A:** Hairline cracks that are visible before installation indicate a manufacturing defect — do not install these tiles and contact us for replacement. Cracks that appear after installation are typically related to substrate movement or installation issues.

**Q650:** How do you handle customer complaints?
**A:** We take all complaints seriously. Our process: acknowledge within 24 hours, investigate thoroughly, propose a resolution, and implement promptly. Our goal is always complete customer satisfaction and preserving the relationship.

**Q651:** Do imported tiles have different quality to local tiles?
**A:** Our imported tiles from Italy and Spain generally offer superior rectification, print technology, and design refinement compared to mass-market local alternatives. However, we also stock high-quality local tiles that meet all SANS standards.

**Q652:** What is the life expectancy of your floor tiles?
**A:** Properly installed and maintained porcelain floor tiles can last 50+ years in residential applications. They are one of the most durable flooring investments you can make, outlasting most alternative floor coverings.

**Q653:** Do you guarantee the slip resistance ratings?
**A:** Slip resistance ratings are manufacturer specifications based on standardised testing. We provide these ratings in good faith and can share test reports. However, slip resistance can be affected by installation finish, maintenance, and contaminants.

**Q654:** What happens if my tiles fade over time?
**A:** Our glazed porcelain tiles have excellent colour fastness and should not fade under normal conditions, including direct sunlight. If fading occurs due to a manufacturing defect within the warranty period, we will investigate and replace as appropriate.

**Q655:** Do you offer extended warranties for premium tiles?
**A:** Some premium manufacturers offer extended warranties beyond the standard terms. Details are available for specific collections. Our advisors can explain warranty terms for each product range during your consultation.

**Q656:** How do I maintain my warranty?
**A:** To maintain warranty validity: install according to manufacturer guidelines and SANS 10107, use recommended adhesives and grouts, follow care and maintenance instructions, and report defects promptly. Keep all purchase documentation.

**Q657:** What is your policy on discontinued lines?
**A:** If a tile line is discontinued, we notify clients with pending orders immediately. We offer alternatives from current collections and can hold remaining stock for clients who need to match existing installations.

**Q658:** Do you have quality issues with tiles from China?
**A:** Our Chinese-sourced tiles come from reputable, quality-certified manufacturers. We rigorously quality-check all imports regardless of origin. Many Chinese factories produce excellent tiles that meet or exceed international standards.

**Q659:** What is "calibre" in tiles and why does it matter?
**A:** Calibre refers to the thickness of a tile. Tiles within the same batch should have consistent calibre (typically ±0.3mm for premium tiles). Calibre consistency is essential for achieving a flat, lippage-free installation.


**Q661:** What is the difference between first-grade and second-grade tiles?
**A:** First-grade tiles meet all manufacturer specifications with no visible defects. Second-grade tiles may have minor cosmetic imperfections but are structurally sound. We sell only first-grade tiles for residential renovations.

**Q662:** Do you offer a pre-installation inspection service?
**A:** For large projects, we can arrange a pre-installation site visit to verify that tiles, quantities, and specifications are correct before installation begins. This prevents costly errors and ensures smooth project execution.

**Q663:** What should I do if I find more defects during installation?
**A:** Stop installing immediately and contact us with details and photographs. Do not install tiles you suspect are defective. We will assess and replace promptly. Installing defective tiles may void replacement rights.

**Q664:** Do you have ISO certification?
**A:** Tiletoria operates under Invicta Holdings, which maintains rigorous quality management standards. Many of our manufacturing partners hold ISO 9001 quality management and ISO 14001 environmental management certifications.

**Q665:** How do I verify that my tiles are the correct product?
**A:** Each box has a product code, batch number, shade code, and calibre printed on it. Cross-reference these with your order confirmation. If anything doesn't match, contact us before opening the boxes.

**Q666:** What is "rectified" quality control?
**A:** Rectified tiles undergo precision machining after firing. Quality control checks include dimensional accuracy, squareness of edges, and surface flatness. Premium rectified tiles have tolerances of ±0.5mm or better on all dimensions.

**Q667:** Do you keep records of my purchase for warranty purposes?
**A:** Yes, we maintain purchase records for warranty purposes. However, we recommend keeping your invoice and delivery note in a safe place as backup documentation for any future warranty claims.

**Q668:** What is your policy on tiles that don't match my expectations?
**A:** If the tiles don't match the sample you approved or the description provided, contact us immediately. If they're uninstalled and in original condition, we will arrange an exchange or refund per our returns policy.

**Q669:** How do you handle out-of-specification tiles?
**A:** Any tiles found to be outside manufacturer specifications during our quality checks are rejected and returned to the manufacturer. We do not dispatch out-of-specification products to clients.

**Q670:** Do your tiles come with installation quality guidelines?
**A:** Yes, all tiles include basic installation guidelines. For complex projects, our technical team provides detailed method statements. We strongly recommend using professional installers familiar with the specific requirements of your selected tiles.

**Q671:** What quality checks do you perform on incoming stock?
**A:** Incoming stock is checked for: correct product codes, batch consistency, shade variation within specification, dimensional accuracy, packaging integrity, and quantity verification before being released for sale.

**Q672:** Can I visit your warehouse to inspect my order before delivery?

**Q673:** Do you have a quality assurance certificate I can give to my insurance company?
**A:** We can provide product compliance certificates, technical data sheets, and purchase documentation that may be required by insurance companies for high-value renovations. Contact us with your specific requirements.


**Q675:** How do I know if a tile defect is manufacturing or installation related?
**A:** Manufacturing defects typically appear uniformly across multiple tiles from the same batch. Installation-related issues (cracking, lifting, lippage) usually have irregular patterns related to substrate conditions or installation technique. Our technical team can assess and advise.

**Q676:** Do you offer insurance for tiles stored on site?
**A:** Tiles stored on site are your responsibility once delivered. We recommend ensuring your builder's all-risk insurance or homeowner's policy covers building materials on site. Transit insurance covers delivery only.

**Q677:** What is the "frost resistance" rating and do I need it?
**A:** Frost resistance indicates a tile's ability to withstand freeze-thaw cycles without cracking. All our porcelain tiles are frost-resistant. This is essential for outdoor use and recommended for homes in cold-climate areas like the Highveld.

**Q678:** Can I get a refund if I change my mind about my tile selection?
**A:** Full, unopened boxes of standard stock tiles can be returned within 30 days for a refund or credit. Special-order and imported tiles cannot be returned unless defective. Samples should be returned to receive deposit refunds.

**Q679:** Do you offer a "right first time" guarantee?
**A:** While we can't guarantee against installation issues, we provide comprehensive pre-installation support — technical data, method statements, installer guidance — to maximise the probability of a successful first-time installation.

**Q680:** How do I store tiles correctly before installation?
**A:** Store tiles flat, indoors, in a dry environment, protected from direct sunlight and moisture. Keep boxes sealed until needed. Stack carefully to prevent damage. Do not store on unprotected outdoor sites.

**Q681:** What is your policy on tiles that arrive with chipped edges?
**A:** Tiles with chipped edges that are visible before installation are considered damaged and will be replaced at no cost. Report damage within 24 hours of delivery with photographs. Do not install chipped tiles.

**Q682:** Do you test your tiles for lead or other harmful substances?
**A:** Our tiles are tested for compliance with international safety standards including lead content, radioactivity, and chemical emissions. Porcelain and ceramic tiles are inert, non-toxic materials safe for all residential applications.

**Q683:** What quality documentation should I keep for my renovation records?
**A:** Keep your invoice, delivery note, product specifications, technical data sheets, warranty documents, waterproofing certificates, and installation compliance certificates. These are valuable for future maintenance, warranty claims, and property sale.

**Q684:** Do you have third-party quality audits?
**A:** Our manufacturing partners undergo regular quality audits, and we conduct our own incoming quality inspections. We're also happy to facilitate independent third-party testing if required for specific projects.

**Q685:** What happens if my tiler damages tiles during installation?
**A:** Damage caused during installation is the responsibility of the installer. We recommend ensuring your tiler has appropriate insurance. We can supply replacement tiles at standard cost if additional quantities are needed.

**Q686:** How do you handle quality issues with special-order tiles?
**A:** Special-order tiles are inspected upon arrival at our warehouse. If quality issues are found, we contact the manufacturer immediately and will not release substandard product. We keep you informed throughout the resolution process.

**Q687:** Do you offer a final inspection after installation?
**A:** We don't conduct formal post-installation inspections as standard, but our technical team can assess installation concerns if you contact us with specific issues. We can also recommend independent inspectors.

**Q688:** What is the shelf life of uninstalled tiles?

**Q689:** Do you have environmental quality certifications for your tiles?
**A:** Many of our European manufacturing partners hold environmental certifications such as Ecolabel, LEED contribution, and ISO 14001. We can provide environmental product declarations for green building projects.

**Q690:** Can I get a letter of conformance for my body corporate?
**A:** Yes, we can provide a letter of conformance confirming that your selected tiles meet relevant SANS standards and are suitable for the intended application. Contact us with your body corporate's specific requirements.

**Q691:** What is your procedure for handling a warranty claim?
**A:** To initiate a warranty claim: contact us with your order details, describe the issue, provide photographs, and allow us to inspect. We will assess whether the issue is covered by warranty and propose a resolution within 5 business days.

**Q692:** How do you ensure tiles are suitable for South African conditions?
**A:** We select tiles specifically suited to South African conditions — UV stability for our strong sun, frost resistance for Highveld winters, and slip ratings appropriate for our building practices. All tiles comply with SANS standards.

**Q693:** Do you offer a "no questions asked" return policy?
**A:** Our returns policy requires tiles to be uninstalled, in original packaging, and returned within 30 days. Special orders cannot be returned. We assess returns to ensure products are resalable before processing refunds.

**Q694:** What quality should I expect from your exclusive our exclusive mosaic range range?
**A:** our exclusive mosaic range range meets the highest quality standards with carefully sourced materials, precise mesh mounting, consistent colour batches, and superior adhesion. It's one of our most popular and trusted exclusive collections.

**Q695:** Do you keep spare stock from my batch in case I need replacements later?
**A:** We don't automatically hold spare stock, but you can order additional boxes from the same batch at the time of purchase. We recommend buying 10-15% extra for wastage and future repairs.

## Category 12: Comparison with Mass Retailers

**Q696:** How is Tiletoria different from competitor retailers?
**A:** Tiletoria focuses on the premium and professional market with exclusive imported collections, dedicated Spec Lab facilities, and comprehensive project support. While competitor retailers is a strong retail brand, we differentiate through design depth, exclusive ranges, and white-glove service for discerning renovators.

**Q697:** Why should I choose Tiletoria over competitor retailers?
**A:** competitor retailers focuses on the mass market with price-competitive, volume-oriented products. Tiletoria offers exclusive imported collections, premium service, professional design consultation, and products you won't find in mass-market retailers. Our clients value design differentiation and quality.

**Q698:** Are your prices higher than mass-market tile retailers?
**A:** Our premium and imported collections are priced higher than mass-market alternatives, typically 20-40% above competitor retailers and similar retailers for comparable categories. However, we also offer mid-range options, and our value extends beyond price to include service, exclusivity, and quality.

**Q699:** Do you price match competitor retailers or competitor retailers?
**A:** We don't blanket price-match mass retailers. However, we can review comparable quotations and discuss how we can add value through service, exclusivity, samples, delivery, and project support. Our focus is on overall project value.

**Q700:** What do you offer that mass retailers don't?
**A:** We offer: exclusive imported collections not available at mass retailers, the Spec Lab professional environment, complimentary design consultations, full-size sample loans, quantity take-off services, project coordination, dedicated account management, and access to our over 20,000m² inventory.

**Q701:** Are your premium tiles really worth the extra cost?
**A:** For affluent renovators, premium tiles offer superior rectification (narrower grout lines), more sophisticated print technology for realistic stone and wood replicas, exclusive designs, better batch consistency, and the design credibility that adds value to high-end homes.

**Q702:** Can I find the same tiles at Tiletoria and competitor retailers?
**A:** Some overlap may exist on select ranges, but our exclusive collections — particularly our exclusive mosaic range, our Italian designer ranges, and Spec Lab imports — are not available at mass retailers. Many clients come to us specifically for tiles they can't find elsewhere.

**Q703:** How does your service compare to retail tile stores?
**A:** Our service model is consultative and relationship-driven rather than transactional. You receive dedicated attention from trained design advisors, private consultation spaces, sample loan services, and ongoing project support — a significant step up from standard retail service.

**Q704:** Do you offer the same brands as other tile retailers?
**A:** We stock many of the same international brands as premium retailers, but we also have exclusive distribution agreements with several Italian, Spanish, and French manufacturers. our exclusive mosaic range and our laminate flooring ranges are exclusive to Tiletoria.

**Q705:** Is the quality of your mid-range tiles comparable to competitor retailers's premium range?
**A:** Our mid-range tiles typically offer comparable or better quality than mass-market premium ranges, with better rectification and more sophisticated design. The key difference is our curation — we select products that meet our quality and design standards.

**Q706:** Why do interior designers prefer Tiletoria over mass retailers?
**A:** Designers value our exclusive collections, Spec Lab facilities, trade pricing, sample library, professional consultation environment, technical support, and the knowledge that their clients won't see the same tiles in every other home.

**Q707:** Do you have a loyalty program like other retailers?
**A:** Yes, our Collector's Club offers loyalty benefits including priority consultation booking, exclusive previews of new collections, invitations to design events, referral rewards, and preferential pricing. It's designed for discerning, repeat clients.

**Q708:** How does your showroom experience differ from a typical tile shop?
**A:** Our showrooms are designed as inspirational design destinations rather than conventional retail stores. The Spec Lab offers private consultation rooms, premium finishes, curated displays, refreshments, and a relaxed, unhurried environment for selection.

**Q709:** Do you have more exclusive designs than competitor retailers or competitor retailers?
**A:** Yes, exclusivity is a cornerstone of our offering. Our Spec Lab collections, our exclusive mosaic range range, and many of our Italian and Spanish imports are exclusive to Tiletoria or have very limited distribution in South Africa.

**Q710:** Are your prices negotiable like at some other retailers?
**A:** We offer competitive pricing and volume discounts as standard. For substantial projects, we can discuss tailored packages that include value-adds rather than simply discounting. Our pricing reflects the quality and service we provide.

**Q711:** What makes Tiletoria the choice for luxury renovations?
**A:** Luxury renovators choose Tiletoria for: exclusive imported collections, professional design support, quality assurance, white-glove service, the Spec Lab experience, project coordination, and the confidence that their finish will be unique and of the highest standard.

**Q712:** Can I see reviews or testimonials from other renovation clients?
**A:** Yes, we have testimonials from renovation clients, interior designers, and architects who have used Tiletoria for premium projects. Our design advisors can share relevant case studies and references during your consultation.

**Q713:** Do you have a price guarantee if I find the same tile cheaper elsewhere?
**A:** For identical products from the same manufacturer and batch, we will review any quotation and discuss how we can match or improve the overall offer. Our exclusive products are not available elsewhere for direct comparison.

**Q714:** How does Tiletoria compare to buying tiles directly from overseas?
**A:** Buying directly from overseas involves significant risks — shipping damage, import duties, VAT, currency fluctuation, no local support, and no warranty recourse. Tiletoria handles all of this, provides local stockholding, and offers full after-sales support.

**Q715:** Do you offer trade discounts like other tile retailers?
**A:** Yes, we offer trade pricing for registered architects, interior designers, quantity surveyors, and builders through our Spec Lab. Trade clients also enjoy priority service, sample library access, and dedicated account management.

**Q716:** What is the difference between your Spec Lab and a regular tile shop?
**A:** The Spec Lab is a purpose-built professional facility with private consultation rooms, comprehensive sample libraries, the full range of premium collections, and a design-focused environment. It's a significant step up from a conventional retail tile shop.

**Q717:** Why should I not just buy tiles online from a discount retailer?
**A:** Online tile purchases don't allow you to see, touch, and compare tiles in person. Colour variation, texture, and quality can only be assessed physically. Our design consultation, sample service, and quality assurance provide value that online discount retailers cannot match.

**Q718:** Do you have the same tile brands as global retailers like our imported Spanish tile ranges?
**A:** We stock many of the same quality tiers as international premium brands. While we may not carry every our imported Spanish tile ranges line, our Italian and Spanish imports offer comparable quality and design at competitive prices in the South African market.

**Q719:** How do I know I'm getting good value at Tiletoria?
**A:** Value at Tiletoria extends beyond the tile price to include: exclusive designs, quality assurance, professional consultation, sample loans, project support, reliable delivery, and the confidence that your renovation will have a unique, premium finish.

**Q720:** Can I get a complete bathroom package cheaper elsewhere?
**A:** Mass retailers may offer cheaper package deals, but our packages include premium products, professional specification, and comprehensive support. For discerning renovators, the quality, design, and service difference justifies the investment.

**Q721:** Do you offer end-of-range sales like other retailers?
**A:** Yes, we periodically have end-of-range promotions on discontinued lines. These offer excellent value for clients who find a tile they love at a reduced price. Collector's Club members receive early notification of sales.

**Q722:** How does your delivery service compare to other tile retailers?
**A:** We offer scheduled, coordinated delivery with 400 tons/day capacity from our over 20,000m² warehousing. We actively coordinate with your tiler's schedule and offer white-glove options — a level of service beyond standard retail delivery.

**Q723:** Do you have more premium options than competitor retailers?
**A:** Our Spec Lab collections, exclusive imports, and specialty ranges give us one of the deepest premium offerings in the South African market. Interior designers and architects specifically come to us for collections not available elsewhere.

**Q724:** What do architects and designers say about Tiletoria vs other suppliers?
**A:** Architects and designers value our exclusive collections, professional environment, technical support, trade pricing, and reliable project execution. Many specify Tiletoria exclusively for their premium residential projects.

**Q725:** Is it true that Tiletoria only serves the trade?
**A:** No, we serve both trade professionals and private consumers. Our showrooms are open to everyone, and our design advisors welcome private renovation clients. The Spec Lab has professional facilities but serves both trade and private clients.

**Q726:** How does your customer service compare after the sale?
**A:** We maintain ongoing relationships with our clients through the Collector's Club, post-installation support, maintenance guidance, and future project assistance. Many clients return to us for subsequent renovations and refer friends and family.

**Q727:** Do you have a wider range than competitor retailers?
**A:** While competitor retailers may have more SKUs in their total range, our range is curated for quality and design. We focus on premium, imported, and exclusive collections that you won't find in the mass market, giving us greater depth in the high-end segment.

**Q728:** Why should I pay more for the same look I can get cheaper elsewhere?
**A:** The "same look" often differs significantly in quality — rectification, print technology, glaze quality, and batch consistency all affect the final appearance. Premium tiles look better, install better, and maintain their appearance longer than cheaper alternatives.

**Q729:** Do you offer interest-free credit like some retailers?
**A:** We don't offer direct credit facilities. However, we provide detailed formal quotations that clients can use for home improvement loans or mortgage access facilities through their banks.

**Q730:** Can I get a better deal if I buy everything from Tiletoria?
**A:** Yes, bundling your tile, flooring, adhesive, grout, and accessory purchases typically qualifies for better volume pricing. Your design advisor will create a comprehensive package with preferential pricing.


**Q732:** Do you have showrooms in the same areas as other tile retailers?
**A:** We have showrooms in Johannesburg (Northriding + Spec Lab Sandton), Cape Town (Paarden Eiland), Cornubia, and Paarden Eiland — covering South Africa's major renovation centres.

**Q733:** What is Tiletoria's market position in South Africa?
**A:** Tiletoria is one of South Africa's largest tile distributors, with Invicta Holdings as our majority shareholder (75%). We occupy the premium-to-luxury segment, serving affluent consumers, trade professionals, and major corporate clients.

**Q734:** Do you offer a "best price" guarantee?
**A:** We guarantee competitive pricing within the premium tile segment. For identical products, we'll review comparable offers. Our value proposition encompasses quality, exclusivity, service, and design — not just price.

**Q735:** How do I justify the higher cost to my renovation partner?
**A:** Frame it as an investment in quality, exclusivity, and long-term value. Premium tiles add property value, won't need replacing, and deliver the aesthetic that justifies the overall renovation spend. The 20-40% tile premium is a small portion of the total renovation budget.

**Q736:** Can I see the difference between your tiles and cheaper alternatives side by side?
**A:** Yes, our showrooms display premium and standard options side by side. The difference in rectification, print quality, texture, and overall refinement becomes immediately apparent when you see them together.

**Q737:** Do you have corporate clients that validate your quality?
**A:** Yes, our corporate clients include major national retailers, major national retailers, major national retailers, major national banks, major national banks, major restaurant chains, major national retailers, major retail groups, major automotive brands, major automotive brands, and major homeware retailers. These brands choose Tiletoria for quality, reliability, and professional service.

**Q738:** How does your returns policy compare to other retailers?
**A:** Our returns policy is standard for the industry — uninstalled, original packaging, within 30 days. Where we differ is in our willingness to work with clients to resolve issues and find solutions that maintain the relationship.

**Q739:** What percentage of your clients are repeat customers or referrals?
**A:** A significant portion of our private renovation clients are repeat customers or referrals from satisfied clients, designers, and architects. This speaks to the quality of our products and the strength of our relationships.

**Q740:** Do you have clearance sections with discounts comparable to other retailers?
**A:** We offer end-of-range promotions, but our clearance stock tends to be premium products at genuinely reduced prices rather than low-quality stock being liquidated. Collector's Club members receive first notification.

## Category 13: Sanware & Bathroom Fixtures

**Q741:** Do you sell bathroom fixtures and tapware?
**A:** Yes, Tiletoria stocks a comprehensive range of sanware including toilets, basins, baths, showers, and tapware from leading brands including our premium tapware range, our tapware range, our tapware range, and our tapware range. We can coordinate your fixture selection with your tile choices.

**Q742:** What our premium tapware range products do you stock?
**A:** We stock our premium tapware range's premium range of mixers, showers, and bathroom accessories. our premium tapware range is renowned for German engineering, exceptional quality, and innovative technologies like SilkMove and StarLight chrome finishes.

**Q743:** What is our tapware range and how does it compare to other brands?
**A:** our tapware range is a quality sanware brand offering contemporary designs at competitive prices. It provides excellent value for discerning renovators who want modern aesthetics without the premium price tag of European brands.

**Q744:** Do you stock freestanding baths?
**A:** Yes, we offer a range of freestanding baths in various styles — modern sculptural designs, traditional clawfoot, and minimalist rectangular forms. Materials include acrylic and stone composite options.

**Q745:** Can I coordinate my tapware finish with my tile trim?
**A:** Absolutely. We recommend selecting your tapware finish (chrome, brushed brass, matte black, brushed nickel) and then choosing matching or complementary tile trim profiles for a cohesive design.

**Q746:** What toilet options do you offer?
**A:** We offer close-coupled toilets, back-to-wall toilets, wall-hung toilets with concealed cisterns, and rimless designs for enhanced hygiene. Our range spans from quality entry-level to premium European designs.

**Q747:** Do you sell bathroom basins?
**A:** Yes, we stock a variety of basins including countertop vessel basins, under-counter basins, inset basins, and wall-hung basins in various shapes, sizes, and materials to suit every bathroom style.

**Q748:** What is the difference between wall-hung and floor-standing toilets?
**A:** Wall-hung toilets have the cistern concealed in the wall, with the bowl suspended above the floor. They create a sleek, modern look and make floor cleaning easier. Floor-standing toilets are the traditional style with a visible cistern.

**Q749:** Do you stock frameless shower screens?
**A:** Yes, we offer frameless and semi-frameless shower screens that complement modern tiled showers beautifully. Frameless glass allows your feature tiles to be fully visible and creates a spacious, luxury feel.

**Q750:** Can you recommend tapware that works with stone-look tiles?
**A:** Stone-look tiles pair beautifully with matte black tapware for contrast, brushed brass for warmth, or chrome for a classic look. Our advisors can show you tapware samples against your selected tiles.

**Q751:** Do you sell bath and basin mixers?
**A:** Yes, we stock a full range of mixers including basin mixers, bath mixers, bath/shower mixers, and concealed shower mixers. Available in various heights, styles, and finishes to suit your design.

**Q752:** What shower systems do you offer?
**A:** Our shower range includes overhead rain showers, hand showers, shower columns, thermostatic mixers, and complete shower systems. We offer both exposed and concealed installation options.

**Q753:** Do you have bidet toilets or bidet spray attachments?
**A:** Yes, we offer both integrated bidet toilets (smart toilets) and handheld bidet spray attachments (health faucets). These are increasingly popular for enhanced hygiene and comfort.

**Q754:** Can I see sanware displayed with tiles in your showroom?
**A:** Yes, our showrooms feature realistic bathroom displays where sanware is installed with tiles, allowing you to see how fixtures and tiles work together in a complete bathroom setting.

**Q755:** Do you offer bathroom accessories like towel rails and soap dishes?
**A:** Yes, we stock a range of bathroom accessories including towel rails, rings and heated towel rails, soap dishes, toilet roll holders, and robe hooks in coordinating finishes.

**Q756:** What is the warranty on your sanware products?
**A:** Warranty periods vary by brand: our premium tapware range typically offers 5-year warranties on mechanical parts and 2 years on finishes. our tapware range, our tapware range, and our tapware range offer 2-5 year warranties depending on the product. Full warranty details are provided with purchase.

**Q757:** Do you have matching accessory sets in different finishes?
**A:** Yes, we offer coordinated accessory sets in chrome, brushed brass, matte black, brushed nickel, and gunmetal finishes. Matching all accessories in the same finish creates a cohesive, designer look.

**Q758:** Can I order sanware that is not in stock?
**A:** Yes, we can special-order items from our supplier catalogues. Lead times vary by brand — typically 2-8 weeks for European imports. A deposit is required for special orders.

**Q759:** Do you sell kitchen sink mixers?
**A:** Yes, we stock kitchen sink mixers including pull-out spray mixers, filtered water taps, boiling water taps, and standard swivel spout designs from our premium tapware range and other leading brands.

**Q760:** What is the price range for our premium tapware range bathroom mixers?
**A:** our premium tapware range bathroom mixers range from approximately R2,500 for entry-level designs to R15,000+ for premium collections with advanced technologies. The investment reflects German engineering quality and long-term reliability.

**Q761:** Do you offer package deals on complete bathroom fixtures?
**A:** Yes, we can create bathroom fixture packages bundling toilet, basin, bath, shower, and tapware at preferential pricing. This ensures all fixtures coordinate in style and finish.

**Q762:** What is a thermostatic shower mixer and do I need one?
