**A:** Ask for the "Contractor Fast-Mover List" at the trade counter or via WhatsApp. This list is updated quarterly and shows the most reliable stock items for quoting with confidence.

**Q9:** What happens if my chosen tile is out of stock?
**A:** Your trade counter consultant will check alternative branches for availability, suggest the nearest equivalent from stock, or provide the lead time for replenishment (typically 8-12 weeks for imports).

**Q10:** How do I ensure batch consistency for my project?
**A:** Always specify the batch number when ordering, and order your full quantity in one go. If the project requires multiple deliveries, we allocate stock from the same batch and reserve it against your order.

**Q11:** What is "shade variation" and how do I manage it?
**A:** Shade variation refers to natural colour differences between tile batches. All tiles have a shade rating (V1-V4) on the box. Always check the shade number on the box, mix tiles from multiple boxes during installation, and order 10% extra from the same batch.

**Q12:** Can you check stock at other branches and transfer it?
**A:** Yes, inter-branch transfers can be arranged. Transfer time is typically 3-5 business days between major branches. A transfer fee may apply. Your account manager can coordinate this.

**Q13:** How much stock do you hold in total?
**A:** We maintain over over 23,000m² of warehousing across 3 locations — Cape Town (10,000m²), Durban (8,000m²), Johannesburg (5,000m²), and Paarden Eiland (2,000m²). Over 200 containers of imported products arrive monthly.

**Q14:** Can I place a forward order for stock that's not yet arrived?
**A:** Yes, for incoming container stock we accept forward orders at current pricing with a deposit (typically 30-50%). Your stock is allocated on arrival and you're notified for collection or delivery.

**Q15:** How do I know when out-of-stock items are back in stock?
**A:** Ask to be added to the "back-in-stock" notification list via WhatsApp. You'll receive an automatic message when the product arrives. You can also check with your account manager for expected container dates.

**Q16:** What does "available to order" mean vs "in stock"?
**A:** "In stock" means the product is on the shelf now and available for immediate collection or delivery. "Available to order" means it's a stocked line currently out but being replenished, with a known lead time.

**Q17:** Can I get a stock report for all products I use regularly?
**A:** Yes, your account manager can provide a personalised stock availability report for your regularly used products, updated weekly if needed. This is useful for planning multiple projects.

**Q18:** Do you keep stock of the same tile for extended periods?
**A:** Core range tiles (top 50 SKUs) are maintained continuously. Seasonal and fashion colours may have limited availability windows. Your account manager can advise which lines are core vs seasonal.

**Q19:** What is a "batch number" and why is it important?
**A:** The batch number (also called tone or shade number) is printed on the tile box. Tiles from different batches may have slight colour variations. Always use the same batch number throughout a single installation area.

**Q20:** Can I check stock availability on weekends?
**A:** WhatsApp stock checks are monitored during Saturday trade hours (8:00 AM - 1:00 PM at most branches). Sunday stock checks are not available, but you can send a message for Monday morning response.

**Q21:** How quickly does stock deplete during busy periods?
**A:** Popular lines can move 500-1000m² per day during peak season (September-November). If you have a confirmed project, always reserve or order in advance rather than relying on walk-in availability.

**Q22:** Can I see photos of actual stock before I collect?
**A:** Yes, WhatsApp a request for a photo of the current batch to your branch. Counter staff can send a photo of the pallet or open box showing the actual tile colour and finish.

**Q23:** What happens if the stock batch number doesn't match my previous purchase?
**A:** If batch numbers differ, inspect the tiles under natural light before leaving the branch. If the shade difference is unacceptable, we can check other branches for matching batch stock or advise on lead time.

**Q24:** Do you hold stock of discontinued lines?
**A:** Discontinued lines are cleared and not restocked. If you need to match a discontinued tile for repairs, bring a sample to the trade counter and we'll identify the closest current equivalent.

**Q25:** Can I get a container date schedule for planning purposes?
**A:** Your account manager can provide an approximate container arrival schedule for specific product origins. This helps you plan orders for 8-12 week lead-time products.

**Q26:** Is stock availability the same at trade counters and showrooms?
**A:** Trade counters hold bulk contractor stock. Showrooms carry display boards and limited carry stock. Always purchase through the trade counter for full availability and contractor pricing.

**Q27:** How do I reserve stock for a project starting next month?
**A:** For projects starting within 30 days, reserve stock with a 30% deposit. For projects beyond 30 days, a forward order with scheduled delivery is recommended.

**Q28:** What is your stock accuracy rate?
**A:** Our warehouse management system maintains 98%+ stock accuracy. Discrepancies are rare and usually resolved within 24 hours. Physical stock counts are conducted monthly.

**Q29:** Can I check stock for multiple products in one WhatsApp message?
**A:** Yes, list all the products you need in one message with codes or descriptions. We'll respond with the availability status for each item. Keep it to 10 items per message for fastest response.

**Q30:** Do you have an app for checking stock?
**A:** We don't currently have a stock-checking app. WhatsApp is our primary real-time stock check channel for contractors. We're exploring app development for future release.

**Q31:** What does "low stock" mean when I check?
**A:** "Low stock" means the remaining quantity is below our reorder threshold (typically less than 200m²). Purchase immediately if you need it, or reserve to avoid disappointment.

**Q32:** Can I get a stock availability guarantee in writing?
**A:** For confirmed orders with deposits or purchase orders, we provide a written stock reservation confirmation. Walk-in availability cannot be guaranteed without reservation.

**Q33:** How do I handle a stock-out mid-project?
**A:** This is every contractor's nightmare. Immediately WhatsApp your account manager with the product code and batch number. We check all branches, identify the closest match from stock, or expedite a replenishment order.

**Q34:** Do you carry more stock of certain sizes?
**A:** Yes, 600x600mm and 800x800mm porcelain floor tiles have the deepest stock holdings as they're the most popular. Large-format (1200x600mm, 1200x1200mm) and mosaic stock is more limited.

**Q35:** Can I order stock that's only available at another branch?
**A:** Yes, we can arrange inter-branch transfer to your preferred collection branch. Allow 3-5 business days. Alternatively, collect directly from the branch that holds the stock.

**Q36:** What is the fastest-moving tile colour right now?
**A:** Stock trends change seasonally, but grey tones (light, mid, and dark grey) consistently move fastest, followed by beige and wood-look porcelain. Ask your account manager for the current "hot list."

**Q37:** Can I set up automatic stock alerts for products I use regularly?
**A:** Yes, your account manager can set up notifications for when specific products go on promotion, are running low, or when new stock arrives. This is useful for products you buy repeatedly.

**Q38:** Do you have clearance stock available?
**A:** Yes, clearance stock is available at the trade counter and announced via WhatsApp. These are end-of-line, discontinued, or overstocked items at significantly reduced prices.

**Q39:** How do I know if a tile is being discontinued?
**A:** Discontinuation notices are sent to trade account holders via WhatsApp and email, typically 3 months before the final stock is sold. Your account manager also flags discontinuations during conversations.

**Q40:** Can I get a pallet or full container price for very large projects?
**A:** Yes, for projects requiring pallet quantities or full containers (1000m²+), speak to your account manager for project-specific pricing and stock allocation.

**Q41:** Do you keep stock of European-imported tiles?
**A:** Yes, we import from Italy, Spain, France, and Turkey. European stock is held in all major branches, with the deepest holding in Cape Town and Johannesburg.

**Q42:** What happens to stock during load shedding?
**A:** Our warehouses have backup power for stock management systems, lighting, and loading operations. Trade counter hours may be affected — check WhatsApp for any branch-specific schedule changes.

**Q43:** Can I request a specific pallet or batch number when ordering?
**A:** Yes, specify your batch number preference when placing the order. If that batch is available, we'll allocate it. If not, we'll advise on the closest available match.

**Q44:** How do I find equivalent tiles if my first choice is out of stock?
**A:** Your trade counter consultant can show you the closest matches from available stock in terms of colour, size, finish, and price. Bring a sample of your first choice for colour matching.

**Q45:** Is stock availability better at certain branches?
**A:** Cape Town (Paarden Eiland) and Cornubia have the largest warehouses and deepest stock. Johannesburg and PE carry full ranges with slightly less depth on some specialty items.

**Q46:** Can I see the stock list for a specific brand?

**Q47:** How do I check if a specific shade of a tile is in stock?
**A:** When checking stock, always include the colour/shade name and the product code if you have it. Some tiles come in multiple shades within the same range.

**Q48:** Can I get a weekly stock availability report sent to me?
**A:** Yes, your account manager can set up a weekly stock report for your core product list, delivered via WhatsApp or email every Monday morning.

**Q49:** What should I do if I arrive and the stock doesn't match what I was told on WhatsApp?
**A:** Speak to the trade counter supervisor immediately. If stock was sold between your check and arrival, we'll locate the nearest equivalent, arrange a transfer, or offer a credit against a future delivery.

**Q50:** Do you have stock of under-tile waterproofing systems?
**A:** Yes, full waterproofing systems (membranes, primers, accessories) are stocked at all branches. These move quickly during wet season, so check availability for large projects.

**Q51:** How do I know the difference between similar-looking tiles in stock?
**A:** Ask to see the actual boxes or samples at the trade counter. Each tile has a unique product code, and staff can explain the differences in specification, origin, and price.

**Q52:** Can I get stock held specifically for my repeat business?
**A:** For contractors with consistent monthly volumes, we can agree on a "contractor hold" arrangement where minimum stock levels are maintained for your core products. Discuss with your account manager.

**Q53:** What is your policy on selling the last pallet of a discontinued line?
**A:** The last pallet of a discontinued line is sold as-is with no returns accepted. We'll advise you of the discontinuation status so you can decide if you want to purchase for spares.


**Q55:** Do you keep stock of SANS-compliant anti-slip tiles?
**A:** Yes, anti-slip tiles meeting SANS requirements (R10-R13 ratings) are stocked at all branches. Specify the slip rating you need when checking stock.

**Q56:** How do I find out about new products before they hit the trade counter?
**A:** Your account manager notifies established contractors about new arrivals before general release. Spec Lab also previews upcoming ranges for premium project work.

**Q57:** Can I request stock to be moved to a specific location in the warehouse for quick collection?
**A:** For large pre-ordered collections, we can pre-stage your stock at the dispatch area. Give us 24 hours' notice and your collection time, and your order will be ready to load.

**Q58:** What happens if stock arrives damaged from the container?
**A:** Damaged container stock is identified during unpacking and not released for sale. If you discover damage after collection, refer to our returns policy for damaged stock exchanges.

**Q59:** Do you have a "Contractor SOS" line for urgent stock requests?
**A:** Each branch has an after-hours emergency contact for urgent stock requests. Your account manager will share this number. Note that after-hours service is for emergencies only.

**Q60:** Can I get stock delivered directly to multiple sites from one order?
**A:** Yes, multi-drop deliveries can be arranged. Each drop incurs a separate delivery charge. Provide your account manager with the delivery schedule and site addresses.

---

## Category 4: Ordering & Collection (55 Q&As)

**Q1:** How do I place an order as a trade account holder?
**A:** Orders can be placed in person at the trade counter, via WhatsApp, by phone, or by email to your account manager. Large or complex orders should be confirmed in writing.

**Q2:** Can I order via WhatsApp?
**A:** Yes, WhatsApp ordering is available at all branches. Send your product list with codes, quantities, and delivery/collection preference. You'll receive an order confirmation and invoice via return WhatsApp.

**Q3:** What are the trade counter hours?
**A:** Trade counter hours are Monday-Friday 7:00 AM - 5:00 PM, and Saturday 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM. Some branches open earlier (6:45 AM) for contractors — check with your local branch.

**Q4:** What is the cut-off time for same-day collection?
**A:** Orders placed by 10:00 AM are typically ready for same-day collection by 2:00 PM. Orders placed after 10:00 AM are ready by 10:00 AM the next business day.

**Q5:** Can I place an order and collect the next morning?
**A:** Yes, next-day collection is standard for orders placed by 4:00 PM. Your order will be picked, checked, and ready for loading from 7:00 AM the next day.

**Q6:** Do I need to place an order in advance, or can I just walk in?
**A:** Walk-in purchases are welcome at the trade counter for stock items. For large quantities (200m²+), advance ordering ensures stock is reserved and picked for faster loading.

**Q7:** Can someone else collect my order on my behalf?
**A:** Yes, authorise your collector by sending their name, ID number, and vehicle registration to your account manager via WhatsApp. They must present ID at collection.

**Q8:** What information do I need to provide when ordering?
**A:** Provide your trade account number, product codes or descriptions, quantities (in m² or number of boxes), your name, contact number, and collection/delivery preference.

**Q9:** Can I change my order after it's been placed?
**A:** Orders can be amended if they haven't been picked yet. Call the trade counter immediately. Once picked, amendments may not be possible, but we can process a separate return/exchange.

**Q10:** How do I know my order is ready for collection?
**A:** You'll receive a WhatsApp or SMS notification when your order is picked and ready. You can also call the trade counter to check order status.

**Q11:** What payment methods are accepted at the trade counter?
**A:** Cash, EFT (proof required), debit card, and credit card. Credit account holders can sign against their account. Instant EFT via SnapScan or Zapper is also available at most branches.

**Q12:** Can I pay on collection rather than in advance?
**A:** Cash and card customers pay on collection. Credit account holders sign and the amount is added to their account. No advance payment is required for cash customers unless it's a special order.

**Q13:** Is there a dedicated collection area for contractors?
**A:** Yes, all branches have a dedicated contractor collection bay with forklift assistance and quick loading. Follow the "Trade Collection" signage at your branch.

**Q14:** Can I collect outside normal hours?
**A:** Pre-arranged early collections (from 6:00 AM) can be organised for large projects. After-hours collections are not generally available. Speak to your account manager to arrange.

**Q15:** How long does it take to load my bakkie at collection?
**A:** For pre-picked orders, loading takes 10-20 minutes depending on quantity. For walk-in purchases, allow 30-45 minutes for picking and loading. Pre-order for fastest turnaround.

**Q16:** Do you help with loading?
**A:** Yes, warehouse staff assist with forklift loading onto bakkies and trailers. For large orders, a forklift is always available. You must secure your own load for transport.

**Q17:** Can I collect from any branch with my trade account?
**A:** Yes, your trade account works at all branches. Your order can be placed at one branch and collected from another if more convenient.

**Q18:** What is a "will-call" order?
**A:** A will-call order is placed in advance and held at the trade counter for collection when you arrive. This is ideal when you're sending a driver or collecting later in the day.

**Q19:** Can I add to my order when I arrive to collect?
**A:** Yes, if the additional items are in stock and your order hasn't been finalised. Additional items are processed as a separate transaction at the trade counter.

**Q20:** Do I need to bring anything when collecting?
**A:** Bring your ID and quote your trade account number. If paying by EFT, bring proof of payment. For large collections, ensure your vehicle is suitable and bring tie-downs.

**Q21:** Can I place a standing order for regular monthly purchases?
**A:** Yes, standing orders can be arranged with your account manager. Specify your regular product list and quantities, and we'll prepare your order on a set date each month for collection or delivery.

**Q22:** How do I cancel an order?
**A:** Contact the trade counter or your account manager immediately. If the order hasn't been picked, cancellation is free. If picked, a restocking fee of 10% may apply.

**Q23:** Can I order samples through the trade counter?
**A:** Yes, sample boards and cut samples are available at the trade counter. Most samples are free for trade account holders. Full-size samples for client approval can be purchased at trade price.

**Q24:** Do you take phone orders?
**A:** Yes, phone orders are accepted during trade counter hours. For accuracy, follow up with a WhatsApp message confirming the products and quantities discussed.

**Q25:** Can I email my order?
**A:** Yes, email orders to your account manager or the branch email address. Include your account number, full product list with codes, quantities, and delivery/collection instructions.

**Q26:** What happens if I order the wrong product?
**A:** If you realise before collection, call immediately to amend. If discovered after collection, our returns policy applies — returns within 14 days in original condition attract a 10% restocking fee.

**Q27:** Can I get my order pre-loaded on a pallet for easy transport?
**A:** Yes, orders can be palletised for R50-80 per pallet. This makes loading/unloading faster and reduces breakage. Request palletising when placing your order.

**Q28:** How do I check if my order has been processed correctly?
**A:** Check your order confirmation (sent via WhatsApp or email) immediately. Verify product codes, quantities, and pricing. Report discrepancies before collection.

**Q29:** Can I pay a deposit to secure an order and pay the balance on collection?
**A:** For special orders and forward orders, a 30-50% deposit is standard. For stock items, full payment (or credit account charge) on collection is standard.

**Q30:** Do you offer order templates for repeat orders?
**A:** Yes, your account manager can create an order template for your standard material list. Re-ordering is then as simple as confirming quantities from your template.

**Q31:** Can I order after hours for next-day collection?
**A:** WhatsApp orders can be sent after hours and are processed first thing in the morning. For urgent next-day collection, send your order by 7:00 PM the day before.

**Q32:** What is the maximum quantity I can order for same-day collection?
**A:** Same-day collection is available for up to approximately 500m² of tile (depending on the branch). Larger orders require next-day preparation. Always order large quantities in advance.

**Q33:** Can I collect part of my order and have the rest delivered?
**A:** Yes, split orders are possible. Specify which items you'll collect and which need delivery when placing the order. Separate delivery charges apply.

**Q34:** Do I get an invoice immediately when I order?
**A:** A pro forma invoice is issued at order placement. A tax invoice is issued at payment/collection. Credit account holders receive an invoice added to their monthly statement.

**Q35:** Can I return uncollected stock to the shelf if my project changes?
**A:** If you haven't collected the stock and notify us within 24 hours, we can cancel with no penalty. After 24 hours, a 10% restocking fee may apply if the stock has been picked.

**Q36:** Is there a fast-track queue for pre-ordered collections?
**A:** Yes, pre-ordered collections have a dedicated fast-track queue at the trade counter. Show your collection reference number and proceed to the "Pre-Orders" counter.

**Q37:** Can I order products from Spec Lab through the trade counter?
**A:** Spec Lab carries some premium ranges not stocked at standard trade counters. These can be ordered through your account manager with delivery to your preferred branch.

**Q38:** How do I handle a back-ordered item in my order?
**A:** Your account manager will advise which items are back-ordered and give you the expected arrival date. You can choose to hold the full order, collect available stock, or substitute the back-ordered item.

**Q39:** Can I get an order acknowledgement in writing?
**A:** All orders placed via WhatsApp or email receive a written acknowledgement with an order number. Phone orders are confirmed by return call or WhatsApp message.

**Q40:** Do you offer pre-dawn opening for contractors on big jobs?
**A:** Some branches open at 6:45 AM for contractors. Check with your local branch. Early-bird contractors appreciate the extra 45 minutes to get to site with materials.

**Q41:** Can I place an order for delivery to a site I'm not personally at?
**A:** Yes, specify the site address and site contact person's details. The driver will call the site contact 30 minutes before arrival. Ensure someone is available to off-load.

**Q42:** What happens if I arrive and my order isn't ready?
**A:** Speak to the trade counter supervisor. If there's been a delay, we'll expedite the picking and may offer a complimentary coffee while you wait. For significant delays, delivery can be arranged.

**Q43:** Can I order by sending a photo of a product I saw at a client's house?
**A:** Yes, send the photo via WhatsApp. Our staff will identify the product or the closest match from our range. If we don't stock it, we may be able to source it.

**Q44:** How do I add a new product to a regular order?
**A:** Simply include the new product in your order message. If you're unsure of the code, describe it and we'll identify it. Your account manager will confirm the addition.

**Q45:** Can I get my order checked for completeness before I leave?
**A:** Yes, request a box count and product check at the loading bay. Staff will cross-check your delivery note against the actual stock loaded.

**Q46:** Do you offer "click and collect" or online ordering?
**A:** We don't currently have an e-commerce platform. WhatsApp ordering is our equivalent — fast, personalised, and confirmed by a real person who knows your account.

**Q47:** Can I pay for my order in instalments?
**A:** For credit account holders, the full amount is due within 30 days. For cash orders, full payment is required on collection. Project orders exceeding R100,000 can be structured with milestone payments.

**Q48:** How do I dispute an order I didn't place?
**A:** Contact your account manager immediately. We'll investigate how the order was placed and take appropriate action. Always protect your account number as you would your bank details.

**Q49:** Can I get my order delivered to my workshop instead of site?
**A:** Yes, delivery can be made to any address you specify — workshop, home, storage yard, or site. Just provide the correct address when ordering.

**Q50:** What happens if I need to change my collection time?
**A:** WhatsApp or call the trade counter with your new estimated arrival time. For significant delays (next day), your order will be re-shelved and re-picked when you confirm.

**Q51:** Can I set up a favourites list for quick re-ordering?
**A:** Yes, your account manager maintains a favourites list on your profile. Re-ordering is as simple as saying "re-order my standard list" via WhatsApp.

**Q52:** Do I need to sign anything when collecting?
**A:** You'll sign a delivery note/picking slip confirming receipt of the correct products and quantities. For credit account purchases, you sign the credit sale slip.

**Q53:** Can I collect on Saturday for a Monday start?
**A:** Yes, Saturday collections are available 8:00 AM - 1:00 PM at most branches. This is popular for contractors starting fresh projects on Monday mornings.

**Q54:** How do I know how many boxes to order for my square metreage?
**A:** Each product page and box label states the coverage per box. Alternatively, provide your m² requirement and we'll calculate the exact box count including your wastage allowance.

**Q55:** Can I get a partial collection if my full order isn't ready?
**A:** Yes, if part of your order is ready and part is pending, you can collect the available stock. The balance will be ready for a second collection or delivered when complete.

---

## Category 5: Delivery to Site (55 Q&As)

**Q1:** Do you deliver to site?
**A:** Yes, we deliver to site within a 60km radius of each branch. Our fleet handles up to 400 tons per day. Delivery is available Monday-Saturday.

**Q2:** What are your delivery charges?
**A:** Delivery charges vary by distance and load size. Within 10km is typically R250-350, 10-30km is R350-500, and 30-60km is R500-750. Large orders over R20,000 may qualify for free delivery within 30km.

**Q3:** What is the minimum order for delivery?
**A:** The minimum order value for delivery is R2,500. Orders below this are collection only. Large-volume sites often qualify for waived delivery fees.

**Q4:** How do I schedule a delivery?
**A:** Request delivery when placing your order via WhatsApp, phone, or at the trade counter. Specify your site address, preferred delivery date, and site contact. Deliveries are typically scheduled within 24-48 hours.

**Q5:** Can I get same-day delivery?
**A:** Same-day delivery is available for orders placed by 9:00 AM within a 20km radius, subject to vehicle availability. A same-day surcharge of R150 may apply.

**Q6:** What are your delivery time windows?
**A:** Standard delivery windows are 7:00 AM - 12:00 PM (morning) or 12:00 PM - 5:00 PM (afternoon). Specific time requests are accommodated where possible but not guaranteed.

**Q7:** Can I request a specific delivery time?
**A:** Specific time requests are noted but not guaranteed due to traffic and route optimisation. For critical deliveries, consider our timed delivery service (additional R300) for a 2-hour window.

**Q8:** Do you deliver on Saturdays?
**A:** Yes, Saturday deliveries are available until 1:00 PM. Place your order by Friday 12:00 PM for Saturday delivery. Saturday surcharge of R150 applies.

**Q9:** Do you deliver to sites outside the 60km radius?
**A:** For sites beyond 60km, we use contracted transport partners. Charges are quoted on request and typically range from R800-R1,500 depending on distance and load. Alternatively, collect from the nearest branch.

**Q10:** Can you deliver to multiple sites on one day?
**A:** Yes, multi-drop deliveries can be arranged. Each additional drop is charged at 50% of the standard delivery rate for that distance. Provide all site addresses and contact details when ordering.

**Q11:** Do your drivers help with off-loading?
**A:** Drivers assist with off-loading to ground level from the truck. Crane off-loading, hi-ab, or carrying into the building is not included. Arranged crane/hi-ab service is available at additional cost.

**Q12:** Can I arrange a crane truck for delivery?
**A:** Yes, crane truck (hi-ab) deliveries can be arranged for sites with no forklift or where rooftop delivery is needed. Quote on request — typically R1,200-R2,000 depending on crane requirement.

**Q13:** What happens if I'm not at site when the truck arrives?
**A:** The driver will call the site contact 30 minutes before arrival. If no one is available to receive the delivery, it returns to the branch and a re-delivery fee of R250 applies.

**Q14:** Can delivery be made to a residential address?
**A:** Yes, deliveries can be made to any address — residential, commercial, or site. Ensure the address is accessible for a truck and someone is available to receive and off-load.

**Q15:** How do I track my delivery?
**A:** Once your delivery is loaded, you receive a WhatsApp message with the estimated arrival time. For real-time tracking on large deliveries, contact your account manager.

**Q16:** What size truck will deliver my order?
**A:** Delivery vehicle is matched to load size — from 2-ton bakkies for small orders to 8-ton trucks for large site deliveries. If your site has access restrictions, inform us when ordering.

**Q17:** Can the driver wait while we count everything?
**A:** Drivers can wait up to 30 minutes for a basic count. For full verification, sign the delivery note noting "subject to count" and report discrepancies within 24 hours.

**Q18:** What if some items are damaged during delivery?
**A:** Note any damage on the delivery note before signing. Take photos and notify your account manager within 24 hours. We'll arrange replacement at no charge for delivery-related damage.

**Q19:** Can I get a delivery note before the truck arrives?
**A:** A copy of the delivery note can be WhatsApped to you when the truck departs. This allows you to prepare for the delivery and verify items on arrival.

**Q20:** Do you deliver after hours?
**A:** Standard deliveries are during business hours. After-hours deliveries (before 7 AM or after 5 PM) can be arranged for an additional surcharge of R500. Book in advance.

**Q21:** Can deliveries be made to high-rise building sites?
**A:** Standard delivery is to ground level at the site entrance. For high-rise sites, arrange a crane or hoist to lift materials. We can deliver to the crane hook on ground level.

**Q22:** What happens if my site doesn't have road access?
**A:** Inform us of access restrictions when ordering. We can arrange smaller vehicle delivery or delivery to the nearest accessible point. Additional charges may apply for difficult access.

**Q23:** Can I change the delivery address after the order is loaded?
**A:** Address changes after loading are difficult and attract a redirection fee of R350 plus additional travel distance charges. Contact dispatch immediately if a change is needed.

**Q24:** Do you deliver to townships and informal areas?
**A:** Yes, we deliver wherever our trucks can safely access. If road conditions are unsuitable for large trucks, we can arrange delivery to the nearest safe point or use a smaller vehicle.

**Q25:** How is delivery charged for very large orders?
**A:** Orders over R20,000 within 30km typically qualify for free delivery. For orders exceeding R50,000, delivery is free within 60km. Confirm with your account manager.

**Q26:** Can I get a regular weekly delivery slot for an ongoing project?
**A:** Yes, standing delivery arrangements can be made for projects running 4+ weeks. We allocate a regular weekly delivery slot, and you confirm quantities 48 hours before each delivery.

**Q27:** Do you deliver on public holidays?
**A:** Deliveries are not made on public holidays. For holidays that fall on weekdays, deliveries are scheduled for the next working day. Plan accordingly around long weekends.

**Q28:** What if it rains on my delivery day?
**A:** Tile deliveries are covered and protected from rain. If site conditions make delivery unsafe or impossible, we'll reschedule at no extra charge. Inform us early if weather is a concern.

**Q29:** Can the driver help carry tiles into the house?
**A:** Standard delivery includes off-loading to ground level at the designated point. Carrying into buildings is not included. Some drivers will assist informally for small quantities, but this is not guaranteed.

**Q30:** How do I prepare my site for delivery?
**A:** Ensure clear vehicle access, a flat off-loading area, and someone available to receive and sign. For large deliveries, having a forklift or enough hands to move materials speeds up the process.

**Q31:** Can I get a delivery to a storage facility or warehouse?
**A:** Yes, deliveries can be made to any commercial address with suitable access. Provide the storage facility name, contact person, and any access codes or procedures.

**Q32:** Do you offer a "delivery-only" service if I buy elsewhere?
**A:** No, our delivery service is for Tiletoria purchases only. However, if you need products we don't stock, ask your account manager — we may be able to source them for you.

**Q33:** Can I split a delivery between morning and afternoon?
**A:** Split deliveries on the same day are treated as two separate deliveries and charged accordingly. Consolidate your requirements into one delivery where possible.

**Q34:** What is the maximum load you can deliver in one truck?
**A:** Our 8-ton trucks can carry approximately 300-400m² of standard porcelain tile plus adhesives and sundries. For larger quantities, multiple trucks or multiple deliveries are arranged.

**Q35:** Can I get a proof of delivery (POD) document?
**A:** Yes, the signed delivery note serves as your POD. A digital copy can be emailed or WhatsApped to you within 24 hours of delivery on request.

**Q36:** Do you deliver to farms and rural properties?
**A:** Deliveries to outlying areas are made via contracted transport partners. Charges depend on distance and road conditions. Obtain a quote from your account manager before ordering.

**Q37:** Can I schedule a delivery for a specific day next week?
**A:** Yes, advance deliveries can be scheduled up to 14 days ahead. This is recommended for project-critical deliveries to secure your preferred day and time slot.

**Q38:** What happens if the delivery is late?
**A:** Traffic and route delays can occur. If your delivery is more than 1 hour outside the window, contact dispatch for an update. For significant delays, a delivery credit may be considered.

**Q39:** Can I request a call 30 minutes before delivery?
**A:** The driver calls the site contact 30 minutes before arrival as standard practice. Ensure the contact number provided is reachable during delivery hours.

**Q40:** Do you offer a "white glove" delivery service for high-end residential?
**A:** For premium residential projects, we can arrange careful handling delivery where tiles are carried into designated rooms and stacked neatly. Additional charges apply — quote on request.

**Q41:** Can I return excess stock via the delivery truck?
**A:** If a delivery truck is returning to the branch, excess stock can sometimes be collected for return. Arrange this in advance — subject to space availability and the driver agreeing.

**Q42:** How do I complain about a delivery issue?
**A:** Contact your account manager or the branch manager via WhatsApp or phone. Provide details of the issue, delivery note number, and photos if applicable. We aim to resolve delivery issues within 24 hours.

**Q43:** Can you deliver to a site with a boom gate or security?
**A:** Yes, provide the security contact number, estate name, and any access codes when placing the order. The driver will follow the estate's delivery protocols.

**Q44:** Do you have refrigerated trucks for certain products?
**A:** Tiles and building materials don't require refrigeration. Our standard covered trucks provide adequate protection from weather.

**Q45:** Can I get a weekend delivery?
**A:** Saturday deliveries are available until 1:00 PM. Sunday deliveries are not standard but can be arranged for emergency situations at premium rates. Contact your branch manager.

**Q46:** What happens if I refuse a delivery?
**A:** If you refuse a delivery due to damage, incorrect products, or site unreadiness, the stock returns to the branch. Note the reason on the delivery note. Re-delivery or credit will be arranged.

**Q47:** Can I get an estimated delivery time on the day?
**A:** Yes, you'll receive a WhatsApp message with an estimated delivery window on the morning of delivery. The driver also calls 30 minutes before arrival.

**Q48:** Do you deliver loose tiles or only boxed?
**A:** We deliver in original factory packaging (boxes) for protection. Loose tiles are only provided as samples. Box quantities are calculated to match your m² requirement.

**Q49:** Can delivery be made to a pavement or kerbside only?
**A:** Standard delivery includes off-loading to a point accessible by the truck, typically kerbside or driveway. If you need materials carried further, arrange additional labour.

**Q50:** How do I change my delivery instructions after ordering?
**A:** Contact the trade counter or your account manager as soon as possible. Changes made before the truck loads are free. Changes after loading may incur redirection fees.

**Q51:** Do you offer consolidated deliveries from multiple branches?
**A:** Orders from multiple branches are delivered separately by each branch's fleet. For large national projects, your account manager can coordinate consolidated logistics.

**Q52:** Can I get a dedicated truck for my large project?
**A:** For projects requiring daily or weekly deliveries exceeding 200m², a dedicated delivery arrangement can be negotiated with your account manager and the branch manager.

**Q53:** What if my site is in a traffic-restricted area?
**A:** Inform us of any traffic restrictions, load limitations, or time-of-day access rules when ordering. We'll schedule delivery accordingly or use an appropriate vehicle.

**Q54:** Can I tip the driver for good service?
**A:** While not expected, drivers appreciate tips for exceptional service. Tiletoria does not prohibit tipping. Great service feedback can also be shared with the branch manager.

**Q55:** How do I rate my delivery experience?
**A:** After delivery, you may receive a short WhatsApp survey. Your feedback helps us improve our delivery service. You can also rate directly through your account manager.

---

## Category 6: Returns & Exchanges (50 Q&As)

**Q1:** What is your returns policy for trade account holders?
**A:** Trade account holders can return unopened, undamaged stock in original packaging within 14 days of purchase for a full credit note. A 10% restocking fee applies. Returns after 14 days are at management discretion.

**Q2:** Can I return excess tiles after my project is complete?
**A:** Yes, excess full boxes in original condition can be returned within 14 days. You must have your original invoice. Part-boxes and cut tiles cannot be returned.

**Q3:** What if my client changes their mind about the tile colour?
**A:** If the stock is unopened and in original condition, return within 14 days with your invoice for a credit note (10% restocking fee). Opened boxes or installed tiles cannot be returned.

**Q4:** Do I need the original invoice to return stock?
**A:** Yes, the original invoice or delivery note is required for returns processing. If lost, we can look up your transaction using your account number and date of purchase.

**Q5:** How long does a credit note take to process?
**A:** Credit notes are processed within 3-5 business days of the physical stock being received and inspected at the branch. The credit is applied to your trade account or refunded via EFT.

**Q6:** Can I exchange rather than return?
**A:** Yes, exchanges are processed as a return + new purchase. The credit note from the return is applied to the new purchase. Any difference is refunded or charged accordingly.

**Q7:** What if the tiles were delivered damaged?
**A:** Delivery damage must be reported within 24 hours with photos. We'll arrange collection of the damaged stock and replacement at no charge. Note damage on the delivery note at time of delivery.

**Q8:** Can I return opened boxes of tiles?
**A:** Opened boxes are generally not returnable unless the product is defective. If you have part-boxes left over, consider keeping them for future repairs or spares.

**Q9:** What is the restocking fee?
**A:** A 10% restocking fee applies to all approved returns. This covers the cost of inspecting, re-labelling, and returning stock to the shelf. No restocking fee applies for damaged or defective stock returns.

**Q10:** Can I return adhesive or grout bags?
**A:** Unopened, undamaged bags of adhesive and grout in original packaging can be returned within 14 days with a 10% restocking fee. Opened or partial bags cannot be returned.

**Q11:** What if I ordered the wrong product?
**A:** Wrong product orders can be returned within 14 days in original condition (10% restocking fee). Double-check product codes when ordering to avoid this.

**Q12:** Can I return stock after 14 days?
**A:** Returns after 14 days are at management discretion. If accepted, a 20% restocking fee applies. Special-order and imported items generally cannot be returned after 14 days.

**Q13:** Do I need to bring the returns to the branch myself?
**A:** Small returns can be brought to the trade counter. For large returns, we can arrange collection at a fee of R250-R500 depending on distance. Alternatively, return via your next delivery driver.

**Q14:** Can I return discontinued or clearance stock?
**A:** Clearance and discontinued stock is sold as "no returns" — this is clearly noted at purchase. Discontinued stock cannot be returned. Regular stock return policies apply to clearance items unless stated otherwise.

**Q15:** What happens if the batch I received is a different shade?
**A:** If the shade is different from what was ordered and the error is ours, full return and replacement is at our cost. If the shade variation is within the product's rated range (V2-V4), this is not a defect.

**Q16:** Can I return underfloor heating or electrical items?
**A:** Underfloor heating systems can be returned unopened within 14 days. Once installed or opened, manufacturer warranty terms apply. Electrical items follow the same policy.

**Q17:** How do I return a product I think is defective?
**A:** Bring a sample of the defect and your invoice to the trade counter. We'll inspect and may send the sample to the manufacturer for assessment. Defective product returns are at our cost if confirmed.

**Q18:** Can I get a cash refund instead of a credit note?
**A:** For cash purchases, a cash refund or EFT refund can be arranged (takes 3-5 business days). For credit account purchases, a credit note is applied to your account.

**Q19:** What if I lost my original invoice?
**A:** We can look up your purchase using your trade account number, date of purchase, and product details. A reprint can be provided for returns processing.

**Q20:** Can I return sanware (taps, toilets, basins)?
**A:** Unopened sanware in original packaging can be returned within 14 days (10% restocking fee). Installed or plumbed sanware cannot be returned. Check compatibility before installation.

**Q21:** Do you offer a "sale or return" arrangement?
**A:** Standard sale or return is not offered. However, for very large projects with uncertain final quantities, discuss a flexible return arrangement with your account manager before ordering.

**Q22:** Can I return flooring products (laminate, LVT, vinyl)?
**A:** Unopened boxes of laminate, LVT, and vinyl can be returned within 14 days in original condition (10% restocking fee). Opened boxes cannot be returned. Check box quantity before opening.

**Q23:** What if the wrong quantity was delivered?
**A:** If we delivered more or less than ordered, note it on the delivery note and contact your account manager. Short deliveries are topped up at our cost. Over-deliveries can be collected or kept and charged.

**Q24:** Can I return stock bought on promotion?
**A:** Promotional stock can be returned under the same 14-day policy unless specifically marked "no returns." The credit note reflects the promotional price you paid.

**Q25:** How do I handle returns for a cancelled project?
**A:** For project cancellations, return all unopened stock within 14 days. If the 14-day window has passed, contact your account manager — we understand projects get cancelled and will try to accommodate.


**Q27:** What is the process for returning a large quantity (100m²+)?
**A:** For large returns, contact your account manager to arrange a collection. The stock must be palletised and ready for loading. A collection fee may apply depending on distance.

**Q28:** Can I return products if I've already installed them and found a defect?
**A:** Installed products with a manufacturing defect may be covered under warranty. Report the defect immediately with photos and installation details. The manufacturer will assess the claim.

**Q29:** Do returns affect my volume rebate calculation?
**A:** Returns are deducted from your quarterly volume for rebate calculation purposes. Only net purchases (purchases minus returns) count toward volume tiers.

**Q30:** Can I return products bought from another Tiletoria branch?
**A:** Returns can be processed at any Tiletoria branch regardless of where the purchase was made. Bring your original invoice or quote your account number for lookup.

**Q31:** What if the product I want to return is now on promotion at a lower price?
**A:** Your credit note reflects the price you actually paid, not the current selling price. You receive a full credit for the amount charged to your account.

**Q32:** Can I return a product and order a different one in the same transaction?
**A:** Yes, the return and new order are processed together. You pay the difference or receive a credit for any overpayment. This is treated as an exchange.

**Q33:** What documentation do I receive for a return?
**A:** You receive a Goods Return Note (GRN) at the time of return, and a Credit Note is issued within 3-5 business days after inspection. Both are sent via email or WhatsApp.

**Q34:** Can I return waterproofing membranes?
**A:** Unopened waterproofing membranes in original packaging can be returned within 14 days (10% restocking fee). Part-used rolls or opened buckets cannot be returned.

**Q35:** What if I receive a product that doesn't match the sample I saw?
**A:** If there's a significant mismatch between the sample and delivered product, contact your account manager immediately. This may be treated as a defective product return at our cost.

**Q36:** Can I return mouldings and trims?
**A:** Unopened packs of trims and mouldings can be returned within 14 days. Cut-to-size or custom trims cannot be returned.

**Q37:** How do I return products bought in error by my site foreman?
**A:** The same returns policy applies regardless of who placed the order. Ensure your authorised buyers know product codes to minimise ordering errors.

**Q38:** Can I get a temporary credit while my return is being processed?
**A:** For large returns from active credit accounts, a temporary credit may be arranged while the physical stock is inspected. Discuss with your account manager.

**Q39:** What happens if returned stock is damaged during transport back?
**A:** You're responsible for ensuring returns are adequately protected during transport. Stock damaged due to inadequate packaging during return may be rejected or partially credited.

**Q40:** Can I return a product that's been slightly damaged on site?
**A:** Slight damage from site handling is not covered under returns. Only stock in original, resaleable condition qualifies for return. Keep your site storage area protected.

**Q41:** Is there a limit to how much I can return?
**A:** There's no formal limit, but excessive returns (more than 20% of purchases) may trigger a review of your account. We work with you to improve ordering accuracy.

**Q42:** Can I return products after I've moved them between sites?
**A:** Yes, provided the stock is unopened, in original condition, and within the 14-day window. The return is processed against your original purchase record.

**Q43:** Do you accept returns of special-order products?
**A:** Special-order and non-stocked items are generally not returnable unless defective. This is confirmed at the time of ordering. Ensure specifications are correct before confirming special orders.

**Q44:** Can I return grout colourant or sealers?
**A:** Unopened containers can be returned within 14 days. Opened or part-used containers cannot be returned. Check colour and compatibility before opening.

**Q45:** What if I want to return something but don't know which branch I bought from?
**A:** Quote your trade account number at any branch — we can look up the purchase across all branches. The return can be processed at whichever branch is most convenient.

**Q46:** How do I dispute a credit note amount?
**A:** If the credit note doesn't match what you expected, contact your account manager with the GRN number and original invoice. We'll investigate and adjust if necessary.

**Q47:** Can I return products during load shedding?
**A:** Returns can be processed during load shedding at branches with backup power. Call ahead to confirm if you're making a special trip for a return.

**Q48:** What happens to my return if I close my trade account?
**A:** Any outstanding credit notes are refunded via EFT when your account is closed. Ensure all returns are processed before requesting account closure.

**Q49:** Can I return a product and get a different size of the same tile?
**A:** Yes, this is processed as an exchange. Return the original size and purchase the new size. If the new size costs more, pay the difference. If less, receive a credit.

**Q50:** Do you have a "no-quibble" returns policy for defective items?
**A:** Confirmed manufacturing defects are returned at our cost with no restocking fee. We collect the defective stock and deliver replacement at no charge. Report defects immediately.

---

## Category 7: Product Technical Data (55 Q&As)

**Q1:** Where do I find technical datasheets for your tiles?
**A:** Technical datasheets are available at the trade counter, via WhatsApp request, or from your account manager. Datasheets include PEI ratings, water absorption, slip ratings, breaking strength, and SANS compliance data.

**Q2:** What is a PEI rating and why does it matter?
**A:** PEI (Porcelain Enamel Institute) rating indicates tile wear resistance. PEI I = walls only, PEI II = light residential floors, PEI III = normal residential/light commercial, PEI IV = commercial, PEI V = heavy commercial. Most residential floors need PEI III+.

**Q3:** What PEI rating do I need for a residential home entrance?
**A:** Home entrances, passages, and living areas should use PEI III or higher due to foot traffic. PEI IV is recommended for rental properties or homes with pets.

**Q4:** What is the difference between R9, R10, and R11 slip ratings?
**A:** R9 = low slip resistance (dry indoor areas), R10 = normal slip resistance (general residential), R11 = enhanced slip resistance (bathrooms, patios, commercial), R12-R13 = high slip resistance (wet areas, pools, industrial). SANS 1449 references these ratings.

**Q5:** What slip rating is required for a wet room or shower floor?
**A:** SANS 10107 recommends R11 or higher for wet room floors and shower areas. We stock anti-slip tiles specifically rated for wet-area application.

**Q6:** Do you provide SANS compliance certificates for your tiles?
**A:** Yes, SANS compliance certificates are available on request for all tiles that carry certification. Your account manager can provide these for NHBRC and council submissions.

**Q7:** What is water absorption and why does it matter?
**A:** Water absorption measures how much water a tile absorbs. Porcelain tiles have <0.5% absorption (impervious), ceramic 0.5-3% (vitreous), and some wall tiles 3-10% (semi-vitreous). Low absorption is critical for outdoor and wet-area tiles.

**Q8:** What is the breaking strength of your porcelain tiles?
**A:** Our porcelain tiles exceed the SANS minimum breaking strength of 1,300N for wall tiles and 1,800N for floor tiles. Most porcelain floor tiles test at 2,000N+. Datasheets show exact values.

**Q9:** What is "rectified" vs "pressed" edge?
**A:** Rectified tiles are cut to precise dimensions after firing, allowing narrower grout joints (1.5-2mm). Pressed (cushion edge) tiles have slight size variation and require wider grout joints (3-5mm). Rectified tiles cost more but give a sharper, more modern look.

**Q10:** What does "calibre" mean on the tile box?
**A:** Calibre refers to the tile's thickness. Always check the calibre number on the box — tiles of different calibres from the same range should not be laid together as it creates lippage. Same calibre = same thickness.

**Q11:** Can I mix tiles from different boxes during installation?
**A:** Yes, in fact you should mix tiles from 3-4 different boxes during installation. This blends any natural shade variation and prevents visible colour-blocking on the floor.

**Q12:** What is "rectified" in relation to large-format tiles?
**A:** Large-format tiles (600x1200mm and larger) are almost always rectified. This allows for minimal grout joints and the seamless look that clients expect. Always use a levelling system with rectified large-format tiles.

**Q13:** Do your tiles comply with NHBRC requirements?
**A:** Yes, our tile range includes products that meet NHBRC specifications for enrolled homes. Compliance certificates and method statements can be provided on request.

**Q14:** What is the difference between glazed and unglazed porcelain?
**A:** Glazed porcelain has a glass-like surface layer available in various colours and finishes. Unglazed (full-body) porcelain has the same colour and pattern throughout, making it more slip-resistant and suitable for heavy commercial use.

**Q15:** What does "V1, V2, V3, V4" mean on the box?
**A:** This is the shade variation rating. V1 = uniform colour (minimal variation), V2 = slight variation, V3 = moderate variation, V4 = substantial variation. Always check this rating and inform your client before installation.

**Q16:** Can I use wall tiles on the floor?
**A:** No — wall tiles are not designed to withstand foot traffic and typically have lower PEI ratings and breaking strength. Using wall tiles on floors voids warranties and may result in cracking.

**Q17:** Can I use floor tiles on the wall?
**A:** Yes, floor tiles can be installed on walls and are often used for feature walls and bathroom walls. Note that floor tiles are heavier — ensure the wall structure can support the weight.

**Q18:** What is "rectified" vs "non-rectified" in terms of installation?
**A:** Rectified tiles require a perfectly flat substrate due to the minimal grout joint. Any substrate irregularity will show as lippage. Non-rectified (pressed) tiles are more forgiving with slight substrate imperfections.

**Q19:** What adhesive coverage do I need per square metre?
**A:** Standard adhesive coverage is 3-5kg/m² depending on trowel size and tile format. Large-format tiles need more adhesive (5-7kg/m²) and a notched trowel. Always check the adhesive manufacturer's specifications.

**Q20:** How much grout do I need per square metre?
**A:** Grout coverage depends on tile size and joint width. As a guide: 300x300mm tiles at 3mm joints = ~0.5kg/m². 600x600mm tiles at 2mm joints = ~0.3kg/m². 100x100mm mosaics at 5mm joints = ~1.2kg/m².

**Q21:** Do you provide NHBRC Enrolment Forms and Method Statements?
**A:** Yes, NHBRC-compliant method statements and product certificates are available from your account manager. These documents support your NHBRC enrolment and inspections.

**Q22:** What is the recommended expansion joint spacing for tile installations?
**A:** Perimeter expansion joints are required at all edges. Intermediate expansion joints should be installed every 8-10 metres in both directions for interior floors, and every 4-6 metres for exterior installations.

**Q23:** Can I install tiles over existing tiles?
**A:** Yes, provided the existing tiles are soundly bonded, clean, and the surface is primed. Use a flexible adhesive designed for tiling over tiles. Total thickness build-up must be checked against door clearances and trim transitions.

**Q24:** What is the minimum curing time before grouting?
**A:** Standard adhesive requires 24 hours before grouting. Rapid-set adhesive allows grouting after 3-4 hours. Full adhesive curing is 28 days, though foot traffic can resume after 24 hours with standard adhesive.

**Q25:** How long before a tiled floor can take foot traffic?
**A:** With standard adhesive: 24 hours for foot traffic, 72 hours for normal use. With rapid-set adhesive: 4 hours for foot traffic, 24 hours for normal use. Full curing is 28 days for both.

**Q26:** Do you have technical data for your adhesives and grouts?
**A:** Yes, technical datasheets for all adhesives, grouts, waterproofing, and ancillary products are available at the trade counter or via WhatsApp. Data includes mixing ratios, coverage, curing times, and compliance standards.

**Q27:** What is the SANS standard for tiling in South Africa?
**A:** SANS 10107 is the code of practice for tiling. It covers substrate preparation, adhesive selection, installation methods, and quality standards. SANS 1449 covers ceramic tiling and related products.

**Q28:** Can I get a product certificate of conformity?
**A:** Yes, certificates of conformity are available on request for products that carry SABS or equivalent certification. Your account manager will arrange these.

**Q29:** What is the difference between "polished" and "glazed" porcelain?
**A:** Polished porcelain is mechanically polished after firing, creating a high-gloss surface. Glazed porcelain has a glass layer applied before firing. Polished porcelain requires sealing and is more slippery when wet.

**Q30:** What is "lapato" or "semi-polished" finish?
**A:** Lapato (semi-polished) tiles have a partially polished surface that gives a soft sheen without the full gloss of polished tiles. They're less slippery than fully polished and hide scratches better.

**Q31:** Do I need to seal porcelain tiles?

**Q32:** What is the " Mohs hardness" rating of your tiles?
**A:** Most porcelain tiles rate 5-7 on the Mohs hardness scale. PEI rating is more commonly used for floor tile selection. Your account manager can provide specific Mohs ratings on request.

**Q33:** Can I get frost-resistance data for outdoor tiles?
**A:** Yes, frost-resistance ratings are available on the technical datasheet. All porcelain tiles with <0.5% water absorption are frost-resistant. Some ceramic tiles are also rated for frost — check the datasheet.

**Q34:** What is the chemical resistance of your tiles?
**A:** Porcelain tiles generally have excellent chemical resistance. Technical datasheets rate resistance to common household chemicals, acids, and alkalis. This is important for commercial kitchen and industrial applications.

**Q35:** Do you provide fire-rating certificates?
**A:** Ceramic and porcelain tiles are naturally fire-resistant (Class A/Class 0). Fire-rating certificates can be provided for commercial projects requiring fire-compliance documentation.

**Q36:** What is "biocuttura" or "monocuttura"?
**A:** These refer to Italian manufacturing processes. Monocuttura tiles are fired once with the glaze applied to the raw body. Biocuttura (double-fired) involves a first firing of the body, then glaze application and a second firing. Most modern tiles are monocuttura.

**Q37:** What is the recommended substrate flatness for tiling?
**A:** SANS 10107 requires the substrate to be flat to within 3mm over a 2-metre straight edge for tiles up to 600x600mm. For tiles larger than 600x600mm, flatness should be within 2mm over 2 metres.

**Q38:** Can I get a comparison sheet between similar products?
**A:** Yes, your account manager can provide a side-by-side comparison of similar products showing technical specifications, origin, price, and recommended applications.

**Q39:** What is the "Crazing Resistance" rating?
**A:** Crazing resistance indicates how well a glazed tile resists fine surface cracking (crazing) under stress. Our glazed tiles meet SANS requirements for crazing resistance. Full-body porcelain doesn't craze as there's no glaze layer.

**Q40:** Do your tiles contain any harmful substances (lead, etc.)?
**A:** Our tiles comply with international standards for chemical safety. Lead-free glazes are standard. Full material safety datasheets (MSDS) are available on request.

**Q41:** What is the difference between "through-body" and "glazed" porcelain?
**A:** Through-body (full-body/unibody) porcelain has the same colour and pattern throughout the entire thickness. Glazed porcelain has a surface glaze layer over a different-coloured body. Chips on through-body tiles are less visible.

**Q42:** Can I get thermal expansion data for outdoor tiling?
**A:** Yes, thermal expansion coefficients are available on technical datasheets. Expansion joints must be designed to accommodate thermal movement — your account manager can advise on spacing for your climate zone.

**Q43:** What is the recommended slope for a shower floor?
**A:** SANS 10107 recommends a minimum fall of 1:50 to 1:100 (1-2%) toward the shower drain for effective water drainage. Pre-formed shower trays can simplify this.

**Q44:** Do you provide installation guides?
**A:** Yes, product-specific installation guides are available at the trade counter and via WhatsApp. These cover substrate prep, adhesive selection, fixing methods, and grouting.

**Q45:** What is "lippage" and how do I avoid it?
**A:** Lippage is the difference in height between adjacent tiles. It's caused by substrate unevenness, inconsistent adhesive bed, or mixing calibres. Use a levelling system for large-format tiles and ensure substrate flatness.

**Q46:** Can I get a warranty document for tiles I'm installing?
**A:** Manufacturer warranties are available for most products. These cover manufacturing defects when installed per SANS 10107. Your account manager provides warranty documents on request.

**Q47:** What does "rectified" mean for mosaic sheets?
**A:** Most mosaic sheets are mounted on mesh or paper backing. "Jointless" mosaic designs exist where individual pieces butt together. Standard mosaic sheets require grouting between individual tesserae.

**Q48:** How do I read the batch information on the box?
**A:** The box shows: product code, description, size, quantity per box, shade/tone number, calibre, date of manufacture, country of origin, and SANS/ISO compliance marks. Always check shade and calibre before installing.

**Q49:** Do you have products specifically for industrial applications?
**A:** Yes, our commercial/industrial range includes heavy-duty porcelain (PEI V), anti-slip industrial tiles, and chemical-resistant options. Your account manager can recommend suitable products.

**Q50:** What is the impact resistance of porcelain tiles?
**A:** Porcelain tiles have high impact resistance due to their density and low porosity. Specific impact resistance data (measured in Newtons) is available on technical datasheets for commercial specifications.

**Q51:** Can I use tiles with underfloor heating?
**A:** Yes, all our porcelain and ceramic tiles are compatible with underfloor heating. Ensure the adhesive and grout are also rated for underfloor heating (thermal cycling). We supply compatible systems.

**Q52:** What is the maximum temperature for underfloor heating under tiles?
**A:** Underfloor heating systems typically operate at 25-29°C floor surface temperature. The thermostat should be limited to 29°C floor temperature to prevent thermal stress on the tile and adhesive.

**Q53:** Do you provide acoustic rating data for tiles?
**A:** Tile-on-screed systems have an IIC (Impact Insulation Class) rating that depends on the total assembly (tile + adhesive + screed + substrate). We don't provide acoustic-rated tile systems specifically, but can advise on best practices.

**Q54:** What is the "rectified" tolerance for your tiles?
**A:** Rectified tiles have dimensional tolerances of ±0.5mm on length and width, and ±0.3mm on thickness. This precision allows for minimal grout joints. Actual tolerances are shown on each product's datasheet.

**Q55:** Can I get a product sample with the technical data printed on it?
**A:** Sample boards at the trade counter include technical data labels. For specification purposes, we can provide A4 datasheets with product images and full technical data for your files.

---

## Category 8: Tile-Specific Contractor Queries (55 Q&As)

**Q1:** Should I recommend porcelain or ceramic for a residential bathroom floor?
**A:** Porcelain is recommended for bathroom floors due to its low water absorption (<0.5%), higher durability, and better slip resistance. Ceramic wall tiles are fine for bathroom walls but not recommended for floors.

**Q2:** What size tile is best for a small bathroom?
**A:** 600x600mm or 800x800mm tiles make small bathrooms appear larger due to fewer grout joints. Avoid small tiles in compact spaces as they create a busy look and more grout lines to clean.

**Q3:** Can I use 1200x600mm tiles in a standard residential home?
**A:** Yes, 1200x600mm is increasingly popular in residential homes. Ensure your substrate is very flat (2mm over 2m), use a levelling system, and allow for the higher adhesive cost (5-7kg/m²).

**Q4:** What tile size should I use for kitchen floors?
**A:** 600x600mm is the standard for kitchen floors. 800x800mm or larger creates a more premium look. Consider the client's budget, kitchen size, and substrate condition when recommending.

**Q5:** Are wood-look tiles practical for homes?
**A:** Yes, wood-look porcelain is extremely popular. It offers the warmth of wood with the durability of porcelain. Available in plank formats (150x900mm, 200x1200mm) with realistic textures. Perfect for wet areas where wood can't be used.

**Q6:** What tile do you recommend for a rental property?
**A:** For rental properties, recommend PEI IV-rated porcelain in neutral colours (grey, beige). These withstand tenant wear, are easy to maintain, and appeal to a broad market. Avoid polished finishes in rentals.

**Q7:** Can I use the same tile indoors and outdoors?
**A:** Yes, if it's a porcelain tile with <0.5% water absorption (frost-resistant) and R10+ slip rating. Many of our porcelain ranges are suitable for both applications. Check the technical data to confirm.

**Q8:** What is the best tile for a patio or veranda?
**A:** Outdoor tiles should be frost-resistant porcelain with R11 slip rating minimum. Textured or structured finishes provide better grip. Avoid polished tiles outdoors. Consider 600x600mm or 800x800mm for clean lines.

**Q9:** How do I handle large-format tiles without breaking them?
**A:** Use a large-format tile cutter (1200mm+ bed) or a wet saw. Handle with suction cups. Score once firmly — don't score multiple times. Support the full tile when cutting and during transport.

**Q10:** What is the best tile for a swimming pool surround?
**A:** Pool surrounds need R11-R12 anti-slip porcelain tiles. Mosaic tiles are popular for the pool waterline. Ensure the tile is frost-resistant and salt/chlorine resistant. We stock pool-specific ranges.

**Q11:** Can I install tiles over a concrete slab that hasn't cured fully?
**A:** No — concrete must cure for at least 28 days before tiling. The moisture content should be below 5% (measured with a moisture meter). Tiling over uncured concrete causes adhesive failure.

**Q12:** What tile do you recommend for a shower floor?
**A:** Shower floors need R11+ slip-rated tiles, typically mosaics (for the grout joints providing grip) or textured small-format tiles. our exclusive mosaic range has excellent shower floor options.

**Q13:** How do I handle tiles that are slightly bowed or warped?
**A:** Slight bowing is normal in large-format tiles and is managed by using a levelling system and proper adhesive bed thickness. Inspect tiles before installing and use the "tipping" technique to minimise visible lippage.

**Q14:** What is the best tile for a commercial kitchen?
**A:** Commercial kitchens need R12-R13 anti-slip, chemical-resistant, PEI V-rated porcelain tiles. Unglazed or structured finishes are best. Consider 300x300mm or 200x200mm for better slip resistance.

**Q15:** Can I use polished tiles in a bathroom?
**A:** Polished tiles can be used on bathroom walls for a luxurious look. On bathroom floors, use polished porcelain with caution — it becomes very slippery when wet. R9 polished tiles are not recommended for wet-area floors.

**Q16:** What is the difference between Italian and Chinese porcelain?
**A:** Italian porcelain typically offers more design variety, better digital print quality, and tighter rectification. Chinese porcelain is more cost-effective and quality has improved significantly. Both meet SANS standards when sourced through reputable distributors.

**Q17:** How do I match existing tiles for a repair or extension?
**A:** Bring a sample of the existing tile (or a photo with dimensions) to the trade counter. We'll match it from our range or source the closest equivalent. Note that batch variation means an exact match may not be possible.

**Q18:** What tile is best for a garage floor?
**A:** Garage floors need PEI V-rated, unglazed or structured porcelain tiles with R11+ slip rating. Avoid glazed tiles — vehicle traffic and oil exposure will damage the glaze over time.

**Q19:** Can I use tiles on a second-floor balcony?
**A:** Yes, but check the structural loading capacity first. Porcelain tiles at 10-12kg/m² plus adhesive and screed add significant weight. Use a lightweight tile adhesive system and ensure waterproofing is specified.

**Q20:** What is the trend in tile sizes right now?
**A:** The trend is toward larger formats — 800x800mm is now standard for premium homes, 1200x600mm is popular for living areas, and 1200x1200mm is emerging for luxury projects. Plank-format wood-look tiles remain very popular.

**Q21:** How do I convince a client to go with a larger tile?
**A:** Larger tiles mean fewer grout joints (easier to clean), a more seamless look, and the perception of more space. Explain the long-term maintenance benefit and show photos of completed installations.

**Q22:** What is the most cost-effective tile for a budget residential project?
**A:** 600x600mm porcelain in a basic grey or beige from our standard ranges offers the best value. These are always in stock, trade-priced aggressively, and suitable for most residential applications.

**Q23:** Can I use tiles with underfloor heating?
**A:** Yes, all porcelain and ceramic tiles work well with underfloor heating. Tiles conduct heat effectively. Use flexible adhesive and grout rated for thermal cycling. We supply compatible underfloor heating systems.

**Q24:** What tile should I use for a feature wall?
**A:** Feature walls are your chance to be creative — consider 3D textured tiles, large-format slabs, mosaic patterns, or bold colours. Spec Lab has an excellent feature wall range. Wall-only tiles can be used as they don't need floor-grade durability.

**Q25:** How do I handle a tile that has a directional pattern or vein?
**A:** Tiles with directional patterns (like-look) should be installed with the pattern running in a consistent direction, or in a "bookmatched" pattern for premium effect. Always check the arrow on the box and plan the layout before fixing.

**Q26:** What is the best tile for a retirement home or accessible bathroom?
**A:** Accessible bathrooms need R11+ slip-rated tiles with minimal grout joints (for wheelchair and walker access). Consider 300x600mm or 600x600mm anti-slip porcelain. Avoid high-gloss finishes.

**Q27:** Can I tile over a timber floor?
**A:** Direct tiling over timber is not recommended. Install a cement-based tile backer board or screed over the timber first, then tile. Alternatively, remove the timber and tile directly onto the subfloor after proper preparation.

**Q28:** What tile works best with underfloor heating in terms of thickness?
**A:** Thinner tiles (8-10mm) heat up faster than thicker tiles (12mm+). Most porcelain tiles are 9-11mm thick, which is ideal. The total system (heating + tile + adhesive) typically adds 15-20mm to floor height.

**Q29:** How do I handle a client who wants a specific tile you don't stock?
**A:** Bring a sample or photo to your account manager. We can often source it through our international supplier network, or recommend the closest equivalent from our existing range.

**Q30:** What is the best grout colour for a seamless look?
**A:** For a seamless look, match the grout colour to the tile colour as closely as possible. For a feature effect, use a contrasting grout colour. We stock multiple grout colours and can advise on matching.

**Q31:** Can I use 300x300mm tiles in a modern home?
**A:** 300x300mm is considered a smaller format for modern homes. It's suitable for bathrooms, feature areas, and budget projects. For main living areas, 600x600mm or larger is more contemporary.

**Q32:** What is the difference between a "tile" and a "slab"?
**A:** Slabs are large-format porcelain panels (1600x3200mm, 1200x2600mm) used for countertops, feature walls, and seamless surfaces. They require specialist handling, cutting equipment, and installation techniques.

**Q33:** How do I know if a tile is suitable for exterior use?
**A:** Exterior tiles must be: porcelain (not ceramic), water absorption <0.5%, frost-resistant, and R11+ slip-rated. Check the technical datasheet for exterior suitability confirmation.

**Q34:** What is the best tile for a braai area or outdoor kitchen?
**A:** Braai areas need heat-resistant, stain-resistant, R11+ slip-rated porcelain tiles. Structured or textured finishes handle the outdoor environment well. Avoid light colours that show soot stains.

**Q35:** Can I use tiles around a fireplace?
**A:** Yes, porcelain tiles are heat-resistant and suitable for fireplace surrounds. Don't install tiles directly in the firebox — use them for the surround, hearth, and feature wall. Ensure proper adhesive rated for heat.

**Q36:** What tile size should I recommend for a small kitchen backsplash?
**A:** 300x600mm subway tiles, mosaic sheets, or 100x300mm metro tiles are popular for kitchen backsplashes. Subway tiles in a brick-bond pattern is a timeless look.

**Q37:** How do I handle rectified tiles on an uneven floor?
**A:** The substrate must be levelled before installing rectified tiles. Use a self-levelling compound to achieve the required flatness (2mm over 2m). Without this, lippage will be visible with the minimal grout joint.

**Q38:** What is the most durable tile finish for a busy household?
**A:** Structured or textured matte porcelain is the most durable and practical for busy homes. It hides scratches, dirt, and water marks better than polished or gloss finishes.

**Q39:** Can I install tiles on drywall or plasterboard?
**A:** Lightweight wall tiles (ceramic, up to 300x600mm) can be installed on drywall using a suitable adhesive. Heavy tiles, large formats, and floor tiles must go onto cement-based backer boards or masonry.

**Q40:** What tile is best for a hotel lobby or commercial entrance?
**A:** Commercial entrances need PEI IV-V, R11+ slip-rated, high-traffic porcelain. Large-format tiles (800x800mm or 1200x600mm) create a premium impression. Consider through-body porcelain for maximum durability.

**Q41:** How do I deal with a client who wants "the cheapest tile"?
**A:** Explain the total cost of ownership — cheap tiles may crack, stain, or wear prematurely. A quality 600x600mm porcelain at trade price offers the best long-term value. Show them the difference in thickness, weight, and finish.

**Q42:** What is the best tile for a spa or wellness centre?

**Q43:** Can I tile over existing floor screed?
**A:** Yes, if the screed is sound, clean, and adequately cured (28+ days). Check moisture content (<5%), prime the surface, and use an appropriate adhesive. The screed must be flat to within SANS tolerances.

**Q44:** What tile should I use for a staircase?
**A:** Staircase tiles need R11+ slip rating, PEI IV+, and bullnose or step-nose profiles for the leading edge. Anti-slip stair tiles with integrated nosing are available. Ensure the substrate can handle point loading.

**Q45:** How do I recommend a tile to match a client's existing décor?
**A:** Bring the client to the showroom or take photos of their space. Match the tile undertone to the existing colour palette. Warm décor = warm beige/grey tiles. Cool décor = cool grey/white tiles.

**Q46:** What is the difference between "gloss" and "polished" tiles?
**A:** Gloss tiles have a shiny glazed surface. Polished tiles are mechanically buffed after firing to a mirror finish. Polished is shinier but more slippery and prone to scratching. Gloss is more forgiving.

**Q47:** Can I use tiles in a lift lobby or elevator area?
**A:** Yes, porcelain tiles are commonly used in lift lobbies. Use PEI IV+, R10+ rated tiles. Consider the weight of the tile + adhesive + screed against the building structure.

**Q48:** What is the most popular tile for bathrooms right now?
**A:** Currently, large-format (600x1200mm) wall tiles in-look or concrete-look designs are the most popular for premium bathrooms. Subway tiles remain popular for traditional styles.

**Q49:** How do I handle tiles that have a "bookmatch" pattern?
**A:** Bookmatched tiles are designed to mirror each other when placed side by side, creating a continuous veining pattern. They're numbered in sequence on the box — install them in the numbered order for the effect.

**Q50:** What tile do you recommend for a wine cellar?

**Q51:** Can I use tiles on a ceiling?
**A:** Lightweight ceiling tiles are available, but standard wall/floor tiles are too heavy for ceilings. Specialised ceiling tile systems exist — ask your account manager if you have a ceiling tile requirement.

**Q52:** What is the best tile for a butchery or food processing area?
**A:** Food processing areas need R12+ anti-slip, chemical-resistant, PEI V, non-porous tiles with flush epoxy-based grout joints for hygiene. Unglazed structured porcelain or quarry tiles are suitable.

**Q53:** How do I handle a tile that has "inkjet" variation?
**A:** Inkjet-printed tiles have digital designs that vary between tiles. Mix tiles from multiple boxes during installation to distribute the variation evenly. This creates a natural, non-repeating look.

**Q54:** What tile is best for a gym or fitness centre floor?
**A:** Gym floors need PEI V, R10+ slip-rated, impact-resistant tiles. Rubber-look porcelain or structured heavy-duty tiles work well. Consider acoustic underlayment to reduce impact noise.
