**A:** Yes. Our DLP Support Plan covers: technical support during the 12-month defect liability period, responsive warranty claim handling, remedial material supply, contractor coordination for remedial work, and final sign-off at DLP expiry. Each phase has its own DLP start date and support plan.

**Q658:** Does Tiletoria offer performance guarantees per phase or for the whole project?
**A:** Performance guarantees can be structured per phase or as a single project-wide guarantee. Per-phase guarantees are smaller and may be easier to obtain approval for. Project-wide guarantees provide simplicity and are often preferred by financiers. Your account executive recommends the optimal structure.

**Q659:** How does Tiletoria manage specification for estate developments with multiple house types?
**A:** Estate developments are managed through: house type specification templates (Type A, B, C etc.), common area specifications (roads, parks, clubhouses), lot-specific call-off schedules, and consolidated procurement to maximise volume discounts. Each house type has its own BoQ and delivery schedule.

**Q660:** Can Tiletoria provide phase completion certificates for sectional title registration?
**A:** Yes. Phase Completion Certificates confirm that all tiling and flooring in the phase has been supplied and installed in accordance with the specification and warranty terms. These certificates support sectional title registration, occupation certificate applications, and body corporate establishment.

**Q661:** Does Tiletoria support "warm shell" vs "cold shell" phase strategies?
**A:** Yes. Warm shell (completed interior) and cold shell (structure only, tenant finish later) strategies require different specification approaches. For warm shell phases, we supply the complete interior finish package. For cold shell, we supply only structural waterproofing and may defer finish selection to a later phase.

**Q662:** How does Tiletoria handle phased development where early phases fund later phases?
**A:** Cash flow-sensitive developments benefit from: minimal deposit requirements for early phases, milestone-based payments aligned to sales receipts, extended terms for later phases as cash flow improves, and volume rebate structures that improve pricing as the development progresses. We structure terms to support your funding model.

**Q663:** Can Tiletoria provide a single point of accountability across all phases?
**A:** Yes. Your dedicated Account Executive is the single point of accountability for all phases. They hold ultimate responsibility for: delivery performance, issue resolution, commercial management, and relationship coordination across the entire project. This single-point accountability simplifies governance for your team.

**Q664:** Does Tiletoria support phased handover where body corporate management transitions gradually?
**A:** Yes. Gradual handover to body corporate management is supported through: phase-specific warranty documentation, phased maintenance guide handover, staged training for body corporate facilities managers, and clear escalation protocols for warranty claims during the transition period.

**Q665:** How does Tiletoria manage stock for "just-in-time" phased delivery?
**A:** JIT phased delivery requires: accurate phase programmes, reliable call-off discipline, buffer stock at our warehouse (not on site), and rapid-response logistics. We deliver 24-48 hours ahead of installation need, minimising site storage and theft risk while ensuring no programme delays.

**Q666:** Can Tiletoria provide benchmarking data comparing our multi-phase project to industry norms?
**A:** Yes. We benchmark your project against anonymised industry data: cost per m² by region and product type, delivery performance norms, waste rates, DLP claims rates, and programme efficiency. This benchmarking helps you understand whether your project is performing above or below industry standards.

**Q667:** Does Tiletoria offer a phase completion bonus or incentive structure?
**A:** Phase completion incentives can be structured into the supply agreement: early completion bonuses (additional discount for phases completed ahead of programme), volume bonus (additional rebate for exceeding cumulative volume targets), and performance bonus (additional discount for achieving 100% on-time delivery). These incentives align our interests with yours.

**Q668:** How does Tiletoria handle warranty periods that span across multiple phase completion dates?
**A:** Warranty periods run from the practical completion date of each individual phase. Phase 1 warranty expires 10 years from Phase 1 completion, Phase 2 from Phase 2 completion, and so on. We maintain separate warranty registers per phase to ensure accurate tracking and timely response to claims.

**Q669:** Can Tiletoria support fast-follow phases where construction starts before the previous phase completes?
**A:** Yes. Fast-follow or overlapping phases require: clear stock allocation between overlapping phases, coordinated delivery scheduling to avoid site congestion, separate site contacts per phase, and vigilant tracking to prevent cross-phase material mix-ups. Our master programme coordinator manages these complex logistics.

**Q670:** Does Tiletoria provide a consolidated maintenance manual across all phases at project completion?
**A:** Yes. At full project completion, we compile a Consolidated Maintenance Manual covering: all products used across all phases, maintenance procedures, cleaning guidelines, warranty terms, contact details for warranty claims, and recommended maintenance schedules. This becomes part of the permanent property management documentation.

**Q671:** How does Tiletoria manage different architects or contractors for different phases?
**A:** Multi-architect/multi-contractor phased developments are managed through: separate specification coordination per architect, unified procurement to maintain volume benefits, clear communication protocols between all parties, and consolidated reporting to the developer. Your account executive acts as the hub connecting all spokes.

**Q672:** Can Tiletoria provide a final project case study for our portfolio?
**A:** Yes. With your permission, we prepare a project case study documenting: project overview, specification summary, supply approach, outcomes (cost, programme, quality), lessons learned, and testimonials. Case studies are valuable for your marketing and can support future project financing applications.

**Q673:** Does Tiletoria offer a "phase readiness" review before commencing supply to a new phase?
**A:** Yes. Before each phase starts, we conduct a Phase Readiness Review covering: specification confirmation, stock availability, contractor readiness, site access, programme alignment, and any lessons from previous phases. This review prevents issues that could delay the phase start.

**Q674:** How does Tiletoria manage spare stock requirements across multiple phases?
**A:** major retail groupse stock for future repairs is held centrally and released as needed across all phases. We recommend holding 2-3% spare stock for the entire development, rather than per phase, to optimise inventory. major retail groupse stock is clearly labelled, stored separately, and available for call-off throughout the warranty period.

**Q675:** Can Tiletoria provide a post-occupancy survey for phased developments?
**A:** Post-occupancy surveys can be arranged to capture: homeowner satisfaction with finishes, maintenance issues, warranty claims, and feedback for future phases. Survey data informs specification refinements and helps identify any systemic issues requiring intervention. We share anonymised survey results with you.

**Q676:** Does Tiletoria offer phased payment terms that improve as the relationship develops?
**A:** Yes. Payment terms can be structured to improve over time: Phase 1 (deposit or secured terms), Phase 2 (standard 30-day terms), Phase 3+ (extended 45-60 day terms based on payment track record). This term escalation rewards reliable payment with improved cash flow support.

**Q677:** How does Tiletoria handle product substitutions mid-programme that affect multiple future phases?
**A:** Programme-wide product substitutions are communicated to: all affected architects, all contractors (current and future), your QS for cost adjustment, and our warehouse for stock management. A substitution bulletin is issued documenting the change, reason, and effective date for all future phases.

**Q678:** Can Tiletoria provide a master asset register of all flooring materials installed across all phases?
**A:** Yes. Our Master Asset Register documents: every product installed, quantities by phase and building, batch numbers, installation dates, warranty expiry dates, and maintenance requirements. This register is handed over at project completion and becomes part of your property asset management system.

**Q679:** Does Tiletoria support phased development with different product grades per phase?
**A:** Yes. Some developers use Phase 1 as the premium launch phase with higher specifications, followed by more cost-effective phases. We manage different grade specifications across phases while maintaining procurement efficiency through: standardised core products, upgrade options per phase, and clear documentation to prevent grade mix-ups.

**Q680:** How does Tiletoria ensure that contractors for later phases don't deviate from the approved specification?
**A:** Specification compliance is monitored through: delivery matching to approved BoQ only, contractor briefing sessions at phase start, site inspections during installation, and phase completion snagging. Contractors cannot substitute products without written approval — all deliveries are from Tiletoria against the confirmed specification.


## Category 13: Compliance & Certification (63 Q&As)

**Q681:** What B-BBEE level is Tiletoria, and how does this benefit our supplier scorecard?
**A:** Tiletoria is a Level 1 or 2 B-BBEE contributor (verification certificate available on request). Engaging Tiletoria as a Level 1/2 supplier maximises your preferential procurement points under the revised B-BBEE Codes. We also support your supplier development and enterprise development criteria through our transformation programmes.

**Q682:** Does Tiletoria provide a B-BBEE certificate for our compliance records?
**A:** Yes. Our current B-BBEE certificate is provided to all development clients for their vendor compliance files. Certificates are renewed annually and updated certificates are distributed proactively. We can also provide a B-BBEE sworn affidavit if required for specific tender processes.

**Q683:** Can Tiletoria provide a tax clearance certificate for our finance applications?
**A:** Yes. Our SARS tax clearance certificate is available on request and confirms compliance with all tax obligations. The certificate is valid for 12 months from issue and is commonly required by development financiers, government tenders, and corporate vendor accreditation processes.

**Q684:** What ISO certifications does Tiletoria hold?
**A:** Tiletoria holds ISO 9001:2015 (Quality Management System) and ISO 14001 (Environmental Management System) certifications. These are audited annually by accredited certification bodies. Certificates are available for your vendor accreditation and quality management system records.

**Q685:** Does Tiletoria have CIDB registration?
**A:** Tiletoria is registered with the CIDB (Construction Industry Development Board) as a supplier of building materials. Our CIDB registration supports contractor grading and government project requirements. Registration details are available on request.

**Q686:** Can Tiletoria provide a certificate of good standing?
**A:** Yes. We issue Certificates of Good Standing confirming: company registration status, tax compliance, B-BBEE status, and absence of legal proceedings. These certificates are commonly required for vendor accreditation, tender submissions, and development finance applications.

**Q687:** What health and safety compliance does Tiletoria provide for site deliveries?
**A:** Our delivery teams comply with all site-specific health and safety requirements including: PPE (hard hats, high-vis vests, safety boots), site safety induction attendance, compliance with site safety rules, and COID (Compensation for Occupational Injuries and Diseases) coverage for our employees. Site-specific H&S documentation is provided on request.

**Q688:** Does Tiletoria comply with the Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA)?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is fully POPIA-compliant. We have a POPIA policy covering: lawful processing of personal information, data security measures, data subject rights, breach notification procedures, and responsible party obligations. Our policy is available on request and our staff are trained on POPIA compliance.

**Q689:** Can Tiletoria provide SANS 1449 compliance certificates for all tile products?
**A:** Yes. SANS 1449 compliance certificates are available for all ceramic and porcelain tile products, confirming compliance with: classification, characteristics, marking, and quality requirements. Certificates are issued per product line and can be bundled into the project documentation pack.

**Q690:** Does Tiletoria provide fire rating certificates for building control submissions?
**A:** Yes. Fire rating certificates are available for all products, showing classification per SANS 10177: ceramic/porcelain tiles (Class A1, non-combustible), LVT (Class Bfl-s1 or Cfl-s1 depending on product), and laminate (Class Cfl-s1 to Bfl-s1). Certificates are issued by SANAS-accredited testing laboratories.

**Q691:** What environmental compliance documentation does Tiletoria provide?
**A:** Environmental documentation includes: ISO 14001 certificate, environmental product declarations (EPDs) where available, recycled content data, VOC emission certificates (GREENGUARD), responsible sourcing documentation, and carbon footprint data. This documentation supports green building certification submissions.

**Q692:** Can Tiletoria provide a vendor accreditation pack for our procurement system?
**A:** Yes. Our Vendor Accreditation Pack includes: company registration documents, B-BBEE certificate, tax clearance, ISO certificates, insurance certificates, financial statements, bank details, reference letters, product catalogues, and compliance certificates. The pack is compiled on request and updated annually.

**Q693:** Does Tiletoria comply with the Consumer Protection Act for product supply?
**A:** Yes. All products are labelled and sold in compliance with the CPA, including: country of origin disclosure, material composition, care instructions, warranty terms, and safety warnings where applicable. We provide CPA-compliant product disclosure schedules for inclusion in your sale agreements.

**Q694:** Can Tiletoria provide municipal submission documentation?
**A:** Yes. We provide documentation to support municipal building plan submissions and occupation certificate applications including: product compliance certificates, SANS references, fire ratings, and technical datasheets. Our compliance letters are addressed to the relevant municipal building control officer.

**Q695:** Does Tiletoria have a health and safety policy for construction site deliveries?
**A:** Yes. Our Construction Site H&S Policy covers: driver and assistant PPE requirements, vehicle safety standards, load securing, site access protocols, incident reporting, and emergency procedures. The policy is aligned with the Construction Regulations and can be submitted to your principal contractor for their H&S file.

**Q696:** Can Tiletoria provide product origin certificates for imported goods?
**A:** Yes. Certificates of Origin are available for all imported products, showing: manufacturing country, factory details, and SADC/EU origin where applicable. Origin certificates support: B-BBEE local content calculations, customs clearance, export documentation, and tariff preference claims.

**Q697:** What compliance training does Tiletoria provide for its staff?
**A:** Staff training includes: product knowledge and technical compliance, health and safety (annual refresher), POPIA and data protection, B-BBEE awareness, customer service standards, and industry regulation updates. Training records are maintained and available for audit if required.

**Q698:** Does Tiletoria provide letters of no objection from regulatory bodies?
**A:** Letters of no objection are issued by regulatory bodies (NHBRC, municipalities) not by suppliers. However, we provide all the product documentation required to support your applications and can liaise with regulators if technical queries arise about our products.

**Q699:** Can Tiletoria provide a supplier code of conduct for our procurement file?
**A:** Yes. Our Supplier Code of Conduct covers: ethical business practices, anti-bribery and corruption, human rights and labour standards, environmental responsibility, health and safety, and quality commitment. The code aligns with international best practice and is signed by all Tiletoria staff.

**Q700:** Does Tiletoria provide compliance with the Construction Regulations 2014?
**A:** Yes. Our compliance with the Construction Regulations includes: H&S policy and procedures, risk assessments, incident reporting protocols, medical surveillance for staff, and COID registration. We cooperate with principal contractors in meeting their obligations under the regulations for material supply to construction sites.

**Q701:** Can Tiletoria provide asbestos-free certificates for its products?
**A:** Yes. Asbestos-free certificates are available confirming that none of our products contain asbestos or asbestos-related materials. This is particularly important for renovation projects and for export to jurisdictions with strict asbestos regulations.

**Q702:** What import/export compliance documentation does Tiletoria provide?
**A:** For cross-border supply, we provide: customs clearance documentation, SADC certificates of origin, export permits where required, commercial invoices, packing lists, and bills of lading. Our freight forwarding partners manage customs compliance for exports to SADC countries.

**Q703:** Does Tiletoria have a registered occupational health and safety officer?
**A:** Yes. We employ a registered occupational health and safety practitioner who oversees our H&S compliance, conducts risk assessments, manages incident investigations, and ensures regulatory compliance. The OHS officer is available for consultation on site-specific H&S matters.

**Q704:** Can Tiletoria provide a company organogram and key personnel CVs for tender submissions?
**A:** Yes. Our tender documentation pack includes: company organogram showing key personnel, CVs of senior management and technical staff, qualifications matrix, and succession planning information. This documentation supports tender submissions requiring evidence of organisational capability.

**Q705:** Does Tiletoria comply with the Skills Development Act and provide training records?
**A:** Yes. We comply with the Skills Development Act through: annual skills plans, SETA registration, workplace skills plans, training expenditure records, and employee training records. Our skills development credentials support your own Skills Development Element B-BBEE score.

**Q706:** Can Tiletoria provide proof of COID (Compensation for Occupational Injuries and Diseases) registration?
**A:** Yes. Our COID registration letter of good standing is available on request, confirming that all employees are covered for occupational injuries and diseases in terms of the Compensation for Occupational Injuries and Diseases Act. This is required for site access on most construction projects.

**Q707:** What product labelling compliance does Tiletoria provide?
**A:** All products carry compliant labelling including: product name and description, country of origin, manufacturer details, batch number, date of manufacture, SANS compliance marking, handling instructions, and safety warnings where applicable. Labelling complies with SANS 1449 and CPA requirements.

**Q708:** Does Tiletoria provide gender pay gap and employment equity documentation?
**A:** Yes. Our employment equity plan and annual report are available for clients requiring evidence of fair employment practices. We are committed to transformation and equality in the workplace. Gender pay equity data can be provided as part of comprehensive ESG reporting.

**Q709:** Can Tiletoria provide a letter confirming compliance with your specific procurement policy?
**A:** Yes. We can issue a letter confirming compliance with your specific procurement requirements including: B-BBEE level, payment terms, insurance levels, H&S standards, and any other criteria specified in your procurement policy. This simplifies your vendor compliance verification process.

**Q710:** Does Tiletoria have professional indemnity insurance for specification services?
**A:** Yes. We carry professional indemnity insurance covering our specification advisory services. This protects both Tiletoria and our clients against claims arising from specification advice. The PI insurance limit is appropriate for our scale of operation and can be disclosed on request.

**Q711:** Can Tiletoria provide annual financial statements for credit assessment?
**A:** Yes. Our audited annual financial statements are available for client credit assessment, vendor accreditation, and due diligence purposes. Financial statements demonstrate our financial stability and capacity to fulfil large development supply commitments. We are a subsidiary of Invicta Holdings, a JSE-listed company.

**Q712:** Does Tiletoria comply with the National Environmental Management Act?
**A:** Yes. Our environmental compliance includes: waste management licences where required, environmental management system (ISO 14001), recycling programmes, responsible disposal of packaging waste, and compliance with air quality and water use regulations. Our environmental policy is available on request.

**Q713:** Can Tiletoria provide a suretyship or guarantee for contract performance?
**A:** Performance guarantees and suretyship arrangements can be provided for large projects. Options include: performance bonds (5-10% of contract value), parent company guarantees from Invicta Holdings, bank guarantees, or retention arrangements. Security requirements are negotiated as part of the supply agreement.

**Q714:** What is Tiletoria's policy on conflict minerals and responsible sourcing?
**A:** Tiletoria does not source minerals covered by conflict minerals legislation. Our responsible sourcing policy requires: ethical manufacturing practices, no child labour, fair wages, safe working conditions, and environmental compliance from all manufacturing partners. Factory audits verify compliance with these standards.

**Q715:** Does Tiletoria provide customs and excise compliance for imported products?
**A:** All imported products are cleared through SARS customs in full compliance with customs and excise legislation. We pay all applicable duties, VAT, and levies. Customs clearance documentation is maintained for audit purposes and can be provided for origin verification if required.

**Q716:** Can Tiletoria provide a board resolution authorising contract signature?
**A:** Yes. A certified board resolution authorising the signatory to execute contracts on Tiletoria's behalf is provided for large supply agreements. This ensures that the contract is validly executed and provides comfort to your legal team regarding signing authority.

**Q717:** Does Tiletoria comply with the Labour Relations Act and provide dispute records?
**A:** Yes. We comply fully with the Labour Relations Act. Our industrial relations record is sound, with established grievance and dispute resolution procedures. We have no history of labour disputes affecting supply to clients. Labour compliance is verified as part of our B-BBEE assessment.

**Q718:** Can Tiletoria provide electrical safety certificates for products (SANS approved)?
**A:** Tiletoria does not supply electrical products requiring SANS electrical safety certification. Our products (tiles, flooring, adhesives, waterproofing) are non-electrical building materials. Where products interface with electrical systems (e.g., underfloor heating), we ensure compatibility with SANS electrical requirements.

**Q719:** Does Tiletoria have a whistleblowing policy?
**A:** Yes. Our whistleblowing policy encourages reporting of unethical conduct, fraud, corruption, or compliance breaches. Reports can be made confidentially to designated senior management or through an independent whistleblowing hotline. The policy protects whistleblowers from retaliation and ensures independent investigation.

**Q720:** Can Tiletoria provide proof of fleet insurance for delivery vehicles?
**A:** Yes. Our fleet insurance covers all delivery vehicles for: goods in transit, third-party liability, passenger liability, and vehicle damage. Insurance certificates can be provided for your logistics file. All drivers are licensed and trained in defensive driving and load safety.

**Q721:** Does Tiletoria provide compliance with the National Building Regulations (SANS 10400)?
**A:** Yes. Our products and specifications comply with the National Building Regulations as referenced in SANS 10400, including: Part T (Fire protection), Part B (Structural), Part K (Floors and stairways), and Part P (Drainage). Compliance letters reference the applicable SANS 10400 parts.

**Q722:** Can Tiletoria provide a risk assessment for our supplier due diligence?
**A:** Yes. We provide a Supplier Risk Assessment covering: financial stability, supply chain risks, operational risks, compliance risks, and reputational risks. The assessment demonstrates that Tiletoria is a low-risk, reliable supplier capable of supporting large-scale development projects.

**Q723:** Does Tiletoria have a modern slavery and human trafficking policy?
**A:** Yes. Our Modern Slavery Policy prohibits all forms of forced labour, human trafficking, and exploitation in our operations and supply chain. We conduct due diligence on manufacturing partners to verify compliance and require contractual commitments to ethical labour practices.

**Q724:** Can Tiletoria provide a data processing agreement for POPIA compliance?
**A:** Yes. For clients requiring formal data protection arrangements, we provide a Data Processing Agreement specifying: processing purposes, data security measures, sub-processor arrangements, breach notification procedures, data subject rights, and audit rights. The DPA ensures POPIA-compliant data sharing.

**Q725:** Does Tiletoria provide compliance training records for your audit?
**A:** Training records can be provided (in anonymised form for privacy) showing: training topics, attendance records, competency assessments, and refresher training dates. These records demonstrate our commitment to staff development and regulatory compliance.

**Q726:** Can Tiletoria provide a letter of comfort from Invicta Holdings?
**A:** Letters of comfort or parent company guarantees from Invicta Holdings can be provided for very large supply contracts or where contractual requirements mandate group-level backing. These are subject to Invicta Holdings' board approval and typically require contracts exceeding R20 million.

**Q727:** Does Tiletoria comply with the Advertising Standards Authority code?
**A:** Yes. All our marketing and product claims are accurate, substantiated, and compliant with the ASA Code of Advertising Practice. We do not make unsubstantiated claims about product performance. Technical datasheets and marketing materials are reviewed for accuracy before publication.

**Q728:** Can Tiletoria provide product recall procedures for our quality file?
**A:** Yes. Our Product Recall Procedure documents: recall triggers, notification protocols, stock tracing, client communication, replacement logistics, root cause analysis, and corrective actions. The procedure is part of our ISO 9001 quality management system and has been tested in simulation exercises.

**Q729:** Does Tiletoria provide a sustainability report for ESG compliance?
**A:** Yes. Our annual Sustainability Report covers: environmental performance (carbon, waste, water), social impact (employment, training, community), governance (ethics, compliance, risk management), and B-BBEE transformation. The report supports your own ESG reporting and responsible procurement credentials.

**Q730:** Can Tiletoria provide a legal opinion on contract enforceability?
**A:** We do not provide legal opinions to clients. Our supply agreements are drafted by qualified attorneys and are enforceable under South African law. We recommend that you have our agreement reviewed by your own attorneys as part of your standard contract review process.

**Q731:** Does Tiletoria comply with the Preferential Procurement Policy Framework Act?
**A:** Yes. Our B-BBEE status, pricing structure, and local content credentials are aligned with the PPPFA and its regulations. We support government and private sector clients in meeting their preferential procurement objectives through competitive pricing and maximum B-BBEE recognition.

**Q732:** Can Tiletoria provide product safety data sheets (SDS) for all chemical products?
**A:** Yes. Safety Data Sheets compliant with the Globally Harmonised System (GHS) are provided for all chemical products: adhesives, grouts, waterproofing membranes, sealants, primers, and cleaning chemicals. SDS sheets include: hazard identification, handling precautions, PPE requirements, first aid, and disposal.

**Q733:** Does Tiletoria have a business continuity plan for supply disruptions?
**A:** Yes. Our Business Continuity Plan covers: alternative supply sources, backup warehouse capacity, emergency logistics, IT disaster recovery, and management succession. The plan is tested annually and updated based on lessons learned. A summary of the BCP is available for client review.

**Q734:** Can Tiletoria provide a directors' declaration for tender compliance?
**A:** Yes. A directors' declaration confirming: no conflicts of interest, no undisclosed principal relationships, no corrupt practices, and compliance with all legal requirements can be provided for tender submissions. The declaration is signed by a duly authorised director.

**Q735:** Does Tiletoria provide compliance with the Financial Intelligence Centre Act (FICA)?
**A:** Yes. We are FICA-compliant and verify the identity of all clients as required by the Financial Intelligence Centre Act. Our FICA policy and procedures are available for review. We do not accept cash payments exceeding the FICA threshold and report suspicious transactions as required.

**Q736:** Can Tiletoria provide a supply chain transparency statement?
**A:** Yes. Our Supply Chain Transparency Statement documents: our manufacturing partners (by country), supplier assessment processes, ethical sourcing commitments, and supply chain risk management. The statement demonstrates our commitment to transparent and responsible supply chain management.

**Q737:** Does Tiletoria have a social responsibility and CSI programme?
**A:** Yes. Our corporate social investment programme focuses on: education and skills development in the construction sector, community development in areas where we operate, and environmental sustainability initiatives. CSI spend and programme details are included in our B-BBEE scorecard and sustainability report.

**Q738:** Can Tiletoria provide a letter of authority for signatories on our account?
**A:** Yes. We maintain a signed letter of authority on file for each client, specifying: authorised order placers, authorised payment approvers, credit limit, and special instructions. Changes to authorised signatories require written notification on company letterhead and verification by our credit department.

**Q739:** Does Tiletoria provide compliance with the Property Practitioners Regulatory Authority?
**A:** While Tiletoria is not a property practitioner, we support estate agent clients with: product information for property listings, maintenance guides for rental properties, and renovation advice for property improvement. Our products are used extensively in the property sector for sales preparation and rental maintenance.

**Q740:** Can Tiletoria provide a credit reference from our bank for your assessment?
**A:** We cannot provide credit references on behalf of our clients. For your credit application with Tiletoria, you should provide your own bank references and financial statements. We may request a banker's reference as part of our credit assessment process with your permission.

**Q741:** Does Tiletoria have a formal risk management policy?
**A:** Yes. Our Risk Management Policy covers: strategic risks, operational risks, financial risks, compliance risks, and reputational risks. The policy is reviewed annually by our board and implemented through risk registers, mitigation plans, and internal audit processes. A summary is available for client review.

**Q742:** Can Tiletoria provide proof of registration with professional bodies (TASMA, SAPOA)?
**A:** Tiletoria is a member of TASMA (Tile Association of South Africa) and our senior staff participate in SAPOA (South African Property Owners Association) and GBCSA (Green Building Council of South Africa) events. Membership certificates are available on request.

**Q743:** Does Tiletoria provide compliance with the Electronic Communications and Transactions Act?
**A:** Yes. Our electronic communications (emails, portal transactions, electronic orders) are compliant with the ECT Act. Electronic signatures are accepted on orders and agreements where both parties agree. We maintain records of all electronic transactions for audit purposes.

## Category 14: Risk Management (63 Q&As)

**Q744:** How does Tiletoria manage price volatility risk for development projects?
**A:** Price volatility is managed through: fixed-price supply agreements (price locked for contract duration), exchange rate hedging for imported products, pre-season stock procurement before price increases, and diversified sourcing across multiple countries. Our price-lock mechanism gives developers cost certainty for the full programme.

**Q745:** Can Tiletoria provide exchange rate hedging for our imported products?
**A:** Yes. For projects with signed agreements, we can lock in exchange rates for up to 12 months. This removes currency risk from your cost plan. The hedging cost is either built into the project pricing or charged as a separate facility fee. Without hedging, ZAR/EUR volatility of 15-20% per annum is possible.

**Q746:** What supply chain resilience measures does Tiletoria have in place?
**A:** Supply chain resilience is built through: multi-country sourcing (Italy, Spain, China, India, Turkey, Brazil), multiple manufacturing partners per product category, 60-90 days safety stock, alternative freight routes, and pre-qualified backup suppliers. No single factory or country represents more than 30% of our supply.

**Q747:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of manufacturer insolvency?
**A:** Manufacturer financial stability is assessed annually through: credit checks, trade references, financial reviews, and factory visits. We avoid over-dependence on any single manufacturer and maintain relationships with alternative sources. If a manufacturer becomes insolvent, we activate alternative supply within 2-4 weeks.

**Q748:** Can Tiletoria provide a supply risk register for our project?
**A:** Yes. Our Project Supply Risk Register identifies: sourcing risks (single-source products), logistics risks (shipping disruptions), quality risks (manufacturer variability), financial risks (exchange rate, price volatility), and geopolitical risks (trade sanctions, tariffs). Each risk is rated and mitigated.

**Q749:** What happens if Tiletoria cannot supply due to force majeure?
**A:** In a force majeure event, we activate our Business Continuity Plan: allocate available stock proportionally across affected clients, source from alternative suppliers, expedite in-transit stock, and communicate transparently about expected recovery timelines. We do not simply suspend supply — we work to find solutions.

**Q750:** How does Tiletoria manage shipping and port disruption risks?
**A:** Shipping risks are managed through: multiple shipping line relationships, alternative port options (Durban, Cape Town, Coega, Maputo), pre-booking of vessel space for large projects, and buffer stock to cover 8-10 weeks of disruption. Our logistics team monitors shipping schedules daily and provides early warning of potential disruptions.

**Q751:** Does Tiletoria carry trade credit insurance to protect against client default?
**A:** Yes. We carry trade credit insurance that protects against client payment default. This insurance enables us to offer extended credit terms with confidence and ensures business continuity even in the event of client insolvency. The existence of this insurance also benefits clients by ensuring our financial stability.

**Q752:** Can Tiletoria provide alternative product recommendations if our specified product becomes unavailable?
**A:** Yes. If a specified product becomes unavailable, we immediately: check our alternative supply sources, identify equivalent products with the same or better specification, provide samples and technical data for approval, and secure the alternative stock. This substitution process is documented and quality-assured.

**Q753:** How does Tiletoria manage quality risk across multiple manufacturing sources?
**A:** Multi-source quality management includes: unified quality specifications for all factories, pre-qualification audits, incoming inspection of all shipments, batch testing for consistency, and performance scorecards per factory. Only factories meeting our quality standards are approved for development supply.

**Q754:** Does Tiletoria have business interruption insurance?
**A:** Yes. Our business interruption insurance covers: loss of income due to insured events (fire, flood, equipment breakdown), additional costs of working during recovery, and loss of gross profit during the interruption period. This insurance ensures we can meet our supply commitments even after an insured disruption.

**Q755:** Can Tiletoria provide price collars to cap our exposure to commodity price movements?
**A:** Yes. Price collar arrangements cap price movements within an agreed band (e.g., +/-5%). Movements within the band are absorbed by Tiletoria; movements beyond the band are shared equally. This caps your exposure while keeping pricing competitive. Collars are negotiated as part of the supply agreement.

**Q756:** How does Tiletoria manage currency risk across its diverse import portfolio?
**A:** Currency risk is managed through: natural hedging (USD, EUR, and TRY revenues vs costs), forward exchange contracts for committed purchases, currency diversification (sourcing from multiple currency zones), and client hedging options. Our treasury team monitors currency markets and provides quarterly forecasts.

**Q757:** What contingency plans exist if a key Tiletoria warehouse is damaged or inaccessible?
**A:** Warehouse contingency plans include: alternative warehouse capacity held on standby, inter-warehouse transfer capability (stock moved between regions within 3-5 days), emergency storage with 3PL partners, and rapid-response logistics to redirect deliveries from the affected warehouse. Each warehouse has documented disaster recovery procedures.

**Q758:** Does Tiletoria have a disaster recovery plan for its IT systems?
**A:** Yes. Our IT disaster recovery plan includes: daily data backups to off-site locations, redundant server infrastructure, cloud-based critical systems, and recovery time objectives (RTO) of 24 hours for critical systems. The plan is tested quarterly and covers: order processing, inventory management, and client portal.

**Q759:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of product non-compliance with changing regulations?
**A:** Regulatory change is monitored through: membership in industry associations (TASMA), engagement with SANS technical committees, regulatory alerts from our compliance advisor, and proactive product testing against draft standards. If regulations change during your project, we notify you immediately and propose compliant alternatives.

**Q760:** Can Tiletoria provide a risk matrix showing probability vs impact for our project?
**A:** Yes. Our Project Risk Matrix rates each identified risk on: probability of occurrence (1-5), impact severity (1-5), and overall risk score. Risks are colour-coded: green (low), amber (medium), red (high). Mitigation strategies are specified for amber and red risks. The matrix is reviewed monthly during the project.

**Q761:** Does Tiletoria conduct stress testing of its supply chain?
**A:** Yes. Annual supply chain stress testing simulates: loss of a major supplier, port closure for 30 days, currency movement of 25%, and demand surge of 50%. Stress test results drive inventory policy, supplier diversification, and logistics contingency planning. Findings are reviewed at board level.

**Q762:** How does Tiletoria manage cyber risk and data breaches?
**A:** Cyber risk is managed through: firewalls and intrusion detection systems, regular penetration testing, staff cybersecurity training, encrypted data transmission, access controls, and incident response plans. In the event of a data breach, affected clients are notified within 72 hours per POPIA requirements.

**Q763:** Can Tiletoria provide supply chain finance to mitigate payment risk?
**A:** Supply chain finance is available through our banking partners, where: a finance provider pays Tiletoria immediately, and you repay the financier over an extended term. This reduces payment risk for Tiletoria while improving your cash flow. SCF is arranged through your account executive and requires credit approval.

**Q764:** Does Tiletoria manage political risk for imports from high-risk countries?
**A:** Political risk is assessed for all sourcing countries. For high-risk origins, we: limit exposure (max 15% of supply from any high-risk country), maintain alternative sources, monitor political developments, and hold elevated safety stock. Trade credit insurance covers political risk events for our import portfolio.

**Q765:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of key personnel loss?
**A:** Key person risk is mitigated through: documented processes and client histories, cross-training of staff, succession planning for all critical roles, and manageable client-to-staff ratios. If your account executive leaves, a structured handover ensures continuity. No client relationship depends on a single individual.

**Q766:** Can Tiletoria provide performance security in the form of a bank guarantee?
**A:** Yes. Bank guarantees can be provided as performance security for large projects. The guarantee is issued by a recognised bank in your favour, callable on demand if we fail to meet our contractual obligations. Guarantee costs are typically absorbed by Tiletoria for contracts exceeding R15 million.

**Q767:** Does Tiletoria manage environmental risk in its supply chain?
**A:** Environmental risk management includes: supplier environmental audits, preference for ISO 14001-certified manufacturers, monitoring of environmental regulations in sourcing countries, carbon footprint assessment of logistics, and progressive shift to lower-carbon transport options. Environmental risk is a factor in supplier selection.

**Q768:** How does Tiletoria manage reputational risk from product failures?
**A:** Reputational risk is managed through: stringent quality control, proactive issue identification, rapid response to quality concerns, transparent communication with affected clients, fair warranty claim resolution, and root cause analysis to prevent recurrence. Our track record of quality performance speaks to the effectiveness of these measures.

**Q769:** Can Tiletoria provide a financial health update during our project?
**A:** As a subsidiary of Invicta Holdings (JSE-listed), our financial performance is reported in Invicta's annual financial statements. For very large projects, we can provide a financial comfort letter from Invicta Holdings confirming their support for Tiletoria's contractual obligations. Detailed financial information is confidential.

**Q770:** Does Tiletoria manage transport risk for deliveries to site?
**A:** Transport risk is managed through: comprehensive goods-in-transit insurance, GPS tracking of all vehicles, driver training and monitoring, vehicle maintenance schedules, load security protocols, and incident response procedures. Delivery damage rates are below 0.3%, reflecting our transport risk management effectiveness.

**Q771:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of specification errors?
**A:** Specification risk is mitigated through: multi-level review of all specifications, architect sample approval before procurement, technical submittals for engineer review, mock-up installations for verification, and clear documentation of all specification decisions. Our Spec Lab process is designed to catch errors before they become costly problems.

**Q772:** Can Tiletoria provide indemnity insurance for specific project risks?

**Q773:** Does Tiletoria have a formal enterprise risk management framework?
**A:** Yes. Our Enterprise Risk Management (ERM) framework follows King IV principles and ISO 31000. Risks are identified, assessed, and managed across: strategic, operational, financial, compliance, and reputational categories. The ERM framework is reviewed annually by our board risk committee.

**Q774:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of late payment impacting supply?
**A:** Payment risk is managed through: credit assessment before facility approval, credit limit monitoring, payment tracking and reminder system, proactive communication before due dates, graduated escalation for overdue accounts, and supply suspension only as a last resort after consultation. We work with clients to avoid payment disruption.

**Q775:** Can Tiletoria provide a supply guarantee letter for our development finance?
**A:** Yes. We issue formal Supply Guarantee Letters for development finance applications, confirming: our commitment to supply the specified materials, delivery timelines, pricing certainty, and stock availability. These letters give financiers confidence that material supply risk has been mitigated.

**Q776:** Does Tiletoria manage concentration risk (too much supply from one source)?
**A:** Concentration risk is actively managed: no single manufacturer exceeds 25% of our total supply, no single country exceeds 35%, and we maintain at least two qualified suppliers for every core product category. This diversification ensures continuity even if a major source is disrupted.

**Q777:** How does Tiletoria manage fraud risk in its operations?
**A:** Fraud prevention includes: segregation of duties, authorisation limits, regular internal audits, whistleblowing mechanisms, staff background checks, supplier verification processes, and forensic accounting reviews. Any fraud allegations are investigated immediately by management and reported to the board.

**Q778:** Can Tiletoria provide business continuity assurance for our critical path items?
**A:** For critical path items where any delay would impact the construction programme, we provide: dedicated stockholding, priority delivery status, backup supply sources identified in advance, and expedited logistics capability. Critical items are flagged at specification stage and managed with heightened attention throughout the project.

**Q779:** Does Tiletoria manage climate-related risks in its supply chain?
**A:** Climate risk assessment covers: flood risk at warehouse locations (mitigated by site selection and drainage), storm risk for deliveries (weather monitoring and rerouting), drought risk for water-intensive manufacturing (diversified sourcing), and long-term climate trends affecting manufacturing regions. Climate adaptation is part of our strategic planning.

**Q780:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of intellectual property infringement?
**A:** IP risk is managed through: original product designs, licensed manufacturing arrangements where applicable, trademark protection for our brands, supplier warranties of non-infringement, and legal review of high-risk products. We do not knowingly infringe third-party IP and require the same commitment from our suppliers.

**Q781:** Can Tiletoria provide a financial risk assessment for our procurement team?
**A:** While we do not provide financial advice, we can share: our financial stability indicators (Invicta Holdings JSE listing, annual turnover, asset base), our credit rating information, and our banking relationships. This information supports your procurement team's supplier financial stability assessment.

**Q782:** Does Tiletoria manage legal and regulatory compliance risk across multiple jurisdictions?
**A:** For multi-jurisdictional supply (SADC exports), we ensure compliance with: import regulations of the destination country, SADC trade agreements, customs documentation requirements, and local product standards where applicable. Our freight forwarders manage jurisdictional compliance on our behalf.

**Q783:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of product obsolescence during a long programme?
**A:** Product obsolescence is managed through: manufacturer continuity agreements (minimum 3-year supply commitment), last-time-buy notifications (6 months' notice), successor product identification and approval processes, and spare stock holding for warranty support. We never leave a project without supply options.

**Q784:** Can Tiletoria provide force majeure insurance for our project?
**A:** Force majeure insurance is not something we provide, but we recommend that developers carry contract works insurance covering force majeure events. Our supply agreement specifies how force majeure affects each party's obligations. We maintain business interruption insurance for our own operations.

**Q785:** Does Tiletoria manage reputational risk from association with controversial projects?
**A:** We assess project associations for reputational risk and reserve the right to decline supply to projects that could damage our reputation or conflict with our values. This is exercised rarely and judiciously. We support legitimate development projects that contribute positively to South Africa's built environment.

**Q786:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of technology failure in its warehouse and logistics systems?
**A:** Technology risk is managed through: redundant IT infrastructure, cloud-based backup systems, daily data backups, uninterruptible power supplies (UPS), manual fallback procedures for critical operations, and IT support contracts with 4-hour response times. System availability exceeds 99.5%.

**Q787:** Can Tiletoria provide a supply chain risk heat map for our project?
**A:** Yes. Our Supply Chain Risk Heat Map visually represents: sourcing risks by product and country, logistics risks by route, financial risks by currency, and operational risks by warehouse. The heat map uses red/amber/green coding and is updated quarterly or when significant risk events occur.

**Q788:** Does Tiletoria manage liquidity risk to ensure it can fund large project procurements?
**A:** Liquidity is managed through: Invicta Holdings group funding facilities, trade finance facilities with major banks, supplier credit terms, invoice discounting arrangements, and working capital planning. Our balance sheet strength supports the procurement of stock for large projects without liquidity constraints.

**Q789:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of stock theft or damage in its warehouses?
**A:** Warehouse security includes: 24/7 security personnel, CCTV surveillance, access control systems, alarm systems linked to armed response, perimeter fencing, stock-taking cycles (monthly full counts, weekly cycle counts), and insurance coverage. Stock shrinkage is below 0.1% annually.

**Q790:** Can Tiletoria provide a guarantee of business continuity for the duration of our project?
**A:** Our supply agreement includes business continuity commitments backed by: our Business Continuity Plan, Invicta Holdings financial backing, alternative supply arrangements, and insurance coverage. For critical projects, a formal Business Continuity Letter can be provided confirming these arrangements.

**Q791:** Does Tiletoria manage sustainability risk in its product sourcing?
**A:** Sustainability risk is managed through: supplier sustainability assessments, preference for certified sustainable products, monitoring of environmental regulations, carbon footprint tracking, and progressive transition to lower-impact products. Sustainability risk is increasingly factored into procurement and supplier selection decisions.

**Q792:** How does Tiletoria manage contract risk in its supplier agreements?
**A:** Supplier contract risk is managed through: written agreements with all key suppliers, defined performance standards, penalty clauses for non-performance, force majeure provisions, termination rights, and dispute resolution mechanisms. Supplier performance is reviewed quarterly against contractual KPIs.

**Q793:** Can Tiletoria provide a formal risk disclosure statement for our due diligence?
**A:** Yes. Our Risk Disclosure Statement summarises: key risks facing our business, mitigation strategies, insurance coverage, financial stability indicators, and supply chain resilience measures. The statement is provided confidentially to clients conducting due diligence for large supply contracts.

**Q794:** Does Tiletoria manage the risk of skills shortages affecting service delivery?
**A:** Skills risk is mitigated through: competitive remuneration and retention strategies, continuous training and development, manageable staff-to-client ratios, cross-training to cover key roles, and partnerships with industry training bodies. Our staff turnover is below industry average, ensuring relationship continuity.

**Q795:** How does Tiletoria manage the risk of product substitution fraud (fake products)?
**A:** Product authenticity is ensured through: direct manufacturer relationships (no grey market sourcing), factory verification visits, batch traceability, quality testing on arrival, and secure supply chain documentation. We guarantee product authenticity and provide origin certificates for all products.

**Q796:** Can Tiletoria provide insurance certificates for our risk register?
**A:** Yes. Certificates of insurance for: public liability, product liability, goods in transit, professional indemnity, and employer's liability are provided for your risk register. Certificates are renewed annually and updated certificates are distributed proactively to all clients who require them.

**Q797:** Does Tiletoria manage geopolitical risk in its international supply chain?
**A:** Geopolitical risk is monitored through: country risk assessments, trade policy tracking, sanctions screening, diversified sourcing, and trade credit insurance covering political risk. We avoid sourcing from countries subject to international sanctions or experiencing active conflict.

**Q798:** How does Tiletoria manage compliance risk across changing regulations?
**A:** Regulatory change management includes: monitoring of regulatory developments through industry associations and legal advisors, impact assessment of proposed changes, proactive adjustment of products and processes, client communication of relevant changes, and compliance training for staff. We stay ahead of regulatory changes rather than reacting to them.

**Q799:** Can Tiletoria provide a continuity plan if key manufacturing partnerships are terminated?
**A:** Manufacturing partner termination triggers our Alternative Source Activation Protocol: pre-qualified backup suppliers are contacted, samples are obtained and tested, specifications are updated if necessary, and stock is procured from the alternative source within the lead time specified in our risk register. Clients are kept fully informed throughout.

**Q800:** Does Tiletoria manage the risk of intellectual property theft or data breaches?
**A:** IP and data protection includes: secure IT systems with access controls, encryption of sensitive data, confidentiality agreements with all staff, restricted access to client information, and POPIA-compliant data handling procedures. We have never experienced a significant data breach or IP theft incident.


## Category 15: Post-Project Support (100 Q&As)

**Q801:** What post-project support does Tiletoria provide during the defect liability period?
**A:** During the 12-month defect liability period, Tiletoria provides: responsive technical support, warranty claim assessment, replacement material supply for genuine defects, contractor coordination for remedial work, and snagging inspection attendance. Support is accessed through your account executive with priority response times.

**Q802:** How does Tiletoria handle warranty claims after project handover?
**A:** Warranty claims are submitted to your account executive with: description of the issue, photographs, batch numbers (if visible), and date of discovery. We assess the claim within 5 working days, conduct a site inspection if required, determine whether the issue is product-related or installation-related, and implement remediation for valid claims at our cost.

**Q803:** What warranty period does Tiletoria offer for tiles and flooring products?

**Q804:** Does Tiletoria provide spare stock for post-completion repairs?
**A:** Yes. We recommend holding 2-3% spare stock from the original order for warranty-period repairs. major retail groupse stock is held at our warehouse and released on request throughout the warranty period. This ensures that repairs can be completed with the same batch-matched product, maintaining visual consistency.

**Q805:** Can Tiletoria assist with homeowner warranty queries after sectional title registration?
**A:** Yes. Once sectional title is registered, homeowners can contact Tiletoria directly for warranty queries. We maintain a homeowner support line and can assess warranty claims directly from unit owners. Our warranty documentation is designed to be homeowner-friendly and clearly explains coverage and claim procedures.

**Q806:** What maintenance guidance does Tiletoria provide for end-users?
**A:** Our Homeowner Maintenance Guide covers: daily and weekly cleaning routines, recommended cleaning products (pH-neutral for routine cleaning), products to avoid (acidic or abrasive cleaners), grout maintenance and sealing, stain removal techniques, protection of floor surfaces, and when to call for professional assistance. Guides are provided per product category.

**Q807:** Does Tiletoria provide a warranty register for our property management team?
**A:** Yes. Our Warranty Register documents: every product installed, warranty start date (phase completion date), warranty expiry date, warranty terms and conditions, claim procedures, and contact details for warranty claims. The register is handed over at project completion and supports your property management team.

**Q808:** How does Tiletoria handle latent defects that appear after the DLP?
**A:** Latent defects covered by our product warranty (10 years for tiles, 10 years for waterproofing) are handled even after the DLP expires. The homeowner or body corporate submits a warranty claim, we investigate the defect, and if it is product-related, we coordinate remediation. Installation-related latent defects fall outside our warranty.

**Q809:** Can Tiletoria provide replacement products if the original specification is discontinued?
**A:** If the original product is discontinued, we identify the closest current equivalent and supply it at no additional cost for warranty repairs. If the equivalent is more expensive, we absorb the difference. We maintain records of all discontinued products and their recommended replacements to ensure continuity of warranty support.

**Q810:** Does Tiletoria offer post-project technical inspections?
**A:** Yes. Post-project inspections can be arranged at: 6 months (mid-DLP check), 12 months (DLP expiry), and on an ad-hoc basis for specific concerns. Inspections cover: product performance, installation quality, maintenance condition, and identification of any emerging issues. Inspection reports are provided within 3 working days.

**Q811:** What is Tiletoria's response time for post-project warranty claims?
**A:** We commit to: acknowledging warranty claims within 24 hours, conducting initial assessment within 5 working days, completing site inspection (if required) within 10 working days, and implementing remediation within 20 working days of claim approval. Emergency claims (active water leaks) are prioritised with same-day response.

**Q812:** Can Tiletoria provide cleaning and maintenance product recommendations?

**Q813:** Does Tiletoria provide training for facilities management teams?
**A:** Yes. Facilities management training covers: product identification (what was installed), routine maintenance procedures, problem identification (when to call for help), warranty claim procedures, cleaning product selection, and protection measures during maintenance work. Training is conducted at site or at our Spec Lab.

**Q814:** How does Tiletoria manage warranty claims in mixed-use developments with multiple owners?
**A:** Mixed-use warranty management involves: clear demarcation of warranty responsibility (developer vs body corporate vs individual owners), separate warranty registers per ownership zone, coordinated response for common area claims, and direct homeowner support for unit-specific claims. Documentation is handed over to each relevant party.

**Q815:** Can Tiletoria provide a maintenance schedule for common area flooring?
**A:** Yes. Our Common Area Maintenance Schedule specifies: daily cleaning (lobbies, corridors), weekly deep cleaning, monthly inspection points, quarterly maintenance tasks (grout inspection, sealant checks), annual professional cleaning, and 5-year maintenance cycles (re-sealing, re-grouting where required). This extends the life of the installation.

**Q816:** Does Tiletoria provide replacement tiles for damage caused by third parties (e.g., contractors doing post-completion work)?
**A:** Damage caused by third-party contractors after handover is not covered by our product warranty. However, we can supply replacement tiles from held spare stock or current equivalent products at development pricing rates. We recommend that all post-completion contractors are required to protect existing finishes.

**Q817:** What is the process for final account settlement at project completion?
**A:** Final account settlement involves: reconciliation of all deliveries against the BoQ, processing of all credit notes and returns, agreement of any variations, issuance of the final invoice or credit note, and formal sign-off by both parties. The final account is typically closed within 30 days of practical completion.

**Q818:** Can Tiletoria provide as-built documentation for the tiling and flooring installation?
**A:** Yes. As-built documentation includes: final product specifications (as installed), batch register, warranty certificates, maintenance guides, cleaning instructions, and contact details for post-project support. As-built documentation is provided in both hard copy and digital formats for your O&M manuals.

**Q819:** Does Tiletoria offer extended warranty options beyond the standard period?
**A:** Extended warranties can be negotiated for specific products or applications. For example, a 15-year tile warranty or 15-year waterproofing warranty may be available with enhanced installation requirements and maintenance obligations. Extended warranty terms and pricing are quoted on request.

**Q820:** How does Tiletoria handle warranty claims where the original contractor is no longer available?
**A:** If the original contractor is unavailable, we can: recommend approved remediation contractors from our network, coordinate remedial work directly with your maintenance team, or provide detailed remediation instructions for your new contractor. Our warranty covers materials and reasonable remediation costs regardless of contractor availability.

**Q821:** Can Tiletoria provide a post-project debrief and lessons learned session?
**A:** Yes. Our Post-Project Review covers: specification outcomes, cost performance, delivery performance, quality results, contractor feedback, homeowner feedback, and recommendations for future projects. This debrief is valuable for continuous improvement and is conducted 3-6 months after practical completion.

**Q822:** Does Tiletoria provide ongoing product support after warranty expiry?
**A:** Even after warranty expiry, we provide: product advice, replacement supply (at market rates), maintenance guidance, and technical support. While we are not obligated to provide free support post-warranty, we value long-term relationships and assist where possible. Loyalty pricing applies for repeat clients.

**Q823:** Can Tiletoria help with NHBRC enrolment and inspection support?
**A:** Yes. We provide: product compliance documentation for NHBRC applications, technical datasheets, method statements, and compliance certificates. Our technical team can attend NHBRC inspections to answer questions about the tiling and flooring specification. NHBRC Homebuilder Enrolment is your responsibility; we support it with documentation.

**Q824:** Does Tiletoria provide a dedicated post-project contact for warranty matters?
**A:** Your account executive remains your primary contact for warranty matters. For large projects, a dedicated Warranty Coordinator may be assigned for the DLP period. Homeowners in sectional title schemes can contact our homeowner support line directly for unit-specific warranty queries.

**Q825:** How does Tiletoria handle batch-matching for repair work months after installation?

**Q826:** Can Tiletoria provide professional cleaning services post-completion?
**A:** While we do not provide cleaning services directly, we can recommend professional cleaning contractors experienced with our products. We provide cleaning contractors with: product-specific cleaning protocols, recommended cleaning products, and guidance on products to avoid. Professional cleaning is recommended before handover.

**Q827:** Does Tiletoria provide a completion certificate for our project file?
**A:** Yes. Our Project Completion Certificate confirms: all materials have been supplied as specified, all deliveries are complete, warranties are in effect, documentation has been handed over, and final account has been settled. The certificate is signed by your account executive and our operations manager.

**Q828:** What homeowner documentation does Tiletoria provide for inclusion in sale agreements?
**A:** Homeowner documentation packs include: product care and maintenance guides, warranty certificates (transferable), cleaning product recommendations, do's and don'ts for each flooring type, contact details for warranty claims, and recommended maintenance schedules. Packs can be branded with your development name.

**Q829:** Can Tiletoria assist with preparing homeowner handover packs for sectional title schemes?
**A:** Yes. We prepare Section 24G-compliant maintenance guides for inclusion in homeowner handover packs. These guides meet the requirements of the Sectional Titles Schemes Management Act and provide homeowners with the information they need to maintain their finishes and preserve warranty coverage.

**Q830:** Does Tiletoria provide support for body corporate AGM maintenance budget planning?
**A:** Yes. We can provide: estimated maintenance costs for common area flooring over 5-10 year periods, recommended maintenance schedules, anticipated replacement timelines, and budget planning templates. This helps body corporaries set realistic maintenance levies and plan for capital replacements.

**Q831:** How does Tiletoria handle emergency repairs during the defect liability period?
**A:** Emergency repairs (active leaks, safety hazards) are prioritised with: same-day response, emergency material supply from held stock or branch inventory, coordination with emergency repair contractors, and temporary protection measures if permanent repair requires lead time. Emergency contact details are provided at project handover.

**Q832:** Can Tiletoria provide a product lifespan estimate for our lifecycle costing?

**Q833:** Does Tiletoria offer a post-project customer satisfaction survey?
**A:** Yes. Approximately 3 months after practical completion, we conduct a satisfaction survey covering: product quality, delivery performance, technical support, communication, issue resolution, and overall satisfaction. Results are reviewed with you and drive our improvement actions. Survey participation is voluntary.

**Q834:** Can Tiletoria provide replacement products for renovation or upgrade work post-warranty?
**A:** Yes. Post-warranty renovations are supported with: current equivalent product recommendations, sample supply, pricing at current market rates (loyalty discounts may apply), and technical advice for removal and replacement. We maintain records of all past specifications to assist with matching.

**Q835:** How does Tiletoria manage warranty claims that arise from body corporate vs individual owner disputes?
**A:** Warranty claim responsibility depends on where the defect occurs: common areas (body corporate responsibility with our support), individual units (owner responsibility with our direct support), and structural elements (developer responsibility during DLP, then body corporate). We assist all parties in understanding and exercising their warranty rights.

**Q836:** Does Tiletoria provide guidance on protecting tiled surfaces during other post-completion trades?
**A:** Yes. Our Surface Protection Guide specifies: appropriate protection materials (corrugated cardboard, hardboard, protective film), protection installation methods, duration of protection requirements, and safe removal procedures. Proper protection during post-completion trades prevents damage and warranty disputes.

**Q837:** Can Tiletoria provide a final project cost analysis comparing budget to actual?
**A:** Yes. Our Final Cost Analysis shows: original budget, final actual cost, variances by category, reasons for variances, value engineering savings achieved, and recommendations for future projects. This analysis supports your project financial review and informs future development budgeting.

**Q838:** Does Tiletoria offer post-project CPD events for the project team?
**A:** Yes. After project completion, we invite your project team to CPD events at our Spec Lab, covering lessons learned from your project, new product developments, and industry updates. These events maintain the relationship and ensure your team benefits from the experience gained.

**Q839:** How does Tiletoria handle product recalls that affect completed projects?
**A:** In the extremely rare event of a product recall, we: notify all affected clients immediately (within 24 hours), provide details of the issue and affected batch numbers, coordinate replacement material supply, arrange remedial work through approved contractors, and bear all associated costs. Our batch traceability enables precise identification of affected projects.

**Q840:** Can Tiletoria provide a testimonial or case study reference for future projects?
**A:** With your permission, we prepare a case study documenting the project outcomes, which can be used by both parties for marketing and reference purposes. We also provide testimonials on request. These references support your future tender submissions and financing applications.

**Q841:** Does Tiletoria provide guidance on acoustic underlay maintenance in apartments?
**A:** Acoustic underlay maintenance guidance includes: avoiding penetration of the underlay (no screws or nails), protecting from moisture ingress, cleaning practices that don't degrade the underlay, and inspection points during flooring maintenance. Proper underlay maintenance preserves acoustic performance and prevents complaints.

**Q842:** How does Tiletoria handle warranty claims for products damaged by tenant misuse?
**A:** Warranty does not cover damage caused by misuse, negligence, or abuse by tenants or homeowners. We assess each claim to determine the cause of damage. If misuse is established, we provide: a report explaining the cause, recommendations for prevention, and a quotation for replacement at the owner's cost.

**Q843:** Can Tiletoria provide a digital handover pack instead of physical documents?
**A:** Yes. Digital handover packs are provided on USB drive or via secure download link, containing: PDF warranties, maintenance guides, cleaning instructions, technical datasheets, and contact details. Digital packs are environmentally friendly, easily searchable, and can be duplicated for body corporate records.

**Q844:** Does Tiletoria offer periodic check-in calls during the defect liability period?
**A:** Yes. Your account executive conducts scheduled check-in calls at: 1 month, 3 months, 6 months, and 11 months post-completion. These calls identify emerging issues early, ensure maintenance guidance is being followed, and provide an opportunity for feedback before the DLP expires.

**Q845:** Can Tiletoria assist with preparing snagging lists during the defect liability period?
**A:** Yes. Our technical team can assist with snagging by: attending snagging inspections, categorising defects (product vs installation vs other trade damage), recommending remedial actions, coordinating remedial material supply, and verifying that snagging items are resolved satisfactorily.

**Q846:** How does Tiletoria manage warranty claims for waterproofing failures after the DLP?
**A:** Waterproofing warranty claims are covered for 10 years regardless of DLP status. Claims are investigated by our waterproofing technical specialist who determines: whether the failure is membrane-related, installation-related, or structural, and recommends appropriate remediation. Valid membrane failures are remedied at our cost.

**Q847:** Does Tiletoria provide guidance on grout colour restoration if discolouration occurs?
**A:** Yes. Grout discolouration guidance covers: identifying the cause (staining, mould, efflorescence, wear), appropriate cleaning methods, re-colouring options (grout colourants), re-grouting procedures for severe cases, and prevention measures. We supply the products and guidance for grout restoration.

**Q848:** Can Tiletoria provide an insurance valuation for the flooring installation for replacement purposes?
**A:** We can provide current replacement costs for insurance valuation purposes, showing: product costs, installation material costs, and indicative labour rates. This helps your insurance broker ensure adequate coverage for the flooring and tiling elements. Valuations are updated annually on request.

**Q849:** Does Tiletoria offer a loyalty discount for repeat development projects?
**A:** Yes. Repeat clients receive: preferred pricing based on cumulative volume, enhanced payment terms based on trading history, priority service during peak periods, first access to new products and promotions, and invitations to exclusive client events. Loyalty benefits increase with relationship tenure and volume.

**Q850:** How does Tiletoria handle complaints about finishes from homeowners or tenants?
**A:** Homeowner complaints are handled through: initial phone assessment to understand the concern, site visit if required (coordinated with the body corporate or managing agent), technical assessment of the issue, explanation of findings (product issue, installation issue, or maintenance issue), and appropriate remediation. We aim for fair and prompt resolution.

**Q851:** Can Tiletoria provide a refurbishment assessment for ageing developments?
**A:** Yes. For developments approaching 10-15 years of age, we provide a Refurbishment Assessment covering: condition of existing flooring, remaining useful life, recommended maintenance vs replacement, cost estimates for refurbishment, and upgrade options. This helps body corporaries plan for capital expenditure.

**Q852:** Does Tiletoria provide post-project spend analysis showing cost per m² achieved?
**A:** Yes. Our Post-Project Spend Analysis shows: actual cost per m² by product category, cost per unit by unit type, cost comparison to original budget, cost comparison to industry benchmarks, and value engineering savings realised. This analysis informs your future project budgeting and specification decisions.

**Q853:** How does Tiletoria manage confidentiality of project data after completion?
**A:** Project data is retained in accordance with our data retention policy (typically 7 years for contractual and warranty data, longer for permanent project records). All data is stored securely and treated as confidential. After the retention period, data is securely destroyed in accordance with POPIA requirements.

**Q854:** Can Tiletoria provide a reference letter confirming satisfactory completion of supply?
**A:** Yes. On request, we issue a Reference Letter confirming: the scope of supply, contract value, supply period, delivery performance, and satisfactory completion. This letter supports your future tender submissions, vendor accreditation processes, and financing applications.

**Q855:** Does Tiletoria offer annual maintenance inspections for large developments?
**A:** Annual maintenance inspections can be arranged for large developments, particularly for common areas and high-traffic zones. Inspections identify: wear patterns, maintenance needs, potential warranty issues before they escalate, and compliance with maintenance requirements. Inspections are charged at a standard daily rate.

**Q856:** Can Tiletoria provide replacement products for insurance claims (storm damage, burst geysers, etc.)?
**A:** Yes. We work with insurance assessors and contractors to provide: product identification (matching existing finishes), supply of replacement materials, technical advice for remediation, and cost estimates for insurance purposes. Rapid response minimises tenant inconvenience and prevents secondary damage.

**Q857:** Does Tiletoria provide guidance on underfloor heating system maintenance in tiled floors?
**A:** Underfloor heating maintenance guidance covers: system commissioning and testing, temperature control settings, seasonal adjustments, troubleshooting common issues, and when to call the heating installer. Tiletoria's warranty covers the tile and adhesive; the heating system warranty is provided by the heating manufacturer.

**Q858:** How does Tiletoria handle warranty claims where the developer has sold the development?
**A:** When a development is sold, warranties transfer to the new owner or body corporate. We update our warranty register with the new contact details and continue to honour all warranty obligations. Our warranty documentation specifies that warranties run with the property, not the original developer.

**Q859:** Can Tiletoria provide cleaning protocol videos for homeowner education?
