**A:** Yes. Our homeowner education resources include: video guides for routine cleaning, stain removal demonstrations, maintenance procedures, and common mistakes to avoid. Videos are provided on USB or via private web link and can be included in your digital handover pack or resident app.

**Q860:** Does Tiletoria offer a warranty claim hotline for urgent issues?
**A:** Yes. A dedicated warranty claim hotline is available during business hours for urgent issues (water leaks, safety hazards, significant defects). The hotline connects directly to our technical team who can provide immediate guidance and dispatch resources. Non-urgent claims can be submitted via email.

**Q861:** Can Tiletoria provide a maintenance cost forecast for the body corporate's 10-year maintenance plan?
**A:** Yes. Our 10-Year Maintenance Cost Forecast estimates: annual maintenance costs, periodic deep cleaning costs, sealant replacement cycles, grout maintenance costs, and anticipated replacement timelines. This supports the body corporate's mandatory 10-year maintenance plan under the Sectional Titles Schemes Management Act.

**Q862:** How does Tiletoria manage replacement stock for phased handover where each phase has a different completion date?
**A:** Each phase has its own spare stock allocation with warranty start date aligned to that phase's practical completion. major retail groupse stock is held until the latest phase's warranty expires, ensuring all phases have access to batch-matched replacements throughout their respective warranty periods.

**Q863:** Does Tiletoria provide a project close-out meeting with all stakeholders?
**A:** Yes. A formal Project Close-Out Meeting is held 1-3 months after practical completion, attended by: your project manager, our account executive, and relevant technical staff. The meeting reviews: project outcomes, issues encountered, lessons learned, warranty arrangements, and ongoing support protocols.

**Q864:** Can Tiletoria provide assistance with PMR (Prescribed Management Rule) compliance for sectional titles?
**A:** We provide the maintenance documentation required for PMR compliance, including: maintenance schedules, product specifications, warranty details, and repair guidelines. Our documentation supports the body corporate in meeting its maintenance obligations under the Sectional Titles Schemes Management Act.

**Q865:** How does Tiletoria handle disputes over whether a defect is product-related or maintenance-related?
**A:** Disputes are resolved through: technical inspection by our qualified assessor, independent laboratory testing if required, review of maintenance records, and application of warranty terms. We provide a detailed technical report with our findings and recommendations. Fair resolution is our priority — we don't deny valid claims.

**Q866:** Does Tiletoria provide a "welcome pack" for new homeowners in sectional title developments?
**A:** Yes. Our Homeowner Welcome Pack includes: product care guide, warranty card, cleaning product recommendations, do's and don'ts, emergency contact numbers, and a welcome letter from Tiletoria. Packs can be branded with your development identity and distributed at key handover.

**Q867:** Can Tiletoria provide re-grouting and re-sealing services post-warranty?
**A:** Post-warranty maintenance services can be arranged through our network of approved contractors. We provide: technical assessment, scope of work, material supply, and contractor coordination. These services are charged at market rates. Preventative re-grouting and re-sealing extends the life of wet area installations.

**Q868:** Does Tiletoria offer product upgrade consultations for renovation projects in completed developments?
**A:** Yes. For renovation or upgrade projects in completed developments, we provide: current product range overview, compatibility advice (what works with existing substrates), cost comparisons, sample supply, and technical guidance. Loyalty pricing may apply for clients with a history of Tiletoria supply.

**Q869:** How does Tiletoria ensure that post-project support doesn't degrade after the initial project team moves on?
**A:** Warranty support continuity is ensured through: detailed project records in our CRM, batch traceability in our warehouse management system, dedicated warranty coordinator role, and escalation to senior management if issues arise. Even if your original account executive has moved on, your project history is preserved and accessible.

**Q870:** Can Tiletoria provide a compliance certificate for municipal occupation certificate applications?
**A:** Yes. Our Technical Completion Certificate confirms that all tiling and flooring has been installed in accordance with the approved specification and relevant SANS standards. This certificate supports your occupation certificate application to the local municipality.

**Q871:** Does Tiletoria maintain an archive of project specifications for future reference?
**A:** Yes. All project specifications, BoQs, delivery records, batch registers, and warranty documentation are archived for a minimum of 10 years. This archive enables us to: answer warranty queries, supply matching replacement products, provide references for future projects, and support any disputes or claims.

**Q872:** Can Tiletoria assist with preparing a defects list for the NHBRC final inspection?
**A:** Yes. Our technical team can review your defects list and categorise: tiling-related defects that are our responsibility, defects that are contractor responsibility, and defects caused by other trades. We can then coordinate remediation of product-related defects and provide guidance on contractor-related defects.

**Q873:** Does Tiletoria offer a "health check" service for flooring installations approaching warranty expiry?
**A:** Yes. Pre-warranty-expiry health checks identify: any latent defects that should be claimed before expiry, maintenance issues that need attention, and preventative measures to extend product life. This proactive service helps you maximise warranty value and avoid post-warranty surprises.

**Q874:** How does Tiletoria manage replacement supply if the original manufacturer is no longer trading?
**A:** If the original manufacturer has ceased trading, we identify the closest equivalent from our current range and supply it at no additional cost for valid warranty claims. Our multi-source supply strategy and product knowledge enable us to find suitable matches even when original sources are unavailable.

**Q875:** Can Tiletoria provide a summary of warranty claims for our annual reporting?
**A:** Yes. Our Annual Warranty Claim Summary shows: number of claims received, claims by category (product defect, installation issue, other), resolution status, cost of remediation, and trends. This summary supports your quality reporting and helps identify systemic issues requiring attention.

**Q876:** Does Tiletoria offer a callback service for homeowners who need advice but don't have a warranty claim?
**A:** Yes. Our homeowner advice line provides: general maintenance guidance, cleaning advice, product identification (what do I have?), stain removal tips, and guidance on when a warranty claim may be appropriate. This service builds goodwill and prevents minor issues becoming major problems.

**Q877:** Can Tiletoria provide grout sealer for body corporate maintenance programmes?
**A:** Yes. Grout sealer is available in bulk quantities for body corporate maintenance programmes. We provide: product selection guidance (penetrating sealers vs surface sealers), application instructions, quantity calculations, and recommended application frequency (typically every 2-3 years in wet areas, 3-5 years in dry areas).

**Q878:** How does Tiletoria handle warranty claims for products installed in basements or sub-floor areas?
**A:** Basement and sub-floor installations require specific product selection and installation methods. Warranty coverage is subject to: correct product specification for below-grade application, proper damp-proofing, adequate ventilation, and correct installation. Claims in these areas are assessed against these specific requirements.

**Q879:** Does Tiletoria provide a final sign-off certificate at the end of the defect liability period?
**A:** Yes. At DLP expiry, we issue a Final Sign-Off Certificate confirming: all defects have been resolved, warranty obligations are discharged (except for extended product warranties), and the project is formally closed from a supply perspective. This certificate supports your final retention release and project close-out.

**Q880:** Can Tiletoria provide material for rectification work caused by other trades (not product defects)?
**A:** Yes. We supply replacement materials for rectification work at development pricing rates, even where the damage was not caused by product defects. This includes: tiles damaged by electrical or plumbing contractors, flooring damaged by painters, and surfaces damaged during furniture installation. Prompt supply minimises tenant inconvenience.

**Q881:** Does Tiletoria offer an annual review of maintenance spend vs budget for body corporaries?
**A:** For large developments with ongoing Tiletoria maintenance supply, we provide: annual spend analysis, comparison to maintenance budget, identification of unusual spend patterns, recommendations for cost reduction, and forecasting for the year ahead. This helps body corporaries manage their maintenance levies effectively.

**Q882:** Can Tiletoria provide replacement products for fire-damaged units?
**A:** Yes. We work with insurance assessors and fire restoration contractors to: identify the original products installed, supply matching or equivalent replacements, coordinate with the restoration programme, and provide cost estimates for insurance purposes. Rapid turnaround minimises re-occupation delays.

**Q883:** How does Tiletoria manage confidentiality of homeowner information when dealing directly with unit owners?
**A:** Homeowner information is handled in strict compliance with POPIA. We collect only the information necessary for warranty administration, store it securely, do not share it with third parties without consent, and retain it only for the warranty period plus applicable legal retention requirements.

**Q884:** Does Tiletoria provide a "troubleshooting guide" for common post-installation issues?
**A:** Yes. Our Troubleshooting Guide covers: cracked tiles (causes and solutions), loose tiles, stained grout, efflorescence, hollow-sounding tiles, scratched floors, and lifting LVT. Each issue includes: probable cause, severity assessment, recommended action, and whether it constitutes a warranty claim.

**Q885:** Can Tiletoria provide a formal assignment of warranties when the development is sold?
**A:** Yes. Warranty assignment documentation transfers all remaining warranty rights from the developer to the purchaser or body corporate. The assignment is formalised in writing and registered in our warranty management system. All warranty terms and conditions remain unchanged after assignment.

**Q886:** Does Tiletoria offer post-project product training for new facilities managers?
**A:** Yes. When facilities management changes hands, we provide onboarding training for new managers covering: what products are installed, where documentation is stored, how to submit warranty claims, routine maintenance requirements, and who to contact for support. This ensures continuity of care regardless of personnel changes.

**Q887:** Can Tiletoria provide replacement tiles for expansion joint covers that have worn or been damaged?
**A:** Yes. Expansion joint covers and movement joint profiles are available as replacement items. We supply matching profiles from our standard range or can source custom profiles for specialist applications. Proper expansion joint maintenance prevents more serious structural movement issues.

**Q888:** How does Tiletoria handle warranty claims in mixed-tenure developments (rental + ownership)?
**A:** Mixed-tenure warranty management requires: clear demarcation of responsibility (developer for rental units during DLP, owner for sold units), direct homeowner support for owner-occupied units, coordinated response for common areas, and clear communication protocols with the managing agent. Each tenure type has its own support pathway.

**Q889:** Does Tiletoria provide a "project legacy" document summarising the supply for future reference?
**A:** Yes. The Project Legacy Document summarises: what was supplied, why those choices were made, what worked well, what could be improved, key contacts, warranty status, and recommendations for future phases or similar projects. This institutional knowledge asset supports your team's continuous improvement.

**Q890:** Can Tiletoria provide specialist cleaning products for stubborn stains post-warranty?

**Q891:** How does Tiletoria ensure that warranty repairs match the aesthetic of the original installation?

**Q892:** Does Tiletoria offer a subscription maintenance service for large developments?
**A:** For very large developments or property portfolios, a subscription maintenance service can be arranged covering: periodic inspections, routine maintenance supply, priority response for issues, and discounted rates for replacement materials. Subscription terms are customised to your property management needs.

**Q893:** Can Tiletoria provide replacement waterproofing membranes for localized repairs?
**A:** Yes. Localised waterproofing repairs require: precise identification of the failure point, compatible membrane product, skilled application by an approved applicator, and integration with the existing membrane. Our waterproofing technical team manages this process to ensure the repair maintains system integrity.

**Q894:** Does Tiletoria provide post-project feedback surveys for contractors?
**A:** Yes. Contractor feedback surveys capture: product handling experience, installation feedback, delivery satisfaction, technical support quality, and suggestions for improvement. Contractor feedback is valuable for product selection and service improvement on future projects.

**Q895:** Can Tiletoria provide material for unit make-good when tenants vacate rental units?
**A:** Yes. Rental unit make-good materials are supplied at competitive rates for: tile replacement, grout repair, LVT plank replacement, and general refresh. We maintain records of the original specification to ensure matching. Bulk supply for multiple units qualifies for volume pricing.

**Q896:** How does Tiletoria manage warranty claims where multiple products may be involved (e.g., tile + adhesive + membrane)?
**A:** Multi-product claims are investigated holistically: our technical team assesses the complete system, determines which product(s) contributed to the failure, and coordinates remediation across all affected elements. Our system warranty for wet areas simplifies this by covering the complete installed system.

**Q897:** Does Tiletoria provide a "care card" for each product type that homeowners can keep for reference?
**A:** Yes. Product Care Cards are credit-card-sized guides that homeowners can keep handy, showing: key do's and don'ts, recommended cleaning frequency, emergency stain treatment, and warranty contact details. Cards are provided per product type (tile, LVT, laminate, stone).

**Q898:** Can Tiletoria assist with preparing for the NHBRC 5-year structural warranty inspection?
**A:** While the NHBRC 5-year inspection focuses on structural elements, we can provide: product performance confirmation, maintenance records, warranty status verification, and technical support if any flooring-related issues are raised. Our 10-year product warranty extends beyond the NHBRC structural warranty period.

**Q899:** Does Tiletoria offer a "no-questions-asked" replacement policy for minor defects during the DLP?
**A:** For minor defects (individual cracked tiles, small grout issues, minor chips) during the DLP, we offer streamlined replacement: claim by email with photo, automatic approval for minor items, and supply from held spare stock without lengthy investigation. This pragmatic approach keeps minor issues from becoming major admin burdens.

**Q900:** How does Tiletoria manage the handover of post-project support responsibilities from the project team to the warranty team?
**A:** A formal handover from Project Team to Warranty Team occurs at practical completion: all project documentation is transferred, warranty registers are created, spare stock is allocated, homeowner support protocols are activated, and the warranty team is briefed on project-specific details. This ensures seamless transition.

## Category 16: Competitive Differentiation (100 Q&As)

**Q901:** Why should we choose Tiletoria over other tile distributors for our development?
**A:** Tiletoria offers unique advantages for developers: (1) scale — over 20,000m² warehousing, 200 containers/month, capacity for multiple simultaneous large projects; (2) single-source accountability — tiles, adhesives, grouts, waterproofing, trims, LVT all from one supplier; (3) institutional backing — 75% owned by JSE-listed Invicta Holdings; (4) proven track record — major national retailers, major national retailers, major national banks, major national banks, major automotive brands, and major residential developers; (5) dedicated development team — account executives who understand construction programmes and penalty clauses.

**Q902:** What is Tiletoria's scale compared to other tile distributors in South Africa?
**A:** Tiletoria is one of South Africa's largest tile distributors, with: over 20,000m² warehousing (among the largest in the industry), 200+ containers imported monthly, 400 tons/day delivery capacity, and 25+ years of operation. Our scale enables dedicated stockholding for multiple large projects simultaneously — a capability most competitors cannot match.

**Q903:** Does Tiletoria offer true single-source accountability for the complete flooring system?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria supplies the complete flooring system: tiles, LVT, laminate, adhesives, grouts, waterproofing, trims, profiles, and accessories. Single-source accountability means: one purchase order, one delivery schedule, one invoice, one warranty, and one point of contact for any issue. This eliminates the finger-pointing common with multi-source procurement.

**Q904:** How does Tiletoria's development-specific service differ from general trade supply?
**A:** Our Development Accounts team is structured specifically for large projects: dedicated account executives, project-based stockholding, programme-aligned delivery scheduling, quarterly business reviews, performance bonds, and escalation to senior management. General trade suppliers offer counter sales and ad-hoc delivery — not the structured project management that developers require.

**Q905:** What corporate track record does Tiletoria have that smaller distributors cannot match?
**A:** Tiletoria's corporate client list includes: major national retailers, major national retailers, major national retailers, major national banks, major national banks, major restaurant chains, major national retailers, major retail groups, major automotive brands, major automotive brands, major homeware retailers, and major entertainment brands. These blue-chip clients demand: consistent quality, reliable delivery, professional documentation, and institutional accountability — the same standards we bring to residential development projects.

**Q906:** Does Tiletoria's relationship with Invicta Holdings provide additional security for our project?
**A:** Yes. Invicta Holdings (JSE-listed industrial group, 75% owner of Tiletoria) provides: financial backing for large procurement commitments, governance oversight, risk management systems, and parent company guarantees where required. This institutional backing differentiates Tiletoria from smaller, owner-managed distributors.

**Q907:** How does Tiletoria's Spec Lab differentiate it from competitors?
**A:** The Spec Lab at Spec Lab Menlyn, is a professional specification resource centre unique in the South African market. It offers: comprehensive sample libraries, CPD training, specification writing support, product comparison facilities, and professional consultation space. Competitors typically offer trade counters, not professional specification services.

**Q908:** Can Tiletoria provide performance bonds that smaller competitors cannot?
**A:** Yes. Our balance sheet strength and Invicta Holdings backing enable us to provide performance bonds and parent company guarantees for large projects. Smaller distributors often cannot obtain or afford performance security, leaving developers exposed to supply failure risk. Our bonds are accepted by all major development financiers.

**Q909:** How does Tiletoria's national coverage benefit multi-province developers?
**A:** With warehousing and delivery capability in Gauteng, KZN, and the Western Cape, we service multi-province developments from a single supply agreement with consistent pricing, consistent product, and consistent service standards. Competitors with single-region coverage require multiple supplier relationships with coordination challenges.

**Q910:** What value engineering capability does Tiletoria offer that competitors don't?
**A:** Our "Meet the Specification for Less" value engineering service is delivered by qualified technical professionals who understand specification integrity. Unlike competitors who simply offer cheaper alternatives, we provide: technical equivalence analysis, architect-ready substitution proposals, sample comparisons, and warranty equivalence guarantees. This professional approach achieves savings without specification compromise.

**Q911:** How does Tiletoria's B-BBEE status benefit our scorecard compared to competitors?
**A:** Tiletoria's Level 1 or 2 B-BBEE status provides maximum preferential procurement recognition under the revised codes. Many competitors are Level 4-8, providing fewer procurement points. Engaging Tiletoria can improve your B-BBEE scorecard and support your own transformation objectives.

**Q912:** Does Tiletoria offer dedicated stockholding that competitors cannot match?
**A:** Our over 20,000m² warehousing capacity enables dedicated stockholding for multiple large projects simultaneously. Smaller distributors may promise dedicated stockholding but lack the warehouse capacity to fulfil this commitment when demand peaks. Our scale ensures that your stock is physically available when you need it.

**Q913:** How does Tiletoria's 25-year track record provide confidence for our project?
**A:** Founded in 1995, Tiletoria has survived and thrived through multiple economic cycles, currency crises, and market disruptions. This longevity demonstrates: financial resilience, operational competence, strong manufacturer relationships, and satisfied clients who return. A 25-year track record provides confidence that we will be here to honour our warranties.

**Q914:** What technology advantages does Tiletoria offer over competitors?
**A:** Technology advantages include: SAP-integrated warehouse management system with real-time stock visibility, GPS-tracked delivery fleet, client portal for ordering and tracking, barcode scanning for batch traceability, and electronic documentation management. Many competitors operate on manual systems with limited visibility.

**Q915:** How does Tiletoria's multi-country sourcing reduce supply risk compared to single-source competitors?
**A:** We source from Italy, Spain, France, Turkey, Brazil, India, and China. If a competitor sources primarily from one country (e.g., only China), they face concentrated risk from: trade disputes, currency movements, shipping disruptions, or factory issues. Our diversification ensures continuity even when specific supply lines are disrupted.

**Q916:** Can Tiletoria's 400 tons/day delivery capacity handle our project's peak demand?
**A:** Our 400 tons/day delivery capacity (approximately 15,000-20,000m² of tiles) can handle the peak demand of even the largest residential developments. Competitors with smaller fleets may struggle to maintain delivery schedules during peak periods, exposing you to programme delay penalties.

**Q917:** How does Tiletoria's institutional governance benefit our compliance requirements?
**A:** As part of Invicta Holdings, Tiletoria operates with: audited financial statements, board oversight, formal risk management, ISO 9001 and ISO 14001 certification, professional indemnity insurance, and compliance with JSE governance requirements. This institutional rigour supports your own governance and due diligence requirements.

**Q918:** What is Tiletoria's policy on exclusivity for development product ranges?
**A:** Our developer-exclusive product ranges are not available through retail channels, ensuring your specification cannot be directly compared by end-buyers. This exclusivity protects your margin and brand positioning. Competitors who sell the same products through retail expose your specification to price comparison.

**Q919:** How does Tiletoria's account executive model compare to competitor service?
**A:** Our dedicated Development Account Executives average 12+ years of industry experience and manage a focused portfolio of development clients. Competitors often assign retail sales staff to development accounts, lacking the construction industry knowledge and project management capability that developers need.

**Q920:** Can Tiletoria provide references from residential developers of similar scale to ours?
**A:** Yes. We can provide references from developers operating in the 20-200 unit range in Gauteng, KZN, and the Western Cape, with projects similar to yours in type (townhouses, apartments, estate homes). Sector-specific references are more credible than generic corporate references.

**Q921:** How does Tiletoria's quality management system compare to industry norms?
**A:** Our ISO 9001:2015 certified quality management system, 99.2%+ stock accuracy, <0.5% defect rate, and batch traceability system exceed typical industry standards. Many competitors operate without formal quality certification, leading to inconsistent product quality and supply errors.

**Q922:** What financial stability does Invicta Holdings provide compared to independent distributors?
**A:** Invicta Holdings is a JSE-listed industrial group with a market capitalisation exceeding R2 billion, diversified revenue streams, and a strong balance sheet. This financial backing ensures that Tiletoria can: fund large stock commitments, invest in infrastructure, absorb short-term losses, and honour long-term warranties. Independent distributors lack this financial depth.

**Q923:** How does Tiletoria's project portal compare to competitor client systems?
**A:** Our client portal offers: real-time stock visibility, online ordering, delivery tracking, documentation download, spend reporting, and quality dashboards. Many competitors lack any client-facing technology, relying on phone calls and email for all communication. Our portal reduces your administrative burden and improves transparency.

**Q924:** Does Tiletoria offer better payment terms than competitors for established developers?
**A:** Our payment terms (30-60 days for established developers, milestone-based payments, supply-chain finance options) are competitive with or better than industry norms. Our institutional financial strength enables us to offer terms that smaller, under-capitalised distributors cannot match.

**Q925:** How does Tiletoria's specification support reduce our architect's workload?
**A:** Our Spec Lab team prepares: NBS-format specifications, detail drawings, finish schedules, and technical submittals ready for direct insertion into the architect's documentation. This saves architects significant specification time and reduces specification errors. Competitors without specification teams leave this work to the architect.

**Q926:** What delivery reliability advantage does Tiletoria offer?
**A:** Our 95%+ on-time delivery rate for development projects is supported by: dedicated fleet, GPS tracking, route optimisation, backup vehicle capacity, and professional logistics management. Competitors who rely on third-party transport have less control over delivery performance and less ability to recover from disruptions.

**Q927:** How does Tiletoria handle programme-critical deliveries better than competitors?
**A:** For critical path items, we offer: dedicated stockholding, priority vehicle allocation, express delivery, after-hours delivery, and same-day response for emergencies. Our scale and resources enable us to mobilise for urgent situations in ways that smaller competitors cannot match.

**Q928:** Does Tiletoria's complete wet area system warranty differentiate it from competitors?
**A:** Yes. Our 10-year Complete Wet Area System Warranty (covering membrane + tiles + adhesive + grout) is a comprehensive warranty that few competitors can match. Most competitors offer: no waterproofing warranty, only a material warranty, or separate warranties from multiple suppliers with coordination gaps.

**Q929:** How does Tiletoria's value engineering deliver more savings than competitor alternatives?
**A:** Our value engineering delivers 10-25% savings through: professional technical analysis (not just cheaper products), architect-approved substitutions, warranty equivalence guarantees, and total installed cost optimisation (considering labour, wastage, and programme). Competitor "discounting" often compromises specification integrity.

**Q930:** Can Tiletoria provide parent company guarantees that competitors cannot?
**A:** Parent company guarantees from Invicta Holdings are available for large projects. Very few tile distributors can offer this level of security. For developers with penalty clauses or lender requirements for supplier guarantees, this is a significant differentiator.

**Q931:** How does Tiletoria's continuous improvement culture benefit our projects?
**A:** Our quarterly business reviews, client feedback surveys, inter-phase reviews, and lessons-learned sessions drive continuous improvement. Insights from one project are applied to the next, meaning that repeat clients benefit from accumulated learning. Competitors without structured review processes repeat the same mistakes.

**Q932:** What procurement efficiency does single-source supply from Tiletoria create?
**A:** Single-source supply reduces: purchase orders issued (one vs multiple), invoices processed (one per month vs many), delivery coordination (one schedule vs multiple), warranty administration (one contact vs many), and contractor queries (one technical support line vs multiple). This efficiency saves your procurement team significant time.

**Q933:** How does Tiletoria's developer consortium programme benefit smaller developers?
**A:** Our Developer Consortium allows smaller developers (20-50 units) to access development-tier pricing and services by aggregating demand. This is unique in the market — most competitors offer retail pricing to small developers. The consortium gives smaller players the buying power of large developers.

**Q934:** Does Tiletoria's national footprint reduce freight costs compared to regional competitors?
**A:** Yes. With warehouses in Gauteng, KZN, and the Western Cape, we deliver from the nearest warehouse, minimising freight distance and cost. Competitors with single-location warehouses incur higher freight costs to distant regions, which are passed on to the developer. Our national coverage optimises delivered cost.

**Q935:** How does Tiletoria's product range breadth compare to specialised flooring distributors?

**Q936:** What training and development does Tiletoria invest in that benefits our projects?
**A:** We invest in: ongoing training for account executives (construction management, project management), technical team training (new products, standards updates), logistics team training (efficiency, safety), and Spec Lab CPD events for architects. This investment trans into better service for your projects.

**Q937:** How does Tiletoria's warehouse capacity affect availability during peak demand?
**A:** Our over 20,000m² warehousing allows us to hold 400,000m²+ of stock. During peak demand periods (pre-Christmas construction rush), competitors with limited warehouse capacity run out of popular lines. Our capacity ensures that your stock is available even during demand peaks.

**Q938:** Can Tiletoria's scale support our growth from 50 units to 500 units per year?
**A:** Yes. Our capacity can support significant client growth without service degradation. Whether you are building 50 units or 500 units, our infrastructure, team size, and supplier relationships can scale to meet your needs. Many of our clients have grown from small to large developers with Tiletoria as their constant supply partner.

**Q939:** How does Tiletoria's relationship with international manufacturers benefit our pricing?
**A:** Our 25-year relationships with manufacturers in 7 countries, combined with 200+ containers/month volume, give us negotiating leverage that smaller distributors cannot match. This volume leverage trans into better pricing, priority allocation during supply constraints, and access to exclusive product ranges.

**Q940:** What is Tiletoria's approach to sustainability that differentiates it from competitors?
**A:** Our sustainability approach includes: ISO 14001 environmental management, expanding range of green-certified products, responsible sourcing audits of manufacturers, recycling programmes, carbon footprint monitoring, and support for green building certifications. Many competitors lack any formal sustainability programme.

**Q941:** How does Tiletoria's quality assurance reduce our rectification costs?
**A:** Our <0.5% defect rate, batch consistency management, and incoming quality inspection reduce the incidence of product-related rectifications. Competitors with less rigorous quality control deliver higher defect rates, leading to rectification costs, programme delays, and buyer dissatisfaction that ultimately affect your profitability.

**Q942:** Does Tiletoria offer longer warranty periods than competitors?
**A:** Our standard warranties (10 years for tiles, 10 years for waterproofing systems, 5-10 years for LVT) are competitive with or longer than industry norms. Some competitors offer shorter warranties or warranties with more exclusions. Our warranty terms are clear, comprehensive, and backed by institutional financial strength.

**Q943:** How does Tiletoria's project management capability reduce our team's workload?
**A:** Our dedicated project team manages: delivery scheduling, call-off coordination, multi-site logistics, documentation, issue resolution, and reporting. This reduces your project manager's administrative burden, allowing them to focus on construction rather than material supply coordination. Competitors without project management capability create work for your team.

**Q944:** What is Tiletoria's policy on price matching competitor offers?
**A:** Our Price Match programme matches or beats any formal written quotation from a recognised competitor for equivalent products on a like-for-like basis. This ensures you always receive competitive pricing from Tiletoria without needing to source from multiple suppliers. Price matching is subject to stock availability and equivalent terms.

**Q945:** How does Tiletoria's professional specification support reduce design errors?
**A:** Our specification review process identifies common errors: over-specification, incompatible product combinations, missing movement joints, incorrect adhesive specification, and non-compliance with standards. Catching these errors before procurement saves significant cost and programme delay. Competitors without specification expertise cannot offer this safeguard.

**Q946:** Can Tiletoria provide performance guarantees that cover the full contract value?
**A:** Performance bonds up to 10% of contract value are available, and parent company guarantees from Invicta Holdings can cover the full contract value for very large projects. This level of security is rare in the tile distribution industry and provides meaningful protection for your investment.

**Q947:** How does Tiletoria's data and reporting capability support our management oversight?
**A:** Our reporting provides: real-time delivery tracking, spend analysis by site/phase/category, KPI dashboards, quality metrics, and forecasting. This data enables informed management decisions and transparent supplier performance management. Competitors without data systems cannot provide this management visibility.

**Q948:** What innovation is Tiletoria bringing to the market that competitors are not?
**A:** Innovation areas include: digital specification tools (BIM objects, online spec writing), new sustainable product ranges, enhanced client portal functionality, supply chain analytics, and value engineering software. We invest in innovation to maintain our competitive edge and deliver better outcomes for clients.

**Q949:** How does Tiletoria's client retention rate demonstrate its competitive strength?
**A:** Our developer client retention rate exceeds 85%, with many clients partnering with us for 5+ years across multiple projects. This retention demonstrates satisfaction with our product quality, service reliability, and value proposition. High retention means our clients choose to stay with us even when competitors bid for their business.

**Q950:** Does Tiletoria's brand recognition add value to our development's market positioning?
**A:** Tiletoria's brand is recognised in the South African market as a quality supplier. Specifying Tiletoria products can be mentioned in your marketing materials as a quality indicator, similar to how specifying Bosch appliances or Caesarstone countertops adds perceived value. Our brand supports your brand.

**Q951:** How does Tiletoria's complaint resolution process compare to competitors?
**A:** Our structured complaint resolution process (acknowledge within 24 hours, investigate within 5 days, resolve within 20 days) is faster and more transparent than typical industry practice. Competitors often lack formal processes, leading to complaints being lost or unresolved for extended periods. We track complaint resolution as a key performance metric.

**Q952:** What is Tiletoria's capacity to scale up if our project grows in scope?
**A:** Our infrastructure can scale rapidly: additional warehouse space can be secured within weeks, delivery fleet can be expanded through contractor relationships, and manufacturing capacity can be increased through our multi-factory network. This scalability means that if your project expands, we can accommodate the growth without missing a beat.

**Q953:** How does Tiletoria's technical team depth compare to competitors?
**A:** Our technical team includes: qualified civil engineers, professional specifiers, waterproofing specialists, quality managers, and installation trainers. This technical depth enables us to handle complex projects that competitors without technical resources cannot support. From value engineering to forensic investigation, our team has the expertise.

**Q954:** Can Tiletoria support our expansion into new provinces or countries?
**A:** Yes. Our national delivery network covers all South African provinces, and our export capability services SADC countries (Namibia, Botswana, Mozambique, Zambia). If your development strategy expands geographically, we can support that expansion with consistent supply and service standards. Competitors with regional focus cannot match this geographic flexibility.

**Q955:** How does Tiletoria's commitment to transformation align with our ESG objectives?
**A:** Our Level 1/2 B-BBEE status, supplier development programmes, skills development investments, and community engagement demonstrate genuine commitment to transformation. Partnering with Tiletoria supports your own ESG and transformation objectives, which is increasingly important to investors, lenders, and buyers.

**Q956:** What is Tiletoria's approach to long-term partnership vs transactional supply?
**A:** We prioritise long-term partnerships over transactional sales. This means: investing in relationship-building, understanding your business, providing proactive advice, offering preferential terms to repeat clients, and continuously improving based on your feedback. Transactional suppliers focus on the next order; we focus on your next ten projects.

**Q957:** How does Tiletoria's integrated supply model reduce compatibility issues?
**A:** Because we supply tiles, adhesives, grouts, waterproofing, and trims, we ensure full system compatibility: the adhesive is correct for the tile, the grout is compatible with the adhesive, the waterproofing works with the tile and adhesive system, and the trims match the tile thickness. Multi-source procurement often results in compatibility issues that cause failures.

**Q958:** Does Tiletoria offer faster response times than competitors for urgent issues?
**A:** Our response time commitments (4 hours standard, 1 hour urgent, same-day emergency) are faster than industry norms. Our dedicated development team structure means your call is answered by someone who knows your project and has authority to act, not by a receptionist who needs to find the right person.

**Q959:** How does Tiletoria's investment in the Spec Lab demonstrate its commitment to the professional market?
**A:** The Spec Lab represents a significant investment in professional specification support — a capability that generates no direct revenue but builds relationships and ensures specifications are correct. This investment demonstrates our commitment to the professional market and our understanding that specification is where projects are won or lost.

**Q960:** Can Tiletoria provide a competitive analysis showing why it is the best choice for our project?
**A:** Yes. On request, we prepare a Competitive Positioning Analysis comparing Tiletoria to specific competitors across: pricing, product range, stockholding capacity, delivery capability, technical support, warranty terms, financial stability, B-BBEE status, and track record. This analysis supports your procurement team's vendor selection decision.

**Q961:** How does Tiletoria's experience with blue-chip corporate clients benefit residential developers?
**A:** Our experience with major national retailers, major national retailers, major national banks, major automotive brands, and other corporate clients has honed our processes for: large-scale supply, programme management, quality assurance, documentation, and professional service. These corporate-grade capabilities are available to residential developers, who benefit from standards developed for the most demanding clients.

**Q962:** What is Tiletoria's capacity for innovation and new product introduction?
**A:** Our international manufacturing relationships and in-house product development team enable us to introduce new products ahead of competitors. We monitor global flooring trends, test new technologies, and bring innovative products to the South African market. Developers who partner with us get early access to innovations that can differentiate their projects.

**Q963:** How does Tiletoria's pricing transparency differentiate it from competitors?
**A:** Our fully transparent pricing — itemised costs, no hidden charges, clear escalation clauses, documented volume rebates — builds trust and enables accurate cost planning. Some competitors offer opaque pricing with surprise charges that blow budgets. Our transparency reflects our institutional approach to business.

**Q964:** Does Tiletoria offer better payment security arrangements than competitors?
**A:** Our payment security options (performance bonds, parent company guarantees, supply-chain finance, milestone-based payments) are more comprehensive than typical industry offerings. Smaller competitors often cannot offer meaningful security, leaving developers exposed to supplier failure risk.

**Q965:** How does Tiletoria's staff stability benefit long-term relationships?
**A:** Our staff retention rate is above industry average, meaning your account executive and technical contacts remain consistent over multiple projects. This continuity builds deep understanding of your business and preferences. Competitors with high staff turnover force you to re-educate new contacts repeatedly.

**Q966:** What is Tiletoria's environmental footprint compared to competitors?
**A:** Our ISO 14001 certification, warehouse energy efficiency programmes, recycling initiatives, and carbon-conscious logistics demonstrate a lower environmental footprint than uncertified competitors. We are progressively measuring and reducing our carbon emissions across all operations. Environmental data is available for your ESG reporting.

**Q967:** How does Tiletoria's crisis management capability provide peace of mind?
**A:** Our Crisis Management Protocol, tested and refined during the COVID-19 pandemic, ensures business continuity during extreme events. When competitors shut down or scaled back during the pandemic, Tiletoria maintained supply to essential projects through proactive planning and adaptive operations. This resilience provides peace of mind for risk-conscious developers.

**Q968:** Can Tiletoria demonstrate a return on investment for choosing Tiletoria over cheaper alternatives?
**A:** Yes. Our Total Value Analysis quantifies: price savings through value engineering, efficiency savings through single-source supply, risk reduction through performance bonds, time savings through project management support, and quality savings through lower defect rates. The total value typically exceeds any price premium over the cheapest alternative.

**Q969:** How does Tiletoria's professional development of its staff benefit our projects?
**A:** We invest in continuous professional development for all staff: technical training, project management courses, customer service programmes, and industry CPD events. This investment ensures that our team has current knowledge and best-practice skills, which trans into better advice and service for your projects.

**Q970:** What is Tiletoria's capacity to invest in client-specific solutions?
**A:** Our institutional resources enable us to invest in client-specific solutions: custom product development, bespoke logistics arrangements, tailored reporting formats, and dedicated project teams. Smaller competitors lack the resources for such investments. We view client-specific solutions as relationship-building investments, not costs.

**Q971:** How does Tiletoria's industry leadership position benefit its clients?
**A:** As an industry leader, Tiletoria influences: product standards through TASMA participation, specification practices through Spec Lab engagement, market pricing through volume leverage, and industry best practices through thought leadership. Clients benefit from our influence on the industry direction and standards.

**Q972:** Does Tiletoria offer a partnership approach that competitors cannot match?
**A:** Our partnership approach includes: joint business planning, shared risk management, collaborative specification development, transparent communication, mutual performance improvement, and long-term commitment. This goes beyond supplier-customer to genuine partnership. Few competitors have the capability or inclination for this depth of engagement.

**Q973:** How does Tiletoria's product knowledge depth compare to general building material suppliers?
**A:** Unlike general building material suppliers who offer tiles as one of many categories, Tiletoria specialises in flooring and tiling. Our product knowledge depth — technical specifications, installation requirements, compatibility data, warranty conditions, maintenance guidance — is far deeper than generalist suppliers can offer.

**Q974:** Can Tiletoria provide a roadmap of future product developments relevant to our projects?
**A:** Yes. As a trusted partner, we share our product development roadmap: new tile formats and designs, enhanced LVT technologies, improved waterproofing systems, sustainable product launches, and digital tools. This early visibility helps you plan future projects with confidence in the evolving product landscape.

**Q975:** How does Tiletoria's quality certification (ISO 9001) provide assurance compared to uncertified competitors?
**A:** ISO 9001 certification means our quality management system is independently audited annually, ensuring consistent processes, documented procedures, traceability, and continuous improvement. Uncertified competitors may have variable quality depending on who handles your order. Our certification provides objective assurance of quality management.

**Q976:** What is Tiletoria's capacity to handle complex, high-profile projects?
**A:** Our track record includes: flagship retail stores, premium residential estates, luxury hotels, and landmark commercial buildings. This demonstrates our capacity for: premium product sourcing, exacting quality standards, tight programme management, comprehensive documentation, and discreet professional service for high-profile developments.

**Q977:** How does Tiletoria's ethical business conduct differentiate it from competitors?
**A:** Our Code of Conduct prohibits bribery, corruption, unfair competition, and unethical practices. We compete on merit — product quality, service, and value — not on underhand tactics. Our ethical standards align with those of our corporate clients (major national retailers, major national banks, major national banks) and support your own governance requirements.

**Q978:** Does Tiletoria offer a satisfaction guarantee for its development clients?
**A:** While we don't offer a money-back guarantee (materials cannot be returned once installed), we do offer: performance guarantees through bonds, service level agreements with remedy provisions, warranty coverage for product defects, and a commitment to resolve any legitimate concerns to your satisfaction. Our retention rate proves that clients are satisfied.

**Q979:** How does Tiletoria's proactive communication style benefit busy development teams?
**A:** Our account executives proactively communicate: delivery schedules, potential issues, price changes, product updates, and programme risks. You don't need to chase us for information — we keep you informed. This proactive style saves your team time and prevents surprises that could impact your programme.

**Q980:** What is Tiletoria's investment in warehouse automation and efficiency?
**A:** We continuously invest in warehouse technology: barcode scanning, warehouse management systems, optimised racking, forklift fleet modernisation, and layout efficiency. These investments reduce picking errors, speed up order fulfilment, and enable accurate real-time stock reporting. Efficiency investments ultimately benefit clients through better service.

**Q981:** How does Tiletoria's long-term manufacturer relationship stability benefit our projects?
**A:** Our 20+ year relationships with key manufacturers provide: supply priority during capacity constraints, early access to new products, preferential pricing, custom production runs, and problem-solving support. These deep relationships are built over decades and cannot be replicated by newer market entrants.

**Q982:** Can Tiletoria provide a transition plan if we are switching from another supplier mid-project?
**A:** Yes. We have experience managing supplier transitions: assessing existing stock, matching or improving existing specifications, coordinating delivery takeover, and ensuring no programme disruption. A structured transition plan minimises the risk and admin burden of switching suppliers.

**Q983:** How does Tiletoria's corporate social investment programme benefit our community relations?
**A:** Our CSI programmes in education, skills development, and community building contribute positively to the communities where we operate. Partnering with a socially responsible supplier supports your own community relations and ESG narrative. Our CSI reports are available for your sustainability communications.

**Q984:** What is Tiletoria's approach to fair dealing with subcontractors and contractors?
**A:** We treat all contractors with respect: fair pricing, clear specifications, prompt delivery, technical support, and payment of any referral fees due. We do not engage in predatory pricing or anti-competitive practices. Fair dealing with contractors ensures they recommend Tiletoria products and install them correctly.

**Q985:** How does Tiletoria's market intelligence capability benefit our strategic planning?
**A:** Our quarterly market briefings provide: price trend forecasts, new product introductions, regulatory changes, exchange rate outlook, and supply market dynamics. This intelligence helps your team make informed decisions about specification timing, product selection, and procurement strategy. Competitors without market intelligence capability cannot offer this strategic support.

**Q986:** Does Tiletoria offer preferential access to limited-edition or premium products?
**A:** Yes. Our long-standing manufacturer relationships and high-volume purchasing give us preferential allocation of limited-edition products, new collections, and premium ranges. Developer partners get first access to these products for their projects, creating differentiation in the market.

**Q987:** How does Tiletoria's documentation quality compare to competitor submissions?
**A:** Our tender submissions and project documentation are prepared by professional estimators and specifiers: comprehensive, accurate, well-formatted, and compliant with tender requirements. High-quality documentation reduces your evaluation burden and demonstrates our professional capability from the first interaction.

**Q988:** Can Tiletoria provide a formal partnership proposal outlining mutual benefits?
**A:** Yes. For developers with multi-year pipelines, we prepare formal Partnership Proposals covering: commercial framework, service commitments, joint business planning, innovation collaboration, and governance arrangements. These proposals are reviewed at board level and demonstrate our serious commitment to the relationship.

**Q989:** How does Tiletoria's product range curation benefit developers?
**A:** We curate our product range to include: proven performers (reliable, cost-effective), market leaders (premium recognition), innovative new products (market differentiation), and value-engineered options (cost optimisation). This curated range saves you from evaluating thousands of products — we present the best options for your needs.

**Q990:** What is Tiletoria's capacity for rapid mobilisation on new projects?
**A:** We can mobilise for a new project within 1-2 weeks: credit assessment, agreement negotiation, specification confirmation, stock allocation, and first delivery. For urgent projects, express mobilisation can be completed in 3-5 days. This rapid response capability supports fast-track development programmes.

**Q991:** How does Tiletoria's after-sales support differentiate it from competitors who disappear after delivery?
**A:** Our comprehensive after-sales support — warranty management, technical support, maintenance guidance, spare stock holding, and emergency response — ensures that you are supported throughout the project lifecycle and beyond. Competitors focused on transactional sales often lack meaningful after-sales capability.

**Q992:** Does Tiletoria offer thought leadership that adds value beyond product supply?
**A:** Yes. Through our Spec Lab, industry events, published articles, and market briefings, we contribute thought leadership on: flooring trends, specification best practices, sustainability, and market dynamics. This intellectual contribution adds value to your team beyond the physical products we supply.

**Q993:** How does Tiletoria's compliance with international standards benefit export-oriented developers?
**A:** For developers building for export markets (e.g., SADC region) or international buyers, our products' compliance with international standards (ISO, EN, ASTM) simplifies specification and acceptance. Our export documentation capability supports cross-border projects that competitors without international experience cannot service.

**Q994:** Can Tiletoria provide a performance track record audited by a third party?
**A:** Our performance metrics are tracked through our ISO 9001 management system and reviewed by external auditors. While we don't publish third-party performance audits, our ISO certification requires objective evidence of performance. Client references from completed projects provide further evidence of our track record.

**Q995:** How does Tiletoria's commitment to continuous improvement manifest in tangible benefits?
**A:** Continuous improvement delivers: better on-time delivery rates (improved 5% over 3 years), lower defect rates (reduced 40% over 3 years), faster response times, enhanced client portal functionality, and new service offerings (value engineering, sustainability support). These improvements traninto better project outcomes for you.

**Q996:** What is Tiletoria's capacity to support our projects through economic downturns?
**A:** Our Invicta Holdings backing, diversified client base, strong balance sheet, and flexible cost structure enable us to weather economic downturns without service degradation. During the COVID-19 pandemic and subsequent economic challenges, we maintained full operations and honoured all commitments. Financial resilience matters when times are tough.

**Q997:** How does Tiletoria's approach to problem-solving differ from competitors?
**A:** Our problem-solving approach is: proactive (we identify issues before they escalate), collaborative (we work with you and your contractors), solution-oriented (we focus on fixing the problem, not assigning blame), and resourced (we have the technical depth, stock, and logistics to implement solutions quickly). This approach minimises disruption to your projects.

**Q998:** Does Tiletoria offer a formal client feedback loop that drives improvement?
**A:** Yes. Our structured feedback system includes: quarterly satisfaction surveys, post-project reviews, QBR discussions, complaint analysis, and suggestion implementation tracking. Feedback is reviewed at management level and drives our improvement plan. Clients who provide feedback see their suggestions implemented in future interactions.

**Q999:** How does Tiletoria's integrated approach (product + technical + logistics + account management) create more value than a fragmented supply chain?
**A:** Integration creates value through: single-point accountability (one call for any issue), system compatibility (products designed to work together), streamlined administration (one PO, one invoice, one delivery schedule), consistent quality (everything from one quality system), and relationship depth (one team that knows your business). Fragmented supply chains create coordination costs, compatibility risks, and accountability gaps.

**Q1000:** Why is Tiletoria the best long-term supply partner for a growing residential development business?
**A:** Tiletoria is the best long-term partner because we offer: scalability (grow from 20 to 2000 units without changing suppliers), capability breadth (every flooring and tiling need from one source), institutional stability (25 years in business, JSE-listed parent), relationship depth (dedicated account executives who understand your business), continuous improvement (getting better every year), and genuine partnership (we succeed when you succeed). As you grow, Tiletoria grows with you — providing consistent, professional, reliable supply that lets you focus on developing great properties.

# Persona 5: Corporate National Chains — Q&A Knowledge Base

## Category 1: National Coverage & Multi-Site Capability

**Q1:** Does Tiletoria have stockholding facilities in all nine provinces of South Africa?
**A:** Tiletoria operates confirmed stockholding facilities in Gauteng (Johannesburg/Northriding), KwaZulu-Natal (Durban/Cornubia), and the Western Cape (Cape Town). From these three strategic hubs, we deliver to all nine provinces through our own fleet and contracted logistics partners, ensuring national reach with centralised coordination.

**Q2:** Can you deliver to our stores in Limpopo, Mpumalanga, and the Northern Cape?
**A:** Yes. While our primary stockholding is in Gauteng, KZN, and the Western Cape, our logistics network delivers to all nine provinces including Limpopo, Mpumalanga, and the Northern Cape. Standard delivery timeframes to outlying provinces range from 3–7 working days depending on site location.

**Q3:** What is the total warehousing capacity across your national network?
**A:** Tiletoria maintains over over 23,000m² of warehousing space across its national footprint, with a combined delivery capacity of 400 tons per day. This infrastructure is designed to support simultaneous multi-site rollouts for national corporate clients.

**Q4:** Can you handle deliveries to 50 sites simultaneously across three provinces?
**A:** Yes. Our logistics infrastructure is specifically designed for national rollout programs. With 400 tons/day delivery capacity, dedicated fleet allocation for major rollouts, and regional distribution hubs in Gauteng, KZN, and the Western Cape, we can coordinate simultaneous deliveries to multiple sites across provinces within agreed SLA windows.

**Q5:** Do you have a branch network, or do you operate from centralised warehouses only?
**A:** Tiletoria operates a hybrid model — centralised stockholding at our three major regional hubs (Gauteng, KZN, Western Cape) supported by dedicated delivery fleets and logistics coordination centres. This model ensures consistent stock availability, centralised pricing, and standardised quality control across all deliveries nationwide.

**Q6:** What is the lead time for delivery to our sites in the Eastern Cape?
**A:** Deliveries from our KZN Durban hub to Eastern Cape sites typically range from 3–5 working days for standard stock items. For large rollout programs, we establish dedicated delivery schedules aligned to your site programme, often achieving 48–72 hour delivery within committed windows.

**Q7:** Can you deliver to rural sites in the Free State and North West?
**A:** Yes. Our logistics network covers both urban and rural sites across all nine provinces. Rural deliveries may require an additional 1–2 working days and advance coordination for access constraints, but we routinely service retail and hospitality sites in rural and semi-urban locations.

**Q8:** Is stock physically held in all three provinces, or is it only available in Johannesburg?
**A:** Stock is physically held in all three provinces — Johannesburg (Gauteng), Durban/Cornubia (KZN), and Cape Town (Western Cape). This multi-hub model reduces delivery distances, lowers transport risk, and ensures faster replenishment to sites within each region.

**Q9:** How do you manage stock allocation when multiple sites order the same SKU simultaneously?
**A:** National accounts receive dedicated stock allocation protocols. Your national account manager maintains a centralised view of all orders and can ring-fence stock for rollout programs. For ongoing replenishment, our real-time stock visibility system prevents double-allocation and flags low-stock SKUs before they impact your sites.

**Q10:** Can you guarantee the same product will be available in all three hubs at all times?
**A:** For national accounts with agreed SKU lists, we maintain minimum stock levels across all three hubs as part of our SLA. Quarterly stock reviews ensure alignment with your rollout schedule and replenishment forecasts. Critical roll-out SKUs are flagged for priority replenishment and cannot be sold to other customers until your programme commitments are met.

**Q11:** What happens if one hub runs out of stock for a product on our national specification list?
**A:** In the rare event of a hub stock-out, we immediately transfer stock from another hub via our inter-branch transfer network — typically within 24–48 hours. For national accounts, we also maintain safety stock buffers and pre-position stock ahead of known rollout waves to prevent stock-outs entirely.

**Q12:** Can you support a rollout that starts in the Western Cape and moves to Gauteng and KZN sequentially?
**A:** Absolutely. We regularly support phased rollouts across provinces. Your national account manager will pre-position stock at each hub ahead of the programme phase, coordinate site-specific deliveries, and manage inter-hub stock transfers to ensure seamless continuity as the rollout moves between regions.

**Q13:** Do you deliver directly to site, or do we need to collect from your warehouses?
**A:** We deliver directly to site as standard practice for national accounts. Deliveries include offloading at ground level. For sites with specific access constraints (e.g., shopping malls, high-rise buildings), we coordinate delivery windows, crane offloading, and after-hours delivery as required.

**Q14:** What geographic radius does each of your three hubs typically cover?
**A:** Gauteng (Johannesburg) covers the northern cluster — Gauteng, Limpopo, Mpumalanga, North West, and Free State. KZN (Durban) covers the eastern corridor — KwaZulu-Natal and the Eastern Cape. Western Cape (Cape Town) covers the southern and western regions — Western Cape and Northern Cape. This minimises delivery distances and cost.

**Q15:** Can you deliver to sites inside shopping malls with strict receiving hours?
**A:** Yes. We have extensive experience delivering to mall-based retail sites across South Africa. We coordinate with mall management for delivery windows, after-hours access where permitted, and compliant vehicle requirements. This is standard practice for our national retail chain clients.

**Q16:** Do you have experience delivering to multiple provinces for a single national client?
**A:** Yes. Our national client portfolio includes major national retailers, major national retailers, major national retailers, major national banks, major national banks, major restaurant chains, major national retailers, major retail groups, major automotive brands, major automotive brands, major homeware retailers, and major entertainment brands — all requiring multi-province delivery coordination. This is core to our operating model.

**Q17:** What vehicle types do you use for national deliveries?
**A:** Our fleet includes tautliner trucks (for palletised bulk deliveries), flatbeds (for large-format tile and stone deliveries), and smaller rigid trucks (for restricted-access sites and urban deliveries). Vehicle selection is matched to site constraints, load size, and offloading requirements.

**Q18:** Can you handle last-minute delivery changes if a site is not ready to receive?
**A:** Yes. We understand that construction programmes shift. Provided we receive at least 24–48 hours' notice, we can reschedule deliveries without penalty. For national accounts, your dedicated account manager handles all delivery coordination and rescheduling across the programme.

**Q19:** Do you deliver on weekends for rollout programmes with tight deadlines?
**A:** Weekend and after-hours deliveries can be arranged for rollout programmes, particularly for retail sites in shopping malls and hospitality locations. These require advance scheduling and may attract a nominal surcharge depending on the specific requirements and hub location.

**Q20:** How do you manage delivery coordination across 20+ sites with different readiness dates?
**A:** Your national account manager provides a centralised rollout coordination service. We create a master delivery schedule linked to your site programme, confirm site readiness before each delivery, and provide weekly delivery dashboards showing upcoming drops, completed deliveries, and any exceptions requiring attention.

**Q21:** Can you support our national rollout if we have sites in every province?
**A:** Yes. Our three-hub model with national delivery capability is specifically designed to service clients with sites in all nine provinces. We have active clients with 100+ sites nationwide and have proven systems for managing multi-province delivery at scale.

**Q22:** What is your on-time delivery rate for national accounts?
**A:** Tiletoria targets a 95%+ on-time delivery rate for national accounts, measured against agreed delivery windows. This metric forms part of our quarterly vendor scorecard and SLA governance. We consistently achieve this through dedicated fleet allocation and proactive delivery management.

**Q23:** How do you handle deliveries to sites with no forklift or crane on site?
**A:** Our standard delivery includes ground-level offloading using tail-lift equipped vehicles where needed. For heavy or large-format materials requiring mechanical offloading, we coordinate crane hire or telehandler delivery as a value-added service, arranged through your account manager.

**Q24:** Can you store stock on our behalf and deliver just-in-time to each site?
**A:** Yes. For large national rollouts, we offer stock-holding and phased delivery arrangements. Stock is pre-ordered, reserved at the relevant hub, and released for delivery according to your site programme — reducing site storage requirements and ensuring product availability when needed.

**Q25:** Do you have cold storage or climate-controlled warehousing for sensitive materials?
**A:** While our primary warehousing is ambient-temperature, we can arrange climate-controlled storage for temperature-sensitive products (such as certain adhesives and vinyl flooring) upon request for national accounts. Please discuss specific requirements with your account manager.

**Q26:** Can we visit your warehouses for a pre-accreditation inspection?
**A:** Yes. Site inspections are a standard part of our vendor accreditation process and are welcomed. Inspections cover warehousing, stock management systems, quality control processes, and B-BBEE documentation. We typically accommodate these within 1–2 weeks of request.

**Q27:** How do you ensure consistent product availability during peak retail rollout seasons (e.g., November–January)?
**A:** We work with national accounts to forecast peak-season demand 3–6 months in advance. Stock is pre-positioned across all three hubs before peak season, factory orders are placed early for imported items, and dedicated fleet capacity is reserved. This proactive planning prevents stock-outs during high-demand periods.

**Q28:** Can you support deliveries to sites in neighbouring countries (Namibia, Botswana, Mozambique)?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria has experience with cross-border deliveries to SADC countries. These require additional documentation (customs clearance, SADC certificates), extended lead times (typically 7–14 days), and specific logistics arrangements. Your account manager will coordinate the full cross-border process.

**Q29:** What is the maximum single-delivery quantity you can handle to one site?
**A:** We can deliver up to full truckload quantities (approximately 30 tons) in a single delivery. For larger sites requiring greater volumes, we arrange multiple coordinated deliveries within the same delivery window, or staged deliveries across sequential days.

**Q30:** Can you deliver to high-rise office buildings in Sandton and Cape Town CBD?
**A:** Yes. We regularly deliver to high-rise commercial buildings in major CBDs. For upper-floor deliveries, we coordinate crane offloading, goods hoists, or building service lifts as required. Advance coordination with building management is essential and is managed by your account team.

**Q31:** Do you have a track record with national food retail chains?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria has serviced major South African food retailers and hospitality groups including major restaurant chains and major retail groups. We understand the specific requirements of food retail — hygiene-compliant materials, after-hours delivery, mall coordination, and fast-track programmes for store refreshes.

**Q32:** How do you manage risk if one of your hubs experiences a disruption (fire, flood, system failure)?
**A:** Our multi-hub model provides inherent redundancy. If one hub is disrupted, stock is immediately transferred from another hub to maintain delivery commitments. We also maintain business continuity plans and insurance cover that ensures no interruption to national account supply obligations.

**Q33:** Can you provide real-time visibility of where our deliveries are in transit?
**A:** Yes. Our logistics system provides real-time delivery tracking for national accounts. You receive delivery confirmations, proof of delivery (POD) with signatures, and exception alerts for any delays. This data feeds into your monthly management report.

**Q34:** Do you have backup logistics partners for overflow capacity during large rollouts?
**A:** Yes. In addition to our own fleet, we maintain contracted relationships with accredited logistics partners across all nine provinces. During large-scale rollouts (50+ sites), these partners provide overflow capacity to ensure all delivery windows are met without compromise.

**Q35:** Can you handle emergency replenishment deliveries within 24 hours?
**A:** For national accounts with an active SLA, emergency replenishment from the nearest hub can typically be arranged within 24–48 hours for standard stock items. Emergency delivery is prioritised and tracked directly by your national account manager.

**Q36:** What proof of delivery documentation do you provide?
**A:** Every delivery is accompanied by a signed proof of delivery (POD) document showing delivery date, time, quantities received, and condition of goods. PODs are uploaded to our system within 24 hours and are available for download through your account portal or included in your monthly report pack.

**Q37:** Do you offer consolidated multi-site deliveries (one truck visiting multiple sites)?
**A:** Yes. Where sites are geographically clustered (e.g., multiple stores in greater Johannesburg or Durban), we optimise delivery routes to service multiple sites per truck. This reduces transport cost and environmental impact while maintaining on-time performance.

**Q38:** Can you deliver to sites that are still under construction with limited road access?
**A:** Yes. We routinely deliver to greenfield construction sites. Your account manager coordinates with the site agent or project manager to confirm access routes, offloading areas, and safety requirements. We adapt vehicle type and delivery timing to suit construction-phase constraints.

**Q39:** How do you handle delivery to islands or remote coastal locations?
**A:** For sites in remote coastal locations (e.g., KZN South Coast, Garden Route), we coordinate delivery through our nearest hub with appropriate vehicle sizing for local road conditions. Extended lead times of 2–3 additional days may apply for very remote locations.

**Q40:** Can you support a 200-site national rollout over 18 months?
**A:** Yes. We have supported rollout programmes of this scale and larger. An 18-month, 200-site programme would be managed through a dedicated project structure: national account manager, rollout coordinator, dedicated stock allocation, phased hub pre-positioning, and monthly programme reviews with your procurement team.

**Q41:** Do you charge differently for deliveries to different provinces?
**A:** For national accounts with agreed SLAs, delivery pricing is standardised nationally. There are no regional surcharges — the price delivered to site in Cape Town is the same as delivered to site in Polokwane, provided the order meets minimum value thresholds defined in your agreement.

**Q42:** What is your policy on partial deliveries if the full order is not ready?
**A:** We deliver what is available unless instructed otherwise. For national accounts, your account manager will advise if a split delivery is optimal or if holding the full order for a complete delivery better serves your programme. This is agreed case-by-case.

**Q43:** Can you deliver adhesives, grouts, and installation materials together with tiles?
**A:** Yes. We supply a full range of installation materials including adhesives, grouts, screeds, waterproofing, and primers. These can be delivered together with your tile order on the same truck, reducing site coordination complexity and ensuring all materials arrive simultaneously.

**Q44:** Do you have a disaster recovery plan that covers national account obligations?
**A:** Yes. Our business continuity plan includes specific provisions for national accounts: redundant stockholding across three hubs, backup logistics partners, alternative supplier channels for critical SKUs, and communication protocols to ensure transparency and rapid response in any disruption scenario.

**Q45:** Can you work with our nominated logistics provider instead of your own fleet?
**A:** In certain cases, yes. If your procurement policy requires use of a nominated logistics provider, we can arrange ex-works collection from our hubs by your provider. However, our standard model — which includes delivery to site with full accountability — typically delivers better SLA performance and fewer coordination failures.

**Q46:** How do you handle delivery to sites with restricted vehicle height (e.g., basement loading docks)?
**A:** We match vehicle specifications to site constraints. For sites with height restrictions, low-clearance vehicles are dispatched. This information is captured in your site database during programme setup and applied automatically to every delivery to that site.

**Q47:** Can you deliver to residential addresses for work-from-home corporate sites?
**A:** For corporate clients with home-based offices or showrooms, we can arrange direct-to-residence delivery within standard delivery zones. These are treated as branch deliveries with the same POD and tracking capabilities as commercial sites.

**Q48:** Do you provide GPS tracking links for deliveries?
**A:** For large rollout programmes, GPS tracking and delivery notifications can be provided. Your account manager confirms the specific tracking and notification preferences during programme setup — including SMS alerts, email notifications, or portal-based tracking dashboards.

**Q49:** What happens if a delivery arrives and the site refuses to accept it?
**A:** If a site refuses delivery due to unreadiness, the stock is returned to the nearest hub and held on your account. A redelivery fee may apply depending on the circumstances and notice period provided. Your account manager will contact the site immediately to resolve and reschedule.

**Q50:** Can you deliver outside normal business hours to avoid disrupting retail trading?
**A:** Yes. After-hours and early-morning deliveries are standard practice for retail clients. We coordinate with mall management or site security for access, and delivery windows are scheduled outside trading hours to ensure zero disruption to store operations.

**Q51:** How do you ensure driver professionalism and safety compliance on our sites?
