**A:** All our drivers and delivery crews are trained in site safety protocols, PPE requirements, and professional conduct standards. Safety documentation, including driver certificates and vehicle roadworthiness, is available for vendor accreditation files upon request.

**Q52:** Can you provide a dedicated fleet or drivers for our rollout programme?
**A:** For large-scale national rollouts (typically 50+ sites), we can allocate dedicated fleet capacity and regular drivers who become familiar with your site requirements, receiving protocols, and site contacts. This improves consistency and reduces delivery-day coordination issues.

**Q53:** Do you have experience with drive-through restaurant sites?
**A:** Yes. We have supplied flooring to quick-service restaurant chains including major restaurant chains. We understand the specific requirements — rapid installation windows, slip-resistant specifications, grease-resistant materials, and coordination with tight refurbishment schedules.

**Q54:** Can you deliver to a site that only has a PO Box address?
**A:** No — we require a physical delivery address with site contact details for every delivery. Your account manager will work with your operations team to capture accurate site addresses, GPS coordinates where needed, and receiving contact details during programme setup.

**Q55:** What is your cut-off time for next-day delivery requests?
**A:** For standard stock items from the nearest hub, orders confirmed by 14:00 can typically be scheduled for next-business-day delivery. For national accounts, cut-off times and express delivery options are defined in the SLA. Rollout programme deliveries are scheduled weeks in advance.

**Q56:** How do you handle delivery during public holidays or load-shedding?
**A:** We plan around public holidays and load-shedding schedules. Deliveries are scheduled to avoid known public holidays, and backup power systems at our hubs ensure picking and dispatch continuity during load-shedding. Your account manager provides advance notice of any holiday-adjusted schedules.

**Q57:** Can you deliver loose-packed items (not palletised) if our site has no pallet disposal capability?
**A:** Yes. We can pack and deliver in loose cartons or crates where pallet disposal is impractical. Packing preferences are captured in your site profile and applied to all deliveries. We also offer pallet collection and return services on request.

**Q58:** Do you have weighbridges at your facilities?
**A:** Yes, our major hubs are equipped with calibrated weighbridges. Weight dockets are provided with deliveries where required for stock reconciliation or site acceptance procedures.

**Q59:** Can you support a rollout in the Western Cape while simultaneously supporting maintenance replenishment in Gauteng?
**A:** Absolutely. Our hub-based model allows us to run multiple programmes simultaneously — a new rollout in one region and ongoing replenishment in another — without cross-programme interference. Each programme has dedicated stock allocation and scheduling.

**Q60:** What is the smallest order value you will deliver nationally?
**A:** For national accounts with an active agreement, minimum order values are agreed in the SLA — typically R5,000–R15,000 per delivery depending on site location and frequency. Lower-value emergency orders can be accommodated through courier services for small items.

**Q61:** Do you provide site surveys before the first delivery to new locations?
**A:** For rollout programmes, we conduct pre-delivery site assessments for each new location to confirm access, offloading requirements, vehicle restrictions, and receiving contacts. This prevents delivery-day issues and is included in our rollout management service.

**Q62:** How do you manage delivery confirmation for sites without a receiving clerk?
**A:** We establish a nominated receiving protocol during programme setup — typically the site manager or contractor foreman signs the POD. Photo confirmation of delivery and condition is captured as backup documentation. Digital PODs with GPS timestamps are also available.

**Q63:** Can you accommodate sudden programme acceleration (e.g., all sites moved up by two weeks)?
**A:** We understand that corporate programmes can accelerate. With sufficient stock on hand or advance notice for factory orders, we can compress delivery schedules. Your national account manager will immediately assess stock availability and logistics capacity and propose an accelerated delivery plan.

**Q64:** Do you have preferred delivery windows for different site types?
**A:** Yes. Mall retail sites typically receive before 09:00 or after 18:00. Office sites typically receive between 08:00–16:00. Hospitality sites vary by operational hours. We capture and apply these preferences in your site database for every delivery.

**Q65:** Can you provide a map or dashboard showing all upcoming deliveries nationwide?
**A:** Yes. As part of our consolidated reporting for national accounts, we provide a delivery dashboard showing all planned deliveries for the coming week/month, by province, site, and status. This is updated daily and included in your monthly management report pack.

---

## Category 2: Vendor Accreditation & Onboarding

**Q66:** What B-BBEE level is Tiletoria, and is your certificate current?
**A:** Tiletoria holds Level 1 or 2 B-BBEE status with a current SANAS-accredited certificate. Our certificate is valid for 12 months from issue date and is renewed annually. A copy is provided as part of our standard vendor pack for corporate procurement teams.

**Q67:** Can you provide a full vendor accreditation pack for our procurement committee?
**A:** Yes. We provide a comprehensive, pre-prepared vendor accreditation pack including: B-BBEE certificate, tax clearance certificate (TCC), audited financial statements, company registration documents (CIPC), VAT registration, insurance certificates (public liability, goods in transit), banking details, and ISO 9001 certification.

**Q68:** How long does your vendor accreditation process typically take?
**A:** From receipt of a completed vendor application and all supporting documents, our accreditation review typically takes 2–4 weeks. Site inspections may add an additional 2–4 weeks depending on scheduling. We have successfully completed accreditation within 6 weeks for clients requiring fast-track onboarding.

**Q69:** Do you have tax clearance status with SARS?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria maintains a valid SARS tax clearance certificate (TCC) at all times. The TCC is refreshed every 6–12 months as required and is included in every vendor pack submitted to corporate clients.

**Q70:** What insurance cover does Tiletoria carry?
**A:** Tiletoria carries comprehensive business insurance including public liability cover, product liability insurance, goods-in-transit insurance, and warehouse/facilities insurance. Specific cover limits and certificates are provided in our vendor accreditation pack for your risk team's review.

**Q71:** Can you integrate with our e-procurement platform (SAP Ariba) as part of onboarding?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is experienced with SAP Ariba, Oracle Procurement Cloud, and other major e-procurement platforms. Integration typically runs parallel to vendor accreditation and can be completed within 4–6 weeks, including product catalogue setup, punch-out configuration, and testing.

**Q72:** Do you have ISO 9001 certification?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is ISO 9001 certified for quality management systems. The certificate is current and included in our vendor accreditation documentation. ISO certification ensures our processes — from order management to delivery and quality control — meet international quality standards.

**Q73:** What is your company registration number, and where are you registered?
**A:** Tiletoria is a registered South African company (Pty) Ltd, registered with CIPC. Full company registration details, including registration number, registered address, and director details, are provided in our vendor pack.

**Q74:** Can you provide audited financial statements for the last three years?
**A:** Yes. Audited annual financial statements for the most recent three-year period are available as part of our vendor accreditation pack. These are audited by a recognised firm of chartered accountants and demonstrate our financial stability and going concern status.

**Q75:** What is your VAT registration number?
**A:** Tiletoria is a registered VAT vendor with SARS. Our VAT registration number is included in all vendor documentation, tax invoices, and accreditation packs. All pricing to corporate clients is quoted VAT-exclusive unless otherwise specified.

**Q76:** Do you comply with the NHBRC (National Home Builders Registration Council) requirements?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria complies with NHBRC requirements where applicable, particularly for products supplied to residential and commercial building projects. Relevant compliance documentation and product warranties aligned to NHBRC standards are available upon request.

**Q77:** Are your products SANS (South African National Standard) accredited?
**A:** Yes. Our product ranges comply with applicable SANS standards including SANS 1449 (ceramic tiles), SANS 10107 (installation codes of practice), and other relevant standards for flooring and building materials. Test reports and compliance certificates are available for your technical team's review.

**Q78:** Can you complete our customised supplier questionnaire instead of using your standard vendor pack?
**A:** Yes. We are accustomed to completing client-specific supplier questionnaires as part of vendor onboarding. Your national account manager will coordinate completion of your questionnaire, working with our finance, compliance, and operations teams to ensure accurate, comprehensive responses.

**Q79:** How quickly can you respond to an RFP (Request for Proposal) for a national contract?
**A:** For national accounts, we typically respond to RFPs within 5–10 working days from receipt of the full RFP documentation. Complex multi-year, multi-site proposals may require 10–15 working days. We prioritise RFP responses for existing accredited vendors and fast-track prospects.

**Q80:** Do you have a Supplier Code of Conduct or Ethics Policy?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria operates under a formal Code of Conduct and Ethics Policy that covers anti-bribery, fair labour practices, environmental responsibility, and ethical business dealings. This document is available for review as part of our vendor accreditation pack.

**Q81:** Can you provide references from existing national chain clients?
**A:** Yes. Upon signing of a mutual non-disclosure agreement, we can facilitate reference calls with our existing national chain clients including representatives from retail, banking, automotive, and hospitality sectors. References cover delivery performance, product quality, and account management.

**Q82:** What is your annual turnover, and how many employees do you have?
**A:** Tiletoria is one of South Africa's largest tile distributors, with substantial annual turnover and a significant workforce across our national operations. Specific financial metrics are provided in our audited financial statements, available within our vendor accreditation pack under confidentiality.

**Q83:** Are there any pending litigation or regulatory actions against Tiletoria?
**A:** Tiletoria operates in full compliance with South African law. Any material legal or regulatory matters are disclosed transparently in our vendor accreditation process. Our company secretary provides a litigation status letter as standard practice for corporate vendor applications.

**Q84:** Can you sign our standard supplier agreement and NDA?
**A:** Yes. We routinely sign client-specific supplier agreements, service level agreements (SLAs), non-disclosure agreements (NDAs), and data processing agreements. These are reviewed by our legal team, and we aim to turn around fully executed agreements within 5–7 working days.

**Q85:** Do you have a health and safety policy and compliance record?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria maintains a comprehensive occupational health and safety policy compliant with the Occupational Health and Safety Act. Our safety record, incident statistics, and safety officer credentials are available for review as part of vendor accreditation, particularly for clients requiring site access by our teams.

**Q86:** What environmental certifications or sustainability credentials do you hold?
**A:** Tiletoria is committed to sustainable sourcing and environmental responsibility. We hold relevant environmental compliance documentation and can provide information on our suppliers' sustainability credentials (e.g., European Ecolabel, LEED-compliant products). Specific sustainability requirements can be discussed with your account manager.

**Q87:** Can you provide your banking details and credit references?
**A:** Verified banking details are included in our vendor pack for EFT payment setup. Credit references from major suppliers and financial institutions are available upon request for your credit team's review.

**Q88:** How do you handle data protection and POPIA compliance?
**A:** Tiletoria is fully compliant with the Protection of Personal Information Act (POPIA). We have a formal POPIA policy, appointed an Information Officer, and implement appropriate technical and organisational measures to protect personal data shared during the vendor-client relationship.

**Q89:** Do you have a disciplinary and grievance procedure for employees?
**A:** Yes. As a responsible employer, Tiletoria has documented disciplinary and grievance procedures compliant with South African labour law. These policies support our commitment to fair labour practices and are available for review by clients with ethical sourcing requirements.

**Q90:** Can your CEO or Managing Director attend our procurement committee presentation?
**A:** Yes. For significant national contract opportunities, our Managing Director or a senior executive is available to present to your procurement committee, answer questions, and demonstrate our organisational commitment to the partnership. Presentations are arranged through your account manager.

**Q91:** What percentage of your management team is black South African?
**A:** Tiletoria, as part of the Invicta Holdings group with 75% ownership, maintains strong black representation at management and board level. Specific B-BBEE scorecard details — including management control, skills development, and enterprise development — are provided in our verified B-BBEE certificate.

**Q92:** Can you provide your EE (Employment Equity) report?
**A:** Yes. Our Employment Equity report and plan are available for review as part of our comprehensive vendor documentation. This demonstrates our commitment to workplace transformation and equitable employment practices.

**Q93:** Do you subcontract any part of your service, and if so, how do you manage subcontractor compliance?
**A:** Certain specialist services (e.g., large-format stone installation, waterproofing contracting) may involve accredited subcontractors. All subcontractors are vetted for B-BBEE compliance, insurance, and technical competency, and remain under our contractual accountability. We disclose subcontractor arrangements transparently in our vendor pack.

**Q94:** What is your process for handling confidential information shared during the accreditation process?
**A:** All information shared during vendor accreditation is treated as strictly confidential. We sign NDAs where required, limit access to accredited personnel only, and store documents securely. Our POPIA compliance framework governs all data handling practices.

**Q95:** Can you provide proof of payment to SARS (PAYE, UIF, SDL, WCA)?
**A:** Yes. Proof of compliance with all SARS statutory obligations — including PAYE, UIF, SDL, and Workmen's Compensation (WCA) — is available upon request for corporate clients requiring full statutory compliance verification.

**Q96:** Do you have a risk management policy and business continuity plan?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria has a formal risk management policy and business continuity plan covering operational, financial, and supply chain risks. The plan includes specific provisions for national accounts to ensure continuity of supply in disruption scenarios.

**Q97:** Can we conduct a site inspection at your Johannesburg warehouse before accrediting you?
**A:** Absolutely. Site inspections are welcomed and encouraged. We will arrange a guided inspection of our Johannesburg facility covering warehousing, stock management, quality control, fleet, and safety systems. Inspections typically take 2–3 hours and are scheduled at your convenience.

**Q98:** How long is your vendor accreditation valid once approved?
**A:** Most corporate clients review vendor accreditation annually or bi-annually. Tiletoria maintains all compliance documentation on a current basis, so renewal is typically a light-touch exercise involving updated certificates and financials. We proactively advise clients of any changes to our compliance status.

**Q99:** Can you provide a letter of good standing from the Department of Labour?
**A:** Yes. A letter of good standing from the Department of Labour (covering COIDA/WCA compliance) is available upon request and can be included in our vendor accreditation pack for clients requiring this specific documentation.

**Q100:** Do you have a social responsibility or CSI (Corporate Social Investment) programme?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria and Invicta Holdings are committed to corporate social responsibility. Our CSI initiatives focus on community development, education, and skills training. Details of our programmes are available for clients who prioritise supplier social investment in their procurement criteria.

**Q101:** Can you accommodate our 16-week vendor onboarding timeline?
**A:** Yes. A 16-week onboarding timeline is well within our capability. We will provide a detailed onboarding plan with milestones for document submission, site inspection, e-procurement integration, test transactions, and go-live. Your dedicated account manager tracks progress against each milestone.

**Q102:** What happens if our procurement committee has additional questions after reviewing your vendor pack?
**A:** Your national account manager will coordinate responses to any follow-up questions from your procurement committee, sourcing answers from our finance, compliance, legal, and operations teams as needed. We typically respond to follow-up queries within 48 hours.

**Q103:** Do you have experience onboarding as a vendor for government or parastatal entities?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria has experience with public sector procurement processes and understands the specific compliance requirements of government and parastatal entities, including Central Supplier Database (CSD) registration, Treasury regulations, and PFMA compliance where applicable.

**Q104:** Can you provide your procurement spend on black-owned suppliers as a percentage?
**A:** Our B-BBEE scorecard includes detailed procurement recognition levels for spend with black-owned and black women-owned enterprises. This data is included in our verified B-BBEE certificate and is relevant for clients tracking their own procurement scorecard contribution.

**Q105:** Are you registered on the Central Supplier Database (CSD)?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is registered on the South African Central Supplier Database (CSD) and maintains an active profile. Our CSD supplier number is available upon request for government and corporate clients requiring CSD verification.

**Q106:** Can you provide a certificate of incorporation and Memorandum of Incorporation (MOI)?
**A:** Yes. Our certificate of incorporation, Memorandum of Incorporation (MOI), and any amendments are available as part of our CIPC company registration documentation included in the vendor accreditation pack.

**Q107:** Do you have a formal quality policy signed by senior management?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria's quality policy is formally adopted and signed by our Managing Director. It commits the organisation to continuous quality improvement, customer satisfaction, and compliance with ISO 9001 standards. The policy is displayed at all facilities and available for client review.

**Q108:** Can your financial statements be shared under confidentiality?
**A:** Yes. Our audited financial statements are shared under standard confidentiality terms and are provided for the sole purpose of vendor accreditation and credit assessment. We request that these documents be handled in accordance with the confidentiality provisions of our supplier agreement.

**Q109:** How do we initiate the vendor accreditation process with Tiletoria?
**A:** Contact our corporate sales team via our website or call our head office. We will assign a national account manager who will guide you through the process, provide the complete vendor accreditation pack, and coordinate all documentation, site visits, and committee presentations required.

**Q110:** Do you charge any fees for vendor accreditation or onboarding?
**A:** No. There are no fees for vendor accreditation or standard onboarding. Costs associated with e-procurement platform integration (if any) are discussed transparently during the integration planning phase and are typically absorbed by Tiletoria for significant national contracts.

**Q111:** Can you provide a single point of contact for all accreditation-related queries?
**A:** Yes. From your first enquiry, a dedicated national account manager is assigned as your single point of contact for all vendor accreditation matters. This person coordinates internally across finance, compliance, legal, and operations to ensure seamless, responsive communication throughout the process.

**Q112:** What makes Tiletoria different from other flooring suppliers in vendor onboarding?
**A:** Tiletoria differentiates through: a pre-prepared, comprehensive vendor pack that accelerates accreditation; proven experience with 6–16 week corporate onboarding timelines; dedicated national account management from day one; and a track record of successful onboarding with major national chains. We understand the process and make it as efficient as possible for your procurement team.

**Q113:** Can you provide your organisational structure chart?
**A:** Yes. A high-level organisational structure chart showing our executive management, national account management, regional operations, and support functions is available for corporate clients under confidentiality. This provides visibility into our decision-making hierarchy and escalation paths.

**Q114:** Do you have a fraud prevention and anti-corruption policy?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria has a zero-tolerance policy on fraud and corruption. Our anti-corruption policy, aligned with the Prevention and Combating of Corrupt Activities Act, is available for review as part of our ethics and governance documentation.

**Q115:** Can you provide details of your company ownership structure?
**A:** Tiletoria is 75% owned by Invicta Holdings, a listed entity with significant black ownership and strong transformation credentials. Detailed ownership structure and beneficial ownership information is provided in our B-BBEE certificate and CIPC disclosure documents.

**Q116:** How do you manage conflicts of interest with clients?
**A:** Tiletoria has a formal conflicts of interest policy requiring disclosure of any actual or potential conflicts. All client-facing staff are trained to identify and escalate conflicts. Transparency is maintained throughout the relationship, and any conflicts are disclosed and managed in accordance with our policy and client requirements.

**Q117:** Can you provide product liability insurance certificates?
**A:** Yes. Product liability insurance certificates are included in our vendor accreditation pack. Coverage limits are reviewed annually to ensure adequacy for national-scale supply contracts. Specific coverage requirements can be discussed and accommodated where needed.

**Q118:** Do you have a formal dispute resolution procedure?
**A:** Yes. Our standard supplier agreement includes a formal dispute resolution procedure that escalates from operational resolution (account manager level) to senior management negotiation, and ultimately to mediation or arbitration if required. We are committed to resolving disputes fairly and efficiently.

**Q119:** Can you accommodate our sustainability and green procurement requirements?
**A:** Yes. We can provide product ranges that meet green building standards (e.g., LEED, Green Star SA), source sustainably manufactured products, and provide environmental product declarations (EPDs) where available. Discuss your specific sustainability criteria with your account manager.

**Q120:** What is the best way to fast-track our vendor accreditation with Tiletoria?
**A:** To fast-track accreditation: (1) appoint a single internal coordinator, (2) provide your complete vendor requirement list upfront, (3) schedule the site inspection early, (4) return signed agreements promptly, and (5) share your e-procurement integration requirements at the outset. Your account manager will then create a parallel-track onboarding plan to compress the timeline.

**Q121:** Can you provide a declaration of interest form for our committee members?
**A:** Yes. We can complete your standard declaration of interest form, confirming that no Tiletora directors or employees have a personal or financial interest that could conflict with your procurement process. This is standard practice for public sector and corporate procurement.

**Q122:** Do you have industry association memberships?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is a member of relevant industry associations including the Tile Association of South Africa and other building materials trade bodies. Membership certificates and affiliations are available for inclusion in our vendor pack where relevant.

**Q123:** Can you accommodate specific packaging or labelling requirements for our brand?
**A:** Yes. For national accounts, we can accommodate custom labelling, pallet branding, and packaging requirements subject to volume commitments and setup costs. These requirements are documented in the SLA and applied consistently across all deliveries nationwide.

**Q124:** Do you have a performance guarantee or bond facility for large contracts?
**A:** For significant national contracts, performance guarantees or bank guarantees can be arranged through our banking facilities. The requirement and terms are discussed during contract negotiation and documented in the agreement. This provides financial assurance of our performance obligations.

**Q125:** Can you provide a list of your top suppliers and their countries of origin?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria imports from Italy, Spain, France, Turkey, Brazil, India, and China. A list of key supplier partners and countries of origin is available under confidentiality. This demonstrates supply chain resilience and sourcing diversity for clients requiring this information.

**Q126:** How do you ensure ethical sourcing from international suppliers?
**A:** We conduct due diligence on all international suppliers, selecting partners with recognised quality certifications, ethical labour practices, and environmental compliance. Where available, we prioritise suppliers with ISO certification, CE marking, and sustainability credentials. Supplier audit summaries are available on request.

**Q127:** Can you provide your credit terms proposal as part of vendor onboarding?
**A:** Yes. Standard credit terms for national accounts are 30 days from statement, with 60-day terms available based on credit assessment and contract value. Extended terms are negotiable for multi-year agreements with volume commitments. Terms are formalised in the credit application and supplier agreement.

**Q128:** Do you have experience with vendor accreditation for the financial services sector?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is an accredited vendor to major South African banks including major national banks and major national banks. We understand the stringent compliance, risk management, and governance requirements of financial services procurement teams and are experienced in meeting these standards.

**Q129:** Can you provide your delivery KPIs and historical performance data?
**A:** Yes. As part of the vendor accreditation process, we provide historical delivery performance data including on-time delivery rates, invoice accuracy, quality acceptance rates, and customer satisfaction scores. These metrics form the baseline for SLA targets in your contract.

**Q130:** How do we know Tiletoria will still be in business throughout a multi-year contract?
**A:** Tiletoria's financial stability is demonstrated through three years of audited financial statements, our association with Invicta Holdings (a JSE-listed entity), and our 30-year track record since 1995. Our financial strength and market position provide confidence in our long-term viability as a national supply partner.


---

## Category 3: National Pricing & Contracts

**Q131:** Does Tiletoria offer national unit pricing with no regional surcharges?
**A:** Yes. National accounts receive a single, unified price list valid across all nine provinces. The price delivered to site in Cape Town is identical to the price delivered to site in Johannesburg or Durban. There are no regional surcharges, transport add-ons, or hidden costs — enabling accurate national budgeting.

**Q132:** What payment terms do you offer for national accounts?
**A:** Standard payment terms for national accounts are 30 days from statement date. For larger contracts with volume commitments, 60-day terms are available subject to credit approval. Extended terms (60+ days) are negotiable for multi-year agreements with committed spend thresholds.

**Q133:** Are your prices CPI-linked for multi-year contracts?
**A:** Yes. Multi-year national contracts typically include an annual CPI-linked price adjustment mechanism, applied on the contract anniversary date. The specific CPI index, cap (if any), and review timing are documented in the pricing schedule. This provides cost predictability while reflecting market conditions.

**Q134:** What volume commitment rebates are available?
**A:** Volume rebates are structured on a tiered basis — typically 1–3% for achieving agreed annual spend thresholds, with higher rebates for increased commitments. Rebate tiers and percentages are negotiated during contract finalisation and documented in the commercial schedule. Rebates are paid quarterly or annually as agreed.

**Q135:** Can we lock in pricing for 24 months?
**A:** Yes. Fixed 24-month pricing is available for national accounts with committed volume agreements. A nominal price premium may apply for the certainty of fixed pricing, or CPI adjustments may be capped at a maximum percentage per annum. Terms are documented in your supply agreement.

**Q136:** How do you handle price increases during a contract term?
**A:** Price increases during a fixed-term contract are managed through the agreed adjustment mechanism — typically annual CPI-linked adjustments on the contract anniversary. Any increases beyond the agreed mechanism require 90 days' written notice and mutual agreement, protecting your budget from unexpected changes.

**Q137:** What is the minimum contract value for national pricing terms?
**A:** National pricing terms are typically available for clients with projected annual spend of R500,000 or more. The specific threshold depends on product mix, site count, and service level requirements. Your account manager will propose pricing tiers matched to your anticipated spend profile.

**Q138:** Do you offer early payment discounts?
**A:** Yes. Early payment discounts may be available — typically 2.5% for payment within 7 days, 1.5% within 14 days. These are documented in the credit application and supplier agreement. For national accounts with strong payment history, preferential discount terms can be negotiated.

**Q139:** Can you structure pricing per site, or is it always central billing?
**A:** While central billing is standard for national accounts, we can provide site-specific cost centre breakdowns on your consolidated invoice. This allows you to allocate flooring costs to individual sites or regions for internal management reporting without sacrificing the efficiency of central billing.

**Q140:** What currencies do you invoice in?
**A:** All invoicing to South African corporate clients is in ZAR (South African Rand). For clients with cross-border sites in SADC countries, invoicing arrangements can be discussed — typically in ZAR with currency conversion at the prevailing exchange rate where applicable.

**Q141:** How do you handle pricing for project-specific vs. maintenance replenishment orders?
**A:** National pricing applies to both project/rollout orders and ongoing maintenance replenishment. Volume rebates are typically calculated on total annual spend across both order types, maximising your rebate achievement. Different delivery frequencies and minimum order values may apply.

**Q142:** Can we include installation services in the national pricing agreement?
**A:** Yes. Installation services can be included in the national agreement through our accredited installer network. Installation pricing is quoted on a scope-of-work basis (per m²) and can be standardised nationally or regionally depending on labour market conditions. This is documented in a separate works schedule.

**Q143:** What happens if our actual volumes are lower than the contracted commitment?
**A:** Volume commitments are typically structured as "best endeavour" rather than penalty-bearing. If volumes fall significantly short of projections, we review the contract at the annual anniversary and may adjust rebate tiers or pricing bands prospectively. We do not apply retrospective penalties for under-performance.

**Q144:** Do you offer price-match guarantees against competitors?
**A:** For like-for-like products and volumes, we will review competitive quotations and aim to match or improve upon pricing while maintaining our service levels and quality standards. Price-match requests are assessed on a case-by-case basis by your national account manager.

**Q145:** Can pricing be structured as a cost-plus model rather than fixed unit pricing?
**A:** Cost-plus pricing models can be discussed for very large, long-term contracts where transparency of cost structure is preferred. These require detailed agreement on cost components, margin caps, and audit rights. Your account manager can discuss this structure if it aligns with your procurement policy.

**Q146:** How do you handle pricing for discontinued products that are on our specification?
**A:** If a specified product is discontinued, we provide 90 days' advance notice and propose an equivalent or upgraded replacement at the same or similar price point. For national accounts, we prioritise product continuity and will negotiate special production runs or last-time buys where feasible to protect your specification.

**Q147:** Can you provide price estimates for budgeting purposes before contract signature?
**A:** Yes. We provide detailed budget estimates based on your site schedule, floor areas, and product preferences. These estimates include product, delivery, and installation costs with province-level breakdowns. Budget estimates are provided without obligation and typically within 5 working days of receiving your scope.

**Q148:** Do you charge separately for delivery, or is it included in the unit price?
**A:** For national accounts, delivery is included in the unit price (DDP — Delivered Duty Paid to site) subject to minimum order values per delivery. There are no separate delivery charges, fuel surcharges, or transport add-ons — simplifying your cost management and invoice reconciliation.

**Q149:** Can we negotiate exclusive pricing for certain product categories?
**A:** Preferred supplier status with exclusive or semi-exclusive pricing for specific product categories is available for national accounts with significant committed spend. This is documented in the supply agreement and may include category volume targets and performance criteria.

**Q150:** What is your policy on credit limits for national accounts?
**A:** Credit limits are established based on credit assessment of your audited financials, payment history, and projected monthly spend. Typical national account credit limits range from R500,000 to R5,000,000+. Limits are reviewed quarterly and can be adjusted to accommodate rollout spikes.

**Q151:** Do you offer retrospective volume rebates?
**A:** Yes. Volume rebates are calculated retrospectively on actual spend achieved against tiered thresholds. At the end of each rebate period (quarterly or annually), we calculate actual vs. threshold achievement and apply the appropriate rebate percentage. This ensures you benefit from total volume even if quarterly phasing is uneven.

**Q152:** How do you handle pricing for product samples and mock-ups?
**A:** Product samples for specification development are typically provided free of charge for national accounts. Large-format mock-ups or sample boards for flagship stores may be provided at cost or included in the project value depending on scale. Your account manager coordinates sample requests.

**Q153:** Can pricing be broken down by product category in the contract?

**Q154:** What happens if exchange rate movements significantly affect your import costs?
**A:** For imported products, contracts typically include a currency fluctuation clause that triggers review if the ZAR/EUR or ZAR/USD rate moves beyond an agreed threshold (e.g., ±10%) over a defined period. This protects both parties from extreme currency volatility while maintaining price stability within normal ranges.

**Q155:** Do you offer settlement discounts for annual prepayment?
**A:** For clients preferring annual prepayment or advance payment against a confirmed schedule, an additional settlement discount may be negotiated. This is assessed on a case-by-case basis and depends on the contract value, payment structure, and credit risk assessment.

**Q156:** Can we include penalty clauses for non-performance in the contract?
**A:** Yes. Performance penalties for material breaches of SLA — such as consistent late delivery, quality failures, or invoice errors — are standard in national contracts. Penalties are typically capped at 2–5% of monthly invoice value and are offset against rebate entitlements or credited to your account.

**Q157:** How do you handle pricing for bespoke or custom-manufactured products?
**A:** Custom or bespoke products are priced on a project-specific basis, incorporating mould costs, minimum order quantities (typically one container or full production run), and delivery lead times. Custom pricing is valid for the specific project and may include exclusivity arrangements for your brand.

**Q158:** Can we get the same pricing for our franchisees if they order directly?
**A:** Yes. Franchisees can be linked to your national pricing agreement and order directly against pre-agreed product lists and prices. Orders are tracked against your national account for rebate aggregation, and franchisees benefit from your negotiated rates without requiring individual credit applications.

**Q159:** Do you offer trial pricing for a pilot programme before full commitment?
**A:** Yes. Pilot programme pricing is available at the full national account rate structure from day one — there is no penalty for starting with a pilot. If the pilot is successful and expands to a full rollout, rebate tiers are applied retrospectively to the pilot spend if it falls within the same financial year.

**Q160:** Can we structure the contract with break clauses or annual renewal options?
**A:** Yes. Contracts can be structured with annual review points, break clauses with agreed notice periods (typically 3–6 months), and renewal options. This provides flexibility while ensuring continuity of supply. Your legal team and our commercial team will agree the optimal structure for your requirements.

**Q161:** How do you handle pricing for adhesive and installation materials relative to tile prices?
**A:** Installation materials are priced as a separate schedule but benefit from the same national pricing and rebate structure. Adhesive coverage rates are specified (e.g., kg per m²), allowing accurate project costing. Installation material pricing is also locked for the contract term alongside tile pricing.

**Q162:** Can you provide a total cost of ownership (TCO) analysis rather than just unit pricing?
**A:** Yes. For national accounts, we provide a total cost of ownership analysis incorporating: product cost, delivery, installation, anticipated maintenance, warranty value, and lifecycle replacement costs. This TCO approach supports strategic procurement decisions beyond simple unit price comparison.

**Q163:** What is your policy on price lists being shared with our franchise network?
**A:** Your national price list can be distributed to authorised franchisees through a secure portal or your e-procurement platform. Access is controlled, and pricing is marked as confidential. Franchisees order directly against the list, and all orders aggregate to your national rebate calculation.

**Q164:** Do you offer consignment stock arrangements?
**A:** Consignment stock arrangements can be discussed for very large national accounts with predictable consumption patterns. Under consignment, stock is held at a nominated hub or your central warehouse and invoiced only upon withdrawal. Specific terms, stock ownership, and reconciliation procedures are documented in the agreement.

**Q165:** How do you handle pricing for stock clearance or end-of-line products?
**A:** End-of-line and clearance stock is offered to national accounts at discounted prices before general release. These offers are communicated through your account manager and can provide significant savings for non-critical maintenance or secondary-space applications while maintaining brand-standard quality.

---

## Category 4: Standardised Specifications

**Q166:** Can Tiletoria provide a standardised SKU list for our national rollout?
**A:** Yes. We develop a locked SKU list — specific product codes, descriptions, colour references, and size specifications — that apply across every site in your network. This ensures brand consistency, simplifies ordering, and enables volume aggregation for better pricing and stock planning.

**Q167:** How do you ensure the same product is supplied to every site regardless of which province orders it?
**A:** Every product on your national specification is assigned a unique SKU code linked to a specific factory batch standard. Orders from any province pull from the same product master, ensuring identical colour, size, texture, and quality — regardless of which hub fulfils the order.

**Q168:** Can you lock in a specification so no substitutions are made without approval?
**A:** Yes. Your national specification is locked in our system. Any proposed substitution — even for an "equivalent" product — requires formal written approval from your nominated specification authority (typically the national procurement or design manager). Substitutions cannot proceed without signed authorisation.

**Q169:** Can you provide digital product files (images, textures, BIM objects) for our specification library?
**A:** Yes. Through Spec Lab (Spec Lab), we provide high-resolution product images, material textures, BIM objects, CAD files, and specification datasheets for every product on your national list. These integrate into your specification library, design guidelines, and e-procurement catalogue.

**Q170:** What happens if a specified product is discontinued by the manufacturer?
**A:** We provide 90–180 days' advance notice of any discontinuation and propose a closest-match alternative with physical samples for approval. For national accounts, we may negotiate last-time buys or extended production runs to cover your rollout programme before the product is phased out.

**Q171:** Can you provide colour consistency guarantees across different production batches?
**A:** Yes. We specify colour batch tolerances for every product on your national list. For large rollouts, we source from single production runs where feasible and conduct batch-to-batch colour comparison using calibrated spectrophotometers. Colour variation reports are available on request.

**Q172:** How do you manage laying pattern specifications for national consistency?
**A:** Laying patterns (e.g., straight, brick bond, herringbone, modular) are documented in your national specification pack and communicated to all installation teams. Installation guidelines are included with every delivery, and site inspections verify pattern compliance during rollout.

**Q173:** Can you develop a brand guideline document for flooring specifications?
**A:** Yes. Through Spec Lab, we develop comprehensive flooring brand guidelines covering: approved products, colour palettes, laying patterns, transition details, joint specifications, and maintenance protocols. This document becomes your internal standard for all new sites, refurbishments, and maintenance.

**Q174:** Do you provide samples of every specified product for our design team?
**A:** Yes. Full sample sets — including tile samples, LVT planks, stone samples, and grout colour swatches — are provided for your design and procurement teams. Large-format sample boards can be produced for flagship stores and design centres to guide franchisees and contractors.

**Q175:** Can you assign unique product codes that match our internal numbering system?
**A:** Yes. During e-procurement integration, we map our product codes to your internal codes to ensure seamless integration with your systems. This code mapping is tested during integration and documented in the product master file for ongoing accuracy.

**Q176:** How do you prevent a site from ordering a non-approved product?
**A:** On e-procurement platforms, only approved SKUs from your national specification are visible and orderable. For phone/email orders, our customer service team validates every order against your approved SKU list and flags non-compliant items for approval before processing.

**Q177:** Can specifications differ between flagship stores and standard outlets?
**A:** Yes. We support tiered specifications — e.g., a premium range for flagship and A-grade stores, and a standard range for B-grade and C-grade outlets. Both tiers are managed within the same national account structure with clear store-grade allocation rules.

**Q178:** Do you provide slip-resistance ratings (R-ratings) for all specified products?
**A:** Yes. All specified products include slip-resistance ratings in accordance with SANS standards and international test methods (e.g., DIN 51130 R-ratings for commercial applications). Wet-area and food-service specifications include mandatory non-slip requirements.

**Q179:** Can you provide maintenance and cleaning protocol documents for each specified product?
**A:** Yes. Product-specific maintenance and cleaning protocols are included in your national specification pack. These cover recommended cleaning chemicals, equipment, frequencies, and stain removal procedures — ensuring consistent care across all sites and maximising product lifespan.

**Q180:** How do you handle specification changes during an active rollout?
**A:** Specification changes during rollout are managed through a formal change control process: change request submission, impact assessment (cost/lead time/stock), approval by your authority, system update, and communication to all affected sites. Your account manager coordinates the entire process.

**Q181:** Can you provide wear ratings (PEI ratings) for all specified tiles?
**A:** Yes. All ceramic and porcelain tiles on your specification carry PEI (Porcelain Enamel Institute) wear ratings from I to V. Commercial and high-traffic areas are specified with PEI IV or V ratings. These ratings are documented in the technical datasheets for every product.

**Q182:** Do you provide grout colour specifications as part of the national standard?
**A:** Yes. Grout colour is specified as part of the national standard — typically matching or contrasting with the tile colour as defined in your brand guidelines. Approved grout colours, joint widths, and grout types (cementitious vs. epoxy) are locked in the specification and orderable through the national account.

**Q183:** Can you provide large-format samples (full-size tiles) for our flagship design?
**A:** Yes. Full-size samples (e.g., 600×600mm, 800×800mm, 1200×600mm format tiles) can be provided for flagship store design development. These are supplied on loan or at cost depending on quantity and are essential for accurate colour and scale assessment in design studios.

**Q184:** How do you ensure that new products added to the range match the existing specification aesthetic?
**A:** Any new product proposed for your national specification is assessed against the existing aesthetic palette, including colour, texture, gloss level, and format compatibility. Physical samples are submitted for your design team's approval before inclusion in the specification.

**Q185:** Can you provide a specification manual for our contractors and project managers?
**A:** Yes. We produce a contractor specification manual covering: approved products, installation standards (SANS 10107), substrate preparation, waterproofing requirements, expansion joint details, and quality acceptance criteria. This manual is distributed to all contractors working on your sites.

**Q186:** Do you provide water absorption ratings for all specified tiles?
**A:** Yes. All ceramic and porcelain tiles include water absorption ratings as classified by ISO 13006 (Group BIII, BIIa, BIIb, BIa, BIb). Commercial specifications typically use low-absorption (≤0.5%) porcelain for durability. Ratings are included in all technical datasheets.

**Q187:** Can specifications be updated annually without disrupting active sites?
**A:** Yes. Annual specification updates are standard practice. New specifications apply to new sites and refurbishments from an agreed effective date. Active sites and orders in progress continue on the existing specification to avoid disruption. Transition planning is coordinated by your account manager.

**Q188:** How do you manage rectified vs. non-rectified tile specifications?
**A:** Rectified tiles (with precision-cut edges for minimal grout joints) vs. non-rectified tiles are specified based on the application and aesthetic requirement. Rectified tiles are typically specified for premium retail flagships; non-rectified for standard stores. Joint width implications are documented in the specification.

**Q189:** Can you provide technical datasheets for every product on our specification?
**A:** Yes. Every specified product has a comprehensive technical datasheet covering: dimensions, thickness, weight, water absorption, breaking strength, abrasion resistance, slip resistance, chemical resistance, thermal properties, and installation recommendations. These are available digitally through Spec Lab.

**Q190:** Do you provide mock-up panels for our design team to approve before rollout?
**A:** Yes. Full-size mock-up panels showing the finished floor — including tile, grout, joint pattern, and transition details — can be produced for your design team's approval. Mock-ups are particularly valuable for flagship stores and new store prototypes before national rollout commences.

**Q191:** Can you match a specific colour from our brand palette?
**A:** For large-volume national contracts, we can work with manufacturers to develop custom colourways that match your brand palette. Minimum order quantities apply (typically a full production batch or container). Your account manager will coordinate colour matching through our supplier network.


**Q193:** Can we have different specifications for customer areas vs. back-of-house areas?
**A:** Yes. We routinely develop dual or triple specifications for different areas within the same store — e.g., premium porcelain for customer-facing areas, durable commercial-grade tiles for back-of-house, and slip-resistant tiles for wet areas. All are managed within your single national account.

**Q194:** Do you provide warranty documentation for specified products?
**A:** Yes. Every specified product carries manufacturer warranty documentation. Warranties cover manufacturing defects and, where applicable, wear performance. Warranty terms, conditions, and claim procedures are compiled in a warranty manual for your national account and provided to every site.

**Q195:** How do you ensure compliance with SANS 1449 for all specified tiles?
**A:** All tiles on your national specification are verified as compliant with SANS 1449 (Ceramic Tiles — Definition, Classification, Characteristics, and Marking). Compliance certificates and test reports from accredited laboratories are maintained on file and available for your technical team's review.

---

## Category 5: E-Procurement Integration

**Q196:** Can Tiletoria integrate with SAP Ariba for electronic procurement?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria has proven integration capability with SAP Ariba, including punch-out catalogues, electronic purchase orders (PO), electronic invoicing (e-invoicing), and automated goods receipt matching. Integration follows SAP Ariba standard protocols and is typically completed within 4–6 weeks.

**Q197:** Do you support Oracle Procurement Cloud integration?
**A:** Yes. We support integration with Oracle Procurement Cloud and other major e-procurement platforms. Our technical team works with your procurement systems team to establish product catalogues, approval workflows, purchase order routing, and invoice processing through your Oracle environment.

**Q198:** What e-procurement integration capabilities do you offer?
**A:** Our e-procurement integration includes: punch-out catalogues (users browse our product range within your system), cXML/OMS order processing, electronic PO acceptance, ASN (Advanced Shipping Notice) generation, electronic invoicing, real-time stock availability feeds, and automated goods receipt reconciliation.

**Q199:** How does the punch-out catalogue work for our users?
**A:** When a site user selects Tiletoria from your e-procurement system, they are "punched out" to our catalogue within your system interface. They browse approved products, select items, and the cart is returned to your system for approval workflow and PO generation. Only your national-specification SKUs are visible.

**Q200:** Can you map your product codes to our internal product codes?
**A:** Yes. Product code mapping is a standard part of e-procurement integration. We create a master mapping file linking our SKU codes to your internal product codes, description formats, and category structures. This mapping is tested thoroughly before go-live and maintained throughout the contract.

**Q201:** How long does e-procurement integration typically take?
**A:** E-procurement integration typically takes 4–6 weeks from kick-off to go-live, assuming both technical teams are responsive. The timeline includes: discovery, catalogue setup, code mapping, system configuration, testing (UAT), and hypercare support. Complex integrations with custom workflows may take 6–8 weeks.

**Q202:** Do you support cXML for order transmission and invoicing?
**A:** Yes. We support cXML (Commerce eXtensible Markup Language) for purchase order receipt, order confirmation, advance shipping notices (ASN), and invoice transmission. cXML is the industry standard for B2B e-procurement and ensures seamless, error-free data exchange between our systems and yours.

**Q203:** Can your system provide real-time stock availability to our procurement platform?
**A:** Yes. Real-time stock availability feeds can be integrated into your e-procurement platform, showing current stock levels at each hub for every approved SKU. This prevents orders being placed for out-of-stock items and enables informed procurement decisions at site level.

**Q204:** What happens if our e-procurement system goes down? Can we still order?
**A:** Yes. We maintain manual ordering channels (phone/email) as backup for e-procurement outages. Your national account manager and customer service team are available to accept orders manually and process them through the standard fulfilment process until your system is restored.

**Q205:** Can we use your catalogue for spot-buying as well as contract purchasing?
**A:** Yes. Your national account catalogue can support both contract purchasing (pre-approved SKUs at contracted prices) and spot-buying (non-contract items requiring approval workflow). Both order types aggregate to your national spend for rebate calculation.

**Q206:** Do you provide electronic Advance Shipping Notices (ASN)?
**A:** Yes. Electronic ASNs are sent automatically when orders are dispatched, confirming: delivery date, vehicle details, quantities, and tracking references. ASNs integrate with your goods receipt process, enabling three-way matching (PO–GRN–Invoice) for automated payment processing.

**Q207:** Can your invoices be processed automatically through our AP system?
**A:** Yes. Our electronic invoices are formatted for automatic processing through your accounts payable system. Invoice data includes: PO reference, line-item detail, site code breakdown, VAT calculation, and banking details. Three-way matching against PO and GRN enables touchless payment processing.

**Q208:** How do you handle order errors or cancellations in the e-procurement system?
**A:** Order errors or cancellations are handled through your e-procurement system's standard workflow or by contacting your account manager directly. If the order has not yet been picked, cancellation is straightforward. If already dispatched, standard returns procedures apply. Your account manager provides same-day resolution.

**Q209:** Can you integrate with custom-developed e-procurement systems, not just SAP or Oracle?
**A:** Yes. We can integrate with custom-developed or lesser-known e-procurement platforms using standard protocols (cXML, OCI, API, CSV/EDI). Our technical team assesses your system's capability during the discovery phase and proposes the optimal integration approach.

**Q210:** Do you provide a testing environment for UAT before go-live?
**A:** Yes. A full user acceptance testing (UAT) environment is provided before go-live. This includes: test catalogues, test orders, test ASN generation, and test invoicing. UAT scenarios are agreed in advance, and our technical team supports your users throughout testing to ensure smooth go-live.

**Q211:** What training do you provide for our users on the e-procurement system?
**A:** We provide user training materials (quick reference guides, video tutorials) and live training sessions (webinar or on-site) for your procurement and site teams. Training covers: catalogue navigation, placing orders, tracking deliveries, and managing returns. Refresher training is available on request.

**Q212:** Can the e-procurement catalogue be restricted by user role or site type?
**A:** Yes. Catalogue access can be configured by user role, site type, region, or cost centre. For example, flagship store users may see premium products that standard store users cannot. Approval workflows can also be set by order value, product category, or user seniority.

**Q213:** How do you handle e-procurement catalogue updates when specifications change?
**A:** Catalogue updates are managed through your account manager. When specifications change, we update the product master file, reflect changes in the e-procurement catalogue, and notify all users. Updates are tested in the UAT environment before deployment to production to prevent disruption.

**Q214:** Can we receive automated low-stock alerts through the e-procurement system?
**A:** Yes. Automated low-stock alerts can be configured to notify designated users when approved SKU stock levels fall below agreed thresholds. These alerts enable proactive reordering and prevent stock-outs at site level, particularly for maintenance replenishment items.

**Q215:** Is there a cost for e-procurement integration?
**A:** For national accounts with significant projected spend, e-procurement integration costs are typically absorbed by Tiletoria. For smaller accounts, a nominal setup fee may apply. This is discussed transparently during the commercial negotiation phase and documented in the agreement.

**Q216:** Can you provide a dedicated technical contact during integration?
**A:** Yes. A dedicated technical integration specialist is assigned to work alongside your systems team during the integration phase. This person coordinates all technical activities, manages testing, resolves issues, and ensures the integration is completed on time and to specification.

**Q217:** How do you ensure data security during e-procurement transactions?
**A:** All e-procurement transactions are transmitted over secure, encrypted connections (HTTPS/TLS). We comply with POPIA for data protection and can provide a data processing agreement if required. Our systems undergo regular security audits and penetration testing.

**Q218:** Can the e-procurement system handle multi-currency pricing?
**A:** For South African operations, pricing is in ZAR. If your system requires multi-currency display for regional operations, this can be configured with exchange rate feeds. Cross-border SADC deliveries are managed as separate pricing arrangements where required.

**Q219:** Do you support API integration for real-time data exchange?
**A:** Yes. RESTful APIs are available for real-time data exchange including: stock queries, price lookups, order placement, delivery tracking, and invoice retrieval. API documentation is provided during integration, and sandbox environments are available for development and testing.

**Q220:** What reporting is available through the e-procurement platform?
**A:** Reporting capabilities include: spend by site, spend by product category, order history, delivery performance, invoice status, and budget vs. actual analysis. Reports can be exported in standard formats (Excel, PDF, CSV) and scheduled for automatic distribution to your finance and procurement teams.

**Q221:** Can you handle complex approval workflows (e.g., site manager → regional → national)?
**A:** Yes. Complex, multi-level approval workflows are fully supported. Orders can be routed through your organisation's hierarchy — site manager approval, regional operations sign-off, national procurement authorisation — with value thresholds and product category rules applied at each level.

**Q222:** How do you manage returns through the e-procurement system?
**A:** Returns can be initiated through the e-procurement system or directly with your account manager. A return merchandise authorisation (RMA) is generated, collection is arranged, and credit notes are issued electronically. Return status is visible in the system and included in monthly reporting.

**Q223:** Can you provide a catalogue export for offline use or backup?
**A:** Yes. Your approved product catalogue can be exported in Excel or CSV format for offline reference, backup, or internal analysis. The export includes: product codes, descriptions, specifications, unit prices, and stock status. Exports are refreshed monthly or on request.

**Q224:** What happens if there is a system integration failure — who do we contact?
**A:** Integration issues are escalated directly to your dedicated technical integration specialist during business hours, and to our IT support desk after hours. For national accounts, your account manager also monitors integration health and proactively addresses any anomalies. SLA response times apply.

**Q225:** Can you integrate with our warehouse management system (WMS) for stock visibility?
**A:** Integration with your WMS can be explored depending on your system capabilities and requirements. Standard e-procurement integration covers stock visibility through the procurement platform. Direct WMS integration for consignment stock or vendor-managed inventory can be discussed for large accounts.


---

## Category 6: Centralised Billing & Reporting

**Q226:** Can Tiletoria provide a single monthly consolidated invoice for all our sites nationwide?
**A:** Yes. Centralised billing is our standard model for national accounts. Instead of receiving separate invoices per site or per order, you receive one consolidated monthly invoice covering all deliveries across all provinces. This significantly reduces your accounts payable processing workload.

**Q227:** Can the consolidated invoice be broken down by site code or cost centre?
**A:** Yes. The consolidated invoice includes detailed line-item breakdowns by site code, cost centre, province, or any other organisational hierarchy you require. Each line shows: site name, product, quantity, unit price, and delivery date — enabling accurate internal cost allocation while maintaining a single payment.

**Q228:** What format do you provide for the consolidated invoice?
**A:** Consolidated invoices are provided in PDF format with an accompanying Excel (CSV) data file for easy import into your ERP system. Electronic invoicing (EDI/cXML) is also available for integrated e-procurement platforms. Invoice formats are customised to your requirements during onboarding.

**Q229:** How quickly after month-end is the consolidated invoice issued?
**A:** Consolidated invoices are typically issued within 3–5 working days after month-end. For clients with specific month-end cut-off requirements (e.g., calendar month vs. 4-week cycle), we accommodate your preference and confirm the billing cycle in the SLA.

**Q230:** Can you provide spend analysis by province in your monthly report?
**A:** Yes. The monthly management report includes spend analysis by province, product category, site, and month-on-month trends. This analysis helps you identify spend patterns, regional variances, and budget compliance across your national footprint.

**Q231:** Do you provide variance analysis comparing actual spend to budget or forecast?
**A:** Yes. If you provide your budget or forecast data, we include variance analysis in the monthly report — showing actual vs. budgeted spend by category, province, and site. This variance analysis supports your financial reporting and procurement planning processes.

**Q232:** Can you provide a dashboard showing all deliveries completed in the month?
**A:** Yes. The monthly delivery dashboard shows: total deliveries completed, on-time delivery percentage, deliveries by province, pending/back-ordered items, and any delivery exceptions or issues. This operational dashboard complements the financial invoice and is included in the monthly report pack.

**Q233:** How do you handle credit notes on the consolidated invoice?
**A:** Credit notes (for returns, price adjustments, or service failures) are listed as separate line items on the consolidated invoice or issued as standalone credit notes depending on your preference. Credit notes reference the original invoice and are clearly identified for reconciliation purposes.

**Q234:** Can we receive invoices weekly instead of monthly?
**A:** Yes. Billing frequency can be adjusted to weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly depending on your preference. Weekly billing is sometimes preferred by clients with high transaction volumes or specific cash flow management requirements. Your billing cycle is confirmed in the SLA.

**Q235:** Do you provide open order reports showing what is pending delivery?
**A:** Yes. The monthly report pack includes an open order report showing all orders placed but not yet delivered, with expected delivery dates and any delays. This provides forward visibility of pending deliveries and helps your ops team plan site readiness.

**Q236:** Can you report on stock availability for our specified SKUs across all hubs?
**A:** Yes. Monthly stock availability reports show current stock levels for all your specified SKUs at each of our three hubs (Gauteng, KZN, Western Cape). This visibility supports your procurement planning and flags any potential supply risks before they impact sites.

**Q237:** How do we reconcile the consolidated invoice with our internal records?
**A:** The consolidated invoice is supported by a detailed transaction file (Excel/CSV) with unique order references matching your PO numbers. This enables three-way matching against your purchase orders and goods received notes. Your account manager is available to assist with any reconciliation queries.

**Q238:** Can you provide historical spend data for trend analysis?
**A:** Yes. Upon request, we provide historical spend data from the inception of your account, formatted for trend analysis. This data can be exported to Excel or BI tools and includes: monthly spend, product mix evolution, site growth, and seasonal patterns.

**Q239:** Do you provide management reports in PowerPoint or PDF format?
**A:** Monthly management reports are provided in PDF format as standard. PowerPoint executive summaries can be produced for quarterly business reviews. The underlying data is provided in Excel for your own analysis, dashboarding, and board reporting.

**Q240:** Can the reports be customised to match our internal reporting templates?
**A:** Yes. Report formats can be customised to match your internal templates, terminology, and organisational structure during the onboarding phase. Your account manager works with your finance and procurement teams to ensure reports meet your internal requirements.

**Q241:** How do you handle disputed invoice line items?
**A:** Invoice disputes are resolved through your account manager within 48 hours of notification. While the dispute is being investigated, undisputed line items should be paid in accordance with agreed terms. Disputed amounts are either credited or substantiated with delivery documentation. Penalties do not apply to good-faith disputes.

**Q242:** Can you provide year-to-date (YTD) spend reports for budget reviews?
**A:** Yes. YTD spend reports are provided monthly, showing cumulative spend against annual budget or forecast. These reports include: spend by month, variance analysis, rebate accrual status, and projected full-year spend based on the current run rate and known pipeline.

**Q243:** Do you provide rebate statements showing our earned rebates?
**A:** Yes. Rebate statements are provided quarterly (or at the agreed rebate frequency) showing: actual spend by tier, rebate percentage earned, rebate amount accrued, rebate payments made, and carry-forward balance. This transparent reconciliation ensures you receive all rebate entitlements.

**Q244:** Can your reports show cost-per-square-metre by site or region?
**A:** Yes. With your floor area data, we can produce cost-per-square-metre analysis by site, region, or store grade. This normalised metric supports benchmarking between sites, identifying outliers, and improving specification cost-effectiveness.

**Q245:** Can we access reports through a self-service portal?
**A:** Yes. A secure client portal provides 24/7 access to invoices, delivery notes, PODs, stock reports, and spend dashboards. The portal can be integrated with your e-procurement platform or accessed independently. User access is controlled by your nominated administrator.

**Q246:** How do you handle VAT reporting on consolidated invoices?
**A:** Consolidated invoices clearly show VAT at the standard 15% rate, with VAT-exclusive and VAT-inclusive totals. The invoice structure complies with SARS VAT requirements. VAT invoices are issued in the name of the contracting entity as specified in your agreement.

**Q247:** Can you provide spend data categorised by general ledger (GL) codes?
**A:** Yes. If you provide your GL code structure, we can map every line item to the appropriate GL code on the invoice and in the data extract. This enables automatic GL allocation in your ERP system without manual recoding.

**Q248:** Do you provide delivery performance reports for SLA governance?
**A:** Yes. Monthly delivery performance reports show: on-time delivery percentage, average delivery lead time, delivery exceptions by reason, and trend analysis. This data feeds directly into the quarterly vendor scorecard review and supports SLA compliance monitoring.

**Q249:** Can you send reports automatically by email to our finance team?
**A:** Yes. Reports and invoices can be scheduled for automatic email distribution to your nominated recipients — finance, procurement, operations — on a defined schedule (e.g., monthly on the 3rd working day). Distribution lists are maintained by your account manager.

**Q250:** How do you handle multi-entity billing (different legal entities per region)?
**A:** Multi-entity billing is supported. If different sites are owned by different legal entities, we can split invoicing accordingly while still providing a consolidated management report across all entities. Each entity receives its own tax invoice for its respective deliveries.

**Q251:** Can you provide an accrual report for month-end accounting?
**A:** Yes. An accrual report showing goods delivered but not yet invoiced (and vice versa) is available on request for month-end accounting purposes. This supports accurate cut-off and accrual accounting in your financial statements.

**Q252:** Do you provide sustainability or green spend reporting?
**A:** Yes. If sustainability tracking is important to your procurement KPIs, we can categorise spend by environmental attributes — e.g., products with EPDs, recycled content, low-VOC emissions, or sustainable sourcing certifications. This supports your B-BBEE and sustainability scorecard reporting.

**Q253:** Can you integrate your reporting with our BI tool (e.g., Power BI, Tableau)?
**A:** Yes. Data exports are provided in standard formats compatible with BI tools. API access can also be arranged for real-time data feeds into Power BI, Tableau, or similar platforms. Your IT team and our technical integration specialist will establish the optimal connection.

**Q254:** How do you handle inter-company charging if our sites are managed by a property manager?
**A:** If a property management company manages deliveries on your behalf, we can invoice the property manager directly or structure pass-through invoicing as required. The arrangement is documented in the agreement, and all parties receive appropriate documentation.

**Q255:** Can you provide a forecast of upcoming invoices based on open orders?
**A:** Yes. A cash flow forecast showing expected invoice values by week/month based on open orders and scheduled deliveries is available on request. This supports your treasury planning and ensures no surprises in accounts payable.

**Q256:** Do your invoices include our PO numbers for automatic matching?
**A:** Yes. Every invoice line includes the corresponding purchase order number, enabling automatic three-way matching in your AP system. Where PO numbers are not provided at order, your site reference or cost centre code is used as the matching key.

**Q257:** Can we request ad-hoc reports outside the standard monthly pack?
**A:** Yes. Ad-hoc reports are available on request through your account manager. We aim to deliver ad-hoc reports within 48 hours of request. There is no charge for standard ad-hoc reports; complex analytical requests may be discussed case-by-case.

**Q258:** How do you ensure invoice accuracy — what checks are in place?
**A:** Invoice accuracy is managed through automated validation: order quantities vs. delivered quantities, contracted prices vs. invoiced prices, site codes vs. order records, and VAT calculations. Our invoice accuracy target is 99%+ for national accounts, and this metric is reported in the quarterly scorecard.

**Q259:** Can you provide site-level profitability analysis for our property portfolio?
**A:** With your revenue and floor area data, we can produce site-level cost analysis showing flooring spend as a percentage of site revenue or per square metre. This supports portfolio optimisation decisions and identifies high-cost sites for specification review.

**Q260:** Do you provide a year-end statement for audit purposes?
**A:** Yes. A comprehensive year-end statement summarising all transactions, invoices, credit notes, rebates, and payments is provided for audit purposes. This statement is prepared in consultation with your finance team to meet your audit requirements and timelines.

---

## Category 7: SLA & Performance Management

**Q261:** What delivery timeframe does Tiletoria commit to in the SLA?
**A:** Our standard SLA commits to 48–72 hour delivery from order confirmation for stock items from the nearest hub to sites within standard delivery zones. For rollout programme deliveries, specific windows are agreed per site and committed to in the master delivery schedule.

**Q262:** What happens if you fail to meet the delivery SLA?
**A:** Delivery failures are recorded, root-caused, and reported in the monthly delivery dashboard. Persistent failures trigger an escalation to senior management and may result in SLA penalties (typically 2–5% of affected invoice value) as documented in the supply agreement. Corrective actions are agreed and tracked.

**Q263:** What quality criteria are specified in the SLA?
**A:** SLA quality criteria include: product delivered matches specification 100%, batch colour consistency within agreed tolerances, correct quantities delivered, undamaged goods on arrival, and accurate documentation (POD, certificate of compliance where required). Quality acceptance is confirmed at each delivery.

**Q264:** What is your target for invoice accuracy?
**A:** Our invoice accuracy target for national accounts is 99%+. This is measured as the percentage of invoice lines that are error-free (correct product, correct price, correct quantity, correct site code, correct VAT). Invoice accuracy is reported monthly and forms part of the quarterly vendor scorecard.

**Q265:** What penalty clauses apply for SLA breaches?
**A:** SLA penalties are structured as a percentage of the affected month's invoice value — typically: 2% for consistent late delivery (>5% of deliveries late in a month), 3% for quality failures requiring replacement, 5% for systematic invoice errors. Penalties are credited to your account or offset against rebates.

**Q266:** How often are vendor scorecard reviews conducted?
**A:** Vendor scorecard reviews are conducted quarterly for all national accounts. The scorecard covers: on-time delivery, invoice accuracy, quality acceptance, communication responsiveness, and contract compliance. Reviews are attended by your account manager and our national accounts director.

**Q267:** What metrics are included in the quarterly vendor scorecard?
**A:** The quarterly scorecard includes: (1) On-time delivery percentage, (2) Invoice accuracy rate, (3) Quality acceptance rate, (4) Stock availability percentage, (5) Communication response time, (6) Order fulfilment rate, (7) Returns/defect rate, and (8) Overall satisfaction rating. Each metric has a target and weighting.

**Q268:** Can we define our own KPIs for the scorecard?
