**A:** Repeated quality failures trigger a formal corrective action procedure: trend analysis, root cause identification, supplier engagement (if manufacturing-related), process improvement, re-inspection protocol enhancement, and enhanced monitoring until performance returns to target.

**Q493:** Can you provide product origin certificates for import duty or compliance purposes?
**A:** Yes. Certificates of origin (COO) are available for all imported products, confirming country of manufacture. These support your import documentation, B-BBEE local content claims, or any compliance requirements related to product origin.

**Q494:** How do you ensure packaging integrity for long-distance deliveries?
**A:** Packaging integrity is ensured through: robust manufacturer packaging designed for export, additional protective wrapping for long-distance or multi-handling journeys, careful loading and securing in vehicles, and driver training on handling protocols. Damaged packaging is flagged and investigated.

**Q495:** Do you provide installation quality supervision as part of the supply contract?
**A:** Installation quality supervision can be provided through our accredited installer network or technical inspectors. Supervision covers: substrate preparation, adhesive application, laying pattern compliance, joint widths, expansion joints, and final quality acceptance. This is quoted separately as a value-added service.

---

## Category 14: Maintenance & Lifecycle Support

**Q496:** Does Tiletoria provide maintenance guidelines for the products supplied?
**A:** Yes. Product-specific maintenance guidelines are provided for every product on your national specification. These cover: daily cleaning procedures, recommended cleaning chemicals and equipment, periodic maintenance tasks, stain removal protocols, and what to avoid (e.g., abrasive cleaners on polished tiles).

**Q497:** Can you provide a cleaning protocol manual for our facilities management team?
**A:** Yes. A comprehensive cleaning protocol manual is developed for your national account, covering all specified flooring types across your portfolio. The manual includes: cleaning frequency charts, chemical dilution rates, equipment recommendations, safety precautions, and troubleshooting guides.

**Q498:** How do we handle warranty claims for defective products across our national portfolio?
**A:** Warranty claims are managed centrally through your National Account Manager. Submit the claim with photos, product details, batch number, and delivery date. We investigate, validate against warranty terms, and arrange replacement or remediation. All claims are tracked and reported.

**Q499:** What warranty periods apply to your commercial flooring products?

**Q500:** Can you conduct periodic inspection visits to assess floor condition across our portfolio?
**A:** Yes. Periodic inspection services are available for national accounts. Our technical team visits representative sites, assesses floor condition, identifies maintenance issues, and provides condition reports with recommendations. These inspections support proactive lifecycle management.

**Q501:** Do you provide training for our cleaning staff on proper floor maintenance?
**A:** Yes. Cleaning staff training can be arranged at site level or regionally. Training covers: correct cleaning product selection, application techniques, equipment use, stain identification and treatment, and damage prevention. Training certificates are provided for your HR records.

**Q502:** How do we order replacement tiles for repairs if our specification is discontinued?
**A:** If your specification is discontinued, we maintain a reference sample and source the closest available match from current stock. For national accounts, we may hold a reserve stock of the discontinued product for warranty and repair purposes, or negotiate a final production run.

**Q503:** Can you provide a floor condition assessment report for our property portfolio?
**A:** Yes. A portfolio-wide floor condition assessment can be conducted, providing: condition grading per site (good/fair/poor), estimated remaining lifespan, recommended maintenance actions, replacement priorities, and budget forecasting for refresh programmes. This supports capital planning.

**Q504:** What cleaning products do you recommend for high-traffic retail environments?
**A:** For high-traffic retail, we recommend: pH-neutral daily cleaners for regular maintenance, enzymatic cleaners for organic stains, alkaline degreasers for grease-prone areas (QSR kitchens), and periodic deep-cleaning with rotary scrubbers. Specific recommendations are provided per product type.

**Q505:** How do we remove stubborn stains without damaging the tile surface?
**A:** Stain removal protocols are provided per product type. Generally: identify the stain type, select the appropriate cleaner (acidic for rust/mineral deposits, alkaline for grease/oil, solvent for paint/adhesive), test in an inconspicuous area, apply and dwell, then rinse thoroughly. Harsh abrasives should be avoided on polished surfaces.

**Q506:** Can you provide a maintenance schedule template for our property managers?
**A:** Yes. A standard maintenance schedule template is provided, showing: daily tasks (sweeping, spot cleaning), weekly tasks (wet mopping), monthly tasks (deep cleaning, joint inspection), quarterly tasks (sealer reapplication if needed), and annual tasks (professional deep clean, condition assessment).

**Q507:** Do you provide grout cleaning and sealing products?
**A:** Yes. Grout cleaning products (acidic cleaners for cementitious grout, specialised cleaners for epoxy-based grout) and grout sealers (penetrating sealers for stain protection) are available. These are specified to match your grout type and are orderable through your national account.

**Q508:** How do we handle efflorescence (white powder) on tiled surfaces?
**A:** Efflorescence is treated with a mild acid wash (phosphoric acid-based cleaner) applied according to manufacturer instructions, followed by thorough rinsing. Persistent efflorescence indicates a moisture issue behind the tile that requires investigation by a tiling specialist. We can provide technical guidance.

**Q509:** Can you provide a lifecycle cost analysis for different flooring options?
**A:** Yes. Lifecycle cost analysis compares flooring options over 10, 15, and 20-year periods, incorporating: initial product and installation cost, maintenance costs (cleaning, sealing, repairs), expected lifespan, and replacement cost. This analysis supports strategic specification decisions.

**Q510:** What should we do if we notice tiles becoming loose or hollow-sounding?
**A:** Loose or hollow-sounding tiles indicate debonding, typically caused by substrate movement, inadequate adhesive coverage, or installation error. The affected area should be inspected by a tiling specialist. If the cause is product-related (rare), it falls under warranty. We can recommend accredited inspectors.

**Q511:** Do you provide anti-slip treatment for existing floors that have become slippery?
**A:** Yes. Anti-slip treatments (chemical etching or coating systems) can be applied to existing tiles to improve slip resistance. These treatments are suitable for commercial kitchens, bathrooms, and entrance areas where slip resistance has deteriorated or standards have changed.

**Q512:** Can you provide a national maintenance product ordering catalogue?
**A:** Yes. A maintenance product catalogue — including cleaning chemicals, sealers, repair products, grout refreshers, and equipment — is provided as part of your national account. These products can be ordered through the same e-procurement channel as tiles for consolidated billing.

**Q513:** How do we manage scratches and wear patterns in high-traffic areas?
**A:** For porcelain tiles, scratches are rare but can be minimised with entrance matting and regular cleaning. For LVT, individual plank replacement is possible. For polished tiles, professional polishing may restore appearance. Your maintenance manual includes specific guidance per product type.

**Q514:** Do you provide expansion joint maintenance guidance?
**A:** Yes. Expansion joint maintenance is covered in your maintenance manual: regular inspection for sealant integrity, cleaning to prevent debris build-up, and resealing when the joint filler shows deterioration. Proper expansion joint maintenance prevents floor failure due to thermal or structural movement.

**Q515:** Can Tiletoria manage a national floor refresh programme for our aging portfolio?
**A:** Yes. A national floor refresh programme is managed like a rollout: condition assessment, prioritisation of sites, specification confirmation (or update), phased delivery and installation, and programme reporting. Your account manager develops a refresh plan aligned to your capital budget cycle.

**Q516:** How do we handle warranty claims for products installed by third-party contractors?
**A:** Warranty claims are assessed based on whether the defect is product-related or installation-related. Product defects are covered regardless of who installed. Installation defects are the contractor's responsibility. We can provide expert assessment to determine the cause and recommend remedial action.

**Q517:** Can you provide a dedicated maintenance hotline for our facilities team?
**A:** Yes. A dedicated maintenance support line is available for your facilities management team to call with: product queries, stain removal advice, repair guidance, and warranty claim initiation. This line is staffed during business hours with after-hours message service.

**Q518:** Do you provide floor care training videos for our cleaning staff?
**A:** Yes. Floor care training videos covering correct cleaning techniques, product application, and equipment use can be provided in digital format for distribution to your cleaning teams. These complement face-to-face training and serve as ongoing reference materials.

**Q519:** What is the expected lifespan of porcelain tiles in a high-traffic retail environment?
**A:** Quality porcelain tiles (PEI IV–V) in high-traffic retail environments typically last 15–25 years with proper maintenance. Lifespan is influenced by: traffic intensity, maintenance quality, subfloor stability, and specification suitability. Our lifecycle analysis provides site-specific estimates.


**Q521:** How do we handle grout that has become discoloured over time?
**A:** Discoloured grout can often be restored with professional grout cleaning (acidic or enzymatic treatment). Severely stained grout may require grout colourant (a topical stain that restores uniform colour) or re-grouting in extreme cases. Prevention through regular cleaning and sealing is recommended.

**Q522:** Do you provide spare stock for future repairs as part of the initial rollout?
**A:** Yes. We recommend ordering 3–5% additional stock during the rollout for future repairs and replacements. This "attic stock" is stored at your nominated location or our hub and drawn upon as needed throughout the product lifecycle. This ensures perfect colour and batch matching for repairs.

**Q523:** Can you provide a digital maintenance manual accessible to all our site managers?
**A:** Yes. A digital maintenance manual is provided through Spec Lab (Spec Lab) or your client portal, accessible to all authorised site managers and facilities teams. The manual is searchable by product type and is updated when specifications change.

**Q524:** How do we handle chemical spills on the floor (e.g., in automotive showrooms)?
**A:** Chemical spill protocols are provided for each flooring type. Generally: contain the spill immediately, absorb with appropriate material, clean with compatible cleaner, rinse thoroughly, and neutralise if acidic/alkaline. Harsh chemicals should not be left on tile surfaces. Specific protocols cover oil, petrol, battery acid, and cleaning chemicals.

**Q525:** Can you provide a warranty claim response time commitment?
**A:** Warranty claims receive an initial response within 48 hours of submission. Investigation and validation typically take 5–10 working days. Replacement or remedial action is initiated within 15 working days of claim approval. Your account manager tracks every claim to resolution.


---

## Category 15: Referenceability & Case Studies

**Q526:** Can Tiletoria provide references from national retail chain clients?
**A:** Yes. Upon signing a mutual non-disclosure agreement, we can facilitate reference calls with our national retail chain clients. References cover: delivery performance, product quality, account management, rollout execution, and overall partnership satisfaction. Contact details are provided for direct reference checks.

**Q527:** Which national retail chains has Tiletoria worked with?
**A:** Tiletoria has supplied national rollouts and ongoing maintenance programmes for: major national retailers, major national retailers, major national retailers, major national retailers, major homeware retailers, and major retail groups. These engagements span multi-year contracts with delivery to hundreds of sites across all nine provinces.

**Q528:** Has Tiletoria worked with banking or financial services clients?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is an accredited supplier to major national banks and major national banks, supplying flooring for branches, offices, and call centres. We understand the governance, compliance, and service level requirements of financial services procurement teams.

**Q529:** What experience does Tiletoria have with automotive dealership rollouts?
**A:** Tiletoria has supported major automotive brands and major automotive brands dealership rollouts, supplying show-quality flooring for showrooms, service reception areas, and workshops. We understand the brand-standard requirements and coordination needs of automotive manufacturer programmes.

**Q530:** Can you share a case study of a successful national rollout?
**A:** Yes. Case studies of successful national rollouts — covering programme scope, challenges faced, solutions implemented, performance metrics, and client testimonials — are available for review under confidentiality. Your account manager presents relevant case studies during the proposal phase.

**Q531:** What were the key success factors in your major national retailers engagement?
**A:** The major national retailers engagement succeeded through: rigorous specification lock-down, dedicated account management, consistent national pricing, after-hours delivery coordination for mall stores, quality assurance protocols, and transparent reporting. Performance consistently met or exceeded SLA targets.

**Q532:** How many sites did you deliver to for major national retailers, and what was the timeframe?
**A:** Tiletoria delivered flooring to numerous major national retailers sites across multiple provinces within programme timeframes. Specific site numbers and programme durations are confidential, but we can confirm that delivery performance met the agreed SLA and rollout schedule.

**Q533:** Can you provide performance metrics from a recent national rollout?
**A:** Performance metrics from recent rollouts include: on-time delivery rates (95%+), invoice accuracy (99%+), quality acceptance rates (99%+), and client satisfaction scores. Specific metrics from your industry sector are shared during the proposal and reference process.

**Q534:** Has Tiletoria worked with hospitality groups like restaurants or hotel chains?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria has supplied major restaurant chains restaurants and other hospitality venues. We understand the requirements: durable materials for high-traffic dining areas, slip-resistant kitchen flooring, rapid installation during refurbishment windows, and brand-consistent aesthetics.

**Q535:** Can we speak directly to your existing clients about their experience?
**A:** Yes. Direct reference calls with existing clients are arranged once an NDA is in place. We coordinate with our reference clients to ensure they are available and briefed. Reference calls typically cover: delivery reliability, issue resolution, account management quality, and value for money.

**Q536:** What is the longest-running national account contract Tiletoria has?
**A:** Tiletoria has maintained national account relationships for 10+ years with several major South African corporates. These long-standing relationships demonstrate our ability to deliver consistent service, adapt to evolving requirements, and maintain performance over extended contract periods.

**Q537:** Can you provide a client testimonial or endorsement letter?
**A:** Yes. Client testimonial letters and endorsement statements are available under confidentiality from clients who have agreed to provide public references. These testimonials speak to our reliability, quality, and partnership approach.

**Q538:** How did Tiletoria handle a difficult situation or failure with a national client?
**A:** We believe in transparent communication when issues arise. In instances of delivery failures or quality issues, we: acknowledged immediately, conducted root cause analysis, implemented corrective actions, applied SLA credits where due, and strengthened processes to prevent recurrence. Clients respect our accountability.

**Q539:** Can you share a case study of a complex multi-province rollout you managed?
**A:** Yes. A case study of a complex multi-province rollout — highlighting the challenges of coordinating deliveries across different provinces, managing varied site conditions, and maintaining specification consistency — is available for review. This demonstrates our operational capability at scale.

**Q540:** What is your Net Promoter Score (NPS) or customer satisfaction rating from national accounts?
**A:** Our national account NPS typically scores in the "excellent" category (50+), reflecting strong client satisfaction and willingness to recommend. Satisfaction ratings are collected quarterly and trended over time as part of our continuous improvement programme.

**Q541:** Has Tiletoria won any industry awards or recognition?
**A:** Tiletoria and Invicta Holdings have received industry recognition for service excellence, transformation, and business performance. Specific awards and accolades are shared during the proposal phase to demonstrate industry standing and credibility.

**Q542:** Can you provide a list of your top 10 national account clients?
**A:** Our national account client list includes: major national retailers, major national retailers, major national retailers, major national retailers, major national banks, major national banks, major restaurant chains, major retail groups, major automotive brands, major automotive brands, major homeware retailers, and major entertainment brands. This list demonstrates our experience across retail, banking, hospitality, automotive, and entertainment sectors.

**Q543:** What was your biggest national rollout in terms of site count?
**A:** Tiletoria has managed national rollouts exceeding 100+ sites for major retail clients. These programmes spanned multiple years, all nine provinces, and included both new store openings and refurbishments. Programme scale and complexity are matched to our dedicated rollout management structure.

**Q544:** How do your clients rate your account management team specifically?
**A:** Account management is consistently rated as one of our strongest capabilities in client satisfaction surveys. Clients value: responsiveness, proactive communication, problem-solving ability, commercial fairness, and the seniority and empowerment of account managers to make decisions.

**Q545:** Can you provide a case study showing cost savings achieved for a national client?
**A:** Yes. Case studies demonstrating cost savings — through volume rebates, specification optimisation, reduced waste, consolidated billing efficiencies, and maintenance cost reduction — are available. Specific savings data is shared under confidentiality during the commercial proposal.

**Q546:** Has Tiletoria ever had a contract terminated by a national client for performance reasons?
**A:** Tiletoria maintains strong client relationships and has not had a national contract terminated for performance failure. Where contracts have concluded, it has been through natural expiry, strategic re-sourcing, or mutual agreement. Our focus is on performance excellence and long-term retention.

**Q547:** Can you provide a site visit to a completed reference store where we can inspect the flooring?
**A:** Yes. Site visits to completed reference stores can be arranged with the client's permission. This allows you to inspect: product quality, installation finish, wear performance after time in service, and overall aesthetic. Your account manager coordinates these visits.

**Q548:** What do your clients say is the biggest benefit of working with Tiletoria?
**A:** Clients consistently cite: national consistency (same product, same price, same quality everywhere), reliable delivery performance, dedicated account management that reduces their coordination burden, and the peace of mind that comes from working with an established, financially stable partner.

**Q549:** Can you provide a performance dashboard from an active national account?
**A:** Yes. An anonymised performance dashboard from an active national account — showing delivery metrics, invoice accuracy, quality acceptance, and scorecard trends — can be shared during the proposal phase. This provides tangible evidence of our operational performance.

**Q550:** How do you measure and improve based on client feedback?
**A:** Client feedback is collected quarterly through satisfaction surveys, scorecard reviews, and informal feedback channels. Feedback is analysed for trends, improvement actions are assigned, and progress is reported at the next QBR. This closed-loop process drives continuous improvement.

**Q551:** Has Tiletoria been referenced in any industry publications or case studies?
**A:** Tiletoria has been featured in industry publications covering: national rollout capability, transformation in the built environment, supply chain excellence, and product innovation. Media coverage and industry recognition are shared during the proposal process.

**Q552:** Can you provide a risk reference — how you handled a crisis for a client?
**A:** Yes. We can share anonymised examples of crisis management — such as urgent replacement of defective stock, emergency supply during supply chain disruptions, or rapid programme acceleration — demonstrating our responsiveness and problem-solving capability under pressure.

**Q553:** What percentage of your national account clients renew their contracts?
**A:** Our national account contract renewal rate exceeds 90%, reflecting strong client satisfaction and the value we deliver through consistent performance, continuous improvement, and strategic partnership. Renewals often include expanded scope and enhanced terms.

**Q554:** Can you provide a comparison of your rollout performance vs. industry benchmarks?
**A:** Yes. Our rollout performance metrics are benchmarked against industry standards and peer performance data. We consistently perform at or above industry benchmarks for on-time delivery, invoice accuracy, and quality acceptance. Benchmark data is shared during the proposal.

**Q555:** How long has Tiletoria been serving national corporate accounts?
**A:** Tiletoria has been serving national corporate accounts for over two decades, since the early 2000s. This long track record has enabled us to refine our systems, build dedicated national account infrastructure, and develop deep expertise in multi-site supply programmes.

**Q556:** Can you provide a reference from a client in our specific industry sector?
**A:** Yes. We will provide references from clients in your specific sector — retail, banking, hospitality, automotive, or entertainment — to ensure relevance. Your account manager identifies the most appropriate references based on your industry and programme requirements.

**Q557:** What do your delivery drivers and logistics team say about working with your clients?
**A:** Our delivery teams take pride in professional, courteous service. Driver feedback indicates that clear site instructions, accessible contacts, and safe offloading areas contribute to smooth deliveries. We share positive driver feedback with clients and address any coordination issues promptly.

**Q558:** Can you provide a case study of a programme accelerated under pressure?
**A:** Yes. Case studies of accelerated programmes — where clients needed to compress timelines due to lease commitments, seasonal demands, or competitive pressures — demonstrate our ability to mobilise resources, prioritise stock, and deliver on compressed schedules.

**Q559:** How has Tiletoria's national account service evolved over the years?
**A:** Our national account service has evolved from simple multi-site delivery to a comprehensive programme management capability: dedicated account teams, e-procurement integration, real-time reporting, SLA governance, transformation partnership, and strategic planning. This evolution reflects client needs and industry best practice.

**Q560:** Can you provide a statement of financial stability from your auditors?
**A:** Yes. A statement of financial stability or accountant's letter confirming going concern status can be provided as part of our vendor accreditation pack. This demonstrates our financial capacity to fulfil multi-year national contracts.

## Category 16: Renewal & Expansion

**Q561:** What are the standard contract renewal terms for national accounts?
**A:** Standard renewal terms include: automatic renewal on anniversary unless either party gives 3 months' notice, pricing review based on CPI and volume performance, SLA target refresh, and specification update. Renewal terms are agreed during initial contract negotiation and documented in the agreement.

**Q562:** Are there early renewal bonuses or incentives for committing to an extension?
**A:** Yes. Early renewal commitments (e.g., signing a 2-year extension 6 months before expiry) may attract enhanced rebate tiers, locked pricing for the extension period, or value-added services at no charge. These incentives are discussed at the annual strategic planning session.

**Q563:** How does Tiletoria approach innovation partnership with national clients?
**A:** Innovation partnership involves: early access to new product ranges, collaborative specification development, joint sustainability initiatives, process improvement suggestions, and participation in your innovation forums. We actively seek opportunities to add value beyond standard supply.

**Q564:** Can the contract scope be expanded to include new product categories?
**A:** Yes. Contract scope can be expanded to include additional product categories (e.g., adding outdoor pavers, wall cladding, or sanitaryware to an existing tile contract). Expanded scope is negotiated through a contract addendum with appropriate pricing and SLA adjustments.

**Q565:** What is the process for adding new sites to an existing national contract?
**A:** New sites are added through a simple administrative process: site details provided to your account manager, site profile created, delivery window established, and the site added to the e-procurement catalogue and billing system. No renegotiation is required unless volumes trigger a rebate tier change.

**Q566:** Can we expand from a single division to a group-wide contract?
**A:** Yes. Single-division contracts can be expanded to group-wide coverage. This typically triggers enhanced national pricing, improved rebate tiers, and dedicated resource allocation. The expansion is documented through a contract variation and may include a revised SLA.

**Q567:** How does Tiletoria reward long-term partnership loyalty?
**A:** Long-term partnership rewards include: enhanced rebate tiers, priority stock allocation, access to limited-stock products, invitation to exclusive product previews, executive relationship engagement, and joint marketing opportunities. Loyalty benefits increase with tenure and spend.

**Q568:** Can we pilot new products through the existing contract without renegotiation?
**A:** Yes. New product pilots can be conducted under the existing contract framework. Pilot products are added to the catalogue on a trial basis, and if adopted into the standard specification, pricing and terms are formalised through a product addendum.

**Q569:** What happens if we want to change our specification significantly at renewal?
**A:** Significant specification changes at renewal are treated as a refresh: new samples provided, design team engagement, mock-up approval, and updated specification documentation. Pricing is reviewed based on the new product mix, and the SLA is updated to reflect any changed requirements.

**Q570:** Can Tiletoria support our expansion into Africa beyond South Africa?
**A:** Yes. Tiletoria supports SADC expansion through cross-border logistics, compliance with local import requirements, and coordination with local contractors. Your national account manager works with our export team to establish supply chains for Namibia, Botswana, Mozambique, and other markets.

**Q571:** How do you approach contract renewal negotiations?
**A:** Renewal negotiations begin 6 months before expiry with a performance review, spend analysis, and market benchmarking. We present a renewal proposal with recommended terms, innovations, and improvements. Negotiation is collaborative, aiming for mutual benefit and long-term continuity.

**Q572:** Can we lock in current pricing for a multi-year renewal?
**A:** Multi-year pricing locks are available for renewals with committed volume agreements. Pricing may be fixed for the renewal term or capped at CPI+ an agreed margin. These arrangements provide budget certainty and are documented in the renewal agreement.

**Q573:** What joint marketing opportunities are available?
**A:** Joint marketing opportunities include: co-branded case studies, trade publication features, conference presentations, award submissions, and sustainability reporting collaboration. These activities raise the profile of both organisations and demonstrate partnership value.

**Q574:** Can Tiletoria participate in our supplier innovation days or forums?
**A:** Yes. We welcome participation in your supplier innovation days, forums, and advisory boards. Our technical and commercial teams can present: new product trends, sustainability innovations, installation technology, and supply chain optimisation ideas.

**Q575:** How does Tiletoria identify opportunities to expand the relationship?
**A:** We proactively identify expansion opportunities through: regular account reviews, understanding your business strategy, monitoring industry trends, and engaging with your operations teams. Expansion proposals are presented at quarterly business reviews and annual planning sessions.

**Q576:** Can we add a trial of sustainable/green products to our existing contract?
**A:** Yes. Sustainable product trials can be added to test environmental performance, customer response, and lifecycle benefits. If successful, green products can be incorporated into your standard specification, supporting your ESG and sustainability objectives.

**Q577:** What is Tiletoria's approach to continuous improvement over the contract term?
**A:** Continuous improvement is formalised through: annual improvement targets in the SLA, quarterly scorecard reviews identifying improvement areas, process innovation suggestions, technology upgrades (e.g., e-procurement enhancements), and client feedback implementation.

**Q578:** Can the contract be expanded to include your installation network nationally?
**A:** Yes. Installation services through our accredited installer network can be added to the contract scope. This provides a single-source supply-and-install solution with unified reporting, consolidated billing, and consistent quality management across all sites.

**Q579:** How do you handle contract amendments for changes in legislation or standards?
**A:** If legislation or standards change during the contract term (e.g., new SANS requirements, B-BBEE Code revisions), we jointly assess the impact and agree contract amendments to ensure continued compliance. Amendment procedures are defined in the contract variation clause.

**Q580:** Can we renegotiate pricing mid-term if market conditions change significantly?
**A:** Mid-term pricing reviews can be triggered by significant market changes (e.g., exchange rate movements beyond the agreed threshold, raw material cost changes). Reviews are conducted in good faith with transparency on cost drivers, aiming for fair adjustment that preserves the partnership.

**Q581:** Does Tiletoria offer a strategic partnership tier for top national accounts?
**A:** Yes. Top national accounts (typically R5m+ annual spend) are designated as Strategic Partners with enhanced benefits: executive sponsorship, priority access to MD, innovation collaboration, joint business planning, first access to new products, and preferential terms.

**Q582:** Can we include performance bonuses for exceeding SLA targets in the renewal?
**A:** Yes. Performance bonus structures — where Tiletoria earns additional margin or incentives for consistently exceeding SLA targets — can be built into renewal contracts. This aligns incentives and rewards exceptional service delivery.

**Q583:** How does Tiletoria plan for the next generation of our contract?
**A:** Next-generation contract planning begins at the annual strategic review: assessing your evolving needs, our capability development, market trends, technology advancements, and sustainability goals. A roadmap for the next contract period is developed jointly.

**Q584:** Can Tiletoria support our ESG reporting requirements as part of contract expansion?
**A:** Yes. ESG data and reporting support can be included in contract scope: carbon footprint data, sustainable sourcing certificates, diversity metrics, community impact data, and environmental product declarations. This supports your corporate ESG disclosure requirements.

**Q585:** What is the notice period for contract termination, and how is exit managed?
**A:** Standard notice period is 3–6 months, agreed during contract negotiation. Exit management includes: orderly wind-down of orders, completion of in-progress deliveries, final billing and reconciliation, rebate settlement, stock return (if applicable), and knowledge transfer to any incoming supplier.

**Q586:** Can we include a right of first refusal for new product categories?
**A:** Yes. A right of first refusal can be included in the contract, giving you priority access to new product categories or services that Tiletoria introduces during the contract term. This ensures you benefit from our evolving capability.

**Q587:** How does Tiletoria handle contract transition from a previous supplier?
**A:** Transition from a previous supplier is managed through: stock audit and takeover, specification alignment, system integration, site contact updates, and a transition period where both suppliers may operate in parallel. Your account manager leads the transition plan to ensure no supply gaps.

**Q588:** Can we add digital services (e.g., VR showroom, digital sampling) to the contract?
**A:** Yes. Digital services including virtual showrooms, digital sampling platforms, augmented reality visualisation, and online training portals can be added to the contract scope. These services enhance the specification and ordering experience for your teams.

**Q589:** What happens to our attic stock if the contract is not renewed?
**A:** Attic stock (spare stock held for repairs) is either delivered to your nominated location at contract end, purchased by you at an agreed price, or returned to Tiletoria stock subject to restocking terms. The arrangement is defined in the contract exit clauses.

**Q590:** Can we structure the renewal with a broader scope but lower unit prices?
**A:** Yes. Expanded scope with increased committed volumes typically enables improved unit pricing through economies of scale. We model the total value of the expanded relationship and propose pricing that reflects the increased partnership value.

**Q591:** How does Tiletoria ensure innovation is shared with us proactively?
**A:** Innovation is shared through: quarterly innovation updates, annual product previews, participation in your supplier forums, and proactive suggestions from your account manager. We monitor global trends and bring relevant innovations to your attention before they become widely available.

**Q592:** Can we include a most-favoured-customer clause in the renewal?
**A:** A most-favoured-customer pricing clause can be discussed for strategic partnership contracts. This ensures that pricing and terms offered to you are at least as favourable as those offered to comparable national accounts. Terms are documented in the commercial schedule.

**Q593:** What role does the Managing Director play in strategic account renewal?
**A:** The MD personally engages in strategic account renewals: attending renewal meetings, approving enhanced terms, discussing strategic partnership opportunities, and ensuring organisational commitment. MD involvement signals the importance of the relationship to Tiletoria.

**Q594:** Can we trial a vendor-managed inventory (VMI) model at renewal?
**A:** Yes. VMI can be trialled at renewal for predictable-consumption items. Under VMI, Tiletoria monitors your stock levels and replenishes automatically based on agreed min/max levels. This reduces your administrative burden and prevents stock-outs.

**Q595:** How does Tiletoria handle contract renewal for clients with multiple divisions?
**A:** Multi-division renewals are coordinated centrally through your account manager. A consolidated renewal proposal covers all divisions, with division-specific pricing, specifications, and service levels. Centralised governance ensures consistency while accommodating divisional needs.

**Q596:** Can renewal include expanded payment terms as a loyalty benefit?
**A:** Enhanced payment terms (e.g., extension from 30 to 60 days) can be offered at renewal as a loyalty benefit for clients with strong payment history and significant annual spend. Enhanced terms are subject to credit review and approval.

**Q597:** What is Tiletoria's vision for the partnership over the next 3–5 years?
**A:** Our vision is to evolve from supplier to strategic partner: deepening specification collaboration, co-developing sustainable solutions, integrating systems for seamless procurement, supporting your expansion (geographic and portfolio), and contributing to your ESG and transformation objectives.

**Q598:** Can the contract include a joint sustainability target?
**A:** Yes. Joint sustainability targets — such as reducing carbon footprint per delivery, increasing recycled content in products, or achieving zero waste to landfill — can be included in the contract with measurable KPIs and regular reporting.

**Q599:** How does Tiletoria ensure competitive positioning at renewal vs. new market entrants?
**A:** We maintain competitive positioning through: continuous operational improvement, investment in technology and capability, market-competitive pricing, strong B-BBEE credentials, deep relationship investment, and proven performance. We benchmark against competitors and adjust proactively.

**Q600:** Can renewal include access to exclusive or limited-distribution products?
**A:** Yes. Strategic partners receive first access to exclusive ranges, limited-distribution designer collections, and early-release products. Exclusivity arrangements (e.g., sole distribution rights in your sector) can be discussed for significant volume commitments.


---

## Additional Q&A — Deep-Dive Supplement

### Extended Coverage: National Operations & Logistics

**Q601:** Can Tiletoria deliver to sites in Richards Bay, leading adhesive manufacturerslito, and other KZN North Coast locations?
**A:** Yes. Our KZN hub in Durban/Cornubia services the entire KZN North Coast including Richards Bay, leading adhesive manufacturerslito, and surrounding areas. Standard delivery lead time is 2–4 working days from the Durban hub depending on exact location and order size.

**Q602:** How do you handle deliveries to sites in Polokwane, Tzaneen, and Limpopo province?
**A:** Limpopo deliveries are fulfilled from our Gauteng hub. Standard lead time to Polokwane is 2–3 working days; to Tzaneen and more remote Limpopo locations 3–5 working days. Regular delivery routes to Limpopo are established for clients with ongoing replenishment needs.

**Q603:** Can you support our national rollout if 40% of our sites are in the Western Cape?
**A:** Absolutely. The Western Cape is one of our three primary hubs, and we have significant capacity and stockholding in Cape Town. A high Western Cape site concentration actually improves delivery efficiency and reduces average lead times for your programme.

**Q604:** What happens if we need to deliver to a site in a township with no formal street address?
**A:** For informal-address sites, we use GPS coordinates, nearby landmarks, and site contact phone coordination. Our drivers call the site contact when approaching to confirm exact location. Pre-delivery site visits are conducted for first-time deliveries to unfamiliar locations.

**Q605:** Can you deliver to our sites in the Vaal Triangle area?
**A:** Yes. The Vaal Triangle (Vereeniging, Vanderbijlpark, Sasolburg) is serviced from our Gauteng hub with standard delivery timeframes of 1–2 working days. This area is within our regular Gauteng delivery radius.

**Q606:** How do you manage deliveries to Nelspruit and Mpumalanga sites?
**A:** Mpumalanga deliveries are dispatched from our Gauteng hub. Nelspruit is 3–4 hours from Johannesburg and typically receives deliveries within 2–3 working days. For large rollout programmes in Mpumalanga, we may schedule dedicated delivery days to optimise routing.

**Q607:** Can you support a rollout with 80% of sites in rural or semi-rural locations?
**A:** Yes. We have experience with rural-heavy rollouts (e.g., major retail groups stores in rural communities). The site database captures access conditions, vehicle restrictions, and contact protocols for each rural site. Delivery timeframes are extended for remote locations, but all sites are serviceable.

**Q608:** Do you have a dedicated fleet for the Western Cape, or do you subcontract?
**A:** We operate our own fleet from all three hubs including the Western Cape, supplemented by contracted logistics partners during peak demand. The Cape Town fleet covers the Western Cape and Northern Cape with regular routes to George, Mossel Bay, and other regional centres.

**Q609:** Can you deliver to Paarden Eiland (Gqeberha) and surrounding Eastern Cape areas?
**A:** Yes. Eastern Cape deliveries are fulfilled from our KZN hub in Durban. Paarden Eiland is typically 3–5 working days from Durban. For large Eastern Cape programmes, we assess whether to position a stock satellite or service directly from Durban.

**Q610:** How do you handle deliveries to Kimberley and the Northern Cape?
**A:** Northern Cape deliveries are serviced from our Western Cape hub. Kimberley typically has a 3–5 day lead time from Cape Town. For very remote Northern Cape locations, extended lead times apply and deliveries may be consolidated into less frequent but larger drops.

### Extended Coverage: Pricing & Commercial Flexibility

**Q611:** Can you provide provisional sums for our development budget before specifications are finalised?
**A:** Yes. We provide provisional sums based on typical commercial specifications and average floor areas for your site types. These provisional sums are accurate enough for development budgeting and are refined once specifications are finalised and site surveys are complete.

**Q612:** How do you handle pricing for sites with significantly different floor areas (200m² vs. 2,000m²)?
**A:** National unit pricing applies regardless of site size. The per-m² product price is the same for a 200m² site and a 2,000m² site. Volume rebates are calculated on total aggregate spend across all sites, so smaller sites still contribute to volume discounts.

**Q613:** Can you provide separate pricing for product-only vs. supply-and-install?
**A:** Yes. Separate pricing schedules are provided for product supply only and for supply-and-install turnkey solutions. This allows you to compare options and select the most appropriate delivery model for each site type or programme phase.

**Q614:** Do you offer price protection for orders placed in advance of delivery?
**A:** Yes. For advance orders (e.g., stock ordered 3 months ahead of a rollout wave), pricing is locked at the order date and protected against price increases until delivery. This price protection is standard for national accounts with confirmed rollout schedules.

**Q615:** Can we negotiate a single national price for all products regardless of delivery location?
**A:** Yes. A single national delivered price is our standard offering for national accounts. This means the price you pay is identical whether we deliver to Sandton, Cornubia, or Cape Town CBD — no transport surcharges, no regional premiums, no hidden costs.

**Q616:** How do you handle pricing for products sourced from different countries with different exchange rates?
**A:** Multi-country sourcing pricing is managed through: hedging where feasible, currency adjustment clauses in the contract, and portfolio pricing that averages exchange rate exposure across the product range. Your account manager provides transparency on currency-sensitive products.

**Q617:** Can you provide a detailed cost breakdown (material, transport, margin) for our finance team?
**A:** A detailed cost breakdown can be provided under confidentiality for your finance team's review. This demonstrates the cost structure behind our pricing and supports your cost analysis and benchmarking activities. The breakdown covers: ex-factory cost, transport, handling, and margin.

**Q618:** Do you offer performance-based pricing where unit price reduces as volume increases?
**A:** Yes. Volume-tiered pricing is standard — the unit price reduces or the rebate percentage increases as annual spend crosses defined thresholds. These tiers are agreed upfront and provide automatic price benefit as your rollout scales and site count grows.

**Q619:** Can you accommodate a pricing review after the pilot programme before full rollout?
**A:** Yes. A post-pilot pricing review is standard practice. After the pilot, we assess actual vs. estimated costs, installation learning, logistics efficiency, and any specification refinements. Pricing is adjusted if warranted, ensuring the full rollout pricing reflects real programme economics.

**Q620:** How do you handle price differences between equivalent products from different manufacturers?
**A:** For national specifications, we recommend a single manufacturer per SKU to ensure consistency. If equivalent alternatives are proposed, we provide a comparison of price, quality, lead time, and warranty. The final decision is yours, documented in the specification lock-in.

### Extended Coverage: E-Procurement & Systems Integration

**Q621:** Can Tiletoria integrate with Coupa e-procurement platform?
**A:** Yes. In addition to SAP Ariba and Oracle, we support integration with Coupa and other major e-procurement platforms. Our technical team is experienced with cXML, OCI, and API-based integrations across multiple platforms.

**Q622:** Do you support punch-out catalogues with real-time pricing updates?
**A:** Yes. Our punch-out catalogues connect to our live pricing database, ensuring that users always see current contracted prices. Price updates are applied centrally and reflect immediately in the catalogue without requiring manual updates by your systems team.

**Q623:** Can your system send automated low-stock alerts to our procurement team?
**A:** Yes. Automated alerts can be configured to notify your procurement team when stock levels for specified SKUs fall below agreed thresholds at any hub. Alerts are sent via email and can be integrated into your e-procurement dashboard.

**Q624:** How do you handle product image and description updates in the e-procurement catalogue?
**A:** Product images and descriptions are maintained by our content team and updated in the e-procurement catalogue whenever products are refreshed. Updates are tested in the UAT environment before deployment to production to ensure quality and accuracy.

**Q625:** Can you support SSO (Single Sign-On) authentication for the e-procurement catalogue?
**A:** SSO integration depends on your e-procurement platform's capability. For platforms supporting SAML or OAuth, SSO can be configured to allow seamless access to the Tiletoria punch-out catalogue without separate login credentials.

**Q626:** Do you provide an open API for custom integration with our proprietary systems?
**A:** Yes. RESTful APIs are available for clients with proprietary systems. API endpoints cover: product catalogue, stock availability, order placement, delivery tracking, and invoice retrieval. Full API documentation and sandbox access are provided during integration.

**Q627:** Can your e-procurement catalogue support multi-language (English/Afrikaans)?
**A:** The e-procurement catalogue is provided in English as standard. Multi-language support can be discussed depending on your platform's capabilities and the specific languages required. Product descriptions can be provided in Afrikaans where needed.

**Q628:** How do you handle system downtime or maintenance windows?
**A:** Planned system maintenance is scheduled during low-usage periods (typically weekends or late evening) with 48 hours' advance notice to all users. Emergency maintenance is communicated immediately. Backup ordering channels (phone/email) are available during any downtime.

**Q629:** Can the e-procurement system enforce our approval workflows automatically?
**A:** Yes. Your platform's approval workflows are applied to Tiletoria orders just like any other supplier. Our catalogue integrates with your workflow engine, routing orders through the correct approval chain based on value, category, and user role.

**Q630:** Do you provide usage analytics showing how our teams interact with your catalogue?
**A:** Yes. Catalogue usage analytics are provided monthly, showing: number of users, search patterns, products viewed, cart abandonment rates, and order conversion. This data helps optimise the catalogue layout and identify training needs.

### Extended Coverage: SLA Governance & Performance

**Q631:** Can you provide a balanced scorecard covering financial, operational, and relationship metrics?
**A:** Yes. A balanced scorecard covering: financial (pricing accuracy, rebate delivery, cost savings), operational (delivery, quality, stock availability), and relationship (responsiveness, innovation, partnership) is provided quarterly. Each dimension has weighted KPIs.

**Q632:** How do you handle SLA disputes where the client and Tiletoria disagree on performance measurement?
**A:** SLA disputes are escalated to the National Accounts Director for resolution. Both parties review the data, agree the measurement methodology, and resolve the discrepancy. If necessary, an independent third party can be engaged to audit the measurement process.

**Q633:** Can we include mystery shopper evaluations as part of the SLA scorecard?
**A:** Yes. Mystery shopper or secret site evaluations can be included as a qualitative KPI. These evaluations assess: delivery professionalism, product condition, documentation completeness, and installer quality (if applicable) from the site team's perspective.

**Q634:** Do you conduct internal audits of your SLA performance data?
**A:** Yes. SLA performance data undergoes internal audit quarterly to ensure accuracy and integrity. Audit findings are shared with clients on request. External audit of performance data can be arranged for an additional fee.

**Q635:** Can the SLA include innovation contribution as a weighted metric?
**A:** Yes. Innovation contribution can be weighted at 5–10% of the total scorecard. Innovation is measured by: number of improvement suggestions implemented, new products trialled, process enhancements delivered, and value-added services provided. This incentivises proactive partnership.

**Q636:** How do you ensure SLA performance is maintained when key personnel are on leave?
**A:** Cross-training and documented procedures ensure SLA performance is maintained during staff absences. Backup account managers, logistics coordinators, and customer service representatives are briefed and have full system access. Performance monitoring continues uninterrupted.

**Q637:** Can we benchmark your SLA performance against our other national suppliers?
**A:** Yes. We encourage benchmarking. Our SLA metrics are presented in a standard format that allows comparison with other suppliers. We also participate in industry benchmarking studies and can share anonymised peer comparison data.

**Q638:** What happens if we consistently score your performance as "exceeds target"?
**A:** Consistently exceeding target triggers enhanced partnership benefits: accelerated rebate payments, preferential stock allocation, executive relationship engagement, and expanded scope opportunities. Exceptional performance is also recognised in our internal incentive programmes.

**Q639:** Can the SLA include sustainability KPIs (e.g., carbon per delivery)?
**A:** Yes. Sustainability KPIs including: carbon emissions per delivery, packaging recycling rate, sustainable product percentage, and waste reduction can be included in the SLA. These are measured, reported, and improved over the contract term.

**Q640:** How do you handle force majeure events in the SLA (e.g., KZN floods, civil unrest)?
**A:** Force majeure provisions suspend SLA penalties during events beyond reasonable control. We activate our business continuity plan, communicate proactively, implement mitigation measures (alternative routing, other hubs), and resume normal SLA once conditions stabilise.

### Extended Coverage: Project & Programme Management

**Q641:** Can you manage a rollout where each site has a different shopfitter?
**A:** Yes. We routinely coordinate deliveries with multiple shopfitters across a national rollout. Each shopfitter receives the site-specific delivery schedule, and we confirm readiness before each delivery. Your rollout coordinator maintains the master schedule linking all shopfitters.

**Q642:** How do you handle a rollout that is paused mid-programme due to our funding cycle?
**A:** Programme pauses are managed by: holding reserved stock at hubs, suspending factory orders (where possible), maintaining the site database, and rescheduling pending deliveries. When the programme resumes, we reactivate the schedule and confirm stock availability for the remaining sites.

**Q643:** Can you support a rollout with simultaneous new builds and tenant-fit conversions?
**A:** Yes. New builds and tenant-fit conversions have different delivery protocols: new builds may have flexible access and crane availability; tenant-fits require after-hours delivery and protection of existing finishes. Both are managed within the same programme with site-type-specific delivery rules.

**Q644:** How do you manage the "snagging" period after installation?
**A:** The snagging period (typically 30 days post-installation) is managed through: site inspection scheduling, defect logging with photos, replacement stock dispatch, and snagging sign-off. Your rollout coordinator tracks all snagging items to resolution before site handover.

**Q645:** Can you provide a critical path analysis for our rollout programme?
**A:** Yes. A critical path analysis identifies: long-lead items (imported products), factory order deadlines, stock pre-positioning dates, delivery windows, and installation durations. This analysis highlights programme risks and float, enabling proactive management of the rollout timeline.

**Q646:** How do you handle site-specific design variations within a standard rollout?
**A:** Site-specific variations (e.g., different floor areas, local authority requirements, landlord conditions) are captured in the site register. Each variation is assessed for impact on product quantities, specifications, and cost, and approved before inclusion in the site-specific delivery.

**Q647:** Can you manage a phased rollout where we open 10 sites per month for 12 months?
**A:** Yes. A phased, monthly wave rollout is our standard model. Each month's wave is planned 6–8 weeks ahead: stock confirmed, deliveries scheduled, contractors briefed, and sites inspected. Monthly programme reviews track progress against the wave plan.

**Q648:** How do you manage the relationship between our rollout programme and your other clients?
**A:** Your rollout programme has dedicated stock allocation and logistics capacity. Other clients' orders do not impact your committed deliveries. During peak periods, additional resources are activated to ensure all programme commitments are met without compromise.

**Q649:** Can you provide a risk assessment for our proposed rollout programme?
**A:** Yes. A comprehensive risk assessment covering: supply chain risks, logistics risks, quality risks, site readiness risks, financial risks, and external risks is provided at programme inception. Each risk has a probability, impact, mitigation plan, and owner.

**Q650:** How do you manage the transition from rollout phase to maintenance phase?
**A:** The rollout-to-maintenance transition is managed through: a final programme review, attic stock handover, maintenance manual distribution, cleaning staff training scheduling, warranty registration completion, and a shift in account management focus from project delivery to ongoing support.

### Extended Coverage: B-BBEE & Transformation Deep-Dive

**Q651:** What is the difference between B-BBEE Level 1 and Level 2 for our procurement scoring?
**A:** Level 1 provides 135% procurement recognition (spend counts as 135% of actual value); Level 2 provides 125%. Both deliver significantly more procurement points than lower levels. Our verified certificate confirms our current level and recognition percentage.

**Q652:** Does Tiletoria's B-BBEE status include bonus points for YES, SEDA, or ESD?
**A:** Yes. Our B-BBEE scorecard may include bonus points for YES (Youth Employment Service) participation, qualifying ESD contributions, and other priority elements. The detailed scorecard breakdown shows all bonus points achieved and their contribution to our overall level.

**Q653:** Can our procurement of Tiletoria products contribute to our preferential procurement points under all seven B-BBEE elements?
**A:** Your procurement spend with Tiletoria primarily contributes to the Preferential Procurement element. However, if structured supplier development or ESD arrangements are in place, spend may also contribute to ESD points. We advise on structuring for maximum scorecard benefit.

**Q654:** Is Tiletoria's B-BBEE certificate verified by a SANAS-accredited agency?
**A:** Yes. Our B-BBEE certificate is issued by a SANAS-accredited verification agency and includes a unique verification certificate number. The certificate is valid for 12 months and can be verified through the SANAS or Department of Trade, Industry and Competition systems.

**Q655:** Can Tiletoria assist with our own B-BBEE verification preparation?
**A:** Yes. We provide all necessary supplier documentation (B-BBEE certificate, sworn affidavit if applicable, CIPC documents, tax clearance) to support your verification. Timely provision of these documents is a standard service for national accounts.

**Q656:** Does Tiletoria have a black-owned subsidiary or division we can channel spend through?
**A:** Our group structure includes entities that may enhance your procurement recognition. Your account manager will explain the optimal contracting structure to maximise your B-BBEE procurement points while maintaining service quality and contractual clarity.

**Q657:** How does Tiletoria support the Department of Trade and Industry's localisation objectives?
**A:** We support localisation by: prioritising South African manufacturers where quality permits, creating local employment across our three hubs, developing local skills through training programmes, and contributing to local economic development in the communities where we operate.

**Q658:** Can Tiletoria provide a transformation partnership plan with measurable targets?
**A:** Yes. A transformation partnership plan is developed jointly with your procurement and transformation teams. The plan includes: measurable targets, timelines, reporting mechanisms, and mutual B-BBEE benefits. Progress is reviewed quarterly and reported in the scorecard.

**Q659:** Does Tiletoria participate in industry transformation initiatives?
**A:** Yes. We participate in industry transformation initiatives through: industry association membership, engagement with the dtic on sector codes, support for emerging contractor development, and advocacy for inclusive procurement practices in the built environment.

**Q660:** How does Tiletoria ensure its own suppliers are transformation-compliant?
**A:** Our supplier accreditation process includes B-BBEE verification review. We prioritise suppliers with strong transformation credentials and monitor our supply chain's aggregate B-BBEE performance. Supplier development initiatives are targeted at improving the transformation profile of our supply base.

### Extended Coverage: Account Management & Relationship

**Q661:** How often should we expect to see our account manager in person?
**A:** For national accounts, face-to-face meetings are recommended monthly (for rollout programmes) or quarterly (for maintenance accounts). Your account manager schedules visits to your head office and representative sites to maintain relationship depth and operational visibility.

**Q662:** Can our account manager visit our sites to understand operational challenges?
**A:** Yes. Site visits by your account manager are encouraged and are part of the standard service. These visits provide valuable context: site conditions, delivery constraints, contractor relationships, and maintenance practices. Insights from site visits inform service improvements.

**Q663:** How does Tiletoria ensure our account manager understands our business strategy?
**A:** Account managers participate in your annual strategic planning session and quarterly business reviews. They are briefed on your business strategy, expansion plans, and operational priorities. This strategic alignment ensures our service evolves with your business needs.

**Q664:** Can we have multiple account contacts for different business units?
**A:** Yes. A single National Account Manager coordinates across your business units, with dedicated operational contacts for each unit if needed. This provides centralised governance with operational specialisation for complex, multi-divisional relationships.

**Q665:** How does Tiletoria handle confidential or sensitive information we share?
**A:** All confidential information is protected under the NDA and our POPIA compliance framework. Access is limited to personnel with a legitimate need. Staff are trained on confidentiality obligations, and breaches are treated as serious disciplinary matters.

**Q666:** Can your account manager attend our annual store managers' conference?
**A:** Yes. Your account manager can attend your store managers' conference to present: product updates, maintenance tips, ordering guidance, and new service offerings. This direct engagement with site-level teams improves operational alignment and reduces order errors.

**Q667:** How does Tiletoria gather and act on feedback from our site teams?
**A:** Feedback is gathered through: delivery satisfaction surveys, site visit conversations, direct communication with site contacts, and your quarterly business review. Feedback is logged, analysed for trends, and drives improvement actions tracked through to completion.

**Q668:** What happens if our primary and backup account managers are both unavailable?
**A:** In the rare event that both primary and backup account managers are unavailable, our National Accounts Director steps in directly. The CRM system ensures full account visibility, and the director has authority to make decisions and resolve issues without delay.

**Q669:** Can we have a direct WhatsApp or instant messaging line to our account manager?
**A:** Yes. Your account manager provides direct contact details including mobile number and WhatsApp for operational queries. Response times for instant messages are typically within 2–4 hours during business hours. This complements formal email and phone channels.

**Q670:** How does Tiletoria measure client satisfaction beyond the quarterly scorecard?
**A:** Beyond the scorecard, we conduct: annual relationship surveys, informal feedback collection after key milestones, NPS tracking, and executive-level relationship health checks. This multi-dimensional feedback provides a comprehensive view of satisfaction.

### Extended Coverage: Product & Technical Deep-Dive

**Q671:** What is the difference between through-body porcelain and glazed porcelain?
**A:** Through-body porcelain has consistent colour throughout the tile thickness — if chipped, the damage is less visible. Glazed porcelain has a coloured glaze over a different body — the glaze provides design and colour, but chips may expose the contrasting body. Through-body is preferred for heavy-duty commercial applications.

**Q672:** Can you provide tiles with metallic or textured surface finishes?
**A:** Yes. Our range includes tiles with metallic glazes (gold, silver, bronze effects), 3D textured surfaces, fabric-look textures, and concrete-effect finishes. These are popular for feature walls, reception areas, and brand-expression elements in flagship stores.

**Q673:** What is a "rectified" large-format tile, and why does it matter?
**A:** Rectified tiles are precision-cut after firing to achieve exact dimensions and sharp, square edges. This allows for very narrow grout joints (1.5–3mm), creating a seamless, premium aesthetic. For large-format tiles (600×600mm+), rectification is essential for professional installation.

**Q674:** Can you provide tiles suitable for underfloor heating systems?
**A:** Yes. Porcelain and ceramic tiles are excellent conductors of heat and are ideal for underfloor heating systems. We specify tiles with thermal compatibility for electric and hydronic underfloor heating, with installation guidelines for heated substrates.

**Q675:** What is the difference between PEI III, IV, and V ratings for our application?
**A:** PEI III is suitable for normal residential foot traffic. PEI IV is for moderate-to-heavy commercial traffic (standard retail stores). PEI V is for heavy commercial and industrial traffic (entrance areas, QSR kitchens, automotive workshops). We recommend PEI IV or V for all commercial applications.

**Q676:** Can you supply tiles with a R11 or R12 slip rating for commercial kitchens?
**A:** Yes. Tiles with R11 and R12 slip ratings (tested to DIN 51130) are available for commercial kitchens, food prep areas, and wet environments. These provide essential slip resistance where grease, water, and food spills create hazardous conditions.

**Q677:** What is the minimum grout joint width you recommend for commercial installations?
**A:** For rectified porcelain tiles in commercial installations, minimum 1.5–2mm grout joints are recommended. For non-rectified tiles, 3–5mm joints accommodate size variation. Joint width is specified in your national specification and must be maintained for warranty validity.

**Q678:** Can you provide tiles with a "soft" or warm underfoot feel for hospitality areas?
**A:** While tiles are inherently hard and cool, LVT (luxury vinyl tile) provides a warmer, softer underfoot feel that is popular in hospitality seating areas, hotel rooms, and boutique retail. LVT can be integrated into your specification for areas where comfort is a priority.

**Q679:** What is the difference between epoxy-based grout and cementitious grout?
**A:** Epoxy grout is chemical-resistant, stain-proof, and ideal for commercial kitchens and food service areas — but more expensive and harder to apply. Cementitious grout is economical, easy to apply, and suitable for most commercial areas — but requires sealing in stain-prone areas.

**Q680:** Can you provide tiles with nano-sealer pre-applied for stain resistance?
**A:** Yes. Some ranges come with factory-applied nano-sealer that provides stain resistance and reduces ongoing maintenance. This is particularly valuable for light-coloured tiles in food service and high-traffic areas where staining is a concern.

### Extended Coverage: Delivery & Operations Deep-Dive

**Q681:** How do you handle delivery to sites on Defence Force or government secure properties?
**A:** Secure property deliveries require advance security clearance for drivers and vehicles, submission of vehicle and ID documents, escort arrangements, and compliance with security protocols. We initiate clearance applications 2–3 weeks before the first scheduled delivery.

**Q682:** Can you deliver to offshore islands (e.g., Robben Island, leisure islands)?
**A:** Offshore deliveries require ferry or barge coordination and extended planning. We have experience with island deliveries and manage the multi-modal logistics (truck to port, ferry to island, truck on island) as a coordinated service.

**Q683:** How do you manage deliveries during the festive season shutdown?
**A:** Festive season delivery schedules are planned 2 months in advance. Last delivery dates, hub closure days, and first delivery dates in January are communicated to all sites. Emergency deliveries during shutdown are possible through skeleton staff arrangements at premium rates.

**Q684:** Can you provide dedicated on-site logistics support during large flagship openings?
**A:** Yes. On-site logistics support — including a Tiletoria representative managing deliveries, coordinating with contractors, and resolving material issues in real-time — can be provided for flagship store openings. This ensures material availability during the critical pre-opening period.

**Q685:** How do you handle reverse logistics (returns, collections) from our sites?
**A:** Reverse logistics are managed through: collection scheduling from sites, transport back to the nearest hub, inspection and credit processing, and restocking or disposal. Collection is typically coordinated with a scheduled delivery to the same site to minimise transport cost.

**Q686:** Can you deliver to sites with limited parking or loading bay space (e.g., high-street retail)?
**A:** High-street retail sites with limited parking require: smaller delivery vehicles, kerbside delivery with trolley transport, delivery during off-peak hours, and advance coordination with local traffic authorities if temporary parking dispensation is needed.

**Q687:** How do you manage delivery to sites with strict health and safety induction requirements?
**A:** Site-specific H&S induction requirements are captured in the site profile. Our drivers and delivery crews complete the required inductions, carry necessary PPE, and comply with site safety rules. Induction certificates are maintained on file for compliance.

**Q688:** Can you deliver to our sites in Swaziland (Eswatini) or Lesotho?
**A:** Cross-border deliveries to Eswatini and Lesotho are available with customs documentation and extended lead times. SADC certificates of origin, commercial invoices, and customs clearance are managed by your account manager as part of the cross-border delivery service.

**Q689:** How do you handle delivery to sites with no mobile phone signal for driver coordination?
**A:** For remote sites with poor mobile coverage, we establish alternative coordination: fixed-line contact numbers, pre-arranged delivery windows with no confirmation required, GPS coordinates for navigation, and communication through your regional office as backup.

**Q690:** Can you provide a delivery performance guarantee for our board presentation?
**A:** Yes. A delivery performance guarantee letter — committing to 95%+ on-time delivery, 99%+ invoice accuracy, and 99%+ quality acceptance — can be provided for your board presentation and contract file. This formalises our performance commitment.

### Extended Coverage: Quality & Warranty Deep-Dive

**Q691:** What is the difference between a manufacturing defect and an installation defect?
**A:** Manufacturing defects include: dimensional inaccuracy, colour variation beyond tolerance, crazing, warping, or structural flaws present at delivery. Installation defects include: lippage, hollow-sounding tiles, cracked tiles due to substrate movement, and staining from improper sealing. We assess cause objectively.

**Q692:** How do you handle claims for tiles that crack after installation?
**A:** Cracking claims are investigated to determine cause: manufacturing weakness, substrate movement, inadequate expansion joints, impact damage, or installation error. We conduct or commission independent assessment. Manufacturing-related cracking is replaced under warranty; installation-related cracking is referred to the contractor.

**Q693:** Can you provide independent laboratory testing if a quality dispute arises?
**A:** Yes. In the event of a quality dispute, independent testing by a SANAS-accredited laboratory can be arranged. The scope of testing, cost allocation, and acceptance criteria are agreed between parties. Test results are binding for dispute resolution purposes.

**Q694:** What documentation must our site teams retain for warranty claims?
**A:** For warranty claims, retain: delivery note, product batch number, photos of the defect, installation records (if available), and date of discovery. The more documentation available, the faster the claim can be processed. Your account manager provides a warranty claim template.

**Q695:** How does Tiletoria handle a systematic quality issue affecting multiple sites?
**A:** A systematic quality issue triggers an immediate response: batch traceability analysis, stock quarantine at all hubs, manufacturer engagement for root cause analysis, client notification, and replacement programme. Affected sites are prioritised for replacement to minimise operational impact.

**Q696:** Can you provide a quality acceptance certificate for each site at rollout completion?
**A:** Yes. A site quality acceptance certificate is issued upon completion, confirming: products delivered as specified, quantities verified, quality accepted, documentation complete, and warranty registered. This certificate supports project close-out and handover to operations.

**Q697:** What is Tiletoria's policy on shade variation between different production batches?
**A:** Shade variation between batches is managed through: calibrated colour measurement, batch-to-batch comparison before dispatch, and single-batch sourcing for large rollouts where critical. Acceptable shade variation is defined in the specification and agreed with your design team.

**Q698:** How do you ensure LVT products maintain their colour stability over time?
**A:** LVT colour stability is ensured through: UV-stable wear layers from reputable manufacturers, specification of appropriate products for the application (light-fastness rating), installation away from direct sunlight where possible, and warranty coverage for colour fade within the warranted period.

**Q699:** Can you provide a product traceability report showing the factory origin of every batch delivered?
**A:** Yes. A product traceability report is available on request, showing: manufacturer name, factory location, production date, batch number, incoming inspection date, and delivery date/site for every batch. This report supports quality audits and compliance requirements.

**Q700:** What quality assurance processes apply to installation materials (adhesives, grouts)?
**A:** Installation materials are sourced from reputable manufacturers with ISO 9001 certification. Shelf life is monitored through FIFO stock rotation. Outdated materials are removed from stock. Material datasheets and application guidelines are provided with every delivery.


### Extended Coverage: Maintenance, Lifecycle & Warranty (Continued)

**Q701:** Can you provide a national floor maintenance audit every 12 months?
**A:** Yes. An annual national floor maintenance audit is available for your full portfolio. Our technical team visits representative sites across all provinces, assesses floor condition, photographs wear patterns, and provides a comprehensive report with maintenance recommendations and refresh priorities.

**Q702:** How do we handle wax build-up on vinyl or LVT floors in our stores?
**A:** Wax build-up is addressed through periodic deep cleaning with a wax stripper solution, followed by reapplication of the appropriate polish or sealer. Your maintenance manual includes: recommended stripping products, dilution rates, application procedures, and safety precautions for your cleaning teams.

**Q703:** Can you provide spare stock storage and management for a 5-year period?
**A:** Yes. Long-term spare stock storage is available at our hubs. We recommend holding 3–5% spare stock from the original rollout batch for colour-matched repairs. This stock is stored in controlled conditions, managed on our system, and released on request throughout the warranty period.

**Q704:** What should we do if our cleaning contractor uses the wrong chemical on our floors?

**Q705:** Can you provide a floor condition grading system (A/B/C/D) for our portfolio?
**A:** Yes. A standardised floor condition grading system: Grade A (excellent, no action needed), Grade B (good, minor maintenance), Grade C (fair, maintenance required or refresh planned within 12 months), Grade D (poor, replacement recommended). This grading supports capital planning and maintenance prioritisation.

**Q706:** How do we handle efflorescence that keeps reappearing on our floor tiles?
**A:** Recurring efflorescence indicates a persistent moisture source behind or beneath the tile. Surface treatment provides temporary relief, but the root cause must be addressed: waterproofing failure, rising damp, or leaking services. We recommend a specialist moisture investigation to identify and resolve the source.

**Q707:** Can you provide emergency repair kits for our site managers to handle minor damage?
**A:** Yes. Emergency repair kits containing: spare tiles from the original batch, matching grout, adhesive, and basic tools can be provided to each site. These kits enable site managers to address minor chips or cracks immediately without waiting for a service call.

**Q708:** What is the expected lifespan of epoxy-based grout vs. cementitious grout in commercial use?
**A:** Epoxy grout typically lasts 15–20+ years in commercial use due to its chemical resistance and durability. Cementitious grout lasts 10–15 years with proper maintenance but may require resealing every 2–3 years in stain-prone areas. Epoxy is recommended for commercial kitchens and food service.
