**A:** Yes. We offer BIM training sessions (carrying CPD credits) covering: ProdLib navigation, Revit family parameter customisation, tile scheduling from BIM, and integration with NBS Chorus specification. Sessions are available at our Spec Lab or online.

**Q4.14:** Do your BIM objects work with NBS Chorus specification?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria BIM objects are compatible with NBS Chorus through API integration. Product selections in your BIM model can auto-populate specification clauses in NBS Chorus, maintaining consistency between model and specification.

**Q4.15:** Can I get point cloud data of installed Tiletoria projects for scan-to-BIM?

**A:** Selected completed projects have point cloud scans available for scan-to-BIM training and reference. Contact our professional services team to arrange access to scan data for educational purposes.

**Q4.16:** Do you provide COBie-compliant data in your BIM objects?

**A:** Yes. Revit families include COBie-compatible parameters for: manufacturer, model number, warranty duration, replacement date, and maintenance instructions. Data can be exported to .xlsx COBie format for asset handover.

**Q4.17:** Are Tiletoria BIM objects available on ARCAT?

**A:** Yes. Our BIM objects are also published on ARCAT (arcatspecs.com) for architects using the ARCAT specification platform. ARCAT objects include linked 3-Part Guide specifications and CAD details.

**Q4.18:** Can I use Tiletoria BIM objects in SketchUp?

**A:** While we don't provide native SketchUp components, our Revit families can be exported to .ifc format and imported into SketchUp. Alternatively, our 3D .dwg models can be imported directly into SketchUp Pro.

**Q4.19:** Do you provide rendering materials for Enscape, Lumion, or V-Ray?

**A:** Yes. High-resolution texture maps and rendering materials are available for: Enscape (custom asset format), Lumion (.mtl files), and V-Ray (VRayMtl presets). Materials include diffuse, bump, reflection, and normal maps at 4K resolution.

**Q4.20:** How do I report a bug or issue with a BIM object?

**A:** Report BIM object issues via email to our digital team or through the ProdLib feedback function. We typically resolve reported issues within 48 hours and issue updated object files within 5 working days.

**Q4.21:** Can your BIM objects be customised for our practice's graphic standards?

**A:** Yes. Revit families use shared parameters and can be adapted to your practice's template. Our BIM team provides a 60-minute template integration session to align Tiletoria objects with your graphic standards and parameter mapping.

**Q4.22:** Do you provide BIM objects for tile accessories like movement joint profiles?

**A:** Yes. BIM objects for accessories include: movement joint profiles (aluminium, brass, stainless steel), transition strips, drain grates, coving pieces, and stair nosing. Each includes accurate dimensions and material specifications.

**Q4.23:** Can I see a sample BIM model showing Tiletoria product integration?

**A:** Yes. We provide sample Revit project files demonstrating: multi-room tile layouts, pattern variations, transition detailing, and schedule generation. These are available for download from the Spec Lab portal as training resources.

**Q4.24:** Do your BIM objects include installation sequence information?

**A:** BIM objects include installation notes in the type comments parameter. For detailed installation sequencing, link to our method statement documents or request a 4D BIM sequence from our technical team.

**Q4.25:** Is there a mobile app for accessing Tiletoria BIM objects on site?

**A:** While we don't have a dedicated mobile app, ProdLib objects can be accessed via mobile browser on tablet devices. Our technical data sheets are also available as downloadable PDFs for offline access on site visits.

**Q4.26:** Can your BIM team collaborate with our BIM manager on a live project?

**A:** Yes. For projects over R10 million in tile value, our BIM coordinator can join your project BIM execution team, attend BIM coordination meetings, and ensure tile model elements integrate correctly with architecture, structure, and MEP models.

**Q4.27:** Do you provide clash detection support for complex tile installations?

**A:** Yes. Our technical team can review clash detection reports involving tile elements, particularly at: drainage intersections, movement joint locations, service penetrations, and transition details. We provide resolution recommendations and revised detail drawings.

**Q4.28:** Can Tiletoria BIM objects export to Excel for quantity schedules?

**A:** Yes. Revit schedules populated with Tiletoria parameters can be exported directly to Excel for quantity surveyor pricing, procurement planning, and order placement. The export includes product codes for direct ordering reference.

**Q4.29:** Do you provide digital mood boards and material palettes?

**A:** Yes. Our digital material palette tool allows you to create and export mood boards combining tile selections, colour palettes, and finish combinations. Exports are available as PDF, PNG, and PowerPoint for client presentations.

**Q4.30:** Can I integrate Tiletoria BIM objects into our practice's central library?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria BIM objects can be loaded into your practice's central Revit library or BIM 360/ACC content management system. We provide a batch download option for the complete object library (approximately 2.5GB).

**Q4.31:** Do you provide specification writing plugins that link to BIM?

**A:** Our NBS Chorus integration allows specification clauses to be linked directly to BIM objects. When the object is selected in Revit, the corresponding NBS specification clause auto-populates. Setup support is provided by our digital team.

**Q4.32:** Are your BIM objects compatible with Vectorworks?

**A:** Tiletoria BIM objects in .ifc format can be imported into Vectorworks. While we don't provide native Vectorworks objects, the .ifc import preserves geometry, parameters, and material assignments. Our team can assist with Vectorworks workflow optimisation.

**Q4.33:** Can I get a virtual reality walkthrough of a Tiletoria-specified project?

**A:** For select completed projects, VR walkthroughs are available via Enscape or Twinmotion exports. Contact our marketing team to arrange a VR demonstration of relevant project types (hospitality, residential, commercial).

**Q4.34:** Do you provide CAD details for common tile installation conditions?

**A:** Yes. Our CAD detail library includes: floor sections, wall sections, movement joint details, drainage details, coving details, transition details, and skirting details. All details are available in .dwg, .dxf, .pdf, and Revit detail component formats.

**Q4.35:** How do I stay updated on new BIM object releases?

**A:** Subscribe to the Spec Lab BIM newsletter, follow our LinkedIn page, or join our quarterly BIM user group. New releases are announced with webinar demonstrations and training session bookings.

**Q4.36:** Can your BIM objects help with acoustic calculations?

**A:** BIM objects include acoustic performance parameters (impact sound reduction, airborne sound reduction) where tested. These values can be extracted into acoustic calculation spreadsheets for building regulation compliance.

**Q4.37:** Do you provide digital sample boards that can be embedded in presentations?

**A:** Yes. Digital sample boards are available as high-resolution PDFs and PowerPoint slides showing: tile samples in context, colour variations, finish options, and installed project photographs. These can be embedded directly into client presentations.

**Q4.38:** Can Tiletoria provide a digital twin of our project for FM handover?

**A:** For projects with comprehensive BIM deliverables, we provide tile-specific COBie data and asset information to populate your digital twin/FM platform. This includes: product codes, warranty dates, maintenance schedules, and replacement procedures.

**Q4.39:** Do you have API access for integrating Tiletoria data into our practice software?

**A:** We are developing API access for practice management and specification software integration. Contact our digital team for early access to the Tiletoria API for product data, pricing, and availability queries.

**Q4.40:** Can I download the entire Tiletoria BIM library at once?

**A:** Yes. A complete library download (.zip) is available for registered specifiers via the Spec Lab portal. The full library is approximately 2.5GB and includes all formats: Revit, ArchiCAD, AutoCAD, and .ifc.

**Q4.41:** Do you provide BIM coordination matrices for tile subcontractors?

**A:** Yes. For contractor BIM coordination, we provide: model element breakdown, LOD matrices, information delivery schedules, and clash detection priorities. These help tile subcontractors deliver compliant BIM elements during construction.

**Q4.42:** Are your BIM objects available on the NBS National BIM Library?

**A:** Our BIM objects are distributed primarily through ProdLib and ARCAT. NBS National BIM Library availability is planned for 2025. In the interim, ProdLib objects integrate seamlessly with NBS Chorus specification workflows.

**Q4.43:** Can I get a custom BIM object developed for a special-order tile?

**A:** Yes. For special-order or custom-manufactured tiles, our digital team can develop bespoke BIM objects within 10 working days. Provide the product dimensions, finish details, and required format for custom object development.

**Q4.44:** Do you provide guidance on BIM object naming conventions?

**A:** Yes. Our BIM objects follow the UK BIM Framework naming convention (Uniclass 2015) and can be adapted to your practice's naming protocol. A naming convention guide is included with the library download.

**Q4.45:** How do Tiletoria BIM objects handle pattern and layout variations?

**A:** Revit families include type parameters for: laying pattern (stretcher, herringbone, basketweave, modular), joint width, joint colour, and laying direction. Pattern preview updates in the family editor and plan views.

**Q4.46:** Can I use Tiletoria BIM objects in openBIM (.ifc) workflows?

**A:** Yes. All Tiletoria BIM objects can be exported to .ifc format with preserved geometry, materials, and property sets. The .ifc export is tested for compatibility with Solibri, BIMcollab, and SimpleBIM model checking software.


## Category 5: Sample & Mock-Up Services

**Q5.1:** Does Tiletoria provide full-size sample boards for architect review?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab maintains a comprehensive Tile Library with full-size sample boards (typically 600x600mm or larger) of our complete product range. Samples can be viewed at our Menlyn showroom or loaned to your practice for project evaluation.

**Q5.2:** Can I borrow samples to present to my client?

**A:** Yes. Our sample loan service allows you to borrow full-size tiles, sample boards, and mosaic sheets for client presentations. Loan periods are typically 2 weeks, extendable on request. A signed loan agreement covers care and return conditions.

**Q5.3:** Does Tiletoria create mock-up panels for large projects?

**A:** Yes. For projects over R3 million in tile value, we produce full-scale mock-up panels showing: tile layout, joint width and colour, pattern arrangement, transition details, and accessory integration. Mock-ups are produced at our Johannesburg facility or on-site by arrangement.

**Q5.4:** What is the typical lead time for a mock-up panel?

**A:** Standard mock-up panels require 10-15 working days from confirmed selection. Complex mock-ups with multiple patterns, inlays, or custom-cut elements require 3-4 weeks. Rush service is available for tender submissions at 5-7 working days.

**Q5.5:** Can you produce a mock-up showing the tile in its intended lighting conditions?

**A:** Yes. We recommend evaluating samples and mock-ups under the project's actual lighting conditions — both natural and artificial. Our Spec Lab has adjustable lighting (2700K-6500K) to simulate different environments, and samples can be loaned for on-site lighting evaluation.

**Q5.6:** Do you provide architect approval samples (AAS) for contract purposes?

**A:** Yes. Architect Approval Samples are provided as part of our contract support package. AAS samples are signed and dated by both the architect and Tiletoria, then sealed as the quality benchmark for site delivery and installation.

**Q5.7:** Can you create a visual mock-up for a client presentation board?

**A:** Yes. We produce presentation-quality visual mock-ups including: mounted sample boards with product labels, room-set photography, material palette cards, and comparative finish options. These are supplied in protective cases for transport to client meetings.

**Q5.8:** Is there a cost for sample boards and mock-ups?

**A:** Sample boards for viewing at the Spec Lab are free. Loan samples are free for registered architects. Mock-up panels for projects are provided free of charge for projects over R3 million tile value. For smaller projects, mock-up costs are credited against the first order.

**Q5.9:** Can you produce a room-set mock-up showing the complete installed effect?

**A:** Yes. Our Spec Lab features permanent room-set displays including: bathroom mock-ups, kitchen splashback displays, lobby floor vignettes, and hotel room settings. Custom room-set mock-ups can be arranged for major projects by appointment.

**Q5.10:** Do you provide sample boxes for overseas client presentations?

**A:** Yes. International presentation sample boxes are available for architects working with overseas clients or design teams. Boxes include: sample tiles, technical data sheets, care instructions, and product catalogues. Courier documentation is provided for international shipping.

**Q5.11:** Can I order duplicate sample sets for multiple project stakeholders?

**A:** Yes. We can produce multiple identical sample sets for: architect, interior designer, client, QS, main contractor, and subcontractor. Bulk sample sets are discounted for projects over R5 million.

**Q5.12:** Do you provide wet samples for slip resistance testing verification?

**A:** Yes. Wet slip resistance verification samples are available for projects requiring independent testing. Samples are supplied with known DIN 51130 classifications and can be sent to your nominated testing laboratory for confirmation.

**Q5.13:** Can you retain samples from the actual production batch for contract reference?

**A:** Yes. For contract orders, we arrange batch retention samples that are sealed and stored for the contract duration. These serve as the reference standard for colour, shade, and calibre consistency throughout the project.

**Q5.14:** Do you provide sample rails or display systems for our studio library?

**A:** Yes. Sample rail systems, desktop sample stands, and mobile sample trolleys are available for architectural practices maintaining an in-house material library. These can be branded and stocked with your preferred Tiletoria selections.

**Q5.15:** Can I see large-format tiles (1200x2600mm) as full-size samples?

**A:** Yes. Full-size large-format samples are displayed at the Spec Lab. For loan, we provide manageable cutting samples (typically 600x600mm or 600x1200mm sections) that represent the full-size product's finish, colour, and texture accurately.

**Q5.16:** Do you provide sample cards with technical data printed on them?

**A:** Yes. All sample cards include: product code, description, size, thickness, PEI rating, slip resistance, water absorption, breaking strength, and SANS 1449 classification. QR codes link to the full technical data sheet online.

**Q5.17:** Can you produce a mock-up showing joint colour variations?

**A:** Yes. Joint colour comparison mock-ups show the same tile with 3-4 different grout colours, helping visualise the impact of joint colour on the overall appearance. This is particularly valuable for large-format tiles where joint width is minimal.


**Q5.19:** Can I get a sample board showing all colour options in a range?

**A:** Yes. Colour range sample boards display all available colourways of a particular tile range on a single board, typically 1200x800mm. This enables quick colour comparison and selection for design development.

**Q5.20:** Do you provide samples of discontinued lines for matching renovation work?

**A:** Where possible, we retain archive samples of discontinued lines for 2-3 years after discontinuation. Contact our archive team with the original product code — we may have retention samples or can suggest the closest current match.

**Q5.21:** Can you produce a mock-up panel with actual lighting installed?

**A:** Yes. Illuminated mock-up panels with integrated LED lighting (2700K, 3000K, 4000K, 6000K) are available for evaluating tile appearance under different colour temperatures. Essential for hospitality and retail projects where lighting design is critical.

**Q5.22:** Do you provide samples for fire resistance testing?

**A:** Yes. Fire testing samples (typically 100x100mm or as required by the test standard) can be supplied for independent fire resistance testing. Samples are provided with material composition declarations for SANS 10400 Part T compliance.

**Q5.23:** Can I get samples delivered to my practice?

**A:** Yes. We offer free sample delivery within Johannesburg, Pretoria, Durban, and Cape Town metropolitan areas. Outlying areas are serviced via courier at cost. Same-day delivery is available in Gauteng for orders placed before 10:00.

**Q5.24:** Do you provide digital sample books for preliminary selection?

**A:** Yes. Digital sample books (.pdf) with high-resolution product photography, colour swatches, and technical summaries are available for initial project scoping. These are useful for early-stage design development before requesting physical samples.

**Q5.25:** Can you create a sample board showing different laying patterns?

**A:** Yes. Pattern demonstration boards show the same tile laid in different patterns: stretcher bond, herringbone, basketweave, modular (French pattern), and random plank layouts. These help visualise the design impact of different laying configurations.

**Q5.26:** Do you provide samples of your LVT and laminate flooring ranges?

**A:** Yes. LVT and laminate samples are available as A4-sized planks, sample boxes with 3-4 plank pieces, and full-size plank samples (typically 1200x180mm or 1800x220mm). Samples show click system profiles and wear layer detail.

**Q5.27:** Can I keep samples in my practice's material library permanently?

**A:** Yes. After project completion, loan samples can be purchased at nominal cost (R50-R200 per sample depending on size) for retention in your material library. This builds your practice's tile reference collection for future projects.

**Q5.28:** Do you provide sample display stands for showroom presentations?

**A:** Yes. Professional sample display stands, countertop sample holders, and wall-mounted sample frames are available for practices and showrooms. These can be customised with your practice branding.

**Q5.29:** Can you produce mock-ups showing different grout joint widths?

**A:** Yes. Joint width comparison mock-ups display the same tile with 1.5mm, 2mm, 3mm, and 5mm joints, helping visualise the aesthetic and practical impact of joint width selection.

**Q5.30:** Do you provide samples of tile accessories (profiles, drains, coving)?

**A:** Yes. Accessory samples include: aluminium and brass movement joint profiles (150mm lengths), stainless steel drain grates, PVC and porcelain coving pieces, transition strips, and stair nosing profiles. Samples show finish quality and dimensional accuracy.

**Q5.31:** Can you create a travelling sample case for a design competition submission?

**A:** Yes. We produce custom travelling sample cases for competition submissions and award entries. Cases are lightweight, protective, and professionally presented with product labels and technical summaries.

**Q5.32:** Do you provide samples suitable for a chemical resistance test?

**A:** Yes. Chemical resistance testing samples are supplied with recommended test protocols (ISO 10545-13) and can be sent to your nominated laboratory. We provide pre-test and post-test comparison guidance.

**Q5.33:** Can I get samples of your mosaic range in sheet format?

**A:** Yes. Mosaic samples are provided as full sheets (typically 300x300mm) showing mesh backing, chip arrangement, and grout joint spacing. Individual loose chips are also available for colour matching with other materials.

**Q5.34:** Do you provide archive samples for post-completion defect assessment?

**A:** Yes. For contract orders over R5 million, we archive batch samples for 5 years post-installation. These can be used for: colour fade comparison, defect assessment, insurance claims, and warranty dispute resolution.

**Q5.35:** Can you produce a mock-up showing tile-to-tile transitions?

**A:** Yes. Transition mock-up panels demonstrate: same-level transitions between different tiles, ramped transitions for wheelchair access, profiled transitions (Z-bar, T-bar), and expansion joint crossings. Each detail is fully annotated.

**Q5.36:** Do you provide samples with different surface finishes of the same tile?

**A:** Yes. For ranges available in multiple finishes (polished, matt, structured, grip, lappato), we provide comparison boards showing all finish options of the same colourway side-by-side.

**Q5.37:** Can I get samples delivered directly to a client meeting venue?

**A:** Yes. Samples can be couriered directly to client meeting venues, hotel conference rooms, or site offices. We coordinate delivery timing to ensure samples arrive before your presentation.

**Q5.38:** Do you provide waterproofing membrane samples for compatibility testing?

**A:** Yes. Membrane samples (liquid-applied and sheet types) are available for adhesion testing with your specified tiles and substrates. We provide recommended adhesive brands and primers based on membrane compatibility testing.

**Q5.39:** Can you create a sample set for a design charrette or workshop?

**A:** Yes. Design charrette sample sets include: 20-30 varied tile samples in a portable case, colour wheel references, finish comparison cards, and technical selection guides. Ideal for early-stage design workshops with client participation.

**Q5.40:** Do you provide sample photography for inclusion in our presentation boards?

**A:** Yes. High-resolution sample photography (white background, consistent lighting, scale reference) is available for all products. Images are provided license-free for your project presentations and competition submissions.

**Q5.41:** Can I get a sample of tile trim and edge finishing profiles?

**A:** Yes. Edge trim samples in aluminium, PVC, and stainless steel are available in various colours (silver, white, black, brass-effect, chrome). Samples show profile shape, finish quality, and typical application.

**Q5.42:** Do you provide samples showing tile variation within a batch (V-rating)?

**A:** Yes. V-rating demonstration sets show: V1 (uniform), V2 (slight variation), V3 (moderate variation), and V4 (substantial variation) using actual tiles. This helps manage client expectations regarding natural material appearance.


## Category 6: Product Range & Innovation

**Q6.1:** What imported tile collections does Tiletoria offer for high-end architectural projects?

**A:** Tiletoria imports premium collections from leading international manufacturers in Italy (our imported Italian tile ranges, our imported tile ranges, our imported tile ranges), Spain (our imported Spanish tile ranges, our imported tile ranges, Inalco), France, Turkey, Brazil, and India. Collections include large-format porcelain-look, stone-look, concrete-look, wood-look, and designer mosaics.

**Q6.2:** What is the largest tile format available from Tiletoria?

**A:** Our largest standard format is 1600x3200mm in 6mm and 12mm thicknesses (sintered stone / porcelain slabs). We also stock 1200x2600mm, 1200x1200mm, and 800x1800mm formats. Custom cutting from large slabs is available for bespoke dimensions.

**Q6.3:** Do you offer-look porcelain that convincingly replicates natural?

**A:** Yes. Our-look porcelain collections feature high-definition inkjet printing with 30+ face variations, veining continuity across tiles, book-matching capability, and authentic surface textures. Collections replicate-look porcelain-look porcelain-look porcelain, Marquina, and Brecciavarieties.

**Q6.4:** What stone-look porcelain ranges do you have for commercial projects?


**Q6.5:** Do you offer concrete-look porcelain tiles?

**A:** Yes. Our concrete-look porcelain ranges capture the raw industrial aesthetic with through-body colour, realistic surface texture, and matte finishes. Available in large formats (600x1200mm, 750x1500mm, 1200x1200mm) with matching structural tiles for heavy-duty applications.

**Q6.6:** What encaustic pattern tiles does Tiletoria stock?

**A:** We offer both traditional cement encaustic tiles and porcelain encaustic-look tiles with traditional and contemporary patterns. Porcelain versions offer superior stain resistance and durability for high-traffic commercial applications.

**Q6.7:** Do you have textured and 3D surface tiles for feature walls?

**A:** Yes. Our decorative range includes: 3D relief tiles, ribbed/fluted tiles, woven patterns, geometric textures, and sculptural surface tiles. These are ideal for hotel lobbies, restaurant feature walls, retail facades, and residential statement walls.

**Q6.8:** What wood-look porcelain ranges are available?

**A:** Our wood-look porcelain collections span: traditional plank formats (150x900mm, 200x1200mm), wide planks (300x1800mm), herringbone modules, and deck board formats (outdoor 600x1200mm). Species replicated include oak, walnut, teak, ash, and exotic hardwoods.

**Q6.9:** Do you offer terrazzo-look porcelain tiles?

**A:** Yes. Our terrazzo-look porcelain captures the classic Italian aesthetic with realistic chip inclusions in a cement matrix. Available in traditional and large formats (600x600mm, 600x1200mm, 750x1500mm) with matt and polished finishes.

**Q6.10:** What mosaic options does Tiletoria offer?

**A:** Our mosaic range includes: glass mosaics (iridescent, frosted, metallic), stone mosaics (, pebble), porcelain mosaics, metal mosaics (stainless steel, copper), mixed material mosaics, and custom mosaic patterns. Mesh-mounted and paper-faced options available.

**Q6.11:** Do you have anti-slip commercial tiles for kitchens and wet areas?

**A:** Yes. Our commercial grip tiles achieve R11-R12 slip resistance (DIN 51130) with structured, abrasive, or textured surfaces. These are specified for commercial kitchens, food processing plants, pool surrounds, and external ramps.

**Q6.12:** What is the price range for your premium imported porcelain tiles?

**A:** Premium imported porcelain from Italy and Spain typically ranges from R450/m² to R1,800/m² depending on format, brand, and collection. Large-format sintered stone slabs (1600x3200mm) range from R1,200/m² to R3,500/m². Budget imported ranges start from R280/m².

**Q6.13:** Do you offer custom colour matching for project-specific requirements?

**A:** For large projects (500m²+ per colour), our manufacturing partners offer custom colour development. Custom colour matching requires 12-16 weeks lead time and minimum order quantities. Colour development samples are provided for architect approval before production.

**Q6.14:** What new product launches are planned for the next 6 months?

**A:** New collection launches are previewed to registered specifiers 30 days before public release. Upcoming launches typically include: new large-format sizes, updated stonereplications, sustainable collections with recycled content, and innovative surface textures. Contact the Spec Lab to join our preview list.

**Q6.15:** Do you offer tiles specifically designed for facade and external wall cladding?

**A:** Yes. Our facade-specific porcelain ranges include: ventilated facade tiles, adhesive-fixed external tiles, rainscreen cladding panels, and structural fixing systems. These achieve A1 fire rating, frost resistance, and wind load compliance for high-rise applications.

**Q6.16:** What is 'sintered stone' and how does it differ from traditional porcelain?

**A:** Sintered stone (also called ultra-compact surfacing) is produced from 100% mineral powders subjected to extreme pressure (>25,000 tons) and temperature (>1,200°C). It offers: larger formats (up to 1600x3200mm), thinner profiles (3-6mm), higher flexural strength (60+ MPa), and UV stability for external applications.

**Q6.17:** Do you offer tiles with metallic and iridescent finishes?

**A:** Yes. Our decorative range includes: metallic-finish porcelain (gold, silver, bronze, copper effects), iridescent glaze tiles, pearl-effect tiles, and tiles with precious metal inlays. These are specified for luxury hospitality, retail, and residential feature applications.

**Q6.18:** What outdoor paving solutions does Tiletoria offer?

**A:** Outdoor paving options include: 20mm thick structural porcelain pavers (800x800mm, 600x1200mm), pedestal-supported paving systems, ramped edge tiles, anti-slip pool surrounds (R11-R12), and permeable paving solutions. All outdoor tiles are frost-resistant and UV-stable.

**Q6.19:** Do you have tiles suitable for industrial and manufacturing facilities?

**A:** Yes. Our industrial tile range includes: technical porcelain with PEI 5 and abrasion volume ≤80mm³, acid-resistant tiles for chemical exposure, anti-static tiles for electronics manufacturing, and heavy-duty coved skirting systems. Technical data sheets include chemical resistance matrices.

**Q6.20:** What is the smallest mosaic chip size you offer?

**A:** Our smallest mosaic chip is 10x10mm (glass mosaic). Other small formats include 15x15mm, 20x20mm, and 23x23mm. These are available in glass, stone, porcelain, and mixed materials for intricate designs and curved surface applications.

**Q6.21:** Do you offer book-matched-look porcelain for statement walls?

**A:** Yes. Our premium-look collections offer book-matched sets where four consecutive tiles create a mirror-image pattern replicating naturalveining. Book-matched sets are available in 1200x2600mm and 1600x3200mm formats.

**Q6.22:** Can I get tiles with custom printing or digital imagery?

**A:** Yes. Digital custom printing on porcelain tiles is available for projects over 200m². Custom imagery, logos, patterns, or photographs can be printed at up to 1200 DPI resolution. Custom print tiles are suitable for feature walls, commercial branding, and public art installations.

**Q6.23:** Do you offer tiles with integrated LED lighting?

**A:** While we don't manufacture LED-integrated tiles, we partner with lighting designers to create tile systems with LED strip integration channels, backlighting panels behind translucent glass tiles, and illuminated mosaic features. Technical coordination drawings are provided.

**Q6.24:** What is the thinnest large-format tile you offer?

**A:** Our thinnest large-format tiles are 3mm slim porcelain panels (1000x3000mm and 1200x2600mm) designed for lightweight facade cladding, furniture surfacing, and overlay applications. Despite their slim profile, they achieve flexural strengths exceeding 50 MPa.

**Q6.25:** Do you offer tiles with recycled glass or eco-friendly content?

**A:** Yes. Our sustainable collections include tiles with 20-40% recycled content (pre-consumer porcelain waste, recycled glass). These collections carry EPDs and contribute to Green Star SA material category points. Recycled content is third-party verified.

**Q6.26:** What is the difference between glazed and full-body porcelain tiles?

**A:** Glazed porcelain has a coloured glaze layer over a porcelain body — the glaze provides aesthetic design while the body provides structural strength. Full-body (through-body) porcelain has consistent colour throughout the tile thickness, making edge chips less visible and providing higher abrasion resistance for commercial applications.

**Q6.27:** Do you have tiles suitable for heritage and period restoration projects?

**A:** Yes. Our heritage collection includes: Victorian-pattern encaustic tiles, traditional subway tiles, period-appropriate terrazzo, classicmosaics, and reproduction Art Deco patterns. These are specified for heritage building restoration and conservation projects requiring SAHRA/PHRA approval.

**Q6.28:** Can you supply tiles for steam room and sauna applications?

**A:** Yes. Steam room tiles must be: frost-resistant, chemically resistant to steam cleaning, and installed with waterproof adhesive and grout on waterproof substrates. We provide steam room-specific specification packages including substrate build-up and ventilation requirements.

**Q6.29:** Do you offer tiles with antimicrobial properties?

**A:** Yes. Selected ranges incorporate silver-ion or photocatalytic ( titanium dioxide) antimicrobial technology, achieving >99% bacterial reduction. These are specified for hospitals, clinics, food preparation areas, and high-hygiene commercial kitchens.

**Q6.30:** What is the lead time for imported tile orders?

**A:** Standard imported tile orders have a 10-14 week lead time from order confirmation to arrival in South Africa. Stocked imported items are available from Johannesburg warehouse within 3-5 days. Air freight options are available at premium cost for urgent requirements.

**Q6.31:** Do you offer tiles for raised computer floor systems?

**A:** Yes. We supply tiles compatible with standard 600x600mm raised access floor systems. Tiles are 10-12mm thick porcelain with point load ratings suitable for office environments. Finish options include anti-static treatments for data centre applications.

**Q6.32:** Can I see the full product range online before visiting the showroom?

**A:** Yes. Our complete product catalogue is available on the Tiletoria website with: product photography, technical data sheets, size availability, price bands, and stock status. The Spec Lab virtual tour provides a 360° walkthrough of our physical showroom displays.

**Q6.33:** Do you offer tiles with through-colour body for heavy traffic?

**A:** Yes. Our full-body technical porcelain tiles have through-body colour that maintains appearance even as the surface wears. These are specified for: airports, railway stations, shopping malls, and industrial facilities where long-term appearance retention is critical.

**Q6.34:** What decorative border and listello options do you offer?

**A:** Our decorative accessory range includes: pencil listellos, border tiles, decorative inserts, feature strips, and coordinated mosaics. Many ranges have matching border options for creating classical border layouts and feature panel designs.

**Q6.35:** Do you have tiles that replicate handmade or artisan ceramics?

**A:** Yes. Our artisan-look collections replicate the character of handmade tiles with: deliberate surface irregularities, varied glaze application, organic edge profiles, and traditional colour palettes. Available in subway, square, and decorative formats for residential and hospitality projects.


## Category 7: Wet Area & Waterproofing Specifications

**Q7.1:** What waterproofing systems does Tiletoria specify for wet areas?

**A:** Tiletoria specifies complete waterproofing systems including: cementitious crystalline membranes (Xypex, leading adhesive manufacturers), liquid-applied polyurethane membranes (leading adhesive manufacturers, Sika), bituminous sheet membranes, epoxy coatings, and combination systems. System selection depends on: substrate type, traffic, chemical exposure, and warranty requirements.

**Q7.2:** Does Tiletoria provide wet area detail drawings for bathrooms?

**A:** Yes. Our wet area detail library includes: shower floor-to-wall junctions, shower curb details, bathtub surrounds, waterproofing termination at door thresholds, floor waste integration, and hobless shower details. All details are SANS 10400 Part P (drainage) and Part B (structural) compliant.

**Q7.3:** What is the minimum waterproofing warranty period available?

**A:** Our waterproofing system warranties range from 7 years (standard cementitious system) to 25 years (full liquid-applied membrane system with accredited installation). The warranty covers membrane integrity and, on full systems, consequential damage cover.

**Q7.4:** Do you provide SANS 10400 Part P compliant wet area specifications?

**A:** Yes. All our wet area specifications comply with SANS 10400 Part P (drainage disposal). We provide: floor fall requirements (1:50 to 1:100), waterproofing coverage extents (minimum 150mm up walls), floor waste specifications, and testing requirements (48-hour flood test).

**Q7.5:** Can you recommend a waterproofing system for a commercial shower facility?

**A:** For commercial showers (gyms, hostels, mines), we recommend: liquid-applied polyurethane membrane (1.5mm thick), reinforced at junctions with polyester fabric, full floor and 180mm wall coverage, with epoxy-based grout and slip-resistant porcelain tiles (R11). System warranty: 10 years.

**Q7.6:** What is the correct substrate preparation for wet area tiling?

**A:** Wet area substrates require: structural concrete or properly cured screed (28 days minimum), surface hardness ≥25 MPa, flatness ±3mm/2m, cleanliness (no laitance, oil, or contaminants), and priming before membrane application. Our method statements detail each step.

**Q7.7:** Do you specify waterproofing behind wall tiles in showers?

**A:** Yes. We specify waterproofing behind all shower wall tiles, extending minimum 1800mm above finished floor level or to shower head height plus 150mm (whichever is greater). For hobless showers, waterproofing extends across the entire bathroom floor and 150mm up all walls.

**Q7.8:** Can Tiletoria provide a complete wet area system warranty?

**A:** Yes. Our Complete Wet Area System Warranty covers: waterproofing membrane, tiles, adhesive, and grout as an integrated system. Available only when all components are specified from our approved system list and installed by a Tiletoria-accredited waterproofing contractor.

**Q7.9:** What drainage solutions do you offer for tiled wet areas?

**A:** Our drainage range includes: stainless steel floor wastes (square and round), linear drains (300-1800mm), tile-insert drains (invisible grate), balcony drains, deck drains, and commercial-grade channel drains. All drains are compatible with our waterproofing systems.

**Q7.10:** Do you have details for curbless (zero-threshold) showers?

**A:** Yes. Our curbless shower detail set includes: recessed floor tray with falls to drain, waterproofing continuity across floor/wall junction, linear drain placement options, slip-resistant tile specification (R10-R11), and door track transition details. Full compliance with SANS 10400 accessibility requirements.

**Q7.11:** What is the recommended tile type for swimming pool interiors?

**A:** For pool interiors, we recommend: porcelain mosaic tiles (20x20mm or 48x48mm on mesh), water absorption ≤0.5%, frost-resistant, chemical-resistant to chlorine and salt, and DIN 51097 Class B or C slip resistance. We supply mesh-backed sheets for efficient installation.

**Q7.12:** Do you provide specifications for sauna and steam room waterproofing?


**Q7.13:** What is the correct waterproofing for balconies and terraces?

**A:** leading adhesive manufacturerscony waterproofing specifications include: liquid-applied or sheet membrane with UV stability, proper drainage falls (1:50-1:100), overflow drains, perimeter upstands (minimum 150mm), movement joints at perimeter and drainage points, and frost-resistant porcelain tiles.

**Q7.14:** Can you provide a waterproofing specification for a green roof with tiles?

**A:** Green roof tile specifications include: root-resistant waterproofing membrane, protection board, drainage layer, growing medium, and paver support system for walkway tiles. Our structural team can advise on loading calculations for saturated green roof conditions.

**Q7.15:** Do you specify waterproofing for plant rooms and service areas?

**A:** Yes. Plant room specifications include: chemical-resistant epoxy or polyurethane membrane, resistance to oils and grease, bunding details for equipment containment, floor drainage with gully traps, and heavy-duty porcelain tiles (PEI 5, R11-R12).

**Q7.16:** What is the flood test requirement after waterproofing installation?

**A:** SANS 10400 Part P requires a 48-hour flood test after membrane curing (typically 3-7 days after application). The flood test must be witnessed and signed off before tile installation proceeds. Our method statements include flood test protocols and documentation templates.

**Q7.17:** Can you provide a wet area specification for a hotel with 200 rooms?

**A:** Yes. For large hotel projects, we provide standardised wet area specifications covering: standard guest bathrooms (shower over bath or walk-in shower), accessible rooms (SANS 10400-S compliant), suites (separate tub and shower), and back-of-house wet areas. Standardisation reduces cost and simplifies procurement.

**Q7.18:** Do you have waterproofing details for movement joints in wet areas?

**A:** Yes. Wet area movement joint details show: waterproofing continuity across the joint, flexible joint sealant selection, stainless steel or PVC cover profiles, and drainage management. These are critical at structural movement joints and large room perimeters.

**Q7.19:** What tile adhesive is recommended for wet area installations?

**A:** For wet areas, we specify: C2TES1 (improved cementitious adhesive, deformable, slip-resistant, extended open time) for floor porcelain; C2TE for wall porcelain; and reaction resin (R2/R3) adhesive for submerged applications (pools, fountains). SANS 10107 compliance is mandatory.

**Q7.20:** Do you provide specifications for waterproofing in basements and retaining walls?

**A:** Yes. Below-grade waterproofing specifications include: negative-side cementitious crystalline systems, positive-side bituminous membranes, cavity drainage systems, and tanking details for water-retaining structures. These integrate with internal floor finishes including tiles.

**Q7.21:** Can Tiletoria provide details for tile fixing to waterproof plasterboard?

**A:** Yes. Our wet area wall details cover: fibre-cement or moisture-resistant plasterboard substrate, priming, waterproofing membrane application, and tile fixing with C2TES1 adhesive. Maximum tile weight and height limitations are specified for each board type.

**Q7.22:** What is the recommended fall for a shower floor?

**A:** The minimum fall for a shower floor is 1:50 (2%) toward the floor waste. For large walk-in showers (1200x1200mm+), falls of 1:40 (2.5%) ensure effective drainage. Our detail drawings show screeding techniques to achieve uniform falls without ponding.

**Q7.23:** Do you specify waterproofing for kitchen and food preparation areas?

**A:** Yes. Commercial kitchen waterproofing specifications include: full floor waterproofing extending 150mm up walls, coved skirting (100-150mm) for hygiene, floor drainage with gully traps, falls (1:50-1:100), and chemical-resistant membrane for cleaning agent exposure.

**Q7.24:** Can you provide a NHBRC-compliant wet area specification for new homes?

**A:** Yes. Our NHBRC wet area specification covers: all bathroom and shower areas, screed preparation, waterproofing application, tiling, and testing. This specification is pre-approved by the NHBRC for TCF (Top Coat Finish) submissions and Home Builder Registration.

**Q7.25:** What is the best waterproofing system for a high-rise building podium?

**A:** For podium decks, we recommend: hot-applied rubberised bitumen membrane (3mm minimum), protection board, insulation (if required), drainage composite, and porcelain paver tiles on pedestals or mortar bed. System includes overflow drains and movement joints at 6m centres.

**Q7.26:** Do you have details for waterproofing around pipe penetrations?

**A:** Yes. Our pipe penetration detail set shows: core-drilled openings (not chiselled), membrane sealing around the pipe, flexible sealant between pipe and tile, and escutcheon cover options. Details are provided for horizontal and vertical pipe penetrations in floors and walls.

**Q7.27:** Can you provide a complete wet area specification for a hospital operating theatre?

**A:** Operating theatre specifications include: seamless epoxy or polyurethane floor with coved skirting 150mm, monolithic wall protection to 1200mm, antimicrobial tiles, joint-free surface (no grout lines on floor), air-tight junction details, and full compliance with infection control protocols.

**Q7.28:** What is the difference between a Type A, B, and C waterproofing system?

**A:** Type A (barrier protection): membrane applied to structure surface. Type B (structurally integral): waterproof concrete with additives. Type C (drained protection): cavity drainage system managing water ingress. We specify Type A systems for tile finishes, often combined with Type B for basements.

**Q7.29:** Do you provide waterproofing warranty for tile-on-tile renovations?

**A:** Tile-on-tile waterproofing warranties depend on: adhesion of existing tiles (>0.5 MPa pull-off), existing membrane condition, proper surface preparation, and primer application. A site inspection is required before warranty can be confirmed. Typical warranty: 5-7 years.

**Q7.30:** Can you specify a waterproofing system for an infinity edge pool?

**A:** Infinity edge pool specifications include: structural concrete pool shell, cementitious crystalline waterproofing, mosaic tile installation with epoxy adhesive and grout, surge tank waterproofing, and hydraulic system integration. Full technical drawings and method statements provided.

**Q7.31:** What is the correct sequence for wet area construction with tiles?

**A:** Correct sequence: (1) structural slab/screed, (2) 28-day cure, (3) surface preparation, (4) priming, (5) waterproofing membrane (floors then walls), (6) membrane cure (3-7 days), (7) flood test (48 hours), (8) tile adhesive, (9) tiles, (10) grout (3+ days after tiles), (11) silicone sealant at junctions.

**Q7.32:** Do you have details for tile installation over under-tile heating in wet areas?

**A:** Yes. Under-tile heating in wet areas requires: heating cable/mat on insulation board, full waterproofing over heating system, flexible adhesive and grout, expansion joints at 8m centres, and GFCI electrical protection. Our details show heating layout and waterproofing integration.

**Q7.33:** Can you provide waterproofing specifications for water features and fountains?

**A:** Water feature specifications include: structural concrete basin, cementitious crystalline waterproofing, mosaic or porcelain tile with epoxy adhesive and grout, pump and filtration integration, and winterisation details for outdoor features.

**Q7.34:** What is the best way to waterproof a tiled roof terrace?

**A:** Roof terrace waterproofing includes: structural concrete slab (slope 1:50), priming, liquid-applied polyurethane membrane (2mm minimum), protection screed, insulation (if required), separation layer, and porcelain paver tiles on adjustable pedestals or bedding mortar. Overflow and drainage details essential.

**Q7.35:** Do you provide wet area specifications for retirement village units?

**A:** Yes. Retirement village wet areas require: level-access showers (SANS 10400-S), slip-resistant tiles (R10-R11 minimum), grab bar fixing reinforcement in walls, lever taps, temperature-limiting valves, and emergency call system integration. Our specifications address all these requirements.

**Q7.36:** Can you provide a waterproofing specification for a car wash facility?

**A:** Car wash specifications include: heavy-duty liquid-applied membrane (3mm+), chemical resistance to detergents and waxes, oil resistance, high-pressure water resistance, floor drainage every 3m, slip-resistant tiles (R12), and hot water resistance for heated wash bays.

**Q7.37:** What is the recommended grout for wet area porcelain tiles?

**A:** For wet areas, we recommend: epoxy-based grout (leading adhesive manufacturers Kerapoxy or equivalent) for maximum water resistance and stain protection; premium cementitious grout with mould inhibitor for residential bathrooms; and flexible cementitious grout (CG2WA) for underfloor heating applications.

**Q7.38:** Do you specify waterproofing for lift pits and elevator shafts?

**A:** Yes. Lift pit waterproofing specifications include: cementitious crystalline negative-side waterproofing, sump pump integration, oil interceptor, and durable porcelain floor tiles. Details address the confined space access, ventilation, and maintenance requirements of lift pits.


## Category 8: Commercial & High-Traffic Applications

**Q8.1:** What PEI rating do you recommend for a shopping mall entrance?

**A:** Shopping mall entrances and atria require PEI Class 5 tiles (heavy traffic, abrasive conditions) with deep abrasion resistance ≤120mm³. We also recommend R11-R12 slip resistance for entrance areas exposed to wet weather and pedestrian traffic of 5,000+ per day.

**Q8.2:** Do you have tiles suitable for airport terminal applications?

**A:** Yes. Airport terminal tiles require: PEI Class 5, R10 slip resistance, high stain resistance, rolling load capacity for luggage trolleys (≥500kg/m²), and resistance to rubber wheel marking. Our technical porcelain ranges meet all these criteria and have been installed at OR Tambo and Cape Town International.

**Q8.3:** What tiles do you recommend for hospital corridors and wards?

**A:** Hospital specifications include: PEI Class 4-5, R9-R10 slip resistance (with consideration of hospital trolley traffic), stain resistance Class 4-5, antimicrobial properties (optional but recommended), coved skirting for hygiene, and sound-reducing underlayment where acoustic comfort is required.

**Q8.4:** Can you provide lifecycle cost analysis for tiles vs carpet in an office building?

**A:** Yes. Our lifecycle cost comparison shows: porcelain tiles have higher initial cost (R450-800/m² installed) vs carpet (R250-400/m²) but lower 20-year lifecycle cost due to: 25+ year service life vs 7-10 year carpet replacement, minimal maintenance costs, and no cleaning chemical costs. Full ROI analysis available.

**Q8.5:** What is the best tile specification for a hotel ballroom?

**A:** Hotel ballrooms require: PEI Class 3-4 (moveable furniture), R9 slip resistance, high stain resistance (red wine, food), compatible with underfloor heating/cooling, suitable for dance floor overlay, and available in large formats for seamless appearance. Our-look porcelain is frequently specified for this application.

**Q8.6:** Do you have tiles for cold storage and freezer room applications?

**A:** Yes. Cold storage tiles require: frost resistance (confirmed to ISO 10545-12), thermal shock resistance, slip resistance R11-R12 (condensation), and installation with frost-resistant adhesive. Tiles must be suitable for temperatures down to -30°C.

**Q8.7:** What tiles are suitable for a commercial kitchen with heavy grease exposure?

**A:** Commercial kitchens require: PEI Class 5, R11-R12 slip resistance, chemical resistance to degreasers and cleaning agents, thermal shock resistance (hot spillage), impervious surface (water absorption ≤0.5%), and installation with epoxy-based grout. Full coving (150mm) is mandatory for hygiene.

**Q8.8:** Do you provide specifications for fire stairwell tiling?

**A:** Fire stairwell specifications include: PEI Class 4-5, R11-R12 slip resistance (especially at landings and nosings), contrasting colour at nosing edge for visibility, non-combustible material (A1 fire rating), and durable stair nosing profiles. Compliance with SANS 10400-T and National Building Regulations.

**Q8.9:** What is the recommended tile for a supermarket floor?

**A:** Supermarket floors require: PEI Class 4-5, R9-R10 slip resistance, resistance to point loading from shelving systems, stain resistance to food spills, compatibility with auto-scrubber cleaning, and availability in large formats for efficient installation. Anti-static properties may be required in electronics areas.

**Q8.10:** Can you specify tiles for a prison or correctional facility?

**A:** Yes. Correctional facility specifications include: maximum security classification (no loose components), impact-resistant tiles, vandal-resistant surfaces, concealed fixing (no accessible edges), R12 slip resistance, and institutional-grade coving. We have supplied to multiple DCS facilities nationally.

**Q8.11:** What tiles do you recommend for university lecture halls and corridors?

**A:** Educational facility tiles: PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance, acoustic performance (impact sound reduction), stain resistance, low maintenance, and design flexibility for wayfinding colour-coding. Our standard commercial porcelain ranges are widely used in universities and schools.

**Q8.12:** Do you have experience with casino floor tile specifications?

**A:** Yes. Casino specifications include: PEI Class 5, high abrasion resistance (heavy 24-hour traffic), R9 slip resistance, design flexibility for custom patterns and inlays, compatibility with gaming equipment point loads, and 24/7 maintenance capability. We have supplied to Sun City, Montecasino, and GrandWest.

**Q8.13:** What is the best tile for a public library reading room?

**A:** Library reading rooms benefit from: PEI Class 3-4, R9 slip resistance, acoustic-friendly surfaces (porcelain with acoustic underlayment), low glare (matte finish), and warm aesthetic tones. Large-format tiles reduce joints and simplify cleaning under shelving.

**Q8.14:** Can you specify tiles for a railway station platform?

**A:** Railway platform tiles require: PEI Class 5, R12 slip resistance (all weather), frost resistance, chemical resistance (de-icing salts), structural strength for dynamic loads, and tactile warning strips at platform edge. We have supplied to PRASA and Gautrain stations.

**Q8.15:** Do you provide maintenance schedules for commercial tile installations?

**A:** Yes. Our Commercial Maintenance Manual provides: daily cleaning protocols, weekly deep-cleaning procedures, recommended equipment and cleaning agents, stain removal guides, annual maintenance inspections, and re-sealing schedules where applicable. Maintenance compliance may be required for warranty validity.

**Q8.16:** What is the point load capacity of your commercial floor tiles?

**A:** Our PEI Class 5 commercial porcelain tiles (10-12mm thick) achieve point load capacities of 500-800 kg on a 25mm² contact area, suitable for: office chair castors, retail shelving, hospital beds, and light industrial equipment. For heavier loads, specify 20mm structural pavers.

**Q8.17:** Can you provide tiles for a pharmaceutical clean room?

**A:** Clean room tiles require: PEI Class 4-5, impervious surface, epoxy-based grout, coved skirting, chemical resistance to disinfectants, anti-static properties (if required), and compatibility with clean room HVAC positive pressure. Our technical porcelain with epoxy installation meets ISO 14644 clean room standards.

**Q8.18:** Do you have tiles suitable for petrol station forecourts and convenience stores?

**A:** Petrol station specifications include: oil and petrol resistance (verified chemical resistance), R12 slip resistance (weather exposure), UV stability, stain resistance, and heavy traffic capability. Our full-body technical porcelain with appropriate slip rating is suitable for both forecourts and convenience stores.

**Q8.19:** What tiles do you recommend for a gym and fitness centre?

**A:** Gym specifications include: PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance (dry), impact resistance (dropped weights), compatibility with rubber mat overlay in free-weight areas, and resistance to sweat and cleaning chemicals. Heavy-duty coving is recommended for wet areas (pool, change rooms).

**Q8.20:** Can you provide a specification for a data centre raised floor?

**A:** Data centre specifications include: anti-static properties, point load rating for server racks (≥5kN), precision flatness (±2mm/2m), fire rating A1, and compatibility with underfloor air distribution. Our porcelain tiles on pedestal systems are widely used in data centre applications.

**Q8.21:** Do you have tiles for brewery and wine cellar applications?

**A:** Brewery/winery specifications include: chemical resistance to acids and sugars, R11 slip resistance (wet conditions), thermal shock resistance, impervious surface, and installation with acid-resistant epoxy-based grout. Our technical porcelain has been supplied to multiple Western Cape wineries.

**Q8.22:** What is the best tile for an art gallery or museum?

**A:** Gallery specifications include: PEI Class 3-4, low gloss (matte or honed) to prevent light reflection, neutral colour (white/grey to not compete with artwork), acoustic properties for footfall reduction, and compatibility with picture hanging and display systems.

**Q8.23:** Can you specify tiles for an abattoir or meat processing facility?

**A:** Abattoir specifications include: R12 slip resistance (wet/greasy), chemical resistance to blood, fats, and cleaning agents, thermal shock resistance (hot wash), impervious surface, epoxy-based grout, coving 150mm, and floor drainage integration. HACCP compliance documentation provided.

**Q8.24:** Do you provide specifications for hotel back-of-house areas?

**A:** Hotel BOH specifications include: PEI Class 5 for corridors and service lifts, R11 slip resistance for kitchen and laundry areas, chemical resistance, heavy-duty coving, and design differentiation from front-of-house. Standardisation across hotel brands reduces procurement complexity.

**Q8.25:** What tiles are suitable for a multi-storey car park?

**A:** Car park specifications include: PEI Class 5, R11-R12 slip resistance (oily/wet conditions), chemical resistance to oil, petrol, and de-icing salts, frost resistance for open decks, and structural capability for vehicle traffic. Our 20mm structural pavers or heavy-duty technical porcelain are recommended.

**Q8.26:** Can you provide a tile specification for a shopping mall food court?

**A:** Food court specifications include: PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance, high stain resistance, compatibility with table/chair fixings, design flexibility for tenant demarcation, and easy maintenance. Consideration for tenant drainage and services access required.

**Q8.27:** Do you have tiles for mosque, church, and religious building applications?

**A:** Yes. Religious building specifications include: PEI Class 3-4, R9 slip resistance, appropriate aesthetic (geometric patterns, calligraphy tiles-look), and durability for high-footfall prayer halls. We have supplied to mosques, churches, and temples nationally.

**Q8.28:** What is the recommended cleaning protocol for commercial porcelain tiles?

**A:** Daily: dry dust mop or vacuum. Weekly: damp mop with pH-neutral cleaner (pH 7). Monthly: deep clean with commercial floor scrubber. Annually: professional deep clean and inspection. Avoid: acid-based cleaners (damage grout), abrasive pads (scratch surface), and excessive water (penetrate joints).


## Category 9: Sustainability & Green Building

**Q9.1:** Does Tiletoria provide Environmental Product Declarations (EPDs) for your tiles?

**A:** Yes. EPDs are available for our major imported collections from Italian and Spanish manufacturers. EPDs are third-party verified to EN 15804 and ISO 14025 standards, covering: raw material extraction, manufacturing, transport, installation, use phase, and end-of-life impacts.

**Q9.2:** How many Green Star SA points can tiles contribute to a project?

**A:** Tiles can contribute to Green Star SA V2 in: Materials category (Mat-1 recycled content, Mat-2 sustainable products with EPDs, Mat-3 volatile organic compounds), Energy (thermal mass contribution), and Innovation (exceptional environmental performance). Maximum potential: 4-6 points depending on product selection.

**Q9.3:** Do you have tiles that are GBCSA Green Star V2 compliant?

**A:** Yes. Our Green Star compliant tile ranges include: products with EPDs, recycled content tiles (20-40% post-industrial), low-VOC tiles (A+ emission rating), and locally manufactured products (reducing transport emissions). A Green Star product guide is available for specifier reference.

**Q9.4:** What percentage of recycled content do your eco-friendly tiles contain?

**A:** Our sustainable collections contain 20-40% recycled content (pre-consumer porcelain waste and recycled glass). Recycled content percentages are verified by third-party assessment and documented for Green Star submission. Standard porcelain tiles typically contain 5-10% recycled content.

**Q9.5:** Do you provide carbon footprint data for your tiles?

**A:** Yes. EPDs include Global Warming Potential (GWP) measured in kg CO₂-eq per m². Typical values: Italian porcelain 10-15 kg CO₂/m², Spanish porcelain 8-12 kg CO₂/m², and locally manufactured tiles 5-8 kg CO₂/m². This data is used for whole-building carbon accounting.

**Q9.6:** Are your tiles ecoASA compliant?

**A:** Yes. Our locally manufactured and imported tile ranges comply with ecoASA (Eco Product Label for the South African built environment) requirements where applicable. Compliance documentation is available for projects pursuing ecoASA certification.

**Q9.7:** What is the environmental impact of porcelain tile manufacturing?

**A:** Porcelain tile manufacturing impacts include: energy consumption (firing at 1,200°C), raw material extraction (clay, feldspar, sand), water use (recycled in closed-loop systems), and transport emissions. Our manufacturers use renewable energy, recycled water, and waste heat recovery to minimise impacts.

**Q9.8:** Do you offer tiles made from recycled ocean plastics or other innovative materials?

**A:** Yes. Our innovation portfolio includes tiles with recycled glass content (up to 40%), tiles incorporating recycled industrial ceramics, and experimental ranges using alternative raw materials. These are typically available by special order for projects prioritising circular economy principles.

**Q9.9:** Can Tiletoria help with Green Star documentation and submission?

**A:** Yes. Our Green Building support service provides: product EPDs, recycled content certificates, VOC test reports, point contribution calculations, and template documentation for GBCSA submission. Our technical team has supported 30+ Green Star projects across South Africa.

**Q9.10:** Do your tiles contribute to LEED certification?

**A:** Yes. Our imported tiles contribute to LEED v4.1 in: MR credit Building Product Disclosure (EPDs), MR credit Sourcing of Raw Materials (recycled content), EQ credit Low-Emitting Materials (VOC emissions), and MR credit Construction and Demolition Waste Management.

**Q9.11:** What is the service life of porcelain tiles for lifecycle assessment purposes?

**A:** For LCA calculations, our PEI Class 4-5 porcelain tiles have a reference service life of 40 years in commercial applications and 50 years in residential applications. This long service life contributes positively to whole-building LCA by reducing replacement frequency.

**Q9.12:** Do you provide end-of-life recycling options for tiles?

**A:** End-of-life recycling for tiles is limited in South Africa. However, our manufacturers participate in take-back programmes in Europe, and crushed porcelain can be recycled as aggregate in concrete or road base. We are working with local recyclers to develop circular economy solutions.

**Q9.13:** Are your imported tiles shipped using low-carbon transport methods?

**A:** Our Italian and Spanish manufacturers are increasingly using rail transport within Europe and low-sulphur maritime shipping. We can provide transport mode documentation for projects requiring supply chain carbon accounting.

**Q9.14:** Do you have tiles that contribute to thermal mass in passive design?

**A:** Yes. Porcelain tiles have high thermal mass (volumetric heat capacity ~1.8 MJ/m³K) and can contribute to passive thermal regulation when installed on concrete slabs. Our technical team can provide thermal mass calculations for energy modelling.

**Q9.15:** What sustainable packaging do you use for tile deliveries?

**A:** Our manufacturers use: recycled cardboard packaging, reusable timber pallets (ISPM 15 certified), plastic wrapping with recycled content, and returnable packaging systems for large projects. We are transitioning to plastic-free packaging where possible.

**Q9.16:** Do you provide locally manufactured tiles to reduce transport emissions?

**A:** Yes. We stock locally manufactured tiles from South African producers for projects prioritising local content and reduced transport emissions. Local tiles typically have 40-60% lower carbon footprint than European imports and support local employment.

**Q9.17:** Can you provide a sustainability comparison between different tile options?

**A:** Yes. Our sustainability comparison tool evaluates: recycled content %, GWP (kg CO₂/m²), EPD availability, transport distance, service life, and end-of-life recyclability. This enables informed selection based on environmental performance criteria.

**Q9.18:** Do your tiles contain any Red List chemicals?

**A:** Our tiles do not contain any ILBC Red List chemicals (asbestos, lead, mercury, formaldehyde, phthalates, etc.). We provide Red List-free declarations for Living Building Challenge and WELL Building Standard projects.

**Q9.19:** What water conservation measures are used in tile manufacturing?

**A:** Our manufacturing partners use: closed-loop water recycling systems (95%+ water reuse), rainwater harvesting, effluent treatment plants, and water-efficient pressing technologies. Water consumption data is included in EPD documentation.

**Q9.20:** Can tiles contribute to WELL Building Standard certification?

**A:** Yes. Tiles contribute to WELL in: Mind (aesthetic biophilic design), Materials (low VOC emissions, transparency), and Water (mould resistance in wet areas). Our VOC test data and material health documentation support WELL submission.

**Q9.21:** Do you offer tiles with photocatalytic air-purifying properties?

**A:** Yes. Selected ranges incorporate titanium dioxide (TiO₂) photocatalytic technology that, when exposed to UV light, activates to reduce NOx and VOCs in surrounding air. These contribute to improved indoor air quality and can support Green Star Innovation credits.

**Q9.22:** What is the embodied energy of porcelain tiles compared to other flooring?


**Q9.23:** Do you provide chain-of-custody documentation for responsible sourcing?


**Q9.24:** Are your wood-look porcelain tiles a sustainable alternative to timber flooring?

**A:** Yes. Wood-look porcelain eliminates: deforestation, timber treatment chemicals, warping/rotting requiring replacement, and maintenance with chemical finishes. Our wood-look collections with 30%+ recycled content offer an environmentally superior alternative to tropical hardwoods.

**Q9.25:** Can you assist with a Net Zero Carbon building tile specification?

**A:** Yes. For Net Zero projects, we provide: lowest-GWP tile options, carbon offset partnerships, EPD data for whole-life carbon calculations, and guidance on tile specification to minimise operational energy (thermal mass contribution).

**Q9.26:** Do your manufacturers have ISO 14001 environmental management certification?

**A:** Yes. All our major manufacturing partners hold ISO 14001 environmental management certification, confirming systematic environmental management including: impact assessment, legal compliance, pollution prevention, and continuous improvement. Certificates are available for due diligence.

**Q9.27:** What role do tiles play in biophilic design?

**A:** Tiles contribute to biophilic design through: natural material replication (stone, wood, terracotta), organic textures and patterns, earth-tone colour palettes, and connection to natural geological processes. Our biophilic tile guide suggests products for nature-inspired interiors.

**Q9.28:** Do you have tiles suitable for Living Building Challenge projects?

**A:** For LBC projects, we provide: Red List-free declarations, EPDs, Just organisation labels, responsible sourcing documentation, and material health disclosures. Tile selection must meet LBC Materials Petal requirements for Imperative 10 (Red List) and Imperative 11 (Responsible Industry).

**Q9.29:** Can you provide a sustainability datasheet for your tiles?

**A:** Yes. Each product in our sustainable collection has a one-page sustainability datasheet summarising: recycled content %, EPD reference, VOC class, GWP, service life, maintenance requirements, and Green Star contribution points. These simplify sustainability documentation.

**Q9.30:** What is Tiletoria's own environmental commitment as a company?

**A:** Tiletoria is committed to: reducing operational carbon footprint, promoting sustainable product selection, minimising packaging waste, supporting Green Building Council initiatives, and developing circular economy solutions for the South African tile industry. Our sustainability report is available on request.

**Q9.31:** Do you provide guidance on specifying tiles for energy-efficient buildings?

**A:** Yes. Our energy efficiency guide covers: tile thermal mass for passive temperature regulation, tile reflectivity for daylighting optimisation (light-coloured tiles), tile integration with underfloor heating/cooling systems, and cool roof tile specifications for reduced cooling loads.

**Q9.32:** Can recycled tiles be used in LEED or Green Star projects?

**A:** Yes. Tiles with recycled content contribute directly to MR credits in both LEED and Green Star. Our recycled content tiles carry third-party verification and can contribute 1-2 points depending on the percentage of recycled content and the project's overall materials strategy.

**Q9.33:** What is the difference between an EPD and a self-declared environmental claim?

**A:** An EPD is a third-party verified, ISO-compliant document based on LCA methodology, ensuring transparency and comparability. Self-declared claims lack independent verification and may not follow standardised methodologies. We only provide third-party verified EPDs.

**Q9.34:** Do your tiles support the UN Sustainable Development Goals?

**A:** Our tile specification can contribute to SDG 9 (Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure through durable buildings), SDG 11 (Sustainable Cities), SDG 12 (Responsible Consumption through recycled content), and SDG 13 (Climate Action through low-GWP options).


## Category 10: Preferred Supplier List (PSL) Inclusion

**Q10.1:** How do I get Tiletoria included on my practice's Preferred Supplier List (PSL)?

**A:** To initiate PSL inclusion, contact our Professional Services Manager with your practice details, project portfolio, and PSL application requirements. We provide: company profile, technical capability statement, project references, financial information, B-BBEE certificate, and insurance documentation. PSL review typically takes 4-6 weeks.

**Q10.2:** What documentation does Tiletoria provide for PSL applications?

**A:** PSL documentation pack includes: company registration documents, B-BBEE certificate, VAT registration, insurance certificates (public liability, professional indemnity), financial statements, ISO certifications, product catalogues, technical data sheets, project reference list, and client testimonials.

**Q10.3:** When is the best time to approach practices for PSL inclusion?

**A:** The optimal timing is October-December for inclusion in the following year's PSL, which is typically reviewed in January-February. Approaching before the review cycle allows time for documentation, presentations, and internal practice approval processes.

**Q10.4:** Does Tiletoria have experience with major architectural practices' PSL processes?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is on the PSL of over 120 architectural practices nationally, including mid-to-large practices that drive the majority of specified tile volume. We understand the documentation, presentation, and compliance requirements of leading South African practices.

**Q10.5:** What is Tiletoria's B-BBEE status?

**A:** Tiletoria holds a Level 2 B-BBEE contributor status. Our B-BBEE certificate is available for PSL submissions, government tender compliance, and client procurement requirements. We are committed to transformation and skills development in the built environment.

**Q10.6:** Can Tiletoria meet the vendor compliance requirements of large corporate developers?

**A:** Yes. We meet the compliance requirements of major corporate developers including: B-BBEE certification, tax clearance, CIDB registration, health and safety compliance, environmental compliance, and supplier code of conduct agreements.

**Q10.7:** Do you have professional indemnity insurance?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria carries professional indemnity insurance of R10 million and public liability insurance of R20 million. Insurance certificates are provided as part of PSL documentation and updated annually.

**Q10.8:** What differentiates Tiletoria from other tile suppliers on PSLs?

**A:** Key differentiators: (1) Dedicated Spec Lab for professional specifiers, (2) SAIA-accredited CPD programme, (3) Comprehensive technical documentation (SANS certificates, BIM objects), (4) 4-hour technical query response guarantee, (5) 25+ year track record on major projects, (6) Full specification support service.

**Q10.9:** Can you provide client references from practices similar to ours?

**A:** Yes. We provide named references from practices in your project sector (commercial, residential, hospitality, healthcare, education) and of similar size. References can be contacted directly or we can arrange reference site visits to completed projects.

**Q10.10:** Does Tiletoria offer practice-level partnership agreements?

**A:** Yes. Practice partnership agreements include: dedicated technical liaison, priority specification support, annual CPD scheduling, early access to new collections, volume discount structures, and joint marketing opportunities. Partnerships are reviewed annually.

**Q10.11:** What is the typical duration of a PSL listing with Tiletoria?

**A:** PSL listings are typically valid for 12 months, aligned with the practice's annual supplier review cycle. We provide renewal documentation 60 days before expiry and notify practices of any changes to our status or documentation.

**Q10.12:** Can Tiletoria provide a supplier scorecard for our vendor evaluation?

**A:** Yes. We provide completed vendor scorecards covering: product quality, technical support, documentation quality, delivery performance, pricing competitiveness, CPD value, BIM availability, sustainability credentials, and innovation. Historical performance data is included where available.

**Q10.13:** Do you have experience with government supplier panel applications?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is registered on multiple government supplier panels and understands the requirements for: National Treasury central supplier database registration, CIDB grading, B-BBEE compliance, and Preferential Procurement Policy Framework Act compliance.

**Q10.14:** What happens if Tiletoria is not selected for a practice's PSL?

**A:** If not selected, we request feedback on areas for improvement and re-approach at the next review cycle. We also offer: ongoing CPD attendance (open to all), showroom access, and technical support regardless of PSL status.

**Q10.15:** Can you provide a capability statement for a specific project type?

**A:** Yes. Project-specific capability statements cover: relevant project experience, technical team qualifications, product suitability, delivery capacity, and project references. These are tailored to the specific project type and scale for tender or PSL submissions.

**Q10.16:** Does Tiletoria have any industry accreditations or memberships?

**A:** Tiletoria and Spec Lab hold memberships with: SAIA (South African Institute of Architects), GBCSA (Green Building Council South Africa), TASA (Tile Association of South Africa), and regional institutes (GIFA, PIA, CIfA, SAIA KZN, SAIA EC). Accreditation certificates are available.

**Q10.17:** How does Tiletoria handle confidential project information?

**A:** We operate under strict confidentiality protocols. All project information is handled on a need-to-know basis, and we sign Non-Disclosure Agreements where required. Our staff are trained in POPIA compliance for handling personal and project data.

**Q10.18:** Can you provide audited financial statements for PSL due diligence?

**A:** Yes. Audited financial statements for the past 3 years are available for PSL applications and client financial due diligence. These demonstrate our financial stability and capacity to support large-scale projects.

**Q10.19:** What is Tiletoria's geographic coverage for project support?

**A:** We provide nationwide coverage from our Johannesburg headquarters with: direct service to Gauteng, KZN, and Mpumalanga; regional technical support in Cape Town and Durban; and dedicated project support for national rollouts (retail, residential estate, hotel chains).

**Q10.20:** Do you offer credit facilities for project procurement?

**A:** Yes. Credit facilities are available for practices and their contractor networks, subject to credit approval. Facilities include: 30-day trading accounts, project-specific credit limits, and structured payment terms aligned to construction milestones.

**Q10.21:** Can Tiletoria accommodate practice-specific procurement requirements?

**A:** Yes. We adapt to: specific invoice formatting, purchase order systems, delivery scheduling, stock holding arrangements, and reporting requirements. Our accounts team works directly with your procurement department to align processes.

**Q10.22:** What is your track record on large commercial projects?

**A:** Tiletoria has supplied tiles to: Mall of Africa, Sandton City refurbishment, Discovery Health headquarters, Standard Bank headquarters, multiple Sun International hotels, Netcare hospitals, and numerous residential estates (50-2,000 units). Full project lists are available on request.

**Q10.23:** Do you have any quality certifications (ISO 9001)?

**A:** While Tiletoria operates as a distributor, our major manufacturing partners hold ISO 9001 (quality management), ISO 14001 (environmental management), and ISO 45001 (occupational health and safety). We provide these certificates for PSL documentation.

**Q10.24:** How does Tiletoria manage product quality consistency across large orders?

**A:** Quality management includes: pre-order sample approval (AAS), factory production monitoring for large orders, batch tracking with shade/calibre numbers, pre-shipment inspection for imports, and delivery verification against approved samples.

**Q10.25:** Can you provide a list of all major projects completed in the last 5 years?

**A:** Yes. Our project portfolio lists major projects completed over the past 5 years, categorised by: commercial, residential, hospitality, healthcare, education, retail, and industrial. Each entry includes: project name, architect, value, and product supplied.
