**Q10.26:** What is your policy on product substitution if specified items are unavailable?

**A:** Our substitution policy requires: written notification within 48 hours of discovering unavailability, provision of equivalent or superior alternative with full technical data, sample submission for architect approval, and no substitution without written architect consent. We maintain buffer stock to minimise disruptions.

**Q10.27:** Do you have a supplier diversity programme?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria's supplier diversity programme includes: preferential procurement from B-BBEE suppliers, support for emerging contractors through training and mentorship, and partnerships with women-owned and youth-owned businesses in the tiling industry.

**Q10.28:** Can you present to our PSL review committee?

**A:** Yes. Our Professional Services team presents to PSL review committees nationwide. Presentations cover: company overview, product range, technical capabilities, CPD programme, sustainability credentials, and Q&A. Typically 30-45 minutes with samples and catalogues.

**Q10.29:** What feedback mechanisms do you have for continuous improvement?

**A:** We conduct: annual client satisfaction surveys, quarterly NPS tracking, post-project debriefs, CPD feedback analysis, and regular specification review outcomes. Feedback directly informs our service improvement initiatives and product range development.


## Category 11: Project-Specific Support

**Q11.1:** Can Tiletoria review my tile specification before it goes to tender?

**A:** Yes. Our specification review service examines your tile specification for: completeness, SANS compliance, performance criteria appropriateness, installation detail accuracy, and value engineering opportunities. Reviews typically take 3-5 working days and are provided free of charge for registered specifiers.

**Q11.2:** Does Tiletoria offer value engineering services for cost reduction?

**A:** Yes. Our value engineering service identifies alternative products within our range that meet or exceed your specified performance criteria at reduced cost. We provide side-by-side comparison sheets showing: performance equivalence, aesthetic similarity, and potential savings for client presentation.

**Q11.3:** Can you provide custom solutions for unusual project requirements?

**A:** Yes. For projects with unusual requirements (custom sizes, bespoke colours, special formats, unique textures), we work with our manufacturing partners to develop custom solutions. Custom development requires 12-16 weeks and minimum order quantities of 500m²+ per custom item.

**Q11.4:** Does Tiletoria arrange factory visits for architects?

**A:** Yes. We arrange factory visits to our manufacturing partners in Italy, Spain, and Portugal for practices specifying significant volumes. Factory visits include: production line tours, R&D facility access, raw material inspection, quality control observation, and direct meetings with factory technical directors.

**Q11.5:** Can you organise an overseas sourcing trip for our practice?

**A:** Yes. For practices specifying R10 million+ annually, we organise curated sourcing trips to: Cersaie (Bologna, Italy), Cevisama (Valencia, Spain), and manufacturer showrooms. Trips include: travel coordination, factory visits, showroom tours, and sample procurement.

**Q11.6:** Can Tiletoria assist with specification for a Design & Build project?

**A:** Yes. For D&B projects, we provide: performance-based specifications with clear minimum criteria, product equivalency guides, substitution evaluation matrices, and contractor briefings. This protects design intent while allowing procurement flexibility.

**Q11.7:** Do you provide specification support for government and public sector tenders?

**A:** Yes. Public sector specification support includes: SANS-compliant specification clauses, B-BBEE documentation, local content verification, preferential procurement compliance, and tender response assistance. We have supplied to DBE, DCS, DoH, and numerous municipalities.

**Q11.8:** Can you help with value management workshops for our project?

**A:** Yes. Our technical team participates in value management workshops, providing: cost-performance analysis, alternative product suggestions, lifecycle cost comparisons, and constructability advice. We contribute tile-specific expertise to optimise value without compromising function.

**Q11.9:** Does Tiletoria provide project-specific technical reports?

**A:** Yes. Project-specific technical reports cover: slip resistance assessment, structural loading calculations, thermal performance analysis, acoustic recommendations, and sustainability scoring. These are signed by our technical director and can be submitted to authorities or design review panels.

**Q11.10:** Can you assist with dispute resolution involving tile specification or installation?

**A:** Yes. Our technical director provides expert opinion services for disputes involving: specification compliance, installation quality, product performance, warranty claims, and defect assessment. Expert opinion is provided impartially based on SANS standards and industry best practice.

**Q11.11:** Do you provide mock specification packages for design competitions?

**A:** Yes. Competition entry support includes: preliminary specification outlines, indicative pricing for feasibility studies, product images and technical data for submission boards, and letters of support confirming product availability.

**Q11.12:** Can Tiletoria support Design Development (DD) and Construction Document (CD) phases?

**A:** Yes. Our specification support spans all phases: DD (product selection, preliminary specs, option analysis), CD (full specification writing, detail drawings, schedules), and CA (submittal review, RFI responses, site inspections).

**Q11.13:** Do you offer post-occupancy evaluation support?

**A:** Yes. For selected projects, we participate in post-occupancy evaluations examining: tile performance vs specification, maintenance requirements, user satisfaction, and lessons learned. Feedback informs our product recommendations and CPD content.

**Q11.14:** Can you provide a dedicated technical liaison for a major project?

**A:** Yes. For projects over R10 million in tile value, we assign a dedicated project technical liaison who: attends design meetings, responds to technical queries, coordinates sample approvals, reviews shop drawings, and supports the project through to completion.

**Q11.15:** Does Tiletoria support fast-track project delivery?

**A:** Yes. Fast-track support includes: expedited sample approval, buffer stockholding, air freight options, priority production allocation at factories, and dedicated project management. We have supported fast-track hotel and retail projects with 8-12 week delivery schedules.

**Q11.16:** Can you assist with tenant installation guidelines for retail developments?

**A:** Yes. For retail mall developments, we provide: tenant design guidelines (tile specification, installation standards, brand compliance), tenant approval sample submission processes, and coordination between base build and tenant shop fit specifications.

**Q11.17:** Do you provide specification standardisation for residential estate developments?

**A:** Yes. Estate standardisation services include: 3-4 tile types covering all wet areas, living areas, kitchens, and external areas; colour palette standardisation; bulk procurement pricing; NHBRC compliance; and sales office display coordination.

**Q11.18:** Can Tiletoria support heritage building restoration projects?

**A:** Yes. Heritage support includes: matching analysis of existing historic tiles, sympathetic replacement sourcing, lime-based adhesive specifications, SAHRA/PHRA submission documentation, and conservation consultant liaison. We have supplied to heritage projects in Cape Town, Durban, and Johannesburg.

**Q11.19:** Do you assist with obtaining municipal approvals for tile specifications?

**A:** Yes. Municipal approval support includes: SANS 10400 compliance letters, fire rating certificates, NHBRC compliance documentation, accessibility compliance confirmation, and method statements. Documentation is formatted for submission to municipal building control officers.

**Q11.20:** Can you provide quantity surveying support for tile packages?

**A:** Yes. Our quantity extraction service provides: preliminary quantities from drawings, wastage calculations (10% standard, 15% complex patterns), accessory quantities, and indicative costing. Final quantities should be verified by the appointed QS.

**Q11.21:** Does Tiletoria offer pre-construction meetings with the tiling contractor?

**A:** Yes. Pre-construction meetings (typically chaired by the architect or main contractor) include: specification review, sample confirmation, substrate inspection requirements, installation sequence discussion, and warranty conditions. Tiletoria technical staff attend as the product supplier representative.

**Q11.22:** Can you support phased project delivery over multiple years?

**A:** Yes. For multi-phase projects (residential estates, campus developments), we provide: phase-specific specifications, batch consistency guarantees, price holding for contract duration, stock reservation, and continuity assurance across phases.

**Q11.23:** Do you provide as-built documentation support?

**A:** As-built support includes: actual product installed documentation, warranty certificates, maintenance manuals, cleaning instructions, and spare tile retention details. Digital as-built packages are provided in PDF and can include COBie data for BIM handover.

**Q11.24:** Can Tiletoria assist with product substitution evaluation during construction?

**A:** Yes. Substitution evaluation includes: technical comparison against original specification, performance verification, sample provision, architect presentation, and documentation for consent. We aim to approve or propose alternatives within 48 hours.

**Q11.25:** Do you offer specification writing workshops for architectural practices?

**A:** Yes. Custom specification writing workshops (carrying CPD credits) teach: SANS-compliant specification structure, performance criteria selection, common specification errors, digital specification tools, and quality assurance processes. Workshops are tailored to your practice's specification template.


## Category 12: Compliance & Certification

**Q12.1:** Does Tiletoria provide NHBRC warranty compliance documentation?

**A:** Yes. We provide NHBRC compliance letters confirming that our specified tiles, adhesives, and installation methods comply with NHBRC Technical Requirements. Documentation supports TCF (Top Coat Finish) submissions and home builder registration applications.

**Q12.2:** Can you provide SANS 1449:2012 classification certificates for all your tiles?

**A:** Yes. SANS 1449:2012 classification certificates are available for every porcelain and ceramic tile in our range, confirming: group classification (BⅠa, BⅠb, etc.), water absorption, breaking strength, surface quality, and dimensional tolerance.

**Q12.3:** Do your tiles comply with SANS 10107:2017 (Design and Installation of Ceramic Tiling)?

**A:** Yes. All our tile specifications align with SANS 10107:2017, covering: substrate requirements, adhesive selection, installation methods, movement joint spacing, grouting, and cleaning. Our method statements are cross-referenced to specific SANS 10107 clauses.

**Q12.4:** What fire ratings do your porcelain tiles achieve?

**A:** Porcelain tiles achieve Class A1 (non-combustible, no contribution to fire) per EN 13501-1 / SANS 428. This is the highest fire classification, meaning tiles produce no smoke, no flaming droplets, and no toxic fumes. Fire test certificates are available for all ranges.

**Q12.5:** Can you provide method statements for tile installation?

**A:** Yes. Project-specific method statements cover: substrate preparation, waterproofing application, adhesive mixing and application, tile fixing, grouting, movement joint installation, protection, and cleaning. Method statements are signed by our technical director and can be submitted to contractors and authorities.

**Q12.6:** Do your tiles meet municipal building control requirements?

**A:** Yes. Our tiles and installation systems comply with National Building Regulations and SANS 10400. We provide compliance documentation for: Part B (structural), Part P (drainage), Part T (fire protection), Part S (facilities for disabled persons), and Part X (environmental sustainability).

**Q12.7:** Can you provide a Certificate of Compliance (COC) for a completed installation?

**A:** Yes. Upon completion, we issue a Certificate of Compliance confirming: products installed match specification, installation methods followed approved method statement, waterproofing tested and passed, and warranty conditions communicated. COCs are provided for full system installations.

**Q12.8:** Do you have products certified for use in food preparation areas (HACCP)?

**A:** Yes. Our commercial kitchen tile systems comply with HACCP (Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points) requirements: impervious surface, coved skirting, slip resistance, chemical resistance, and cleanability. HACCP compliance letters are provided for food facility projects.

**Q12.9:** What accessibility compliance do your tiles meet?

**A:** Our tiles comply with SANS 10400-S (facilities for disabled persons) requirements: slip resistance (wet pendulum ≥36 or R10 minimum), flush transitions (≤5mm with 1:2 bevel), tactile warning indicators, colour contrast (30% Light Reflectance Value difference), and accessible route widths.

**Q12.10:** Can you provide test data for acoustic compliance in buildings?

**A:** Yes. Impact sound reduction data is available for tile systems with various underlayments. Typical ratings: 14dB (standard installation), 17dB (6mm acoustic underlayment), 22dB (12mm acoustic mat). Project-specific acoustic calculations can be provided for BB93 or SANS 10400 Part K compliance.

**Q12.11:** Do your tiles meet requirements for healthcare facilities (OHSA, infection control)?

**A:** Yes. Healthcare tile specifications comply with: OHSA requirements, infection control protocols (impervious, joint minimisation where possible), HTM guidelines (where referenced), and antimicrobial performance where specified. We have supplied to Netcare, Mediclinic, and Life Healthcare facilities.

**Q12.12:** Can you provide structural engineering data for facade tile applications?

**A:** Yes. Facade engineering data includes: wind load resistance (tested to EN 12825), dead load calculations, fixing pull-out strengths (mechanical and adhesive), thermal expansion coefficients, and seismic movement accommodation. Data is provided for structural engineer review and sign-off.

**Q12.13:** Do your tiles comply with SANS 10400 Part T (fire protection) for high-rise buildings?

**A:** Yes. Our porcelain tiles achieve A1 fire classification with zero smoke development and zero flaming droplets, fully compliant with SANS 10400 Part T for high-rise buildings, atrium applications, and fire escape routes. Fire resistance ratings (REI) for tile systems are available.

**Q12.14:** What is the difference between SANS 1449:2012 and EN 14411 classifications?

**A:** SANS 1449:2012 is the South African standard (harmonised with ISO 13006) using Group designations (BⅠa, BⅠb, etc.). EN 14411 is the European standard using Type/Group/Class designations (e.g., Type BⅠa Group 1). Both classify tiles by water absorption and breaking strength. We provide cross-reference tables.

**Q12.15:** Can you provide compliance letters for insurance purposes?

**A:** Yes. Insurance compliance letters confirm: product suitability for the insured application, installation compliance with standards, warranty validity, and maintenance requirements. These support building insurance applications and claims.

**Q12.16:** Do your tiles meet the requirements for SABS mark certification?

**A:** Our imported tiles carry CE marking (mandatory for Europe) and are manufactured in ISO 9001-certified factories. While SABS mark certification is not mandatory for imported tiles, we provide equivalent third-party certification from internationally accredited laboratories.

**Q12.17:** Can you provide a warranty for tile installation on problematic substrates?

**A:** Warranty on problematic substrates (timber, existing tiles, underfloor heating) requires: pre-installation inspection, approved substrate preparation system, accredited installer, and compliance with our method statement. Warranty may be limited to 5-7 years for non-standard substrates.

**Q12.18:** Do you have tiles suitable for certified passive house (Passivhaus) projects?

**A:** Yes. For Passivhaus projects, we provide: thermal bridging analysis (tile impact on insulation continuity), airtightness detailing at junctions, thermal conductivity data, and compatibility with underfloor heating systems. Tiles have minimal impact on building airtightness when properly detailed.

**Q12.19:** Can you provide chain-of-custody and responsible sourcing certification?

**A:** Yes. For projects requiring responsible sourcing certification (BREEAM, Green Star), we provide: quarry origin documentation, manufacturer ethical audit reports, fair labour practice confirmations, and conflict mineral free declarations.

**Q12.20:** Do your tiles comply with SANS 10400 Part X and XA (environmental sustainability)?

**A:** Our sustainable tile ranges contribute to Part X and XA compliance through: thermal mass contribution to energy efficiency, low-VOC emissions, recycled content, and responsible sourcing. Compliance documentation is provided as part of our Green Building support service.

**Q12.21:** Can you provide seismic resistance data for tiles in earthquake zones?

**A:** For seismic zones, we provide: tile flexural strength data, adhesive shear strength, recommended movement joint spacing, and fixing details for seismic conditions. Our technical team can review details with your structural engineer for Zone 1-4 applications.

**Q12.22:** Do you provide installation quality inspection checklists?

**A:** Yes. Our installation inspection checklist covers: substrate preparation, waterproofing integrity, adhesive application, tile alignment, joint spacing, grout quality, movement joints, cleaning, and protection. The checklist is used for quality assurance and warranty validation.

**Q12.23:** Can you provide compliance documentation for mining and industrial regulations?

**A:** Yes. Mining/industrial compliance documentation includes: DMR (Department of Mineral Resources) compliance for underground applications, slip resistance certification, fire rating confirmation, and chemical resistance data for process plant applications.

**Q12.24:** What is your policy on product recalls and safety notifications?

**A:** In the event of a product recall or safety notification, we immediately notify all affected specifiers and contractors by email and phone, publish a public notice on our website, and coordinate product return/replacement at no cost. We have never had a product safety recall in our operational history.

**Q12.25:** Do you provide method statements for working at height (facade installations)?

**A:** Yes. Facade installation method statements include: working at height protocols, scaffold and mast climber requirements, tile lifting equipment, wind speed limitations, temporary fixing methods, and fall arrest considerations. These supplement the main contractor's health and safety plan.


## Category 13: Detail Drawings & Technical Resources

**Q13.1:** Does Tiletoria provide detail drawings for movement joints in tile installations?

**A:** Yes. Our movement joint detail library includes: floor-to-floor joints, floor-to-wall perimeters, wall-to-wall joints, structural movement joints, corner joints, T-junctions, and expansion joint crossings. Details show: profile type, sealant specification, waterproofing continuity, and finish options.

**Q13.2:** Can I get CAD drawings of tile transition details?

**A:** Yes. Our CAD detail library (.dwg format) includes: tile-to-carpet transitions (Z-bar), tile-to-timber (T-bar/compensator), tile-to-VCT (reducer), tile-to-epoxy, tile-to-concrete, and level-access transitions. Each detail shows: profile section, dimensions, fixing method, and recommended products.

**Q13.3:** Do you provide drainage detail drawings for wet areas?

**A:** Yes. Drainage details include: floor waste installation (square and round), linear drain integration, tile-insert drain layout, balcony drain details, shower floor drainage with falls, and commercial gully trap details. All details show waterproofing integration and fall requirements.

**Q13.4:** Can I get detail drawings for coved skirting in hygiene-sensitive areas?

**A:** Yes. Coving detail sets show: internal coving (concave), external coving (convex), coving-to-wall junction, coving-to-drainage junction, and specialist coving for operating theatres. Details include: coving dimensions, adhesive specification, and joint sealing requirements.

**Q13.5:** Do you provide skirting detail drawings for different skirting types?

**A:** Yes. Skirting detail options include: flat tile-on-tile skirting (100mm), recessed shadow-line skirting, metal skirting covers (aluminium, stainless steel), coved porcelain skirting, and integration with wall vinyl systems. Each detail shows: section, height, fixing method, and junction treatment.

**Q13.6:** Can you provide stair nosing detail drawings?

**A:** Yes. Stair nosing details include: aluminium profile nosing with non-slip insert, brass nosing, stainless steel nosing, porcelain bullnose step tiles, and LED-integrated nosing profiles. Details show: tread and riser dimensions, nosing projection, fixing method, and contrast strip requirements for visibility.

**Q13.7:** Do you have detail drawings for tile fixing to plasterboard walls?

**A:** Yes. Plasterboard fixing details cover: direct fixing (lightweight tiles only), cement board overlay (recommended), waterproof plasterboard for wet areas, adhesive specification by tile weight, maximum tile height limitations, and movement joint requirements at board joints.

**Q13.8:** Can I get details for large-format tile installation?

**A:** Yes. Large-format details include: substrate flatness requirements (±3mm/2m), adhesive application (large-format notch trowel), back-buttering technique, lippage control systems, suction cup handling, cutting equipment requirements, and movement joint spacing (8-10m centres).

**Q13.9:** Do you provide details for tile installation on underfloor heating?

**A:** Yes. Underfloor heating details show: heating cable/mat layout, insulation board specification, waterproofing over heating, flexible adhesive and grout specification, expansion joint placement (8m centres), temperature sensor location, and thermostat integration.

**Q13.10:** Can I get balcony and terrace tiling detail drawings?

**A:** Yes. leading adhesive manufacturerscony/terrace details include: structural slab, waterproofing membrane with UV resistance, drainage falls (1:50), drainage outlet integration, movement joints at perimeter, tile bedding on pedestals or mortar, and balustrade fixing details.

**Q13.11:** Do you have details for pool tiling including expansion joints?

**A:** Yes. Pool tiling details include: pool shell waterproofing, mosaic tile installation on mesh sheets, expansion joints at 3m centres (perimeter and cross joints), gutter and overflow details, coping stone fixing, and pool equipment integration. Details reference SANS 10107 pool requirements.

**Q13.12:** Can you provide tile-on-tile renovation detail drawings?

**A:** Yes. Tile-on-tile details show: existing tile assessment (minimum adhesion 0.5 MPa), surface preparation (cleaning, scoring, priming), levelling compound application, adhesive specification for overlay, and maximum additional build-up height. Weight loading implications are noted.

**Q13.13:** Do you have details for tile installation in seismic zones?

**A:** Yes. Seismic details include: movement joint spacing calculations, flexible adhesive specification, perimeter isolation joints, structural movement accommodation, and post-seismic inspection requirements. Details are developed in consultation with structural engineers for seismic Zones 1-4.

**Q13.14:** Can I get details for ramped floor transitions (wheelchair access)?

**A:** Yes. Ramp details show: maximum 1:12 slope gradient, slip-resistant surface specification (R11 minimum), level landing at door thresholds, contrasting colour strip at level change, and handrail integration where required. Full SANS 10400-S compliance documented.

**Q13.15:** Do you provide details for tiled lift lobby and lift car flooring?

**A:** Yes. Lift lobby/car details include: level transition between lift car and landing, threshold plate integration, floor waste placement, slip resistance (R9-R10), and weight limitations for lift car floor build-up. Coordination with lift manufacturer requirements addressed.

**Q13.16:** Can you provide detail drawings for tile feature panels and inserts?

**A:** Yes. Feature panel details show: decorative tile border layout, inlay patterns, mosaic feature strip integration, recessed panel with lighting, and shadow gap details. Each detail includes: setting out dimensions, adhesive specification, and cutting requirements.

**Q13.17:** Do you have details for fixing tiles to lightweight steel frame walls?

**A:** Yes. Steel frame fixing details include: stud spacing requirements, cement fibre board specification, board fixing to studs, waterproofing (wet areas), adhesive selection by tile weight, maximum tile dimensions for stud spacing, and board joint treatment.

**Q13.18:** Can I get details for movement joints in large-format facade installations?

**A:** Yes. Facade movement joint details include: vertical movement joints at column lines, horizontal movement joints at floor slabs, corner movement accommodation, panel fixing allowing movement, and sealant specification for weatherproofing. Wind load and thermal movement calculations provided.

**Q13.19:** Do you provide details for tile cleaning and control joints in industrial floors?

**A:** Yes. Industrial floor details include: control joint saw-cut locations, joint filler specification (epoxy or polyurea), heavy-duty load transfer plates, chemical-resistant coving, drainage channel integration, and slip-resistant surface treatment.

**Q13.20:** Can I get 3D detail drawings for complex junctions?

**A:** Yes. 3D detail drawings (isometric and perspective) are available for complex junctions including: multi-plane wet area corners, stair landings with changes of direction, ramp-to-level transitions, and facade corner details. These aid contractor understanding and reduce installation errors.

**Q13.21:** Do you have details for tile installation over waterproofing membranes?

**A:** Yes. Membrane-to-tile details show: membrane type and thickness, priming requirements, compatible adhesive selection, protection requirements during construction, flood test protocol, and tile fixing method. Compatibility between membrane and adhesive is critical and verified.

**Q13.22:** Can you provide details for tiled fireplace surrounds and hearths?

**A:** Yes. Fireplace details include: non-combustible tile specification (A1 fire rating), minimum clearances to combustibles, hearth extension dimensions, expansion joint at firebox opening, and suitable adhesive/grout for temperature cycling. Full compliance with SANS 10400 Part T.

**Q13.23:** Do you have details for external step and threshold tiling?

**A:** Yes. External step details include: slip-resistant tread (R11-R12), drainage groove options, step nosing profile, falls away from building, movement joint at top and bottom of flight, and handrail fixing integration. Threshold details show: weatherproof sealing, drainage, and transition to internal floor.

**Q13.24:** Can I get details for tiled countertops and vanity tops?

**A:** Yes. Countertop details show: substrate (marine plywood or cement board), waterproofing, large-format or quartz slab installation, cut-out details for basins and taps, edge treatment (pencil edge, waterfall, or profile), and sealing at wall junction.

**Q13.25:** Do you provide setting out drawings for complex tile patterns?

**A:** Yes. Setting out drawings show: tile laying direction, pattern starting point, cut tile minimisation, border placement, feature panel centring, and symmetrical layout from focal points. These drawings help achieve the design intent and reduce installation waste.

**Q13.26:** Can I get details for tile repair and replacement without damaging adjacent tiles?

**A:** Yes. Repair details show: grout removal technique, tile removal (drill and chisel method), substrate preparation, adhesive re-application, replacement tile fitting, and grout matching. Colour blending advice for weathered adjacent tiles is also provided.

**Q13.27:** Do you have details for installation of tiles in cold rooms and freezers?

**A:** Yes. Cold room details include: floor insulation specification, vapour barrier placement, frost-resistant tile and adhesive, movement joints for thermal contraction, threshold details at door, and heating cable placement at door opening (anti-icing).

**Q13.28:** Can you provide details for tile splashbacks in commercial kitchens?

**A:** Yes. Kitchen splashback details show: full-height waterproofing behind cooking equipment, fire-rated substrate where required, grease-resistant tile and grout, sealed junctions at bench and overhead canopies, and access panels for services behind splashback.

**Q13.29:** Do you have details for tile expansion joints in long corridors?

**A:** Yes. Corridor expansion joint details show: joint placement at 6-8m centres, intermediate movement joints at 4-5m, profile specification (aluminium with rubber insert), colour matching options, and coordination with lighting and services layouts.

**Q13.30:** Can I get BIM detail components for common tile junctions?

**A:** Yes. Revit detail components are available for: movement joint profiles, drainage grates, transition strips, coving pieces, and stair nosings. These can be loaded into your Revit detail library and scaled appropriately for 1:5 and 1:10 detail drawings.

**Q13.31:** Do you provide photographic examples of installed detail work?

**A:** Yes. Our detail library includes photographs of installed details from completed projects, showing: actual construction quality, common pitfalls to avoid, and best-practice examples. These are valuable for contractor briefings and quality expectation setting.

**Q13.32:** Can you create custom details for an unusual project condition?

**A:** Yes. Our drawing office produces custom details for unique project conditions within 5-7 working days. Provide: sketches or reference images, dimensions, project constraints, and design intent. Custom details are provided in .dwg, .pdf, and Revit format.

**Q13.33:** Do you have details for acoustic isolation of tile floors?

**A:** Yes. Acoustic isolation details show: acoustic underlayment types (rubber, cork, foam), thickness by acoustic rating required, perimeter isolation strip, impact sound reduction values, and compliance with SANS 10400 Part K or BB93.

**Q13.34:** Can I get details for tile installation over existing timber floors?

**A:** Yes. Timber floor overlay details include: existing floor assessment (deflection ≤L/360), cement particle board overlay, flexible adhesive (S1 classification), movement joints at perimeter and board joints, and maximum tile weight limitation (typically 25kg/m²).

**Q13.35:** Do you provide details for tiled access panels and hatches?

**A:** Yes. Access panel details show: tile-compatible access panel frames, tile-infill panels, hinge and lock specifications, support framing, and alignment with surrounding tile layout. Panels are specified for: services access, drainage clean-outs, and underfloor services.


## Category 14: Peer Referral & Collaboration

**Q14.1:** Can Tiletoria provide named project references from other architects?

**A:** Yes. We provide named references from leading practices including: DHK, SAOTA, Boogertman + Partners, MDS Architecture, Incline Architects, and many others. References can be contacted directly to discuss their experience with our specification support, product quality, and project delivery.

**Q14.2:** Does Tiletoria collaborate on SAIA Awards submissions?

**A:** Yes. We support SAIA Awards of Excellence and Merit submissions by providing: project photography, product information for submission boards, technical data for innovation categories, and letters of support. We have contributed to multiple award-winning projects.

**Q14.3:** Can you arrange site visits to completed projects for reference purposes?

**A:** Yes. We arrange site visits to completed projects (with owner permission) for architects evaluating our products. Recent site visits have included: hotels, hospitals, residential estates, office buildings, and retail centres across South Africa.

**Q14.4:** Does Tiletoria sponsor architectural awards or competitions?

**A:** Yes. We sponsor select architectural awards and student competitions, including: SAIA regional awards, university design competitions, and emerging architect programmes. Sponsorship may include: prize funding, product vouchers, and publicity support.

**Q14.5:** Can Tiletoria participate in our practice's design review charrettes?

**A:** Yes. Our technical director and senior specification consultants participate in design charrettes, contributing: material selection advice, technical feasibility input, cost-performance analysis, and innovative product solutions. Participation is free for practice partners.

**Q14.6:** Do you facilitate networking events for architects and designers?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab hosts quarterly networking events including: new collection launches, design trend presentations, manufacturer visits, and peer-to-peer knowledge sharing. Events are invitation-only for registered specifiers and architects.

**Q14.7:** Can you connect me with architects who have used your products on similar projects?

**A:** Yes. We facilitate introductions to architects who have completed projects similar to yours — by building type, scale, or location. These peer connections enable: lessons learned sharing, specification advice, and professional networking.

**Q14.8:** Does Tiletoria collaborate with interior designers on joint projects?

**A:** Yes. We work closely with interior designers and interior architects, providing: material palette coordination, FF&E specification support, showroom visits with design focus, and joint CPD sessions (IID-accredited where applicable).

**Q14.9:** Can Tiletoria provide a testimonial letter for our practice's marketing?

**A:** Yes. Upon project completion, we provide testimonial letters confirming: products supplied, specification support provided, and project outcomes. These can be used in your practice's marketing materials, award submissions, and portfolio documentation.

**Q14.10:** Do you participate in architectural institute events (GIFA, PIA, CIfA, SAIA KZN)?

**A:** Yes. We are active participants in all regional architectural institutes, sponsoring events, exhibiting at conferences, delivering CPD sessions, and contributing to institute publications. Contact your regional institute for our upcoming participation schedule.

**Q14.11:** Can Tiletoria introduce us to other consultants for multidisciplinary projects?

**A:** Yes. Our professional network includes: structural engineers, MEP engineers, landscape architects, quantity surveyors, and specialist contractors. We facilitate introductions for projects requiring multidisciplinary coordination.

**Q14.12:** Do you have case studies of successful architect collaborations?

**A:** Yes. Our case study library documents successful collaborations with: specific architects, project types, and innovative product applications. Case studies include: project overview, design challenge, product solution, installation process, and outcomes.

**Q14.13:** Can Tiletoria host student groups from architecture schools?

**A:** Yes. We host student groups from Wits, UCT, KZN, TUT, and other architecture programmes. Tours include: product range overview, technical demonstration, sustainability discussion, and career guidance in architectural specification.

**Q14.14:** Do you collaborate with architectural photographers for project documentation?

**A:** Yes. We work with leading architectural photographers to document completed projects. High-resolution project photography is available for: your practice portfolio, our marketing, and industry publication editorial coverage.

**Q14.15:** Can Tiletoria provide expert speakers for our institute's technical event?

**A:** Yes. Our technical director and senior staff are available as expert speakers for: institute technical evenings, conference sessions, university guest lectures, and professional development workshops. Topics cover all aspects of tile specification and installation.

**Q14.16:** Does Tiletoria have a specifier recognition programme?

**A:** Yes. Our Specifier Recognition Programme acknowledges architects who consistently specify Tiletoria products. Benefits include: VIP event invitations, early access to new collections, dedicated support line, and co-marketing opportunities.

**Q14.17:** Can you arrange meetings with international manufacturers' technical teams?

**A:** Yes. For significant projects, we arrange video conferences or in-person meetings with international manufacturers' technical directors. These meetings cover: product customisation, technical queries, production capacity, and quality assurance protocols.

**Q14.18:** Do you participate in pro bono or community architecture projects?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria supports community architecture projects through: product donations, technical support, and specification advice. We have contributed to: school building projects, community centres, and healthcare clinics through architect-led pro bono programmes.

**Q14.19:** Can Tiletoria feature our project in your marketing materials?

**A:** Yes. Completed projects can be featured in: our product catalogues, website project gallery, social media, and industry publication advertisements. We seek your written permission and provide credit to your practice for all published content.

**Q14.20:** Do you have an architect advisory panel?

**A:** Yes. Our Architect Advisory Panel meets twice yearly to provide feedback on: product range development, service quality, CPD topics, and industry trends. Panel members are selected from diverse practice types and regions.

**Q14.21:** Can Tiletoria support our practice's research and development initiatives?

**A:** Yes. We support practice R&D through: product samples for prototyping, technical data for research papers, manufacturer introductions for collaborative development, and co-funding for innovation projects related to tiling and surfacing.

**Q14.22:** Do you collaborate with architectural publications and media?

**A:** Yes. We contribute to: Architecture SA, Visi, Habitat, Design Mind, and other architectural publications. Editorial contributions include: technical articles, product features, and project case studies. We can facilitate media coverage of your projects featuring our products.

**Q14.23:** Can Tiletoria introduce us to emerging architects and practices?

**A:** Yes. Through our emerging architect network, we facilitate connections between established and emerging practices for: mentorship, collaboration, knowledge sharing, and joint project opportunities.

**Q14.24:** Do you support architects' continuing professional development outside of formal CPD?

**A:** Yes. Beyond accredited CPD, we offer: informal technical workshops, product knowledge sessions, site visit learning opportunities, and manufacturer presentation tours. These support broader professional development even where formal credits are not available.

**Q14.25:** Can Tiletoria participate in our client design presentations?

**A:** Yes. Our senior staff can attend client presentations to: explain technical aspects of tile specification, present samples and mock-ups, answer product and performance questions, and provide credibility to the specification. This service is available for major projects.


## Category 15: Product Discontinuation & Lifecycle

**Q15.1:** How does Tiletoria notify architects when a specified product is discontinued?

**A:** We provide written discontinuation notices at least 6 months before a product is discontinued, sent via email to all registered specifiers who have used the product. Notices include: discontinuation date, replacement recommendations, technical comparison, and sample availability for evaluation.

**Q15.2:** What happens if a product is discontinued after I've specified it but before construction?

**A:** If discontinuation occurs post-specification, we immediately notify the architect and provide: the closest available replacement with full technical data, comparison sheets showing performance differences (if any), samples for approval, and assistance with specification amendment and substitution documentation.

**Q15.3:** Does Tiletoria provide end-of-life notifications for product lines?

**A:** Yes. Products approaching end-of-life are flagged 12 months in advance on our specifier portal. Quarterly 'product lifecycle' updates are included in our specifier newsletter, highlighting products with limited availability or planned discontinuation.

**Q15.4:** Can I get a product availability guarantee for the duration of my project?

**A:** Yes. For projects over R5 million, we provide a Product Availability Guarantee confirming: stock will be held for the contract duration, discontinuation will not occur without 12 months' notice, and replacement products will be performance-matched if substitution becomes necessary.

**Q15.5:** What is Tiletoria's typical product lifecycle for imported collections?

**A:** Imported collections typically remain available for 5-8 years, with 'core' ranges (plain colours, standard formats) often remaining indefinitely. Fashion-driven collections (decorative, trend colours) may have shorter lifecycles of 3-5 years. Lifecycle status is available on request.

**Q15.6:** Do you provide archive samples of discontinued products for matching?

**A:** Yes. We retain archive samples of discontinued products for 2-3 years after discontinuation. These can be used for: renovation matching, insurance replacement, and warranty assessment. Contact our archive team with the original product code.

**Q15.7:** Can you help me find a replacement for a tile specified from another supplier that has been discontinued?

**A:** Yes. Our Product Matching Service identifies the closest equivalent in our range based on: technical performance, aesthetic appearance, size/format, and price point. We provide comparison sheets and samples for evaluation, even for products originally specified from competitors.

**Q15.8:** How do I update my practice's specification library when products change?

**A:** We provide: updated specification clauses in digital format, notification of changes to product codes or descriptions, revised technical data sheets, and BIM object updates. Subscription to our specification update service ensures your library remains current.

**Q15.9:** Does Tiletoria have a policy on keeping 'classic' or 'evergreen' products available?


**Q15.10:** What happens to my warranty if a product is discontinued?

**A:** Discontinuation does not affect warranties on already-installed products. Warranty terms remain valid for the full warranty period. For projects mid-construction, we provide equivalent replacement products with identical or superior warranty coverage.

**Q15.11:** Can Tiletoria provide a product roadmap showing upcoming launches and planned discontinuations?

**A:** Yes. Our annual product roadmap (shared with practice partners and PSL-listed specifiers) outlines: planned new launches, range extensions, format additions, and planned discontinuations. This enables forward planning for multi-year projects.

**Q15.12:** Do you offer stockholding for projects with long construction programmes?

**A:** Yes. For projects over 12 months duration, we offer: bonded stockholding in our warehouse, scheduled call-off deliveries, batch consistency guarantees, and price protection for the contract duration. Minimum quantities and holding fees may apply.

**Q15.13:** How do I handle product discontinuation in a phased residential estate?

**A:** For phased estates, we recommend: specifying from our Permanent Collection, securing batch stock for the entire programme, or accepting a phased colour transition between phases. Our estate team provides continuity planning for multi-year developments.

**Q15.14:** Can you provide a letter confirming product availability for a specific contract period?

**A:** Yes. We issue Product Availability Letters confirming: the specified product will remain available for the contract duration (typically 12-24 months), stock will be reserved upon order, and notification will be given if circumstances change. These support contract documentation and client assurance.

**Q15.15:** What is the notification timeline if a manufacturing partner ceases production?

**A:** If a manufacturing partner ceases production, we receive immediate notification and pass this to specifiers within 5 working days. We simultaneously identify alternative suppliers, evaluate equivalent products, and begin transition planning for affected specifications.

**Q15.16:** Do you provide comparison data between current and superseded products?

**A:** Yes. Product supersession reports compare: technical performance, aesthetic characteristics, size availability, price, and warranty terms between old and new products. These reports support specification updates and client communication.

**Q15.17:** Can Tiletoria guarantee colour consistency between different production batches?

**A:** We guarantee shade consistency within a single order by supplying tiles from the same production batch. For extended projects requiring multiple deliveries, we coordinate production batch matching at the factory level. Shade variation between batches is managed through our V-rating system.

**Q15.18:** What is your policy on 'last chance' ordering for discontinued products?

**A:** When a product is discontinued, we announce a 'last chance' order window (typically 3 months) allowing specifiers to place final orders for: project completion, spare tiles for future repairs, and archive samples. After this window, the product is no longer available.

**Q15.19:** Do you track which architects have specified which products for discontinuation alerts?

**A:** Yes. Our specifier database tracks products specified by each practice, enabling targeted discontinuation alerts. Practices can also subscribe to product-specific alerts for their current and pipeline projects.

**Q15.20:** Can you help with specification revision when a product is discontinued?

**A:** Yes. Our specification revision service provides: revised specification clauses, substitution documentation for contractor notification, amended schedules, and updated drawings. We also notify the QS of any cost implications.

**Q15.21:** Does Tiletoria offer product customisation to avoid discontinuation issues?

**A:** For large ongoing programmes (residential estates, hotel chains), we can arrange: custom colour development with guaranteed availability for the programme duration, exclusive product ranges, and private label arrangements. Minimum annual volumes apply.

**Q15.22:** How do I find out if a product I've used on a past project is still available?

**A:** Contact our technical team with the product name, code, or a sample photograph. We can confirm: current availability status, replacement options if discontinued, and lead time if still available. Our product records extend back to 1995.

**Q15.23:** Do you provide spare tile recommendations at project completion?

**A:** Yes. We recommend retaining 3-5% spare tiles (minimum 2-3 boxes) for future repairs. major retail groupse tiles should be stored in a clean, dry environment and clearly labelled with product code, colour, batch number, and installation date.

**Q15.24:** Can Tiletoria provide a product history report for a specific tile line?

**A:** Yes. Product history reports track: launch date, range extensions, format additions, technical upgrades, and discontinuation date (if applicable). These reports are useful for: heritage assessment, warranty claims, and matching on renovation projects.

**Q15.25:** What contingencies do you have for supply chain disruptions affecting product availability?

**A:** Our supply chain resilience measures include: diversified manufacturing partnerships across 8+ countries, 6 months' buffer stock for core products, alternative supplier identification for all product categories, and air freight capability for urgent requirements.

**Q15.26:** Do you offer product lifecycle cost analysis for different specification options?

**A:** Yes. Our lifecycle cost analysis compares: initial supply cost, installation cost, maintenance costs (annual cleaning, periodic treatments), replacement cost, and disposal cost over a 25-40 year period. This supports informed specification decisions beyond first cost.

**Q15.27:** Can I get a 'product family' recommendation where multiple sizes share the same colour/finish?

**A:** Yes. Our product family recommendations identify ranges where the same colour and finish is available in multiple formats (e.g., 600x600mm, 600x1200mm, 1200x1200mm floor tiles plus matching wall tiles and mosaics). This ensures design continuity while optimising costs.

**Q15.28:** How do you manage batch-to-batch variation for large orders?

**A:** Large orders are produced in a single production run where possible. Where multiple batches are required, factory quality control ensures shade and calibre matching between batches. Pre-production samples are submitted for architect approval before main production begins.

**Q15.29:** Does Tiletoria have a product archive for research or matching purposes?

**A:** Yes. Our product archive contains: physical samples, technical data sheets, catalogues, and specification clauses for all products sold since 1995. The archive is accessible to specifiers for: research, matching, and historical project documentation.


## Category 16: Communication & Responsiveness

**Q16.1:** What is Tiletoria's technical query response time guarantee?

**A:** We guarantee a response to all technical queries within 4 hours during business hours (08:00-17:00, Monday-Friday). For urgent queries on active projects, we aim to respond within 1 hour. After-hours queries are handled via our on-call technical manager.

**Q16.2:** Does Tiletoria assign a dedicated architectural liaison to practices?

**A:** Yes. Practice partners and high-volume specifiers are assigned a dedicated architectural liaison who: knows your practice's preferences and project history, provides consistent communication, anticipates your needs, and escalates issues directly to technical management when required.

**Q16.3:** What communication channels are available for technical queries?

**A:** Technical queries can be submitted via: dedicated specifier email, telephone (direct line to technical team), WhatsApp (text and images for site queries), Microsoft Teams chat, and our online technical query portal. All channels are monitored during business hours.

**Q16.4:** Can I reach someone after hours for an urgent site issue?

**A:** Yes. Our on-call technical manager is available after hours (17:00-20:00 weekdays, and Saturday mornings) for urgent site issues including: installation problems, delivery queries, and waterproofing emergencies. The on-call number is provided to all practice partners.

**Q16.5:** How does Tiletoria handle RFI (Request for Information) responses during construction?

**A:** RFIs are logged in our project management system with guaranteed response within 24 hours. Responses include: technical answer, reference to relevant standards, and supporting documentation (detail drawings, test certificates) where applicable. Urgent RFIs are escalated for faster response.

**Q16.6:** Do you provide WhatsApp support for quick queries and site photos?

**A:** Yes. Our WhatsApp support line accepts: quick technical questions, site photos for problem diagnosis, sample requests, appointment bookings, and document requests. Responses are typically within 30 minutes during business hours.

**Q16.7:** What is the best way to contact Tiletoria for a complex specification query?

**A:** For complex queries, we recommend: email with supporting documents (drawings, specifications) to our technical team, followed by a scheduled video call if needed. This allows our team to review materials before the discussion, ensuring a productive and technically substantive conversation.

**Q16.8:** Does Tiletoria provide progress updates on large orders?

**A:** Yes. Large orders (R1 million+) receive: order confirmation within 24 hours, production progress updates (factory dependent), shipping notifications, customs clearance updates, and delivery scheduling confirmations. A dedicated project coordinator manages communication.

**Q16.9:** How do I escalate an issue if I'm not satisfied with the response?

**A:** If you're not satisfied with a response, escalate directly to: the Technical Director (technical matters), the Professional Services Manager (service matters), or the Managing Director (strategic matters). All escalations receive a response within 4 hours.

**Q16.10:** Do you provide a single point of contact for multi-disciplinary projects?

**A:** Yes. Multi-disciplinary projects are assigned a Project Lead who coordinates: specification support, sample management, technical queries, delivery logistics, and contractor liaison. The Project Lead provides consolidated communication across all project stakeholders.

**Q16.11:** Can I schedule regular check-in calls with my architectural liaison?

**A:** Yes. Practice partners can schedule: weekly check-in calls during active projects, monthly catch-up calls during design development, and quarterly business reviews covering: specification pipeline, service feedback, and upcoming product launches.

**Q16.12:** How does Tiletoria handle complaints or service failures?

**A:** Complaints are logged in our quality management system, investigated by management within 24 hours, and resolved with: corrective action, root cause analysis, preventive measures, and follow-up to ensure satisfaction. All complaints are reported to senior management monthly.

**Q16.13:** Do you provide site visit support for technical inspections?

**A:** Yes. Our technical team conducts site visits for: substrate inspection before tiling, installation quality checks, problem diagnosis, waterproofing flood test witness, and final inspection. Site visits are charged for projects under R2 million and included for larger projects.

**Q16.14:** Can I get a response in writing for all technical advice?

**A:** Yes. All technical advice can be provided in writing via email or formal letter. Written advice includes: reference to applicable standards, specific recommendations, and any caveats or limitations. Verbal advice is always confirmed in writing upon request.

**Q16.15:** Does Tiletoria use a CRM system to track practice interactions?

**A:** Yes. Our CRM system tracks: all interactions, specification history, project pipeline, sample loans, CPD attendance, and service preferences. This ensures continuity when your regular liaison is unavailable and enables personalised service based on your practice's history.

**Q16.16:** How do you handle technical queries from contractors on our projects?

**A:** Contractor queries are welcomed and answered. However, we always copy the architect on responses to contractor queries to maintain design team visibility. We do not authorise changes to the specification without written architect approval.

**Q16.17:** Can I get an out-of-office notification with alternative contacts when my liaison is away?

**A:** Yes. All staff out-of-office notifications include: alternative contact details, expected return date, and escalation contact. For planned absences (annual leave), we brief the covering liaison on your active projects.

**Q16.18:** Do you provide client feedback surveys?

**A:** Yes. We conduct: post-specification satisfaction surveys, post-delivery surveys, post-CPD feedback surveys, and annual client satisfaction surveys. Feedback is reviewed by management and drives our service improvement programme. Our NPS from architects averages 72.

**Q16.19:** What languages does your technical team communicate in?

**A:** Our technical team communicates primarily in English. Afrikaans support is available from our Cape Town and Pretoria-based staff. We are developing isiZulu and isiXhosa capability for KZN and Eastern Cape projects.

**Q16.20:** How does Tiletoria handle confidential or sensitive project information?

**A:** All project information is treated as confidential. We sign NDAs where required, operate under POPIA compliance, and restrict project information access to staff directly involved. Staff are bound by confidentiality clauses in their employment contracts.

**Q16.21:** Can I get copies of all correspondence related to my project?

**A:** Yes. Upon request, we provide a complete correspondence file for your project including: emails, technical advice letters, meeting minutes, site visit reports, and RFI responses. This supports project records and dispute resolution.

**Q16.22:** Do you provide a project handover report at completion?

**A:** Yes. Project handover reports include: products actually supplied (vs specification), batch numbers, warranty documentation, maintenance instructions, spare tile details, as-built documentation, and contact details for post-completion support.

**Q16.23:** How does Tiletoria handle public holiday and festive season communication?

**A:** During festive season closures (typically 20 December - 5 January), an emergency contact number is provided for urgent site issues. Email and WhatsApp are monitored daily for urgent matters. All non-urgent queries are queued for response on return.

**Q16.24:** Can I get a technical opinion letter signed by your Technical Director?

**A:** Yes. Formal technical opinion letters are signed by our Technical Director and include: scope of opinion, findings, references to applicable standards, and professional qualifications. These are used for: dispute resolution, insurance claims, and authority submissions.

**Q16.25:** Does Tiletoria provide out-of-hours emergency support for waterproofing failures?

**A:** Yes. For waterproofing emergencies (active leaks, failed flood tests), our on-call technical manager provides telephone guidance outside business hours. On-site attendance can be arranged within 24 hours for critical situations. Emergency call-out fees may apply.


## Category 17: Showroom & Library Access

**Q17.1:** Where is the Spec Lab / Spec Lab located?

**A:** The Spec Lab is located at Spec Lab Menlyn, Johannesburg. It is a dedicated professional showroom for architects, interior designers, property developers, and specifiers, featuring high-end imported collections from Italy, Spain, and other top international manufacturers.

**Q17.2:** What are the Spec Lab opening hours?

**A:** The Spec Lab is open Monday to Friday 08:00-17:00 and Saturday 09:00-13:00. Private consultations and after-hours appointments can be arranged for practices requiring exclusive access or confidential client presentations.

**Q17.3:** How do I book a private consultation at the Spec Lab?

**A:** Book a private consultation via: email to specstudio@tiletoria.co.za, phone (011) 656-6100, or our online booking portal. Specify your project type, preferred date/time, and areas of interest so we can prepare relevant samples and presentations.

**Q17.4:** Does the Spec Lab offer virtual tours for architects outside Johannesburg?

**A:** Yes. Our virtual tour provides a 360° walkthrough of the Spec Lab, allowing remote exploration of displays, sample libraries, and room-set mock-ups. Virtual tours are hosted on our website and can be combined with a live video consultation with our technical team.

**Q17.5:** Can I bring my client to the Spec Lab for a presentation?

**A:** Yes. Client presentations at the Spec Lab are welcome and encouraged. We provide: private meeting spaces, sample preparation in advance, presentation boards, technical staff on hand to answer questions, and refreshments. Book at least 48 hours in advance.

**Q17.6:** Is there parking available at the Spec Lab?

**A:** Yes. Secure parking is available on-site at Spec Lab Menlyn. Visitor parking bays are located directly in front of the showroom entrance. Security personnel are present during all opening hours.

**Q17.7:** Does the Spec Lab have all Tiletoria products on display?

**A:** The Spec Lab showcases our premium and mid-range collections with full-size samples of all major product lines. Our complete product range is accessible through the Tile Library, where samples of every stocked item can be retrieved for examination.

**Q17.8:** What is the Tile Library?

**A:** The Tile Library is a comprehensive physical archive of all Tiletoria products, organised by type (porcelain, ceramic, mosaic, stone, LVT, etc.). Architects can browse the library, compare samples side-by-side, and borrow samples for project evaluation. New collections are added as they are launched.

**Q17.9:** Can I borrow samples from the Spec Lab to take to site or client meetings?

**A:** Yes. Our sample loan service allows you to borrow full-size samples, sample boards, and sample cases for up to 2 weeks. A signed loan agreement covers care and return. Duplicate sample sets can be prepared for multiple stakeholders.

**Q17.10:** Does the Spec Lab have room-set displays showing installed effects?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab features permanent room-set displays including: bathroom mock-ups (walk-in shower, vanity area, freestanding tub), kitchen splashback displays, hotel lobby vignettes, commercial office settings, and residential living spaces. These show installed effects under realistic lighting.

**Q17.11:** Can I book the Spec Lab for a CPD session for my practice?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab is available for private CPD sessions for groups of 6-40 architects. Sessions include: dedicated presentation area, AV equipment, sample examination, lunch/refreshments, and SAIA-accredited CPD certificates. Book 2-3 weeks in advance.

**Q17.12:** Is the Spec Lab accessible for architects with mobility impairments?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab has: level access from parking, wide doorways and aisles between displays, accessible restroom facilities, and display samples at wheelchair height. Please inform us of any accessibility requirements when booking.

**Q17.13:** Can I see large-format tiles (1200x2600mm, 1600x3200mm) at the Spec Lab?

**A:** Yes. Full-size large-format tiles and sintered stone slabs are displayed at the Spec Lab, allowing you to appreciate the scale, impact, and surface quality of these products. Lifting and handling demonstrations can be arranged for contractor briefings.

**Q17.14:** Does the Spec Lab host new collection preview events?

**A:** Yes. New collection previews are hosted quarterly, inviting registered specifiers to be the first to see and evaluate new products. Previews include: manufacturer presentations, sample examination, technical briefings, and exclusive pre-order opportunities.

**Q17.15:** Can I schedule a virtual consultation instead of visiting in person?

**A:** Yes. Virtual consultations via Microsoft Teams or Zoom include: live showroom walkthrough (staff-guided), sample close-ups, technical discussion, and digital document sharing. Virtual consultations are popular with out-of-town and international specifiers.


**Q17.17:** Can I bring my interior design team to the Spec Lab for a collaborative session?

**A:** Yes. Collaborative sessions for architecture and interior design teams are encouraged. We can prepare: cross-discipline sample selections, palette coordination boards, and technical briefings relevant to both architectural and interior design specifications.

**Q17.18:** Is there a cost to visit or use the Spec Lab facilities?

**A:** No. Access to the Spec Lab, Tile Library, sample examination, and technical consultations is free of charge for registered architects, interior designers, and specifiers. CPD sessions, sample loans, and mock-up production are also free for qualifying projects.

**Q17.19:** Does the Spec Lab have displays of tile accessories and profiles?

**A:** Yes. Accessory displays include: movement joint profiles (aluminium, brass, stainless steel), transition strips, stair nosing profiles, drain grates, coving pieces, skirting profiles, and edge trims. These can be examined alongside tile samples for coordination.

**Q17.20:** Can I see different grout colour effects on the same tile at the Spec Lab?

**A:** Yes. Grout comparison displays show the same tile with different grout colours and joint widths, demonstrating the significant impact grout selection has on the final appearance. This is particularly valuable for large-format tiles and mosaic installations.

**Q17.21:** Does the Spec Lab have outdoor/paving tile displays?

**A:** Yes. External displays showcase: 20mm structural pavers, pedestal-supported paving systems, pool surround tiles, ramp tiles, and outdoor stair solutions. Displays show installed effects including drainage falls and joint patterns.

**Q17.22:** Can I take photographs at the Spec Lab for my project documentation?

**A:** Yes. Photography for project documentation and client presentations is permitted. For social media or publication photography, please seek permission from staff. High-resolution professional photography of our displays is available on request.

**Q17.23:** Does the Spec Lab have a material testing area or technical demonstration space?

**A:** Yes. Our technical demonstration area hosts: slip resistance testing demonstrations, stain resistance tests, abrasion comparisons, and large-format handling demonstrations. Technical CPD sessions are delivered in this space with hands-on participation.

**Q17.24:** Can I request specific samples to be prepared before my visit?

**A:** Yes. When booking your appointment, list the products or project types you're interested in, and our team will prepare: relevant samples, comparison boards, technical data sheets, and specification clauses. This maximises the productivity of your visit.

**Q17.25:** Is the Spec Lab child-friendly if I need to bring my child?

**A:** While we welcome visitors of all ages, please note that the Spec Lab contains heavy tile samples and display materials. Children must be supervised at all times. We recommend scheduling child-free visits for detailed technical consultations.

**Q17.26:** Can I purchase tiles directly from the Spec Lab?

**A:** The Spec Lab is a professional specification facility, not a retail store. Tiles are specified here for supply through Tiletoria's commercial sales channels. For retail purchases, visit a Tiletoria retail store or our online shop.

**Q17.27:** Does the Spec Lab have Wi-Fi for working during visits?

**A:** Yes. Free Wi-Fi is available throughout the Spec Lab. Power points for laptop charging are available in the meeting/consultation areas. The space is designed for productive working sessions.

**Q17.28:** Can I book a group visit for my architecture student class?

**A:** Yes. Student group visits (maximum 20 students) can be arranged with advance booking. Tours include: product range overview, technical demonstration, sustainability discussion, and career guidance. We regularly host students from Wits, UJ, and other institutions.

**Q17.29:** Does the Spec Lab have facilities for client entertainment or events?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab can host: client entertainment events, practice year-end functions, product launch events, and industry networking functions. Facilities include: AV equipment, catering space, bar area, and valet parking arrangements.

**Q17.30:** Can I request a callback instead of visiting the showroom?

**A:** Yes. If you prefer not to visit in person, request a video callback. Our team will: walk through relevant displays with a camera, show samples up close, answer technical questions live, and courier samples to your office for evaluation.

**Q17.31:** Is the Spec Lab located near public transport?

**A:** The Spec Lab in Menlyn is approximately 2km from the Gautrain Sandton station. Uber and taxi services are readily available. For those driving, secure on-site parking is provided. Directions are sent with booking confirmations.

**Q17.32:** Does the Spec Lab have displays specifically for healthcare/hospital projects?

**A:** Yes. Healthcare-specific displays include: antimicrobial tiles, slip-resistant flooring, coved skirting systems, operating theatre finishes, and hygiene-critical junction details. These are accompanied by healthcare compliance documentation.

**Q17.33:** Can I see mosaic displays at the Spec Lab?

**A:** Yes. Mosaic displays include: glass mosaics (iridescent, frosted, metallic), stone mosaics, porcelain mosaics, metal mosaics, and mixed material designs. Mosaics are displayed as full sheets and in installed mock-ups (shower niches, feature walls, pool waterlines).

**Q17.34:** Does the Spec Lab have a coffee shop or refreshment area?

**A:** Yes. A hospitality area provides complimentary coffee, tea, and light refreshments during your visit. For extended consultations or group sessions, catering (breakfast, lunch, or snacks) can be arranged in advance.


## Category 18: Comparison with Competitor Suppliers

**Q18.1:** How does Tiletoria's technical documentation compare to other tile suppliers?

**A:** Tiletoria provides the most comprehensive technical documentation in the South African market, including: full SANS 1449:2012 certificates, DIN 51130 slip resistance data, EPDs, BIM objects, NBS-format specifications, and detail drawings. Many competitors provide only basic product brochures.

**Q18.2:** What makes Tiletoria's CPD programme different from other suppliers?

**A:** Three key differentiators: (1) SAIA Category One accreditation on all sessions, (2) Purely technical content with no product sales pitches, (3) Presenters are qualified engineers and technologists, not sales staff. Our NPS from CPD attendees averages 72, significantly above industry norms.

**Q18.3:** Does Tiletoria offer better BIM resources than competitor suppliers?

**A:** Tiletoria is the only major South African tile supplier with: full ProdLib integration, parametric Revit/ArchiCAD/AutoCAD objects, NBS Chorus compatibility, and quarterly BIM updates. Most competitors offer only basic 2D CAD blocks or no digital resources at all.

**Q18.4:** How does your response time compare to other tile suppliers?

**A:** Our 4-hour technical query response guarantee is industry-leading. Independent surveys show competitor response times averaging 24-72 hours for technical queries. For urgent site issues, we aim to respond within 1 hour.

**Q18.5:** Does Tiletoria have a wider product range than competitors?


**Q18.6:** How does Tiletoria's specification support compare to other suppliers?

**A:** Few suppliers offer the depth of specification support that Tiletoria provides: pre-authored NBS clauses, specification review service, value engineering analysis, mock-up production, and dedicated project liaisons. Most competitors expect architects to write their own specifications.

**Q18.7:** Is Tiletoria's pricing competitive compared to other premium tile suppliers?

**A:** Tiletoria's pricing is competitive within the premium segment. While budget suppliers may offer lower prices, our value proposition includes: superior technical support, comprehensive documentation, CPD credits, BIM objects, specification support, and reliable delivery — reducing project risk and total cost.

**Q18.8:** How does the Spec Lab compare to competitor showrooms?

**A:** The Spec Lab is the only dedicated professional tile specification facility in South Africa designed specifically for architects and designers. Unlike retail showrooms, it offers: private consultations, CPD delivery, technical demonstrations, the Tile Library, and direct access to qualified technical staff.

**Q18.9:** Does Tiletoria have better project track record than competitors?

**A:** Tiletoria has supplied to more major projects than any other South African tile distributor, including: Mall of Africa, Sandton City, Discovery headquarters, Netcare hospitals, Sun International hotels, and numerous residential estates. Our 25+ year track record demonstrates reliability.

**Q18.10:** How does your sample and mock-up service compare to competitors?

**A:** Tiletoria offers: free sample loans, free mock-up panels for projects over R3 million, architect approval sample documentation, and custom presentation boards. Many competitors charge for samples and do not offer mock-up services.

**Q18.11:** What sustainability credentials does Tiletoria have that competitors don't?

**A:** Tiletoria leads in sustainability documentation: EPDs for major ranges, Green Star SA support, recycled content verification, carbon footprint data, and WELL Building Standard contribution data. Few competitors offer any sustainability documentation.

**Q18.12:** Does Tiletoria provide better warranty terms than competitors?

**A:** Our standard 10-year manufacturing defect warranty matches industry norms, but our system warranties (tile+adhesive+waterproofing) of up to 25 years exceed most competitors. Warranty validity is supported by our comprehensive method statements and installation oversight.

**Q18.13:** How does your technical team qualification compare to competitors?

**A:** Our technical team includes: qualified ceramic engineers, civil engineers, and architectural technologists with an average 15+ years industry experience. Most competitor technical staff are former sales staff with limited formal technical qualifications.

**Q18.14:** Does Tiletoria offer digital specification tools that competitors don't?

**A:** Yes. Our digital tools include: ProdLib BIM integration, NBS Chorus specification linking, virtual showroom tours, digital sample books, online CPD booking, and a specifier portal for document access. These digital capabilities are not matched by any South African competitor.

**Q18.15:** How does Tiletoria's imported collection range compare to competitors?

**A:** Tiletoria directly imports from 15+ leading international manufacturers, offering exclusive access to Italian and Spanish premium collections not available through other South African distributors. Our direct factory relationships enable customisation and competitive pricing.

**Q18.16:** What makes Tiletoria more reliable for large commercial projects than competitors?

**A:** Large project reliability factors: 25+ year track record, dedicated project management, bonded stockholding, batch consistency guarantees, air freight contingency, financial stability, and professional indemnity insurance. Smaller competitors often lack the infrastructure for major projects.

**Q18.17:** Does Tiletoria have better relationships with architectural institutes than competitors?

**A:** Tiletoria maintains formal relationships with: SAIA national office, all regional institutes (GIFA, PIA, CIfA, SAIA KZN, SAIA EC), IID, and GBCSA. We are the preferred CPD provider for many institutes, reflecting our commitment to professional development.

**Q18.18:** How does your discontinuation management compare to competitors?

**A:** We provide 12 months' advance notice of discontinuation, replacement recommendations, specification revision support, and archive samples for 2-3 years. Many competitors provide little or no notice, causing specification disruption.

**Q18.19:** Does Tiletoria offer more comprehensive detail drawings than competitors?

**A:** Yes. Our detail library includes 200+ CAD details covering: movement joints, drainage, transitions, coving, skirting, stairs, wet areas, facades, and underfloor heating. Most competitors provide minimal or no detail drawings.

**Q18.20:** How does your CPD quality compare to competitor supplier CPD?

**A:** Independent architect feedback consistently rates Tiletoria CPD as the highest quality in the industry. Key feedback themes: 'purely technical, no sales pitch', 'presenters know their subject', 'relevant to real projects', 'well-organised and professional'.

**Q18.21:** Does Tiletoria provide better post-specification support than competitors?

**A:** Our post-specification support includes: submittal review, RFI response, site inspections, contractor briefings, problem diagnosis, and as-built documentation. Many competitors consider their role complete at the point of sale.

**Q18.22:** What innovation does Tiletoria offer that competitors don't?

**A:** Innovations include: sintered stone/slim porcelain expertise, large-format installation technical support, antimicrobial tile specification, photocatalytic air-purifying tiles, BIM integration, and digital specification tools. We invest in emerging technologies ahead of competitors.

**Q18.23:** How does Tiletoria's stockholding and delivery capacity compare?

**A:** We maintain over 20,000m² of warehouse space with buffer stock for core products. Our logistics capability handles: full container loads, mixed pallets, express deliveries, and national distribution. Many smaller competitors order on demand with 8-12 week lead times.

**Q18.24:** Does Tiletoria have a better understanding of South African building standards than international suppliers?

**A:** Our technical team has deep expertise in SANS standards (1449, 10107, 10400), NHBRC requirements, and local building practices — expertise that international suppliers lack. We bridge the gap between international products and local compliance requirements.

**Q18.25:** How does your practice partnership programme compare to competitor loyalty schemes?

**A:** Our Practice Partnership Programme offers: dedicated liaison, priority support, annual CPD scheduling, early access to new products, joint marketing, and volume pricing. It is a genuine professional relationship, not a points-based loyalty scheme.

**Q18.26:** Does Tiletoria offer more comprehensive wet area system warranties than competitors?

**A:** Yes. Our integrated wet area system warranty (up to 25 years) covers: waterproofing membrane, tiles, adhesive, and grout as a single system. Most competitors offer separate, limited warranties or no system warranty at all.

**Q18.27:** How does Tiletoria handle technical complexity better than competitors?
