**A:** Complex technical projects benefit from: our multi-disciplinary technical team, international manufacturer technical backup, structural engineering consultation, and 25+ years of problem-solving experience. We thrive on technically challenging projects that competitors avoid.

**Q18.28:** Does Tiletoria have a stronger national presence than competitors?

**A:** With direct operations in Gauteng, KZN, and Western Cape, plus project support nationwide, we offer broader geographic coverage than most competitors who are concentrated in single provinces.

**Q18.29:** How does Tiletoria's PSL inclusion process compare to competitors?

**A:** Our PSL process is streamlined and professional: comprehensive documentation pack, practice presentation capability, B-BBEE Level 2 status, reference network, and flexible procurement terms. We understand what major practices require and deliver it efficiently.

**Q18.30:** Does Tiletoria provide better product training for contractors than competitors?

**A:** Yes. We offer: accredited contractor training, large-format installation certification, waterproofing applicator training, and project-specific contractor briefings. Well-trained contractors ensure your specification is installed correctly.

**Q18.31:** How does Tiletoria's product quality compare to direct international sourcing?

**A:** Our direct factory relationships ensure product quality equal to international sourcing, with the added benefits of: local technical support, SANS compliance verification, local warranty, and no import logistics complexity. Quality is consistent and verified.

**Q18.32:** Does Tiletoria offer more competitive pricing for large-volume projects?

**A:** Our volume pricing for large projects (R5 million+) is highly competitive, leveraging: direct factory relationships, container-load logistics, and efficient distribution. We provide transparent pricing with value engineering options to meet budget constraints.


---

# Supplementary Q&A: Additional Topics

The following Q&A pairs cover advanced, specialist, and niche topics that extend beyond the 18 primary categories.


## Supplementary Section 1: Additional Topics: Advanced CPD, Technical Data & Specification Deep-Dive

**S1.1:** Can I attend a Tiletoria CPD session if I'm not currently practicing in South Africa?

**A:** Yes. Our CPD sessions are open to architects registered with other national institutes, provided you hold valid professional registration. CPD certificates will reflect attendance but may need to be submitted to your home institute for credit recognition.

**S1.2:** Do you offer CPD sessions on emerging tile technologies like 3D printing?

**A:** Yes. Our innovation CPD covers: 3D printed tiles, digital inkjet advancement, sintered stone technology, photocatalytic surfaces, and smart tiles with integrated sensors. This session is updated annually to reflect technological developments.

**S1.3:** Can I earn CPD credits for writing a technical article about tile specification?

**A:** While we don't directly award credits for article writing, we can facilitate publication opportunities in Architecture SA and other journals. Credits would be claimed through SAIA's self-directed learning category, which we can guide you through.

**S1.4:** Do you offer CPD for landscape architects?

**A:** Selected CPD sessions are relevant to landscape architects, particularly those covering: outdoor paving, facade tiles, pool tiling, and sustainable materials. SACLAP credit recognition may be possible — check with the South African Council for the Landscape Architectural Profession.

**S1.5:** Can your CPD sessions be structured as interactive workshops rather than lectures?

**A:** Yes. Workshop-format CPD includes: hands-on slip resistance testing, adhesive mixing and application demonstrations, mock-up construction, and problem-solving case studies. Workshops carry the same SAIA Category One accreditation as lecture sessions.

**S1.6:** Do you record attendance for CPD sessions for SACAP audit purposes?

**A:** Yes. All attendance is recorded with: full name, SACAP registration number (where provided), session topic, date, credit value, and certificate number. This data is retained for 7 years and can be provided as evidence during SACAP audits.

**S1.7:** Can I combine multiple short CPD sessions to reach a full day's credit?

**A:** Yes. Multiple sessions can be combined. For example: three 0.5-credit sessions and one 1.5-credit session combine to 3.0 credits (a typical full-day value). We can structure a bespoke programme to meet your credit target.

**S1.8:** Do you offer CPD on the business aspects of tile specification?

**A:** Our 'Professional Practice' CPD covers: specification writing for liability protection, substitution clause strategies, fee structures for specification services, and professional indemnity considerations related to material specification.

**S1.9:** Can I get a CPD session recorded for my practice's internal training library?

**A:** Recording of CPD sessions is permitted for internal practice use only, with written permission from Tiletoria. Recordings cannot be shared externally or uploaded to public platforms. SAIA credits are only awarded for live attendance.

**S1.10:** Do you offer CPD on international standards comparison (SANS vs EN vs ASTM)?

**A:** Yes. Our International Standards Comparison CPD covers: SANS 1449 vs EN 14411 vs ASTM C373, DIN 51130 vs pendulum test, ISO vs ASTM test methods, and how to specify when international consultants are involved.

**S1.11:** Can you provide Vicat softening point data for PVC edge trims?

**A:** Vicat softening point data for PVC accessories is available for projects where heat resistance of trim components is critical (e.g., near cooking equipment, high-temperature process areas). Aluminium alternatives are recommended for high-heat applications.

**S1.12:** Do you have data on tile thermal conductivity for underfloor heating efficiency?

**A:** Porcelain tile thermal conductivity typically ranges from 1.2-1.8 W/mK, making tiles excellent conductors for underfloor heating efficiency. We provide thermal conductivity data for energy modelling and underfloor heating system design.

**S1.13:** What is the specific heat capacity of porcelain tiles?

**A:** Porcelain tile specific heat capacity is approximately 0.84 kJ/kg·K, contributing to thermal mass benefits in passive design. Combined with density of 2,300-2,400 kg/m³, porcelain provides significant thermal storage capacity per m² of floor area.

**S1.14:** Can you provide data on tile reflectance values for daylight modelling?

**A:** Light Reflectance Values (LRV) range from 5% (black polished) to 85% (white matte) for our tiles. LRV data is provided for daylight modelling, Green Star IEQ-11 compliance, and SANS 10400-S colour contrast requirements.

**S1.15:** Do you have pendulum test slip resistance data (AS 4586)?

**A:** Yes. In addition to DIN 51130, we provide AS 4586 pendulum test data (Wet Pendulum Class W, Oil-Wet Inclining Platform Class R) for projects requiring Australian standard compliance or export facility specification.

**S1.16:** What is the maximum temperature resistance of your tiles?

**A:** Standard porcelain tiles are suitable for continuous exposure up to 200°C and intermittent exposure to 300°C. Special high-temperature tiles for industrial applications withstand up to 600°C. Temperature limitations are determined by the glaze type — full-body tiles have higher thermal resistance.

**S1.17:** Can you provide data on tile resistance to UV degradation for exterior applications?

**A:** Our exterior-grade tiles undergo 2,000+ hours of QUV accelerated weathering testing per ASTM G154. Results show: colour stability ΔE<1.0, no surface degradation, and maintained slip resistance. 10-year exterior warranty available with approved installation.

**S1.18:** Do you have data on the electrical resistivity of your tiles?

**A:** Electrical resistivity data is available for anti-static and electrostatic discharge (ESD) applications. Standard porcelain resistivity: 10¹²-10¹⁴ Ω. Anti-static tiles: 10⁶-10⁹ Ω. ESD tiles: 10⁴-10⁶ Ω. Data supports IEC 61340-5-1 compliance.


**S1.20:** What is the bond strength of your recommended tile adhesives?

**A:** Our recommended C2TES1 adhesives achieve: initial adhesion ≥1.0 N/mm², adhesion after water immersion ≥1.0 N/mm², adhesion after thermal cycling ≥1.0 N/mm², and shear adhesion ≥2.0 N/mm². These values exceed SANS 10107 minimum requirements.

**S1.21:** How do I specify tiles for a building with multiple tenancies and different requirements?

**A:** Multi-tenancy specifications use a 'base build + tenant fit-out' approach: specify standard commercial-grade tiles for common areas, circulation, and toilets; provide tenant design guidelines for shop-fit areas; and include performance criteria that all tenant tiles must meet.

**S1.22:** Can you provide a master specification that covers all possible tile applications in a mixed-use building?

**A:** Yes. Our mixed-use master specification includes: sections for residential, retail, office, hospitality, and parking components; each with appropriate performance criteria, installation methods, and detail references. The master spec is edited to delete irrelevant sections per component.

**S1.23:** How should I specify tiles for a project with phased handover?

**A:** Phased handover specifications include: stockholding requirements, batch consistency guarantees across phases, price protection clauses, and continuity requirements. Specify from our Permanent Collection to minimise discontinuation risk between phases.

**S1.24:** What is the best way to specify tiles for a PPP (Public Private Partnership) project?

**A:** PPP specifications require: performance-based criteria (not proprietary), whole-life cost analysis, maintainability requirements, warranty periods aligned to concession period, and transfer-condition requirements. We provide PPP-compliant specification templates.

**S1.25:** How do I write a tile specification for a BOT (Build-Operate-Transfer) project?

**A:** BOT specifications emphasise: durability (25+ year service life), low maintenance, lifecycle cost optimisation, warranty aligned to operation period, and transfer-condition standards. Our technical team can assist with lifecycle cost modelling for BOT specifications.

**S1.26:** Can you provide specification clauses for repair and maintenance during the defects liability period?

**A:** Yes. DLP specification clauses cover: defect notification procedures, repair methodology, replacement tile sourcing (same batch if available), access requirements, and making good of adjacent work. These protect both architect and contractor during the liability period.

**S1.27:** How do I specify tiles when the final colour selection will be made by the client after contract award?

**A:** Use a 'schedule of rates' approach: specify the tile type, size, performance criteria, and installation method in the contract documents; include a provisional sum for the tile supply; and state that final colour selection from the approved range will be made by the client/contractor with architect approval.

**S1.28:** What should I include in a tile specification for a design competition entry?

**A:** Competition specifications should include: design intent description, indicative performance criteria, preliminary product suggestions, and budget guidance. Avoid locking in specific products at competition stage — focus on aesthetic and performance intent.

**S1.29:** Can you help me write a tile specification that complies with SANS 10400-XA (energy efficiency)?

**A:** Yes. SANS 10400-XA compliance through tile specification includes: thermal mass contribution (specify thick porcelain on slab), light-reflective tiles for daylighting (LRV >60%), and integration with underfloor heating/cooling systems.

**S1.30:** How do I specify tiles for a project that will be LEED-certified?

**A:** LEED tile specification includes: EPD documentation (MR credit Building Product Disclosure), recycled content percentage (MR credit Sourcing of Raw Materials), VOC emissions data (EQ credit Low-Emitting Materials), and regional material status (within 800km for MR credit Regional Materials).

**S1.31:** Can Tiletoria BIM objects be used in Dynamo or Grasshopper scripts?

**A:** Yes. Revit family parameters can be accessed and manipulated through Dynamo scripts, enabling: parametric tile layout generation, automatic quantity extraction, pattern optimisation, and design option comparison. Sample Dynamo scripts are available from our digital team.

**S1.32:** Do you provide BIM objects for tile patterns like herringbone and basketweave?

**A:** Yes. Pattern-specific Revit families are available for: herringbone (single and double), basketweave, running bond, stacked bond, modular (French pattern), and pinwheel. Each family includes correct joint spacing and tile orientation parameters.

**S1.33:** Can I use Tiletoria BIM objects in Navisworks for clash detection?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria BIM objects exported to .ifc or .nwc format can be used in Navisworks clash detection, particularly for: drainage intersections, movement joint locations, service penetrations, and transition detail coordination.

**S1.34:** Do you provide Level of Development (LOD) specifications for tile BIM objects?

**A:** Yes. Our BIM objects are developed to LOD 300 for design development and construction documentation. LOD 400 (fabrication-level) objects are available for prefabricated tile panel systems and complex facade installations.

**S1.35:** Can Tiletoria BIM objects generate automatic material take-offs?

**A:** Yes. When properly scheduled in Revit, Tiletoria BIM objects automatically calculate: tile quantities (including wastage), adhesive volumes, grout quantities, and accessory counts. This data exports to Excel for QS pricing and procurement.

**S1.36:** Do you provide guidance on BIM object naming conventions for our practice?

**A:** Yes. Our BIM Naming Convention Guide aligns with UK BIM Framework (Uniclass 2015) and can be customised to your practice's standards. The guide covers: object naming, parameter naming, material naming, and family categorisation.

**S1.37:** Can I get a Revit template pre-loaded with Tiletoria materials and families?

**A:** Yes. A Revit template (.rte) pre-loaded with: Tiletoria material library, common tile families, tile schedule templates, and detail components is available for practice partners. This jump-starts project setup with tile content ready to use.

**S1.38:** Do you provide clash detection matrices for tile vs MEP coordination?

**A:** Yes. Clash detection priority matrices identify: high-priority clashes (drainage, floor wastes, expansion joints), medium-priority (transition profiles at doorways), and low-priority (aesthetic coordination). Resolution workflows are provided for each clash type.

**S1.39:** Can Tiletoria BIM data be exported to FM systems like IBM TRIRIGA or Archibus?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria COBie data exports in .xlsx format compatible with major FM systems including: IBM TRIRIGA, Archibus, FAMIS, and Qube Planet. Data includes: asset tags, warranty dates, maintenance schedules, and replacement costs.

**S1.40:** Do you provide Revit detail items for tile installation sections?

**A:** Yes. Revit detail items (2D section components) are available for: floor sections, wall sections, movement joints, drainage details, and transition details. These are loaded into your detail library and scaled for 1:5 and 1:10 detail drawings.

**S1.41:** Can you produce a mock-up showing tile ageing and weathering effects?

**A:** Yes. Accelerated weathering mock-ups using QUV exposure chambers demonstrate: colour stability, surface degradation, and slip resistance changes over time. These are valuable for exterior applications and long-term performance expectations.

**S1.42:** Do you provide 'room box' mock-ups showing tile in a miniature room setting?

**A:** Yes. Room box mock-ups (typically 1:10 or 1:20 scale) show: tile layout, joint patterns, colour combinations, and lighting effects in a miniature room setting. These are popular for client presentations and design competitions.

**S1.43:** Can you create a digital mock-up rendering before producing a physical mock-up?

**A:** Yes. Digital renderings (photorealistic 3D visualisations) of proposed tile installations can be produced before committing to physical mock-ups. This saves time and cost during the design development phase.

**S1.44:** Do you provide 'sample chains' showing gradient colour progressions across a range?

**A:** Yes. Colour gradient sample chains show the progression from light to dark across a tile range's colour palette, helping architects understand the full spectrum and select intermediate shades that may not be obvious from individual samples.

**S1.45:** Can you produce a mock-up panel with intentional defects to show quality standards?

**A:** Yes. 'Good vs bad' mock-up panels can be produced showing: acceptable vs unacceptable lippage, proper vs improper joint spacing, correct vs incorrect grout application, and acceptable vs unacceptable shade variation. These are valuable for contractor briefings.

**S1.46:** What is the difference between rectified and non-rectified (calibrated) tiles?

**A:** Rectified tiles are mechanically trimmed after firing to achieve precise dimensions (±0.2% tolerance), enabling narrow joints (1.5-2mm). Non-rectified (calibrated) tiles have natural fired dimensions (±0.5% tolerance) requiring wider joints (3-5mm) to accommodate variation.

**S1.47:** Do you offer tiles with rectified edges in large formats?

**A:** Yes. Most of our large-format tiles (600x1200mm and above) are rectified, enabling minimal joint widths that enhance the seamless, monolithic appearance that architects specify for contemporary interiors.

**S1.48:** What is 'double-charged' porcelain and when should I specify it?

**A:** Double-charged porcelain has a thicker layer of coloured powder pressed into the tile body before firing, creating a deeper pattern penetration (2-3mm) than standard glazed tiles. Specify for: high-traffic commercial areas where surface wear is a concern, and applications requiring through-pattern appearance.

**S1.49:** Can you explain the difference between R10, R11, and R12 slip ratings?

**A:** R10: moderate slip resistance, suitable for dry commercial interiors and occasional wet exposure. R11: good slip resistance, suitable for commercial kitchens, bathrooms, and external walkways. R12: high slip resistance, suitable for industrial wet areas, pool surrounds, and ramps. DIN 51130 test method.

**S1.50:** What is 'lappato' finish and where is it best used?

**A:** Lappato (or semi-polished) finish is a satin surface with partial polish, creating a subtle sheen that reflects light softly without the mirror effect of full polish. Best used for: residential living areas, hotel lobbies, and feature walls where moderate reflectivity and slip resistance (R9-R10) are desired.

**S1.51:** Do you offer tiles with a 'grip' or 'anti-slip' finish for external areas?

**A:** Yes. Our external grip tiles feature: structured surface texture, abrasive grain integration, or micro-roughening to achieve R11-R12 slip resistance. These are specified for: external walkways, pool surrounds, ramps, and areas exposed to wet weather conditions.

**S1.52:** What is the maximum tile size available in your wood-look porcelain range?

**A:** Our wood-look porcelain is available in plank formats up to 300x1800mm and 200x2400mm. Large plank formats enhance the realistic wood appearance with fewer joints and are suitable for expansive commercial and residential floors.

**S1.53:** Do you offer tiles suitable for chemical laboratories?

**A:** Yes. Our acid-resistant tile range withstands exposure to: sulphuric acid (up to 98%), hydrochloric acid, nitric acid, and caustic soda. These are specified with acid-resistant epoxy-based grout and bedding for laboratory floors, chemical storage areas, and battery rooms.

**S1.54:** Can you supply tiles for monumental and memorial applications?

**A:** Yes. Monumental tiles include:plaques, commemorative tiles with custom inscriptions (laser-engraved or sandblasted), memorial mosaics, and dedication tiles. These are specified for: memorial walls, donor recognition displays, and heritage markers.

**S1.55:** What tiles do you recommend for synagogue, mosque, and church flooring?

**A:** Religious building recommendations: mosques — large-format tiles for prayer halls with geometric border patterns, R9 slip resistance, and mihrab feature tiles. Churches —-look or stone-look with traditional aesthetic, good acoustic properties. Synagogues — durable tiles for high-traffic prayer halls with appropriate decorative elements.

**S1.56:** What is the minimum screed thickness for wet area tiling?

**A:** Minimum screed thickness is 40mm for bonded screeds and 50mm for unbonded floating screeds over waterproofing. Screed must achieve 25 MPa compressive strength and be cured for minimum 7 days (preferably 28 days) before waterproofing application.

**S1.57:** Can I tile directly over a concrete slab without a screed in a bathroom?

**A:** Yes, if the concrete slab achieves: surface hardness ≥25 MPa, flatness ±3mm/2m, and proper falls to drainage. Direct fixing saves build-up height but requires careful slab quality control during concrete placement.

**S1.58:** What is the difference between a screed and a self-levelling compound?

**A:** A screed (cementitious, 20-50mm thick) provides structural levelling and falls. A self-levelling compound (2-10mm thick) provides final surface levelling only and does not create falls. Screeds are used for wet area falls; self-levelling for dry area flatness correction.

**S1.59:** How long must waterproofing cure before flood testing?

**A:** Cementitious membranes: 48 hours minimum at 20°C. Liquid polyurethane membranes: 24-48 hours depending on thickness and ambient conditions. Always check manufacturer's data sheet — cure time varies by 50% between 15°C and 30°C ambient temperatures.

**S1.60:** Can waterproofing be applied over existing tiles?

**A:** Yes, with proper preparation: clean and degrease existing tiles, abrade surface for keying, apply bonding primer, then install compatible liquid-applied membrane. This 'tile-on-tile' waterproofing is common in renovations but requires pull-off testing of existing tiles first.

**S1.61:** What is the minimum overlap for sheet membrane seams in wet areas?

**A:** Sheet membrane seams require minimum 50mm overlap with full-width heat welding or solvent welding. All seams must be integrity-tested (air lance or vacuum box) before tile installation. Lap details are shown on our waterproofing detail drawings.

**S1.62:** How do I specify waterproofing for a planter box on a podium slab?

**A:** Planter box waterproofing includes: root-resistant membrane (HDPE or modified bitumen), protection board, drainage layer, geotextile filter, growing medium, and overflow drainage. Our details show: integration with podium waterproofing, through-wall drainage, and plant selection guidance.

**S1.63:** Do you specify waterproofing for internal gutters behind parapet walls?

**A:** Yes. Internal gutter waterproofing includes: continuous membrane from roof surface up and over parapet, overflow scuppers, leaf guards, and compatible downpipe connections. This is critical for flat roof buildings where waterproofing integrity depends on gutter performance.

**S1.64:** Can I use the same waterproofing system for a pool and the surrounding deck?

**A:** While the same membrane type can be used, pool and deck have different requirements: pool needs full submersion-rated membrane (≥1.5mm), while deck needs UV-stable, traffic-bearing membrane. We specify compatible systems that transition seamlessly at the pool edge.

**S1.65:** What is the best waterproofing system for a roof garden with pedestrian traffic?

**A:** Roof garden specification: FPO or TPO single-ply membrane (root-resistant), protection board (≥4mm HDPE), drainage composite, filter fabric, growing medium (150-300mm), and porcelain pavers on pedestals for walkway areas. Load capacity must be verified by the structural engineer.

**S1.66:** What tile specification do you recommend for a school science laboratory?

**A:** School laboratory: PEI Class 4-5, chemical resistance to acids and solvents, R10 slip resistance, full coving 100mm, epoxy-based grout, and sealed junctions at benches and sinks. Specification should reference Department of Basic Education laboratory standards.

**S1.67:** Can you specify tiles for a cinema and theatre foyer?

**A:** Cinema foyer: PEI Class 4, R10 slip resistance, high stain resistance (food/beverage spills), acoustic-friendly surface, and design flexibility for branding and wayfinding. Consider LED-integrated floor tiles for feature effects.

**S1.68:** What tiles are suitable for an aquarium or marine facility?

**A:** Aquarium specifications include: saltwater-resistant tiles (tested to ASTM C97), marine-grade epoxy-based grout, waterproofing rated for saltwater exposure, non-slip surfaces for back-of-house areas, and chemical resistance to cleaning disinfectants.

**S1.69:** Do you have tiles for brewery fermentation halls?

**A:** Brewery tiles: PEI 5, R11-R12 slip resistance, thermal shock resistance (temperature cycling during cleaning), chemical resistance to acids (phosphoric, nitric) and caustic cleaners, and impervious surface. Acid-resistant tile specification is essential.

**S1.70:** What is the best tile for a veterinary clinic?

**A:** Veterinary clinic: PEI Class 4-5, R10-R11 slip resistance, antimicrobial properties, chemical resistance to disinfectants, seamless coved skirting for hygiene, and calming colours (animals respond better to neutral tones).

**S1.71:** Can you specify tiles for a golf clubhouse with high foot traffic?

**A:** Golf clubhouse: PEI Class 4 for bar/restaurant, R10 slip resistance, stain resistance (food, beverages, grass stains), design appropriate to clubhouse aesthetic (traditional or contemporary), and durability for spike-heeled traffic in changing areas.

**S1.72:** What tiles do you recommend for a museum storage facility?

**A:** Museum storage: PEI Class 3-4 (low traffic), chemical neutral surface (no off-gassing), smooth surface for easy cleaning, controlled environment compatibility, and non-abrasive surface to protect any objects placed on the floor.

**S1.73:** Do you have tiles for a funeral home and mortuary?

**A:** Funeral home: PEI Class 4, R10 slip resistance, calming aesthetic (neutral tones), coved skirting for hygiene, chemical resistance to embalming fluids and disinfectants, and dignified appearance appropriate to the setting.

**S1.74:** Can you specify tiles for an ice rink foyer and skate hire area?

**A:** Ice rink foyer: PEI Class 5 (heavy traffic, skate blades), R11 slip resistance (wet from melted ice), frost resistance (temperature near 0°C), impact resistance, and resistance to rubber skate guard marking.

**S1.75:** What tiles are suitable for a go-kart track pit area?

**A:** Go-kart pit area: PEI Class 5, R12 slip resistance (oil exposure), chemical resistance to petrol and oil, impact resistance, and ease of cleaning. Heavy-duty technical porcelain or epoxy flooring is recommended for this extreme environment.

**S1.76:** How do tiles compare to vinyl flooring in terms of environmental impact?

**A:** Porcelain tiles have higher embodied energy than vinyl but significantly longer service life (40 vs 10 years), resulting in lower annualised environmental impact. Tiles contain no plasticisers or halogenated compounds, do not emit VOCs, and are fully inert at end-of-life.

**S1.77:** Can tiles contribute to BREEAM certification?

**A:** Yes. Tiles contribute to BREEAM in: Mat 01 (Life cycle impacts via EPDs), Mat 03 (Responsible sourcing), Mat 06 (Material efficiency via durability), Hea 02 (Indoor air quality via low VOC), and Wst 01 (Construction waste management).

**S1.78:** What is the recycled content of grout and adhesive products?

**A:** Our recommended cementitious adhesives contain 10-20% recycled content (fly ash, porcelain tile filler). Epoxy grouts contain 5-10% recycled content. We are working with manufacturers to increase these percentages and achieve EPDs for adhesive products.

**S1.79:** Do you provide documentation for the circular economy assessment of tiles?

**A:** Yes. Circular economy documentation covers: design for durability (40+ year life), material efficiency (recycled content), maintenance and repair capability, recycling potential at end-of-life, and take-back programme availability. This supports circular economy-focused client briefs.

**S1.80:** Can tiles help achieve net-zero water consumption in a building?

**A:** While tiles don't directly affect water consumption, permeable paving tiles support rainwater harvesting by allowing groundwater recharge. Additionally, tile surfaces require minimal water for cleaning compared to alternatives, contributing to operational water reduction.

**S1.81:** Do you have tiles with Cradle to Cradle certification?

**A:** Several of our European manufacturing partners have achieved Cradle to Cradle certification (Bronze to Gold levels) for selected ranges. C2C certification covers: material health, recyclability, renewable energy use, water stewardship, and social fairness.

**S1.82:** What is the SMaRT certification and do any of your products have it?

**A:** SMaRT (Sustainable Materials Rating Technology) is a comprehensive sustainability certification for building products. Several of our international manufacturing partners hold SMaRT certification, covering: environmental, social, and governance criteria across the product lifecycle.

**S1.83:** Can you provide data on the water footprint of your tiles?

**A:** Water footprint data is included in our EPDs, covering: blue water (surface/groundwater consumption), green water (rainwater use), and grey water (pollution assimilation). Our manufacturers use closed-loop water systems with 95%+ recycling rates.

**S1.84:** Do your tiles support the SANS 10400-XA deemed-to-satisfy rules for energy efficiency?

**A:** Tiles support XA compliance through: thermal mass contribution (when installed on concrete slabs in occupied zones), which moderates temperature fluctuations and reduces HVAC energy demand. Thermal mass calculations can be provided for XA compliance documentation.

**S1.85:** Can you provide a sustainability scorecard for comparing tile options?

**A:** Yes. Our sustainability scorecard evaluates tiles across 12 criteria: recycled content, GWP, EPD availability, VOC emissions, local sourcing, service life, maintenance requirements, water footprint, energy footprint, social compliance, packaging sustainability, and end-of-life recyclability.

**S1.86:** Does Tiletoria participate in supplier diversity programmes of large corporate clients?

**A:** Yes. We participate in supplier diversity programmes through: B-BBEE Level 2 status, preferential procurement from diverse suppliers, and partnerships with women-owned and youth-owned businesses. Supplier diversity scorecards are provided on request.

**S1.87:** Can you accommodate our practice's specific invoicing and payment terms?

**A:** Yes. We accommodate various invoicing requirements: milestone-based billing, progress payment schedules, hold-back provisions, and 30-day payment terms. Our accounts team works with your finance department to align invoicing with your practice's cash flow requirements.

**S1.88:** Does Tiletoria have experience with JVs (Joint Ventures) between practices?

**A:** Yes. We support JV arrangements by: providing documentation to both JV partners, attending JV design meetings, coordinating specification input from both practices, and ensuring consistent product selection across JV project portfolios.

**S1.89:** Can you provide construction phase payment guarantees or bonds?

**A:** For large projects, we can provide: advance payment guarantees, performance bonds, and retention bonds through our financial institution relationships. These are arranged on a project-specific basis with appropriate security and fee structures.

**S1.90:** Does Tiletoria offer framework agreements for multi-year, multi-project relationships?

**A:** Yes. Framework agreements cover: pre-agreed pricing structures, standardised specification templates, annual CPD scheduling, dedicated resource allocation, and KPI reporting. Frameworks are typically 2-3 years with annual review and renewal options.

**S1.91:** Can you support our practice's ISO 9001 quality management requirements?

**A:** Yes. We provide: quality certificates, product traceability documentation, complaint handling procedures, corrective action reports, and supplier evaluation scorecards. These support your practice's ISO 9001 supplier management requirements.

**S1.92:** Does Tiletoria have professional liability insurance covering specification advice?

**A:** Yes. We carry professional indemnity insurance of R10 million covering: specification advice, technical recommendations, and design support services. Insurance certificates are provided as part of PSL documentation.

**S1.93:** Can you provide references from quantity surveyors who have priced your projects?

**A:** Yes. QS references are available from leading practices including: Davis Langdon (AECOM), Berry Smith & Jamieson, Napier Partnership, and others. These references can speak to pricing accuracy, documentation quality, and variation management.

**S1.94:** Does Tiletoria have any black ownership or empowerment credentials?

**A:** Yes. Tiletoria is a Level 2 B-BBEE contributor with 51%+ black ownership through Invicta Holdings. Our ownership structure, skills development programmes, and socio-economic development contributions support our B-BBEE credentials.

**S1.95:** Can you support our practice's preferential procurement policy for local suppliers?

**A:** Yes. We support local procurement through: locally manufactured tile options, local warehousing and distribution, local employment, and local content certification. Local content percentages are provided for government tender compliance.

**S1.96:** Can Tiletoria support value engineering workshops with the client and contractor?

**A:** Yes. Our technical team participates in VE workshops, providing: cost-performance analysis, alternative product suggestions with performance data, budget comparison sheets, and recommendation summaries. We focus on maintaining design intent while achieving cost savings.

**S1.97:** Do you provide tender clarification responses for your specified products?

**A:** Yes. During the tender period, we respond to: contractor requests for product information, substitution evaluation, technical compliance confirmation, and sample requests. All tender clarifications are copied to the architect and QS.

**S1.98:** Can you assist with Contractor Proposals (CPs) for alternative products?

**A:** Yes. When contractors propose alternatives, we provide: technical comparison against the specified product, performance gap analysis, recommendation (accept/reject with reasons), and documentation for the architect's evaluation and decision.

**S1.99:** Do you provide installation monitoring for critical applications?

**A:** Yes. For critical applications (hospitals, pools, facades), our technical team provides: installation monitoring visits, quality checklists, photographic documentation, and progress reports. Issues are flagged immediately for corrective action.

**S1.100:** Can Tiletoria support dispute resolution between architect and contractor?

**A:** Yes. Our technical director provides impartial expert opinion on: specification compliance, installation quality, product performance, and defect causation. Expert opinions are based on SANS standards and industry best practice.

**S1.101:** Do you provide as-built drawing support for the tiling package?

**A:** As-built support includes: confirmation of products actually installed, documentation of any substitutions or changes, warranty certificate compilation, maintenance manual provision, and marked-up drawings showing installed conditions.

**S1.102:** Can you assist with final account reconciliation for tile packages?

**A:** Yes. Final account support includes: quantity reconciliation (measured vs ordered vs installed), variation documentation, credit note processing for over-orders, and cost allocation across cost centres. This supports the QS's final account preparation.

**S1.103:** Do you provide training for our junior architects on tile specification?

**A:** Yes. Our 'Tile Specification for Emerging Architects' training covers: SANS standards basics, performance criteria selection, common specification errors, detail drawing interpretation, and digital tools. This can be structured as a mentorship programme over 3-6 months.

**S1.104:** Can Tiletoria support our practice's quality management system with supplier evaluations?

**A:** Yes. We provide: quarterly supplier performance reports, KPI scorecards, corrective action reports for any issues, and annual business reviews. These support your practice's ISO 9001 supplier evaluation requirements.

**S1.105:** Do you provide project close-out reports with lessons learned?

**A:** Yes. For major projects, we prepare close-out reports documenting: specification outcomes, installation challenges and solutions, product performance observations, contractor feedback, and recommendations for future projects. These support your practice's continuous improvement.

**S1.106:** Can you provide a Certificate of Conformity for SANS 1449:2012?

**A:** Yes. A formal Certificate of Conformity (COC) is issued for each product batch, confirming: SANS 1449:2012 group classification, test certificate references, manufacturing date, and batch number. COCs are provided with delivery documentation.

**S1.107:** Do your tiles comply with the National Building Regulations (NBR) Amendment?

**A:** Yes. Our tiles and installation systems comply with the current National Building Regulations and all published amendments. Compliance documentation is updated within 30 days of any regulatory change.

**S1.108:** Can you provide test certificates in English for international projects?

**A:** Yes. All test certificates are issued in English. For projects requiring translation into other languages (French, Portuguese, Arabic), certified translations can be arranged through our documentation team.

**S1.109:** Do you have tiles certified for use in explosive atmospheres (ATEX)?

**A:** For ATEX zones, we provide: anti-static tile specifications, electrical resistance data, earthing detail drawings, and compliance with IEC 60079-0 for explosive atmospheres. These are specified for chemical plants, grain silos, and fuel handling facilities.

**S1.110:** Can you provide a Method Statement for working at height on facade tile installation?

**A:** Yes. Facade method statements include: scaffold requirements, mast climber specifications, tile lifting equipment, wind speed limitations, temporary fixing methods, material restraint, and integration with the main contractor's fall protection plan.

**S1.111:** Do your tiles meet NSF/ANSI standards for food equipment?

**A:** Selected commercial kitchen tiles meet NSF/ANSI 2 (food equipment) and NSF/ANSI 51 (food contact materials) standards. These certifications are required for food processing facilities exporting to NSF-mandated markets.

**S1.112:** Can you provide documentation for SAMTRAC or OHS compliance?

**A:** Yes. Health and safety documentation includes: Material Safety Data Sheets (MSDS) for adhesives and sealants, tile cutting dust hazard information, silica content declarations, and recommended PPE for tile handling and cutting.

**S1.113:** Do you have tiles tested to ASTM standards for US-funded projects?

**A:** Yes. ASTM test data is available including: ASTM C373 (water absorption), ASTM C648 (breaking strength), ASTM C1028 (slip resistance), ASTM C501 (abrasion resistance), and ASTM E84 (surface burning characteristics). Full ASTM-compliant submittals are provided.

**S1.114:** Can you provide Euroclass fire rating certificates?

**A:** Yes. Euroclass fire ratings per EN 13501-1 are available for all our porcelain ranges: Class A1 (non-combustible) for porcelain, with smoke production s1 (no/smoke) and flaming droplets d0 (none). These support European standard compliance.

**S1.115:** Do your tiles meet Dubai Civil Defence (DCD) requirements?

**A:** For Middle East projects, we provide: DCD fire rating certificates, Civil Defence approval documentation, and test reports from DCD-approved laboratories. Our technical team has experience with Gulf region project requirements.

**S1.116:** Can you provide setting out drawings for circular or curved tile layouts?

**A:** Yes. Curved layout setting out drawings show: radial tile cutting patterns, curved border details, focal point geometry, and wastage calculations. These are produced using CAD and can be exported for CNC waterjet cutting where required.

**S1.117:** Do you have details for tiled columns and curved wall applications?

**A:** Yes. Column and curved wall details show: small-format tile or mosaic specification for tight radii, flexible adhesive requirements, cutting patterns for large-format curved applications, and movement joint placement on curved surfaces.

**S1.118:** Can you provide details for tile expansion joints at structural building joints?

**A:** Structural joint details show: minimum 20mm wide expansion joint, compressible filler board, flexible joint sealant (polyurethane or silicone), cover plate options (aluminium, stainless steel), and waterproofing continuity across the joint.

**S1.119:** Do you have details for tile fixing to existing painted surfaces?

**A:** Painted surface fixing details include: paint removal assessment, mechanical abrasion or stripping, primer application, adhesive selection for non-absorbent surface, and maximum tile weight limitations. Direct fixing to paint is generally not recommended.

**S1.120:** Can you provide details for tile installation in seismic expansion joints?

**A:** Seismic expansion joint details accommodate: ±25mm movement in all directions, flexible support systems, non-rigid tile fixing, compressible fillers, and cover systems that maintain appearance during seismic movement. Structural engineer coordination required.

**S1.121:** Do you have details for tile-over-timber floor with underfloor heating?

**A:** Timber + UFH details include: timber floor assessment (deflection, stability), cement particle board overlay, insulation board, heating cable/mat, flexible adhesive, porcelain tiles (thermal shock resistant), and expansion joints at 8m centres.

**S1.122:** Can you provide details for tiled window sills and reveals?

**A:** Window sill details show: sill tile with fall to exterior, waterproofing at sill/wall junction, thermal break considerations, sealant joint at frame, and condensation management. Reveal details cover: tile fixing to reveals, corner treatments, and integration with window frame.

**S1.123:** Do you have details for tile splashbacks with power point cut-outs?

**A:** Splashback details include: tile layout around power points (centred or aligned), cut-out dimensions and clearances, edge treatment at cut-outs, waterproofing behind splashback, and sealant at bench/splashback junction.

**S1.124:** Can you provide details for tiled planters and built-in seating?

**A:** Planter/seating details show: waterproofing for planters, drainage provision, tile fixing to vertical and horizontal surfaces, coving at internal corners, and structural loading considerations for built-in seating with tile finish.

**S1.125:** Do you have details for tile integration with floor ducts and service outlets?

**A:** Floor duct details show: tile cutting around floor outlet boxes, removable tile sections for access, support framing for cut tiles, and level transition between tile and floor outlet cover. Coordination with MEP services essential.

**S1.126:** Can Tiletoria facilitate a peer review of my tile specification by another architect?

**A:** Yes. Through our architect network, we can facilitate peer review of tile specifications by experienced architects who have completed similar projects. Peer review provides independent validation and identifies potential issues before tender issue.

**S1.127:** Do you sponsor architectural student competitions or thesis prizes?

**A:** Yes. We sponsor select student competitions and thesis prizes through: prize funding, product vouchers, mentorship from our technical team, and publicity for winning entries. Contact us to discuss sponsorship of your institution's awards.

**S1.128:** Can Tiletoria host a practice social event or year-end function?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab is available for practice events including: year-end functions, client entertainment, team building, and celebration events. Facilities include: AV equipment, catering space, bar area, ample parking, and showroom access for guests.

**S1.129:** Do you facilitate introductions between architects and international manufacturers?

**A:** Yes. We facilitate: factory visit arrangements, manufacturer-hosted dinners, international trade show coordination (Cersaie, Cevisama), and direct video conferences with manufacturer technical directors. These connections enable deeper product knowledge and relationship building.

**S1.130:** Can Tiletoria participate in our practice's design quality review process?

**A:** Yes. Our technical director can join your design quality reviews, contributing: technical feasibility assessment, buildability advice, cost implication analysis, and risk identification related to tile specification and installation.

**S1.131:** Do you have an alumni network for architects who have worked with Tiletoria?

**A:** Yes. Our architect alumni network maintains connections with: retired practitioners, former specification contacts who have moved practices, and international architects who have returned home. The network supports knowledge sharing and professional networking.

**S1.132:** Can you provide a letter of support for our practice's award submission?

**A:** Yes. We provide letters of support confirming: products supplied, specification collaboration, technical innovation, and project outcomes. These letters are tailored to the specific award category and judging criteria.

**S1.133:** Does Tiletoria collaborate with architectural researchers or academics?

**A:** Yes. We collaborate with: university research programmes, industry research organisations (CSIR), and individual academic researchers. Collaboration areas include: sustainable materials, installation technology, performance testing, and industry surveys.

**S1.134:** Can you arrange a factory tour for our practice's professional development trip?

**A:** Yes. Factory tours to Italy and Spain can be arranged for groups of 4-12 architects. Tours include: production line visits, R&D facility access, new product previews, and meetings with factory technical teams. Travel coordination assistance provided.

**S1.135:** Do you host virtual networking events for architects in different cities?

**A:** Yes. Our virtual networking events connect architects across South Africa for: knowledge sharing, project discussion, manufacturer presentations, and CPD sessions. Virtual events have expanded our reach to practices in smaller centres and neighbouring countries.

**S1.136:** What happens if a colour within a range is discontinued but other colours remain?

**A:** When a specific colour is discontinued within an active range, we notify all specifiers who have used that colour and provide: the discontinuation timeline, suggested alternative colours from the same range, samples for comparison, and assistance with specification amendment.

**S1.137:** Can Tiletoria guarantee that a product will remain available for the full defects liability period?

**A:** Yes. For projects using our specified products, we guarantee availability throughout the DLP (typically 12 months post-practical completion) for: replacement tiles, matching accessories, and compatible adhesives/grouts. This supports defect remediation without product substitution.

**S1.138:** Do you offer 'last time buy' notifications for products nearing end-of-life?

**A:** Yes. Last time buy notifications are sent 3 months before final discontinuation, allowing specifiers to: place final orders for current projects, procure spare tiles for maintenance, and obtain archive samples for future reference.

**S1.139:** How does Tiletoria manage product transitions when introducing a new generation?

**A:** New generation transitions are managed through: parallel availability of old and new products (typically 6 months), technical comparison documentation, sample sets of both generations, and specification update support. This minimises disruption to ongoing projects.

**S1.140:** Can you provide a 'product family tree' showing how ranges have evolved over time?

**A:** Yes. Product family trees show: range launches, extensions, format additions, colour introductions, technical upgrades, and discontinuations over time. These help architects understand product lineage and select current equivalents for renovation matching.

**S1.141:** Do you notify architects when a product they've specified has a technical upgrade?

**A:** Yes. Technical upgrade notifications are sent when a product's performance is enhanced (e.g., improved slip rating, new size added, recycled content increased). These are generally positive changes that don't require specification amendment.

**S1.142:** Can Tiletoria provide batch samples from original production for warranty claims years later?

**A:** Yes. For contract orders, we archive batch samples for 5 years post-installation. These can be used for: colour fade comparison, defect assessment, insurance claims, warranty dispute resolution, and matching on extension projects.

**S1.143:** What is Tiletoria's policy on colour 'drift' within a long-running product line?

**A:** We monitor colour consistency within long-running lines through: factory quality control, batch-to-batch comparison, and specifier feedback. If colour drift is detected, we notify affected specifiers and can arrange custom colour matching for critical projects.

**S1.144:** Do you have a contingency plan if a major manufacturing partner goes out of business?

**A:** Yes. Our contingency plan includes: alternative manufacturing partnerships for all product categories, 6 months' buffer stock for core products, rapid product sourcing capability, and specification support for transition to alternative products if required.

**S1.145:** Can you provide a forward-looking product availability statement for a 5-year development programme?

**A:** Yes. For long-term programmes, we provide an annual Product Availability Statement confirming: continued availability of specified products, any planned changes, and contingency plans. These statements support programme-level risk management.

**S1.146:** Does Tiletoria provide a monthly newsletter for specifiers?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab Specifier Newsletter is published monthly, covering: new product launches, CPD event calendar, technical tips, project case studies, industry news, and discontinuation alerts. Subscription is free for registered architects and specifiers.

**S1.147:** How does Tiletoria handle communication during a product recall or safety issue?

**A:** In the event of a recall or safety issue, we activate our crisis communication protocol: immediate email and phone notification to all affected specifiers, public notice on website and social media, coordination with regulatory authorities, and product return/replacement at no cost.

**S1.148:** Can I get automated notifications when new products matching my interests are launched?

**A:** Yes. Our preference-based notification system sends alerts when: new products match your specified interests (e.g., large-format-look, commercial-grade), new technical data is published, or CPD events in your areas of interest are scheduled.

**S1.149:** Does Tiletoria have a specifier portal for self-service document access?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab Portal provides 24/7 access to: technical data sheets, specification clauses, detail drawings, BIM objects, CPD booking, sample requests, and query submission. Registration is free for professional specifiers.

**S1.150:** How does Tiletoria handle timezone differences for international project communication?

**A:** For international projects, we coordinate communication across time zones through: scheduled video calls at mutually convenient times, email responses within 4 business hours, and dedicated international project coordinators who adjust their hours for key meetings.

**S1.151:** Can I get a dedicated phone line for a critical project?

**A:** For critical projects (R20 million+), a dedicated project hotline is provided, connecting directly to the project team. This ensures immediate response for urgent queries without navigating general switchboard queues.

**S1.152:** Does Tiletoria record phone calls for quality assurance?

**A:** Phone calls are not routinely recorded. However, all technical advice given by phone is confirmed in writing via email. If call recording is required (e.g., for dispute prevention), this can be arranged with prior notification and consent.

**S1.153:** How do you ensure continuity of communication when staff change?

**A:** Communication continuity is ensured through: comprehensive CRM records of all interactions, structured handover procedures, overlapping transition periods, and designated backup contacts for every specifier. Your communication history is never lost due to staff changes.

**S1.154:** Can Tiletoria provide communication in languages other than English?

**A:** Primary communication is in English. Afrikaans support is available from Cape Town and Pretoria staff. For international projects, we can arrange communication in Italian, Spanish, and Portuguese through our manufacturing partner relationships.

**S1.155:** Do you provide a communication protocol document for large projects?

**A:** Yes. For large projects, a Communication Protocol is issued at kick-off, defining: contact persons by discipline, escalation paths, response time commitments, meeting schedules, reporting formats, and document management procedures.

**S1.156:** Does the Spec Lab have parking for visitors with trailers or large vehicles?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab has ample parking including space for trailers and delivery vehicles. If you're collecting large sample orders, please inform us in advance so we can arrange loading assistance.

**S1.157:** Can I book a 'quiet hour' visit for focused specification work without interruptions?

**A:** Yes. 'Quiet hour' appointments (typically 08:00-09:00 or after 16:00) are available for architects requiring focused, uninterrupted time with samples. These slots are limited to one booking at a time to ensure privacy.

**S1.158:** Does the Spec Lab have facilities for video conferencing during my visit?

**A:** Yes. Meeting rooms are equipped with: large-screen displays, video conferencing capability (Teams, Zoom), high-speed internet, and presentation equipment. You can conduct remote client or team consultations while physically examining samples.

**S1.159:** Can I arrange a 'breakfast briefing' at the Spec Lab for my project team?

**A:** Yes. Breakfast briefings (07:30-09:00) are available for project teams, combining: catered breakfast, technical presentation, sample examination, and Q&A. These are productive pre-work sessions that minimise disruption to the working day.

**S1.160:** Does the Spec Lab have accessible facilities for elderly or mobility-impaired visitors?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab is fully accessible: level entry, wide aisles, accessible restroom, ample seating throughout, and samples displayed at accessible heights. We can also arrange home/office sample visits for those unable to travel.

**S1.161:** Can I see tiles under different lighting conditions at the Spec Lab?

**A:** Yes. Adjustable lighting throughout the Spec Lab allows you to examine tiles under: warm white (2700K), neutral white (4000K), and cool white (6500K) to simulate different project lighting conditions. Natural daylight viewing is also available.

**S1.162:** Does the Spec Lab have a print facility for printing specification documents during visits?

**A:** Yes. A print and copy facility is available for: printing specification clauses, copying technical data sheets, printing detail drawings, and producing presentation materials. A4, A3, and A0 printing is available.

**S1.163:** Can I store my practice's sample selection at the Spec Lab for client visits?

**A:** Yes. Practice sample storage is available for partner practices. Your selected 'standard range' samples are held in a dedicated area, ready for your client presentations without needing to request them each time.

**S1.164:** Does the Spec Lab have outdoor space for examining external tile samples?

**A:** Yes. An outdoor terrace displays external paving, pool surrounds, and facade tiles in natural weathering conditions. This allows examination of: slip performance when wet, colour under natural daylight, and drainage characteristics.

**S1.165:** Can I arrange a 'lock-in' session at the Spec Lab for an intensive specification day?

**A:** Yes. Full-day lock-in sessions (09:00-17:00) are available for practices working on major projects. The Spec Lab is reserved exclusively for your team, with dedicated technical staff, catering, and full sample access throughout the day.

**S1.166:** How does Tiletoria's large-format tile expertise compare to competitors?

**A:** Tiletoria has the deepest large-format expertise in South Africa: we stock the widest range of 1200x2600mm and 1600x3200mm formats, provide specialist large-format installation training, have dedicated large-format lifting equipment, and have completed more large-format projects than any competitor.

**S1.167:** Does Tiletoria offer better contractor training than competitors?

**A:** Yes. Our contractor training programme includes: accredited installation courses, large-format certification, waterproofing applicator training, and project-specific briefings. Training is delivered by qualified technical staff, not sales representatives.

**S1.168:** How does your sustainability documentation compare to direct European suppliers?

**A:** Our sustainability documentation matches what European suppliers provide (EPDs, recycled content, carbon footprint) with the added advantage of: local Green Star SA expertise, SANS compliance integration, and local support for sustainability submission documentation.

**S1.169:** Does Tiletoria have better stock availability than online-only tile retailers?

**A:** Yes. Unlike online retailers who order on demand (8-12 week lead times), we maintain physical stock of core products in our Johannesburg warehouse, enabling 3-5 day delivery for stocked items. This is critical for project schedules.

**S1.170:** How does your specification review service compare to independent specification consultants?

**A:** Our specification review combines: product-specific technical expertise, SANS standard knowledge, installation best practice, and cost-performance optimisation. Unlike independent consultants, we provide this service free of charge as part of our specifier partnership.

**S1.171:** Does Tiletoria offer better after-sales support than competitors?

**A:** Our after-sales support includes: warranty claim management, defect assessment, maintenance guidance, spare tile sourcing, and long-term product support. Many competitors provide minimal after-sales service, particularly for discontinued products.

**S1.172:** How does your project track record in healthcare compare to competitors?

**A:** Tiletoria has supplied to more healthcare facilities than any South African tile distributor, including: Netcare, Mediclinic, Life Healthcare, and government hospitals. Our healthcare expertise covers: infection control, antimicrobial tiles, operating theatres, and wet area compliance.

**S1.173:** Does Tiletoria have stronger relationships with international manufacturers than competitors?

**A:** Our 25+ year relationships with leading Italian and Spanish manufacturers provide: exclusive access to certain collections, priority production allocation, custom development capability, and direct technical backup that smaller distributors cannot match.

**S1.174:** How does your digital presence and online resource library compare to competitors?

**A:** Our digital resources (BIM objects, virtual tour, specifier portal, online CPD booking, digital sample books) represent the most comprehensive online offering in the South African tile industry. Most competitors have basic websites with limited technical content.

**S1.175:** Does Tiletoria offer more comprehensive project-specific support than competitors?

**A:** Yes. Our project support spans: specification writing, value engineering, mock-up production, site inspections, contractor briefings, RFI response, submittal review, and as-built documentation. This end-to-end support is unique in the South African market.


## Supplementary Section 2: Additional Topics: Project-Specific, Niche Applications & Advanced Queries

**S2.1:** Can Tiletoria's CPD sessions be counted toward ECSA CPD requirements?

**A:** While our CPD is SAIA-accredited for architects, many engineering professionals successfully claim our sessions under ECSA Category 2 (informal learning). We provide attendance certificates with detailed session descriptions to support ECSA claims. Check with ECSA for your specific requirements.

**S2.2:** Do you offer CPD on the psychology of colour and material in architectural design?

**A:** Yes. Our 'Colour and Material Psychology' CPD explores: how tile colour affects spatial perception, material selection for user wellbeing, biophilic design principles, and evidence-based design for healthcare and education. This session bridges technical performance with human-centred design.

**S2.3:** Can your CPD be delivered as a panel discussion with other industry experts?

**A:** Yes. Panel discussion format CPD sessions bring together: architects, interior designers, engineers, and contractors to discuss topics from multiple perspectives. These interactive sessions are particularly popular for complex subjects like waterproofing failures and facade design.

**S2.4:** Do you offer CPD on construction contract law as it relates to tile specification?

**A:** Our 'Specification and Liability' CPD covers: professional indemnity considerations, substitution clause strategies, warranty implications, and dispute avoidance through clear specification writing. This session is relevant to practice principals and project architects alike.

**S2.5:** Can I get a CPD session on emerging African design trends and material usage?

**A:** Yes. Our 'African Design Trends' CPD explores: contemporary African architectural aesthetics, locally appropriate material selections, climate-responsive design with tiles, and case studies of award-winning African projects featuring innovative tile specification.

**S2.6:** Can you provide data on tile surface roughness (Ra value) for clean room applications?

**A:** Surface roughness (Ra) data is available for our technical porcelain ranges. Clean room applications typically require Ra < 0.5 μm for ISO Class 5-6 environments. Our polished porcelain achieves Ra 0.1-0.3 μm, suitable for pharmaceutical and electronics clean rooms.

**S2.7:** What is the dielectric strength of your porcelain tiles?

**A:** Porcelain tile dielectric strength is approximately 20-30 kV/mm, making them excellent electrical insulators. This property is relevant for: electrical substation flooring, switchgear rooms, and high-voltage equipment areas where electrical isolation is required.

**S2.8:** Can you provide data on tile resistance to gamma radiation for nuclear medicine facilities?

**A:** Porcelain tiles demonstrate excellent resistance to gamma radiation with no degradation at medical facility exposure levels. For nuclear medicine and radiotherapy facilities, we provide radiation resistance data and can recommend suitable tile systems for hot lab and treatment areas.

**S2.9:** Do you have data on the fatigue resistance of tiles under cyclic loading?

**A:** Fatigue resistance data under cyclic loading (repeated foot traffic, rolling loads) is available for our commercial porcelain ranges. PEI Class 5 tiles maintain structural integrity beyond 10 million cycles, equivalent to 40+ years of heavy commercial traffic.

**S2.10:** What is the minimum bending radius for your flexible tile products?

**A:** Flexible or bendable tile products (special thin porcelain, 3-4mm) can achieve bending radii of 300-500mm, suitable for curved columns, reception desks, and architectural features with gentle curves. Standard porcelain tiles are rigid and cannot be bent.

**S2.11:** How do I specify tiles for a PPP project with a 25-year concession period?

**A:** For long concession periods, specify: minimum 40-year service life tiles, whole-life cost analysis, maintenance contracts, replacement fund provisions, performance guarantees, and transfer-condition standards. Our team provides lifecycle cost modelling for PPP specifications.

**S2.12:** Can you help write a performance specification that allows only Tiletoria products?

**A:** While we can help write performance specifications, we recommend including 'or equal approved' clauses to maintain competitive tendering. The performance criteria can be set at levels that effectively favour Tiletoria products without being anti-competitive.

**S2.13:** How should I specify tiles for a project with multiple NHBRC warranty periods?

**A:** Multi-period NHBRC specifications include: core tile selection for all houses, optional upgrades with defined performance impact, standardised wet area details, and consistent waterproofing systems. Standardisation simplifies compliance while allowing client choice.

**S2.14:** What specification approach should I use for a renovation where existing tiles must be retained?

**A:** Retention specifications include: condition assessment criteria, cleaning and repair methods, matching new tiles to existing (colour, size, texture), compatible adhesives for tile-on-tile, and transition details between retained and new areas.

**S2.15:** Can you provide a master specification that I can customise for different project scales?

**A:** Yes. Our tiered master specification includes: Tier 1 (luxury/high-end), Tier 2 (mid-market), and Tier 3 (budget-conscious). Each tier has pre-selected performance criteria and product categories. Edit by deleting the tiers not applicable to your project.

**S2.16:** Can Tiletoria BIM objects be used with Dynamo for generative tile layouts?

**A:** Yes. Our Revit family parameters are fully accessible in Dynamo, enabling generative design workflows: algorithmic pattern generation, optimised cutting layouts, responsive tile sizing to room dimensions, and automated quantity extraction from parametric designs.

**S2.17:** Do you provide IFC export guidance for tile objects in Solibri model checking?

**A:** Yes. IFC export guidance covers: property set mapping, classification alignment, geometry optimisation, and COBie data inclusion. Our BIM team can assist with Solibri model checking setup for tile-specific rule sets and information take-off.

**S2.18:** Can your BIM objects include pricing data for 5D cost estimation?

**A:** BIM objects include list price parameters that can be linked to 5D cost estimation workflows. For project-specific pricing, we provide Excel price lists that can be imported into cost estimation software (CostX, Vico Office, etc.).

**S2.19:** Do you provide guidance on using Tiletoria BIM objects with Speckle for cross-platform collaboration?

**A:** Yes. Our BIM team can advise on using Speckle streams to share tile data between Revit, ArchiCAD, Rhino, Grasshopper, and web platforms. This enables real-time collaboration between architects, engineers, and contractors on tile specification.

**S2.20:** Can Tiletoria BIM objects be integrated with Power BI for project dashboards?

**A:** Yes. Revit schedule data exported to Excel can be connected to Power BI dashboards showing: tile quantities by room, cost breakdowns, specification compliance status, and procurement progress. We provide sample Power BI templates for tile project dashboards.

**S2.21:** Can you produce a 'material palette' sample board with tiles, stone, timber, and metal together?


**S2.22:** Do you provide accelerated weathering samples for exterior project evaluation?

**A:** Accelerated weathering samples (QUV exposed for 500, 1000, and 2000 hours) demonstrate: colour stability, surface degradation, gloss change, and slip resistance evolution. These are valuable for projects in harsh coastal or high-UV environments.

**S2.23:** Can I get a 'maintenance comparison' sample showing clean vs dirty tile surfaces?

**A:** Yes. Maintenance comparison samples show: the same tile type with clean, lightly soiled, and heavily soiled surfaces, demonstrating cleanability and stain resistance. These help set realistic client expectations for maintenance requirements.

**S2.24:** Do you provide samples for adhesion pull-off testing on specific substrates?

**A:** Yes. Pull-off testing samples (typically 100x100mm) can be provided for adhesion testing on your project's actual substrate. We recommend testing at least 3 months before installation to allow time for adhesive system optimisation if needed.

**S2.25:** Can you produce samples showing tile edge profiles (rectified vs calibrated)?

**A:** Yes. Edge profile comparison samples show: rectified edges (sharp, precise), calibrated edges (slightly rounded), bevelled edges, and pressed edges. These demonstrate the joint width and aesthetic differences between edge types.

**S2.26:** What is the maximum format available in your-look book-matched porcelain?

**A:** Our premium-look book-matched porcelain is available in 1200x2600mm (4-panel book-match) and 1600x3200mm (4-panel book-match) formats. These create stunning feature walls with continuous veining across 4-6 metre widths.

**S2.27:** Do you offer tiles with integrated LED lighting or luminous effects?

**A:** Yes. Our luminous tile range includes: translucent porcelain panels for backlighting, phosphorescent tiles (glow-in-the-dark for wayfinding), and tiles with integrated LED channels. These are specified for: feature walls, signage, safety wayfinding, and theatrical effects.

**S2.28:** Can you supply tiles with custom corporate logos or branding embedded?

**A:** Yes. Custom logo tiles are produced by: waterjet cutting logo shapes from contrasting tiles, digital printing corporate imagery onto tiles, or mosaic inlay of logos and crests. Minimum quantities and setup fees apply.

**S2.29:** What is the thickest structural porcelain paver you offer for outdoor use?

**A:** Our thickest structural porcelain paver is 20mm, available in 600x600mm and 600x1200mm formats. These achieve breaking strength >3,000N and can be installed on pedestals, gravel beds, or mortar beds for outdoor pedestrian and light vehicle traffic.

**S2.30:** Do you have tiles that replicate traditional South African patterns or Ndebele art?

**A:** Yes. Our African Heritage collection includes: geometric pattern tiles inspired by Ndebele art, traditional Cape Dutch patterns, contemporary African designs, and custom pattern development for culturally significant projects. These support local design identity.

**S2.31:** What is the correct waterproofing specification for a concrete swimming pool?

**A:** Concrete pool specification: structural concrete shell (minimum 25 MPa), cementitious crystalline waterproofing (Xypex or equivalent) applied to positive and negative sides, mosaic tile installation with epoxy adhesive and grout, expansion joints at 3m centres, and 48-hour flood test before filling.

**S2.32:** How do I specify waterproofing for a green roof with pedestrian access?

**A:** Accessible green roof: structural slab, priming, FPO/TPO root-resistant membrane (≥1.5mm), protection board (6mm HDPE), drainage composite, geotextile, growing medium (150-300mm), and porcelain pavers on adjustable pedestals for walkway areas. Structural loading must include saturated soil weight.

**S2.33:** Can you provide a waterproofing specification for a helipad on a building roof?

**A:** Helipad waterproofing: liquid-applied polyurethane membrane (≥3mm) with UV stability, fuel and hydraulic fluid resistance, protection board, anti-slip surface treatment, and drainage integration. Must withstand: point loads from helicopter landing gear, fuel spills, and jet blast.

**S2.34:** What is the best waterproofing system for a water treatment plant?

**A:** Water treatment plant: chemical-resistant epoxy membrane (3-5mm), resistance to chlorine, aluminium sulphate, and caustic soda, non-slip surface (R12), full coving 150mm, and drainage falls 1:50 to gully traps. System must handle continuous wet conditions and chemical exposure.

**S2.35:** Do you specify different waterproofing systems for potable vs non-potable water applications?

**A:** Yes. Potable water applications require: WRAS-approved or AS/NZS 4020-certified membranes certified safe for drinking water contact. Non-potable applications (irrigation, process water) have less stringent requirements but still need impermeable, durable membranes.

**S2.36:** What tile specification do you recommend for a film studio floor?

**A:** Film studio: PEI Class 4-5, matte finish (no glare for lighting), neutral grey colour (for chroma key and colour grading), seamless appearance, acoustic properties, and compatibility with camera dollies and heavy equipment. Consider painted floor areas with durable tile base.

**S2.37:** Can you specify tiles for a zoo enclosure and veterinary area?

**A:** Zoo specifications include: PEI Class 5 (animal traffic and cleaning), R12 slip resistance (wet conditions), chemical resistance to disinfectants, thermal shock resistance (steam cleaning), impact resistance, and non-toxic materials safe for animal contact.

**S2.38:** What tiles are suitable for a distillery and aging warehouse?

**A:** Distillery: PEI Class 5, R11 slip resistance (spirit spillage), chemical resistance to alcohol and cleaning agents, coving for hygiene, and ability to withstand barrel rolling loads. Stone-look porcelain provides the traditional aesthetic with modern performance.

**S2.39:** Do you have tiles for a paintball arena or adventure sports facility?

**A:** Adventure sports: PEI Class 5, maximum impact resistance, R12 slip resistance, easy cleaning of paint/mud, and ability to withstand abrasive conditions. Full-body technical porcelain or epoxy flooring systems are recommended for these extreme environments.

**S2.40:** Can you specify tiles for a crematorium and chapel of rest?


**S2.41:** Can you provide Social Life Cycle Assessment (S-LCA) data for your tiles?

**A:** Social LCA data is available for select manufacturing partners, covering: worker safety records, fair wage compliance, community impact, gender equality metrics, and human rights due diligence. This supports projects with comprehensive sustainability requirements.

**S2.42:** Do you have tiles that contribute to the WELL Building Standard 'Mind' concept?

**A:** Tiles contribute to WELL Mind through: biophilic design patterns (wood-look, stone-look, natural textures), calming colour palettes, acoustic comfort (reducing stress from noise), and spaces that connect occupants with natural materials and patterns.

**S2.43:** Can tiles be specified to meet the Passive House EnerPHit standard?

**A:** For EnerPHit retrofits, tiles contribute through: thermal mass (stabilising internal temperatures), airtightness detailing at floor-wall junctions, and compatibility with insulation systems. Our technical team can advise on tile integration with Passive House details.

**S2.44:** Do you provide Health Product Declarations (HPDs) for your tiles?

**A:** Health Product Declarations (HPDs) per Health Product Declaration Collaborative standards are available for select ranges. HPDs disclose: material ingredients, presence of hazardous substances, and exposure risks. These support WELL Building Standard and LEED v4.1 compliance.

**S2.45:** Can you provide a Declare label for any of your products?

**A:** Declare labels (product 'nutrition labels' listing all ingredients) are available for select manufacturing partners participating in the International Living Future Institute's Declare programme. Red List-free Declare labels support Living Building Challenge projects.

**S2.46:** Does Tiletoria offer joint marketing opportunities for featured projects?

**A:** Yes. Joint marketing includes: project case studies, co-branded photography, social media features, publication editorial, award submission support, and project open day coordination. Both Tiletoria and the architect practice are credited in all marketing materials.

**S2.47:** Can Tiletoria provide a letter of intent for projects at concept stage?

**A:** Yes. Letters of Intent confirm: willingness to supply specified products, indicative pricing (subject to final quantities), availability assurance, and technical support commitment. These support client funding applications and development approval submissions.

**S2.48:** Do you have experience with Development Finance Institution (DFI) funded projects?

**A:** Yes. We have supplied to projects funded by: DBSA, IDC, and international DFIs. DFI compliance includes: B-BBEE requirements, local content verification, environmental compliance, and community benefit documentation.

**S2.49:** Can you accommodate SANS 10845-compliant procurement processes?

**A:** Yes. SANS 10845 (Construction Procurement) compliance includes: standardised conditions of tender, objective criteria evaluation, transparency in procurement, and fair opportunity for all suppliers. Our tender submissions are formatted to SANS 10845 requirements.

**S2.50:** Does Tiletoria offer project bank account (PBA) compliance?

**A:** For projects requiring Project Bank Accounts (construction works >R50 million), we can structure our invoicing and payment processes to comply with PBA requirements, ensuring timely payment while meeting statutory obligations.

**S2.51:** Can you support our practice's ISO 14001 environmental management requirements?

**A:** Yes. We provide: environmental policy statements, product environmental data, carbon footprint information, waste management procedures, and supplier environmental performance reports. These support your practice's ISO 14001 environmental management system.

**S2.52:** Do you provide expert witness services for litigation involving tile failures?
