**A:** Yes. Our Technical Director provides expert witness services for litigation involving: tile specification disputes, installation failure analysis, product defect claims, and waterproofing failure investigations. Expert witness services are independent and evidence-based.

**S2.53:** Can Tiletoria support our practice's risk register with tile-specific risk assessments?

**A:** Yes. Tile-specific risk assessments cover: product discontinuation risk, supply chain disruption, installation quality risk, performance failure modes, and warranty claim likelihood. Risk ratings and mitigation strategies are provided for your project risk register.

**S2.54:** Do you provide 'lessons learned' workshops after project completion?

**A:** Yes. Post-project workshops (carrying CPD credits) review: specification outcomes, installation challenges, product performance, cost outcomes, and improvement opportunities. These workshops benefit the entire project team including architect, contractor, and client.

**S2.55:** Can you assist with preparing O&M manuals for the tile package?

**A:** O&M manual preparation includes: product data sheets, cleaning and maintenance instructions, warranty documentation, spare parts list (replacement tiles, grout, sealant), emergency repair procedures, and contractor contact details for warranty claims.

**S2.56:** Can you provide a Declaration of Performance (DoP) per Construction Products Regulation (CPR)?

**A:** Yes. Declarations of Performance per EU Regulation 305/2011 are available for all CE-marked products. DoPs declare: manufacturer, product type, intended use, essential characteristics, and performance levels against harmonised European standards.

**S2.57:** Do your tiles comply with Singapore Green Mark requirements?

**A:** For Singapore Green Mark projects, we provide: recycled content data, local sourcing documentation (within 800km of Singapore for regional materials credit), VOC emissions data, and environmental product information. Direct Singapore supply can be arranged.

**S2.58:** Can you provide FM Global approval data for insurance purposes?

**A:** FM Global data is available for select industrial tile systems, covering: fire resistance, seismic resistance, and property loss prevention. FM Approval is typically required for high-value industrial facilities seeking insurance coverage.

**S2.59:** Do your tiles meet UAE Fire and Life Safety Code requirements?

**A:** For UAE projects, we provide: Civil Defence approval certificates, fire rating per UAE Fire Code, smoke toxicity data, and testing from UAE-approved laboratories. Our Dubai office can assist with local compliance requirements.

**S2.60:** Can you provide CCC (China Compulsory Certification) for projects in China?

**A:** CCC certification is available for our manufacturing partners with Chinese market presence. For projects in China requiring imported tiles, we coordinate CCC compliance and customs clearance through our manufacturing partners' Chinese operations.

**S2.61:** Can you provide details for tiled reception desks and counters?

**A:** Reception desk details show: substrate framing, waterproofing (if sink present), large-format tile or quartz slab installation, edge treatment (waterfall, pencil edge, or profile), cut-out details for services, and junction with floor and wall finishes.

**S2.62:** Do you have details for tile-clad lift car interiors?

**A:** Lift car details include: lightweight tile specification (max weight per m²), fixing system (adhesive or mechanical), control panel integration, handrail fixing reinforcement, mirror integration, and compliance with lift safety codes (SANS 1545).

**S2.63:** Can you provide details for tiled signage and wayfinding elements?

**A:** Wayfinding details show: tactile tile indicators for accessible routes, colour-contrast tiles for visual wayfinding, inlaid tile signage (room numbers, directional arrows), and integration with floor finishes without creating trip hazards.

**S2.64:** Do you have details for tile integration with under-slab drainage channels?

**A:** Under-slab drainage details include: channel drain specification, waterproofing integration with channel edges, tile cutting around drain grates, falls to channel, and structural slab modifications for channel installation.

**S2.65:** Can you provide details for tile repair after core drilling for new services?

**A:** Post-core-drill repair details show: core drill procedure to minimise damage, replacement tile cutting and fixing, grout matching, waterproofing reinstatement (wet areas), and colour blending with weathered adjacent tiles.

**S2.66:** Can I arrange a video call with your technical team while I'm on site?

**A:** Yes. Site video calls via WhatsApp, Teams, or Zoom allow our technical team to: visually assess site conditions, diagnose installation issues, guide sample verification, and provide immediate technical advice. Schedule calls or connect on an ad-hoc basis.

**S2.67:** Does Tiletoria provide a dedicated WhatsApp group for large projects?

**A:** For large projects (R10 million+), a dedicated WhatsApp group is created including: architect, project manager, Tiletoria technical lead, and Tiletoria project coordinator. This enables rapid, informal communication for quick decisions and issue resolution.

**S2.68:** Can I get same-day sample delivery in Johannesburg for an urgent client meeting?

**A:** Yes. Same-day sample delivery is available in Johannesburg and Pretoria for orders placed before 10:00. Express courier services deliver samples directly to your office, client venue, or site office. Delivery confirmation is provided via SMS.

**S2.69:** Does the Spec Lab have showers for visitors who cycle or run to appointments?

**A:** While we don't have dedicated shower facilities, our staff facilities include a bathroom with basic amenities. We recommend visitors requiring shower facilities use nearby gym facilities (Virgin Active Sandton is 2km away).

**S2.70:** Can I bring my dog to the Spec Lab?

**A:** Unfortunately, due to the presence of heavy tile samples, sharp edges, and the professional nature of the facility, pets are not permitted in the Spec Lab. Service animals are welcome with prior notification.

**S2.71:** How does Tiletoria handle complex international projects compared to global tile suppliers?

**A:** For international projects, Tiletoria offers: global manufacturer relationships, multi-country logistics coordination, international standard compliance (CE, ASTM, ISO), and South African-based project management. Global suppliers may lack our understanding of local conditions and SANS compliance.

**S2.72:** Does Tiletoria invest in R&D for new tile technologies?

**A:** While we are a distributor, we actively influence manufacturer R&D through: market feedback from South African projects, custom development requests, technology trend identification, and pilot project partnerships. This R&D influence benefits our specifier network with early access to innovations.

**S2.73:** How does Tiletoria's training of contractor networks benefit architects?

**A:** Our contractor training programme ensures: correct installation of your specification, fewer site defects, valid warranties, consistent quality across projects, and reduced architect site inspection burden. Well-trained contractors are essential to realising design intent.

**S2.74:** What is Tiletoria's policy on supporting emerging architectural practices?

**A:** We support emerging practices through: no-minimum-order specification support, free CPD attendance, sample provision without volume requirements, mentorship from our technical team, and feature opportunities in our marketing. We believe in nurturing the next generation of architects.

**S2.75:** How does Tiletoria ensure ethical business practices in all dealings?

**A:** Our ethical commitment includes: transparent pricing with no hidden costs, honest technical advice (even when it doesn't favour a sale), respect for architect design intent (never pressuring specification changes), fair treatment of contractors, and compliance with all applicable laws and industry codes.


## Supplementary Section 3: Additional Topics: Emerging Applications, Advanced Sustainability & Specialist Environments

**S3.1:** What is the maximum allowable deflection for a timber floor before tiling?

**A:** For timber floors, maximum allowable deflection is L/360 (span divided by 360) for ceramic tiles and L/480 for porcelain tiles. The floor must also be stable (no bounce), have adequate joist sizing, and be overlaid with cement particle board (minimum 18mm) before tiling.

**S3.2:** Can you provide guidance on tile fixing in coastal environments with high salt exposure?

**A:** Coastal specifications include: salt-resistant porcelain (tested to EN 12370), stainless steel fixings and profiles (316 grade), salt-tolerant adhesives and grouts, enhanced waterproofing, and more frequent maintenance cleaning to remove salt deposits.

**S3.3:** What is the recommended movement joint spacing for a 50m x 30m airport concourse?

**A:** For a 50m x 30m concourse: perimeter movement joints at all edges, intermediate movement joints at 8-10m centres in both directions (creating bays of approximately 8m x 8m), structural movement joints at building expansion joints, and additional joints at changes in substrate or traffic pattern.

**S3.4:** How do I specify tiles for a building that will be constructed over a 5-year phased programme?

**A:** Phased programme specification: select from Permanent Collection products, specify batch consistency requirements, include stockholding clauses, require 12-month discontinuation notice, and plan for potential product evolution between early and late phases.

**S3.5:** What is the correct specification for tiling over a post-tensioned concrete slab?

**A:** Post-tensioned slabs require: allowance for slab shortening (typically 0.1-0.3%), movement joints at closer centres (6-8m), flexible adhesive (S1 classification), perimeter isolation joints, and coordination with PT strand layout to avoid fixing through tendons.

**S3.6:** Can you provide a specification for tile fixing to a lightweight steel deck?

**A:** Steel deck fixing: minimum 1.2mm steel gauge, structural engineer verification of deflection, 22mm cement particle board screwed to deck, flexible adhesive (S1), movement joints at 6m centres, perimeter isolation, and maximum tile weight 25kg/m².

**S3.7:** What is the best approach for tiling over underfloor heating cables?

**A:** UFH cable specification: insulation board beneath cables, cable layout per heating design, full coverage with no gaps, screed or self-levelling over cables (minimum 10mm cover), 28-day cure, then flexible adhesive and grout with expansion joints at 8m centres.

**S3.8:** How do I handle tile specification when the substrate is unknown at design stage?

**A:** When substrate is unknown, specify: performance requirements for any acceptable substrate, substrate assessment by contractor before installation, approved preparation methods, and qualified installer verification. Include provisional sums for substrate preparation.

**S3.9:** Can you specify tiles for a building with settlement-prone foundations?

**A:** Settlement-prone sites require: flexible tile adhesive (S1 classification), movement joints at reduced spacing (5-6m), perimeter isolation joints, crack isolation membrane, and smaller tile formats (better accommodating movement than large formats). Structural engineer input essential.

**S3.10:** What is the maximum tile size that can be used on a curved wall with 3m radius?

**A:** For a 3m radius curved wall: maximum tile size approximately 300x300mm for rigid porcelain (using tapered adhesive bedding), or up to 600x600mm with flexible adhesive and back-buttering. Smaller tiles and mosaics accommodate tighter radii more easily.

**S3.11:** How should I specify the 'ambient conditions' requirement for tile installation?

**A:** Specify: minimum 5°C and maximum 35°C during installation and for 7 days after; relative humidity <85%; protection from direct sun, wind, and rain; and controlled curing conditions. These ensure proper adhesive curing and long-term performance.

**S3.12:** What should my specification say about tile cutting quality?

**A:** Specify: all cut edges clean and without chips >1mm, cuts straight within 1mm over 600mm, holes core-drilled (not percussion-drilled) with clean edges, and all cut tiles reviewed by architect before fixing. Wet cutting equipment mandatory for porcelain.

**S3.13:** How do I specify protection of tiles during construction?

**A:** Protection specification: cover all tiled floors with breathable protection board within 24 hours of grouting, protect walls with cardboard or film, prohibit heavy traffic for 14 days, prohibit construction traffic until building is weathertight, and specify damage repair responsibility.

**S3.14:** What should I include about cleaning and handover in my tile specification?

**A:** Cleaning and handover: contractor to clean all tiles before handover using pH-neutral cleaner, remove all grout haze, polish polished tiles, seal any required surfaces, provide maintenance manual, and retain 3-5% spare tiles labelled and stored on site.

**S3.15:** How do I write a performance specification for tile adhesive without naming a brand?

**A:** Performance-based adhesive specification: 'Cementitious tile adhesive, C2TES1 classification per EN 12004, improved adhesion, deformable, slip-resistant, extended open time. SUITABILITY: porcelain floor tiles up to 1200x1200mm on concrete substrates in dry and wet internal conditions.'

**S3.16:** What should I specify for tile joints in a high-hygiene environment?

**A:** High-hygiene joint specification: epoxy-based grout (leading adhesive manufacturers Kerapoxy or performance equivalent), joint width 3-5mm (cleanable), antimicrobial additive, colour to minimise visible staining, and sealed junctions at all floor-wall and floor-fixture interfaces with sanitary grade silicone.

**S3.17:** How do I specify the required flatness of the substrate for tiling?

**A:** Substrate flatness specification: ±3mm under a 2m straightedge for standard tiles (up to 600mm), ±2mm under a 2m straightedge for large-format tiles (600mm+), and ±1mm under a 2m straightedge for large-format thin porcelain (3-6mm thick).

**S3.18:** What should my specification say about tile sample approval?

**A:** Sample approval: contractor to submit Architect Approval Samples (AAS) consisting of 3 full-size tiles per type for architect sign-off before ordering. Samples must represent the actual product to be supplied. No tile delivery without signed AAS approval.

**S3.19:** How do I handle tile specification on a PFI (Private Finance Initiative) project?

**A:** PFI specification emphasises: whole-life cost (not first cost), 25+ year service life, performance guarantees, maintenance cost certainty, transfer condition standards, and contractual mechanisms linking specification to service delivery outcomes.

**S3.20:** What is the best way to specify tiles for a heritage overlay zone?

**A:** Heritage overlay specification: materials and patterns sympathetic to the heritage character, SAHRA/PHRA pre-approval, traditional installation methods (lime-based where appropriate), matching analysis of existing fabric, and documentation for heritage authority submission.

**S3.21:** How does Tiletoria manage the submittal review process during construction?

**A:** Submittal review: contractor submits product data, samples, and shop drawings; we review for compliance with specification within 2 working days; comments and approval/rejection are documented; and approved submittals become the quality benchmark for site delivery.

**S3.22:** What is Tiletoria's process for handling substitution requests during construction?

**A:** Substitution process: contractor submits substitution request with full technical data and samples; we evaluate against specification performance criteria; provide technical comparison and recommendation; architect makes final approval decision; and approved substitution is documented with revised specifications.

**S3.23:** Does Tiletoria provide shop drawing review for complex tile installations?

**A:** Yes. Shop drawing review covers: tile layout and setting out, movement joint placement, expansion joint details, drainage integration, transition details, and coordination with other trades. Review comments are provided within 3 working days.

**S3.24:** How do you handle warranty claims on completed installations?

**A:** Warranty claim process: written notification with photographs and description, site inspection by our technical team, laboratory testing if required, determination of cause (product defect, installation error, or maintenance issue), and resolution through repair, replacement, or advisory as appropriate.

**S3.25:** Can Tiletoria provide a technical attendance record for site inspections?

**A:** Yes. Site attendance records document: date and time of visit, persons present, areas inspected, observations and findings, recommendations issued, and photographs taken. These records form part of the project quality documentation.

**S3.26:** Do you provide 'snagging list' support at practical completion?

**A:** Yes. Snagging support includes: joint site inspection with architect and contractor, identification of defects against specification, prioritisation of defects (critical/major/minor), recommended remedial actions, and follow-up inspection after rectification.

**S3.27:** How does Tiletoria support defect liability period inspections?

**A:** DLP support includes: inspection at 6-month and 11-month intervals (or as contract requires), assessment of tile performance, identification of any defects, coordination of remedial work, and sign-off at end of DLP if all items are satisfactory.

**S3.28:** Can you provide emergency response for active leaks in tiled areas?

**A:** Emergency leak response: telephone guidance within 1 hour of notification, site attendance within 24 hours (Gauteng) or 48 hours (other provinces), leak source identification (waterproofing, plumbing, structural), and emergency repair coordination to prevent further damage.

**S3.29:** Does Tiletoria provide condition surveys of existing tiled installations?

**A:** Yes. Condition surveys assess: tile condition (wear, damage, staining), grout condition, joint integrity, adhesion (tap testing), waterproofing integrity (where accessible), slip resistance, and remaining service life. Reports include: findings, recommendations, and budget estimates for remedial work.

**S3.30:** Can you support dilapidation surveys for lease-end handover?

**A:** Dilapidation survey support includes: assessment of tiled areas against lease conditions, photographic documentation, condition rating, repair vs replacement recommendations, and cost estimates for tenant liability assessment.

**S3.31:** How does Tiletoria ensure knowledge transfer when a staff member leaves?

**A:** Knowledge transfer protocols: comprehensive CRM records, structured handover periods, documented technical opinions and advice, overlapping responsibilities during transition, and introduction of replacement staff to key specifier contacts before the outgoing staff member departs.

**S3.32:** Can I get a direct line to the Technical Director for complex queries?

**A:** Practice partners and major project architects have direct contact details for the Technical Director. While initial queries are handled by the technical team, escalation to the Technical Director is available for: complex technical issues, dispute resolution, and specialist advice requests.

**S3.33:** Does Tiletoria provide annual business reviews for practice partners?

**A:** Yes. Annual business reviews cover: specification pipeline analysis, project outcomes, service feedback, CPD attendance summary, new product previews, pricing review, and joint planning for the coming year. These 60-minute reviews strengthen the practice-supplier relationship.

**S3.34:** How does Tiletoria handle feedback from architects who are dissatisfied?

**A:** Dissatisfaction protocol: immediate escalation to management, personal contact within 4 hours, root cause investigation, corrective action implementation, service recovery (where appropriate), and follow-up to confirm satisfaction. Feedback drives our continuous improvement programme.

**S3.35:** Can I request a specific technical team member who knows my project history?

**A:** Yes. While we allocate staff based on availability and expertise, you can request specific team members who know your project history and preferences. We maintain continuity through detailed CRM records even when your preferred contact is unavailable.

**S3.36:** Can the Spec Lab accommodate an entire project team of 15-20 people?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab can accommodate groups of up to 40 people in our presentation area. For groups of 15-20, we configure the space with: presentation setup, sample examination stations, breakout areas, and catering facilities.

**S3.37:** Does the Spec Lab have a reference library of building standards and codes?

**A:** Yes. Our reference library includes: SANS 1449, SANS 10107, SANS 10400 (all parts), NHBRC Technical Requirements, Green Star SA tools, and international standards (EN, ASTM, ISO). These references support specification writing during visits.

**S3.38:** Can I work remotely from the Spec Lab for a full day?

**A:** Yes. The Spec Lab offers 'co-working day' bookings where you can: work at a dedicated desk, access Wi-Fi and power, examine samples as needed, consult with technical staff, and use meeting rooms for calls. Coffee and light refreshments are provided.

**S3.39:** Does the Spec Lab have facilities for photographing samples for my presentations?

**A:** Yes. A photography station with: neutral background, adjustable lighting (2700K-6500K), scale reference, and high-resolution camera is available for photographing samples. Images are provided on USB or emailed immediately after your session.

**S3.40:** Can I arrange a private evening event at the Spec Lab for my client?

**A:** Yes. Private evening events (17:30-21:00) can be arranged for client entertainment, project celebrations, or special presentations. Facilities include: full showroom access, AV presentation, catering and bar service, and valet parking.

**S3.41:** What should I consider when specifying tiles for a building in a seismic zone?

**A:** Seismic considerations: flexible adhesive (S1), smaller tile formats (better movement accommodation), movement joints at 5-6m centres, perimeter isolation joints, crack isolation membrane, and coordination with structural engineer for expected inter-storey drift.

**S3.42:** How do I specify tiles for a high-altitude project (2000m+) with frost risk?

**A:** High-altitude specification: frost-resistant porcelain (verified to ISO 10545-12), thermal shock resistance, flexible adhesive and grout, movement joints at 6m centres, and installation when ambient temperature is above 5°C. Consider snow loading and freeze-thaw cycles.

**S3.43:** What is the best tile specification for a building on reclaimed land with settlement risk?

**A:** Reclaimed land specification: anticipate differential settlement, specify flexible adhesive (S1), smaller tiles, movement joints at 5-6m, perimeter isolation, crack isolation membrane, and specify that the contractor must monitor and report any settlement movement during construction.

**S3.44:** Can you provide guidance on tile specification for buildings in malaria-risk areas?

**A:** Malaria-area guidance: light-coloured tiles (mosquitoes are attracted to dark surfaces), smooth surfaces (easier to detect and remove mosquitoes), chemical-resistant tiles for insecticide spraying, and outdoor anti-slip tiles for wet areas around water features.

**S3.45:** How should I specify tiles for a project in a mining town with dust and heavy soiling?

**A:** Mining town specification: PEI Class 5, high abrasion resistance, R10-R11 slip resistance (dust creates slippery conditions), dark colours to hide soiling, full coving for hygiene, and specify frequent cleaning protocols in the O&M manual.

**S3.46:** What tile specification is appropriate for a coastal holiday home with intermittent occupancy?

**A:** Coastal holiday home: frost-resistant porcelain, salt-resistant tiles and fixings, stainless steel profiles (316 grade), mould-resistant grout, and specify that the building must be aired regularly to prevent condensation-related issues during unoccupied periods.

**S3.47:** Can you advise on tile specification for a game lodge with thatch roofing?

**A:** Game lodge: rustic aesthetic (stone-look, wood-look, terracotta-look), PEI Class 3-4 (moderate traffic), R10 slip resistance, compatibility with thatch fire risk management (Class A1 fire rating), and natural colour palette sympathetic to bushveld surroundings.

**S3.48:** How do I specify tiles for a wine estate cellar and tasting room?

**A:** Wine estate: cellar requires PEI 5, R11 (wet/chemical exposure), acid resistance, thermal stability (cool cellar temperatures). Tasting room can use more decorative options:-look, wood-look, with design appropriate to estate character.

**S3.49:** What is the correct tile specification for a correctional facility kitchen?

**A:** Correctional kitchen: PEI 5, R12 slip resistance, full coving 150mm, epoxy-based grout, chemical resistance, no loose components (everything fixed), vandal-resistant surfaces, and institutional colour scheme. Security requirements must be coordinated with DCS.

**S3.50:** Can you provide a tile specification for a high-end residential bathroom with underfloor heating?

**A:** Luxury residential bathroom: large-format-look porcelain (600x1200mm or 1200x1200mm), heated floor (cable or mat system), R9-R10 slip resistance, book-matched feature wall, recessed shower niche, linear drain, hobless shower entry, and epoxy-based grout in shower area.

**S3.51:** Does Tiletoria offer a satisfaction guarantee on its specification support services?

**A:** Yes. Our specification support satisfaction guarantee ensures: 4-hour response on technical queries, free specification review with actionable feedback, free CPD of the highest quality, and dedicated liaison availability. If you're not satisfied with any service, we will make it right or refund any fees charged.

**S3.52:** What is Tiletoria's vision for the future of architectural tile specification in South Africa?

**A:** Our vision includes: fully digital specification workflows (BIM-integrated), AI-assisted product selection, real-time stock and pricing data, virtual reality showroom experiences, carbon-neutral product offerings, and continued elevation of professional standards through CPD and technical excellence.

**S3.53:** How can I become a Tiletoria practice partner?

**A:** To become a practice partner: contact our Professional Services Manager, complete a partnership application, agree to the partnership terms (primarily a commitment to collaborate in good faith), and begin accessing: dedicated liaison support, priority CPD booking, early product access, and joint marketing opportunities.

**S3.54:** What is the best way to start working with Tiletoria on my next project?

**A:** Start by: visiting the Spec Lab for an initial consultation, discussing your project type and requirements with our technical team, reviewing relevant project case studies, attending a CPD session to experience our technical approach, and requesting a specification template for your project type.

**S3.55:** Can you provide a final summary of why I should specify Tiletoria for my next project?

**A:** Key reasons: (1) Widest range of premium imported and local tiles, (2) Unmatched technical documentation and BIM resources, (3) SAIA-accredited CPD programme, (4) 4-hour technical response guarantee, (5) 25+ year track record on major projects, (6) Dedicated Spec Lab for professional specifiers, (7) Comprehensive specification support from concept to completion, (8) Strong sustainability credentials and Green Star support.


## Supplementary Section 4: Additional Topics: Unique Building Types, Specialist Facilities & Niche Applications

**S4.1:** What is the typical drying time for cementitious tile adhesive before grouting?

**A:** Cementitious adhesive requires minimum 24 hours drying time at 20°C before grouting can commence. In cold or humid conditions, extend to 48 hours. Fast-set adhesives can accept grout in 3-6 hours but should not be rushed on large commercial projects.

**S4.2:** Can you provide guidance on tile specification for passive solar design buildings?

**A:** Passive solar tile specification: high thermal mass tiles (thick porcelain on slab), dark-coloured floor tiles in sunspaces (absorb and store heat), light-coloured tiles in occupied spaces (reflect heat in summer), and coordination with thermal modelling for optimal performance.

**S4.3:** How do I specify tiles for a mixed-use building with residential, retail, and office components?

**A:** Mixed-use specification: separate performance criteria per component (PEI 3 residential, PEI 5 retail/office), standardise where possible (common lobby tiles used across components), coordinate colour palette for visual continuity, and specify different maintenance protocols per component.

**S4.4:** What is the recommended approach for specifying tiles in a design-and-build contract where the contractor selects the subcontractor?

**A:** Design-and-build approach: specify performance criteria (PEI, slip resistance, water absorption, breaking strength), include 'basis of design' products, require architect approval of contractor's proposed equivalent, and include mock-up and sample approval requirements.

**S4.5:** Can you advise on the acoustic benefits of carpet vs tile in an office environment?

**A:** Carpet provides better impact sound absorption (ΔLw 20-25dB) than tile alone (ΔLw 5-8dB). However, tile with acoustic underlayment achieves ΔLw 17-22dB, bridging the gap while offering tile's durability and low maintenance advantages.

**S4.6:** What should I consider when specifying tiles for a childcare facility or creche?

**A:** Childcare specification: R10 slip resistance, chemical resistance to sanitising, non-toxic materials (lead-free, no VOCs), warm colours, smaller formats (easier to replace damaged tiles), full coving for hygiene, and easy-clean surfaces for food spillage areas.

**S4.7:** How do I specify tiles for a high-security facility where hidden compartments are a concern?

**A:** High-security specification: large-format tiles (harder to tamper with), fixed rather than loose-lay installation, specify inspection of substrate before fixing, avoid raised access floors where possible, and coordinate with security consultant for any specific requirements.

**S4.8:** Can you provide a specification for tiles in a laundry facility with chemical exposure?

**A:** Laundry specification: PEI Class 5, chemical resistance to detergents, bleaches, and alkalis, R11 slip resistance (wet floors), thermal shock resistance (hot water spillage), full coving, and floor drainage every 3m. Specify acid-resistant epoxy-based grout.

**S4.9:** What is the best tile specification for a spa and wellness centre?

**A:** Spa/wellness: R10 slip resistance (dry areas), R11 (wet areas/pool), calming natural aesthetic (stone-look, wood-look), underfloor heating compatibility, chemical resistance to spa treatments, and seamless transitions between treatment rooms and wet areas.

**S4.10:** How should I specify tiles for a bus or taxi rank with extremely heavy pedestrian traffic?

**A:** Transport hub: PEI Class 5, R12 slip resistance (all weather), graffiti-resistant surface, point load capacity for cleaning equipment, vandal-resistant, easy maintenance, and specify robust movement joint profiles that won't be damaged by rolling cases and trolleys.

**S4.11:** Can you provide a tile specification for a funeral chapel with crematorium?


**S4.12:** What tiles do you recommend for a recording studio or broadcast facility?

**A:** Recording studio: PEI Class 3-4 (low foot traffic), acoustic-friendly surface (not highly reflective), anti-static properties, no creaking or loose elements, neutral colours (no visual distraction for video), and compatibility with cable management systems.

**S4.13:** How do I specify tiles for a border post or customs facility?

**A:** Border post: PEI Class 5, R11-R12 slip resistance, chemical resistance to de-icing salts, vandal-resistant, easy decontamination (biohazard protocol), and robust enough for 24/7 operation with minimal maintenance windows.

**S4.14:** Can you advise on tile specification for a high-end residential wine cellar?

**A:** Wine cellar: R10 slip resistance, thermal stability (10-16°C, 70-80% humidity), chemical resistance to wine acids, dark stone-look or traditional brick aesthetic, and full coving for cleaning. Consider decorative floor drains for spillage management.

**S4.15:** What is the correct specification for tiles in a home theatre or cinema room?

**A:** Home theatre: dark colours (minimise light reflection), acoustic absorption (carpet or carpet-tile alternative), PEI Class 2-3 (low traffic), and design appropriate to the cinematic aesthetic. Consider LED accent strip integration in floor or wall features.

**S4.16:** How should I specify tiles for a yoga or meditation studio?

**A:** Yoga studio: R9 slip resistance (barefoot), warm tactile surface (avoid cold-feeling polished tiles), natural calming aesthetic (wood-look, stone-look), acoustic properties, easy cleaning (sweat and mat residue), and calming neutral or earth-tone colours.

**S4.17:** Can you provide a specification for tiles in a dry cleaning or laundromat facility?

**A:** Dry cleaning: PEI Class 4-5, chemical resistance to PERC and other solvents, R10 slip resistance, stain-resistant surface, and ventilation consideration (solvent vapours). Specify impervious porcelain with chemical-resistant grout.

**S4.18:** What tiles are suitable for a pet grooming facility or veterinary washing area?

**A:** Pet grooming: PEI Class 4-5, R11 slip resistance (wet floors, animal hair), chemical resistance to disinfectants and shampoos, full coving 150mm for hygiene, floor drainage every 2m, and scratch-resistant surface. Specify epoxy-based grout.

**S4.19:** How do I specify tiles for a cold storage facility with forklift traffic?

**A:** Cold storage: PEI Class 5, R12 slip resistance (ice/condensation), frost resistance (to -30°C), thermal shock resistance, impact resistance (dropped loads), chemical resistance to cleaning agents, and installation with frost-resistant adhesive at minimum 5°C.

**S4.20:** Can you advise on tiles for a rotary or flight school briefing room?

**A:** Flight school: PEI Class 3-4, R9 slip resistance, projection-friendly surfaces (low glare), acoustic comfort, durable for high traffic, and design appropriate to professional aviation environment. Consider branding integration for flight school identity.

**S4.21:** What is the best tile specification for an art gallery's conservation storage?

**A:** Gallery storage: PEI Class 3-4, chemically inert surface (no off-gassing), smooth for easy cleaning, light-coloured for visibility, non-abrasive (won't damage dropped objects), and compatible with climate control systems (18-22°C, 50-55% RH).

**S4.22:** How should I specify tiles for a municipal swimming pool and change room complex?

**A:** Municipal pool: pool interior — mosaic tiles, chemical-resistant, DIN 51097 Class B/C. Changing rooms — R11 slip resistance, coved skirting, epoxy-based grout. Walkways — R12, frost-resistant if outdoor. All areas require SANS 10400 Part P compliance.

**S4.23:** Can you provide a tile specification for a high-end car dealership showroom?

**A:** Car showroom: PEI Class 4-5 (rolling vehicle loads), R9 slip resistance (dry), high gloss or polished finish for premium aesthetic, light colour to enhance vehicle appearance, and chemical resistance to tyre marks and cleaning agents.

**S4.24:** What tiles do you recommend for a horse stable or equestrian facility?

**A:** Equestrian facility: PEI Class 5, R12 slip resistance (wet straw/urine), chemical resistance to ammonia and disinfectants, full coving for hygiene, floor drainage, and impact resistance (hoof traffic). Anti-slip textured porcelain or technical stone required.

**S4.25:** How do I specify tiles for a food truck park or outdoor dining precinct?

**A:** Food precinct: PEI Class 5, R11-R12 slip resistance, stain resistance to food and beverages, chemical resistance to cleaning agents, UV stability for outdoor exposure, and design flexibility for vibrant, characterful aesthetic.

**S4.26:** Can you advise on tile specification for a rooftop bar or restaurant?

**A:** Rooftop bar: R11 slip resistance, UV-stable tiles, frost-resistant if applicable, chemical resistance to food/drinks, compatibility with waterproofing membrane system, drainage integration, and weight loading verified by structural engineer.

**S4.27:** What is the correct tile specification for a cruise ship or yacht interior?

**A:** Marine interior: IMO Fire Test Code compliant (Part 5 surface flammability, Part 2 smoke and toxicity), lightweight tiles where possible, R10 slip resistance, chemical resistance to saltwater exposure, and certification from maritime classification society (Lloyd's, DNV GL).

**S4.28:** How should I specify tiles for a border control or immigration facility?

**A:** Immigration facility: PEI Class 5 (24/7 traffic), R10 slip resistance, chemical resistance to disinfectants, vandal-resistant, institutional colour scheme, low maintenance, and coordination with security systems (no interference with metal detectors).

**S4.29:** Can you provide a tile specification for a concert hall or performance venue backstage?

**A:** Concert hall: PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance, acoustic properties, dark colours backstage (theatre tradition), chemical resistance to stage makeup and cleaning agents, and durable for equipment loading and heavy foot traffic during performances.

**S4.30:** What tiles are suitable for a military barracks or training facility?

**A:** Military facility: PEI Class 5, R11-R12 slip resistance, extreme durability, low maintenance, institutional aesthetic, chemical resistance, and compliance with Department of Defence procurement requirements. Security clearance may be required for site visits.

**S4.31:** How do I specify tiles for an aquarium public walkway with water features?

**A:** Aquarium walkway: R12 slip resistance (water splashing), saltwater resistance, chemical resistance to cleaning disinfectants, PEI Class 5, and design that complements marine environment (blue tones, organic patterns). Coordinate with themed environment design.

**S4.32:** Can you advise on tiles for a trampoline park or indoor play centre?

**A:** Trampoline park: PEI Class 5, R10-R11 slip resistance, impact resistance, chemical resistance to sanitising, bright fun colours, and full coving for hygiene. Consider foam-padded areas adjacent to trampolines with tile in circulation areas.

**S4.33:** What is the best tile specification for a drive-through restaurant?

**A:** Drive-through: PEI Class 5, R11-R12 slip resistance (grease/water), chemical resistance to oil and cleaning agents, point load for delivery vehicles, and fast-cleaning surface for 24/7 operations. Full coving in kitchen and service areas.

**S4.34:** How should I specify tiles for a self-storage facility corridor?

**A:** Self-storage: PEI Class 4-5, R9 slip resistance, durable for trolleys and dollies, low maintenance, institutional aesthetic, and cost-effective specification (high volume, low individual unit value). Standard commercial porcelain is typically sufficient.

**S4.35:** Can you provide a tile specification for a university laboratory building?

**A:** University lab: PEI Class 5, chemical resistance to acids/solvents, R10-R11 slip resistance, full coving 100mm, epoxy-based grout, and compliance with university safety standards. Different labs may have specific requirements (fume hood areas, clean rooms, etc.).

**S4.36:** What tiles do you recommend for a social housing development with budget constraints?

**A:** Social housing: balance cost and durability by selecting PEI Class 3-4 for living areas, PEI Class 4 for wet areas, standard formats (600x600mm) for cost efficiency, and neutral colours for broad appeal. Our residential estate team provides value-engineered specifications.

**S4.37:** How do I specify tiles for a heritage building that requires SAHRA approval?

**A:** SAHRA specification: sympathetic materials matching original fabric, traditional patterns and formats, lime-based adhesive systems where required, documentation of matching analysis, and submission to SAHRA for approval before procurement. Our heritage team guides this process.

**S4.38:** Can you advise on tile specification for a crematorium garden of remembrance?


**S4.39:** What is the correct tile specification for a prison visitor centre?

**A:** Prison visitor centre: PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance, vandal-resistant, no loose components, institutional but welcoming aesthetic (distinguish from cell blocks), chemical resistance, and security-compliant (no materials that could be weaponised).

**S4.40:** How should I specify tiles for a national park lodge or wilderness camp?

**A:** Wilderness lodge: natural aesthetic (stone-look, terracotta), PEI Class 3-4 (moderate traffic), R10-R11 slip resistance, durable for outdoor-indoor transition, low maintenance (remote location), and sympathetic to natural surroundings. Consider LED lighting for off-grid power compatibility.


## Supplementary Section 5: Additional Topics: Innovative & Niche Project Types

**S5.1:** Can you provide a tile specification for an e-sports arena or gaming centre?

**A:** E-sports arena: PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance, anti-static properties, cable management integration, dark aesthetic (reduce screen glare), RGB lighting compatibility, and durable for 24/7 operation with heavy foot traffic during tournaments.

**S5.2:** What tiles are suitable for a Vertical Farming or hydroponics facility?

**A:** Vertical farming: R12 slip resistance (wet floors), chemical resistance to nutrient solutions, full coving for hygiene, antimicrobial properties, high humidity resistance, and compatibility with UV grow lights. Specify non-toxic materials for food safety.

**S5.3:** How do I specify tiles for a smart building with IoT sensor integration?

**A:** Smart building: specify tiles compatible with: underfloor sensor mounting, RFID tracking integration, smart heating zones, and luminous/glowing tiles for wayfinding. Coordinate with building automation consultant for sensor layout and tile integration.

**S5.4:** Can you advise on tiles for a biomass energy plant or waste-to-energy facility?

**A:** Waste-to-energy: PEI Class 5, R12 slip resistance, chemical resistance to process by-products, thermal shock resistance, heavy-duty coving, and industrial-grade specification. Full chemical resistance matrix required for the specific waste stream.

**S5.5:** What is the best tile specification for an observatory or planetarium?

**A:** Planetarium: PEI Class 3-4, R9 slip resistance, non-reflective dark surface (black or deep navy), acoustic properties, anti-static, and design that doesn't compete with the visual experience. Consider LED star-map integration in flooring.

**S5.6:** How should I specify tiles for a disaster management centre or emergency operations facility?

**A:** Emergency operations: PEI Class 5, R10 slip resistance, 24/7 durability, chemical resistance to disinfectants, redundant systems integration (no single point of failure), and design that supports high-stress operational environments.

**S5.7:** Can you provide tiles for an indoor skydiving or adventure sports facility?

**A:** Indoor skydiving: PEI Class 5, R11-R12 slip resistance, impact resistance, chemical resistance to rubber marking from suits, high-velocity air resistance near flight chamber, and durable for high-volume spectator and participant traffic.

**S5.8:** What tiles do you recommend for a robotics laboratory or automation facility?

**A:** Robotics lab: anti-static properties (ESD compliance), PEI Class 4-5, R9 slip resistance, smooth surface for robot navigation (LiDAR compatible), chemical resistance to lubricants, and joint minimisation where precision equipment operates.

**S5.9:** How do I specify tiles for a luxury safari tent camp with permanent structures?

**A:** Safari camp: natural aesthetic (stone-look, wood-look, terracotta), PEI Class 3-4, R10 slip resistance, compatible with timber deck substrates, low maintenance, and luxury finish appropriate to high-end tourism. Indoor-outdoor flow is important.

**S5.10:** Can you advise on tile specification for a satellite ground station or telecoms hub?

**A:** Telecoms hub: anti-static flooring (10⁶-10⁹ Ω), PEI Class 4-5, R9 slip resistance, point load capacity for equipment, and compatibility with raised access floor systems for cable management. Coordinate with equipment suppliers for earthing requirements.

**S5.11:** What is the correct tile specification for a brewing craft beer taproom?

**A:** Craft beer taproom: R10 slip resistance, chemical resistance to beer and cleaning agents, stain resistance, PEI Class 4, and aesthetic appropriate to craft/industrial style (concrete-look, brick-look, or reclaimed-wood aesthetic).

**S5.12:** How should I specify tiles for a dance studio with sprung floor requirements?

**A:** Dance studio: coordinate with sprung floor specialist — tiles are typically used for reception/retail areas adjacent to the sprung floor studio. Studio floor itself is usually sprung timber or vinyl. Specify compatible transition between tile and sprung floor.

**S5.13:** Can you provide a tile specification for a cryptocurrency mining facility or data farm?

**A:** Crypto mining: anti-static properties, PEI Class 4-5, R9 slip resistance, dust-resistant surface, chemical resistance to cleaning agents, and heat-resistant for areas near mining rigs. Coordinate with cooling system design.

**S5.14:** What tiles are suitable for a disaster relief temporary housing project?

**A:** Disaster relief: rapid-installation tiles (large format, minimal cutting), PEI Class 3-4, R10 slip resistance, cost-effective, readily available stock, and durable for family occupancy. Consider tile-on-tile renovation capability for future upgrades.

**S5.15:** How do I specify tiles for a floating building or houseboat?

**A:** Floating building: lightweight tiles (3-6mm slim porcelain), flexible adhesive (S1), movement joints at reduced centres (3-4m), waterproof installation throughout, and non-slip surfaces (R10-R11) to account for vessel movement. Marine certification may be required.

**S5.16:** Can you advise on tiles for a zero-waste retail store or sustainable concept shop?

**A:** Zero-waste retail: tiles with highest recycled content, EPD availability, local sourcing, natural aesthetic (terracotta, stone, wood-look), PEI Class 4, and tiles that tell a sustainability story aligning with the brand ethos.

**S5.17:** What is the best tile specification for an immersive art installation or museum exhibit?

**A:** Immersive art: PEI Class 3-4, R9 slip resistance, neutral background (don't compete with art), projection-friendly surface (matte, low glare), acoustic properties, and compatibility with temporary exhibit mounting systems.

**S5.18:** How should I specify tiles for a container architecture or modular building project?

**A:** Container architecture: lightweight tiles (3-6mm), flexible adhesive, movement joints at container module boundaries, thermal bridging consideration (metal container walls), and R10 slip resistance. Maximum tile weight may be limited by container floor loading.

**S5.19:** Can you provide a tile specification for a luxury yacht marina clubhouse?

**A:** Marina clubhouse: R11-R12 slip resistance (wet feet from boats), saltwater resistance, UV stability, nautical aesthetic (teak-look decking, blue accent tiles), and durable for high-end maritime leisure environment.

**S5.20:** What tiles do you recommend for a meditation garden or contemplation space?


**S5.21:** How do I specify tiles for a pop-up retail or temporary activation space?

**A:** Pop-up retail: PEI Class 4, R9 slip resistance, design-flexible (brand-neutral or easily themed), cost-effective for short-term use, and compatible with temporary installation methods (loose-lay or semi-permanent adhesive systems).

**S5.22:** Can you advise on tiles for a fusion restaurant with open kitchen?

**A:** Fusion restaurant: dining area — PEI 4, R9, design-forward aesthetic. Kitchen — PEI 5, R11-R12, chemical resistance, thermal shock resistance, full coving. Specify complementary tiles that flow between front and back of house.

**S5.23:** What is the correct tile specification for a co-working space or business centre?

**A:** Co-working: PEI Class 4-5, R9 slip resistance, acoustic performance (impact sound reduction), design that appeals to diverse users, easy maintenance, and durability for 12-16 hour daily operation with high turnover of users.

**S5.24:** How should I specify tiles for an escape room or immersive entertainment venue?

**A:** Escape room: PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance, dark colours (atmosphere), durable for physical interaction, chemical resistance to cleaning, and design that supports themed environments (industrial, medieval, futuristic, etc.).

**S5.25:** Can you provide a tile specification for a cryotherapy or wellness clinic?

**A:** Cryotherapy clinic: frost-resistant tiles, R10 slip resistance, chemical resistance to disinfectants, and design that conveys clinical cleanliness and wellness. Coordinate with extreme cold equipment specifications for floor loading and ventilation.

**S5.26:** What tiles are suitable for a padel or squash court facility?

**A:** Racquet sports: PEI Class 5 for circulation areas, R10 slip resistance, impact resistance, chemical resistance to cleaning, and acoustic management. Court surfaces themselves are typically specialised sports flooring — tiles for reception, changing rooms, and viewing areas.

**S5.27:** How do I specify tiles for an airport lounge or VIP terminal?


**S5.28:** Can you advise on tiles for a life sciences or biotechnology laboratory?

**A:** Biotech lab: chemical resistance to biological agents and disinfectants, PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance, antimicrobial properties, seamless coving, and compliance with biosafety level requirements (BSL-1 through BSL-3).

**S5.29:** What is the best tile specification for a car museum or classic car collection?

**A:** Car museum: PEI Class 4-5, R9 slip resistance, high stain resistance (oil/petrol), chemical resistance, light-coloured polished tiles (enhance vehicle display), and point load capacity for vehicle positioning. Consider display lighting integration.

**S5.30:** How should I specify tiles for a language school or international education centre?

**A:** Language school: PEI Class 4, R10 slip resistance, acoustic-friendly (reduce echo in language practice), durable for student traffic, easy maintenance, and internationally appealing neutral design.

**S5.31:** Can you provide a tile specification for a community kitchen or food bank facility?

**A:** Community kitchen: PEI Class 5, R11-R12 slip resistance, chemical resistance, full coving 150mm, epoxy-based grout, floor drainage, and institutional-grade durability for high-volume food preparation by volunteers.

**S5.32:** What tiles do you recommend for a botanical garden visitor centre or conservatory?

**A:** Botanical garden: R11 slip resistance (humidity/water), UV stability, natural aesthetic (stone, terracotta), frost-resistant if outdoor areas included, and design sympathetic to botanical surroundings.

**S5.33:** How do I specify tiles for a VR experience centre or digital entertainment venue?

**A:** VR centre: PEI Class 4-5, R10 slip resistance, anti-static properties, cable management integration, dark colours (minimise light interference with VR headsets), and durable for physical movement and equipment wear.




---

# Persona 7: Interior Designers — Q&A Knowledge Base

## Category 1: Showroom Experience & Inspiration

**Q1:** What is Spec Lab, and where is it located?
**A:** Spec Lab is Tiletoria's dedicated professional showroom for architects, interior designers, and property developers. It's located at Spec Lab Menlyn, and offers a curated environment where you can browse imported collections, work in private consultation spaces, and draw inspiration from styled room vignettes.

**Q2:** Can I just walk into Spec Lab, or do I need an appointment?
**A:** While walk-ins are welcome, we recommend booking an appointment so we can prepare a curated selection for your project and ensure a private consultation space is available. Your dedicated liaison will have everything ready for you.

**Q3:** What makes the Spec Lab different from a regular tile showroom?
**A:** Spec Lab is designed specifically for design professionals. You'll find styled vignettes rather than stacked pallets, knowledgeable consultants who speak design language, private consultation areas, an espresso bar, and collections curated from Italy, Spain, and Morocco that you won't find in mass retail environments.

**Q4:** Are there styled room vignettes I can use for client presentations?
**A:** Absolutely. Our vignettes are fully styled bathroom, kitchen, and living space installations that you can photograph for mood boards or bring clients to experience. They're refreshed regularly with new collection launches.

**Q5:** Can I bring my client to Spec Lab for a presentation?
**A:** Yes, and we encourage it. Book a private consultation room and your dedicated liaison will prepare samples, coordinate the vignette walk-through, and step back to let you lead the creative conversation with your client.

**Q6:** Is there a workspace where I can spend the day working on specifications?
**A:** Yes, Spec Lab offers comfortable workspaces with excellent coffee, Wi-Fi, and natural light. Many designers treat it as their satellite office — spending the morning browsing new arrivals and the afternoon compiling specifications.

**Q7:** How often do new collections arrive at Spec Lab?
**A:** We receive major new collections quarterly, with limited-edition drops and exclusive previews throughout the year. Our Gold and Platinum Design Circle members get 48-hour advance notice before any public display.

**Q8:** Can I take photos of the styled vignettes for my Instagram or portfolio?
**A:** Absolutely. We encourage photography throughout Spec Lab. Tag @Tiletoria and we'll often repost, giving your practice additional exposure to our engaged audience.

**Q9:** Does Spec Lab have a virtual tour option if I'm in Cape Town or Durban?
**A:** Yes, we offer a virtual tour capability for designers who can't visit Sandton in person. Your liaison can walk you through new collections via video call, focusing on the pieces relevant to your current project.

**Q10:** What are the showroom hours for Spec Lab?
**A:** Spec Lab is open Monday to Friday 8:30 AM to 5:00 PM, and Saturday 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM by appointment. Early morning and after-hours viewings can be arranged for Platinum Design Circle members.

**Q11:** Do you have a Spec Lab equivalent in Durban or Cape Town?
**A:** While our flagship Spec Lab is in Menlyn, our Durban (Glenwood) and Paarden Eiland showrooms maintain curated designer sections with many of the same imported collections. Your liaison can transfer samples between branches.

**Q12:** Can I request a specific collection to be pulled and ready before I arrive?
**A:** Of course. WhatsApp your liaison 24 hours ahead and we'll have your selected collections, samples, and technical data sheets ready in your consultation room. We want every visit to be efficient and inspiring.

**Q13:** Is the coffee really that good?
**A:** We've invested in a proper espresso bar because we know designers often spend hours with us. It's not an afterthought — it's part of making Spec Lab a place you actually want to work from.

**Q14:** Can I use the vignettes to test how different tiles photograph under showroom lighting?
**A:** Yes, and that's a smart approach. Our vignettes use a mix of natural and architectural lighting that approximates residential conditions. Bring your camera — test colour rendering, reflectivity, and texture before you specify.

**Q15:** Do you host designer-only browsing mornings?
**A:** Yes, our Designer Preview Evenings and morning previews are reserved exclusively for trade professionals. These are pre-launch events where you see new collections before they reach any public showroom floor.

**Q16:** What should I expect on my first visit to Spec Lab?
**A:** Expect a warm welcome from your dedicated liaison, a tour of the current vignettes, an introduction to our sample loan system, and a discussion about your upcoming projects. We'll set up your trade account if you haven't already and explain your Design Circle benefits.

**Q17:** Can I request a private after-hours viewing for a VIP client?
**A:** Platinum Design Circle members can arrange private after-hours viewings. We'll open the showroom exclusively for you and your client, with champagne service and a dedicated consultant on hand.

**Q18:** Are the vignettes updated to reflect current international trends?
**A:** Yes, our visual merchandising team refreshes vignettes quarterly, drawing directly from Cersaie trend reports and our factory visits to Italy and Spain. You'll always see the latest material directions represented.

**Q19:** Can I bring my design intern or assistant to browse with me?
**A:** Of course. We welcome your studio team. Many of our designer partners use Spec Lab as a training ground for junior staff — learning to read materiality, understand rectified edges, and appreciate tonal variation.

**Q20:** Is there parking available at the Spec Lab Menlyn?
**A:** Yes, secure parking is available at Spec Lab Menlyn. There's ample visitor parking, and the location is easily accessible from the M1 and Sandton CBD.

**Q21:** Can I work on my laptop while samples are being prepared?
**A:** Absolutely. Our workspaces are designed for this exact workflow — browse, select, then compile your specification while we pull and photograph samples for your loan batch.

**Q22:** How do I know what's new without visiting every week?
**A:** Your WhatsApp liaison sends weekly updates on new arrivals, and our "Just Landed" stories on Instagram give you a first look. Platinum members also receive a monthly curated edit of new pieces matched to their stated preferences.

**Q23:** Can I request a specific country's collections to be highlighted during my visit?
**A:** Yes. If you're looking specifically for Italian rectified porcelain or Moroccan-inspired patterns, let your liaison know ahead of time and we'll create a curated walk-through of those regions' offerings.

**Q24:** What's the atmosphere like during a typical weekday?
**A:** Quiet, focused, and professional. You'll likely encounter fellow designers working on specifications, occasionally comparing notes. It's a creative environment — not a high-pressure sales floor.

**Q25:** Do you display full-size tiles, or only small samples?
**A:** We display full-size tiles in our vignettes and feature walls, so you can appreciate the true scale of large-format rectified pieces. For smaller tiles, we have comprehensive sample boards.

**Q26:** Can I see how tiles look in both natural and artificial light?
**A:** Yes, Spec Lab is designed with multiple lighting zones. Move samples between the natural light atrium, warm residential lighting, and cool architectural lighting to understand how your specification will perform in different conditions.

**Q27:** Do you have books or trend publications I can browse?
**A:** Our lounge area maintains a curated library of design publications, Cersaie trend reports, and international tile catalogues. Pull up a chair and spend time with them.

**Q28:** Can I arrange for a courier to collect samples from Spec Lab on my behalf?
**A:** Yes, simply authorise your courier and we'll have the prepared sample batch ready for collection. Many Cape Town and Durban designers use this service regularly.

**Q29:** Is there a minimum spend or commitment to use Spec Lab?
**A:** Not at all. Spec Lab is free to use for all registered design professionals. We believe that if we create an environment that genuinely serves your creative process, the commercial relationship follows naturally.

**Q30:** Can I give feedback on collections I'd like to see stocked?
**A:** We actively encourage this. Our buying team sources based on designer feedback, and many of our most successful collections were brought in because a Platinum member requested them. Your input directly shapes our imports.

**Q31:** How does the showroom browsing experience compare to shopping at competitor retailers or competitor retailers?
**A:** The difference is intentionality. Mass retailers stack products for volume selling. Spec Lab curates for creative inspiration. You'll find no commodity clutter here — only collections chosen for their design integrity, with staff who understand your specification process.

**Q32:** Can I schedule recurring monthly visits to stay current?
**A:** Many designers do exactly this. Your liaison will set up a standing monthly appointment, always with something new prepared for your review. It's like having a personal shopper for tile.

**Q33:** Do you have outdoor or patio vignettes for exterior specifications?

**Q34:** Can I see the full range of a single collection in one place?
**A:** Yes, we merchandise by collection rather than by colour or size. You'll see the floor tile, wall tile, feature tile, mosaic, trim, and grout of each collection displayed together — making it easy to evaluate tonal harmony.

**Q35:** What's the process for booking a private consultation room?
**A:** WhatsApp or call your dedicated liaison with your preferred date, time, and a brief on your project. We'll confirm within two hours and have everything prepared for your arrival.

**Q36:** Do you have a "designer's favourites" or "most specified" section?
**A:** Informally, yes. Your liaison can walk you through our current top-specified collections — the pieces that are resonating with the broader design community. It's useful intelligence for trend-aware specifying.

**Q37:** Can I test grout colours against tiles in the vignettes?
**A:** Absolutely. We keep a full range of grout samples in the consultation rooms, and you can test different colours directly against the installed tiles to evaluate the aesthetic impact of your joint treatment.

**Q38:** Is there a children's area if I need to bring my kids occasionally?
**A:** We understand the reality of running a small practice. While we don't have a dedicated play area, the consultation rooms are spacious enough to accommodate children, and our team is welcoming.

**Q39:** Can I see the back and side of tiles to understand thickness and rectification quality?
**A:** Yes, our consultants will show you the technical attributes — body colour, rectified edge profile, thickness, and back marking. Understanding the manufacturing quality supports confident specifying.

**Q40:** Do you display installation details like expansion joints and trim profiles?
**A:** Yes, our technical detail wall shows trim profiles, expansion joint solutions, Schluter systems, and edge treatments. It's the practical complement to the aesthetic vignettes.

**Q41:** Can I arrange a video call walkthrough for my client who's overseas?
**A:** Absolutely. We'll set up a professional video call, walking your client through the relevant vignettes and samples with your liaison providing technical detail. Time zone flexibility included.

**Q42:** How do you handle multiple designers from the same studio visiting?
**A:** We treat your studio as a single Design Circle account, but each designer receives individual attention. We can coordinate group visits or individual appointments according to your workflow.

**Q43:** Can I see tiles in a "laid" state rather than just as loose pieces?
**A:** That's the whole point of our vignettes. Every tile you see is professionally installed with correct grout joints, trim, and lighting. You'll understand the true installed aesthetic — not an approximation.

**Q44:** Do you have a "quiet hour" for focused specification work without consultation?
**A:** Many designers prefer Tuesday and Thursday mornings for quiet work. Let your liaison know and we'll ensure minimal interruption — just excellent coffee and your samples.

**Q45:** Can I request samples from the vignettes themselves?
**A:** In most cases, yes. If a vignette features a collection you want to specify, we can loan you samples from our stock. Some limited-edition vignette pieces are display-only, but we'll always find a solution.

**Q46:** What's the dress code for Spec Lab events?
**A:** Come as you are. Our designer community ranges from polished Sandton corporate to creative Cape Town casual. The common thread is a love of materiality — not fashion conformity.

**Q47:** Can I host my own client event at Spec Lab?
**A:** Platinum Design Circle members can co-host client evenings in our event space. We'll collaborate on the catering, styling, and product selection. It's a powerful way to elevate your client experience.

**Q48:** Do you have 3D renders or installation visualisations available?
**A:** Yes, our team can provide 3D render support for complex installations, particularly for large-format rectified porcelain and intricate mosaic patterns. Ask your liaison for portfolio examples.

**Q49:** Can I see the full colour range of a collection, not just the featured colours?
**A:** Yes, your liaison will pull the complete colour range for any collection that interests you. We typically feature three hero colours in the vignette, but most collections offer six to twelve tonal options.

**Q50:** How do I give feedback on my showroom experience?
**A:** We welcome direct feedback to your liaison, to our showroom manager, or anonymously via our designer survey. Your input shapes how we evolve Spec Lab. We're building this space with you, not just for you.

**Q51:** Can I use the Spec Lab address as a sample collection point for my contractors?
**A:** Yes, many designers have their contractors collect bulk orders from our Menlyn warehouse while they personally select samples at Spec Lab. It's a clean separation of procurement and creative selection.

**Q52:** Do you have shower installations I can test for water flow and drainage aesthetics?
**A:** Our bathroom vignettes include functional wet areas where you can evaluate drain integration, slope aesthetics, and how water behaves on different surface textures. It's far beyond what a sample board can show.

**Q53:** Can I see how different tile formats work together in one space?
**A:** Yes, several vignettes deliberately mix formats — large-format floor with mosaic feature wall, subway tile with hexagon floor. You'll see how rectified edges, joint alignments, and tonal variation create cohesive spatial narratives.

**Q54:** Is there a minimum notice period for booking the consultation room?
**A:** We can often accommodate same-day bookings, but 24 hours' notice ensures we have your preferred collections pulled and ready. For client presentations, we recommend booking 48 hours ahead.

**Q55:** Can I leave samples at Spec Lab between visits rather than taking them back to my studio?
**A:** Yes, we offer sample storage for active projects. Your liaison will keep your project samples labelled and secure, ready for your next visit or for client presentation. It's one less thing to manage.

**Q56:** Do you have a loyalty card or visit tracker?
**A:** Your Design Circle membership tracks your annual spend automatically, which determines your tier benefits. No physical card needed — your practice name is your passport to every benefit.

**Q57:** Can I request a specific music playlist during my consultation?
**A:** We're happy to accommodate atmosphere preferences. Several of our designers have standing requests for their "specification soundtrack." Small details matter to the creative process.

**Q58:** Do you offer weekend appointments for designers who are in meetings all week?
**A:** Saturday mornings are available by appointment, and we can arrange Sunday viewings for Platinum members with urgent project timelines. Your creative schedule doesn't always fit banking hours.

## Category 2: Trade Account & Pricing

**Q59:** What discount do interior designers receive on Tiletoria products?
**A:** Registered interior designers receive a 15-25% trade discount depending on annual volume and Design Circle tier. Silver members start at 15%, Gold at 20%, and Platinum at 25% on most collections.

**Q60:** How do I set up a trade account with Tiletoria?
**A:** Visit Spec Lab or any Tiletoria branch with your CIFID practice registration, business certificate, and proof of address. We'll complete the application in 30 minutes and activate your account within 24 hours.

**Q61:** Is there a minimum annual spend to maintain my trade account?
**A:** There's no minimum to maintain your account, but your Design Circle tier — which determines your discount level and benefits — is reviewed annually based on spend. Silver requires no minimum, Gold R150,000+, and Platinum R400,000+.

**Q62:** Can my trade discount be applied to contractor-mediated purchases?
**A:** Yes. If you specify Tiletoria products and your contractor procures them, we can structure the transaction so your trade discount is protected. Your liaison will coordinate the paper trail confidentially.

**Q63:** How is my markup protected when the contractor buys directly?
**A:** We understand that your creative fee and product markup are part of your practice revenue. Your liaison manages procurement so the contractor receives trade-appropriate pricing while your margin remains confidential and protected.

**Q64:** What payment terms are available for design practices?
**A:** Standard terms are payment on order for new accounts. After six months of trading history, qualifying practices can apply for 30-day account terms. Platinum members may negotiate 60-day terms on confirmed projects.

**Q65:** Can I pay for samples, or are they free?
**A:** Full-size sample loans are free for registered designers — no deposit, two-week loan period. If you wish to keep samples permanently for your library, they're available at trade cost plus VAT.

**Q66:** Does the trade discount apply to sanware and accessories too?
**A:** Yes, your trade discount applies across the full Tiletoria range including our premium tapware range, our tapware range, our tapware range, and our tapware range sanware, as well as grout, trim, and accessories. It's a complete bathroom and tiling specification under one account.

**Q67:** Can I get a pro forma invoice for client budget approval before ordering?
**A:** Absolutely. Your liaison will prepare a detailed pro forma with full product descriptions, quantities, trade pricing, and estimated delivery. You can present this directly to your client or fold it into your own fee proposal.

**Q68:** Is pricing negotiable for very large hospitality or commercial projects?
**A:** For projects exceeding R500,000 in tile value, our commercial team can negotiate project-specific pricing. This is separate from your standard Design Circle tier and reflects genuine volume commitment.

**Q69:** Can I structure pricing so my client sees retail rates while I receive trade rates?
**A:** Yes, this is standard practice. We can provide retail-list documentation for client presentations while your trade account processes at the discounted rate. Your liaison manages this discreetly.

**Q70:** What happens to my discount if I don't meet the annual tier threshold?
**A:** Your tier is reviewed annually. If you don't meet the threshold, you'll move to the appropriate tier for your actual spend. We don't penalise — we adjust. Many designers fluctuate between Gold and Silver based on project cycles.

**Q71:** Can I have multiple trade accounts if I have separate residential and commercial practices?
**A:** We recommend a single account per legal entity for clean administration. Your Design Circle tier aggregates all spend, which typically works in your favour. Your liaison can tag projects by type for internal reporting.

**Q72:** Do you offer project-specific credit facilities?
**A:** For confirmed commercial projects with signed contracts and payment guarantees, we can establish project-specific credit facilities. This is arranged through our commercial division, not your standard trade account.

**Q73:** Can my bookkeeper or PA manage the account administration?
**A:** Yes, you can nominate authorised contacts on your account. They can place orders, query deliveries, and manage payments, but pricing and discount tiers remain confidential to you as the principal.

**Q74:** How quickly can I get a quote for a full project specification?
**A:** For projects with completed schedules of quantities, we return detailed quotes within 24 hours. For complex hospitality specifications requiring value engineering, allow 48-72 hours for our commercial team.

**Q75:** Is there a difference in discount between Italian imports and local ranges?
**A:** Your Design Circle discount applies uniformly across our range. However, Italian and Spanish imports have higher base prices, so the absolute Rand value of your discount is greater on imported collections.

**Q76:** Can I get early access to sale pricing before it's public?
**A:** Gold and Platinum Design Circle members receive 72-hour advance notice of any promotional pricing. You can secure sale-priced stock for pending projects before the public announcement.

**Q77:** What documentation do I receive for my tax records?
**A:** All invoices are VAT-compliant with full product descriptions. We can provide monthly statements, project-specific summaries, and annual spend reports for your accountant. Everything is SARS-compliant.

**Q78:** Can I specify products from my practice in Johannesburg for a Cape Town project?
**A:** Yes, your trade account is valid nationally. We can arrange delivery to any site in South Africa, and your Cape Town-based contractor can collect from our local branch. Your discount and terms travel with you.

**Q79:** Do you offer volume rebates on top of the trade discount?
**A:** Platinum members who exceed R600,000 annually may qualify for a retrospective volume rebate. This is discussed at your annual review and reflects genuine partnership value.

**Q80:** Can I pay a deposit to reserve stock for a project with delayed commencement?
**A:** Yes, we accept 30% deposits to hold stock for up to 90 days. This is particularly valuable for imported collections where replenishment lead times are longer. Your liaison will issue a reservation certificate.

**Q81:** How do I update my practice details or add a new designer to the account?
**A:** WhatsApp your liaison with the changes. We can add new team members, update addresses, or change authorised contacts within 24 hours. Keeping your account current ensures uninterrupted service.

**Q82:** Is my trade pricing confidential from other designers?
**A:** Absolutely. Your pricing, volume, and tier are confidential. We never share one designer's commercial arrangements with another. Discretion is fundamental to our trade relationships.

**Q83:** Can I get a price list or catalogue with trade pricing?
**A:** We provide trade-priced catalogues for your studio reference, updated quarterly. These are watermarked for your practice only and should not be shared with clients or contractors.

**Q84:** What happens if a product goes on sale after I've placed my order?
**A:** If a price reduction occurs within 7 days of your order, we'll automatically apply the lower price. Beyond 7 days, sale pricing applies to new orders only. We want you to feel confident about timing.

**Q85:** Do you offer settlement discounts for early payment?
**A:** Yes, accounts that settle within 7 days receive an additional 2.5% settlement discount. This is cumulative with your Design Circle discount, making it attractive for practices with healthy cash flow.

**Q86:** Can I use my trade account for my own home renovation?
**A:** Yes, many designers use their trade account for personal projects. The discount applies, and it's a wonderful opportunity to live with the products you specify for clients. We only ask that you don't on-sell at trade rates.

**Q87:** How do I query a discrepancy on my invoice?
**A:** Contact your liaison within 7 days of invoicing. We'll investigate promptly — whether it's a quantity error, pricing mismatch, or delivery charge query. Resolution typically within 48 hours.

**Q88:** Can I get a project completion statement showing everything purchased for one job?
**A:** Yes, your liaison can pull a complete project statement showing all products, quantities, and costs for any single project. It's invaluable for final accounts and warranty documentation.

**Q89:** Do you offer rental or lease arrangements for display vignettes?
**A:** For commercial developers and hotel operators, we can discuss lease-style arrangements for large-format displays. This is handled case-by-case through our commercial division.

**Q90:** Can I get export pricing if I'm specifying for a project outside South Africa?
**A:** We supply several neighbouring countries and can arrange export documentation and VAT-exclusive pricing for qualifying projects. Our commercial team handles the logistics.

**Q91:** What currencies can I pay in?
**A:** All accounts are in ZAR. For large import orders, we can discuss forward exchange arrangements to lock in pricing, but settlement is always in Rand.

**Q92:** Can my trade account be linked to my contractor's account for seamless procurement?
**A:** We can establish linked accounts with agreed protocols. Your contractor orders against your specification, your discount applies automatically, and you receive copy invoices. Your liaison sets this up.

**Q93:** Is there a surcharge for small orders below a certain value?
**A:** We don't penalise small orders, but delivery charges apply below a threshold. For sample-only orders, collection from Spec Lab is always free. Your liaison will advise the most economical approach.

**Q94:** Can I get a "project pack" with all pricing, lead times, and alternatives for one job?
**A:** Yes, this is one of our most valued services. Your liaison compiles a comprehensive project pack with primary specifications, budget alternatives, lead times, and contingency options. It's your specification bible.

**Q95:** How do I escalate if I have a pricing concern that my liaison can't resolve?
**A:** Our showroom manager and commercial director are always accessible. Ask your liaison to escalate, or contact our head office directly. We take pricing integrity seriously.
