**Q96:** Can I earn Design Circle tier benefits retroactively for the current year?
**A:** If a large project pushes you over a tier threshold mid-year, we'll upgrade your benefits immediately. You don't need to wait for the annual review. We want you enjoying Platinum benefits the moment you qualify.

**Q97:** Do you offer special terms for non-profit or community projects I'm donating design services to?
**A:** We respect designers who give back. For registered non-profit projects, we can extend additional discounting on a case-by-case basis. Speak to your liaison about the project details.

**Q98:** Can I get a pre-approved credit limit for competitive tendering?
**A:** Yes, established Platinum practices can request a pre-approved credit facility letter, useful when tendering for commercial work. It demonstrates your supply chain credibility to developers.

**Q99:** Can I get a consolidated monthly invoice rather than per-order invoices?
**A:** Yes, we can arrange consolidated monthly invoicing for established accounts. All orders within the month are compiled into a single statement with full product detail, simplifying your bookkeeping.

**Q100:** Do you offer retainer arrangements for practices with consistent monthly spend?
**A:** For practices with predictable monthly volumes, we can discuss retainer-style arrangements that lock in pricing, guarantee stock allocation, and streamline administration. Contact our commercial director to explore.

## Category 3: Sample Services

**Q101:** What sample services do you offer for interior designers?
**A:** We offer full-size sample loans with no deposit required, a two-week loan period, sample board preparation, high-res cut-out photography, and project-specific sample curation. It's designed for client presentation, not just product evaluation.

**Q102:** How big are the samples? Can I get full-size pieces?
**A:** Yes, our standard is full-size samples — not those postage-stamp chips you find at hardware stores. For large-format rectified porcelain, that means 600x600mm, 600x1200mm, or even 1200x1200mm pieces. You can appreciate the true scale and presence.

**Q103:** Is there really no deposit for sample loans?
**A:** Correct. For registered designers with active trade accounts, we require no deposit for standard sample loans. We trust our professional community. The two-week loan period keeps the system flowing for everyone.

**Q104:** What happens if I need samples for longer than two weeks?
**A:** Simply WhatsApp your liaison for an extension. We understand that client decision cycles can stretch, especially on large projects. Extensions are routinely granted for active projects — just keep us informed.

**Q105:** Can I borrow samples for a photoshoot or magazine feature?
**A:** Yes, and we love supporting editorial content. Let your liaison know it's for a photoshoot and we'll extend the loan period, provide back-up pieces, and ensure the samples are in pristine condition.

**Q106:** Do you prepare sample boards for client presentations?
**A:** We can prepare sample boards with your selected tiles mounted on board with product labels and collection references. They're professional enough for high-end client presentations. Allow 48 hours for preparation.

**Q107:** Can I get grout colour samples to pair with my tile selections?
**A:** Absolutely. We include grout colour swatches with every sample loan, and you can take home multiple grout options to test against your tiles in the actual lighting conditions of your project space.

**Q108:** How many samples can I borrow at one time?
**A:** There's no hard limit, but we ask for consideration of other designers. For a typical bathroom project, 8-12 samples is standard. For a full hotel specification, 30-40 samples is manageable. Your liaison will advise if quantities are unusually large.

**Q109:** Can you courier samples to me in Cape Town or Durban?
**A:** Yes, we courier samples nationally. Full-size rectified porcelain is carefully crated, and mosaic samples are delivered in protective cases. Courier costs are absorbed for Gold and Platinum members; Silver members pay actual cost.

**Q110:** Do samples come with technical data sheets?
**A:** Every sample loan includes the relevant technical data sheets — PEI ratings, slip coefficients, water absorption, rectified edge specifications, and maintenance guidelines. Your specification is incomplete without them.

**Q111:** Can I get trim and accessory samples too?
**A:** Yes, pencil trims, skirting pieces, mosaic sheets, drain covers, and transition profiles are all available as samples. It's the complete system — not just field tiles.

**Q112:** What if a sample gets damaged while it's in my care?
**A:** Accidents happen. Inform your liaison and we'll assess whether the sample can be repaired or needs replacement. We don't charge for minor wear on well-used samples, but significant damage may incur a replacement cost.

**Q113:** Can I keep a permanent sample library at my studio?
**A:** Many designers build reference libraries over time. Samples kept beyond the loan period are charged at trade cost. Your liaison can help you curate a core library representing your most-specified collections.

**A:** Yes, stone samples are available though in smaller pieces due to material value and weight. We also provide sealed and unsealed samples so you can show clients the difference in maintenance requirements.

**Q115:** Can I get samples of tiles that aren't currently in stock?
**A:** For imported collections, we can request samples from our international suppliers. Lead time is typically 3-4 weeks. There's a small courier charge for international sample requests, which is credited against your first order.

**Q116:** Do you have a digital sample library for remote selection?
**A:** Yes, our high-res product photography is available for download through your liaison. While nothing replaces seeing and touching material, our digital assets are accurate for initial shortlisting and mood boarding.

**Q117:** Can I return samples by courier, or do I need to bring them back personally?
**A:** Either works. Drop them at any Tiletoria branch, or we'll arrange courier collection from your studio. Platinum members enjoy complimentary sample collection; others pay standard courier rates.

**Q118:** Do you have a "sample amnesty" if I find old samples I forgot to return?
**A:** We run periodic sample amnesties where you can return forgotten samples without question or charge. Watch for the announcement on our designer WhatsApp group. No judgment — we know how busy studios get.

**Q119:** Can I get samples in multiple colourways of the same collection?
**A:** Yes, and we encourage it. Borrowing three or four colourways of the same Italian rectified porcelain helps you evaluate tonal options in situ. Your client will appreciate seeing the range, not just one option.

**Q120:** Are samples labelled with collection names, country of origin, and sizes?
**A:** Every sample carries a professional label with collection name, manufacturer, country of origin, available sizes, and your liaison's contact details. It's the detail that elevates your presentation.

**Q121:** Can I get wet samples to test slip resistance?
**A:** For projects where slip rating is critical — pool surrounds, hospitality bathrooms — we can provide tiles that have been pre-conditioned for slip testing, along with the relevant SANS test documentation.

**Q122:** Do you offer "sample sets" curated by style — e.g., "coastal bathroom" or "urban loft"?
**A:** Yes, your liaison can prepare themed sample sets based on your project brief. Tell us the aesthetic direction and we'll curate a selection of tiles, mosaics, trims, and grout colours that speak to that narrative.

**Q123:** Can I get samples of discontinued or clearance stock for value-engineering?
**A:** Yes, our clearance section is a treasure trove for value-engineering. Samples of discontinued lines are available at reduced cost, and once they're gone, they're gone. Your liaison maintains a current list.

**Q124:** How do I request a specific sample? WhatsApp, email, or in person?
**A:** WhatsApp is fastest — send a photo of the tile from our catalogue or Instagram, and your liaison will confirm availability and dispatch within 24 hours. For complex curation requests, a brief call works best.

**Q125:** Can I get samples delivered directly to my client's home?
**A:** We prefer to deliver to your studio so you control the presentation. However, for remote projects, we can courier directly to site with your authorisation. Your liaison coordinates the delivery window.

**Q126:** Do samples come cleaned and sealed, or in their raw state?

**Q127:** Can I get matching paint colour recommendations with my tile samples?
**A:** While we don't manufacture paint, your liaison can recommend paint colours from major South African brands that harmonise with your selected tiles. We've built a cross-reference database over years of designer collaboration.

**Q128:** What if the batch I receive looks different from the sample?
**A:** This is exactly why we maintain rigorous batch control. If you notice variation beyond acceptable manufacturing tolerances, contact your liaison immediately. We'll pull replacement stock from the same batch as your approved sample or exchange the full order.

**Q129:** Can I borrow samples for a competition submission or tender?
**A:** Absolutely. Many designers borrow samples for competition boards and tender presentations. Extended loan periods are available for this purpose. We want your submission to look as compelling as possible.

**Q130:** Do you have a minimum order quantity once I've approved a sample?
**A:** Minimum orders vary by collection. Local ranges typically have no minimum. Imported collections may require a minimum box quantity — usually one to two boxes. Your liaison will confirm at sample stage.

**Q131:** Can I get samples of installation accessories — adhesives, grouts, sealers?
**A:** Yes, we provide sample sachets of adhesives, grout colour samples, and sealer testers. For complex installations, we can arrange a full system sample showing tile, adhesive, grout, and sealer working together.

**Q132:** How do I know if a sample is from the current batch or old stock?
**A:** Every sample is marked with its batch number and date received. When you place your order, we match to that exact batch or advise if a new batch has arrived with any perceptible variation.

**Q133:** Can I get large-format samples cut to a specific size for my sample board?
**A:** For sample boards requiring uniform sizing, we can cut large-format samples to your specified dimensions. There's a nominal cutting charge, but the result is a professional presentation board that wins work.

**Q134:** Do you offer sample loans for your sanware ranges too?
**A:** Sanware samples include finish chips — brushed brass, matte black, chrome — that show the true metal finish. For larger sanware pieces, we can arrange showroom viewings or manufacturer catalogue loans.

**Q135:** Can I get "sample packs" for my design interns to learn from?
**A:** We love supporting design education. Sample packs curated for educational purposes are available at nominal cost — showing different materials, formats, edge types, and finishes. It's a practical learning resource.

**Q136:** What if I need a sample urgently — same day?
**A:** For Menlyn-based designers, same-day courier is available for samples in stock at Spec Lab. Order before 10 AM for afternoon delivery. There is an express courier charge for this service.

**Q137:** Can you hold samples at Spec Lab for me to collect at my convenience?
**A:** Yes, your liaison will keep prepared samples at reception for up to 5 days. Just let us know when you're collecting and they'll be ready and waiting. No appointment needed for collection.

**Q138:** Do you offer sample loans for outdoor and patio tiles?

**Q139:** Can I get samples with different surface finishes — natural, honed, polished — of the same stone?

**Q140:** Do samples include care and maintenance instructions?
**A:** Every sample loan includes a maintenance guide specific to that material. It's designed for you to pass to your client at handover — professional, comprehensive, and branded with your practice details if you wish.

**Q141:** Can I get "dark room" samples for areas with no natural light?
**A:** Your liaison can advise on which collections perform best in artificial-light-only environments. We can provide samples of high-LRV (light reflectance value) tiles that maintain their presence even in basement bathrooms and internal corridors.

**Q142:** Do you have a sample wishlist or notification system?
**A:** If a collection you're interested in is out of stock for sampling, your liaison will add it to your wishlist and notify you the moment samples arrive. You won't miss out on limited-edition drops.

**Q143:** Can I get samples of tiles from previous seasons or archive collections?
**A:** Our archive holds samples from discontinued collections for up to two years. If you're matching or extending an existing installation, your liaison can search the archive for the exact original specification.

**Q144:** How do I clean samples before returning them?
**A:** Wipe with a damp cloth and mild detergent. For textured or porous samples, a soft brush removes most marks. We don't expect showroom condition — reasonable care is all we ask.

**Q145:** Can I get a "designer's emergency kit" — essential samples and tools for my car?
**A:** Your liaison can assemble a compact designer's kit — essential grout colour swatches, trim profile samples, a tape measure, tile spacers, and cleaning product samples — in a branded case that lives in your car. You'll never be caught without reference materials on a site visit.

## Category 4: Unique & Exclusive Collections

**Q146:** What makes Tiletoria's Italian collections different from what I can find elsewhere in South Africa?
**A:** Our Italian rectified porcelain features through-body veining that continues through the entire tile body, not just the surface glaze. This means cut edges and mitred corners show the same veining as the face — essential for high-end bathrooms and feature walls. You won't find this level of quality at mass retailers.

**Q147:** What's the difference between rectified and pressed-edge porcelain?
**A:** Rectified tiles are mechanically trimmed after firing, creating perfectly square edges that allow for 2mm grout joints. Pressed edges have a slight cushion from the mould, requiring 3-5mm joints. For seamless, contemporary aesthetics, rectified is the designer's choice.

**Q148:** Do you have tiles that I genuinely can't find at competitor retailers or competitor retailers?
**A:** Yes. Our exclusive imports from small-batch Italian manufacturers, limited-edition Spanish encaustic-look collections, and Moroccan artisanal mosaics are sourced specifically for the design trade. Many collections have exclusivity agreements for the South African market.

**Q149:** What are through-body veining tiles, and why do designers specify them?
**A:** Through-body veining means theor stone pattern runs through the entire tile thickness, not just printed on the surface. When you cut or chip a through-body tile, the veining continues. It's the difference between a convincing imitation and a disappointing reveal.

**Q150:** Can I get samples of your limited-edition collections before they sell out?
**A:** Platinum and Gold Design Circle members receive 48-hour advance notice of limited-edition drops, with sample priority. Many limited collections sell out within two weeks of public launch — early access is a genuine advantage.

**Q151:** What Spanish collections do you carry?
**A:** Our Spanish ranges include bold encaustic-look patterned tiles, terrazzo-inspired porcelain, and warm Mediterranean colour palettes. Spanish manufacturers excel at glaze depth and pattern complexity — perfect for statement floors and feature walls.

**Q152:** Do you have Moroccan-inspired or zellige-style tiles?
**A:** Yes, our Moroccan-inspired collection captures the irregular, hand-cut character of traditional zellige with the consistency of modern manufacturing. The glaze variation, undulating surface, and rich jewel tones create extraordinary feature walls and kitchen backsplashes.

**Q153:** What artisanal mosaic options do you offer?

**Q154:** Can I order custom mosaic blends for a specific project?
**A:** For hospitality and large residential projects, we can commission custom mosaic blends from our international suppliers. Minimum quantities apply — typically 50 square metres — but the result is genuinely bespoke. Lead time is 8-10 weeks.

**Q155:** What does "hand-finished" actually mean in the context of your tiles?
**A:** Hand-finished tiles receive manual glaze application, edge distressing, or surface texturing after machine forming. The slight irregularities — finger marks, drip lines, thickness variation — are intentional and valued. No two tiles are identical, giving installations a living, breathing quality.

**Q156:** Do you have exclusive colourways that are only available through Tiletoria?
**A:** Yes, several of our Italian and Spanish manufacturers produce exclusive colourways for our market. These are developed in collaboration with our buying team and aren't available through any other South African distributor.

**Q157:** What's the story behind your most exclusive collection?
**A:** Our Italian-look rectified porcelain is manufactured by a family-owned factory in Modena using digital scanning of actual-look porcelainslabs. The veining algorithm randomises across the production run, so no two tiles repeat in a typical installation. It's technology in service of authenticity.

**Q158:** Can I get large-format tiles — 1200x1200mm or 1200x2400mm?
**A:** Yes, our large-format Italian porcelain goes up to 1200x2400mm in selected collections. These slabs are specified for seamless feature walls, luxury bathroom floors, and high-end commercial spaces where grout lines are minimised.

**Q159:** Do you carry tiles with metallic finishes or iridescent glazes?
**A:** Yes, our specialty collections include tiles with metallic oxide glazes, pearlescent finishes, and iridescent surfaces that shift colour with viewing angle. They're extraordinary for feature walls, bar fronts, and hospitality spaces that need to photograph dramatically.

**Q160:** What encaustic cement tile options do you have?
**A:** We carry both genuine cement encaustic tiles and porcelain encaustic-look alternatives. The cement versions are hand-poured and require sealing but offer authentic patina. The porcelain versions are maintenance-free with the same bold patterning. Your liaison can advise on the right choice for your project.

**Q161:** Can I see the factory where these exclusive tiles are made?
**A:** Platinum Design Circle members are invited on our annual factory trips to Italy, Spain, and occasionally Morocco. You'll see the manufacturing process, meet the artisans, and often preview collections before they're launched anywhere in the world.

**Q162:** Do you have tiles that work for both Art Deco and contemporary interiors?
**A:** Our geometric pattern collections — particularly the Spanish encaustic-look ranges — bridge Art Deco and contemporary aesthetics beautifully. The bold geometry reads as period-appropriate in heritage buildings and strikingly modern in new architecture.

**Q163:** What makes a tile "designer-grade" versus standard retail?
**A:** Designer-grade tiles have rectified edges for minimal joints, through-body colouring for cut-edge integrity, digital glaze variation for natural appearance, calibrated thickness for level installation, and consistent tone across the full batch. Mass retail often compromises on these details.

**Q164:** Do you have tiles with a "soft-touch" or velvety finish?
**A:** Yes, several of our Italian collections feature a soft-touch matte glaze that feels almost velvety underfoot. It's extraordinary for bathroom floors and bedrooms where tactile luxury matters as much as visual appeal.

**Q165:** Can I get tiles that look like poured concrete or micro-cement?
**A:** Our concrete-look porcelain collections are among our most specified. They capture the tonal variation, hairline cracks, and patina of real micro-cement without the installation risk and maintenance burden. Available in grey, warm beige, and anthracite.

**Q166:** Do you carry any tiles made from recycled or sustainable materials?
**A:** Several of our European manufacturers use recycled content in their porcelain body and have environmental certifications. Your liaison can identify sustainable options for projects where green credentials are required.

**Q167:** What's the most unusual tile you stock?
**A:** Our lava stone collection — volcanic rock from Mount Etna glazed in jewel tones — is genuinely unique in South Africa. Each piece is a slice of geological history with a glass-like glaze surface. It's extraordinary for kitchen islands and statement hearths.

**Q168:** Can I get tiles that look like wood but perform like porcelain?
**A:** Our wood-look porcelain planks are extraordinarily convincing, with embossed grain texture, saw-mark detailing, and natural colour variation. Available in long plank formats up to 200x1200mm, they're the practical choice for bathrooms, kitchens, and high-traffic areas where real wood fails.

**Q169:** Do you have tiles suitable for heritage or period restoration projects?
**A:** Yes, our encaustic-look, subway tile, and decorative border collections are widely used in heritage restoration. We can match period patterns and advise on sympathetic specification for SANS-graded heritage buildings.

**Q170:** What's the price range for your exclusive Italian collections?
**A:** Italian rectified porcelain typically ranges from R850 to R2,500 per square metre at trade pricing, depending on format, finish, and exclusivity. Large-format slabs and limited editions sit at the premium end. Your liaison provides exact pricing per collection.

**Q171:** Can I get tiles with 3D relief or sculptural surfaces?
**A:** Yes, our architectural tile collections feature three-dimensional relief patterns — waves, chevrons, geometric facets — that create extraordinary shadow play. They're stunning for feature walls in lobbies, bedrooms, and dining rooms.

**Q172:** Do you have tiles that work specifically for African contemporary interiors?
**A:** Our Moroccan-inspired, terrazzo-look, and warm earth-tone collections resonate beautifully with African contemporary design. The materiality and colour palettes complement local craft traditions while meeting international design standards.

**Q173:** Can I request a "designer's edit" — your buyer's top 10 exclusive pieces?
**A:** Your liaison prepares a quarterly "Buyer's Edit" highlighting the most exciting new arrivals and timeless exclusives. It's a curated shortlist for designers who want to stay ahead of specification trends.

**Q174:** What's the lead time for exclusive imported collections?
**A:** In-stock exclusives deliver within 5-7 days. For replenishment or special order, Italian collections typically take 8-10 weeks and Spanish collections 6-8 weeks. Your liaison tracks container arrivals and will reserve stock for confirmed projects.

**Q175:** Do you have tiles with a "raw" or unglazed finish?

**Q176:** Can I specify tiles that are truly one-of-a-kind?

**Q177:** Do you have any collections designed by named designers or design houses?
**A:** Yes, several of our Italian collections are designed by named ceramic artists and design studios. These collaborations bring fashion-house aesthetics to tile. Your liaison can detail current designer collaborations.

**Q178:** What's the difference between your Italian and Spanish collections in terms of aesthetic?
**A:** Italian collections tend toward refined minimalism —looks, concrete looks, and sophisticated neutrals with flawless execution. Spanish collections are bolder — patterned encaustics, warm terracottas, and expressive glazes. Both are exceptional; the choice depends on your design narrative.

**Q179:** Can I get tiles with integrated LED or luminous effects?
**A:** While we don't stock integrated LED tiles, we carry collections with high-gloss and metallic finishes that amplify ambient lighting beautifully. Combined with thoughtful lighting design, these create luminous effects without embedded electronics.

**Q180:** Do you stock tiles specifically for fireplace surrounds and hearths?

**Q181:** Can I get border tiles, listellos, and decorative accents?
**A:** Many of our collections include coordinating border tiles, listellos, and decorative inserts. These finishing pieces are what elevate a competent installation to a considered design. Your liaison will identify the coordinating accents for your chosen collection.

**Q182:** Do you have any tiles made using traditional craftsmanship techniques?
**A:** Our Moroccan-inspired zellige collection uses traditional glazing techniques that create deliberate variation in colour and thickness. Our lava stone collection involves hand-glazing volcanic rock. These artisanal techniques produce tiles with authentic character that machine manufacturing cannot replicate.

**Q183:** What's the most expensive tile you stock, and what makes it special?

**Q184:** Can I request a custom colour from one of your exclusive manufacturers?
**A:** For large commercial or hospitality projects, our Italian and Spanish manufacturers will consider custom colour development at significant minimum quantities — typically 500+ square metres. Your liaison manages the custom development process.

**Q185:** Do you have tiles that reference South African materials — Karoo stone, Drakensberg?

**Q186:** What's the story behind your Moroccan collection?
**A:** Our Moroccan-inspired tiles are developed with manufacturers who study traditional zellige techniques — the glaze recipes, the cutting methods, and the intentional imperfection that defines the aesthetic. The result captures Moroccan craft spirit with the consistency required for modern installation.

**Q187:** Can I get tiles with gold, silver, or platinum metallic accents?
**A:** Yes, several of our decorative collections incorporate genuine metallic oxide glazes — gold leaf accents, silver shimmer, and copper tones. These are statement pieces for feature walls, bar fronts, and powder rooms where a touch of opulence is appropriate.

**Q188:** Do you have tiles that look like fabric or textile?
**A:** Our textile-look porcelain collections interpret linen, tweed, and woven textures in ceramic. They're extraordinary for bedroom walls, hotel lobbies, and any space where textile warmth is desired but practical durability is required. The tactile quality is remarkably convincing.

**Q189:** What's the most unusual format you stock?
**A:** Beyond standard squares and rectangles, we stock: hexagons (multiple sizes), fish-scale and fan shapes, chevron parallelograms, arabesque and lantern shapes, penny rounds, and custom mosaic patterns. Unusual formats are where design personality emerges.

**Q190:** Can I get tiles with embedded LED lighting?
**A:** We don't stock tiles with embedded electronics, but we can recommend lighting designers who create integrated tile-and-light installations. The combination of our reflective or translucent tiles with professional lighting design creates extraordinary luminous effects.

**Q191:** Do you have any collections that are "limited edition" — genuinely scarce?
**A:** Yes, our limited-edition drops — announced exclusively to Platinum and Gold members — are produced in finite quantities and not repeated. Once sold, they're gone. These are for clients who value genuine rarity and for designers who want to specify something nobody else can get.

**Q192:** What's the difference between a "designer collection" and a standard range?
**A:** Designer collections are either created in collaboration with named designers or curated specifically for the design trade with higher aesthetic ambition. They often feature more sophisticated glaze technology, braver colour palettes, and formats that push manufacturing boundaries.

**Q193:** Can I see the production process for your artisanal tiles?
**A:** For Platinum members on factory trips, we visit artisanal workshops where you can observe hand-glazing, manual pressing, and traditional firing. It's an extraordinary experience that deepens your appreciation for craft and informs how you specify these materials.

**Q194:** Do you have tiles that change appearance with different lighting?
**A:** Our iridescent and pearlescent collections shift in appearance as viewing angle and light source change. They're subtle — not disco-ball dramatic — but the variation adds life and movement to feature walls. Natural daylight, warm artificial light, and candlelight each reveal different qualities.

**Q195:** What's the most geographically remote place you've sourced tiles from?
**A:** Our lava stone from Mount Etna in Sicily is arguably the most exotic — volcanic rock formed by one of Europe's most active volcanoes, glazed by artisans working in the shadow of the mountain. Every piece is literally a slice of geological history.

**Q196:** Can I get tiles with a "vintage" or "reclaimed" look?
**A:** Yes, our vintage-look collections replicate the patina of reclaimed materials — weathered wood, aged terracotta, centuries-worn stone. The distressing is deliberate and controlled, creating authentic character without the structural uncertainty of genuine reclaimed materials.

**Q197:** Do you have tiles suitable for a "grandmillennial" or "modern traditional" interior?
**A:** Grandmillennial style — traditional patterns used in fresh ways — is perfectly served by our encaustic-look collections, subway tiles with coloured grout, herringbone patterns, and decorative border tiles. It's about honouring tradition with contemporary confidence.

**Q198:** Can I request a " designer's sample set" of your most unique pieces?
**A:** Your liaison can prepare a "greatest hits" sample set featuring our most extraordinary and unique tiles — the pieces that generate the most conversation, the limited editions, the artisanal one-offs. It's an inspiring collection to have in your studio.

**Q199:** What's the difference between your "exclusive" and "special order" collections?
**A:** Exclusive collections are items we stock regularly and promote as unique to Tiletoria. Special order items are available from our international suppliers but not held in local stock — they require a lead time and minimum quantity. Both are unavailable at mass retail, but special order requires more planning.

**Q200:** Do you have tiles that reference specific art movements — Art Deco, Bauhaus, Memphis?
**A:** Our geometric collections speak fluently to Art Deco and Bauhaus aesthetics — bold geometry, disciplined pattern, and material honesty. For Memphis design (playful, colourful, postmodern), our brightest encaustic patterns and mixed mosaic blends capture that energy.

## Category 5: Collection Cohesion & Curation

**Q201:** Do your collections include floor, wall, feature, and trim pieces that work together?
**A:** Yes, our curated collections are designed as complete systems. A typical collection includes a floor tile, wall tile, feature or mosaic accent, pencil trim, skirting, and sometimes a decorative insert — all sharing tonal DNA. It removes the guesswork from coordination.

**Q202:** How do I know which tiles within a collection work together?
**A:** Each collection is displayed as a family at Spec Lab. Your liaison can walk you through the relationships — how the floor tile's undertone reappears in the mosaic, how the feature tile's pattern picks up the wall tile's glaze variation. It's curatorial, not accidental.

**Q203:** Can you help me create a tonal scheme using multiple collections?
**A:** Absolutely. Cross-collection coordination is where our consultants excel. We can pull pieces from different manufacturers and countries that share tonal temperature — warm greys from Italy, terracotta accents from Spain — creating a cohesive palette with visual interest.

**Q204:** What is grout colour coordination, and why does it matter?
**A:** Grout colour can either emphasise the grid (contrast) or disappear it (match). For seamless large-format installations, matching grout creates a monolithic surface. For patterned tiles, contrasting grout emphasises the geometry. We coordinate grout to your design intent, not as an afterthought.

**Q205:** Do you have "design-ready" palettes that I can specify as a package?
**A:** Yes, our curated palettes — "Warm Minimalist," "Coastal Calm," "Urban Industrial," "Mediterranean Spirit" — combine floor, wall, feature, and trim tiles with specified grout colours. They're starting points you can customise or specify exactly as presented.

**Q206:** Can I see an entire collection installed together in one of your vignettes?
**A:** Yes, our vignettes are designed to showcase collections in dialogue — floor meeting wall meeting feature meeting trim. You'll see how the grout colour, joint alignment, and tonal relationships create a unified spatial experience.

**Q207:** How do I avoid a "showroom floor" look where everything matches too perfectly?
**A:** The art is in controlled contrast. We recommend staying within a tonal family but varying texture and format — polished floor against matte wall, large format against mosaic feature. Your liaison helps you find the tension point between cohesion and visual interest.

**Q208:** Do you offer coordinated floor and wall tiles in different formats but the same colour?
**A:** Many collections offer the same colourway in multiple formats — 600x600mm floor, 300x600mm wall, mosaic feature. The colour is consistent, but the scale and texture variation create sophisticated layering. It's one of our most popular specifying approaches.

**Q209:** Can I get a "collection card" showing all the pieces in a coordinated range?
**A:** Yes, every collection has a specification card showing all available sizes, finishes, trim pieces, mosaic options, and coordinating grout colours. It's your reference tool for specification and client presentation.

**Q210:** How do I know if a floor and wall tile from different collections will work together?
**A:** Bring both samples into the same light and compare undertones. Our consultants can assess temperature compatibility — warm greys with warm greys, cool greys with cool greys. We never recommend combining tiles we haven't seen in dialogue.

**Q211:** What trim pieces are typically available in a coordinated collection?
**A:** Most premium collections offer pencil trim (15-20mm wide), skirting or coving pieces, step-nosing for stairs, and bullnose or quarter-round edge pieces. These finishing elements are what separate amateur installation from professional specification.

**Q212:** Can you create a custom palette for my specific project?
**A:** For Platinum members and significant projects, your liaison will create a bespoke project palette — selecting floor, wall, feature, and trim tiles with grout specifications and a written rationale. It's a value-added specification service.

**Q213:** Do you have collections specifically designed for open-plan living where kitchen, dining, and living flow together?
**A:** Yes, our open-plan collections offer consistent flooring across all zones with subtle variation — perhaps the kitchen area has a patterned feature strip, the living area is plain, but the base tile unifies the space. Continuity with definition.

**Q214:** How important is it to specify the same brand's grout with their tiles?
**A:** While not mandatory, using the same manufacturer's grout ensures colour accuracy against their tile. We stock grout colour-matched to our major collections, and your liaison will recommend the precise shade for your installation.

**Q215:** Can I get samples of the full collection family at once?
**A:** Yes, request a "collection family set" and your liaison will prepare samples of every size, finish, and trim piece in the collection. It's the most efficient way to evaluate a collection's full potential.

**Q216:** Do you have coordinated outdoor and indoor tiles for seamless indoor-outdoor living?
**A:** Several collections offer 10mm indoor tiles and 20mm outdoor pavers in matching or complementary designs. The visual continuity between interior and terrace is a hallmark of high-end residential design, particularly in Cape Town and Durban coastal homes.

**Q217:** What's the difference between a "collection" and a "range"?
**A:** We use "collection" for a curated family of tiles designed to work together — floor, wall, feature, trim. "Range" is broader — all the tiles from a particular manufacturer or country. A collection is a ready-made design solution; a range requires curation.

**Q218:** Can I mix Italian and Spanish tiles in one project?
**A:** Absolutely, and many of our most interesting projects do exactly that. The key is tonal coordination — a warm Spanish terracotta floor with a cool Italian grey wall can be extraordinary if the temperature contrast is intentional, not accidental.

**Q219:** Do you have any collections that include both tile and matching sanware finishes?
**A:** While tiles and sanware come from different manufacturers, we can coordinate finishes precisely. A matte black tap from our premium tapware range with a charcoal porcelain floor, or brushed brass accessories with warm beige terrazzo — your liaison curates the cross-category coordination.

**Q220:** How do I specify a "feature wall" tile that relates to but doesn't compete with the floor?
**A:** The rule is either complement or contrast, never compete. A patterned feature wall works against a plain floor. A textured feature works against a smooth floor. A dark feature works against a light floor. Your liaison will model the relationship with samples.

**Q221:** Can you help me with a "whole home" tile specification where every room connects?
**A:** Yes, this is a favourite challenge. We specify a consistent base tile throughout with room-specific variations — the same warm grey in 600x600mm on the floor, 300x600mm on bathroom walls, mosaic in the shower, and a feature strip in the kitchen. One family, many expressions.

**Q222:** Do coordinated collections cost more than buying individual tiles separately?
**A:** Not necessarily. Collections are priced individually, so you pay for each piece. The value is in the curation — knowing the pieces work together, having one supplier, one delivery, one warranty. The time saved in coordination is worth the investment.

**Q223:** Can I see how a collection ages? Do you have installations that are a few years old?
**A:** Our commercial team can arrange site visits to older installations — hotels, restaurants, residential developments — where you can see how collections perform and patinate over time. It's invaluable research for long-term specifications.

**Q224:** What happens if a trim piece in my chosen collection is discontinued?
**A:** We give advance notice of any discontinuations and typically recommend holding stock of critical trim pieces. We can also suggest alternative trims that coordinate tonally. Your liaison monitors your specified collections for supply continuity.

**Q225:** Do you offer 2D or 3D layout visualisations of how a collection will look installed?
**A:** Yes, our team can provide layout visualisations showing running bond, stack bond, herringbone, and custom laying patterns. For complex hospitality projects, we can commission full 3D renders showing the collection in situ.

**Q226:** Can I get a "sample installation" photo for my client showing the exact collection we're specifying?
**A:** We maintain an extensive project photography library showing our collections in completed installations. Your liaison can provide high-res images of your specified collection in a similar project type — hotel, residence, or restaurant.

**Q227:** How do you handle it when a client wants to swap one piece of a coordinated collection?
**A:** We evaluate the proposed substitution against the original palette. Sometimes the swap works beautifully; other times it disrupts the tonal harmony. We'll give you an honest design assessment, not just process the order.

**Q228:** Do you have a checklist for specifying a complete collection?
**A:** Yes, our specification checklist covers floor tile, wall tile, feature tile, mosaic accent, all trim pieces, grout colours, adhesive type, sealant requirements, and recommended laying pattern. It's the foundation of a bulletproof specification.

**Q229:** Can you create "mood boxes" — physical boxes with tiles, grout, paint chips, and fabric swatches?
**A:** For Platinum members, we can create mood boxes that extend beyond tile to include coordinating paint references, fabric suggestions, and material samples. It's a holistic design tool for client presentations.

**Q230:** How do I ensure my specification doesn't look dated in five years?
**A:** We recommend "timeless with a twist" — a classic base collection with one contemporary feature element that can be updated. A neutral stone-look floor with a trendy mosaic feature is easier to refresh than an all-trend specification. Your liaison advises on longevity.

**Q231:** Can I get matching tiles for a project that needs to be extended years later?
**A:** We always recommend holding stock for future extensions — buy 10% extra and store it. If you haven't, we can check batch continuity or suggest the closest current match. For imported collections, we can sometimes arrange a special production run at manufacturer minimums.

**Q232:** Do you have any "hero" collections that every designer should know?
**A:** Our Italian rectified-look porcelain, Spanish encaustic-look patterned tile, Moroccan-inspired zellige collection, and concrete-look large-format ranges are our "big four" — the collections that define our offering and appear in the country's most published interiors.

**Q233:** Can you help me create a "material story" that connects tile to other finishes in the project?
**A:** Yes, we think in material narratives. If your project uses oak joinery, we'll recommend tiles with warm undertones that pick up the wood's honey notes. If there's brass hardware, we'll find tiles with subtle metallic warmth. The tile doesn't exist in isolation — it's part of a material conversation.

**Q234:** What's the relationship between tile format and perceived ceiling height?
**A:** Vertical formats (300x600mm, 300x900mm) laid in stack bond draw the eye upward, increasing perceived height. Horizontal formats and large squares emphasise width. In rooms with low ceilings, consider vertical wall tiles and large-format floor tiles — the contrast in format direction creates spatial drama.

**Q235:** Do you have any "install-only" instructions that my contractor can follow?
**A:** Every specification includes installation guidelines covering adhesive type, grout selection, joint width, expansion joint placement, and sealing requirements. These aren't a substitute for a professional tiler's expertise, but they ensure your design intent is clearly communicated.

## Category 6: Aesthetic Selection Support

**Q236:** Can you help me choose between warm and cool tones for my project?
**A:** Absolutely. Bring your mood board, paint swatches, and fabric samples to Spec Lab. We'll assess the undertone temperature of your overall palette and recommend tiles that harmonise. Warm projects need warm greys and beiges; cool projects need cool greys and whites.

**Q237:** What's the difference between matte and polished tiles in terms of look and maintenance?
**A:** Polished tiles reflect light, making spaces feel larger and more luxurious, but they show water spots and scratches more readily. Matte tiles absorb light, creating a softer, more intimate atmosphere and hiding imperfections. For bathrooms, matte floor with polished wall is a classic combination.

**Q238:** When should I specify rectified edges versus pressed edges?
**A:** Specify rectified edges when you want minimal grout joints (2mm) and a seamless, contemporary aesthetic. Rectified is essential for large-format tiles and modern minimalism. Pressed edges suit traditional settings where slightly wider joints (3-5mm) are acceptable and the slight cushion feels appropriate.

**Q239:** Can you advise on the best tile format for a small bathroom?
**A:** Counterintuitively, large-format tiles (600x600mm or 600x1200mm) often work best in small bathrooms — fewer grout joints create visual calm and the space feels larger. Avoid tiny mosaics on floors; they create busy grout grids. A feature mosaic on one wall adds interest without overwhelming.

**Q240:** What laying patterns do you recommend beyond basic grid?
**A:** Herringbone adds dynamism to rectangular formats, running bond (brick pattern) softens rigid spaces, chevron creates directional energy, and basket-weave brings traditional elegance. For large-format rectified tiles, stack bond (perfect grid) is the contemporary standard. Your liaison can show you examples of each.

**Q241:** How do I choose between a bold feature tile and a subtle texture?
**A:** Consider the viewing duration. In a powder room where guests spend two minutes, a bold feature is delightful. In a master bathroom where clients spend an hour daily, subtle texture endures better. Also consider the client's personality — are they adventurous or conservative?

**Q242:** What's the current trend in tile formats — getting larger or smaller?
**A:** Both directions simultaneously. Large-format slabs (1200x1200mm and above) dominate luxury residential and commercial floors for their seamless minimalism. Meanwhile, small-format artisanal tiles (100x100mm, hexagons) are trending for feature walls and backsplashes where craft and detail are valued. The middle ground is less fashionable.

**Q243:** Can you help me specify tiles for a dark, moody interior?
**A:** Yes, dark interiors require careful material selection. We recommend tiles with some surface variation or texture to avoid flatness — concrete-look porcelain in charcoal, dark stone with veining, or black tiles with a subtle metallic sheen. Lighting design is critical; we can coordinate with your lighting consultant.

**Q244:** What's the best way to add texture without adding pattern?
**A:** 3D relief tiles, ribbed surfaces, or tiles with subtle surface undulation add tactile interest without visual pattern. Our soft-touch matte collections and linen-look textures are perfect for "quiet luxury" interiors where texture speaks louder than pattern.

**Q245:** How do I choose tiles for a client who wants "something different" but is risk-averse?
**A:** The "safe statement" approach — a neutral, high-quality base tile over most of the surface with a small, contained feature area in something bolder. A patterned floor in the shower niche only, or a bold mosaic on the vanity wall only. They get their "different" without overwhelm.

**Q246:** What colour temperature should I consider for north-facing versus south-facing rooms?
**A:** North-facing rooms (in the Southern Hemisphere) receive warmer, direct sunlight — cool-toned tiles balance the warmth. South-facing rooms receive cooler, indirect light — warm-toned tiles prevent the space from feeling cold. East-facing mornings want warm welcomes; west-facing afternoons need cooling influence.

**Q247:** Can you help me specify tiles that work with a specific architectural style — mid-century modern, for example?
**A:** Yes, our consultants understand architectural context. Mid-century modern calls for terrazzo-look or geometric patterned tiles in warm, saturated tones. Contemporary minimalism wants large-format rectified in concrete or stone looks. Cape Dutch heritage needs encaustic patterns and subway tiles. We speak architecture.

**Q248:** What's the rule for mixing gloss and matte tiles in one room?
**A:** There's no hard rule, but a successful formula is: matte on floors (safety and practicality), gloss or satin on walls (light reflection and cleaning ease), and one feature surface in either finish depending on intent. Avoid mixing gloss levels randomly — it should be purposeful.

**Q249:** How do I specify tiles for a client who loves Scandinavian design?
**A:** Scandinavian specification favours light, warm greys; wood-look planks in pale oak; hexagon mosaics in white or soft grey; and matte finishes throughout. The palette is restrained but the material quality is high. Our concrete-look and wood-look collections in light tones are perfect.

**Q250:** What tile choices make a small room feel larger?
**A:** Four strategies: large-format tiles with matching grout (fewer joints, visual continuity); light colours with good LRV (light reflectance value); gloss or polished finishes (light bounce); and running the same tile from floor to wall (blur the boundaries). Your liaison will model these options.

**Q251:** Can you help me specify for a "Japandi" interior — Japanese-Scandinavian fusion?
**A:** Japandi calls for natural materiality, muted tones, imperfect texture, and extreme restraint. Our concrete-look porcelain in warm grey, unglazed porcelain with subtle variation, and wood-look planks in walnut or wenge tones capture the aesthetic. One material, used generously, is the Japandi way.

**Q252:** How do I choose the right white tile — there are so many shades?
**A:** Whites range from crisp blue-white through true white to creamy warm white. Hold your white options against your client's existing whites — sanitaryware, kitchen cabinets, paint. The white that disappears against their existing palette is the right white. Never specify white in isolation.

**Q253:** What tiles work best for a Hamptons-style coastal interior?

**Q254:** Can you advise on tile selection for a biophilic design approach?

**Q255:** What's the most common aesthetic mistake designers make when specifying tiles?
**A:** Specifying tiles from photographs without seeing them in person. Colour temperature, surface texture, and scale are impossible to judge accurately from a screen. Always borrow samples and view them in the project's actual light. Our sample loan system exists to prevent this mistake.

**Q256:** How do I specify tiles for a client who collects art and wants the architecture to be a backdrop?
**A:** The gallery approach — large-format, pale, matte tiles with matching grout that create a calm, continuous surface. Concrete-look or stone-look in warm white or very light grey. The tile should recede; the art should advance. Rectified edges for minimal visual interruption.

**Q257:** Can you help me with a "maximalist" interior — bold pattern, rich colour, layered texture?
**A:** Maximalist specification is our playground. Layer patterned encaustic floors with textured feature walls, metallic mosaic accents, and rich grout colours. We can pull our boldest Spanish patterns, jewel-toned Moroccan glazes, and dramatic Italian large-format veined porcelain. More is more.

**Q258:** What's the best way to transition between different tiles in an open-plan space?
**A:** Transition strips, threshold pieces, or simply a grout joint where materials meet. For seamless transitions, use the same tile in different formats. For deliberate transitions, a metal Schluter profile in brass or chrome creates a clean, intentional break. Your liaison will show you transition options.

**Q259:** How do I choose between a patterned floor and a patterned wall?
**A:** The rule of balance: bold floor, quiet walls OR quiet floor, bold walls. Never both. In small rooms (powder rooms), a patterned floor is dramatic and contained. In large rooms, a patterned feature wall anchors a seating area or vanity zone. Consider where you want the eye to travel.

**Q260:** Can you advise on tiles that work with underfloor heating?

**Q261:** What tile specification would you recommend for a wine cellar or tasting room?
**A:** Wine cellars call for cool, atmospheric materiality. Our-look porcelain, dark stone-look tiles, or genuinein charcoal or wine-red tones create the right mood. For tasting rooms, consider terrazzo-look with wine-coloured stone chips — a subtle thematic nod.

**Q262:** How do I ensure my tile specification photographs well for magazines and social media?
**A:** Matte and soft-touch finishes photograph more flatteringly than high-gloss, which can create hot spots with flash. Light-to-mid tones show detail better than very dark or very pale. Rectified edges with matching grout create the clean lines that editors love. We can advise specifically for photography-sensitive projects.

**Q263:** Can you help me specify for a luxury safari lodge — something that feels African but refined?
**A:** Safari lodge specification blends raw materiality with refined execution. Our stone-look porcelain in warmtones, wood-look planks in weathered teak, and unglazed porcelain with natural variation all work. The key is authentic texture — nothing too polished or urban.

**Q264:** What's the best way to specify a "quiet luxury" bathroom?
**A:** Quiet luxury speaks through material quality rather than pattern. Large-format rectified porcelain in warm white or soft grey, soft-touch matte finish, matching grout, and minimal trim. One extraordinary piece — perhaps a backlitfeature or a hand-finished artisan accent — provides the only statement.

**Q265:** How do I choose tiles that will age gracefully rather than date quickly?
**A:** Avoid overt trends — the tile equivalent of fashion colours. Invest in material quality: rectified edges, through-body colour, natural tonal variation. Stone looks, concrete looks, and wood looks have decades of proven longevity. Use trend elements in easily replaceable accents, not permanent installations.

**Q266:** Can you advise on specifying tiles for a client with a very specific vision — "I want it to feel like a riad in Marrakech"?
**A:** Bring us the reference images and we'll build the specification. For a Marrakech riad, we'd layer zellige-inspired tiles in jewel tones, patterned cement-look floors, ornate mosaic borders, and tadelakt-finish wall tiles. We tranmood boards into material specifications.

**Q267:** What's the relationship between tile format and perceived ceiling height?
**A:** Vertical formats (300x600mm, 300x900mm) laid in stack bond draw the eye upward, increasing perceived height. Horizontal formats and large squares emphasise width. In rooms with low ceilings, consider vertical wall tiles and large-format floor tiles — the contrast in format direction creates spatial drama.

**Q268:** Can you help me specify tiles for a music studio or home theatre — acoustically and aesthetically?
**A:** Hard tiles reflect sound, so they're not ideal for acoustic-critical spaces unless paired with extensive soft furnishing. For home theatres, consider our textile-look or leather-look tiles that suggest softness while being easy to clean. For music studios, tiles work in wet areas only; specify acoustic panels elsewhere.

**Q269:** How do I specify for a multi-generational home where tastes vary widely?
**A:** The "classic canvas" approach — specify large-format, warm-neutral tiles in shared spaces (kitchens, living areas, main bathrooms) that everyone can live with, then allow individual expression in private spaces (teen bathrooms, guest loos). Shared spaces need consensus; private spaces permit personality.

**Q270:** Can you advise on tiles for a home gym or wellness area?
**A:** Home gyms need high-durability, easy-clean flooring with some slip resistance. Our commercial-grade porcelain in concrete-look or stone-look finishes works perfectly. For wellness areas — yoga studios, meditation rooms — consider wood-look porcelain for warmth underfoot or soft-touch finishes for tactile comfort.

**Q271:** What's the best tile choice for a kitchen that needs to be both beautiful and bulletproof?
**A:** Large-format rectified porcelain in a concrete or stone look — 600x600mm or 600x1200mm. Matte or soft-touch finish hides spills and crumbs. Choose mid-tone colours (medium grey, warm beige) that don't show every speck of dirt. Rectified edges mean minimal grout lines to trap grease.

**Q272:** How do I specify for a heritage building that needs modern performance?
**A:** Our encaustic-look porcelain gives the appearance of traditional cement tiles with modern durability and stain resistance. For Victorian-era homes, subway tiles with pencil trim and decorative borders are authentic. We can match period details while meeting contemporary building standards.

## Category 7: Hospitality & Commercial Design

**Q273:** What tile specifications do you recommend for hotel lobbies and reception areas?

**Q274:** How do your tiles perform in high-traffic commercial environments?
**A:** Our commercial-grade porcelain tiles are rated PEI 4 or 5, meaning they're suitable for heavy commercial traffic — hotels, restaurants, retail spaces, and office lobbies. The through-body colour means wear doesn't reveal a different colour underneath, maintaining appearance for decades.

**Q275:** What's the slip rating requirement for public spaces like hotel restaurants?
**A:** SANS 10107 and insurance requirements typically mandate R10 or R11 slip ratings for commercial wet areas — hotel bathrooms, restaurant kitchens, pool surrounds. We provide full slip test documentation for all commercial-specified tiles and can recommend appropriate products for each zone.

**Q276:** Can your tiles withstand commercial cleaning chemicals and machinery?

**Q277:** Do you have tiles specifically designed for restaurant kitchens?
**A:** Yes, our commercial kitchen specification includes high-slip-rated (R11-R12) quarry tiles and textured porcelain that perform under grease, water, and heavy foot traffic. They're also resistant to thermal shock from hot equipment and thermal cycling from refrigeration.

**Q278:** What tiles work best for hotel bathrooms where every room must look identical?
**A:** We recommend porcelain collections with controlled digital variation for hotel bathrooms — consistent enough that room 101 matches room 201, but not so uniform they look artificial. Large-format wall tiles minimise grout maintenance, and rectified edges create the luxury aesthetic guests expect.

**Q279:** Can you supply tiles for a 200-room hotel development?
**A:** Absolutely. Our commercial division handles large-scale hospitality projects regularly. We manage batch consistency across large orders, coordinate phased deliveries to site, and provide dedicated project management. Annual volumes of this scale qualify for project-specific pricing.

**Q280:** Do you have experience with boutique hotel specifications?
**A:** Extensive experience. Boutique hotels are where our exclusive collections shine — each room can have a unique character using our artisanal mosaics, Moroccan-inspired features, and hand-finished tiles. We've supplied numerous Western Cape wine estate hotels and KZN coastal boutique properties.

**Q281:** What warranty do you offer on tiles for commercial installations?
**A:** Our standard commercial warranty covers manufacturing defects for 10 years on porcelain and ceramic tiles. For project-specific warranties, including performance guarantees, our commercial team can negotiate extended terms based on specification compliance and correct installation.

**Q282:** Can you provide SANS compliance certificates for commercial projects?
**A:** Yes, we provide full technical documentation including SANS 1449 compliance certificates, slip test reports, chemical resistance data, and fire classification certificates. This documentation is essential for NHBRC compliance and commercial insurance requirements.

**Q283:** How do you ensure batch consistency across a large commercial order?
**A:** For large commercial orders, we pull stock from a single production batch or coordinate factory production to ensure batch consistency. Your commercial project manager oversees this process and provides batch certificates for quality assurance records.

**Q284:** What tiles do you recommend for spa and wellness areas?
**A:** Spa areas require slip-rated floor tiles (R10 minimum, R11 preferred), large-format wall tiles for easy cleaning, and a calming aesthetic. Our concrete-look and stone-look porcelain in warm tones create the serene atmosphere essential for wellness. For steam rooms, specific heat-resistant adhesives are required.

**Q285:** Can your tiles be used for swimming pool interiors and surrounds?
**A:** Yes, we supply glass mosaic pool tiles, porcelain pool surrounds with R11 slip rating, and coping pieces in matching or contrasting materials. Pool tiles must be specifically rated for immersion — we never recommend standard wall tiles for pool interiors.

**Q286:** What specification support do you offer for architects working on commercial projects?
**A:** Our Spec Lab team provides full specification writing support, technical data sheets, CAD details, BIM-compatible product information, and CPD presentations. We can also attend design team meetings and present options to your client or project architect.

**Q287:** Can you handle phased deliveries for a multi-phase hotel development?
**A:** Yes, phased delivery is standard for commercial projects. We hold stock in our warehouse and release it according to your construction programme. Each phase is batch-controlled, and your project manager coordinates delivery windows with your site team.

**Q288:** Do you have tiles that meet green building certification requirements?
**A:** Several of our European-manufactured tiles carry environmental certifications and are produced in factories with renewable energy and water recycling. We can provide the environmental product declarations (EPDs) and recycled content data required for Green Star or LEED certification.

**Q289:** How do I specify tiles for a restaurant where spills, dropped plates, and heavy foot traffic are daily realities?
**A:** Restaurant specification needs: high PEI rating (4-5), stain-resistant glaze, R10+ slip rating for floors, large-format to minimise grout joints, and mid-tone colours that don't show every crumb. Our concrete-look and stone-look commercial porcelain is specified in restaurants across Sandton and the Atlantic Seaboard.

**Q290:** Can you supply tiles for exterior commercial facades?
**A:** Yes, our exterior cladding tiles include porcelain stoneware rated for direct fixing, rainscreen systems, and ventilated facades. These are specifically manufactured to withstand UV exposure, thermal cycling, and wind load. Technical fixing details are provided for each system.

**Q291:** What tiles work best for retail environments where product display is critical?
**A:** Retail floors need to showcase merchandise, not compete with it. Light-to-mid-tone large-format porcelain with minimal pattern, high LRV for good lighting performance, and PEI 4+ durability. For flagship stores, our polished and semi-polished finishes add luxury while maintaining practicality.

**Q292:** Do you have anti-bacterial or hygienic tiles for healthcare and food preparation?
**A:** Several of our collections include anti-bacterial glaze technology — silver ion integration that inhibits bacterial growth. These are specified for healthcare bathrooms, commercial kitchens, and spa changing rooms where hygiene is paramount. Technical certification is available.

**Q293:** Can you help with value engineering if my commercial project is over budget?

**Q294:** How do you handle replacement tiles for commercial projects years after installation?
**A:** We recommend that commercial clients hold a maintenance stock of 3-5% of the original order for future repairs. If maintenance stock wasn't held, we can check for remaining batch stock or suggest the closest current match. For critical projects, we can arrange factory reproduction at minimum order quantities.

**Q295:** What tiles do you recommend for school and educational environments?
**A:** Educational environments need extreme durability, slip resistance, stain resistance, and easy maintenance. Our commercial-grade porcelain in practical colours with R10+ slip ratings is standard. For younger children, consider slightly textured surfaces that hide scuff marks and provide grip for active feet.

**Q296:** Can you supply tiles for elevator lobbies and lift cars?
**A:** Yes, elevator lobbies are a design moment — many designers specify our feature collections, metallic finishes, or book-matched stone-look porcelain here. For lift cars, we supply tiles rated for the specific weight and fixing requirements of elevator manufacturers.

**Q297:** Do you have experience with retirement village and aged care specifications?
**A:** Yes, aged care requires specific consideration: R11+ slip ratings throughout, contrasting colours at level changes for visual impairment safety, warm tones that feel residential rather than institutional, and large-format tiles that accommodate walking frames and wheelchairs smoothly.

**Q298:** What support do you offer during the defect liability period?
**A:** For commercial projects, our project manager remains available throughout the defect liability period, attending site inspections, addressing any manufacturing defect claims, and coordinating replacements. We're a specification partner, not just a supplier.

**Q299:** Can you help me specify for an airport or transport hub?
**A:** Transport hubs require the highest durability ratings — PEI 5, R12 slip ratings, chemical resistance to aviation fuels and de-icing agents, and the ability to withstand rolling luggage and heavy trolleys. Our commercial team has experience with airport-grade specifications and can provide the required technical documentation.

**Q300:** How do I handle expansion joints in large commercial floor areas?
**A:** Expansion joints are critical in commercial installations. We provide technical guidance on joint placement — typically every 8-10 metres in both directions, at building expansion joints, and around columns. Schluter movement joint profiles in matching aluminium or brass complete the detail.

**Q301:** Do you have tiles suitable for gym and fitness centre floors?
**A:** Gym floors need high impact resistance, slip resistance even when wet with sweat, and compatibility with rubber gym matting. Our commercial-grade textured porcelain in dark tones performs well. For free weight areas, additional subfloor protection is recommended beyond the tile specification.

**Q302:** Can your tiles be used for rooftop terraces and outdoor dining areas?
**A:** Yes, our 20mm outdoor porcelain pavers are specifically designed for raised pedestal installation on rooftops and terraces. They provide the durability of porcelain with the installation flexibility of a paver system — perfect for outdoor dining areas in hotels and restaurants.

**Q303:** What's the typical lifespan of your commercial-grade tiles?
**A:** Properly specified and installed commercial-grade porcelain should perform for 20+ years in high-traffic environments. Many of our tiles in hotel lobbies and shopping centres have been in service for decades with minimal visible wear. Porcelain doesn't wear out — it outlasts the building's design cycle.

**Q304:** Can you provide references from other commercial projects you've supplied?
**A:** Our commercial team can provide references and case studies from similar project types — hotels, restaurants, office developments, retail centres. We can also arrange site visits to completed projects where you can assess performance firsthand.

## Category 8: Delivery & Project Coordination

**Q305:** What are your standard delivery lead times for in-stock products?
**A:** In-stock products deliver within 3-5 working days to major centres (Johannesburg, Pretoria, Durban, Cape Town). Regional areas may take 5-7 days. For Spec Lab collections, we often deliver within 48 hours for urgent requirements.

**Q306:** How accurate are your lead time estimates? I can't afford delays.
**A:** We understand that late delivery is your biggest operational pain point. Our stated lead times are accurate 95% of the time. For critical-path items, we confirm stock availability before quoting and hold stock against a deposit. If delays occur, your liaison communicates proactively.

**Q307:** Can you deliver directly to site, or only to my studio?
**A:** We deliver to any address — your studio, the construction site, your contractor's yard, or a storage facility. Site delivery is standard practice for commercial projects. Just provide clear access instructions and a site contact number.

**Q308:** Do you offer white-glove delivery for high-value or fragile tiles?

**Q309:** What happens if my delivery arrives and quantities are wrong?
**A:** Check quantities against your delivery note at receipt. If there's a discrepancy, photograph the delivery and contact your liaison within 24 hours. We'll investigate and arrange immediate despatch of any shortfall or collection of excess.

**Q310:** Can you coordinate delivery with my installer's schedule?
**A:** Yes, we actively coordinate with your installer. Provide us with their preferred delivery window and we'll schedule accordingly. For complex projects, your liaison will call the installer directly to confirm timing. The tile should arrive when they're ready.

**Q311:** Do you deliver on weekends?
**A:** Saturday delivery is available in major centres at a surcharge. Sunday delivery can be arranged for critical commercial projects. Your liaison will confirm availability and pricing for weekend delivery when you place your order.

**Q312:** How are large-format tiles protected during delivery?
**A:** Large-format tiles (600x1200mm and above) are delivered in reinforced wooden crates with foam edge protection and interleaving sheets. Crates are forklift-compatible for site unloading. Damage in transit is extremely rare with our packaging protocol.

**Q313:** Can you hold my order in your warehouse and deliver in stages?
**A:** Yes, split delivery is standard for projects with phased construction. We hold your stock free of charge for up to 30 days, releasing it according to your programme. For longer storage periods, a nominal warehousing fee applies.

**Q314:** What should I do if tiles arrive damaged?
**A:** Note the damage on the delivery note before signing, photograph the damaged tiles and packaging, and contact your liaison immediately. We'll arrange replacement stock from the same batch if available, or provide alternatives with your approval.

**Q315:** Do you deliver to rural areas and small towns?
**A:** Yes, we deliver nationally. Rural deliveries may take an additional 2-3 days and utilise third-party courier networks. Your liaison will confirm the delivery route and timing for non-metro addresses.

**Q316:** Can my contractor collect directly from your warehouse?
**A:** Yes, contractor collection is common and efficient. We'll prepare a collection note with your authorisation, and your contractor collects from our designated warehouse during business hours. This avoids delivery charges and gives your contractor control over timing.

**Q317:** How much notice do you need for an urgent delivery?
**A:** For in-stock items in major centres, 24 hours' notice is usually sufficient for next-day delivery. For urgent same-day delivery, we use express courier services at a premium. Your liaison will confirm feasibility and cost.

**Q318:** Do you provide a delivery tracking service?
**A:** Yes, all deliveries are tracked. Your liaison provides a waybill number and the driver's contact details. For large commercial deliveries, your project manager provides real-time updates on delivery progress.

**Q319:** What are your delivery charges?
**A:** Delivery charges vary by distance, order volume, and product type. Orders above a threshold value qualify for free delivery within major centres. Your liaison provides exact delivery costs with every quotation — no surprises.

**Q320:** Can I specify a delivery time window rather than a full day?
**A:** For major centres, morning (8-12) or afternoon (12-5) windows are standard. For precise time slots, express delivery services are available at a premium. Commercial sites with crane scheduling or road closure requirements receive priority coordination.

**Q321:** Do you offer crane offloading for large commercial deliveries?
**A:** For high-rise construction projects, we coordinate crane-offloading through your site team. Tiles are packaged in crane-liftable stillages with certified lifting points. Your project manager attends the offload to ensure safe handling.

**Q322:** How do you handle delivery to Sandton CBD with traffic and access restrictions?
**A:** Our Sandton delivery team knows the area intimately — loading zones, traffic patterns, and building access protocols. We schedule deliveries during optimal windows and coordinate with building management for basement access where required.

**Q323:** Can I change my delivery address after placing the order?
**A:** Yes, if the order hasn't already been dispatched. Contact your liaison as soon as possible with the new address. For dispatched orders, redirection may incur additional courier charges depending on the carrier's policy.

**Q324:** Do you deliver to neighbouring countries — Botswana, Namibia, Mozambique?
**A:** Yes, we export to SADC countries regularly. Export orders require additional documentation — customs declarations, export invoices, and transit insurance. Our commercial team handles the logistics. Lead times are typically 7-14 days depending on destination.

**Q325:** What happens if my project is delayed and I need to postpone delivery?
**A:** Contact your liaison as soon as you know about the delay. We'll hold your stock in our warehouse and reschedule delivery at no charge for up to 30 days. Beyond 30 days, we discuss the best approach — extended storage or order amendment.

**Q326:** Can you provide a certificate of origin for imported tiles?
**A:** Yes, we provide certificates of origin for all imported collections — Italian, Spanish, and Moroccan. These are sometimes required for customs clearance on re-export projects or for client documentation in luxury developments.

**Q327:** Do you have a minimum order value for delivery?
**A:** There's no minimum order value, but delivery charges on small orders can be disproportionate. For sample-only or small replacement orders, collection from your nearest branch is more economical. Your liaison will advise the most cost-effective option.

**Q328:** How do I know if the tiles delivered are from the same batch as my sample?
**A:** Every delivery note includes the batch number, which you can cross-reference against your sample. If batch consistency is critical, your liaison confirms the batch number before delivery and ensures all tiles for your project come from matched batches.

**Q329:** Can you provide a dedicated truck for my large order rather than a shared load?
**A:** For orders exceeding a full truckload or for delicate large-format tiles, we provide dedicated delivery vehicles. This is standard for commercial projects and available for large residential orders. Your project manager arranges the logistics.

**Q330:** What COVID or health protocols do your delivery teams follow?
**A:** Our delivery teams follow all current health protocols — masking on request, sanitising hands before handling products, and maintaining social distance. For healthcare or sensitive sites, we can arrange contactless delivery with photographic confirmation.

**Q331:** Can you deliver after hours to avoid disrupting business operations?
**A:** Yes, after-hours and weekend delivery is standard for commercial fit-outs where daytime delivery would disrupt trading. Your commercial project manager coordinates these deliveries as part of the project programme.

**Q332:** Do you offer installation services, or only supply?
**A:** We supply tiles and installation materials only — we don't offer installation services. However, your liaison can recommend experienced installers who have worked with our products. We believe in separating supply from installation to avoid conflict of interest.

**Q333:** How do I calculate the correct quantity to order, including wastage?
**A:** We recommend 10% wastage for standard installations, 15% for diagonal or herringbone patterns, and 20% for complex rooms with many cuts. Your liaison will calculate exact quantities from your floor plans and confirm the order includes appropriate wastage.

**Q334:** Can you do a site measure before I finalise my order?
**A:** For complex commercial projects, our commercial team can arrange a site measure. For residential projects, we work from your architect's drawings or your contractor's measurements. If you're uncertain, we recommend the contractor measures before final order.

**Q335:** What if I order too much? Can I return excess stock?
**A:** Unopened, undamaged full boxes can be returned within 14 days of delivery for a credit. Special-order or imported items may have restocking charges. Always discuss the return policy for your specific order with your liaison before purchasing.

**Q336:** Can you provide a detailed delivery schedule for a multi-room project?
**A:** Yes, your liaison prepares a room-by-room delivery schedule showing what delivers when. This prevents site congestion and ensures tiles arrive when the relevant room is ready for installation. It's part of our project coordination service.

**Q337:** Do you have experience delivering to Atlantic Seaboard apartments with access restrictions?
**A:** Extensive experience. Cape Town's apartment buildings have strict access, loading bay time limits, and lift size constraints. Our Cape Town team coordinates with building management, books loading bays, and uses specialist handlers for tight-access deliveries.

**Q338:** Can you store my tiles in climate-controlled conditions?

**Q339:** How do you handle very fragile or high-value mosaic deliveries?
**A:** Mosaic sheets are delivered in flat, rigid boxes with foam interleaving. Hand-finished and artisanal mosaics receive additional individual wrapping. For high-value mosaic orders, white-glove delivery with installation-ready inspection is standard.

**Q340:** Can you provide proof of delivery with a signature?
**A:** All deliveries require a signature on the delivery note, which we retain for our records. For your convenience, we can also provide photographic proof of delivery showing the goods in situ at your specified address.

**Q341:** What do I do if my installer is delayed and the tiles have already arrived on site?
**A:** Store tiles flat, indoors, protected from moisture and direct sunlight. Our delivery packaging is designed for short-term site storage. If the delay extends beyond a few weeks, consider moving tiles to secure off-site storage to protect them from construction activity.

**Q342:** Do you offer Saturday morning delivery for weekend installer appointments?
**A:** Yes, Saturday morning delivery is popular with designers whose installers work weekends. Book by Thursday midday for Saturday delivery in major centres. It's a small surcharge for significant schedule flexibility.
