**Q343:** Can I add to an order that's already been dispatched?
**A:** Once dispatched, additions are treated as a new order. However, if the delivery truck hasn't yet left our depot, we can sometimes add items to the same delivery. Contact your liaison immediately — speed matters.

**Q344:** How do you handle multi-drop deliveries for projects with multiple sites?
**A:** Multi-drop deliveries are standard for developers and designers with simultaneous projects. We route the delivery truck through your multiple sites, dropping the correct quantities at each location. A small routing surcharge applies.

**Q345:** Can you provide tiles in stillages for forklift handling on large commercial sites?
**A:** Yes, commercial orders are palletised or stillaged for mechanical handling. Each stillage is labelled with the project name, floor level, and room destination. It streamlines site distribution and reduces manual handling damage.

**Q346:** Do you have experience with Florida Road and Glenwood residential renovations?
**A:** Our Durban team has extensive experience with the narrow-access Victorian and Edwardian homes in Glenwood and the Florida Road area. We use smaller delivery vehicles, coordinate with heritage contractors, and understand the logistical challenges of renovating these characterful properties.

**Q347:** What do I do if my client changes their mind after delivery but before installation?
**A:** Contact your liaison immediately. If the tiles are unopened and undamaged, we'll arrange collection and credit. If the new selection is in stock, we can cross-ship. There may be delivery charges for the exchange, but we handle the logistics smoothly.

**Q348:** Can you provide a dedicated project manager for large projects?
**A:** Commercial projects exceeding R500,000 in tile value are assigned a dedicated project manager who coordinates everything from specification through delivery. They're your single point of contact, available by phone and WhatsApp throughout the project.

**Q349:** How do you handle deliveries during load shedding?
**A:** Our warehouses and delivery operations have backup power to maintain operations during load shedding. Delivery schedules may need minor adjustment, but we communicate proactively. Your liaison will confirm any impact on your specific delivery.

**Q350:** Can you arrange international shipping for my project outside South Africa?
**A:** Our commercial division handles exports to SADC and beyond. We coordinate container packing, export documentation, marine insurance, and customs clearance at destination. It's a full door-to-door service for international projects.

## Category 9: Grout, Trim & Accessory Coordination

**Q351:** What grout colours do you offer, and how do I choose?
**A:** We stock a full spectrum of grout colours — from pure white through warm beige, grey (light, mid, dark, charcoal), and accent colours like black and brown. The choice depends on your design intent: matching grout minimises the grid, contrasting grout emphasises pattern, and mid-tone grout is forgiving.

**Q352:** Can I get grout colour samples to test against my tiles?
**A:** Yes, grout colour swatches are included with every sample loan, and we can provide cured grout sample sticks showing the actual dried colour. Wet grout looks different from cured grout — always evaluate the cured sample before deciding.

**Q353:** What's the difference between cement-based grout and epoxy-based grout?
**A:** Cement-based grout is traditional, easier to apply, and suitable for most installations. Epoxy grout is virtually stain-proof, chemical-resistant, and ideal for commercial kitchens, showers, and stain-prone areas. It's harder to apply and costs more, but the performance is unmatched.

**Q354:** Do you recommend epoxy-based grout for all bathroom floors?
**A:** Epoxy grout is excellent for shower floors and wet areas where mould and staining are concerns. For general bathroom floors, high-quality cement grout with a grout sealer is usually sufficient and more economical. We recommend epoxy for the shower and cement grout for the main bathroom floor.

**Q355:** What trim pieces are available for tile edges and corners?
**A:** We stock pencil trims, L-angle trims, bullnose pieces, quarter-round, coving, and step-nosing profiles. These are available in matching porcelain, coordinating metal (brass, chrome, black), and aluminium from Schluter. The right trim elevates installation from competent to refined.

**Q356:** Can I get metal trim in brushed brass or matte black to match my tapware?
**A:** Yes, our Schluter trim range includes brushed brass, matte black, polished chrome, and brushed nickel finishes. Specifying trim in the same finish as your our premium tapware range or our tapware range tapware creates a coordinated, considered detail that clients notice and photographers capture.

**Q357:** What's the best way to finish a tile edge where it meets a different material?
**A:** A Schluter L-profile or T-profile in matching metal creates a clean, intentional transition between tile and carpet, timber, or screed. The profile protects the tile edge and provides a crisp line that looks designed, not incidental. Your liaison will recommend the right profile for each transition.

**Q358:** Do you have skirting tiles that match your floor collections?
**A:** Many of our premium collections include matching skirting or coving pieces — typically 100-150mm high with a finished top edge. For collections without matching skirting, we can recommend a complementary option or a metal skirting profile for a contemporary detail.

**Q359:** What's the standard grout joint width for rectified tiles?
**A:** Rectified tiles are designed for 2mm grout joints — the minimum practical width that accommodates slight size variation and allows for grout application. Pressed-edge tiles typically use 3-5mm joints. Never go below 2mm, even with rectified tiles; the joint is structurally necessary.

**Q360:** Can I get coloured grout in epoxy formulation?
**A:** Yes, epoxy-based grout is available in a wide colour range including white, grey, beige, and black. The colours are more consistent than cement grout and don't fade. For design-forward installations, coloured epoxy-based grout can be a deliberate aesthetic choice.

**Q361:** What trim do I need for a shower niche?
**A:** A shower niche benefits from L-angle trim on the exposed edges — either in matching tile material or metal. The trim protects the cut tile edges from chipping and creates a clean frame for the niche. Some designers prefer a shadow-line detail instead, which requires precise cutting but looks ultra-minimal.

**Q362:** How do I choose between a pencil trim and a metal profile for my edges?
**A:** Pencil trim (ceramic or stone, typically 15-20mm wide) provides a traditional, integrated look — the edge reads as part of the tile installation. Metal profiles read as a deliberate design detail and suit contemporary interiors. The choice is aesthetic: seamless integration versus intentional accent.

**Q363:** Do you have matching caulk or silicone for your grout colours?
**A:** Yes, we stock silicone sealants matched to our most popular grout colours. Using matching silicone at movement joints, perimeter joints, and shower corners creates a seamless appearance rather than the jarring white silicone that ruins many otherwise beautiful installations.

**Q364:** What accessories do you stock beyond grout and trim?
**A:** Our accessory range includes tile spacers, levelling systems, grout floats, notched trowels, sponges, tile cutters (manual and electric), drill bits, hole saws, and cleaning products. It's a one-stop specification for everything your installer needs.

**Q365:** Can you provide a "trim schedule" for my project showing every trim piece required?
**A:** Yes, your liaison prepares a trim schedule listing every edge, corner, transition, and step in your project with the recommended trim profile and quantity. It's part of a complete specification and prevents the installer from improvising on site.

**Q366:** What's the difference between a Schluter strip and a standard tile trim?
**A:** Schluter is a brand of premium metal profiles that provide edge protection, transitions, and expansion joints. They're manufactured to precise tolerances in a range of finishes and heights. Standard tile trim may be ceramic or lower-grade metal. For high-end projects, Schluter is the designer's standard.

**Q367:** How do I handle the junction between floor tile and shower glass?
**A:** The floor tile should run under the glass channel or U-channel where possible. If the glass sits on the tile, use a matching silicone sealant at the junction. For frameless glass, the tile edge visible through the glass must be perfectly cut — rectified edges are essential here.

**Q368:** Do you stock tile levelling systems for large-format tiles?
**A:** Yes, we stock levelling clip systems that ensure perfectly flat installations with large-format tiles. These systems are essential for lippage-free results on 600x600mm and larger formats. Your liaison will recommend the right system for your tile thickness and format.

**Q369:** What's the best grout colour for a seamless, monolithic look?
**A:** Choose a grout colour as close as possible to the tile's face colour — ideally one shade lighter than the tile, as grout typically dries slightly darker than the sample. For our concrete-look collections, we have precisely matched grout colours that create the poured-concrete effect designers love.

**Q370:** Can I get glitter or metallic grout for a statement installation?
**A:** Metallic and glitter grouts are available for specialty installations — bars, powder rooms, and feature walls where a little drama is desired. They're not for everyone, but in the right context, they're unforgettable. Your liaison can show you samples.

**Q371:** What expansion joint covers do you recommend for large floor areas?
**A:** Schluter movement joint profiles in matching aluminium or stainless steel provide the expansion control large floors need while maintaining a clean aesthetic. For very wide joints, cover plates in brass or stainless steel can be specified. Your liaison provides the technical sizing.

**Q372:** Do you have drain covers and gullies that coordinate with your floor tiles?
**A:** Yes, we stock tile-insert drain covers that allow your floor tile to continue across the drain opening — creating an almost invisible drainage solution. We also have stainless steel drain covers in various finishes. For linear drains, we can source tileable covers to match your installation.

**Q373:** How do I finish the top edge of a tiled half-wall or bath surround?
**A:** A bullnose piece with a rounded top edge is traditional. For a contemporary look, a Schluter L-angle trim in matching metal provides a crisp, thin edge. Alternatively, a shadow-line or recessed detail where the plaster meets the tile creates a sophisticated, minimal finish.

**Q374:** Can I get pre-mixed grout, or only powder?
**A:** We stock both powder grout (mixed on site with water or additive) and pre-mixed grout in buckets. Pre-mixed grout offers consistency and convenience but costs more. For epoxy-based grout, pre-mixed is standard. Your installer will have a preference based on their workflow.

**Q375:** What grout additive do you recommend for shower areas?
**A:** A grout additive with anti-fungal properties and improved water resistance is recommended for all shower installations. It transforms standard cement grout into a more resilient, mould-resistant joint without the cost and application difficulty of full epoxy.

**Q376:** Do you have matching corner shelves or soap dishes for your tile collections?
**A:** Yes, we stock ceramic corner shelves, soap dishes, and toilet roll holders that can be tiled in to match your installation. These integrated accessories look far more refined than surface-mounted alternatives and are standard in luxury bathroom specifications.

**Q377:** How do I specify for a "tile-to-tile" corner without trim?
**A:** A butt-jointed corner with precisely cut tiles and colour-matched silicone at the junction creates a minimal, trim-free detail. It requires skilled cutting — mitred edges on rectified tiles for the sharpest result. Show your installer the detail drawing before they start.

**Q378:** Can you provide a sample board showing tile, grout, and trim together?
**A:** Yes, your liaison can prepare a complete sample board showing your selected tile, two or three grout options, and the proposed trim profile. It's the most effective way to evaluate how the finished installation will look before committing.

**Q379:** What's the most common trim mistake you see on projects?
**A:** Using bright white aluminium trim on warm-toned tiles — the visual clash is jarring. The second most common is omitting trim entirely at exposed edges, leaving raw tile edges that chip and look unfinished. Trim is a design decision, not an afterthought.

**Q380:** Do you have tile-in heating grill covers or vent covers?
**A:** Yes, tile-insert floor vents and grill covers are available. Your floor tile sits within the vent frame, making the ventilation point almost invisible. They're specified for underfloor heating systems and air conditioning floor outlets in high-end residential projects.

**Q381:** How do I choose the right grout width for patterned encaustic tiles?
**A:** Patterned encaustic tiles look best with slightly wider joints (4-5mm) in a contrasting colour that emphasises the geometric pattern. The grid becomes part of the design. For our encaustic-look collections, we recommend charcoal or dark grey grout in a 4mm joint.

**Q382:** Can you supply waterproofing membranes and tanking systems?
**A:** Yes, we stock complete waterproofing systems including tanking membranes, waterproofing compounds, bandage for joints and corners, and primers. A full wet-area specification includes waterproofing, tile adhesive, tile, grout, and sealant — all available from us.

**Q383:** What do you recommend for the junction between tile and freestanding bath?
**A:** A freestanding bath should sit on a perfectly finished tile floor with no sealant visible — the bath's silicone foot seal is hidden underneath. Ensure the floor is perfectly level before tiling, as freestanding baths reveal any floor irregularity. No trim is needed; the bath itself is the transition.


**Q385:** Can I get samples of trim and grout before making final decisions?
**A:** Absolutely. Grout colour samples, trim profile samples, and silicone colour samples are all available for loan. We encourage you to see the complete system together before your installer begins. It's the details that separate good design from great design.

**Q386:** How do I handle the edge where a tiled wall meets a painted wall?
**A:** A shadow-line or recessed junction where the plaster stops 3-5mm short of the tile face creates a clean, designed edge. Alternatively, a thin metal L-profile trim in white or matching the paint provides a crisp termination. The key is intentionality — the junction should look designed, not accidental.

**Q387:** Do you have step-nosing trim for tiled stairs?
**A:** Yes, Schluter step-nosing profiles in aluminium and brass provide slip-resistant, durable edges for tiled staircases. They're essential for safety and protect the tile edge from impact. Various profiles are available depending on the tile thickness and aesthetic preference.

**Q388:** Can you supply the complete specification for my installer — tile, adhesive, grout, trim, and accessories?
**A:** Yes, we provide a comprehensive specification sheet for every project listing all products, quantities, application instructions, and manufacturer references. Your installer receives everything they need in one document — no chasing for missing items or incompatible products.

## Category 10: Social Media & Portfolio Support

**Q389:** Will Tiletoria feature my completed projects on their Instagram or social media?
**A:** Absolutely. We love showcasing designer projects. Send us professional photographs of your completed installation along with the product specification, and we'll feature it across our Instagram, Facebook, and LinkedIn channels with full designer credit and tags. It's mutual amplification.

**Q390:** How do I submit my project for a "Designer Spotlight" feature?
**A:** Send high-res project photographs (minimum 1200px wide), a brief project description, the full Tiletoria product specification, and your practice bio to your liaison. Our marketing team reviews submissions monthly, and selected projects receive a dedicated spotlight post and story series.

**Q391:** Does Tiletoria provide professional photography for completed projects?
**A:** Platinum Design Circle members receive complimentary professional photography for one completed project annually. Gold members receive photography at a subsidised rate. Our photographer specialises in architectural and interior photography, delivering images you can use across your portfolio, website, and social media.

**Q392:** Will you tag my design practice in your social media posts?
**A:** Always. Every project feature includes prominent tagging of your practice's Instagram handle, credit in the caption, and a link to your website where possible. We want our audience to find and follow you. Your creative work deserves attribution.

**Q393:** Can I use Tiletoria's product photography in my own portfolio and social media?
**A:** Yes, our high-res product photography is available for designers to use in mood boards, client presentations, and social media content. Credit @Tiletoria when using our photography publicly. For private client presentations, no attribution is required.

**Q394:** Do you offer "Instagram-worthy" tiles that photograph particularly well?
**A:** Several of our collections are genuinely photogenic — the Moroccan-inspired glazes catch light beautifully, our-look porcelain photographs with authentic depth, and our patterned encaustic tiles create graphic, shareable images. Your liaison can point you to our "most photographed" collections.

**Q395:** Can Tiletoria help promote a project open day or launch event I'm hosting?
**A:** Yes, we'll share your event on our social media channels and designer network if the project features our products. For significant projects, we may co-host or sponsor the event. It's a powerful partnership — your event gains reach, and we celebrate beautiful design.

**Q396:** What's the best way to photograph tiles for my portfolio?
**A:** Shoot in natural light where possible; avoid flash which can create hot spots on glossy tiles. Capture both wide room shots and detail close-ups showing grout lines, trim details, and texture. Include people for scale. Tag us and we'll reshare quality project photography.

**Q397:** Do you have a hashtag I should use when posting projects with your products?
**A:** Use #TiletoriaDesigner and #TiletoriaInYourHome. We monitor these hashtags and regularly repost designer content. It's the easiest way to ensure we see your work and can share it with our community.

**Q398:** Can I get access to your professional product photography library?
**A:** Yes, registered designers have access to our high-resolution product photography library — clean cut-outs, lifestyle shots, installation detail photography, and collection flat lays. Contact your liaison for download access. These assets are perfect for mood boards and client presentations.

**Q399:** Will you write a case study about my project for your website?
**A:** For exceptional projects that showcase our products beautifully, our marketing team creates detailed case studies featuring designer interview, project challenges, product specification rationale, and professional photography. Platinum members' projects receive priority for case study treatment.

**Q400:** Can I feature the Tiletoria brand partnership in my own marketing?
**A:** Yes, you're welcome to reference Tiletoria as a preferred supplier in your practice marketing, tender documents, and client proposals. We can provide a partner logo and brand guidelines on request. We're proud to be associated with leading design practices.

**Q401:** Do you share press coverage or magazine features that mention your products?
**A:** Yes, when our products appear in editorial features, we share the coverage across our channels tagging the designer and publication. If your project is featured in a magazine using our tiles, send us the spread and we'll amplify it digitally.

**Q402:** Can I get video content of my project for my social media?
**A:** For Platinum members, our complimentary photography service includes short-form video — walk-throughs, detail pans, and atmospheric clips — suitable for Instagram Reels and TikTok. Video content significantly outperforms static posts in engagement.

**Q403:** Do you run any social media competitions or collaborations for designers?
**A:** Yes, we periodically run designer-focused social media campaigns — "Best Bathroom of the Quarter," "Mood Board Challenge," and similar collaborations that offer prizes, exposure, and creative engagement. Follow our designer WhatsApp group for announcements.

**Q404:** How can I use your styled vignettes for my own social media content?
**A:** Visit Spec Lab, photograph our vignettes, and share with credit to @Tiletoria. Many designers use vignette photography to illustrate aesthetic concepts to clients who can't visit in person. It's a resource we encourage you to use.

**Q405:** Can Tiletoria introduce me to publications or editors for potential coverage?
**A:** Our PR team has relationships with South African design and lifestyle publications. While we can't guarantee coverage, we can introduce exceptional projects and designers to editors we work with. The combination of great design and our exclusive products makes for compelling editorial content.

**Q406:** Do you have a media kit or press pack I can use?
**A:** Yes, our media kit includes brand story, product highlights, high-res images, designer testimonials, and showroom details. It's useful for publications preparing features or for designers including Tiletoria in their own press materials. Request from your liaison.

**Q407:** Can I get a "supplied by Tiletoria" plaque or sign for my completed project?
**A:** We can provide discreet branded plaques for significant commercial projects — hotel lobbies, restaurant entrances, retail flagships. These are tasteful brass or acrylic plaques that credit the tile supplier without compromising your design. Request through your liaison.

**Q408:** Do you share analytics or engagement data on featured projects?
**A:** For Designer Spotlight features and major project posts, we share engagement analytics — reach, impressions, saves, and story replies. It helps you understand which projects resonate with audiences and informs your own content strategy.

**Q409:** Can I co-create content with Tiletoria — for example, a "behind the design" Reels series?
**A:** We love co-created content. Propose your concept to your liaison — process videos, specification explainers, installation transformations, or designer interviews. Our marketing team will collaborate on production and cross-promote across both our channels.

**Q410:** How do I ensure my project gets maximum visibility when featured?
**A:** Send high-quality photography (minimum 10 images), a compelling project narrative, complete product specification, your practice bio, and any relevant press links. The more complete your submission, the more prominently we can feature your work.

**Q411:** Do you feature emerging designers, or only established practices?
**A:** We feature design excellence regardless of career stage. Some of our most popular posts have been from emerging designers with extraordinary vision. Great design is great design — if your project is beautiful and well-photographed, we want to share it.

**Q412:** Can I use Tiletoria project features in my award submissions?
**A:** Yes, being featured by a leading supplier adds credibility to award submissions. We can provide a feature certificate or screenshot confirming the publication. Award recognition benefits both your practice and our brand — we're invested in your success.

**Q413:** Do you have a Pinterest account where my projects might be pinned?
**A:** Yes, we maintain active Pinterest boards organised by style — "Coastal Bathrooms," "Urban Kitchens," "Hotel Inspiration," etc. When we feature your project on Instagram, it typically gets pinned to the relevant board, creating lasting discoverability beyond the social media feed.

**Q414:** Can I get a round-up of my year's projects that you featured for my annual review?
**A:** At year-end, we can provide a summary of all your projects we featured, with engagement statistics. It's useful for your own marketing review and demonstrates the value of our partnership to your practice partners or investors.

## Category 11: Design Circle Loyalty Program

**Q415:** What is the Design Circle, and how does it work?
**A:** The Design Circle is Tiletoria's loyalty programme for interior designers, architects, and design professionals. Membership is automatic with your trade account and structured in three tiers — Silver, Gold, and Platinum — each offering escalating benefits based on your annual spend.

**Q416:** What are the annual spend thresholds for each Design Circle tier?
**A:** Silver membership is automatic with your trade account — no minimum spend required. Gold tier is achieved at R150,000 annual spend. Platinum tier is achieved at R400,000 annual spend. Tiers are reviewed annually, with mid-year upgrades for significant project milestones.

**Q417:** What discount does each Design Circle tier receive?
**A:** Silver members receive 15% trade discount, Gold members receive 20%, and Platinum members receive 25% on most collections. These discounts apply across our full range — tiles, sanware, accessories, and grout. Volume commercial projects may qualify for additional negotiation.

**Q418:** What benefits come with Gold Design Circle membership?
**A:** Gold benefits include: 20% trade discount, 72-hour advance notice of new collections and sales, priority sample dispatch, subsidised professional photography (one project annually), complimentary courier on sample loans, invitations to Designer Preview Evenings, and a dedicated WhatsApp liaison.

**Q419:** What exclusive benefits do Platinum members receive?
**A:** Platinum benefits include: 25% trade discount, 48-hour exclusive preview of all new collections, complimentary professional photography (one project annually), free courier on all sample loans, private after-hours showroom access, co-hosted client events, factory trip invitations, complimentary mood board creation, and a dedicated senior liaison.

**Q420:** How is my annual spend calculated for tier purposes?
**A:** Your spend is calculated on a rolling 12-month basis — the total value of products purchased (after discount, before VAT) over the previous 12 months. We review tiers quarterly, and if a large project pushes you over a threshold, we upgrade you immediately.

**Q421:** Can multiple designers in my studio combine their spend for tier qualification?
**A:** Yes, a single practice can aggregate spend across all designers on the account. If your studio has three designers each spending R60,000, your combined R180,000 qualifies the practice for Gold tier. All designers on the account enjoy the tier benefits.

**Q422:** What happens if my spend drops below my current tier threshold?
**A:** We review tiers annually. If your spend drops below the threshold, you'll move to the appropriate tier for your actual spend. We don't penalise — we adjust. Many designers' tier moves with their project cycle. You'll always retain Silver membership regardless of spend.

**Q423:** Do Design Circle benefits apply to contractor-mediated purchases?
**A:** Yes, purchases made through your specification count toward your Design Circle tier regardless of procurement method — whether you buy directly, through a linked contractor account, or via a project-specific arrangement. Your liaison tracks all specification-linked purchases.

**Q424:** Can I earn Design Circle tier benefits on personal or residential projects?
**A:** Yes, all purchases on your trade account count toward your tier — personal projects, studio projects, and commercial projects. Many designers use their trade account for their own homes, which helps maintain or advance their tier status.

**Q425:** How do I check my current Design Circle tier and spend?
**A:** Your liaison can provide your current tier status, spend-to-date, and progress toward the next tier at any time. Just send a WhatsApp request. We also include tier information on your monthly statements.

**Q426:** What are "priority previews" of new collections?
**A:** Before any new collection is displayed publicly, Gold and Platinum members receive an exclusive preview — photographs, samples, pricing, and specification details. Platinum members get 48 hours exclusively; Gold members get 72 hours before public launch. It's your competitive advantage in specifying fresh material.

**Q427:** Can I bring a guest to Designer Preview Evenings?
**A:** Silver members may attend solo. Gold members may bring one guest — typically a client or design colleague. Platinum members may bring up to two guests. These intimate events are designed for genuine discovery and conversation, not crowds.

**Q428:** What are the "factory trips" that Platinum members are invited on?
**A:** Our annual factory trips visit tile manufacturers in Italy, Spain, and occasionally Morocco. You'll see the manufacturing process, preview collections in development, meet the artisans, and enjoy curated design experiences. Platinum members receive priority invitation; Gold members may be invited if spaces permit.

**Q429:** Can I downgrade my tier if I don't want to maintain Platinum requirements?
**A:** Tier movement is automatic based on spend. If your spend naturally falls below the threshold, you'll move to the appropriate tier for your actual spend. We don't force maintenance of any tier — the programme rewards actual purchasing, not commitment to spend.

**Q430:** Do Design Circle benefits ever change or get updated?
**A:** We review and enhance the Design Circle programme annually, often adding new benefits based on designer feedback. Any changes are communicated 60 days in advance and always aim to increase value, not reduce it.

**Q431:** Is there a sign-up fee for the Design Circle?
**A:** No, Design Circle membership is complimentary with your trade account. There are no membership fees, annual charges, or hidden costs. It's our way of recognising and rewarding our professional design community.

**Q432:** Can I transfer my Design Circle benefits to another designer if I leave the practice?
**A:** Design Circle benefits are tied to the trade account, not the individual. If you leave a practice, your new practice will need to establish its own trade account and Design Circle tier. We don't transfer tiers between entities.

**Q433:** How do Designer Preview Evenings work?
**A:** These are intimate, after-hours events at Spec Lab where we unveil new collections with canapes, wine, and expert commentary. A maximum of 30 designers attend, ensuring genuine conversation and discovery. Platinum and Gold members receive invitations automatically.

**Q434:** What's included in the "complimentary mood board creation" for Platinum members?
**A:** Our team prepares a professional digital mood board for your project using high-res product photography, cut-outs, and layout software. The mood board is suitable for client presentation and can be printed up to A1 size. Up to two mood boards per project are included.

**Q435:** Can I earn tier benefits faster by paying early or in cash?
**A:** Tier qualification is based on spend volume, not payment method or timing. However, early settlement discounts (2.5% for 7-day payment) improve your overall value, even though they don't affect tier calculation.

**Q436:** What are "co-hosted client events" for Platinum members?
**A:** Platinum members can host client evenings at Spec Lab in partnership with us. We provide the venue, styling, catering, and product curation; you provide the clients and creative direction. It's an elevated client experience that positions your practice as having privileged industry access.

**Q437:** Do Design Circle members get exclusive access to discontinued or clearance stock?
**A:** Yes, before any clearance sale is made public, Design Circle members receive first look at discounted stock. For limited clearance items, this early access can secure extraordinary value on premium imported collections.

**Q438:** Can I use my Design Circle discount on sale items?
**A:** Your Design Circle discount applies to the sale price where applicable — giving you the trade discount on already reduced items. For some clearance lines, the listed price may be final. Your liaison will confirm the pricing for any sale purchase.

**Q439:** How do I maximise my Design Circle benefits?
**A:** Concentrate your specification spend with Tiletoria rather than fragmenting across multiple suppliers. The higher your annual spend, the greater your benefits. Also, take advantage of every service included in your tier — photography, mood boards, preview events — as these have real value.

**Q440:** Is there a Design Circle tier above Platinum for very large practices?
**A:** For practices or developers spending significantly above R1 million annually, we create bespoke partnership agreements beyond the standard Design Circle structure. These include dedicated commercial account management, project-specific pricing, and custom service arrangements. Contact our commercial director to discuss.

**Q441:** Can I gift my Platinum photography benefit to another designer?
**A:** The photography benefit is non-transferable and must be used by the qualifying practice within the membership year. However, if your practice has multiple designers, the photography session can feature any project from the practice.

**Q442:** Do you publish a Design Circle newsletter?
**A:** Yes, our monthly Design Circle newsletter includes new arrivals, trend intelligence, upcoming events, featured designer projects, and exclusive offers. It's distributed by email and WhatsApp to all active Design Circle members.

**Q443:** Can I negotiate custom benefits if the standard tiers don't match my practice's needs?
**A:** For practices with unusual requirements — perhaps very high volume in one product category but low overall — we're open to discussing customised benefit arrangements. Contact your liaison or our commercial director to explore options.

## Category 12: Sanware & Coordinated Fixtures

**Q444:** What sanware brands do you stock?
**A:** We stock premium sanware from our premium tapware range, our tapware range, our tapware range, and our tapware range — covering taps, mixers, showers, bathroom accessories, and bathroom furniture. These brands offer the quality, finish consistency, and warranty support that high-end specifications demand.

**Q445:** Can I coordinate my tile specification with matching sanware finishes?
**A:** Absolutely. Our sanware ranges offer finishes in chrome, brushed brass, matte black, brushed nickel, and gunmetal — and we can recommend tiles that harmonise with each. A brushed brass tap against warm beige terrazzo, or matte black mixers on charcoal concrete-look walls — the coordination elevates the whole bathroom.

**Q446:** Do you have brushed brass tapware? It's very on-trend.
**A:** Yes, brushed brass is one of our most requested finishes across our premium tapware range and our tapware range ranges. It offers the warmth of gold without the ostentation, and it develops a beautiful patina over time. We also stock matching brushed brass accessories — towel rails, toilet roll holders, and soap dispensers.

**Q447:** What's the difference between our premium tapware range and our tapware range?
**A:** our premium tapware range is a premium international brand known for engineering excellence, innovative technology (like SmartControl showers), and extensive finish options. our tapware range offers excellent quality at a more accessible price point with a strong local warranty and service network. Both are specified regularly by our designer community.

**Q448:** Can I get matte black tapware and accessories?
**A:** Yes, matte black is available across our premium tapware range, our tapware range, and our tapware range ranges. It's a defining finish of contemporary bathroom design. We recommend pairing matte black sanware with large-format light-toned tiles for maximum contrast and visual impact.

**Q449:** Do you stock freestanding baths?
**A:** Yes, we carry a curated selection of freestanding baths in acrylic and stone resin. The sculptural quality of a freestanding bath is often the centrepiece of a luxury bathroom design. Your liaison can provide dimensional drawings and installation requirements.

**Q450:** Can I see the sanware installed in your bathroom vignettes?
**A:** Yes, our Spec Lab bathroom vignettes display fully functional sanware installations — taps running, showers operating, accessories mounted. You can test the water flow, feel the handle operation, and assess the finish quality in person. A catalogue can't replace this experience.

**Q451:** What's the warranty on your sanware ranges?
**A:** our premium tapware range offers a 5-year warranty, our tapware range offers a 5-year warranty, and our tapware range offers a 3-year warranty, all against manufacturing defects. Warranty requires correct installation by a qualified plumber and registration of the product. Your liaison provides warranty documentation with every order.

**Q452:** Do you have matching shower drains and floor drains?
**A:** Yes, we stock linear drains, square drains, and tile-insert drains in finishes to match our sanware ranges — chrome, brushed brass, matte black, and stainless steel. The drain is a small detail that becomes visible when it's mismatched; coordination matters.

**Q453:** Can I get bathroom furniture — vanities, mirrors, cabinets?
**A:** Yes, our bathroom furniture range includes vanities in various sizes and finishes, LED mirrors with demister pads, mirror cabinets, and storage units. The furniture is designed to coordinate with our sanware ranges for a complete bathroom specification.

**Q454:** Do you have rimless toilets?
**A:** Yes, rimless toilet designs are available across our ranges. The rimless design is more hygienic — water flushes the entire bowl surface without the hidden rim where bacteria collect. It's become the standard specification for luxury bathrooms.

**Q455:** Can I coordinate my shower door or frame with my tapware finish?
**A:** We stock frameless and semi-frameless shower screens with hardware in chrome, brushed brass, matte black, and brushed nickel — matching our tapware finishes exactly. A matte black frameless shower with matte black taps is a cohesive, considered specification.

**Q456:** What's the lead time for sanware orders?
**A:** In-stock sanware delivers within 3-5 days. Imported our premium tapware range specials may take 3-4 weeks. For large commercial projects, we can arrange forward ordering to ensure all items arrive simultaneously. Your liaison confirms availability at specification stage.

**Q457:** Do you have water-saving options for eco-conscious clients?
**A:** Yes, our premium tapware range EcoJoy range reduces water consumption by up to 50% without compromising performance. All our showers and mixers are rated for WELS compliance. We can provide flow rate data for green building certification requirements.

**Q458:** Can I get sanware samples to show my client?
**A:** We provide finish chips — small samples of brushed brass, matte black, chrome, and other finishes — for client presentation. For larger pieces, your client can visit Spec Lab to see full-size installations. Some tap models are available as display loans for VIP presentations.

**Q459:** Do you have advice on specifying thermostatic mixer showers?
**A:** Thermostatic mixers maintain constant water temperature regardless of pressure fluctuations — essential for family homes, hospitality, and any installation where scalding risk must be minimised. our premium tapware range's thermostatic technology is industry-leading and worth the investment for safety and comfort.

**Q460:** Can you supply the complete bathroom — tiles, sanware, accessories, and furniture?
**A:** Yes, we offer complete bathroom specification from floor to ceiling. Tiles, grout, trim, taps, showers, toilets, basins, baths, furniture, mirrors, accessories, and even waterproofing systems. One supplier, one delivery, one warranty contact. It simplifies specification and reduces coordination risk.

**Q461:** What accessories do you stock — towel rails, toilet roll holders, robe hooks?
**A:** Our accessory ranges include towel rails (heated and unheated), towel rings, toilet roll holders, robe hooks, soap dispensers, soap dishes, toothbrush holders, and grab bars. All are available in matching finishes to coordinate with your tapware specification.

**Q462:** Do you have sensor taps for commercial bathrooms?
**A:** Yes, sensor-activated taps are available for commercial and hospitality specifications. They reduce water consumption, improve hygiene, and meet accessibility requirements. Battery and mains-powered options are available in our premium tapware range and our tapware range commercial ranges.

**Q463:** Can I specify underfloor heating with my tile order?
**A:** While we don't manufacture underfloor heating systems, we can recommend compatible systems and supply the installation accessories — thermally rated adhesives, expansion joint covers, and floor sensors. Porcelain tiles are excellent conductors of underfloor heat.

**Q464:** What's the price range for your our premium tapware range tapware at trade pricing?
**A:** our premium tapware range basin mixers start around R3,500 at trade pricing, with premium SmartControl shower systems reaching R25,000+. our tapware range offers excellent value from R1,500 for basin mixers. Your liaison provides a detailed trade price list for specification.

**Q465:** Do you have disabled access and universal design sanware options?
**A:** Yes, our ranges include lever-handle mixers (easier grip than crossheads), thermostatic controls, comfort-height toilets, grab bars in matching finishes, and walk-in shower solutions. Universal design doesn't mean institutional — our accessible options are beautifully designed.

**Q466:** Can I get spare parts for sanware years after installation?
**A:** our premium tapware range and our tapware range maintain spare parts availability for current ranges for at least 10 years after discontinuation. We can source cartridges, aerators, handles, and other replacement parts through our supplier network. Keep your installation records for easy parts identification.

**Q467:** Do you have installation guides for your sanware?
**A:** Every sanware product comes with a manufacturer installation guide. For complex installations — concealed mixers, rain shower systems, wet room drainage — we recommend qualified plumbers experienced with the brand. Your liaison can recommend certified installers.

**Q468:** Can I match my kitchen tap to my bathroom tapware?
**A:** Yes, all our sanware ranges offer kitchen mixers in the same designs and finishes as their bathroom counterparts. Specifying a consistent tapware family throughout the home is a hallmark of considered design. Your liaison will identify the matching kitchen mixer for your bathroom choice.

**Q469:** What's the difference between a concealed and exposed mixer?
**A:** Concealed mixers have the valve body hidden in the wall, with only the control plate and outlet visible — the minimal, contemporary choice. Exposed mixers have the full valve body mounted on the wall — easier to install and service, with a more traditional aesthetic. The design intent drives the choice.

**Q470:** Do you have rain shower heads?
**A:** Yes, overhead rain showers are available from 200mm to 400mm diameter in round and square profiles. The immersive rainfall experience has become standard in luxury bathrooms. Consider ceiling-mounted installation for the most minimal, integrated look.

**Q471:** Can I get LED mirrors with demister and Bluetooth?
**A:** Yes, our LED mirror range includes options with integrated demister pads (no post-shower fog), touch controls, colour temperature adjustment, and Bluetooth speakers. They're the intersection of bathroom technology and design aesthetics.

**Q472:** Do you have bidet fittings or washlets?
**A:** Yes, we stock bidet spray kits and can source our premium tapware range Sensia washlets — the bidet toilet seat with integrated washing, drying, and heating functions. Washlets are increasingly specified in luxury bathrooms for hygiene and comfort.

**Q473:** How do I specify a complete shower system — mixer, head, hand shower, and body jets?
**A:** Your liaison creates a shower system specification showing all components: concealed thermostatic mixer, overhead rain shower, hand shower on slide rail, body jets (if required), and the correct rough-in dimensions for each. It's a system, not just a collection of parts.

**Q474:** Can I get sanware in custom finishes — rose gold, for example?
**A:** While our standard finishes cover most design requirements, our premium tapware range offers some models in special-order finishes including rose gold and bespoke PVD coatings. Lead times are 6-8 weeks and minimum quantities may apply. Your liaison can explore custom options.

**Q475:** Do you have floor-mounted bath mixers for freestanding baths?
**A:** Yes, floor-standing bath fillers are available in chrome, brushed brass, matte black, and brushed nickel. They're the elegant accompaniment to a freestanding bath — a sculptural element in their own right. Floor-mounted mixers require specific plumbing rough-in.

**Q476:** What's the maintenance requirement for brushed brass and matte black finishes?
**A:** Brushed brass develops a natural patina over time — some clients love this living finish; others prefer the original brightness (easily restored with brass cleaner). Matte black is powder-coated and simply wipes clean with mild detergent. Both are far lower maintenance than chrome, which shows water spots.

**Q477:** Can I get a "bathroom package" price if I'm specifying the complete room?
**A:** For full bathroom specifications — tiles, sanware, furniture, and accessories — we can package pricing that reflects the complete-project value. Your liaison will identify any additional discounting available for comprehensive specifications.

**Q478:** Do you have commercial-grade sanware for hotel and office bathrooms?
**A:** Yes, our commercial sanware ranges include sensor taps, time-flow mixers, robust WC suites designed for high-frequency use, and vandal-resistant accessories. These meet SANS compliance for public facilities while maintaining design credibility. our premium tapware range's commercial division offers specification support for large projects.

## Category 13: porcelain tile alternatives & Specialty Materials

**A:**, porcelain tile, and porcelain tile. Each stone has distinct characteristics — veining in, grain in, porosity in— and our stone specialist helps you select the right material for your application.

**Q480:** Do you have-look porcelainor-look porcelain?
**A:** Yes, we import both-look porcelain (soft grey veining on white)-look porcelain (bold grey veining on bright white)from Italy. These are the benchmark stones of luxury interior design. Availability varies by slab size and current stock; your liaison will show you available selection.

**Q481:** What's the difference between honed and polished?
**A:** Polishedhas a glossy, reflective surface that deepens the colour and shows the veining dramatically. Honedhas a satin, matte surface that's more subtle and hides scratches better. For bathroom floors, honed is safer (less slippery) and more practical.


**Q483:** Can I get a sample of sealed and unsealed stone to show my client the difference?
**A:** Absolutely. We provide paired samples showing the same stone in sealed and unsealed condition. The sealed sample is slightly darker and more vibrant; the unsealed sample is lighter and more porous. It's the most effective way to explain sealing to clients.

**Q484:** What's the maintenance commitment forbathroom floors?
**A:** porcelain tile flooring require regular cleaning with pH-neutral stone cleaner (never vinegar or acidic products), prompt wiping of spills (especially wine, citrus, and oils), and periodic re-sealing. For clients who lovebut fear maintenance, our-look porcelain offers the aesthetic without the commitment.

**Q485:** Do you have book-matchedslabs for feature walls?
**A:** Yes, book-matchedslabs are available for dramatic feature walls, fireplace surrounds, and bathroom vanities. Book-matching creates a mirror-image effect across adjacent slabs — the veining flows continuously from one slab to the next. It's extraordinary and requires expert installation.

**A:** Yes, with correct specification. Use honed (not polished) stone for slip resistance, ensure the stone is adequately sealed, install with waterproofing and appropriate adhesive, and advise the client on maintenance. Some stones — particularly— perform beautifully in showers and develop character over time.


**Q488:** Do you supplyfor kitchen countertops?

**Q489:** Can I get stone mosaics — herringbone, basket-weave, hexagon?

**Q490:** What is, and where is it best used?
**A:**is a form of porcelain tile with distinctive pitting and natural cavities that give it rustic, warm character. It's ideal for floors, walls, and outdoor paving in Mediterranean, farmhouse, and warm contemporary interiors. The holes can be filled for a smoother surface or left unfilled for authentic texture.

**Q491:** Do you havefor floors and roofs?
**A:** Yes, we supply tile surfaces tiles in various colours — Brazilian grey-green, African black, and multi-colour rustic.is extraordinarily durable and has a natural cleft surface that provides excellent slip resistance.


**Q493:** What's the difference between your-look porcelain and real?
**A:** Realis a natural material with unique veining, depth, and patina that develops over time. It requires sealing and maintenance.-look porcelain captures the aesthetic with perfect consistency, zero maintenance, and lower cost. For clients who value authenticity, specify real stone. For clients who value practicality, specify porcelain.

**Q494:** Do you supply stone for outdoor use — patios, pool surrounds, and driveways?
**A:** Yes, and porcelain tile pavers for outdoor applications. These must be rated for exterior use — frost-resistant, non-slip when wet, and UV-stable. Your liaison will specify the correct thickness (typically 20-30mm for outdoor paving) and installation detail.

**Q495:** Can I get custom-cut stone shapes — circular table tops, curved vanity tops?
**A:** Yes, through our fabrication partners we offer custom cutting, edging, and finishing of stone slabs. Circular table tops, curved vanity tops, shower trays, and bespoke fireplace hearths are all possible. Templates and lead times (typically 2-3 weeks) apply.

**A:** Italiantypically take 8-10 weeks from order. Indianare usually 4-6 weeks. For urgent requirements, we sometimes have selected stone in stock at our warehouses. Your liaison confirms availability before quoting.

**Q497:** Do you haveor semi-precious stone options?
**A:** Yes, we can sourceslabs — extraordinary translucent stone that backlights beautifully for bar counters, feature walls, and bathroom vanities.is a statement material for clients who want something truly unique. Pricing is available on application due to the bespoke nature of each slab.

**Q498:** Can I visit your stone yard to select specific slabs?
**A:** Yes, our stone yard is open to registered designers by appointment. Selecting specific slabs is essential for— every slab is unique. Bring your client for the selection process; it's an experience that builds appreciation for natural material.

**Q499:** What's the best stone for a kitchen that will actually be cooked in?
**A:** For serious cookingis the practical choice — it withstands heat, resists acidic spills (wine, citrus, tomatoes), and doesn't scratch easily. If your client insists on, specify a honed finish and educate them about cutting boards, trivets, and prompt spill management.

**Q500:** Do you have pebble mosaics for shower floors or feature walls?
**A:** Yes, our pebble mosaic range includes river pebble, sliced pebble, and polished stone pebble in various colours. They create extraordinary texture underfoot in showers and stunning feature walls. The tactile quality is unmatched — a genuine spa-like experience.

**Q501:** Is it worth paying more for through-body veining in a-look rectified tile?

**Q502:** Do you stock large-format stone-look tiles in 1200×600mm?
**A:** Yes, 1200×600mm has become the standard large-format dimension for stone-effect porcelain, and we stock extensive ranges in this size. Calibrated for rectified joints, they create a beautifully seamless visual field with minimal grout interruption. You'll find, and porcelain tile effects in this format, available for immediate collection or project delivery.

**Q503:** What's the difference between porcelain and ceramic tiles, really?
**A:** Porcelain tiles are fired at higher temperatures using finer clay, making them denser, less porous, and far stronger than ceramic. A porcelain tile absorbs less than 0.5% water, meaning it's suitable for wet areas, outdoor use, and underfloor heating. Ceramic tiles are fine for walls and dry residential floors but lack the structural integrity and freeze-thaw resistance that porcelain offers. For designer specifications, porcelain is generally the safer default.

**Q504:** Can I specify the same tile for both floor and wall to create visual continuity?
**A:** Yes, many of our rectified porcelain collections are dual-rated for floor and wall use. This is a powerful design move — running the same surface from bathroom floor up the wall behind a freestanding bath creates a spa-like visual envelope. Just confirm the slip rating is appropriate for the floor application. Our consultants can help you select ranges that work beautifully as a unified surface treatment.

**Q505:** Do you have tiles that look like poured concrete?
**A:** Yes, industrial-inspired concrete-effect tiles are one of our strongest design-driven categories. We stock ranges that replicate everything from smooth polished concrete to board-formed cast textures. Sizes typically run 600×600mm up to 1200×600mm, and the colour palette spans warm grey through cool charcoal and even tinted tones. The realism in the digital print is remarkable — subtle aggregate detail, hairline cracks, and tonal variation.

**Q506:** What's a lapato finish, and when would I specify it?
**A:** A lapato finish is semi-polished — the tile undergoes a light polishing process that removes the matte surface texture without reaching full gloss. It sits beautifully between matte and polished, catching light softly and creating subtle luminosity. Specify lapato when you want a refined, luxurious feel but don't want the high-maintenance reflectivity of a full polished surface. It's particularly effective in living spaces and boutique hospitality.

**Q507:** Are your tiles suitable for underfloor heating?
**A:** Yes, all porcelain and ceramic tiles are compatible with underfloor heating systems. Porcelain is an excellent thermal conductor, making it the ideal floor finish over heated screeds. We recommend working with your heating installer to ensure the subfloor build-up, insulation, and expansion joints are properly engineered. Tiles with through-body colour won't be affected by the thermal cycling.

**Q508:** Do you offer any tiles that replicate natural materials like terrazzo or microcement?
**A:** Yes, both terrazzo-effect and microcement-look tiles are growing categories in our showroom. Terrazzo-effect porcelain replicates the classic chip-in-matrix look in large formats, eliminating the maintenance issues of real terrazzo. Microcement-look tiles offer the seamless, hand-trowelled aesthetic without the cracking or sealing concerns. These are popular choices for contemporary residential and commercial interiors where material authenticity meets practical durability.

**Q509:** What is rectification, and why does it matter for my design work?
**A:** Rectification is a mechanical process where each tile edge is precisely ground to ensure all tiles in a batch are exactly the same size. This allows for tight, consistent grout joints — typically 1.5mm to 2mm — creating a sleek, seamless appearance that non-rectified tiles cannot achieve. For interior designers, rectification is essential whenever you're creating a refined, contemporary look where the tile surface, not the joint, should read as the dominant visual element.

**Q510:** Can I mix different tile sizes from the same collection in one room?
**A:** Yes, many of our collections are designed as modular systems with multiple sizes that work together — 600×600mm, 600×300mm, and 300×300mm from the same design family, for example. This creates a cohesive yet visually dynamic floor layout. Herringbone with the 600×300mm format, border with the 300×300mm, and field with the 600×600mm — all reading as one unified surface story.

**Q511:** Do you stock tiles with a textured, three-dimensional surface?
**A:** Yes, three-dimensional relief tiles are an important part of our offering. These range from subtle wave or linear textures to more dramatic geometric relief patterns. They're typically specified as feature walls, headboard backdrops, or reception focal points where light and shadow create living surface interest. Many are rectified and suitable for both residential and commercial interiors.

**Q512:** What are your most popular feature tiles for designer projects?
**A:** Our most specified feature tiles include Moroccan-inspired zellige-effect glazed tiles, handcrafted-look subway tiles with undulating surfaces, bold geometric encaustic-look patterns in 200×200mm, and three-dimensional relief tiles in concrete or stone-effect finishes. The common thread is that each creates a focal moment — behind a vanity, as a kitchen splashback, or as a fireplace surround — that anchors the design narrative.

**Q513:** Do you have tiles suitable for outdoor patio and entertainment areas?
**A:** Yes, we carry porcelain pavers designed specifically for outdoor applications. These are thicker (typically 20mm), fully vitrified, and resistant to thermal shock and frost. We stock stone-effect, concrete-effect, and wood-effect outdoor pavers that flow seamlessly from interior to exterior — a design move that extends the living space visually and functionally. Our team can advise on pedestal systems if you're working over a membrane or uneven substrate.


**Q516:** Do you stock mosaics for feature walls and wet areas?

**Q517:** Can I get large-format tiles in a handmade, artisanal look?
**A:** Yes, the zellige-effect and artisan ranges we stock capture the irregular edges, tonal variation, and glazed character of handmade tiles but in formats up to 300×900mm and beyond. This gives you the best of both worlds — the warmth and imperfection of artisan ceramics with the installation efficiency and dimensional stability of modern manufacturing. Specify these for splashbacks, vanity walls, and anywhere you want light to dance across a living surface.

**Q518:** Do you have tiles that work well with warm timber interiors?
**A:** Absolutely. For timber-rich interiors, we recommend warm-toned stone-effect tiles —, porcelain tile, and warm— alongside natural concrete-effect tiles in taupe and sand tones. Wood-effect porcelain itself can complement real timber beautifully, extending the material language into wet areas like bathrooms. Our consultants can help you match tile undertones to your timber species for a harmonious palette.

**Q519:** What tiles would you recommend for a client who wants a Mediterranean feel?

**Q520:** Do you stock tiles with a metallic finish?
**A:** Yes, metallic-effect tiles are available in our range — from subtle brushed steel and champagne bronze through to more dramatic aged copper and gunmetal. These work beautifully as feature strips, vanity splashbacks, or as accent elements within a larger neutral scheme. The metallic glaze catches light dynamically, adding movement and luxury to otherwise restrained interiors.

**Q521:** What's the best tile for a steam room or sauna area?

**Q522:** Do you have tiles specifically designed for kitchen splashbacks?
**A:** Yes, splashback tiles are a major category for us. Subway tiles (both flat and bevelled), zellige-effect glazes, geometric patterns, and glass mosaics are all popular splashback choices. The key considerations are ease of cleaning (gloss or semi-gloss glazes wipe clean easily), resistance to grease and heat, and of course visual impact — the splashback is often the personality piece in a kitchen.


**Q524:** Do you offer tiles with an anti-bacterial or hygienic coating?
**A:** Some of the porcelain tiles we stock incorporate anti-microbial surface treatments, particularly in ranges marketed for healthcare and food-service applications. For general residential and hospitality projects, the inherent properties of glazed porcelain — non-porous, easy to clean, resistant to mould and bacteria — provide excellent hygiene without additional coatings. Our team can point you to ranges with enhanced hygienic properties if your project requires them.

**A:** Honed stone has a smooth, matte finish achieved by grinding the surface without polishing. It feels velvety, doesn't show scratches or etching as readily as polished stone, and offers a more understated, contemporary look. Polished stone has a glossy, reflective surface that maximises colour depth and veining drama but is more prone to showing wear and water spots. For most modern interiors, honed is the safer and more sophisticated choice.

**Q526:** Do you stock tiles suitable for swimming pools and water features?
**A:** Yes, we carry a dedicated range of pool tiles — glass mosaics, porcelain mosaics, and stone-look tiles designed for immersed applications. These are frost-resistant, chemically resistant to pool water treatments, and available in colours specifically chosen to look beautiful under water. Our pool tile range includes classic Mediterranean blues, contemporary aquamarines, and sophisticated greys and blacks.

**Q527:** Can I get tiles that look like traditional encaustic cement tiles?
**A:** Yes, encaustic-look porcelain tiles are one of our most popular designer categories. They replicate the bold, multi-colour patterns of traditional hydraulic cement tiles but in a far more durable, lower-maintenance porcelain body. Available in 200×200mm and larger formats, they bring instant pattern and heritage character to floors and walls without the sealing and staining concerns of real cement tiles.

**Q528:** Do you have tiles with a wood-look that can be used in bathrooms?
**A:** Yes, wood-effect porcelain is one of our most specified categories for bathrooms. It delivers the warmth and grain character of timber in a material that's completely waterproof, stain-resistant, and compatible with underfloor heating. Plank formats — 1200×200mm, 900×150mm — laid in herringbone or staggered patterns create stunning bathroom floors and even shower walls. No more warping, rotting, or water damage.

## Category 14: Trend Forecasts & Design Authority

**Q529:** What tile trends are you seeing emerge for South African interiors in 2025?
**A:** We're seeing several converging trends: oversized formats with minimal grout lines creating seamless surfaces; warm, earthy tones replacing the long-dominant cool greys; handmade and artisan-look tiles with intentional imperfection; stone-effect porcelain that reads genuinely likeor; and biophilic design connecting interiors to nature through organic textures and natural colour palettes. Texture is king — designers are moving beyond flat surfaces to tiles with relief, tactility, and light-reactive qualities.

**Q530:** Is grey still a safe colour choice, or are warmer tones taking over?
**A:** While grey remains a versatile neutral, the market has definitively shifted toward warmer undertones — greige, taupe, warm sand, and mushroom. These tones feel more organic, more welcoming, and better connected to the South African landscape. That said, charcoalgrey still have their place in industrial and contemporary schemes. The key is choosing greys with warm undertones rather than cold blue-greys. Our consultants can guide you through the tonal shifts in our current collections.

**Q531:** What's the next big thing in bathroom design for South African homes?

**Q532:** Are large-format tiles a trend or a permanent shift?
**A:** Large-format tiles represent a permanent structural shift in the industry, not a passing trend. Once clients experience the seamless, expansive look of 1200×600mm or larger tiles with minimal grout lines, there's no going back. The manufacturing technology has matured, the price premium has narrowed, and the aesthetic benefits — fewer joints, easier maintenance, more dramatic material statement — are permanent. We expect formats to keep growing, with 1600×800mm and 2400×1200mm becoming more accessible.

**Q533:** What's happening in the world of grout — are coloured grouts becoming popular?
**A:** Coloured grout is absolutely having a moment. Designers are using grout as a design element rather than an invisible necessity — matching grout to tile for a seamless monolithic look, or contrasting dark grout with light tiles for a graphic, grid-like statement. Epoxy grouts in particular are available in a wide colour range and offer the added benefit of stain resistance. Our team can show you examples of both approaches in our displays.

**Q534:** Are patterned tiles still on trend, or has minimalism taken over completely?
**A:** Patterned tiles remain very much on trend, but their application has evolved. Rather than covering entire floors, they're now used more surgically — a feature strip, a splashback, a fireplace surround, or an entrance foyer moment. The pattern itself has also shifted from busy, multi-colour encaustic to more restrained geometric and linear patterns in limited palettes. Pattern hasn't disappeared; it's simply become more considered and intentional in its use.

**Q535:** What colour palettes are trending for kitchen splashbacks right now?
**A:** Current kitchen splashback palettes favour either bold singular moments or whisper-quiet continuity. Bold options include deep emerald green zellige, cobalt blue subway, and terracotta geometric patterns. Quiet options include large-format stone-look tiles that continue the countertop material up the wall, or barely-there cream and ivory glazes that disappear into the architecture. The common thread is intentionality — the splashback is no longer an afterthought.

**Q536:** Is biophilic design influencing tile selection?
**A:** Biophilic design — connecting interior spaces to nature — is profoundly influencing tile selection. We're seeing strong demand for stone-effect porcelain that references natural geology, organic textures that mimic weathered surfaces, earth-toned palettes drawn from the South African landscape, and formats that minimise the manufactured grid in favour of expansive, landscape-like surfaces. Even in urban apartments, clients want their floors and walls to carry the memory of natural material.

**Q537:** What's the trend in bathroom tapware finishes for 2025?
**A:** The era of chrome dominance is over. Brushed brass and gunmetal black are now the default choices for high-end bathroom specifications, with chrome reserved for classic or minimalist schemes where it reads as a deliberate choice rather than a default. Rose gold and brushed nickel have their niches. The real trend is mixed metals — brass taps with black shower frames, for instance — creating a layered, collected feel rather than a matched set.

**Q538:** Are terrazzo tiles making a comeback?
**A:** Terrazzo never really left, but it's enjoying a significant resurgence in contemporary South African interiors. Modern terrazzo-effect porcelain replicates the classic look in large formats with far greater practicality. The palette has also evolved — beyond traditional grey and white bases, we're seeing terrazzo in blush pink, forest green, and charcoal bases with oversized aggregate chips. It works beautifully in bathrooms, kitchens, and commercial spaces where personality meets durability.

**Q539:** What's the future of tile technology — what innovations should I be aware of?
**A:** Several innovations are reshaping the industry: digital glaze technology is achieving photorealistic stone and wood replication; thin large-format panels (3-6mm thick, up to 3m lengths) are enabling tile to be used on walls, furniture, and even door skins; anti-slip nano-coatings are improving safety without sacrificing aesthetics; and sustainability certifications are becoming standard. At Tiletoria, we actively curate our portfolio to include these technological advances as they mature.

**Q540:** Are cement-look tiles still popular, or are they being replaced?
**A:** Cement-look tiles remain extremely popular, but they've evolved from the industrial raw-cement aesthetic to softer, warmer interpretations — polished concrete in greige tones, board-formed concrete with timber grain impressions, and microcement-look surfaces with hand-trowelled character. The industrial edge has softened; concrete-look now reads as sophisticated and warm rather than cold and utilitarian.

**Q541:** What's the trend in outdoor-indoor flow for South African homes?
**A:** The outdoor-indoor connection is a defining characteristic of premium South African residential design. We're seeing interior porcelain formats extended to covered patios and entertainment areas using thicker 20mm pavers, consistent flooring creating visual continuity, and complementary but distinct outdoor collections that reference the interior palette. The goal is a home that breathes — where the boundary between inside and outside becomes almost imperceptible.

**Q542:** Are bold colours making a comeback in tile design?
**A:** Yes, but selectively. After years of grey and beige dominance, we're seeing confident use of colour in specific applications — deep green vanity walls, midnight blue shower enclosures, terracotta and ochre feature walls. The difference from previous colourful eras is that these bold choices are now paired with disciplined, neutral surrounds. One bold tile moment per room, anchored by a restrained palette elsewhere.

**Q543:** What's happening with tile texture — are smooth tiles out?
**A:** Smooth tiles aren't out, but textured tiles are definitely having their moment. Designers are drawn to surfaces that engage the senses — linen-effect textures, chiselled stone surfaces, three-dimensional geometric relief, and hand-formed undulations. These textures add warmth, shadow, and tactile interest that smooth tiles cannot provide. For living spaces, textured tiles create a sense of crafted luxury.

**Q544:** How are South African designers approaching sustainability in tile specification?

**Q545:** What's the trend in hotel and boutique lodge design for tiles?
**A:** Boutique hospitality is moving toward residential warmth — large-format stone-look tiles, timber-look porcelain in guest rooms, zellige-effect tiles in bathrooms, and curated feature moments that tell a local story. The cookie-cutter hotel look is out; guests expect spaces that feel personal, textured, and place-specific. We work with hospitality designers to curate palettes that create this sense of intimate luxury at scale.

**Q546:** Are black tiles becoming more popular?

**Q547:** What tile choices define a luxury bathroom in 2025?
**A:** A luxury bathroom in 2025 is defined by: large-format rectified tiles with through-body veining (oreffect); freestanding sculptural bath as a centrepiece; backlit mirror or vanity mirror with integrated lighting; brushed brass or matte black tapware; underfloor heating; a recessed shower niche tiled in a complementary material; frameless glass shower enclosure; and considered lighting that creates atmosphere. Every element is deliberate, and the materials read as high-end even at close inspection.

**Q548:** Is the subway tile trend finally over?
**A:** Subway tiles have evolved rather than ended. The classic 100×200mm white glossy subway will always have its place, but the trend has expanded to include: elongated formats (75×300mm, 100×400mm); bevelled surfaces that catch light; handmade-look undulating glazes; and bold colours beyond white. Subway tiles are no longer a default safe choice — they're a deliberate design selection with many expressive variations.

**Q549:** What's the trend in commercial office design for tiles?
**A:** Commercial offices are moving away from generic corporate finishes toward spaces that feel more residential and hospitality-inspired. Polished concrete-look large-format tiles, timber-look planks in warm tones, and stone-effect porcelain in neutral palettes are replacing traditional commercial carpet and vinyl. Reception areas in particular are becoming more dramatic, with feature walls in three-dimensional tiles or bold patterns that communicate brand identity.

**Q550:** Are mosaic tiles still relevant in contemporary design?
**A:** Mosaics are very much alive, but their application has become more targeted. Rather than entire bathroom walls, they're now used for: shower floor inserts (small format for drainage slope); niche backings that create a jewelled moment; feature strips in contrasting materials; and pool interiors where glass mosaics create extraordinary water colour effects. Mosaics remain essential for wet-area functionality even as their decorative use has become more considered.

**Q551:** What's the trend in fireplace design and surrounding materials?

**Q552:** How are designers using tiles to create feature moments?
**A:** The most effective feature moments are those that feel inevitable rather than arbitrary — a zellige splashback that picks up the kitchen island colour, a-effect feature wall behind a freestanding bath that extends the floor material upward, a patterned tile entrance foyer that sets the tone for the home. The key principle is that the feature tile should relate to the broader palette while providing a concentrated moment of personality.

**Q553:** What's trending in retail and restaurant interior design for tiles?
**A:** Retail and restaurant interiors are using tiles to create memorable, Instagram-worthy moments — bold patterned floors, three-dimensional feature walls, and material transitions that guide customer flow. Durability is paramount in these high-traffic spaces, so designers are selecting commercial-grade porcelain with high PEI ratings while still achieving distinctive aesthetic outcomes. The Tiletoria hospitality team works closely with designers on these specifications.

**Q554:** Are wall tiles different from floor tiles, and is the distinction blurring?
**A:** The distinction is definitely blurring. Many of our rectified porcelain collections are rated for both floor and wall use, allowing designers to create continuous visual planes that flow seamlessly from floor to wall to ceiling. That said, floor tiles require slip resistance (particularly in wet areas) and greater structural strength, so not all wall tiles can transition to the floor. Our consultants will always confirm the suitability rating for your intended application.

**Q555:** What should I know about tile trends in the global market versus South Africa?
**A:** South Africa tends to follow European trends with a 12-24 month lag, but with local adaptations. European minimalism trans here into a preference for warmth over cold austerity. Italian-effect porcelain is universally popular. The key difference is climate — our warmer conditions mean outdoor-indoor flow, pool areas, and alfresco living are far more important here than in Northern European markets. We curate our collections with these local conditions in mind.

**Q556:** Where does Tiletoria publish trend content that I can share with my clients?
**A:** We publish regular trend reports, new collection launches, and design inspiration through our social media channels, email newsletters, and in-showroom lookbooks. Platinum Design Circle members receive advance access to new collections and trend previews. Our showroom displays themselves are trend capsules — visiting the Spec Lab is one of the best ways to stay current. Ask your consultant about our design inspiration resources.

## Category 15: Comparison with Mass Retailers

**Q557:** How is Tiletoria different from buying tiles at a mass retail hardware store?

**Q558:** Are your prices competitive with major retail chains?

**Q559:** Do you stock the same brands that are available at retail stores?
**A:** Some overlap exists at the entry level, but our exclusive and premium ranges are not available through mass retail channels. We work directly with manufacturers in Italy, Spain, Turkey, and other production centres to import collections specifically curated for the design market. If you're looking for products that differentiate your work from what every retail shopper can buy, our exclusive ranges are where you'll find them.

**Q560:** Can my clients buy Tiletoria products directly if they find our showroom?
**A:** Our trade-focused model means that while we do sell to the public, our pricing structure, service model, and product curation are optimised for professional designers, architects, and contractors. Many of our premium ranges and all trade discounts are available exclusively through registered trade accounts. If you're concerned about retail access undercutting your specification, speak to us — we understand the importance of protecting your design integrity and procurement relationship.

**Q561:** Why should I specify from Tiletoria rather than letting my client shop retail?
**A:** When you specify from Tiletoria, you gain access to curated, design-forward collections that aren't available at retail; you benefit from trade pricing that protects your margin; you receive technical support and specification guidance; you have a dedicated point of contact for project coordination; and you avoid the risk of your client substituting an inferior retail product that compromises your design. Your specification is your reputation — it deserves the support of a professional supplier.

**Q562:** Do retail stores offer the same technical advice and installation support?

**Q563:** Is there a difference in product quality between your tiles and retail tiles?

**Q564:** Do you offer bulk or project pricing that retail stores can't match?
