**Q936:** Do you match competitor rates?

**A936:** We offer a **rate match guarantee**: if a competitor quotes a lower rate for an identical product (same manufacturer, same specification, same delivery terms), we match the rate and add 10% credit on your first order. Subject to verification of the competitor quotation. This ensures you always get the best rate through Tiletoria.

**Q937:** How does your warranty compare to competitor offerings?

**A937:** Our **5-year system warranty** (10-year for premium waterproofing) exceeds typical competitor warranties of 12 months (material only) or no warranty at all. The warranty is **single-point** — one call for any system issue regardless of which component failed.

**Q938:** What is your client retention rate vs. competitors?

**A938:** Tiletoria's QS client retention rate is **91%** (clients who continue using our service year-on-year). Industry average for building material suppliers is 65–75%. Retention is the ultimate measure of value delivery — our clients stay because the service works.

**Q939:** How does your payment terms compare to competitors?

**A939:** Our standard **30-day account** matches industry norms. Differentiators: **60-day option** for established clients (competitors rarely offer), **early payment discounts** (7-day = 2.5%, 14-day = 1.5%), and **project-specific credit limits** (competitors typically require cash upfront for large orders).

**Q940:** Do competitors provide the same level of technical site support?

**A940:** Technical site support is **rare among tile distributors**. Most competitors sell the product and provide no installation guidance. Tiletoria offers: pre-start meetings, site visits during installation, troubleshooting support, and practical completion inspection. Site support reduces defects and warranty claims.

**Q941:** How does your product range compare to competitors?


**Q942:** Can I use Tiletoria alongside competitor suppliers?

**A942:** Yes. Many QS practices use Tiletoria as their **primary floor finishes data source** while maintaining secondary suppliers for specific products or price benchmarking. Our rate match guarantee ensures you're never disadvantaged by using us as primary. We welcome the opportunity to earn your full trust over time.

**Q943:** How does Tiletoria's estimating team size compare to competitors?

**A943:** Tiletoria employs **12 dedicated estimating professionals** across three regions: 6 in Gauteng, 3 in KZN, 3 in Western Cape. This is the largest specialist floor finishes estimating team in South Africa. Competitors typically have 1–3 generalist estimators covering all product categories.

**Q944:** What percentage of your business comes from QS referrals?

**A944:** Approximately **45% of new QS client acquisitions** come from referrals from existing QS clients. This high referral rate (vs. industry average of 20–25%) reflects client satisfaction and willingness to recommend. Our Net Promoter Score among QS clients: 72 (excellent).

**Q945:** How does Tiletoria's digital investment compare to competitors?

**A945:** Tiletoria has invested **R4.2 million** in digital tools over the past 3 years: QS portal development, API infrastructure, BIM integration, coverage calculator suite, and mobile app. Most competitors have minimal digital presence — basic websites with no interactive tools. Our digital advantage trans to time savings for QS practices.

**Q946:** What is your client churn rate and why do clients leave?

**A946:** Annual client churn: **9%** (91% retention). Reasons for leaving: practice closure/merger (4%), change in specification away from tile (2%), competitor price match on a single large project (2%), and service dissatisfaction (1%). Of the 1% who leave for dissatisfaction, 30% return within 12 months.

**Q947:** How does Tiletoria handle sustainability compared to competitors?

**A947:** Sustainability differentiators: **ECO-label product range** (300+ certified products), **carbon footprint reporting** per product, **recycled content tracking** for Green Star credits, **local sourcing preference** (reduced transport emissions), and **solar-powered warehouses**. Competitors have limited sustainability data and fewer certified products.

**Q948:** What is your market share in the QS-specified tile market?

**A948:** Tiletoria's estimated share of the **QS-professional-specified tile market** is 35–40% by value (based on industry survey data and Berg Cost Data reference frequency). This makes us the largest single data source for floor finishes in South African QS practice. Market share growing at 3–5% per annum.

**Q949:** How does Tiletoria's stock holding compare to competitors?

**A949:** Tiletoria holds **R85 million in stock** across three distribution centres — the largest floor finishes inventory in South Africa. Competitors average R15–30 million. Larger stock holding means: better rate stability (buffer against manufacturer price changes), faster delivery (stock available immediately), and lower stock-out risk (99.2% line item fill rate).

**Q950:** What is your Net Promoter Score among QS clients?

**A950:** Tiletoria's **Net Promoter Score (NPS)** among QS clients is **+72** (2024 survey), classified as 'Excellent'. Industry average for building material suppliers: +15 to +25. NPS breakdown: Promoters (9–10): 78%; Passives (7–8): 16%; Detractors (0–6): 6%. Detractor feedback drives immediate corrective action.

**Q951:** How does Tiletoria's technical team size compare to competitors?

**A951:** Tiletoria employs **8 full-time technical professionals**: 3 technical managers (SANS compliance, substitution defence, specification), 2 installation specialists (site support, methodology), 2 product specialists (imports, new products), and 1 quality assurance manager. Most competitors have 1–2 technical staff or none.

**Q952:** How does Tiletoria handle product quality issues compared to competitors?

**A952:** Quality issue resolution: **reported within 24 hours** → investigation initiated within 48 hours → independent laboratory testing if required → replacement or credit issued within 10 business days of confirmed defect. Competitor average: 3–6 weeks resolution. Our 10-day commitment is the fastest in the industry.

**Q953:** How does Tiletoria's product innovation pipeline compare to competitors?

**A953:** Tiletoria introduces **150–200 new products annually** vs. industry average of 30–50. New product categories tracked: large-format (1200×2400mm), ultra-thin (3.5mm), anti-bacterial, photocatalytic (self-cleaning), and 3D textured surfaces. Innovation rate reflects our direct relationships with 25+ international manufacturers.

**Q954:** How does Tiletoria's social responsibility programme compare to competitors?

**A954:** Social responsibility: **Skills development** (12 learnerships annually), **Enterprise development** (R2.5 million annual spend on SMME development), **Community projects** (pro-bono flooring for 2–3 community buildings per year), and **Environmental initiatives** (solar-powered warehouses, recycling programme). ASAQS-recognised supplier development partner.

**Q955:** How does Tiletoria handle stock-outs compared to competitors?

**A955:** Stock-out performance: **99.2% line item fill rate** (industry average: 92–95%). When stock-outs occur: we notify within 24 hours, propose equivalent alternative with compliance confirmation, offer air freight for critical items (at our cost if our error), and provide revised delivery schedule. Stock-out occurred on 0.8% of line items in 2024 — average resolution: 3.5 days.


---


## Category 18: Payment Terms & Commercial Terms

**Q956:** What are Tiletoria's standard payment terms?

**A956:** Standard payment terms are **30 days from date of invoice** (net monthly account). Invoices are issued on dispatch of materials. Statements are emailed monthly with itemised transaction history. Accounts can be settled by EFT, cheque, or electronic debit order.

**Q957:** Do you offer early payment discounts?

**A957:** Yes. Early payment discounts: **7-day settlement**: 2.5% discount on material value; **14-day settlement**: 1.5% discount; **30-day settlement**: standard rate (no discount). Early payment discounts exclude delivery charges and labour. Significant savings available on large projects with cash-flow flexibility.

**Q958:** What volume tier discounts are available?

**A958:** Volume discounts on material value: **R50,000–R100,000**: 2% discount; **R100,000–R250,000**: 3% discount; **R250,000–R500,000**: 5% discount; **R500,000–R1,000,000**: 7% discount; **>R1,000,000**: 10% discount. Discounts apply to cumulative orders within a 12-month period.

**Q959:** Can I get 60-day payment terms?

**A959:** **60-day terms** are available for established clients with: 12+ months trading history, consistent on-time payment record, and satisfactory credit reference. 60-day terms carry a 1.5% pricing premium vs. 30-day terms. Application via your estimating manager with financial statements.

**Q960:** Do you offer project-specific credit facilities?

**A960:** Yes. **Project credit facilities** provide a dedicated credit limit for a specific project, separate from your general account. Useful for large projects where the standard credit limit is insufficient. Facility requires: project details, employer/client name, contract value, and programme.

**Q961:** What is your order consolidation policy?

**A961:** **Order consolidation** combines multiple product lines (tile, adhesive, grout, waterproofing, trims) into a single delivery with one invoice. Benefits: reduced delivery charges, coordinated delivery timing, single payment, and volume discount aggregation. We recommend consolidation for all full-system orders.

**Q962:** Do you require deposits for large orders?

**A962:** Deposits are required for: **import orders** (30% deposit to confirm vessel booking); **custom/bespoke orders** (50% deposit, 50% on completion); **large project orders >R2m** (20% deposit, balance 30 days from invoice). Standard stock orders on approved account require no deposit.

**Q963:** Can I pay by credit card?

**A963:** Credit card payments are accepted for **orders under R50,000**. A 2.5% card processing fee applies. For orders exceeding R50,000, EFT or debit order is required. Credit card payments processed through our secure online portal or in-person at our trade counters.

**Q964:** Do you offer settlement discounts for cash upfront payment?

**A964:** Yes. **Cash upfront** (payment before dispatch) attracts a **3% discount** on material value (in addition to volume discounts). This is our maximum discount tier — combines with volume discount for total saving of up to 13% on large orders.

**Q965:** What happens if payment is late?

**A965:** Late payment policy: **Days 1–7 overdue**: reminder email; **Days 8–14**: phone call from accounts + 1.5% late payment levy; **Days 15–30**: account placed on hold (no further deliveries) + 3% levy; **Day 31+**: debt collection process initiated. We encourage proactive communication if payment difficulties are anticipated.

**Q966:** Can I set up a debit order for regular payments?

**A966:** Yes. **Monthly debit order** arrangements are available for clients with regular ordering patterns. Debit orders processed on the 1st of each month for the previous month's statement balance. Removes payment administration and ensures you never miss the early payment discount window.

**Q967:** Do you offer retention terms for contractor accounts?

**A967:** Yes. Contractor accounts can include a **2.5% retention** held for 12 months (aligned with JBCC defect liability period). Retention released on: practical completion certificate, final inspection approval, and confirmation of no material defects. Retention held in a separate trust account.

**Q968:** Can I get provisional billing before final quantities are confirmed?

**A968:** Yes. **Provisional invoices** can be issued based on estimated quantities for cash flow purposes, with a final adjustment invoice issued after confirmed quantities. This supports monthly payment certificate cycles where final quantities aren't available until month-end.

**Q969:** Do you offer bulk order forward pricing?

**A969:** Yes. **Forward Pricing Agreements** lock material rates for up to 12 months for confirmed bulk orders. A 20% deposit secures the rate and stock allocation. leading adhesive manufacturersance payable 30 days before each scheduled delivery. Ideal for phased projects with predictable material requirements.

**Q970:** What are your delivery charges?

**A970:** Delivery charges are **quantity-banded**: Under 100m² = R25–35/m²; 100–300m² = R18–25/m²; 300–500m² = R14–18/m²; 500m²+ = R10–15/m²; Full truckload = flat rate R8,500–12,500 per load. Metro area deliveries. Regional/long-haul additional. Free delivery on orders >R150,000 in Gauteng metro.

**Q971:** Can I collect from your warehouse to avoid delivery charges?

**A971:** Yes. **Collection from our warehouses** in Johannesburg, Durban, or Cape Town is welcome. Collection hours: 07:30–16:00 Monday–Friday, 08:00–12:00 Saturday. Collection discount: R8–15/m² off the delivered rate. Loading assistance provided.

**Q972:** Do you have minimum order quantities?

**A972:** Minimum order values: **Stock items**: R2,500 minimum order value; **Import/special order items**: R5,000 minimum; **Delivery**: R2,500 minimum for free delivery (below R2,500 = R450 delivery charge). No minimum per product line — you can order 1 box if the total order exceeds the minimum.

**Q973:** Can I return unused materials?

**A973:** Unused materials in **original, undamaged packaging** can be returned for credit within 30 days of delivery. A **15% restocking fee** applies. Special-order, imported, or custom-manufactured items may not be returnable — confirmed at order placement. Credit processed within 14 days of return receipt.

**Q974:** Do you offer trade-in on old stock or discontinued lines?

**A974:** Trade-in is not standard practice. However, for large contractor accounts with surplus stock from completed projects, we may offer **stock swap arrangements** on a case-by-case basis. Contact your estimating manager to discuss.

**Q975:** What commercial terms apply to government projects?

**A975:** Government projects operate under **NTC (National Treasury Conditions)**: 30-day payment from invoice, B-BBEE compliance documentation, CIDB contractor grading requirements, and local content certification. We are fully compliant with all NTC requirements and registered on CSD (Central Supplier Database).

**Q976:** Can you invoice the employer directly for nominated supplier items?

**A976:** Yes. For **nominated supplier** scenarios, we can invoice the employer directly (bypassing the contractor) with the contractor's agreement. This protects the employer from contractor margin-loading and ensures the nominated rate is paid. Separate invoicing arrangement required.

**Q977:** Do you offer performance bonds for large contracts?

**A977:** For material supply contracts exceeding R5 million, we can provide a **performance bond** (typically 10% of contract value) through our trade credit insurer. The bond guarantees supply per the agreed schedule. Premium: 0.5–1.0% of bond value, typically borne by the client.

**Q978:** What is your policy on price adjustments after order placement?

**A978:** Once a firm order is placed and deposit received (if applicable), the **rate is locked**. We absorb any cost increases between order and delivery. If rates decrease (e.g., exchange rate strengthens), we pass the saving to you. Fair dealing in both directions.

**Q979:** Can I get extended payment terms for NHBRC warranty projects?

**A979:** NHBRC warranty projects can qualify for **extended terms** (up to 45 days) reflecting the payment certificate cycle typical of residential developments. NHBRC compliance documentation required. Contact your estimating manager for NHBRC-specific terms.

**Q980:** Do you offer supplier development contributions for B-BBEE?

**A980:** Yes. As a **Level 2 B-BBEE contributor**, we support your B-BBEE scorecard through: preferential procurement points, supplier development contributions (negotiable for large frameworks), and enterprise development support. B-BBEE certificate available on request.

**Q981:** How do you handle payment queries and invoice disputes?

**A981:** Payment queries handled by our **Accounts Team**: email accounts@tiletoria.co.za or call 010 140 0161. Queries resolved within 2 business days. Invoice disputes must be raised within 14 days of invoice date. No late payment levy applied during active dispute resolution.

**Q982:** Can I pay in instalments for large orders?

**A982:** **Instalment payment plans** are available for orders exceeding R500,000: 30% deposit, 40% at mid-delivery, 30% on completion. No interest charged for instalment plans within 90 days. Longer-term instalments (3–6 months) available against credit approval with nominal interest.

**Q983:** Do you offer price protection for orders placed during exchange rate volatility?

**A983:** For import orders placed during volatile periods, we offer **7-day rate protection**: the exchange rate at order placement is held for 7 days to allow EFT clearance. Beyond 7 days, if the rate moves adversely >2%, we absorb the first 2% and share the balance 50/50.

**Q984:** What are your terms for sample supply?

**A984:** Samples for **registered QS clients** are provided free of charge (up to 10 samples per project). A4 boards, individual tile samples, and colour charts are included. Large sample boards (600×600mm) and full-size sample panels may incur a nominal charge (R150–450) refundable on order.

**Q985:** Can I get a pro forma invoice for budgeting purposes?

**A985:** Yes. **Pro forma invoices** are issued on request showing exact rates, quantities, and totals without creating an accounts receivable entry. Pro formas are valid for 14 days and convert to firm invoices on order confirmation. Useful for employer budget approval and cash flow planning.

**Q986:** Do you offer insurance on delivered materials?

**A986:** Materials are insured **during transit** (marine cargo insurance for imports, goods-in-transit for local delivery). Risk transfers to the client on delivery/offloading unless otherwise agreed. Extended storage insurance can be arranged for materials stored on site pre-installation.

**Q987:** How do I apply for a trade account?

**A987:** Trade account application: email **accounts@tiletoria.co.za** with your business registration documents, B-BBEE certificate, CIDB registration (if applicable), trade references (2 suppliers, 1 client), and banking details. Applications processed within 48 hours. Credit limit determined by credit assessment.

**Q988:** Can I get commercial terms in writing before placing my first order?

**A988:** Yes. Your estimating manager provides a **Commercial Terms Sheet** on request summarising: payment terms, discount structure, delivery charges, return policy, warranty terms, and credit limit. This document can be shared with your finance team for pre-approval.

**Q989:** Do you offer dynamic discounting (earlier payment = higher discount)?

**A989:** Yes. **Dynamic discounting** through our supplier finance platform: payment within 5 days = 3% discount; within 10 days = 2.5%; within 15 days = 2.0%; within 20 days = 1.5%. Discount calculated automatically on the invoice. No manual intervention required — simply pay earlier for a higher discount.

**Q990:** Can I get a letter of intent for material supply for my client's funding application?

**A990:** Yes. A **Letter of Intent** confirms: our willingness to supply materials for the project at agreed rates, estimated total material value, delivery timeframe commitment, and commercial terms. Letters of Intent support client funding applications, board approvals, and JV partner presentations.

**Q991:** Do you offer supply chain finance (reverse factoring)?

**A991:** Yes. **Supply chain finance** available through our banking partner: we receive early payment (improved cash flow), you extend payment terms to 60 or 90 days (improved working capital). Finance cost: 0.8–1.2% per month depending on credit rating. Arranged through our commercial manager.

**Q992:** Can I set up a materials escrow account for a large project?

**A992:** Yes. **Escrow accounts** hold client funds in a third-party trust account, releasing payment to Tiletoria on verified delivery. Escrow protects both parties: client funds are secure, supplier payment is guaranteed on delivery. Escrow arrangement cost: 0.5% of transaction value (shared 50/50).

**Q993:** Can I get project-specific insurance for high-value tile deliveries?

**A993:** Yes. **Marine cargo insurance** (for imports) and **goods-in-transit insurance** (for local delivery) can be extended to **all-risk project insurance** covering materials on site pre-installation. Premium: 0.3–0.5% of insured value. Arranged through our insurance broker.

**Q994:** Do you offer cryptocurrency or alternative payment methods?

**A994:** No. Tiletoria accepts payment in **South African Rand (ZAR)** only. Electronic funds transfer (EFT), cheque, and debit order are the accepted methods. We do not accept cryptocurrency, foreign currency cash, or third-party payment platforms (PayPal, etc.) for domestic transactions.

**Q995:** Can I get a documentary letter of credit for import orders?

**A995:** Yes. **Documentary Letters of Credit (LC)** are accepted for import orders exceeding R500,000. LC terms: irrevocable, confirmed by our bank, payable on presentation of shipping documents. LC fees borne by the applicant (typically 0.5–1.5% of LC value). Contact our commercial manager to arrange LC terms.

**Q996:** Do you offer barter or contra arrangements for payment?

**A996:** Barter/contra arrangements are **not standard practice** and considered on a case-by-case basis only. Where a client has products or services of value to Tiletoria (e.g., transport capacity, marketing services), a contra arrangement may be negotiated. All contra arrangements require board approval and formal agreement.

**Q997:** Can I get advance rate notifications before public price increases?

**A997:** Yes. **Framework clients** receive **48-hour advance notice** of manufacturer price increases before public announcement. This allows early ordering at old rates or budget revision before the increase takes effect. Advance notice is a framework-exclusive benefit — not available to ad-hoc clients.

**Q998:** Can I set up a parent guarantee for my subsidiary's account?

**A998:** Yes. **Parent company guarantees** are accepted for subsidiary accounts where the parent has stronger creditworthiness. Guarantee covers: payment of all invoices, adherence to credit limits, and compliance with commercial terms. Guarantee format reviewed by our legal team. Common for developer group structures.

**Q999:** Can I get a rate validity extension beyond 90 days for a slow procurement project?

**A999:** Yes. **Extended validity** beyond 90 days: 91–180 days = CPI+3% cap applies; 181–365 days = CPI+5% cap + 1.5% premium; 366+ days = fixed-rate agreement required with 5% deposit. Extended validity provides certainty for projects with extended feasibility-to-procurement cycles (common in public sector).

**Q1000:** Can I get a rate lock for a future order without placing the order now?

**A1000:** Yes. **Rate Lock without Order**: lock current rates for up to 60 days with a 10% deposit. The deposit is fully refundable if you don't place the order (rate lock only). If you do place the order, the deposit is credited against the order. This provides rate certainty without commitment.

**Q1001:** Can I get a material advance (pre-payment) facility for large import orders?

**A1001:** Yes. **Material Advance Facility**: we receive 30% deposit from you, we pay the international supplier immediately, we hold stock in our bonded warehouse, and you take delivery as needed over 90 days. Benefits: locks exchange rate and price, secures stock availability, and spreads payment over the programme. Facility fee: 1.5% of material value.


---

*End of Persona 8: Quantity Surveyors Q&A Knowledge Base*

**Total Q&A Pairs: 1001**
**Categories: 18**

- Email: qs.registration@tiletoria.co.za
- Escalation: escalation@tiletoria.co.za



---

# Persona 9: Smaller Flooring Merchants (Wholesale) — Q&A Knowledge Base

---

## Category 1: Wholesale Account Setup

**Q1:** How do I open a wholesale account with Tiletoria?
**A:** Contact our wholesale team on 021 555 5700 or email wholesale@tiletoria.co.za. You'll need to complete a wholesale account application form, provide your company registration documents, VAT certificate, valid trade references from two existing suppliers, and banking details. Our wholesale team will review your application within 3-5 business days and advise on credit terms or pro forma arrangements.

**Q2:** What documents do I need to submit for a wholesale account application?
**A:** You'll need: (1) Company registration documents (CCR14 or equivalent), (2) VAT registration certificate, (3) Proof of business address (utility bill or lease agreement), (4) Banking details for EFT payments, (5) Two trade references from existing building material or tile suppliers, and (6) ID copies of the owner/director(s). For CCs or trusts, all member/trustee IDs are required.

**Q3:** How long does wholesale account approval take?
**A:** Standard wholesale account approval takes 3-5 business days from receipt of a complete application. If trade references respond promptly, it can be as quick as 2-3 days. Pro forma accounts (payment upfront) can often be opened within 24-48 hours. Credit account applications may take 5-7 business days depending on credit reference turnaround.

**Q4:** Can I start buying on a cash basis while my credit application is being processed?
**A:** Yes — most new merchants start on a pro forma (payment upfront) basis while their credit application is being assessed. This allows you to begin trading immediately. Once your credit application is approved, your account is converted to 30-day terms and any pro forma purchases are reflected on your credit record with us.

**Q5:** What trade references do you accept for a new wholesale account?
**A:** We require two trade references from existing building material suppliers, tile distributors, sanitaryware suppliers, or builders merchants you've traded with for at least six months. References from hardware stores, plumbing suppliers, or building material wholesalers are preferred. References from cash-only suppliers carry less weight than those who extended credit terms.

**Q6:** I'm a new business with no trade references. Can I still get a wholesale account?
**A:** Yes, but you'll start on a pro forma (payment upfront) basis. After six months of consistent purchasing and payment history with Tiletoria, you can apply for credit terms. Some new merchants also provide a personal surety from the owner/director or a bank guarantee to accelerate credit approval.

**Q7:** Do I need a physical showroom to qualify for a wholesale account?
**A:** Not necessarily. While most of our wholesale customers operate from showrooms or shop-front premises, we also supply contractors' yards, builders merchants with dedicated flooring sections, and specialist bathroom retailers. Your business model needs to demonstrate a route to market for tile and flooring products.

**Q8:** Can I have multiple wholesale accounts if I have more than one branch?
**A:** Yes, many of our multi-branch customers operate separate wholesale accounts per branch for stock management purposes. Alternatively, you can operate a single group account with multiple delivery addresses. Your account manager will advise on the best structure based on your business setup and credit profile.

**Q9:** What is the minimum turnover requirement for a wholesale account?
**A:** We don't enforce a strict minimum turnover threshold. We work with merchants turning R5 million to R50 million annually. What matters more is your business model, market reach, and commitment to stocking and promoting our product ranges. Even smaller specialist retailers are welcome if they demonstrate growth potential.

**Q10:** Is there a membership fee or annual subscription for wholesale accounts?
**A:** No, there is no membership fee or annual subscription to maintain a wholesale account with Tiletoria. Your account remains active as long as you maintain regular purchasing activity (at minimum, one order every six months) and settle any outstanding amounts within agreed terms.

**Q11:** Can I visit your warehouse before opening an account to see the stock range?
**A:** Absolutely — we encourage prospective wholesale customers to visit our warehouses. Our Cape Town (Paarden Eiland), Durban, Johannesburg, and Paarden Eiland facilities are open for trade viewings by appointment. Contact the wholesale team to arrange a guided tour with one of our account managers.

**Q12:** What is the difference between a wholesale account and a retail cash sale?
**A:** Wholesale accounts receive preferential pricing significantly below our retail price list, access to exclusive wholesale-only product ranges, dedicated account management, delivery services, credit terms (once approved), volume discount structures, and first access to clearance and promotional stock. Retail cash sales are at marked prices with none of these benefits.

**Q13:** Do you offer wholesale accounts to online-only retailers?
**A:** We evaluate online-only retailers on a case-by-case basis. Key considerations include your existing web traffic, customer base, digital marketing capability, and whether you hold physical stock. We generally prefer merchants with a showroom or trade counter, but proven e-commerce operators in the building materials space are considered.

**Q14:** Can my buying manager be the primary contact for the wholesale account, or does it need to be the owner?
**A:** Your buying manager can absolutely be the primary day-to-day contact for ordering, stock queries, and range discussions. However, the owner or MD must sign the credit application and account opening documents. We recommend designating your buying manager as an authorised contact on the account for operational efficiency.

**Q15:** What happens after I submit my wholesale application — what is the process?
**A:** (1) Application received and acknowledged within 24 hours, (2) Credit department verifies documents and contacts trade references (Days 1-3), (3) Credit assessment completed (Days 3-5), (4) Account approved with credit limit and terms, or pro forma status advised, (5) Welcome pack issued with price lists, ordering details, and account manager contact, (6) First order can be placed immediately upon approval.

**Q16:** If my credit application is declined, can I reapply later?
**A:** Yes, you can reapply after six months. Common reasons for initial decline include insufficient trading history, adverse credit bureau information, or weak trade references. During the six-month period, trading on a pro forma basis and building a payment history with Tiletoria significantly strengthens a reapplication.

**Q17:** Do you offer a trial period or first-order discount for new wholesale accounts?
**A:** New wholesale accounts receive our standard wholesale pricing from day one. During pre-season show periods (typically February-March and August-September), new accounts can also access show pricing on their first order if they attend or place orders during the promotional window. Speak to your account manager about current new-account incentives.

**Q18:** Can I get a wholesale account if I'm based outside South Africa in a neighbouring country?
**A:** Yes, we supply merchants across Southern Africa. Accounts for Botswana, Namibia, Mozambique, Zimbabwe, and Zambia are handled through our export division. Terms may differ — typically pro forma or confirmed LC for the first 12 months. Export documentation and cross-border logistics are arranged through our team.

**Q19:** What is your policy on account confidentiality — will my competitors know I'm buying from you?
**A:** We treat all customer information as strictly confidential. We do not disclose your purchasing activity, pricing, or account details to other merchants, including those in the same town or region. Your commercial relationship with Tiletoria is private, and we expect the same discretion from our wholesale customers regarding our pricing.

**Q20:** How do I update my account details if my business address or banking details change?
**A:** Email your updated details to accounts@tiletoria.co.za with "Account Update" in the subject line. Attach supporting documentation (new utility bill for address changes, stamped bank letter for banking changes). Changes are processed within 2-3 business days. Banking detail changes require verification for security purposes.

**Q21:** Do I need to sign a wholesale supply agreement or contract?
**A:** Yes, all wholesale accounts sign our standard Wholesale Trading Terms and Conditions. This covers pricing confidentiality, payment terms, returns policies, delivery terms, and minimum marketing standards for displaying our brands. The agreement is not an exclusive supply contract — you're free to buy from other suppliers.

**Q22:** Can my Tiletoria wholesale account be suspended or closed?
**A:** Accounts may be suspended for persistent late payment (accounts over 60 days overdue), fraudulent activity, or breach of trading terms. We'll always attempt to resolve issues through discussion before any suspension. If you wish to close your account voluntarily, simply settle any outstanding balance and notify your account manager.

**Q23:** What is the difference between the wholesale accounts at Tiletoria branches — is pricing the same nationwide?
**A:** Wholesale pricing structures are consistent nationwide, but freight costs vary by region, affecting delivered pricing. Each branch manages its own wholesale accounts, but your account manager can coordinate orders from multiple branches. National group accounts receive unified pricing and centralised reporting.

**Q24:** Who will be my main point of contact once my wholesale account is active?
**A:** You'll be assigned a dedicated wholesale account manager based at your nearest Tiletoria branch. For larger accounts, you may also have access to a regional wholesale manager and our head office category management team for range planning and strategic discussions.

**Q25:** Can I get a price list before opening an account to evaluate your competitiveness?
**A:** We provide indicative pricing on request to serious prospective wholesale customers who have submitted an account application or are in the process of doing so. Full wholesale price lists are issued upon account approval. Our account managers can also quote on specific product categories you're interested in during the evaluation phase.

---

## Category 2: Wholesale Pricing & Margins

**Q26:** What kind of markup can I expect as a wholesale customer buying from Tiletoria?
**A:** Tiletoria's wholesale pricing is structured to allow you a 25-45% markup to retail, depending on the product category. Fast-moving ceramic wall tiles typically allow 30-35% markup, while specialist porcelain and imported designer tiles can support 40-45%. Your account manager can provide category-specific margin guidance.

**Q27:** How often do wholesale price lists get updated?
**A:** Wholesale price lists are updated quarterly (March, June, September, December) to reflect exchange rate movements, factory price adjustments, and freight cost changes. Major price movements due to currency volatility may trigger interim adjustments. You'll receive 14 days' notice of any price increases.

**Q28:** Is there a volume discount structure on top of wholesale pricing?
**A:** Yes. Beyond standard wholesale pricing, additional volume rebates apply at quarterly and annual tiers based on your total purchase value. Typical thresholds are R250,000, R500,000, R750,000, and R1 million+ per quarter. Your account manager will confirm your specific rebate structure upon account opening.

**Q29:** What is pre-season show pricing, and how does it work?
**A:** Pre-season show pricing is promotional wholesale pricing offered during our annual buying windows (February-March for winter stock and August-September for summer ranges). Prices are typically 5-15% below standard wholesale for forward orders placed during these periods. Stock is delivered according to agreed schedules.

**Q30:** How do I access pre-season show pricing if I can't attend the trade show in person?
**A:** Show pricing is available to all active wholesale accounts during the promotional window, regardless of attendance. Your account manager will send you the show catalogue and price list via email. Orders can be placed by email, phone, or through your online portal. Early ordering secures the best stock availability.

**Q31:** Can you recommend retail pricing for my market, or is that my decision?
**A:** We provide a Manufacturer's Recommended Selling Price (MRSP) on most wholesale price lists as a guideline. However, retail pricing is entirely your commercial decision. We encourage you to price according to your local market conditions, competitor positioning, and service level. The MRSP is a reference point only.

**Q32:** How do I know if my current supplier's pricing is competitive against Tiletoria's wholesale prices?
**A:** Our account managers are happy to do a competitive price comparison on specific product categories or SKUs you're currently buying elsewhere. Submit your current price list or key product prices (with supplier names redacted if preferred), and we'll benchmark against our wholesale pricing. No obligation to switch.

**Q33:** Does the exchange rate affect my wholesale pricing, and how is this managed?
**A:** Yes, most of our imported product pricing is linked to EUR, USD, or factory currency. We fix pricing at landed cost when stock arrives and is costed into our system, so your price list reflects actual landed costs rather than daily spot rates. Quarterly price list updates capture cumulative exchange rate and cost movements.

**Q34:** Are there settlement discounts for early payment?
**A:** Yes, accounts that settle within 7 days of statement date qualify for a settlement discount — typically 2.5-3% depending on your account tier. This is over and above any volume rebates. To qualify, payment must clear into our account within the 7-day window. Discounts are credited to your account monthly.

**Q35:** What is the price difference between local SA-manufactured tiles and imported tiles at wholesale?
**A:** Locally manufactured tiles (Johnson Tiles, Ceramic Industries) are generally 15-30% cheaper at wholesale than equivalent-quality European imports, due to shorter supply chains and no import freight costs. However, European imports command higher retail prices and margins. We recommend a mix: local products for volume, imports for margin.

**Q36:** How do I protect my margins when competing against competitor retailers, competitor retailers, or competitor retailers?
**A:** Focus on products and services the chains can't match: exclusive ranges (our exclusive mosaic range, our laminate flooring, our laminate flooring), personalised service, local stock availability, installation support, and product knowledge. Avoid competing solely on price for commodity products. Your account manager can help you develop a differentiated product mix.

**Q37:** Can I get special pricing for a large project order that exceeds my normal stock purchases?
**A:** Yes, for significant project orders (typically R100,000+ wholesale value), your account manager can negotiate project-specific pricing with our commercial team. This considers the total volume, product mix, delivery schedule, and payment terms. Project pricing is quoted per job and doesn't affect your standard wholesale pricing.

**Q38:** Are there price differences between buying ex-branch and having stock delivered?
**A:** Wholesale price lists are quoted ex-branch (EXW). Delivery costs are additional and calculated based on distance, volume, and weight. For larger orders within 60km of a branch, delivery may be included depending on your account tier. Your account manager can provide a delivered pricing schedule for your area.

**Q39:** How do I know when a price increase is coming so I can stock up beforehand?
**A:** We provide 14 days' written notice of all price increases via email to all active wholesale accounts. Your account manager will also call key accounts to discuss the impact. Smart merchants typically place forward orders before the effective date to lock in current pricing — this is standard practice and encouraged.

**Q40:** Do you price match against other tile wholesalers if I find a product cheaper elsewhere?
**A:** We evaluate price match requests case by case. If you can demonstrate that an identical or directly comparable product is available from a reputable competitor at a lower wholesale price, we'll review our pricing. We need the competitor's written quote and product specification to verify comparability.

**Q41:** What is the typical margin difference between ceramic wall tiles and porcelain floor tiles at retail?
**A:** Ceramic wall tiles are more commoditised with typical retail markups of 25-35%. Porcelain floor tiles, particularly large-format (600x600mm and above) and rectified porcelain, support markups of 35-45% due to their technical advantages, durability ratings, and perceived value. Polished porcelain commands the highest margins.

**Q42:** Are there any hidden costs I should factor into my landed cost besides the wholesale price?
**A:** Beyond the ex-branch wholesale price, factor in: (1) Delivery/freight costs if applicable, (2) Your financing cost if buying on credit (30-day terms), (3) Handling and warehousing costs, (4) Breakage and damage allowance (typically 2-3%), (5) Display and marketing costs, and (6) Shrinkage and slow-moving stock risk.

**Q43:** How do your wholesale prices for laminate flooring compare to other laminate distributors?
**A:** our laminate flooring and our laminate flooring exclusive ranges are competitively priced against Kronotex, Finfloor, and leading adhesive manufacturersterio distribution. Because these are exclusive Tiletoria brands, you won't face direct price competition from other merchants carrying the same brand — a significant margin protection advantage.

**Q44:** Can I get a fixed price agreement for 12 months if I commit to a volume target?
**A:** For larger wholesale accounts with proven track records and committed annual volume targets (typically R2 million+), we can discuss fixed-price agreements on core ranges for 12 months. This provides certainty for your retail pricing and margin planning. Speak to your regional wholesale manager about qualifying.

**Q45:** What is the price differential between full container load (FCL) and less-than-container load (LCL) wholesale pricing?
**A:** Full container load purchases typically attract a 5-10% price advantage over LCL/break-bulk due to lower per-unit freight costs. However, FCL requires significant capital commitment and carries higher inventory risk. Most smaller merchants prefer LCL purchasing through our stock — you benefit from our container volumes without the capital tie-up.

**Q46:** How do I calculate my actual gross margin after accounting for returns, damages, and slow stock?
**A:** Start with your retail selling price minus wholesale cost. Then deduct: (1) Delivery costs to customer (if absorbed), (2) Returns and damages (typically 3-5% of sales), (3) Slow-moving stock markdowns (typically 5-10% of inventory value annually), (4) Display and showroom costs (2-3% of sales), and (5) Finance costs on stock holding. Your net margin should still target 18-28%.

**Q47:** Do you offer introductory pricing or first-order incentives for merchants switching from a competitor?
**A:** We occasionally run merchant acquisition promotions, particularly during pre-season show periods. These may include enhanced pricing on your first two orders, extended payment terms for the first month, or free marketing materials. Ask your account manager about any current new-merchant incentives.

**Q48:** How does the rebate system work — is it a retrospective discount or upfront?
**A:** Volume rebates are calculated retrospectively based on your quarterly or annual purchases and credited to your account. Credits can be offset against future purchases or, for accounts in good standing, applied as a payment reduction on your next statement. Rebate structures are agreed upfront so you know exactly what you're working towards.

**Q49:** What is the wholesale price difference between standard grade and first-grade tiles?
**A:** Standard grade (or commercial grade) tiles are typically 10-20% cheaper at wholesale than first-grade equivalent. However, first-grade tiles command higher retail prices and have lower defect rates, reducing returns and customer complaints. For retail showrooms, we recommend stocking first-grade. Standard grade suits contractor-focused yards where price is paramount.

**Q50:** Can I request a custom price list filtered only to the categories I stock?
**A:** Yes, your account manager can provide a customised price list showing only the product categories you actively trade in. This makes ordering and price checking more efficient. As your range expands, the price list can be updated. You can also access the full catalogue anytime through our online portal.

---

## Category 3: MOQ & Ordering Flexibility

**Q51:** What is the minimum order quantity (MOQ) for wholesale orders?
**A:** Standard wholesale MOQ is one full pallet per tile size/design, typically ranging from 40m² to 80m² depending on tile format. For laminate flooring, MOQ is typically 20-30 cartons. For sanware, MOQ varies by product — 5-10 units for fast-moving items, single units for high-value items like baths and vanities.

**Q52:** Can I order less than a full pallet if I'm testing a new product in my market?
**A:** Yes, we accommodate sample/test orders below standard MOQ for new products or new customers. Typically, you can order a quarter-pallet (10-20m²) or even a few boxes for product evaluation. These are priced at standard wholesale rates. Speak to your account manager to arrange — we want you to range the right products for your market.

**Q53:** Do you offer mixed pallet options so I can get variety without over-committing?
**A:** Yes, mixed pallets are available for selected product categories, particularly ceramic wall tiles and decorative mosaic sheets. A mixed pallet might contain 4-6 different designs in the same size format. This is ideal for building display boards or testing multiple colours without holding full pallets of each.

**Q54:** What is the smallest order value you'll deliver to outlying areas like Kimberley or East London?
**A:** For outlying deliveries, we typically require a minimum order value of R8,000-R12,000 to justify the freight cost, depending on distance from the nearest branch. For smaller orders, you can arrange your own transport collection ex-branch, or we can hold stock until your next order reaches the minimum.

**Q55:** Can I place a single order across multiple branches and have it consolidated?
**A:** Consolidated orders across branches can be arranged for larger accounts. Your primary account manager coordinates stock availability across branches and arranges consolidated delivery from the branch with the majority of stock, or split deliveries if required. A consolidation fee may apply depending on logistics complexity.

**Q56:** How quickly can I get stock for an emergency jobber order from a contractor?
**A:** For stock items at your nearest branch, same-day or next-day collection is available during branch hours. If you need delivery, next-day service applies within 60km of major branches. For outlying areas, 2-3 days is typical. Having an accurate stock availability check before promising your contractor is key — call your account manager or check online.

**Q57:** Can I place a backorder for out-of-stock items, or do I need to wait for them to arrive?
**A:** Yes, backorders are accepted and encouraged. When you place a backorder, the stock is allocated to you upon arrival at our warehouse. For fast-moving SKUs, we recommend placing backorders immediately to secure your allocation, particularly for imported lines where restock lead times are 6-12 weeks.

**Q58:** Is there a maximum order size, or can I order multiple containers?
**A:** There is no maximum order size. We regularly fulfil multi-pallet and multi-container orders for larger merchants. For very large orders (R500,000+), we recommend discussing with your account manager in advance to ensure stock availability, delivery scheduling, and any special pricing considerations.

**Q59:** Can I modify or cancel an order after it has been placed?
**A:** Orders can be modified or cancelled if they haven't yet been picked or dispatched from our warehouse. For import forward orders, cancellations are generally accepted up until the stock departs the factory. Once stock is in transit, cancellation may incur freight and handling costs. Contact your account manager immediately if you need to change an order.

**Q60:** Do you offer order consolidation — holding my smaller orders and shipping them together?
**A:** Yes, for accounts in outlying areas, we offer weekly or fortnightly consolidation. You place orders throughout the week, and we consolidate for delivery on your scheduled delivery day. This reduces per-order freight costs and ensures you always meet minimum order values. Your account manager will set up a delivery schedule.

**Q61:** What is a typical pallet quantity for 600x600mm porcelain floor tiles?
**A:** A standard pallet of 600x600mm porcelain tiles contains approximately 40m² (roughly 110-112 tiles), depending on thickness and packaging. This represents 4-5 cartons. A full truckload (FTL) carries approximately 24-28 pallets, or 960-1,120m² of this format.

**Q62:** Can I split a pallet with another merchant in my area if we both want the same product but can't justify a full pallet each?
**A:** We don't facilitate merchant-to-merchant pallet splitting directly, but if both merchants have Tiletoria wholesale accounts, each can order a half-pallet or quarter-pallet at standard wholesale pricing. Alternatively, one merchant can order the full pallet and arrange their own resale to the other party.

**Q63:** How do I place a forward order for stock that hasn't arrived in SA yet?
**A:** Forward orders are placed through your account manager using our import pipeline reports. These show products currently in production, in transit, or arriving at port. You place your order with an estimated delivery date, and stock is allocated to you upon arrival. A deposit may be required for large forward orders.

**Q64:** What deposit is required for forward orders on incoming container stock?
**A:** For forward orders under R50,000, no deposit is typically required for established accounts. For orders between R50,000 and R150,000, a 30% deposit secures your allocation. For orders over R150,000, a 50% deposit is standard. Deposits are offset against your final invoice when stock arrives.

**Q65:** Can I order samples of new products before committing to a pallet quantity?
**A:** Yes, sample tiles and sample boards are available for most products. Individual sample tiles are free for active wholesale accounts (up to 10 tiles per month). Full sample boards can be purchased at cost or provided free with a committed forward order. Contact your account manager to request samples.

**Q66:** Is there a cut-off time for placing orders for next-day delivery?
**A:** Orders for next-day delivery must be placed by 2:00 PM the previous business day for standard stock items. For large or complex orders (5+ pallets), 24 hours' notice is preferred. Orders placed after 2:00 PM are processed for delivery the following day. Friday afternoon orders are delivered on Monday.

**Q67:** Can I set up a standing order for fast-moving products that I reorder regularly?
**A:** Yes, standing orders are available for core range products that you sell consistently. Your account manager sets up a recurring order schedule (weekly, fortnightly, or monthly) with agreed quantities. You can modify quantities or skip deliveries with 48 hours' notice. Standing orders ensure you never run out of bestsellers.

**Q68:** What happens if the stock I ordered arrives damaged or short?
**A:** Report any shortages or damage within 24 hours of delivery. We verify against the picking slip and dispatch docket. Shortages are replaced or credited immediately. Damaged stock is collected and replaced at our cost if the damage occurred in transit. Damage must be noted on the delivery note at time of delivery.

**Q69:** Can I order products that are not in your standard stock range but that you can import for me?
**A:** Yes, we facilitate special imports for wholesale customers. You specify the product (size, design, finish, quantity), and we source from our factory network. Special imports require full-container quantities (FCL) or significant LCL volumes. Lead times are 8-16 weeks depending on source country. A deposit of 50-70% is required.

**Q70:** How flexible are you on MOQs for exclusive ranges like our exclusive mosaic range or our laminate flooring?
**A:** Our exclusive ranges have standard MOQs that align with factory minimums. For our exclusive mosaic range, minimums vary by product line — typically 20-50m² per design. For our laminate flooring, full-carton orders apply (typically 2.5-3m² per carton). Your account manager can advise on specific MOQs and may accommodate flexibility for range launches.

**Q71:** Can I place an order now and delay delivery until my showroom expansion is complete?
**A:** Yes, we can hold stock for up to 30 days at our warehouse at no charge for established accounts. For longer holding periods, a nominal storage fee may apply. Your account manager will confirm stock has been allocated to you and will coordinate delivery when you're ready. Advance payment may secure longer holding.

**Q72:** What is the process for ordering custom-made or bespoke mosaic patterns?
**A:** Custom mosaic designs are available through our exclusive mosaic range division. Submit your design or concept to your account manager, who liaises with the our exclusive mosaic range team. Custom orders require minimum quantities (typically 50-100m² depending on complexity) and carry a 10-20% premium over standard designs. Lead time is 10-14 weeks.

**Q73:** Can I get a pro forma invoice before placing an order to confirm the total cost?
**A:** Yes, pro forma invoices are available on request. Submit your product list and quantities to your account manager, and a detailed pro forma including product costs, VAT, and delivery charges will be issued within 24 hours. Pro forma invoices are valid for 7 days, after which prices may be subject to change.

**Q74:** Do you offer a 'call-off' system where I pay for stock upfront but take delivery in batches?
**A:** Yes, call-off arrangements are available for larger accounts. You commit to and pay for a quantity upfront (at a negotiated price), then call off stock as needed over an agreed period — typically 30-90 days. This secures your stock and pricing while managing your cash flow and warehouse space.

**Q75:** If I order a product and it doesn't sell in my market, can I exchange it for something else?
**A:** Standard exchanges are subject to our returns policy — stock must be in original packaging, undamaged, and returned within 30 days. A restocking fee of 10-15% may apply. For slow-moving stock exchanges outside this window, discuss with your account manager. We may offer exchange options on a case-by-case basis, particularly if you're replacing with a higher-velocity product.

---

## Category 4: Stock Range & Selection

**Q76:** What is the core wholesale range that most independent merchants should stock?
**A:** Our recommended core range for independents includes: (1) 300x600mm and 400x800mm ceramic wall tiles in white, beige, grey (top sellers), (2) 600x600mm and 600x1200mm porcelain floor tiles in neutral tones, (3) A wood-look porcelain option, (4) A-look porcelain option, (5) 200x100mm subway tiles, and (6) Mosaic accent strips. This gives you 40-60 SKUs covering 80% of typical consumer demand.

**Q77:** What are your current bestselling tile SKUs that I should definitely have in my range?
**A:** Consistent bestsellers include: large-format grey porcelain floor tiles (600x600 and 600x1200), white and grey-look porcelain, wood-look porcelain planks (200x1200mm), neutral ceramic wall tiles (300x600mm and 400x800mm), and classic white subway tiles. Your account manager can provide a current bestseller report with exact SKU references and monthly sales velocity.

**Q78:** Should I focus on imported European tiles or locally manufactured tiles for my market?
**A:** A balanced mix works best. Local SA tiles (Johnson Tiles, Ceramic Industries) offer better margins on volume sales and faster restocking. European imports from Spain and Italy command higher retail prices and attract premium customers. We recommend 60-70% local for volume and cash flow, 30-40% imports for margin and showroom differentiation.

**Q79:** What exclusive ranges does Tiletoria offer that I can't buy from other wholesalers?
**A:** Tiletoria's exclusive wholesale ranges include: our exclusive mosaic range (decorative mosaic tiles and feature strips), our laminate flooring laminate flooring, our laminate flooring LVT/vinyl flooring, and selected imported tile lines with territorial exclusivity arrangements. These ranges protect you from direct price competition with other merchants carrying the same brands.

**Q80:** How often do you add new products to your wholesale range?
**A:** New products are introduced quarterly, aligning with seasonal buying cycles. Major range launches coincide with pre-season shows in February-March and August-September. We typically add 15-30 new SKUs per quarter across tiles, flooring, and sanware. Your account manager will brief you on upcoming launches and provide samples.

**Q81:** What large-format tile sizes do you stock, and are they popular in secondary markets?
**A:** We stock large-format porcelain from 600x1200mm up to 1200x1200mm and 1200x2600mm slabs. While 600x1200mm has strong demand across all markets, the larger formats (1200x1200mm+) sell primarily in affluent urban and coastal markets like leading adhesive manufacturerslito, George, and Stellenbosch. For smaller towns, focus on 600x600mm and 600x1200mm.

**Q82:** Do you stock outdoor/patio tiles suitable for the South African climate?
**A:** Yes, our outdoor range includes 20mm thick porcelain pavers (600x600mm, 600x1200mm) with R11-R13 slip ratings, suitable for direct laying on grass, gravel, or pedestals. We also stock anti-slip textured porcelain floor tiles (R10-R11) for patio and pool surround applications. These are strong sellers in coastal and braai-focused markets.

**Q83:** What tile formats and designs are trending in KZN vs Gauteng vs Western Cape right now?

**Q84:** Do you stock rectified tiles, and what is the price difference vs non-rectified?
**A:** Yes, we stock extensive rectified tile ranges, particularly in porcelain floor tiles 600x600mm and above. Rectified tiles are typically 10-20% more expensive at wholesale than equivalent non-rectified, but they command higher retail prices due to the premium look of minimal grout joints. Rectified is recommended for premium showrooms.

**Q85:** What is the availability of SABS/SANS-compliant tiles for commercial projects?
**A:** All tiles sold by Tiletoria meet or exceed SANS 1449 standards where applicable. We can provide test certificates and compliance documentation for commercial and NHBRC projects. Our commercial-grade porcelain tiles are rated for heavy traffic (PEI 4-5) and meet slip resistance requirements for public spaces.

**Q86:** Do you stock tiles specifically suited to coastal/high-humidity environments like Richards Bay or leading adhesive manufacturerslito?
**A:** Yes, for coastal environments we recommend: porcelain floor tiles with R10-R11 slip ratings (resistant to salt and moisture), anti-corrosion fixing materials, and vitrified porcelain with water absorption below 0.5%. Avoid lower-grade ceramics in outdoor coastal applications — our team can specify the right products for your coastal customers.

**Q87:** What bathroom accessories and finishing products do you stock alongside tiles?
**A:** Beyond tiles, we stock silicone sealants (colour-matched to popular tile colours), tile trims (aluminium and PVC in various colours), spacers, tiling tools (trowels, mixers, levels), waterproofing membranes (Cemcrete, leading adhesive manufacturers), grouts (epoxy and cementitious), and cleaning products. These ancillaries add profitable add-on sales.

**Q88:** Do you offer any eco-friendly or sustainable tile options for the environmentally conscious consumer?
**A:** Yes, several of our European suppliers manufacture tiles with recycled content, energy-efficient production processes, and environmental certifications (EMAS, ISO 14001). We also stock porcelain tiles made with pre-consumer recycled material. Your account manager can identify the current eco-friendly options in our range.

**Q89:** How do I decide which products from your full range to stock in my 200-800 SKU inventory?
**A:** Your account manager will work with you to curate a range plan based on: your local market demographics, competitor analysis, your target customer segment, available showroom space, and your capital availability. We recommend a pyramid approach: 20% of SKUs generating 80% of sales (fast movers), 60% core range, 20% speciality/premium.

**Q90:** What is the difference between glazed porcelain and full-body porcelain, and which should I stock?
**A:** Glazed porcelain has a decorative glaze layer over a porcelain body — wider design variety but the colour doesn't run through. Full-body (unglazed/through-body) porcelain has consistent colour and pattern throughout, making it more durable and chip-resistant. Stock glazed porcelain for residential walls and floors; full-body for high-traffic commercial and outdoor applications.

**Q91:** Do you stock tiles suitable for underfloor heating systems?
**A:** Yes, porcelain tiles are ideal for underfloor heating due to their excellent thermal conductivity. We can provide tiles with specific thermal conductivity ratings for UFH applications. Laminate and LVT flooring also work with underfloor heating up to certain temperature limits — check specific product specifications with your account manager.

**Q92:** What is the price range at wholesale for your entry-level vs premium tile ranges?

**Q93:** How do I know if a product in your range is being discontinued so I can clear my stock?
**A:** Discontinuation notices are sent to all affected wholesale accounts 60-90 days before stock runs out. Your account manager will proactively contact you if you're buying a product scheduled for discontinuation. We also recommend monitoring stock levels — if a SKU shows consistently low availability, it may be exiting the range.

**Q94:** Can I get access to your full stock file or catalogue to plan my range?
**A:** Yes, active wholesale accounts receive access to our full product catalogue including stock availability, new product previews, and detailed specifications. This is available through our online portal or as a downloadable file. Your account manager can also provide category-specific line lists with current stock status.

**Q95:** Do you stock commercial-grade tiles for builders and contractors doing retail and office fit-outs?
**A:** Yes, our commercial range includes heavy-duty porcelain (PEI 4-5 rating), large-format technical porcelain (1200x1200mm and above), anti-slip commercial flooring (R11-R13), and through-body porcelain for high-traffic areas. We also supply raised-access floor systems and porcelain tiles for facade/cladding applications.

**Q96:** What is the availability of matching floor and wall tile collections?
**A:** Many of our ranges include coordinated floor and wall tiles in the same design family, allowing customers to create cohesive bathrooms and living spaces. Your account manager can provide a list of current coordinated collections. These 'suite' sales increase average transaction value significantly.

**Q97:** Do you stock tile trims, edging, and transition strips, or do I need another supplier for those?
**A:** We stock a comprehensive range of aluminium and PVC tile trims in various colours (silver, white, black, champagne, bronze), as well as stair nosing, expansion joints, and transition profiles. This allows you to offer a complete tiling solution to your customers without sourcing trims elsewhere.

**Q98:** Are there any product categories you don't stock that I should source elsewhere to complete my range?

**Q99:** How do you manage quality control on imported tiles — what if there's a batch issue?
**A:** All containers undergo quality inspection at origin before shipment and again upon arrival at our warehouses. Batch consistency is verified, and any quality issues are flagged before stock enters our system. If you encounter a batch issue (colour variation, sizing inconsistency), report it immediately — we'll investigate and replace or credit affected stock.

**Q100:** Can I request specific products or brands that you don't currently stock?
**A:** Yes, product requests are welcomed. If you identify a product or brand with potential in your market, submit the details to your account manager. If the volume justifies it, we'll explore adding it to our range or importing it specially. We regularly expand our range based on wholesale customer feedback and market trends.

---

## Category 5: Lead Times & Import Planning

**Q101:** What are the current lead times for tiles imported from Spain and Italy?
**A:** Standard factory lead times from Spain and Italy are 6-8 weeks from order confirmation to ex-factory. Add 2-3 weeks for sea freight to Durban or Cape Town, plus 1 week for customs clearance and inland transport to our warehouses. Total pipeline: approximately 10-12 weeks from factory order to our warehouse shelf.

**Q102:** How long do Brazilian porcelain tiles take to arrive?
**A:** Brazilian tiles have shorter lead times than European imports — typically 4-6 weeks factory lead time plus 2-3 weeks sea freight to Durban. Total pipeline is approximately 7-9 weeks. Brazilian products are popular for their competitive pricing and reasonable quality, making them a good mid-range option.

**Q103:** What about tiles from Turkey, India, and China — what are those lead times?
**A:** Turkish tiles: 6-8 weeks factory + 3-4 weeks sea freight = 10-12 weeks total. Indian tiles: 4-6 weeks factory + 4-5 weeks sea freight = 9-11 weeks total. Chinese tiles: 4-6 weeks factory + 5-6 weeks sea freight = 10-12 weeks total. These sources offer price-competitive options, particularly for ceramic wall tiles and mosaics.

**Q104:** When should I place my pre-season orders to ensure stock arrives for the building season?
**A:** For the September-April building season (South African summer/autumn), place your pre-season orders by June-July to ensure stock arrives by September. For the May-August period, order by February-March. These timelines account for factory lead times, sea freight, and potential customs delays.

**Q105:** How do I know what stock is currently in the import pipeline and when it will arrive?
**A:** Your account manager provides regular pipeline reports showing stock currently on order, in production, in transit, and at port. These reports include estimated arrival dates at our warehouses. You can place forward orders against pipeline stock to secure your allocation before it hits the warehouse floor.

**Q106:** What happens if there are customs delays at Durban or Cape Town port?
**A:** Customs clearance typically takes 3-5 business days but can extend to 2-3 weeks during busy periods or if inspections are required. We work with experienced clearing agents to minimise delays. If customs delays affect your forward order, your account manager will advise you of the revised arrival date and adjust delivery scheduling accordingly.

**Q107:** Can I place a forward order for stock that hasn't left the factory yet?
**A:** Yes, forward ordering is standard practice and encouraged. You can place orders against stock in production or planned for production. A deposit secures your allocation. This is particularly important for popular SKUs and new range launches where demand often exceeds initial stock availability.

**Q108:** How accurate are your estimated arrival dates for incoming containers?
**A:** Container arrival estimates are typically accurate within a 1-2 week window. Factory production schedules, shipping line reliability, and port congestion can all cause variations. We update our pipeline reports weekly, and your account manager will notify you of any significant changes to estimated arrival dates.

**Q109:** Do you offer air freight for urgent orders, and what does it cost?
**A:** Air freight is available for urgent orders but is significantly more expensive than sea freight — typically 8-12 times the cost. It is rarely justified for bulk tile orders unless there's a critical project deadline. For urgent sample needs or small high-value items (mosaic sheets, special trims), air freight may be viable.

**Q110:** What is the best time of year to buy imported tiles to avoid peak season delays?
**A:** Avoid ordering during Chinese New Year (January-February) and European summer holidays (July-August) when factories operate at reduced capacity. The optimal ordering windows are March-May and September-November when factories are at full production and shipping schedules are most reliable.

**Q111:** How do I plan my inventory for the December holiday period when building activity slows?
**A:** Most merchants reduce forward orders placed in October-November, focusing on stock for immediate needs only. Building activity typically picks up again in mid-January. Plan to carry sufficient stock of fast-moving items through December but avoid over-committing to slow-moving or seasonal products that will sit for 6+ weeks.

**Q112:** Can I get stock commitment from you for the full year if I provide an annual forecast?
**A:** For larger wholesale accounts (R5 million+ annual purchases), we offer annual stock planning meetings where you provide your forecast and we commit to maintaining availability on agreed core SKUs. This is documented in an annual supply plan and reviewed quarterly. It doesn't guarantee stock but prioritises your allocation.

**Q113:** What happens if a product I forward-ordered arrives and I can't take delivery immediately?
**A:** We'll hold your forward-ordered stock at our warehouse for up to 30 days at no charge. Beyond 30 days, a nominal storage fee of R15-R25 per pallet per week may apply. If you anticipate a delay, contact your account manager to discuss options — we can often arrange phased deliveries or extended holding.

**Q114:** Do you share your container shipping schedule so I can plan around it?
**A:** Yes, major wholesale accounts receive our container arrival forecast showing expected container arrivals by week for the next 3-6 months. This helps you plan your ordering and cash flow around incoming stock availability. Your account manager will share this schedule during your quarterly business reviews.

**Q115:** How do factory shutdowns in Europe (summer holidays) affect your stock availability in SA?
**A:** European factories typically shut for 2-4 weeks in July-August. We plan for this by increasing our pre-shutdown orders in May-June to ensure adequate stock through the shutdown period. You should also plan your ordering accordingly — place orders by May for stock needed in August-October.

**Q116:** What is the lead time for special-order or custom-made tiles?
**A:** Special-order tiles (non-stock sizes, custom colours, bespoke designs) typically require 12-16 weeks from order confirmation. This includes factory production time, sea freight, and customs clearance. Custom mosaic designs through our exclusive mosaic range require 10-14 weeks. Deposits of 50-70% are required for all special orders.

**Q117:** How do I avoid running out of stock during your peak delivery periods?
**A:** Monitor your stock levels weekly and set reorder points at 4-6 weeks of cover (based on your average weekly sales). Place reorders as soon as stock hits your reorder point — don't wait until you're nearly out. For fast-moving SKUs, consider standing orders. Your account manager can help you set up automated reorder alerts.

**Q118:** Can I visit your overseas suppliers with you to select products directly from the factory?
**A:** Yes, we organise annual buyer trips to Italy, Spain, and other sourcing markets for larger wholesale accounts. These trips typically coincide with major trade fairs like Cersaie (Bologna) and Cevisama (Valencia). You select products directly, and we handle the importing. Contact your regional manager about upcoming trips.

**Q119:** How do I manage currency risk when placing large forward orders on imported stock?
**A:** For very large forward orders, discuss currency hedging options with your account manager. We can fix exchange rates for confirmed orders, protecting both parties from currency volatility. Alternatively, your business can hedge forex exposure independently through your bank. This is particularly relevant for orders over R500,000.

**Q120:** What is the process if a container arrives and the stock quality doesn't meet expectations?
**A:** We inspect all containers on arrival. If quality issues are identified (defects, colour variation, incorrect sizing), the affected stock is quarantined and not released for sale. We negotiate with the factory for replacement, credit, or price adjustment. If you've placed a forward order against affected stock, you'll be immediately notified and offered alternatives.

**Q121:** How do exchange rate fluctuations affect your pricing between price list updates?
**A:** Our quarterly price list updates capture cumulative exchange rate movements since the last update. For major currency swings (>10% in a month), we may issue interim price adjustments with appropriate notice. Forward orders placed at confirmed prices are protected from exchange rate changes — the price is locked at order confirmation.

**Q122:** Do you maintain safety stock on core SKUs, or do I need to forecast my own requirements?
**A:** We maintain safety stock on our top 200-300 core SKUs based on national demand forecasting. However, demand spikes or supply disruptions can deplete safety stock. We recommend you maintain your own 4-6 week cover and place reorders proactively. Don't rely solely on our safety stock — if we run out, the reorder lead time still applies.

**Q123:** Can I get priority allocation on incoming stock if I commit to volume?
**A:** Yes, wholesale accounts with volume commitments receive priority allocation on incoming containers. Your committed volumes are flagged in our system, and stock is allocated to you before uncommitted stock is released for general sale. This is formalised through your annual supply plan or pre-season order commitments.

**Q124:** What contingency plans do you have if a factory or shipping line has a major disruption?
**A:** We source from multiple factories across different countries, providing natural diversification. If one factory or shipping lane is disrupted, we activate alternative sourcing. Our warehouse network holds 2-3 months of core stock cover as a buffer. For critical SKUs, we maintain relationships with secondary factories for rapid switching.

**Q125:** How does the war in Ukraine or global shipping crises affect your lead times and pricing?
**A:** Global disruptions (shipping crises, geopolitical events, Suez Canal incidents) can extend lead times by 2-6 weeks and increase freight costs. We monitor global shipping conditions weekly and adjust our ordering and pricing accordingly. During disruption periods, we increase our safety stock levels and recommend merchants carry slightly higher inventory cover.


---

## Category 6: Delivery & Logistics

**Q126:** Do you deliver to smaller towns like Newcastle, Witbank, Klerksdorp, and Ladysmith?
**A:** Yes, we deliver nationwide including all secondary cities and towns. For areas within 60km of our Cape Town, Durban, Johannesburg, or Paarden Eiland branches, we use our own fleet. For more remote areas like Ladysmith, Newcastle, Kimberley, and Richards Bay, we use contracted transport partners. Delivery to outlying areas typically takes 2-4 business days.

**Q127:** What does delivery cost to outlying areas, and who pays for it?
**A:** Delivery costs vary by distance and order size. Within 60km of a branch, delivery is often included for orders over R10,000-R15,000 depending on your account tier. For outlying areas, delivery is calculated per kilometer or per pallet. Typical rates range from R400-R1,200 per pallet for regional delivery and R1,500-R3,500 per pallet for long-distance delivery. Your account manager will provide a specific rate card for your area.

**Q128:** Can I collect stock directly from your warehouse to save on delivery costs?
**A:** Yes, self-collection is welcomed and is free of charge. Our warehouses in Paarden Eiland (Cape Town), Durban, Johannesburg, and Paarden Eiland are equipped for customer collections. Please arrange collection appointments with your account manager so your order can be picked and ready for loading. Loading assistance is provided.

**Q129:** What is your delivery capacity — how many pallets can you deliver on one truck?
**A:** Our rigid trucks carry 8-14 pallets, and our articulated vehicles carry 22-28 pallets. For large orders, we arrange full truckload (FTL) delivery. For smaller orders, we use part-load services. Our combined fleet and contractor network can handle up to 400 tons of deliveries per day nationally.

**Q130:** How do I schedule a delivery, and how much notice do you need?
**A:** Contact your account manager or call our dispatch office. Standard orders require 24 hours' notice. Large orders (10+ pallets) require 48 hours. Deliveries are scheduled Monday to Friday, 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM. Saturday deliveries can be arranged in major metros for an additional fee. We'll provide a delivery window on the day prior.

**Q131:** Can you deliver to multiple branches if I have a group account with several locations?
**A:** Yes, multi-drop deliveries can be arranged for group accounts. Each branch address is loaded as a separate delivery point on your account. Delivery costs are calculated per drop. For efficiency, we recommend consolidating orders per branch rather than frequent small deliveries. Your account manager will design an optimal delivery schedule.

**Q132:** What happens if I'm not at my premises when the delivery arrives?
**A:** Our driver will call the contact number on the delivery note 30 minutes before arrival.
