If no one is available to receive the delivery, it may be returned to our warehouse and a redelivery fee will apply. Please ensure someone is available to check and sign for the delivery during the agreed window.

**Q133:** Do your drivers assist with offloading, or do I need to have staff available?
**A:** Our drivers assist with offloading to the kerbside or designated offloading area using tail-lift and pallet jack equipment. Offloading into your warehouse or upstairs is your responsibility. For large orders, ensure you have a forklift or sufficient staff available. Special offloading requirements must be discussed when booking delivery.

**Q134:** Can you deliver to a construction site instead of my showroom/warehouse?
**A:** Yes, site deliveries are common for project orders. Provide the site address and contact person details when placing your order. Note that construction sites must have suitable access for our vehicles and a safe offloading area. Site deliveries to remote locations may incur additional charges due to access difficulty.

**Q135:** What areas do you consider 'outlying' and what is the delivery timeframe to these areas?
**A:** Outlying areas are those more than 100km from our three branches. Examples include: George, Mossel Bay (3-4 days from Cape Town), Kimberley, Upington (3-5 days from Johannesburg), Richards Bay, Empangeni (2-3 days from Durban), and East London (2-3 days from PE or Durban). Your account manager can confirm exact timeframes for your location.

**Q136:** Do you offer tracking for deliveries so I can tell my customer when stock will arrive?
**A:** For deliveries within our own fleet's 60km radius, same-day tracking is available via phone to dispatch. For contractor deliveries to outlying areas, our transport partners provide waybill tracking. Your account manager can provide the driver's contact number or contractor tracking reference on request.

**Q137:** What happens if stock is damaged during transit to my premises?
**A:** All deliveries must be checked at the time of delivery. Any damage must be noted on the delivery docket and signed by the driver. Report damage to your account manager within 24 hours with photos. We'll arrange collection of damaged stock and replacement at our cost if the damage occurred in our custody.

**Q138:** Can I arrange my own transport to collect from your warehouse?
**A:** Yes, many of our outlying customers arrange their own transport — particularly those using shared load services or returning empty trucks. Notify your account manager of your collection arrangements so we can have your order ready and provide the warehouse contact details and collection procedures.

**Q139:** Do you deliver across borders to Botswana, Namibia, Zimbabwe, or Mozambique?
**A:** Yes, cross-border deliveries are arranged through our export division and contracted cross-border transport partners. Delivery includes all export documentation, customs clearing at the border, and onward transport. Lead times are 5-10 days depending on the destination and border queues. Pricing is available in ZAR or USD.

**Q140:** What is the difference between your own fleet delivery and contractor delivery in terms of service?
**A:** Our own fleet (60km radius from branches) offers next-day delivery, direct driver communication, and full control over handling. Contractor deliveries to outlying areas may take 2-4 days and involve hand-offs between transport legs. Both services include the same damage liability and insurance coverage. We monitor contractor performance closely.

**Q141:** How do you handle deliveries during load shedding?
**A:** All our warehouses have backup power systems to maintain picking and dispatch operations during load shedding. Our fleet operates normally. However, delivery schedules may experience minor delays during extended load shedding periods due to traffic congestion. We prioritise cold chain and time-sensitive deliveries.

**Q142:** Can I request a specific delivery date or time window for my order?
**A:** Yes, preferred delivery dates can be requested when placing your order. For time-sensitive deliveries, we offer morning (8 AM-12 PM) or afternoon (12 PM-4 PM) windows in major metros. Specific time requests (e.g., 10 AM sharp) cannot be guaranteed due to traffic and routing variables, but we'll do our best to accommodate.

**Q143:** Do you charge a redelivery fee if I miss the first delivery attempt?
**A:** Yes, a redelivery fee of R350-R750 applies for missed deliveries depending on distance from the branch. This covers the additional transport cost. To avoid redelivery fees, ensure someone is available during the delivery window or notify us at least 4 hours in advance if you need to reschedule.

**Q144:** Is there an option for after-hours or weekend delivery?
**A:** After-hours and Saturday deliveries can be arranged in major metropolitan areas (Cape Town, Durban, Johannesburg, Pretoria) for an additional surcharge of R500-R1,000. Weekend and after-hours delivery must be booked at least 48 hours in advance. These services are not available in outlying areas.

**Q145:** How do you handle delivery of fragile items like mosaic sheets and decorative tiles?
**A:** Mosaic sheets and decorative tiles are packed in protective packaging with reinforced corners. They're loaded on top of heavier pallets and clearly marked as fragile. Despite this care, we recommend ordering 5-10% extra on mosaic sheets to account for potential breakage in transit — this is standard industry practice.

**Q146:** What is the maximum weight per pallet, and does this affect delivery cost?
**A:** Standard tile pallets weigh 800-1,200kg depending on tile format and thickness. Our trucks and tail-lifts are rated for these weights. Delivery costs are typically calculated per pallet space rather than by weight, so a heavier pallet of thick porcelain costs the same to deliver as a lighter pallet of ceramic wall tiles.

**Q147:** Do you offer crane truck or hiab delivery for sites without forklift access?
**A:** Crane truck delivery can be arranged at an additional cost for sites without forklift access or where pallets need to be lifted over walls or onto upper floors. This must be requested when booking delivery so we can allocate the right vehicle. Hiab delivery costs R1,500-R3,500 additional depending on location and lift requirements.

**Q148:** Can you deliver to my customer's home address if they buy directly from me?
**A:** Direct-to-customer delivery is not a standard wholesale service — our delivery agreement is to your business premises. However, for large project orders, we can deliver to the end customer's address if you request it in writing and accept liability for the delivery. A surcharge and delivery indemnity form apply.

**Q149:** How do I update my delivery address or add a new delivery location to my account?
**A:** Email your account manager with the new delivery address and contact details. New addresses are verified and added to your account within 24 hours. For regular multi-location deliveries, we can set up multiple delivery points on your account with individual rate cards.

**Q150:** What insurance coverage do you have for goods in transit?
**A:** All goods are insured while in our custody and during transit on our vehicles or contracted transport. Coverage is at wholesale value. Claims for transit damage must be reported within 24 hours with photographic evidence and the signed delivery note. Claims are processed within 5-7 business days.

---

## Category 7: Payment Terms & Credit

**Q151:** What standard credit terms do you offer wholesale accounts?
**A:** Standard wholesale credit terms are 30 days from statement date. Statements are issued monthly, and payment is due by the last day of the following month. For example, all purchases in January are due for payment by the last day of February. No interest is charged if paid within terms.

**Q152:** Is there a credit limit on my wholesale account, and how is it determined?
**A:** Yes, each credit account has a credit limit based on your annual turnover, trading history with Tiletoria, credit bureau assessment, and trade reference feedback. New accounts typically start with limits of R50,000-R150,000. Limits are reviewed quarterly and increased as your trading history and payment record justify.

**Q153:** What happens if I exceed my credit limit?
**A:** If you approach your credit limit, your account manager will contact you to discuss options: (1) Making a payment to free up credit, (2) Requesting a temporary credit limit increase, or (3) Placing further orders on a pro forma basis until the account is within limit. We aim to avoid disrupting your supply.

**Q154:** Can I request a credit limit increase, and what is the process?
**A:** Yes, credit limit increases can be requested through your account manager. We review your purchase history, payment record, and updated financial information. Accounts with consistent on-time payment for 6+ months typically qualify for increases. Temporary limit increases can also be arranged for large seasonal orders.

**Q155:** Do you offer settlement discounts for early payment?
**A:** Yes, a settlement discount of 2.5-3% applies to accounts that settle their full monthly statement within 7 days of the statement date. The discount is credited to your account in the following month. To qualify, payment must clear in our bank account within the 7-day window — EFT payments should be made by Day 5 to allow for clearance.

**Q156:** What payment methods do you accept?
**A:** We accept EFT (electronic funds transfer) — our preferred method, debit order (for accounts in good standing), and direct deposit at major national banks and major national banks branches. We do not accept credit card payments for wholesale accounts. Cash payments above R25,000 require FICA documentation.

**Q157:** Can I set up a debit order for automatic monthly payment?
**A:** Yes, debit order facilities are available for established wholesale accounts (6+ months trading history, good payment record). Debit orders are processed on the 25th of each month for the current statement balance. This ensures you never miss a payment and always qualify for settlement discount. Debit order forms are available from our accounts department.

**Q158:** What happens if I can't pay my account on time — do you have a hardship policy?
**A:** We understand that cash flow challenges affect all businesses from time to time. If you're experiencing difficulty, contact your account manager or our credit controller before the due date — not after. We can discuss payment plan arrangements, temporary extensions, or converting to pro forma for a period. Communication is key.

**Q159:** Do you charge interest on overdue accounts?
**A:** Accounts not settled within 60 days of statement date may be charged interest at the prevailing prime rate plus 3%, calculated monthly on the outstanding balance. However, we prefer to resolve payment issues through discussion rather than punitive charges. Interest is discretionary and typically waived for first-time late payments with valid reasons.

**Q160:** How do I reconcile my account if I think there's a discrepancy on my statement?
**A:** Contact our accounts department on 021 555 5700 or email accounts@tiletoria.co.za with your query. Provide the invoice numbers and amounts in question. We'll investigate and respond within 3 business days. Common discrepancies include duplicate invoicing, unallocated payments, or delivery note mismatches — all are quickly resolvable.

**Q161:** Can I pay individual invoices as they fall due, or do I have to wait for the monthly statement?
**A:** You can pay individual invoices at any time — many merchants prefer this for cash flow management. Each invoice shows its due date based on the invoice date plus 30 days. However, settlement discounts are calculated on the monthly statement total paid within 7 days, so individual invoice payments don't qualify for the settlement discount.
**A:** (continued) If you prefer to manage cash flow by paying invoices as they fall due rather than waiting for the monthly statement, you can do so. Each invoice has its own due date (invoice date + 30 days). However, settlement discounts are calculated on the full monthly statement balance paid within 7 days of statement issue, so this approach may forfeit settlement discounts.

**Q162:** What is your policy on holding post-dated cheques?
**A:** We do not accept cheques as standard payment. All payments must be by EFT, debit order, or direct deposit. This policy applies to all wholesale accounts regardless of trading history or account size.

**Q163:** Do you offer trade finance or stock finance facilities?
**A:** We don't provide direct trade finance, but we have referral arrangements with commercial finance providers who specialise in inventory and trade finance for building material retailers. These facilities can fund your stock purchases against confirmed orders or warehouse receipts. Ask your account manager for an introduction.

**Q164:** How do I get a statement of account, and how often are they issued?
**A:** Monthly statements are emailed to your designated accounts contact by the 3rd working day of each month. Statements show all invoices, credit notes, payments, and the current balance. If you need an interim statement or account query at any time, contact our accounts department — statements can be issued on demand.

**Q165:** Can I have multiple authorised signatories on my account for payment approvals?
**A:** Yes, you can designate multiple authorised signatories for your account. Each signatory must be listed on your account mandate form. For credit limit increases, payment plan arrangements, or account changes, any listed signatory can authorise. We recommend having at least two signatories for operational continuity.

**Q166:** What happens to my credit account if my business changes ownership or structure?
**A:** If your business undergoes a change of ownership, amalgamation, or restructuring, notify us immediately. The new owner/director will need to submit updated documentation and may need to reapply for credit. During transition periods, the account may revert to pro forma until new credit assessments are completed.

**Q167:** Do you report payment behaviour to credit bureaus?
**A:** We report account payment behaviour to major South African credit bureaus (Experian, TransUnion, XDS) for accounts with credit facilities. Positive payment behaviour strengthens your business credit profile. Adverse information is reported only after prolonged default and following formal notification processes.

**Q168:** Can I get a letter of good standing or trade reference from Tiletoria for my other suppliers?
**A:** Yes, we provide trade references for accounts in good standing upon written request. Allow 2-3 business days for processing. Trade references confirm your account status, trading history, credit limit, and payment record. We respond to all trade reference requests from other suppliers as a matter of course.

**Q169:** What is your policy on credit holds — will you stop supplying me if I'm a few days late?
**A:** We don't place accounts on immediate credit hold for minor delays. Accounts are reviewed when 15 days overdue, and a friendly reminder is sent. At 30 days overdue, supply may be placed on hold until payment is made or a payment plan is agreed. We always attempt contact before placing any account on hold.

**Q170:** Can I get 60-day or 90-day terms if I've been trading with you for several years?
**A:** Extended terms (60 days) may be considered for large, long-standing accounts with exemplary payment records and significant annual purchase volumes (R5 million+). 90-day terms are not standard. Extended terms are reviewed annually and require regional manager approval. Settlement discounts don't apply to extended-term accounts.

**Q171:** Do you offer consignment stock arrangements where I pay only when I sell?
**A:** Pure consignment is not standard, but we offer "sale-or-return" arrangements on selected new product launches where you can return unsold stock within 90 days at full credit. This is available for accounts with strong track records and is negotiated per product launch. Your account manager will advise when such arrangements are available.

**Q172:** How quickly are payments reflected on my account after I make an EFT?
**A:** EFT payments between major South African banks typically reflect within 24-48 hours. To ensure prompt allocation, use your Tiletoria account number as the payment reference. Email proof of payment to accounts@tiletoria.co.za for immediate allocation. Same-day payments must be made before 2:00 PM.

**Q173:** Can I offset my volume rebate against my account balance instead of taking it as a credit?
**A:** Yes, quarterly and annual volume rebates can be applied as a credit to your account balance rather than held for future purchases. For accounts with outstanding balances, rebates are automatically offset against amounts owing unless you request otherwise. Contact accounts to specify your rebate preference.

**Q174:** What documentation do you provide for VAT purposes?
**A:** All invoices and credit notes include full VAT breakdowns at the prevailing rate (currently 15%). Monthly statements summarise VAT per transaction. At year-end, we provide a VAT reconciliation statement on request. All documentation is SARS-compliant and includes our VAT registration number.

**Q175:** If I'm switching from another supplier, can you match or improve their payment terms?
**A:** We review payment term requests on merit. If you can demonstrate favourable terms with a comparable supplier and have a strong credit profile, we'll consider matching or improving terms. New accounts typically start on 30-day terms, with extension to 60 days considered after 12 months of proven payment performance.

---

## Category 8: Returns & Stock Issues

**Q176:** What is your policy on returning defective stock?
**A:** Defective stock can be returned for full credit if reported within 30 days of delivery. The defect must be a manufacturing fault (cracks, chips, glazing defects, colour variation beyond industry norms) rather than damage caused by incorrect handling or installation. Return the stock in original packaging with a copy of the invoice.

**Q177:** How do I report a quality issue with stock I've received?
**A:** Contact your account manager within 24 hours of discovering the issue. Provide photos of the defect, the batch number from the packaging, your invoice number, and the quantity affected. We'll assess the complaint and arrange collection if validated. For minor issues, a credit may be issued without collection.

**Q178:** What is the timeframe for returning stock I ordered in error?
**A:** Stock ordered in error can be returned within 14 days of delivery provided it is in original, undamaged packaging and in resaleable condition. A restocking fee of 10-15% applies to returns due to customer ordering error. Special-order and imported items are not returnable unless defective.

**Q179:** Do you accept returns of stock that has been in my warehouse for several months but hasn't sold?
**A:** Standard returns are accepted within 30 days. For slow-moving stock returns beyond 30 days, we may accept returns on a case-by-case basis, particularly if you're exchanging for higher-velocity products or the stock is part of a discontinued range. A restocking fee of 15-20% typically applies to returns over 30 days old.

**Q180:** What happens if stock is damaged in transit to me — who is liable?
**A:** Tiletoria is liable for damage occurring during transit on our vehicles or contracted transport. Report transit damage within 24 hours, note it on the delivery docket, and provide photos. We'll collect the damaged stock and issue a credit or replacement. Damage not noted on delivery may not be claimable.

**Q181:** Can I return part-used pallets or broken boxes?
**A:** Full, unopened boxes or pallets are required for standard returns. Part-used boxes are not returnable unless the entire contents are defective. However, we may offer a partial credit for significantly over-ordered quantities where a genuine mistake was made — discuss with your account manager.

**Q182:** What is the process for returning stock — do you collect or do I need to bring it back?
**A:** For defective stock or our error: we arrange collection at our cost. For customer-error returns: you can deliver the stock back to our warehouse or we can collect at your cost (R350-R750 depending on distance). All returns require a returns authorisation number (RA number) from your account manager — unauthorised returns will not be processed.

**Q183:** How long does it take to process a credit note for returned stock?
**A:** Credit notes for approved returns are processed within 3-5 business days of the stock arriving back at our warehouse. The credit is applied to your account and reflected on your next statement. If you need the credit urgently, request an immediate interim credit note from accounts.

**Q184:** What if my customer returns tiles to me after installation — can I return them to Tiletoria?
**A:** Installed tiles cannot be returned to Tiletoria unless a manufacturing defect is proven. Standard industry practice is that tiles, once installed, are the installer's and end-customer's responsibility. If a genuine manufacturing defect is identified post-installation, we handle it through our product liability process — contact your account manager to initiate an investigation.

**Q185:** Do you have a warranty on your tiles, and what does it cover?
**A:** All tiles carry a manufacturer's warranty against defects in materials and workmanship. Warranty periods vary: ceramic wall tiles typically carry 5-year warranties, porcelain floor tiles 10-10 years (some lifetime), and laminate flooring 15-25 years depending on the range. Warranties cover manufacturing defects, not installation errors, wear and tear, or damage.

**Q186:** What if the colour of the tiles I receive doesn't match the sample I was shown?
**A:** Tile samples are representative, but batch-to-batch colour variation can occur within accepted industry tolerances. If the variation is significant and beyond industry norms, it constitutes a quality issue and can be returned. Always order from the same batch number for a single installation, and advise your customers to check batch numbers.

**Q187:** Can I return samples and sample boards that I no longer need?
**A:** Individual sample tiles given to you free of charge do not need to be returned — they're yours to keep or dispose of. Sample boards that were purchased or provided on loan must be returned in good condition. Loaned sample boards that are damaged or not returned may be charged to your account at replacement cost.

**Q188:** What is your policy on shade variation between different batches of the same tile?
**A:** Shade variation is inherent in tile manufacturing and is classified under ISO 10545 (V1-V4). V1 = uniform appearance, V4 = significant variation. The variation rating is indicated on our product specifications. We recommend ordering the full quantity from the same batch number to ensure consistency. Different batches should not be mixed in the same installation.

**Q189:** If stock arrives and it's the wrong product (not what I ordered), what happens?
**A:** Wrong product deliveries are corrected at our cost. We arrange collection of the incorrect stock and delivery of the correct product. If you need the correct stock urgently before we can collect the wrong delivery, we'll arrange an immediate replacement delivery and collect the error later. Report wrong deliveries immediately.

**Q190:** Do you offer a warranty on laminate and LVT flooring products?
**A:** Yes, our laminate flooring laminate carries warranties ranging from 15 years (residential AC3) to 25 years (commercial AC5). our laminate flooring LVT carries 10-20 year warranties depending on the wear layer thickness (0.3mm to 0.55mm). Warranties cover wear-through, staining, fading, and water resistance (for water-rated products) under normal use conditions.

**Q191:** What happens if I receive stock with broken cartons inside an intact pallet?
**A:** Broken cartons within an apparently intact pallet should be noted on the delivery docket at the time of delivery. Take photos before unpacking. Report to your account manager within 24 hours. We'll replace the broken cartons or credit the affected quantity. This type of damage usually occurs during rough handling in transit.

**Q192:** Can I return discontinued stock that I can no longer sell?
**A:** Discontinued stock returns are handled on a case-by-case basis. If we discontinue a product you hold significant stock of, we'll typically offer a stock buyback or exchange arrangement to assist your transition. For stock you've held beyond reasonable selling periods, a restocking fee applies. Contact your account manager to discuss.

**Q193:** What is your policy on returning sanware products like taps, toilets, and baths?
**A:** Sanware returns follow the same general policy: defective products can be returned within 30 days. Unopened, resaleable sanware can be returned within 14 days with a 10-15% restocking fee. Installed sanware cannot be returned. Special-order sanware items (non-stock colours, imported specials) are not returnable unless defective.

**Q194:** How do I handle a customer complaint about tile quality that I believe is an installation issue, not a product defect?
**A:** Many post-installation complaints are installation-related (lippage due to incorrect adhesive, cracking due to substrate movement, staining due to inadequate sealing). Refer the complaint to a qualified tiler or our technical team for assessment. If product defect is confirmed, we'll handle the warranty claim. If installation error, advise your customer accordingly.

**Q195:** Do you have a formal product recall process if a batch is found to be defective after distribution?
**A:** Yes, in the rare event of a product recall, we immediately notify all affected wholesale accounts with batch numbers, quantities supplied, and recommended actions. Affected stock is collected and credited, and replacement stock is expedited. Recalls are also posted on our trade portal and communicated by phone to all affected accounts.


**Q197:** What documentation do I need to provide when returning stock?
**A:** For all returns, you need: (1) A returns authorisation (RA) number from your account manager, (2) The original invoice number or copy, (3) A note explaining the reason for return, and (4) The stock in original packaging where possible. Without an RA number, returns cannot be processed and will be refused at the warehouse.

**Q198:** If I have a stock quality issue on a weekend or after hours, who do I contact?
**A:** For urgent after-hours quality issues, call your account manager's mobile number (provided in your welcome pack). If unreachable, leave a detailed voicemail and send photos via WhatsApp to the branch number. Non-urgent issues can be reported on the next business day. We monitor after-hours messages and respond to genuine emergencies.

**Q199:** How do you handle credits for price discrepancies — if I was charged the wrong price?
**A:** Price discrepancy credits are processed immediately upon verification. Email accounts@tiletoria.co.za with the invoice number, the product code, the price charged, and the correct price per your price list. Once verified against the current price list, a credit note is issued within 24 hours. No RA number is required for price adjustment credits.

**Q200:** Can I get an exchange instead of a credit for returned stock?
**A:** Yes, exchanges are preferred over credits where possible. When you request a return, specify whether you want a credit to your account or an exchange for alternative stock. Exchanges are processed immediately — the replacement stock is dispatched as soon as your return is collected or arrives at our warehouse.

---

## Category 9: Competing with National Chains

**Q201:** How can I compete with competitor retailers, competitor retailers, and competitor retailers when they have national advertising and buying power?
**A:** Your advantages over national chains are personal service, local knowledge, flexibility, and product differentiation. Stock exclusive ranges that chains don't carry (our exclusive mosaic range, our laminate flooring, our laminate flooring), offer expert advice that chains can't match, build relationships with local contractors, and provide services the chains won't — like free local delivery and after-hours support.

**Q202:** What exclusive products do you have that competitor retailers and competitor retailers cannot stock?
**A:** Our exclusive wholesale ranges include our exclusive mosaic range decorative mosaics, our laminate flooring laminate flooring, our laminate flooring LVT, and selected imported tile lines with exclusive distribution rights. When you stock these ranges, your customers cannot find the exact same products at the national chains — eliminating direct price comparison.

**Q203:** How do I prevent the chains from undercutting me on price for the same products?
**A:** Focus on products the chains don't stock. For commodity products where price competition is inevitable, differentiate through service: free local delivery, expert tiling advice, better displays, faster response times, and credit terms for good contractors. Build loyalty with local builders who value reliability over the lowest price.

**Q204:** Can I use Tiletoria's national reputation to build credibility with my local customers?
**A:** Yes, you're welcome to reference that you're an authorised Tiletoria wholesale partner and that your products are sourced from one of South Africa's largest tile distributors. We provide point-of-sale materials and branding that help communicate quality and reliability to your customers. However, you must not claim to be a Tiletoria-owned store.

**Q205:** What margin protection do I have if Tiletoria also supplies a merchant near me?
**A:** While we don't enforce strict territorial exclusivity, we respect the customer relationships you've built. We won't actively solicit your established contractor customers. If a competing merchant opens nearby, we supply both but don't share your pricing or customer information. Your competitive advantage comes from service and relationships, not supplier exclusivity.

**Q206:** How do national chains' prices compare to what I can offer as an independent?
**A:** National chains typically retail at 20-35% above their cost (including marketing and overheads). As an independent with lower overheads and Tiletoria's wholesale pricing, you can match or beat chain pricing on many products while maintaining healthy margins. Your advantage is greatest on exclusive ranges and service bundles.

**Q207:** Can I offer installation services to compete with chains that do?
**A:** Yes, many independents partner with local tilers or employ in-house installation teams. We can recommend qualified tilers in your area and provide installation training resources. Offering a supply-and-install package significantly increases your average transaction value and differentiates you from competitors who only supply.

**Q208:** How do I handle it when a customer comes in with a competitor retailers or competitor retailers quote asking me to beat it?
**A:** Ask to see the quote and identify the exact products specified. If it's a comparable product we stock, quote competitively but focus on value-adds: better service, faster delivery, expert advice, or a superior alternative product. Don't engage in a race to the bottom on price — sell the benefits of buying from a local merchant.

**Q209:** What role does showroom presentation play in competing with the chains?
**A:** Showroom presentation is critical — chains invest heavily in display. We provide display support including sample boards, display stands (for laminate and LVT), lifestyle imagery, and merchandising guidance. A well-displayed exclusive product range creates a showroom experience that chains can't replicate, justifying premium pricing.

**Q210:** How can I build loyalty with local contractors to protect against chain competition?
**A:** Contractors value reliability, credit terms, quick turnaround, and personalised service. Offer contractor loyalty programmes, volume discounts, priority ordering, dedicated parking/loading, early opening hours, and expert technical support. Build personal relationships — contractors stick with suppliers who make their lives easier.

**Q211:** Should I focus on the premium market, budget market, or both to avoid chain competition?
**A:** Most successful independents serve both segments with distinct product tiers: a budget-friendly entry-level range to compete with chains on price, and a premium imported range for customers who value quality and design. The premium segment often generates higher margins and more repeat business from discerning homeowners.

**Q212:** How do I use Tiletoria's buying power to my advantage when marketing to customers?
**A:** Communicate that your products are sourced from a major national importer with rigorous quality control and significant buying power. This implies quality assurance, consistent supply, and competitive pricing. Use phrases like "Sourced through South Africa's leading tile distributor" in your marketing materials.

**Q213:** What digital marketing support do you offer to help me compete online with the chains?
**A:** We provide product imagery, lifestyle photography, and product descriptions for your website and social media. Our marketing team can advise on digital strategy for independents. We also feature our wholesale partners on our trade referral network, directing consumer enquiries to local stockists.

**Q214:** Can I match the chains' promotional pricing during their sales events?
**A:** You can, but it's not always advisable. Chains run sales at very thin margins to drive foot traffic. Instead of matching their sale prices, run your own promotions on exclusive ranges where you have margin protection, or bundle products with services (free delivery, installation discount) that add value without eroding price.

**Q215:** How do I compete when a chain opens a new store in my town?
**A:** When a chain opens nearby, double down on your strengths: personal relationships with contractors, exclusive product ranges, local community involvement, and superior service. Don't try to outspend them on advertising. Instead, ensure every customer who walks into your store gets an experience they can't get at a chain. Retain your contractor base — they're your revenue foundation.

**Q216:** Do you supply any of the national chains, and does that create channel conflict?
**A:** Tiletoria supplies various national retailers and builders merchants (major building retailers, major building retailers, etc.). We manage channel conflict by maintaining different product ranges and brand positioning for different channels. Exclusive ranges like our exclusive mosaic range and our laminate flooring are reserved for independent merchants, not supplied to competing national tile retailers.

**Q217:** How can I compete on the commercial project market against chains with dedicated specification teams?
**A:** For commercial projects, leverage our Spec Lab facility in Menlyn, Johannesburg. We can support your commercial bids with product specifications, technical data, CAD support, and showroom visits for your clients. You act as the local supplier with Tiletoria providing the back-end specification and product support.

**Q218:** What is the 'shop local' movement, and how can I leverage it?
**A:** The shop local movement encourages consumers to support independent local businesses. Promote that you're locally owned, employ local people, pay local taxes, and reinvest in the community. Many consumers actively prefer buying from independents when the experience and product range meet their needs.

**Q219:** How important is contractor loyalty programme in competing with chains?
**A:** Contractor loyalty programmes are very important — contractors represent 40-60% of independent merchants' revenue. A well-structured programme offering volume rebates, credit terms, priority service, and rewards builds stickiness that chains struggle to match due to their bureaucratic processes.

**Q220:** Can I create my own private label brand using Tiletoria products to differentiate from chains?
**A:** Private label arrangements can be discussed for significant volume accounts. We can supply unbranded or white-label products for your own brand. This requires minimum annual volumes (typically R2 million+ in the product category) and a formal agreement. Contact your regional wholesale manager to explore.

**Q221:** How do I handle price-shopping customers who use my expertise but buy from chains?
**A:** Set clear boundaries: provide general advice freely but detailed specification and design advice only to committed customers. Charge a design consultation fee that is credited against their purchase. This protects your intellectual capital and ensures you're compensated for your expertise even if they don't buy immediately.

**Q222:** What after-sales services can I offer that chains typically don't?
**A:** Offer free post-installation check-ins, replacement tile holding (keep a box of their tiles for future repairs), cleaning and maintenance advice, and rapid response to any issues. These services cost little but build enormous loyalty. Chains are generally poor at after-sales service — it's your competitive advantage.

**Q223:** How do I build word-of-mouth referrals to compete with chain advertising budgets?
**A:** Deliver exceptional service on every transaction. Follow up after installation to ensure satisfaction. Ask happy customers for Google reviews and testimonials. Partner with local kitchen and bathroom designers, architects, and builders who can refer clients. Sponsor local community events for brand visibility.

**Q224:** Can I access the same imported designer tiles that competitor retailers stocks, or do they have exclusives?
**A:** Many Italian and Spanish factory lines are available to multiple distributors, and we source from the same premium factories as major chains. However, some chains have exclusive distribution agreements on specific brands or designs. Our import team can source comparable alternatives from the same or similar factories.

**Q225:** How do I maintain profitability when chains run aggressive 'loss leader' promotions?
**A:** Don't match loss leaders — chains use them to drive traffic, not profit. Focus on your full-margin products and value-added services during chain promotion periods. Your regular customers will remain loyal if you've built strong relationships. Use the quiet period during chain sales to plan your own exclusive range promotions.

---

## Category 10: Marketing & POS Support

**Q226:** What marketing materials do you provide to wholesale customers?
**A:** We provide a range of marketing support including: product catalogues (printed and digital), sample boards, colour cards, lifestyle photography, product specification sheets, brand logos and artwork, window posters, promotional banners, and social media content. Most materials are free for active wholesale accounts.

**Q227:** Can I get sample boards and display materials for my showroom?
**A:** Yes, sample boards are available for all major tile ranges and are either provided free with a committed forward order or purchased at cost. Full-size display panels are available for key porcelain and decorative ranges. Laminate and LVT display stands are provided on loan for stocked ranges. Contact your account manager to order display materials.

**Q228:** Do you provide lifestyle photography that I can use on my website and social media?
**A:** Yes, we have an extensive library of professional lifestyle photography showing our products installed in bathrooms, kitchens, living areas, and outdoor spaces. These high-resolution images are available for your website, social media, and printed materials. Contact marketing@tiletoria.co.za to request access to the image library.

**Q229:** Can you help me design my showroom layout to maximise sales?
**A:** Our wholesale team can provide showroom layout guidance and merchandising best practices based on successful independent merchants. For significant showroom investments, we can arrange a showroom design consultation. We also provide planograms for tile display systems and recommendations for display-to-stock ratios.

**Q230:** Do you supply printed catalogues that I can give to my customers?
**A:** Yes, printed wholesale catalogues are available in bulk quantities for your showroom. We can also provide co-branded catalogues featuring your business logo alongside the Tiletoria ranges you stock. Digital catalogues (PDF and flipbook format) are available for email distribution and website embedding.

**Q231:** What point-of-sale materials are available for promotional campaigns?
**A:** Point-of-sale materials include: A-frame sidewalk boards, window posters, in-store banners, price tags and shelf talkers, promotional flyers, and counter displays. For major promotions (pre-season shows, year-end sales), we provide complete POS kits. Order POS materials through your account manager 2-3 weeks before your promotion.

**Q232:** Can I get branded merchandise with my own logo for my showroom?
**A:** We don't produce customer-branded merchandise directly, but we can introduce you to our promotional merchandise partners who offer competitive rates on branded items: tote bags, tape measures, t-shirts, caps, and promotional gifts for contractors. Some items include Tiletoria co-branding.

**Q233:** Do you run national advertising that benefits my store as a stockist?
**A:** Tiletoria focuses its marketing on trade and specification channels rather than direct-to-consumer advertising. However, our brand presence at trade shows, in industry publications, and online benefits all our stockists. We also feature our wholesale partners on our website's stockist locator, driving consumer enquiries to your store.

**Q234:** Can I use Tiletoria branding in my own marketing materials?
**A:** Yes, authorised wholesale partners can use the Tiletoria logo and brand assets in their marketing, subject to our brand guidelines. You may describe yourself as an "Authorised Tiletoria Wholesale Partner" or "Stockist of Tiletoria Products." Brand guidelines and logo files are available from our marketing department.

**Q235:** Do you provide social media content that I can share on my business pages?
**A:** Yes, we regularly create social media content including product shots, installation photos, trend boards, and promotional graphics. This content is shared via our trade WhatsApp broadcast and email newsletter for you to repost. Using this content ensures consistent, professional messaging without you needing to create your own.

**Q236:** What type of showroom display system do you recommend for tile retailers?
**A:** We recommend a combination of: (1) Vertical sample boards (600x600mm or 600x1200mm) for browsing, (2) Full-size floor panels showing large-format tiles, (3) Bathroom and kitchen vignettes for lifestyle context, (4) A feature wall showcasing decorative and mosaic options, and (5) A clearance/specials area. Your account manager can recommend display suppliers.

**Q237:** Do you provide display stands for laminate flooring samples?
**A:** Yes, our laminate flooring and our laminate flooring display stands are provided on loan to wholesale customers who stock these ranges. The stands hold 20-40 sample boards and include branding and technical information. Display stands are maintained and updated by our flooring team. Contact your account manager to arrange delivery of a display stand.

**Q238:** Can I get custom-sized samples for my showroom displays?
**A:** Custom sample sizes can be arranged for bulk display requirements. Standard sample tiles are 100x100mm or 150x150mm cuttings. For full-size display panels (600x600mm or 600x1200mm), we can supply at a nominal cost or provide as part of a display support package for larger accounts.

**Q239:** Do you offer co-op marketing funds or advertising subsidies?
**A:** Co-op marketing funds are available for larger wholesale accounts (R3 million+ annual purchases). These funds are calculated as a percentage of purchases and can be used for joint advertising, showroom upgrades, or local marketing campaigns. Discuss co-op eligibility and fund usage with your regional wholesale manager.

**Q240:** How do I keep my showroom looking fresh and current without constant investment?
**A:** Rotate your displays quarterly to feature new arrivals and seasonal promotions. Use our free POS materials to refresh signage. Move bestsellers to prominent positions. Create themed displays ("Summer Outdoor Living," "Winter Warmth") using our lifestyle imagery. Regular showroom refreshes cost little but significantly impact sales.

**Q241:** Do you provide product training for my sales staff so they can better sell your ranges?
**A:** Yes, product training sessions are available for your sales team, either at your premises or at our nearest branch. Training covers product features, benefits, installation basics, competitor comparisons, and sales techniques. For new range launches, we provide training packs and can conduct team briefings via video call.

**Q242:** Can I get video content for my website showing product features and installations?
**A:** We have a growing library of product and installation video content. These include factory tour videos, product feature videos, installation technique demonstrations, and customer testimonial videos. Video files are available on request for embedding on your website or sharing on social media platforms.

**Q243:** What is the best way to display mosaic tiles to make them appealing to customers?
**A:** Mosaic tiles should be displayed on vertical boards with good lighting to highlight their texture and shimmer. Display them in lifestyle context — as feature strips in bathrooms, kitchen splashbacks, or pool surrounds. our exclusive mosaic range team provides display guidance and can supply pre-made display boards showcasing popular combinations.

**Q244:** Do you provide templates for advertising in local newspapers or community magazines?
**A:** Yes, advertising templates are available in various formats (quarter-page, half-page, full-page) that you can customise with your business details and current promotions. We also provide copy suggestions and design guidelines. High-resolution product images for advertising use are available from our marketing library.

**Q245:** Can I run joint promotions with other businesses (kitchen shops, bathroom shops) using your materials?
**A:** Absolutely — joint promotions with complementary businesses are highly effective. Our marketing materials can be adapted for joint campaigns. Many successful independents partner with local kitchen designers, plumbers, and interior decorators for cross-referral and bundled promotions.

**Q246:** How do I use your online tools to help customers visualise tiles in their homes?
**A:** We can provide access to room visualiser tools that allow customers to see how different tiles look in bathroom, kitchen, and living room settings. These tools can be embedded on your website or used on in-store tablets. Visualisers significantly increase conversion rates by helping uncertain customers make decisions.

**Q247:** Do you provide branded packaging or carry bags for my customers?
**A:** Branded Tiletoria carry bags are available for sample collections and small purchases. For larger orders, standard industry packaging applies. Custom co-branded packaging can be arranged for significant volume accounts. Contact our marketing team to discuss packaging options and minimum order quantities.

**Q248:** What signage do you recommend for the exterior of my store?
**A:** Exterior signage should be visible, professional, and clearly communicate what you sell: "Tiles | Flooring | Bathrooms" at minimum. Consider A-frame boards for daily promotions, window vinyl with lifestyle imagery, and illuminated signage for evening visibility. Your account manager can share examples of effective independent merchant signage.

**Q249:** How often should I update my showroom displays to reflect new trends?
**A:** Major display updates should coincide with new range arrivals — typically 2-4 times per year. Minor refreshes (rotating featured products, updating promotional signage) should happen monthly. Customers who visit regularly should see something new each time. Our seasonal buying calendar aligns display updates with peak selling periods.

**Q250:** Can I get access to your product data feeds for my e-commerce website?
**A:** Yes, product data feeds are available for wholesale partners with e-commerce platforms. The feed includes product codes, descriptions, specifications, pricing, stock status, and image URLs. Feeds are updated daily and can be integrated with most e-commerce platforms. Contact our IT support team for technical integration details.


---

## Category 11: Product Training & Knowledge

**Q251:** Do you offer product training sessions for my sales staff?
**A:** Yes, we offer comprehensive product training sessions for your sales team, either at your premises or at our nearest branch. Sessions cover product features, benefits, technical specifications, installation basics, and competitor comparisons. Training is free for active wholesale accounts and can be tailored to your team's experience level.

**Q252:** How often do you run training sessions, and how do I book one?
**A:** Training sessions are scheduled monthly at each branch and can also be arranged on-demand for your team. To book, contact your account manager with your preferred dates, topics, and number of attendees. We recommend quarterly training sessions to keep your team updated on new products and industry developments.

**Q253:** What technical knowledge do my sales staff need to sell tiles effectively?

**Q254:** Do you provide installation training for my contractor customers?
**A:** We conduct installation workshops and demonstrations for contractor groups in partnership with adhesive and grout manufacturers (leading adhesive manufacturers, leading adhesive manufacturers). These cover proper substrate preparation, adhesive selection and mixing, tile laying techniques (including large-format), grouting, and sealing. Contact your account manager to arrange a contractor workshop.

**Q255:** Can I get training specifically on your exclusive ranges like our exclusive mosaic range and our laminate flooring?
**A:** Yes, dedicated training is available for all our exclusive ranges. our exclusive mosaic range training covers mosaic types (glass, stone, metal, mixed), installation techniques (mesh-mounted vs paper-faced), cutting methods, and design applications. our laminate flooring training covers AC ratings, underlay requirements, click-system installation, and maintenance.

**Q256:** How do I advise a customer on whether to choose porcelain or ceramic tiles?
**A:** Ceramic tiles are suitable for light-traffic walls and residential floors. Porcelain tiles (vitrified, low water absorption) are recommended for high-traffic floors, outdoor areas, commercial spaces, and wet areas. Porcelain is harder, more durable, and frost-resistant but costs more. Ask about the application, traffic level, and budget to guide the recommendation.

**Q257:** What is the difference between PEI 1, 3, and 5 ratings, and why does it matter?
**A:** PEI (Porcelain Enamel Institute) ratings indicate wear resistance: PEI 1 = wall use only, PEI 2 = light-traffic residential floors (bedrooms), PEI 3 = medium-traffic residential floors (living areas, kitchens), PEI 4 = heavy-traffic residential and light commercial, PEI 5 = heavy commercial traffic. Recommend PEI 3+ for general residential floors.

**Q258:** How do I explain rectified vs non-rectified tiles to a customer?
**A:** Rectified tiles are mechanically cut after firing to achieve precise, uniform dimensions with minimal size variation. This allows for very narrow grout joints (1.5-2mm) and a seamless, premium look. Non-rectified tiles have natural size variation and require wider grout joints (3-5mm). Rectified tiles cost more but deliver a superior aesthetic.

**Q259:** What slip resistance rating should I recommend for different applications?
**A:** R9 = dry interior floors (living areas, bedrooms). R10 = general residential wet areas (kitchens, bathrooms). R11 = outdoor patios, pool surrounds, commercial kitchens. R12-R13 = commercial wet areas, ramps, industrial applications. Always recommend R10 or higher for bathrooms and R11+ for outdoor areas in South Africa.

**Q260:** How do I handle technical questions I can't answer — is there a helpline?
**A:** Yes, our technical support line is available during business hours for product and installation queries. Your account manager can also escalate technical questions to our product managers and specification team. For complex commercial or architectural queries, our Spec Lab team provides expert guidance. Never guess — always verify technical advice.

**Q261:** Can you provide competitor product comparisons so my staff can sell against other brands?
**A:** Yes, we maintain competitor comparison data for major brands in the South African market. These comparisons cover price positioning, quality ratings, design variety, warranty terms, and unique selling points. Use these to train your staff on how to position our products against competing brands your customers may mention.

**Q262:** What is the correct way to store tiles in my warehouse to prevent damage?

**Q263:** How do I calculate the correct quantity of tiles for a customer's project?
**A:** Calculate the wall or floor area (length x width), then add 10-15% for cutting waste (15-20% for diagonal patterns or complex layouts, 20%+ for mosaic features). For large-format tiles, use 15% waste. Also account for feature strips, borders, and any design elements. Our sales team can verify your calculations.

**Q264:** What advice should I give customers about tile maintenance and cleaning?

**Q265:** How do I explain the difference between polished, matte, and textured tile finishes?
**A:** Polished tiles have a glossy, reflective surface that looks luxurious but can be slippery when wet — best for walls and dry floors. Matte tiles have a flat, non-reflective surface that's slip-resistant and hides marks — ideal for floors. Textured tiles have a tactile surface providing excellent grip for outdoor and wet areas.

**Q266:** Do you provide technical data sheets and safety sheets for your products?
**A:** Yes, technical data sheets (TDS) are available for all products covering dimensions, weight, water absorption, breaking strength, chemical resistance, and slip rating. Safety data sheets (SDS) are provided for chemical products (grouts, sealers, adhesives). These are available through our online portal or from your account manager.

**Q267:** Can my staff visit your warehouse for hands-on product training?
**A:** Absolutely — warehouse visits are an excellent training opportunity. Your staff can see the full range, handle different products, compare quality levels, and understand packaging and batching. Contact your account manager to arrange a guided warehouse tour with product demonstrations. We recommend this for new staff inductions.

**Q268:** What training do you offer on selling to architects and interior designers?
**A:** Our Spec Lab team offers training on specification selling, including: understanding the specification process, presenting to architects and designers, providing technical specifications and CAD drawings, creating mood boards, and participating in design workshops. This training is available at our Menlyn showroom or via webinar.

**Q269:** How do I advise customers on choosing the right grout colour?
**A:** Generally recommend: matching grout (similar to tile colour) for a seamless, spacious look — popular for large-format tiles. Contrasting grout (darker than tile) for definition — popular with subway tiles and geometric patterns. Mid-grey is the safest all-rounder. For busy areas, darker grout hides dirt. Provide grout colour cards for visual selection.

**Q270:** What should I know about tiling over existing tiles vs removing them first?
**A:** Tiling over existing tiles is possible if the existing tiles are soundly bonded, level, and clean. Use a high-quality flexible tile-on-tile adhesive. However, removal is recommended for: uneven surfaces, loose existing tiles, height constraints (door frames, fittings), or where the additional weight is a concern. Always assess the substrate condition.

**Q271:** How do I train my staff to upsell from basic ceramic to premium porcelain?
**A:** Train staff to ask lifestyle questions: "Is this a forever home or are you renovating to sell?" "Do you have pets or children?" "How long do you want the floor to last?" Position porcelain as an investment: "For an extra R5,000, you're getting a floor that will last 25+ years instead of 10-15." Demonstrate the quality difference physically.

**Q272:** What are the common installation mistakes that lead to customer complaints, and how do I prevent them?

**Q273:** Do you provide training on new product categories like LVT and large-format slabs?
**A:** Yes, new product category training is provided whenever we expand our range. Our LVT training covers subfloor preparation, click-lock installation, heat welding, and maintenance. Large-format slab training covers handling (2-person minimum), cutting equipment, adhesive application (back-buttering), and levelling systems. Book through your account manager.

**Q274:** How do I stay updated on new products and industry trends without attending every trade show?
**A:** We keep wholesale accounts informed through: monthly product bulletins, quarterly trend reports, new product launch webinars, trade show summaries, and your account manager's regular visits. Follow our trade WhatsApp channel and subscribe to our wholesale newsletter for real-time updates on new arrivals and industry news.

**Q275:** Can I get certified training certificates for my staff to display in my showroom?
**A:** Yes, we issue certificates of attendance for completed training sessions. These can be displayed in your showroom to demonstrate staff expertise and product knowledge to customers. Certified staff add credibility and can justify premium positioning. Some commercial projects require evidence of supplier-trained staff.

---

## Category 12: Inventory Planning Support

**Q276:** How do I know which products are your fastest movers so I can prioritise my stock investment?
**A:** Your account manager provides a monthly bestseller report showing the top-selling SKUs nationally and in your region. This report includes sales velocity, stock turn rates, and margin analysis. Use this data to ensure your top 20% of SKUs (which generate 80% of sales) are always in stock.

**Q277:** What stock turn ratio should I aim for as an independent tile merchant?
**A:** Aim for 4-6 stock turns per year overall. Fast-moving ceramic wall tiles should turn 8-12 times annually. Porcelain floor tiles typically turn 4-6 times. Niche and premium products may turn 2-3 times. If your overall stock turn falls below 4, you're likely overstocked or holding too much slow-moving inventory.

**Q278:** Do you provide regional sales data so I can understand what's selling in my area?
**A:** Yes, we share anonymised regional sales data showing product trends in your province or broader region. This helps you understand local preferences (e.g., warm tones in KZN, industrial looks in Gauteng) and stock accordingly. Your account manager discusses regional trends during quarterly business reviews.

**Q279:** How much safety stock should I hold on my top 10 bestselling SKUs?
**A:** Hold 6-8 weeks of cover on your top 10 SKUs based on your average weekly sales. For imported bestsellers with longer restock lead times (10-12 weeks), increase to 10-12 weeks of cover. Monitor weekly and set automatic reorder points. Running out of bestsellers sends customers straight to competitors.

**Q280:** Can you help me identify slow-moving stock in my inventory that I should clearance?
**A:** Yes, we offer inventory analysis services where we compare your purchase history against regional sales trends to identify potential slow-movers. We also notify you when products are scheduled for discontinuation. For clearance stock, we may offer buyback arrangements or support your promotional clearance with discounted replacement stock.

**Q281:** What is the ideal inventory value for a merchant my size (turnover R10m-R20m)?
**A:** At 4-6 stock turns annually, a R10m turnover merchant should hold R1.7m-R2.5m in stock. A R20m turnover merchant should hold R3.3m-R5m. If your stock value exceeds these benchmarks, review for slow-movers and overstock. If below, you may be losing sales due to stock-outs on key lines.

**Q282:** Do you provide seasonal buying guides to help me plan my purchasing calendar?
**A:** Yes, we publish seasonal buying guides twice yearly — in January (for the autumn/winter building season) and July (for the spring/summer season). These guides recommend stock levels by category, highlight upcoming trends, identify promotional opportunities, and provide a purchasing timeline aligned with import lead times.

**Q283:** How do I balance having enough stock with not tying up too much cash in inventory?

**Q284:** Can I get advance notice of clearance or end-of-line stock to pick up bargains?
**A:** Yes, clearance notifications are sent to all wholesale accounts via email and WhatsApp before clearance stock is publicly advertised. This gives you first pick of discounted stock. Clearance stock is typically 20-40% below wholesale price and can be excellent margin opportunities if the product suits your market.

**Q285:** What is the financial cost of holding too much inventory, and how do I calculate it?
**A:** Inventory carrying costs typically amount to 15-25% of inventory value annually, comprising: financing cost (prime rate on borrowed capital or opportunity cost of own capital), warehouse space rental, insurance, handling labour, shrinkage/damage, and obsolescence risk. For every R1 million in excess stock, you're spending R150,000-R250,000 per year to hold it.

**Q286:** How do I plan for the festive season when building activity slows in December-January?
**A:** Reduce your forward orders placed in October-November to essential restocking only. Focus on quick-turn promotional stock for DIY customers (home improvement is popular over holidays). Ensure you have stock of popular formats for emergency repairs. Plan for a restocking surge in mid-January when building resumes.

**Q287:** Do you offer inventory analysis or range review services for your wholesale customers?
**A:** Yes, our wholesale team provides annual range reviews for larger accounts. We analyse your purchase history, stock turn by SKU, margin contribution, and compare against regional benchmarks. We then recommend additions, deletions, and stock level adjustments to optimise your inventory investment and profitability.

**Q288:** How do I know when to drop a product from my range vs keeping it for occasional sales?
**A:** Review products quarterly: if an SKU hasn't sold in 6 months and shows no pipeline demand, consider dropping it or reducing to a display sample only. If it sells 1-2 times per quarter at good margin, keep it as a special-order item. Your account manager can provide sales velocity benchmarks by category.

**Q289:** What are the signs that I'm overstocked, and what should I do about it?
**A:** Signs of overstocking: stock turn below 4x annually, warehouse congestion, aged stock over 12 months old, frequent discounting to move stock, and cash flow pressure. Action: run a clearance sale on slow-movers, return excess stock to us (where policy allows), negotiate sale-or-return on new ranges, and tighten your purchasing discipline.

**Q290:** Can I get a stock availability report showing what you hold vs what I hold to identify gaps?
**A:** Yes, your account manager can generate a comparative stock report showing your current holdings against our core range recommendations. This identifies gaps where you're missing key SKUs that competitors likely stock. We update this quarterly as part of your range review process.

**Q291:** How do I manage the cash flow impact of placing large pre-season orders?
**A:** Spread your pre-season ordering across the buying window (6-8 weeks) rather than placing one large order. Use our forward order system to commit to stock with a deposit rather than full upfront payment. Time orders to align with your cash flow cycles. For very large seasonal commitments, discuss staged payment terms with your account manager.

**Q292:** Do you provide forecasting tools or templates to help me plan my inventory?
**A:** We provide Excel-based inventory planning templates that factor in your historical sales, lead times, desired stock cover, and seasonal adjustments. Your account manager can help you populate the template and set reorder points for each SKU category. More sophisticated merchants can integrate our data feed with their ERP system.

**Q293:** What products should I always keep in stock vs those I can order as needed?

**Q294:** How do I plan my range for a new branch opening?
**A:** For a new branch, we recommend a curated opening stock package based on the branch location's demographics and our regional sales data. The package includes: core range essentials (60% of SKUs), local trend-aligned products (25%), and a premium selection (15%). Your account manager will develop a customised opening stock plan with estimated investment.

**Q295:** Can I return excess stock from a branch that is closing or downsizing?
**A:** For branch closures or significant downsizing, contact your regional wholesale manager. We may accept returns of current-range, resaleable stock at wholesale value less a 10-15% handling fee. Discontinued or aged stock is handled case by case. Early communication allows us to plan alternative distribution for the returned stock.

**Q296:** How do I optimise my stock mix for maximum profit rather than maximum turnover?
**A:** Analyse margin contribution per SKU, not just sales volume. High-turnover, low-margin products (basic ceramics) generate cash flow. Lower-turnover, high-margin products (imported porcelain, mosaics) generate profit. Aim for a mix: 60% of sales from volume products at 25-30% margin, 40% from premium products at 40-50% margin.

**Q297:** Do you help with planogram or category management for my showroom?
**A:** Yes, we provide category management guidance including planograms, category adjacency recommendations, and stock-to-display ratios. Our wholesale team studies successful independent merchants and shares best practices for category layout, promotional positioning, and impulse-buy product placement.

**Q298:** How do I plan for sudden spikes in demand (e.g., a major development starts in my town)?
**A:** When you become aware of major developments in your area, notify your account manager immediately. We can pre-position stock for anticipated demand and set up project-specific supply arrangements. For very large projects (100+ units), we can establish a dedicated supply pipeline with regular scheduled deliveries.

**Q299:** What is the lead time from your warehouse to my store, and how does this affect my reorder timing?
**A:** Lead time depends on your location: same-day/next-day for collections from nearest branch, next-day delivery within 60km, 2-3 days for regional delivery, and 3-5 days for remote areas. Factor this into your reorder point calculation: reorder when stock = (weekly sales x lead time in weeks) + safety stock.

**Q300:** Can I access your stock levels in real-time to check availability before promising my customer?
**A:** Yes, active wholesale accounts have access to our online stock availability portal showing real-time stock levels across all branches. You can check stock before placing an order or promising a delivery date to your customer. The portal also shows incoming pipeline stock with expected arrival dates.

---

## Category 13: Territory & Exclusivity

**Q301:** Do you offer territorial exclusivity for your wholesale customers?
**A:** We don't offer absolute territorial exclusivity as a standard policy. However, we respect the customer relationships you've built and won't actively target your established contractor base. For accounts with significant volume commitments, we discuss territorial protection arrangements on a case-by-case basis, particularly for exclusive ranges.

**Q302:** If I open a Tiletoria wholesale account in George, will you stop supplying my competitor across town?
**A:** We generally won't cease supplying an existing account because a new merchant opens nearby. However, we manage account density carefully and may limit the number of wholesale accounts in very small towns. Your competitive advantage should come from service, range curation, and relationships rather than supplier exclusivity.

**Q303:** What protection do I have if a major national chain opens in my town?
**A:** While we can't prevent chains from opening, we ensure our exclusive ranges (our exclusive mosaic range, our laminate flooring, our laminate flooring) are not available through competing national tile retailers. This gives you product differentiation that chains can't match. We also support your competitive response with marketing materials and range advice.

**Q304:** Can I get exclusive distribution rights for our exclusive mosaic range or our laminate flooring in my town?
**A:** Exclusive distribution of our exclusive mosaic range and our laminate flooring can be discussed for accounts that demonstrate commitment through minimum annual purchase targets, dedicated display space, and trained staff. Exclusivity is typically granted within a defined radius (e.g., 15-20km) and reviewed annually. Contact your regional manager to discuss.

**Q305:** How do you decide whether to open a new wholesale account in an area where you already have merchants?
**A:** We assess market size and growth potential, existing merchant performance and satisfaction, and the applicant's business model and differentiation. In growing markets, multiple accounts can thrive. In static markets, we may decline new applications to protect existing partners. New applicants are always discussed with existing local accounts where possible.

**Q306:** Can I prevent you from supplying a merchant who undercuts everyone on price in my area?
**A:** We don't engage in predatory pricing and expect all our wholesale customers to maintain reasonable retail margins. If a merchant is consistently selling at unsustainable prices that damage the market, we may review their account. Report such concerns to your account manager with evidence (advertised prices, quotes).

**Q307:** Do you have a policy on minimum advertised pricing (MAP) for your products?
**A:** We don't enforce strict MAP policies, but we encourage all wholesale customers to maintain healthy retail margins (25-45%) that sustain their businesses and the brand's market positioning. Merchants consistently advertising below reasonable margins may be reminded of the importance of sustainable pricing.

**Q308:** If I invest heavily in showroom displays for an exclusive range, what guarantee do I have that you won't give it to my competitor?
**A:** When you invest in displays for exclusive ranges, we formalise the arrangement with a display support agreement that includes territory preferences for those ranges. While not legally binding exclusivity, we honour the spirit of these agreements and actively support your investment by prioritising your supply and limiting competing distribution of those specific ranges in your immediate area.

**Q309:** How do you handle situations where two of your wholesale customers are competing for the same large project?
**A:** In project situations, we supply both customers impartially. We don't play favourites or share one merchant's pricing with another. Each merchant's success depends on their relationship with the client, their service offering, and their pricing strategy. We remain neutral and supply to whichever customer places the order.

**Q310:** Can I expand my territory by opening additional branches and still get wholesale terms?
**A:** Yes, multi-branch merchants are an important part of our wholesale network. Each new branch can be added to your group account or operate as a separate account. Volume discounts and rebate calculations can be consolidated across branches. Notify your account manager of expansion plans so we can plan supply and support accordingly.

**Q311:** What happens if another Tiletoria wholesale customer opens a branch in my town?
**A:** If a multi-branch merchant from another town opens in your area, we'll typically notify you in advance as a courtesy. Your existing relationship, local knowledge, and established customer base are your defences. We encourage you to differentiate through service and exclusive ranges. Formal territorial protection can be discussed if you're significantly impacted.

**Q312:** Do you share my sales data or customer information with other wholesale customers?
**A:** Absolutely not. Your sales data, customer information, and account details are strictly confidential. We do not share any customer-specific information with other merchants, even those in the same region. Aggregate, anonymised regional trend data may be shared, but never attributable to individual accounts.

**Q313:** Can I get a non-compete or non-solicitation agreement from Tiletoria regarding my customers?
**A:** We don't sign formal non-compete agreements, but our Wholesale Trading Terms include confidentiality and fair dealing clauses. We commit to not actively soliciting your established customer relationships. If you have specific concerns, discuss them with your regional manager — we value long-term partnerships and act accordingly.

**Q314:** How does your territory management work for exclusive imported lines?
**A:** Some of our exclusive imported tile lines have limited distribution by design — we may grant exclusive rights to one merchant per town or region for these lines. This is agreed in writing with minimum volume commitments and display requirements. Your account manager can advise which current lines have limited distribution.

**Q315:** What is your approach to online-only retailers who might sell into my territory at lower prices?
**A:** We evaluate online-only accounts carefully and require them to maintain reasonable retail pricing that doesn't undermine bricks-and-mortar partners. Online accounts must not undercut the market systematically. If an online retailer is damaging your business with predatory pricing, report it to your account manager.

**Q316:** If I move my business to a new location within the same town, does my wholesale account transfer?
**A:** Yes, your wholesale account transfers with you. Notify your account manager of the new address and trading details at least two weeks before the move. Update your delivery address, and we'll ensure uninterrupted supply during your transition. A site visit to the new premises may be arranged.

**Q317:** Can I sub-distribute Tiletoria products to other retailers in my area?
**A:** Sub-distribution is not permitted without a formal sub-distributor agreement. Our wholesale terms are for your direct retail or contractor sales only. If you're interested in becoming a regional sub-distributor, discuss this with our wholesale director — it requires different terms, volume commitments, and territorial agreements.

**Q318:** How do you manage wholesale accounts in very small towns where the market is limited?
**A:** In small towns, we typically work with one primary merchant or builders merchant who serves the local market. We may limit additional wholesale accounts if the market can't sustain multiple stockists. The existing merchant often receives preferential support and exclusivity discussions are more readily entertained.

**Q319:** If I achieve exclusivity on a range, what are my obligations to maintain it?
**A:** Exclusive range rights typically require: minimum annual purchase volumes (e.g., R500,000-R1 million per year), dedicated showroom display space, trained staff, active promotion of the range, and adherence to recommended retail pricing. Exclusivity is reviewed annually and can be withdrawn if obligations aren't met.

**Q320:** Do you communicate with me before opening an account for someone new in my area?
**A:** Where practical, we notify existing accounts in the same area before opening a new nearby account, particularly in smaller towns. While we don't require your permission, we value transparency and long-term relationships. This courtesy allows you to prepare and potentially strengthen your competitive position.

**Q321:** What recourse do I have if I feel another Tiletoria customer is unfairly competing with me?
**A:** If you believe another Tiletoria customer is engaging in unfair practices (predatory pricing, poaching your staff, misrepresenting products), document the issue and raise it with your regional wholesale manager. We investigate all complaints and may mediate between parties or take account action if terms are being breached.

**Q322:** How does territory work for project-specific supply where the project is in my area but awarded to an out-of-town contractor?
**A:** If a contractor from another town wins a project in your area, they may source from their regular supplier. However, we encourage them to support the local merchant where possible. If the contractor approaches us directly, we may refer them to you as the local Tiletoria stockist, supporting your local market position.

**Q323:** Can I get first right of refusal on new ranges in my territory?
**A:** Long-standing accounts with good performance records often receive first right of refusal on new exclusive ranges in their area. This means you're offered the range before it's offered to other local merchants. Declining within a reasonable timeframe (typically 14 days) allows us to offer it elsewhere.

**Q324:** How do you handle wholesale accounts in neighbouring towns that are only 20-30km apart?
**A:** In closely spaced towns (e.g., Empangeni and Richards Bay, George and Mossel Bay), we may supply multiple accounts recognising that each serves its local community. Customers generally shop locally for convenience. We don't view nearby towns as direct competitors if each has its own catchment area.

**Q325:** If I want to develop a new market area, will Tiletoria support me exclusively?
**A:** If you're pioneering a new market where no Tiletoria stockist exists, we'll support your development actively and may offer temporary exclusivity or preferred supplier status for 12-24 months.
