This gives you time to establish before other merchants potentially enter. Discuss market development plans with your regional manager.

---

## Category 14: Digital Tools & Ordering

**Q326:** Do you have an online ordering portal for wholesale customers?
**A:** Yes, our wholesale portal allows you to browse products, check real-time stock availability, view pricing, place orders, track deliveries, and download invoices. The portal is accessible 24/7 and is optimised for desktop and mobile use. Login credentials are provided upon account activation.

**Q327:** How do I get login credentials for the online wholesale portal?
**A:** Contact your account manager or email it@tiletoria.co.za with your account number and preferred email address for login. Credentials are issued within 24 hours. The primary account holder can request additional user accounts for staff members, each with customised permissions (order placement, view-only, etc.).

**Q328:** Can I check stock availability in real-time before placing an order?
**A:** Yes, the wholesale portal displays real-time stock levels across all three branches. You can see exactly how many pallets or boxes are available at each location. The portal also shows incoming pipeline stock with expected arrival dates, helping you decide whether to order available stock or wait for incoming inventory.

**Q329:** Is there a mobile app for ordering, or do I need to use the website?
**A:** Currently, ordering is via our responsive web portal which works on mobile browsers. A dedicated mobile app is in development. In the meantime, many wholesale customers use WhatsApp for quick stock checks and order confirmations, with the portal used for detailed browsing and order placement.

**Q330:** Can I place orders via WhatsApp?
**A:** Yes, many wholesale customers place orders via WhatsApp for speed and convenience. Save your account manager's WhatsApp number and send your order as a message or voice note. For complex orders, a follow-up email or portal entry confirms the details. WhatsApp is ideal for urgent restocking of known SKUs.

**Q331:** What are your branch WhatsApp numbers for quick orders and queries?
**A:** WhatsApp numbers for each branch are provided in your welcome pack and available from your account manager. Each branch has a dedicated trade WhatsApp line monitored during business hours by the wholesale team. After-hours messages are responded to the next business day.

**Q332:** Can I email orders instead of using the online portal?
**A:** Yes, emailed orders to your account manager are accepted and commonly used, particularly for large or complex orders with multiple lines. Email orders should include product codes, quantities, and your preferred delivery date. You'll receive an order confirmation within 4 hours during business hours.

**Q333:** How do I get my price list in an electronic format that I can import into my system?
**A:** Price lists are available in Excel/CSV format for import into most accounting and POS systems. Contact your account manager to request the electronic price file. Updates are issued quarterly and can be set up for automatic email delivery. The file includes product codes, descriptions, wholesale prices, and VAT breakdowns.

**Q334:** Is there an API that integrates your stock and pricing with my ERP or POS system?
**A:** API access is available for larger wholesale accounts with integrated ERP systems. The API provides real-time stock feeds, pricing updates, and order placement functionality. Integration support is provided by our IT team. Setup typically takes 2-3 weeks including testing. Contact it@tiletoria.co.za for API documentation.

**Q335:** Can I set up automated alerts for when products I'm interested in come back into stock?
**A:** Yes, the wholesale portal allows you to set stock alerts on specific SKUs. When the product comes back into stock or reaches a threshold quantity, you receive an automatic email or WhatsApp notification. This is particularly useful for fast-moving products that are frequently out of stock.

**Q336:** How do I access my order history and invoices online?
**A:** Your full order history, invoices, credit notes, and statements are available through the wholesale portal. You can search by date range, product, or invoice number. Documents can be downloaded as PDFs for your records. Statements can also be emailed automatically to your accounts contact monthly.

**Q337:** Can multiple users from my business access the portal with different permissions?
**A:** Yes, you can set up multiple user profiles under your master account. Typical permission levels include: Administrator (full access), Buyer (can place orders and view pricing), Viewer (can browse and check stock only), and Accounts (can view invoices and statements only). User management is handled through the portal settings.

**Q338:** What happens if the portal shows stock available but when I order it's actually out of stock?
**A:** Stock levels update in real-time, but very occasionally a timing overlap occurs where another order is processed simultaneously. If this happens, your account manager will immediately contact you with alternatives: backorder at the same price, substitute with a comparable product, or cancel the line item.

**Q339:** Can I save favourite product lists or shopping carts for quick reordering?
**A:** Yes, the portal allows you to create saved product lists (e.g., "Monthly Restock," "Project X," "Core Range") for one-click reordering. You can also duplicate previous orders and adjust quantities. These features save significant time for regular reorders of the same product mix.

**Q340:** How do I update my account email address or contact details on the portal?
**A:** Contact details can be updated through the "My Account" section of the portal, or by emailing your account manager. Banking detail changes require verification for security and must be submitted with supporting documentation (stamped bank letter or bank statement showing the new account).

**Q341:** Is there a minimum order value for online orders vs phone/email orders?
**A:** No, minimum order values are the same regardless of ordering channel. Standard wholesale MOQs apply (typically one pallet per tile design). For delivery, minimum order values vary by distance from branch. Online orders don't have additional restrictions — all ordering channels are treated equally.

**Q342:** Can I track my delivery through the portal once the order has been dispatched?
**A:** For deliveries within our own fleet, dispatch notifications with estimated delivery windows are sent via email and SMS. For contractor deliveries to outlying areas, waybill tracking references are provided. Full real-time GPS tracking is available for major metro deliveries and is being rolled out to regional contractors.

**Q343:** How secure is the online portal — what measures protect my pricing and order data?
**A:** The portal uses SSL encryption, two-factor authentication (optional but recommended), and role-based access control. Your pricing data is confidential and only visible to authorised users on your account. We conduct regular security audits and penetration testing. Never share your login credentials with unauthorised personnel.

**Q344:** Can I download product images and specifications from the portal for my own use?
**A:** Yes, product images (lifestyle and product shots), technical data sheets, installation guides, and specification documents are downloadable from the portal for use in your marketing and customer presentations. Usage is subject to our brand guidelines — these materials are for promoting Tiletoria products, not general resale.

**Q345:** What do I do if I forget my portal password or get locked out?
**A:** Use the "Forgot Password" link on the login page to reset your password via email. If you're locked out after multiple failed attempts, contact it@tiletoria.co.za or your account manager to unlock your account. For security, accounts are automatically locked after 5 failed login attempts.

**Q346:** Is there a quick-order feature where I can just paste a list of product codes and quantities?
**A:** Yes, the quick-order feature allows you to paste a list of product codes and quantities (from Excel or a text list) directly into the order form. The system matches codes and populates your cart instantly. This is the fastest way to place large orders with many line items.

**Q347:** Can I see my volume rebate progress on the portal?
**A:** Yes, the portal dashboard displays your year-to-date purchases, current quarter progress, and rebate tier achievement. You can see exactly how close you are to the next rebate threshold. This helps you time additional orders to maximise rebate earnings.

**Q348:** How do I provide feedback or report bugs on the online portal?
**A:** Feedback and bug reports are welcomed via it@tiletoria.co.za or through the "Feedback" button on the portal. Our IT team reviews all submissions and implements improvements based on wholesale customer feedback. Major feature requests are discussed at our quarterly wholesale customer forum.

**Q349:** Can I place orders outside business hours for processing the next day?
**A:** Yes, the online portal accepts orders 24/7. Orders placed after hours are processed first thing the next business day. Orders placed over weekends are processed on Monday morning. Urgent after-hours orders can be placed via WhatsApp for next-morning attention.

**Q350:** Do you offer EDI (Electronic Data Interchange) for large accounts with automated procurement systems?
**A:** Yes, EDI integration is available for large wholesale accounts with automated procurement systems. We support common EDI formats and can customise integration for specific ERP platforms (SAP, Sage, Oracle, etc.). EDI setup requires initial technical consultation and testing — contact it@tiletoria.co.za to discuss.

---

## Category 15: porcelain tile alternatives & Specialty Products


**A:** Entry-levelstart from R120-R180/m² at wholesale. Mid-rangeand porcelain tile surfaces are R200-R400/m². Premium(-look porcelain-look porcelain)from R400-R800/m² at wholesale. Large slabs and book-matched pieces can exceed R1,000/m². Prices vary with currency and quarry availability.

**A:** Yes, we supply stone slabs for countertop and vanity applications. Slabs are typically 20mm or 30mm thick, available in various sizes up to 3000x1500mm depending on the stone type. MOQs apply, and cutting/fabrication is your customer's responsibility (or yours if you offer the service). Your account manager can advise on slab availability.

**A:** Stone in stock at our warehouses is available for immediate delivery. For special-order stone from overseas quarries (Italy, Turkey, India, Brazil), lead times are typically 10-14 weeks including sea freight and customs clearance. Some popular stones we hold as running lines; others are indent-only.

**Q359:** How do I advise customers on the suitability of different stones for different applications?
**A:**: beautiful but porous, requires sealing, suitable for bathrooms and feature walls, not ideal for high-traffic kitchens.: very hard and durable, excellent for kitchen countertops and floors.: classic look, good for floors and bathrooms, requires sealing.: durable, slip-resistant, great for outdoor areas.: warm tones, suitable for patios and feature walls.

**Q360:** Do you stock engineered quartz or sintered stone (Dekton, Neolith) for wholesale?

**A:** Tumbled stone has a soft, aged appearance with rounded edges and a textured surface — ideal for rustic, traditional, or farmhouse-style interiors. Honed stone has a smooth, matte surface with sharp edges — more contemporary and refined. Tumbled stone has better natural slip resistance; honed stone is easier to clean.

**A:** Natural variation is a characteristic, not a defect — educate your customers upfront. Show multiple sample pieces before purchase and have them sign an acknowledgment of natural variation. Advise customers to mix tiles from different boxes during installation for balanced distribution of veining and colour. Returns due to expected natural variation are not accepted.


**A:** Yes, pebble and river stone tiles are available in various colours and sizes, mounted on mesh backing for easy installation. These are popular for shower floors, spa bathrooms, and feature walls. MOQs are typically 10-20m². Sample boards are available to show the texture and colour variations.

**A:** Custom cutting can be arranged through our stone processing partners. Common custom cuts include: French pattern (Versailles) layouts, custom slab sizes, bullnose edges, and waterjet-cut designs. Custom cutting requires minimum quantities (typically 50-100m²) and adds 2-3 weeks to lead time. A cutting fee applies.

**A:** Consistent bestsellers include:-look porcelain(white with grey veining) in 600x600 and 600x1200 honed finishClassic in 400x400 and 600x600, Black/Nero Marquinafor feature walls, and outdoor tile surfaces for outdoor applications. Grey-look porcelain has partially replaced some naturaldemand due to lower maintenance.


**A:** For commercial stone projects, contact your account manager early in the specification process. We can: provide test samples and technical data, arrange quarry visits for large projects, ensure batch consistency across large orders, and coordinate phased deliveries to site. Commercial projects may qualify for project-specific pricing.


**A:** Provide: a care and maintenance guide (printed or emailed), recommended sealer product, contact details for follow-up questions, and an annual maintenance reminder. Stone customers are typically higher-value and appreciate ongoing support. This builds loyalty and generates referrals to similarly discerning customers.


---

## Category 16: Laminate & LVT Wholesale

**Q376:** What laminate flooring brands do you distribute, and are they exclusive to Tiletoria?
**A:** We exclusively distribute our laminate flooring and our laminate flooring laminate and LVT ranges in South Africa. These brands are not available through competing distributors, giving you significant protection from direct price competition. Both ranges offer AC3, AC4, and AC5 wear ratings for residential and commercial applications.

**Q377:** What is the difference between our laminate flooring and our laminate flooring?
**A:** our laminate flooring is our premium laminate range offering AC3-AC5 grades, water-resistant options, and a variety of wood-look decors. our laminate flooring is our LVT (Luxury Vinyl Tile) and SPC (Stone Polymer Composite) range, offering waterproof flooring solutions suitable for bathrooms, kitchens, and commercial spaces where laminate is not ideal.

**Q378:** What does AC3, AC4, and AC5 mean, and which should I stock?
**A:** AC (Abrasion Class) ratings indicate wear resistance: AC3 = heavy residential/light commercial (bedrooms, living rooms), AC4 = general commercial (offices, retail, restaurants), AC5 = heavy commercial (public buildings, schools, high-traffic retail). Stock AC3 for residential-focused stores, AC4 for mixed residential/commercial, and AC5 only for commercial project-focused merchants.

**Q379:** What are the MOQs for laminate flooring orders?
**A:** Laminate flooring MOQs are typically one full carton (containing 2-3m² depending on plank width). For wholesale pricing, minimums are usually 20-30 cartons per order (approximately 50-90m²). Mixed-container orders (multiple decors) are available, allowing you to offer variety without over-committing to single decors.

**Q380:** Do you provide display stands for laminate and LVT samples?
**A:** Yes, professional display stands are provided on loan to wholesale customers who commit to stocking our laminate flooring and/or our laminate flooring. The stands hold 20-40 sample boards and include branded headers and technical information. Our flooring team installs and maintains the displays. Contact your account manager to arrange a display stand.

**Q381:** What is the price range for laminate flooring at wholesale?
**A:** Entry-level AC3 laminate starts from approximately R120-R160/m² at wholesale. Mid-range AC4 laminate is R160-R220/m². Premium AC5 and water-resistant options range from R220-R320/m² at wholesale. LVT/SPC ranges from R180-R350/m² at wholesale depending on wear layer thickness and construction.

**Q382:** What markup can I apply to laminate and LVT at retail?
**A:** Laminate and LVT typically support 30-40% retail markup, with premium waterproof LVT supporting up to 45% in markets where customers understand the technology. The flooring category often generates higher average transaction values than tiles alone — a typical laminate job is 40-80m² vs 15-25m² for a bathroom tile job.

**Q383:** Do you provide installation training for laminate and LVT?
**A:** Yes, installation training is provided for both our laminate flooring (click-lock floating floor system) and our laminate flooring LVT (click-lock and glue-down options). Training covers subfloor preparation, moisture barrier installation, underlay selection, laying techniques, expansion gap requirements, and finishing details. Training can be at your premises or our branch.

**Q384:** What underlay options do you stock for laminate flooring?
**A:** We stock various underlay options: standard foam underlay (2mm, 3mm), acoustic underlay for multi-level buildings, thermal underlay for colder climates, and DPM (damp-proof membrane) underlay for concrete subfloors. Underlay is sold in rolls and should be recommended as part of every laminate installation.

**Q385:** How do I display laminate and LVT alongside tiles in my showroom?
**A:** We recommend creating dedicated flooring zones in your showroom: a laminate display stand near the entrance (high-impulse item), LVT samples displayed near bathroom tile sections (natural cross-sell), and large floor panels showing installed looks. Use lifestyle imagery showing laminate/LVT in lounge and bedroom settings to inspire customers.

**Q386:** Can I get large sample boards or mini-rooms to show the installed look?
**A:** Yes, large display panels (600x900mm) showing the installed floor pattern are available for key decors. These are more impactful than small sample boards. For significant showroom investments, we can arrange mini-room displays or access to installed reference sites where customers can see the flooring in real homes.

**Q387:** What is the warranty on our laminate flooring and our laminate flooring products?
**A:** our laminate flooring AC3 carries a 15-year residential warranty; AC4 carries 20 years; AC5 carries 25 years. our laminate flooring LVT carries 10-20 year warranties depending on wear layer thickness (0.3mm = 10 years, 0.5mm = 15 years, 0.55mm = 20 years). Commercial warranties are shorter — typically 5-10 years. Warranty documents are provided with every installation.

**Q388:** How do I handle moisture concerns for laminate flooring — can it go in bathrooms and kitchens?
**A:** Standard laminate is not recommended for bathrooms or areas with high moisture risk. However, our laminate flooring water-resistant range and our laminate flooring LVT (which is fully waterproof) are suitable for kitchens, bathrooms, and laundry rooms. Position LVT as the bathroom/kitchen waterproof solution and laminate as the living area/bedroom solution.

**Q389:** What accessories do I need to stock alongside laminate and LVT?
**A:** Essential accessories include: underlay (various types), Scotia/beading (matching colour options), expansion gap covers, transition strips (door bars), stair nosing, pipe collars, DPM sheeting, and cleaning kits. These ancillaries add 10-15% to the average job value and are profitable add-on sales.

**Q390:** Do you provide technical support if my installer has a problem during installation?
**A:** Yes, our flooring technical helpline is available during business hours for installation queries. Common issues we can resolve: subfloor moisture readings, creaking floors, expansion gap problems, and transition details. For complex issues, we can arrange a site visit from our flooring technician (major metros only).

**Q391:** How does laminate flooring compare to engineered wood flooring, and which should I recommend?
**A:** Laminate is more affordable (R120-R320/m² retail), more scratch-resistant, easier to install, and requires less maintenance. Engineered wood is more prestigious, can be sanded and refinished, feels warmer underfoot, and has natural variation. Recommend laminate for budget-conscious and high-traffic homes; engineered wood for premium customers who value authenticity.

**Q392:** Can I order laminate flooring with my tile order, or do they ship separately?
**A:** Laminate and tile orders can be consolidated on the same delivery where stock is available from the same branch. Mixed pallet deliveries are standard practice. However, due to weight distribution, very large mixed orders may require separate vehicles. Your account manager will optimise the delivery arrangement.

**Q393:** What is the lead time for laminate and LVT orders?
**A:** Standard laminate and LVT stock items are available for immediate delivery from our warehouses. For large volume orders or specific decors not held as running lines, lead time is typically 4-8 weeks. We maintain healthy safety stock on popular decors to ensure rapid fulfilment for wholesale customers.

**Q394:** Do you offer samples that my customers can take home to check colour matching?
**A:** Yes, loose sample boards (A4 and A5 size) are available for customers to take home. These are provided free to active wholesale accounts for customer lending. Sample boards should be returned within a few days, but most merchants treat them as a marketing cost. Larger sample panels are available at a nominal charge.

**Q395:** How do I stay updated on new decors and flooring trends?
**A:** New laminate and LVT decors are launched twice yearly, aligned with European design trends. We provide launch previews, sample packs, and training for all new decors. Subscribe to our flooring product bulletin and attend our seasonal product launches to stay ahead of trends. Your account manager will notify you of all new releases.

**Q396:** Can I get point-of-sale materials specifically for the flooring ranges?
**A:** Yes, dedicated POS materials for laminate and LTV include: branded posters, technical specification cards, installation warranty booklets, room scene imagery, price tags, and promotional banners. Flooring-specific POS is provided free with your display stand order. Additional materials can be ordered through your account manager.

**Q397:** What is the typical stock investment needed to launch laminate and LVT in my store?
**A:** A basic laminate/LVT launch requires approximately R80,000-R150,000 in stock: 8-12 popular decors in laminate (mixed AC3/AC4), 4-6 LVT decors, underlay (3 types), and accessories. This investment typically generates R250,000-R400,000 in annual flooring sales in the first year for a well-positioned store.

**Q398:** Do you offer financing or payment terms for a flooring range launch?
**A:** For established wholesale accounts adding flooring to their range, we offer extended payment terms on the initial flooring stock order (typically 60 days on the first order). Display stands and POS materials are provided on loan at no cost. This reduces the cash flow impact of launching a new product category.

**Q399:** How do I train my staff to sell flooring as an add-on to tile sales?
**A:** Train staff to ask open questions: "Are you also looking at flooring for other rooms?" "What flooring do you currently have in the lounge?" Position flooring as a natural extension of a renovation project. Cross-display flooring near tile sections. Offer package discounts for customers buying both tiles and flooring.

**Q400:** What are the common customer objections to laminate flooring, and how do I overcome them?
**A:** Common objections: "It looks fake" — show high-quality decors with embossed texture and bevelled edges. "It doesn't last" — explain AC ratings and warranty periods. "It's noisy" — recommend quality acoustic underlay. "It can't get wet" — introduce water-resistant laminate or LVT. "It's expensive" — compare to tiling the same area.

---

## Category 17: Sanware Wholesale

**Q401:** What sanware brands do you stock for wholesale distribution?
**A:** Our sanware range includes: our premium tapware range (premium taps and showers), our tapware range (contemporary bathroom fittings), our tapware range (mid-range sanitaryware), and our tapware range (value-oriented bathroom products). We also stock bathroom accessories, mirrors, cabinets, and vanity units. This range allows you to offer complete bathroom solutions alongside tiles.

**Q402:** What is the wholesale margin potential on sanware products?
**A:** Sanware typically offers attractive wholesale-to-retail margins of 30-45%, with premium brands like our premium tapware range supporting higher margins due to brand prestige. Vanity units and bathroom furniture can achieve 40-50% margins. Bundling sanware with tiles (complete bathroom package) increases average transaction value significantly.

**Q403:** Do I need to be a qualified plumber or have a plumbing licence to sell sanware?
**A:** No, you don't need a plumbing licence to sell sanware products. However, installation must be done by a licensed plumber. We recommend building relationships with licensed plumbers in your area who can install the products you sell — this creates a referral network that drives sales both ways.

**Q404:** What sanware products are your best sellers that every merchant should stock?
**A:** Essential sanware stock includes: single-lever basin mixers (chrome and matte black), overhead showers with rail, toilet suites (close-coupled and back-to-wall), countertop basins, freestanding baths (acrylic), shower doors (frameless and framed), and bathroom accessories (towel rails, toilet roll holders, soap dispensers).

**Q405:** Do you provide display units for sanware products in my showroom?
**A:** Yes, display support for sanware includes: branded display boards for tap ranges, countertop basin display stands, shower head demonstration units, and vanity unit floor displays. For larger showrooms, we can arrange bathroom vignette displays showing coordinated tile and sanware combinations. Contact your account manager for display support.

**Q406:** How does sanware coordinate with tile sales — should I display them together?
**A:** Absolutely — displaying tiles and sanware together significantly increases basket size. Create bathroom vignettes showing coordinated tile, tapware, basin, and shower combinations. Display tapware samples alongside your tile displays so customers can visualise the complete bathroom. Our showroom design team can advise on optimal layouts.

**Q407:** What is the warranty on our premium tapware range and other sanware brands you stock?
**A:** our premium tapware range carries a 5-10 year warranty depending on the product (taps typically 5 years, ceramics 10 years). our tapware range offers 5-year warranties. our tapware range and our tapware range offer 3-5 year warranties. Warranties cover manufacturing defects and cartridge failures under normal use. Warranty documentation is provided with each product and should be passed to your customer.

**Q408:** Do you stock replacement parts and cartridges for the tapware you sell?
**A:** Yes, we stock common replacement parts including cartridges, aerators, flexible hoses, seal kits, and shower hose replacements. Carrying spare parts enables you to offer after-sales service that competitors can't match. Your account manager can advise on which parts to hold as running stock based on your sales volume.

**Q409:** What is the lead time for sanware orders?
**A:** Most sanware is held as running stock at our warehouses and available for immediate delivery. Premium our premium tapware range lines and some imported specials may have 2-4 week lead times. Large vanity units and baths that require special handling may need advance ordering. Your account manager can confirm availability on specific items.

**Q410:** Can I order sanware mixed with my tile and flooring orders for single delivery?
**A:** Yes, sanware can be consolidated with tile and flooring orders for single delivery. Sanware is packed separately with protective packaging to prevent damage. For very large mixed orders, sanware may travel on separate pallets for protection. We optimise delivery arrangements to minimise your freight costs.

**Q411:** Do you stock bathroom furniture (vanities, cabinets, mirror cabinets) for wholesale?
**A:** Yes, our bathroom furniture range includes vanity units (wall-hung and floor-standing), mirror cabinets, storage units, and bathroom shelves. These come in various finishes (white gloss, wood-look, matte colours) and sizes (600mm to 1500mm). Bathroom furniture is a high-margin category with strong consumer demand.

**Q412:** How do I handle after-sales service on sanware — do I refer to you or handle it myself?
**A:** For warranty claims, you handle the initial customer contact and verification, then process the claim through your account manager. We honour warranties directly with our wholesale customers, who then resolve with their end customer. This allows you to maintain the customer relationship while we handle product replacement.

**Q413:** What are the trending finishes in tapware — is matte black still popular?
**A:** Matte black remains very popular but is now complemented by brushed brass/gold, gunmetal, brushed nickel, and mixed finishes (black and gold combinations). Chrome remains the volume seller due to its competitive pricing and timeless appeal. Stock chrome as your volume line and 2-3 alternative finishes for design-conscious customers.

**Q414:** Do you stock freestanding baths, and what price range are they at wholesale?
**A:** Yes, we stock freestanding acrylic baths in various shapes (oval, rectangular, slipper) and sizes (1500mm-1800mm). Wholesale prices range from R4,500-R12,000 for standard acrylic freestanding baths, and R15,000-R35,000 for premium stone resin or imported options. Freestanding baths are strong sellers in affluent markets.

**Q415:** What toilet suite options do you have — close-coupled, back-to-wall, wall-hung?
**A:** We stock all three types: close-coupled (most popular, easiest to install), back-to-wall (contemporary look, concealed cistern), and wall-hung (premium, space-saving, requires frame system). Close-coupled suites suit the mass market; back-to-wall and wall-hung suit design-focused renovations. We also stock bidets for the luxury market.

**Q416:** Do you provide installation guides for sanware products?
**A:** Yes, installation guides are provided for all sanware products. These cover plumbing connections, sealing requirements, pressure specifications, and maintenance instructions. Guides should be made available to your installing contractors. Technical specification sheets with dimensions and connection details are also available.

**Q417:** How do I compete with dedicated plumbing merchants on sanware pricing?
**A:** Don't compete on price — compete on the complete bathroom solution. Customers buying tiles from you will often add sanware for convenience and design coordination. Bundle discounts (tile + sanware packages), design advice, and the one-stop-shop experience justify competitive pricing. Your our premium tapware range premium range also differentiates from plumbing merchant offerings.

**Q418:** What is your returns policy on sanware?
**A:** Unopened, undamaged sanware can be returned within 14 days with a 10-15% restocking fee. Defective products can be returned within 30 days for full credit or replacement. Installed sanware cannot be returned unless defective. Special-order or imported sanware items are non-returnable unless faulty.

**Q419:** Do you offer any sanware training for my sales staff?
**A:** Yes, sanware product training is available covering: product features and benefits, technical specifications, installation basics, warranty terms, and sales techniques. our premium tapware range-specific training covers their product hierarchy, unique selling points, and after-sales service. Training can be conducted at your premises or our branch.

**Q420:** Can I get sanware sample boards or individual pieces for my showroom?
**A:** Yes, individual tap and shower samples are available for showroom display at cost or provided free with a committed sanware stock order. Display boards showing the full tap range are also available. For bathroom furniture, display units can be arranged on loan or at a reduced display price.

**Q421:** What are the key selling points of our premium tapware range that I should communicate to customers?
**A:** Key our premium tapware range selling points: German engineering and quality, StarLight chrome finish (durable and scratch-resistant), SilkMove ceramic cartridge (smooth operation), EcoJoy water-saving technology (reduces water use by up to 50%), and comprehensive warranty. our premium tapware range is a recognised premium brand that justifies premium pricing.

**Q422:** How do I handle water pressure compatibility questions for showers and taps?
**A:** Most modern mixers work with municipal pressure (typically 1-5 bar in South Africa). For low-pressure systems (geysers on the roof), specific low-pressure taps are required. For high-pressure systems, pressure-reducing valves may be needed. Our product specifications indicate pressure requirements — train your staff to ask about the customer's water system.

**Q423:** Do you stock shower enclosures and doors, and what sizes are available?
**A:** Yes, we stock shower doors (hinged, sliding, pivot), shower enclosures (square, rectangular, quadrant), and walk-in shower panels. Standard sizes range from 800mm to 1400mm widths, with custom sizes available on indent. Frameless glass options are popular for premium bathrooms; framed options offer better value.

**Q424:** What bathroom accessories should I stock as add-on sales?
**A:** Essential accessories include: towel rails (heated and non-heated), toilet roll holders, soap dispensers, robe hooks, toilet brushes, and grab bars (for accessibility). These are impulse purchases with good margins — display them near the checkout or in bathroom vignettes. Heated towel rails are particularly popular in colder climates.

**Q425:** How do I create a compelling bathroom package offer combining tiles and sanware?
**A:** Create tiered bathroom packages: "Essential" (basic tiles + close-coupled toilet + standard mixer), "Designer" (premium tiles + back-to-wall toilet + our premium tapware range mixer + freestanding bath), "Luxury" (imported tiles + wall-hung toilet + full our premium tapware range suite + frameless shower). Price packages at a slight discount to individual items to drive bundle sales.

---

## Category 18: Trade Show & Event Engagement

**Q426:** Which trade shows does Tiletoria participate in each year?
**A:** We participate in competitor retailers Expo (typically February/March), AfricaBuild (various dates), and Decorex (selected cities). We also host our own pre-season wholesale buying events at our branches. These shows are the primary opportunities to preview new ranges, access show-only pricing, and network with suppliers and industry peers.

**Q427:** What are the dates and locations for this year's trade shows?
**A:** Trade show dates vary year to year. Contact your account manager or check our trade WhatsApp broadcasts for current year's confirmed dates and venues. Major shows are typically held at Gallagher Convention Centre (Midrand), Cape Town International Convention Centre, or Durban Exhibition Centre. We'll notify all wholesale accounts well in advance.

**Q428:** What is "show-only pricing" and how much can I save?
**A:** Show-only pricing is special promotional wholesale pricing available exclusively during trade show periods or our pre-season buying events. Savings typically range from 5-15% below standard wholesale prices for forward orders placed during the show window. This is our best pricing of the year and is available to all active wholesale accounts.

**Q429:** Do I have to attend the trade show in person to get show pricing?
**A:** No — show pricing is extended to all active wholesale accounts during the promotional window, even if you can't attend in person. Your account manager will send you the show catalogue and pricing via email. Orders can be placed remotely by email, phone, or through the online portal. However, attending gives you the advantage of seeing products firsthand.

**Q430:** What new ranges are typically launched at trade shows?
**A:** Trade shows are our primary launch platform for: new tile collections from European factories (spring and autumn collections), new laminate and LVT decors, new sanware lines, and new our exclusive mosaic range designs. Shows also feature trend forecasts for the upcoming season. Your account manager will brief you on expected launches before each show.

**Q431:** Can I bring my staff or contractor customers to the trade show with me?
**A:** Trade show access is typically open to industry professionals. As a Tiletoria wholesale partner, you and your staff receive complimentary entry. Some shows allow you to bring contractor customers — check the specific show's entry policy. Bringing key contractors is valuable as they can see new products and provide input on range selection.

**Q432:** Do you host private events or buyer evenings for wholesale customers?
**A:** Yes, we host pre-season buyer evenings at our branches where wholesale customers can preview new ranges in a relaxed setting with refreshments. These events include product presentations, trend briefings, and exclusive show pricing. Invitations are sent to all active wholesale accounts 2-3 weeks in advance.

**Q433:** Is there a minimum order required to attend a trade show or buyer event?
**A:** No, there is no minimum order requirement to attend. However, show pricing is only available on orders placed during the promotional window. We encourage you to come prepared with your range plan and budget so you can take advantage of the pricing. Our team is available at shows to help you plan your order.

**Q434:** How do I prepare for a trade show to get the most value from it?
**A:** Before the show: review your current sales data to identify gaps and opportunities, set a budget, and note any products you've been asked for but don't stock. At the show: focus on new launches, handle the products, ask about exclusivity, and negotiate. After the show: place your forward orders promptly to secure show pricing and allocation.

**Q435:** What networking opportunities are available at trade shows for independent merchants?
**A:** Trade shows bring together merchants, contractors, architects, and suppliers from across the industry. Use the opportunity to: meet other independent merchants (non-competing regions) to share best practices, connect with architects who can specify your products, and build relationships with factory representatives. Our team can introduce you to key contacts.

**Q436:** Do you organise buyer trips to international trade fairs like Cersaie in Italy?
**A:** Yes, we organise annual buyer trips to Cersaie (Bologna, Italy — September) and Cevisama (Valencia, Spain — February) for larger wholesale accounts. These trips include factory visits, product selection sessions, and trend immersion. Costs are typically shared, with significant contributions from Tiletoria for high-volume accounts.

**Q437:** How do I get invited to international buyer trips?
**A:** International buyer trips are offered to accounts with significant annual purchases (typically R5 million+) and strong growth trajectories. Express your interest to your regional wholesale manager. Selection considers purchase history, payment record, showroom standards, and commitment to promoting our exclusive ranges.

**Q438:** What happens at a Tiletoria pre-season wholesale buying event?
**A:** Pre-season events include: new range previews with physical samples, trend presentations for the upcoming season, show-only pricing announcements, food and networking, one-on-one range planning sessions with your account manager, and exclusive previews of products not yet publicly available. Events typically run 2-3 hours.

**Q439:** Do you offer virtual trade show attendance for merchants who can't travel?
**A:** Yes, for major trade shows and product launches, we offer virtual participation via live-streamed product presentations, virtual showroom tours, and video consultations with our team. While not a complete substitute for in-person attendance, virtual participation ensures you don't miss key announcements and can still access show pricing.

**Q440:** Can I request a private product preview at your showroom instead of attending the trade show?
**A:** Yes, private product previews can be arranged at our Spec Lab showroom in Menlyn, Johannesburg, or at any branch. Your account manager will set up a dedicated session with samples, catalogues, and pricing. This is ideal if you prefer a focused, one-on-one buying experience without the trade show crowds.

**Q441:** How do trade show orders differ from regular orders in terms of delivery timing?
**A:** Trade show orders are typically forward orders for stock that is either in the import pipeline or scheduled for production. Delivery is phased according to an agreed schedule (e.g., 30% immediately, 40% in 6 weeks, 30% in 12 weeks). This allows you to lock in show pricing without taking all the stock at once. Delivery schedules are flexible within reason.

**Q442:** What is the advantage of placing my trade show order early vs waiting until the last day?
**A:** Early ordering secures: the best stock allocation (first pick of incoming containers), confirmed delivery slots in our schedule, and peace of mind that your order is locked in at show pricing. Late orders may face stock shortages on popular new lines or delayed delivery slots. We process orders on a first-come, first-served basis.

**Q443:** Do you have a loyalty or rewards programme tied to trade show participation?
**A:** While we don't have a formal points-based loyalty programme, consistent trade show participation and ordering is recognised in your account reviews. Active participants often receive priority on new range allocations, enhanced co-op marketing funds, and preferential treatment on limited-stock items. Your engagement matters.

**Q444:** What should I wear and bring to a tile trade show?
**A:** Wear comfortable shoes — you'll be walking and standing for hours. Bring: business cards, a notebook or tablet for recording notes, your current price list for comparison, a list of products you're looking for, a camera phone for photographing displays, and a bag for collecting samples and brochures. A wheeled sample case is useful for larger shows.

**Q445:** Can I get samples of new products at trade shows to take back to my showroom?
**A:** Yes, sample tiles, boards, and brochures are available at trade shows for wholesale customers. For major new launches, we provide sample packs to key accounts after the show. Sample availability may be limited for very new products — priority goes to accounts who place forward orders at the show.

**Q446:** How do you communicate trade show details and schedules to wholesale customers?
**A:** Trade show information is communicated via: email invitations (4-6 weeks in advance), WhatsApp broadcasts, phone calls from your account manager, our trade newsletter, and the wholesale portal events calendar. We also post reminders on our trade social media channels. Ensure your contact details are up to date to receive all communications.

**Q447:** What is the competitor retailers Expo, and why should I attend?

**Q448:** Do you sponsor or subsidise travel and accommodation for trade show attendance?
**A:** For major accounts and long-standing partners, we may contribute to travel and accommodation costs for key trade shows, particularly international trips. Local show attendance costs are typically borne by the merchant, though we provide complimentary entry tickets. Discuss sponsorship with your regional wholesale manager.

**Q449:** Can I place orders at the trade show for delivery to multiple branches?
**A:** Yes, trade show orders can be split across multiple delivery addresses if you operate multi-branch. Specify the split when placing your order (e.g., 60% to Branch A, 40% to Branch B). Each branch receives stock according to the agreed schedule. Split orders may have different delivery timings based on branch proximity to stock.

**Q450:** What follow-up happens after a trade show to ensure I received everything I ordered?
**A:** After the trade show, your account manager confirms your order details, delivery schedule, and any special requirements. You'll receive an order summary within 48 hours. As delivery dates approach, your account manager provides updates on stock arrival and confirms delivery logistics. Any discrepancies are resolved before delivery.

---

## Category 19: Relationship & Support

**Q451:** Who is my dedicated wholesale account manager?
**A:** Upon account activation, you'll be assigned a dedicated wholesale account manager based at your nearest Tiletoria branch. Their name, direct phone number, email, and WhatsApp contact are provided in your welcome pack. Your account manager is your primary contact for all operational and commercial matters.

**Q452:** How often will my account manager visit me at my premises?
**A:** Account manager visit frequency depends on your account tier and location: A-tier accounts (R5 million+ annual purchases) receive monthly visits, B-tier accounts (R2-5 million) receive bi-monthly visits, and C-tier accounts (under R2 million) receive quarterly visits or phone check-ins. Outlying accounts may have less frequent in-person visits but regular phone contact.

**Q453:** What does my account manager do during a visit?
**A:** Typical account manager visits include: reviewing your sales performance and stock levels, discussing new products and upcoming promotions, addressing any operational issues, training your staff on new ranges, assisting with range planning, collecting feedback, and strengthening the business relationship. Visits are tailored to your current priorities.

**Q454:** Can I contact my account manager after hours for urgent issues?
**A:** Yes, all account managers provide their mobile numbers for urgent after-hours contact. Use this for genuine emergencies only: stock-outs on critical jobs, delivery issues, quality problems requiring immediate attention, or payment disputes. Routine queries should be handled during business hours (8:00 AM - 5:00 PM, Monday-Friday).

**Q455:** What if my account manager is on leave or unavailable — who do I deal with?
**A:** Each account manager has a designated backup colleague who handles their accounts during leave periods. You'll be notified of leave arrangements in advance with backup contact details. For urgent matters, you can also call the branch wholesale desk directly — any team member can access your account information and assist.

**Q456:** Can I request a meeting with the branch manager or regional wholesale manager?
**A:** Yes, meetings with branch managers and regional wholesale managers can be arranged. These are typically scheduled for strategic discussions: credit limit increases, territorial arrangements, performance reviews, dispute resolution, or significant business changes. Contact your account manager to request a meeting with senior management.

**Q457:** How do you handle disputes or complaints — what is the escalation process?
**A:** Dispute resolution follows this path: (1) Discuss with your account manager, (2) If unresolved, escalate to the branch wholesale manager, (3) If still unresolved, escalate to the regional wholesale director, (4) Final escalation to the wholesale director at head office. Most issues are resolved at Level 1 or 2. We aim to resolve all complaints within 5 business days.

**Q458:** Do you conduct formal business reviews with wholesale customers?
**A:** Yes, formal quarterly business reviews (QBRs) are conducted for A and B-tier accounts. QBRs cover: sales performance vs targets, stock turn analysis, margin review, new opportunities, operational feedback, and forward planning. These reviews are valuable strategic planning sessions. C-tier accounts receive annual reviews.

**Q459:** What support do you offer if I'm struggling with a specific aspect of my business?
**A:** Our wholesale team provides consultative support on: range planning and inventory optimisation, showroom layout and merchandising, pricing strategy, marketing support, staff training, and operational efficiency. While we're not business consultants, we share best practices from successful merchants in our network. Just ask your account manager.

**Q460:** Can I get a direct line to the warehouse or dispatch team for urgent order queries?
**A:** Your account manager coordinates all warehouse and dispatch communication. However, for urgent same-day collection or dispatch queries, your account manager can provide the direct warehouse phone number. Please use direct warehouse contact sparingly and only for time-critical operational matters — your account manager remains your primary liaison.

**Q461:** How do I provide feedback on Tiletoria's products or services?
**A:** Feedback is welcomed and actively sought. Provide feedback to your account manager during visits, via email, through our quarterly wholesale survey, or at our annual wholesale customer forum. We use customer feedback to drive improvements in our product range, service levels, and operational processes. Your input shapes our business.

**Q462:** Do you have a wholesale customer advisory panel or forum?
**A:** Yes, we hold an annual wholesale customer forum where key merchants from each region meet with senior management to discuss industry trends, product direction, service improvements, and strategic initiatives. Additionally, we form ad-hoc advisory panels for specific topics (e.g., new portal features, range planning). Ask your account manager about participation.

**Q463:** What happens if I'm unhappy with my account manager — can I request a change?
**A:** Yes, if you feel your account manager relationship isn't working, you can request a change by contacting the branch wholesale manager. We'll discuss your concerns and, if appropriate, assign a different account manager. We want you to have a productive, trusting relationship with your primary contact.

**Q464:** How do you recognise and reward loyal, high-performing wholesale customers?
**A:** Recognition includes: priority access to new ranges and limited stock, invitations to exclusive events and international trips, enhanced co-op marketing funds, featured placement on our stockist locator, nomination for industry awards, and personalised service from senior management. Long-term partnerships are the foundation of our wholesale business.

**Q465:** Can I invite Tiletoria representatives to my store opening, anniversary event, or customer day?
**A:** Yes, we love supporting our wholesale partners' events. Your account manager or a senior representative can attend your store opening, anniversary celebration, or contractor customer day to show support, provide product expertise, and strengthen the partnership. Give us at least 2-3 weeks' notice for event attendance.

**Q466:** What is Tiletoria's approach to long-term partnerships with wholesale customers?
**A:** We view our wholesale customers as long-term business partners, not transactional accounts. We invest in your success through: consistent supply, competitive pricing, product training, marketing support, and consultative advice. Our best wholesale relationships span 10-20+ years, built on mutual trust, honest communication, and shared growth.

**Q467:** How do I know if Tiletoria values my business as a smaller merchant vs a large one?
**A:** Every wholesale account receives professional service regardless of size. While larger accounts receive more frequent visits and strategic attention, smaller accounts have equal access to our product range, pricing structures, training, and support. Many of our smaller merchants have grown into large accounts — we value the journey.

**Q468:** Do you celebrate business milestones (5 years, 10 years) with your wholesale customers?
**A:** Yes, we recognise long-term partnerships with anniversary acknowledgements, gifts, and features in our trade communications. Milestone partnerships (5, 10, 15, 20+ years) are celebrated at our annual forum and in our wholesale newsletter. Loyalty matters to us, and we make sure our partners know it.

**Q469:** What is the best way to build a strong relationship with my account manager?
**A:** Be transparent about your business goals and challenges. Communicate regularly — not just when there's a problem. Pay on time and honour commitments. Provide feedback (positive and constructive). Invite them to your premises so they understand your operation. A strong relationship leads to better support, flexibility, and mutual success.

**Q470:** If I have a great idea for a new product or service, will Tiletoria listen?
**A:** Absolutely — we actively solicit product ideas and service suggestions from our wholesale customers. You're in the market every day and often spot opportunities before we do. Submit your ideas to your account manager or at our customer forum. Ideas that are adopted are recognised, and we've launched several successful products based on merchant feedback.

**Q471:** How do you handle communication during crises (COVID-style disruptions, riots, floods)?
**A:** During crises, we activate our wholesale emergency communication protocol: daily updates via WhatsApp and email, direct phone calls to affected accounts, flexible payment arrangements, rerouting of deliveries, stock prioritisation for affected merchants, and operational contingency plans. We supported our merchants through every major disruption and will continue to do so.

**Q472:** Can I get references from other wholesale customers about their experience with Tiletoria?
**A:** Yes, with permission, we can connect you with existing wholesale customers in non-competing regions who can share their experience of working with us. This is particularly valuable for new merchants evaluating a supplier relationship. Ask your account manager for a referral to a merchant in a similar market.

**Q473:** What makes Tiletoria different from other tile wholesalers in terms of relationship management?
**A:** Key differentiators: (1) Dedicated account management — you're not a number, (2) Large enough for reliability, small enough for flexibility, (3) Investment in your success through training and support, (4) Transparent communication — good news and bad, (5) Long-term partnership orientation — we want you to grow and succeed.

**Q474:** Do you send regular newsletters or updates to keep me informed?
**A:** Yes, we publish a monthly wholesale newsletter covering: new product launches, promotional offers, industry news, merchant success stories, trend updates, and operational notices. The newsletter is emailed to all active wholesale accounts. Ensure your email address is up to date and the newsletter isn't landing in your spam folder.

**Q475:** How can I nominate my business or a staff member for recognition in your trade communications?
**A:** We feature successful wholesale partners in our newsletter and trade communications. If you have a business milestone, renovation success story, or outstanding staff member you'd like to highlight, email marketing@tiletoria.co.za with the details. Recognition builds your profile in the industry and among potential customers.

---

## Category 20: Rest of Africa Export

**Q476:** Do you supply wholesale customers outside South Africa?
**A:** Yes, we supply wholesale customers across Southern and East Africa including Botswana, Namibia, Zimbabwe, Mozambique, Zambia, Malawi, Mauritius, and Kenya. Export accounts are managed by our dedicated export division with expertise in cross-border logistics, documentation, and regional market requirements.

**Q477:** What is the process for opening a wholesale export account?
**A:** Export account applications follow a similar process to domestic accounts: complete an application form, provide company registration documents, trade references, and banking details. Export accounts typically start on pro forma (payment upfront) for the first 12 months. Credit terms may be considered after establishing a payment history.

**Q478:** What payment terms do you offer for export customers?
**A:** New export accounts operate on pro forma (100% payment before dispatch). After 12 months of consistent trading, we may offer 30-day terms against a confirmed letter of credit or trade credit insurance. Large, well-established regional merchants may negotiate extended terms with appropriate security instruments.

**Q479:** Can I pay in USD or other currencies instead of ZAR?
**A:** Yes, we accept payment in USD, EUR, GBP, and major African currencies for export orders. Pricing can be quoted in your preferred currency. Currency conversion is based on the exchange rate at the time of invoice. For USD pricing, we typically fix the rate for 30 days from quotation.

**Q480:** What export documentation do you provide for cross-border shipments?
**A:** We provide all required export documentation including: commercial invoice, packing list, certificate of origin, SADC certificate (for duty-free trade within SADC), bill of lading/CMR, export customs clearance, SARS customs documentation, and any product-specific certificates (SABS, quality certificates). Our export team handles all documentation.

**Q481:** How long does delivery take to Botswana, Namibia, Zimbabwe, and Mozambique?
**A:** Botswana and Namibia: 3-5 days from Johannesburg via road freight. Zimbabwe: 5-7 days via Beitbridge border. Mozambique: 5-8 days via Lebombo or Ressano Garcia border. Zambia and Malawi: 7-12 days. Mauritius and other island destinations: 14-21 days via sea freight from Durban.

**Q482:** Do you handle customs clearance at the border, or is that my responsibility?
**A:** We arrange South African export customs clearance. Import customs clearance in the destination country is typically your responsibility, though we can recommend experienced clearing agents in most regional markets. For full-service delivery (DDP — Delivered Duty Paid), we can arrange clearance on your behalf at additional cost.

**Q483:** What are the freight costs for cross-border delivery?
**A:** Cross-border freight costs depend on destination, volume, and transport mode. Indicative rates per pallet: Botswana/Namibia R1,500-R2,500, Zimbabwe R2,000-R3,500, Mozambique R2,500-R4,000, Zambia R3,500-R5,500. Full container loads (FCL) are more cost-effective per unit. Your export account manager will provide a detailed freight quotation.

**Q484:** Do you have distributors or agents in other African countries?
**A:** We work with appointed distributors in several regional markets and are always looking for reliable distribution partners. If you're interested in becoming a country distributor rather than a direct importer, discuss distribution rights with our export director. Distributorships are granted based on market potential, capability, and commitment.

**Q485:** What products are most popular in African markets outside South Africa?
**A:** Popular export products include: ceramic wall tiles (300x600mm and 400x800mm in white and beige), 600x600mm porcelain floor tiles, basic sanitaryware, and laminate flooring. Premium imported tiles sell well in affluent markets like Mauritius, Zambia's mining towns, and Kenya's urban centres. Your export manager can advise on market-specific trends.

**Q486:** Do you comply with SADC trade agreement requirements for duty-free access?
**A:** Yes, we provide SADC certificates of origin for qualifying products, allowing duty-free or reduced-duty access to SADC member states. Products must meet rules of origin requirements (sufficient transformation in South Africa or a SADC country). Our export team ensures all documentation complies with SADC and AfCFTA requirements where applicable.

**Q487:** Can smaller merchants in neighbouring countries buy LCL (less than container load) quantities?
**A:** Yes, LCL shipments are available for smaller export orders. We consolidate LCL freight through our freight forwarders, departing weekly from Johannesburg and Durban. LCL is cost-effective for orders of 2-8 pallets. Minimum LCL export order value is typically R25,000-R50,000 depending on destination.

**Q488:** What are the biggest challenges of exporting to Africa, and how do you help overcome them?
**A:** Common challenges: border delays, customs complexity, currency volatility, and payment risk. We mitigate these through: experienced freight forwarders with established cross-border routes, complete documentation packages, currency hedging options, secure payment methods (LC, escrow), and local market knowledge. Our export team guides you through every step.

**Q489:** Do you attend trade shows in other African countries?
**A:** Yes, we participate in trade shows in Botswana, Zambia, Kenya, and Mauritius to support our export customers and develop new markets. These shows are opportunities for our regional merchants to see new products, access show pricing, and network. We also host buyer events in key regional cities.

**Q490:** Can I get the same wholesale pricing as South African merchants, or is export pricing different?
**A:** Export pricing is structured similarly to domestic wholesale pricing, with adjustments for documentation, freight, and currency management costs. For significant volume commitments, export customers can achieve pricing comparable to South African merchants. Pricing is quoted ex-warehouse with freight as a separate line item for transparency.

**Q491:** How do you handle warranty claims and returns for export customers?
**A:** Warranty claims follow the same process as domestic claims: report the issue with photos and documentation, and we assess and resolve. For returns due to defects, we arrange collection from your premises at our cost if the defect is confirmed. Due to logistics complexity, we prioritise credit/replacement over physical returns for export accounts where possible.

**Q492:** What incoterms do you offer for export shipments?
**A:** We offer: EXW (ex-warehouse — you collect from our warehouse), FCA (free carrier — we deliver to your nominated carrier), FOB (free on board — for sea freight from Durban/Cape Town), CIF (cost, insurance, freight — we arrange to your port), and DAP (delivered at place — we arrange to your premises). DDP is available for selected destinations.

**Q493:** Do you provide marketing support for your export partners?
**A:** Yes, export partners receive: product catalogues (digital and printed), lifestyle photography, product specification sheets, trand materials where required, and market-specific trend guidance. For significant markets, we can provide co-branded marketing materials and support local advertising. Our export marketing team assists with brand building in your market.

**Q494:** Can I bring my customers or staff to visit Tiletoria in South Africa?
**A:** Yes, we welcome visits from our export partners and their teams. We can arrange warehouse tours, product training, showroom visits, and meetings with our management team. For significant partners, we can assist with accommodation recommendations and logistics planning. Contact your export account manager to arrange a visit.

**Q495:** How do I stay updated on changes to import regulations in my country?
**A:** While you're responsible for understanding your country's import regulations, our export team monitors regulatory changes in key markets and notifies affected customers. We also maintain relationships with clearing agents in each destination who can advise on current requirements. For major regulatory changes, we issue advisory notices to all export customers in the affected market.

**Q496:** What is the minimum order for export, and can I combine tile, flooring, and sanware in one shipment?
**A:** Minimum export order value is typically R25,000-R50,000 depending on destination. Yes, you can combine tiles, flooring, sanware, and accessories in a single shipment. Mixed-container shipments are standard practice and help you achieve better freight rates per unit. Our export team optimises packing for mixed shipments.

**Q497:** Do you help with product registration or standards compliance in my country?
**A:** We provide product test reports, SABS certificates, and technical data sheets that support product registration in your market. However, actual registration with local standards bodies is typically your responsibility. Our export team can liaise with your standards authority if specific documentation is required beyond what we routinely provide.

**Q498:** How do you handle payment from countries with currency controls or banking restrictions?
**A:** For countries with currency controls (Zimbabwe, for example), we accept payment via: bank transfers through authorised dealer banks, trade finance facilities, confirmed letters of credit, and in some cases, third-party payments from South Africa-based entities. Our export team works with you to find a practical payment solution.

**Q499:** Can I get container loads shipped to my own premises, or do I need to use a clearing agent?
**A:** Full container loads (FCL) can be delivered directly to your premises if you have suitable offloading facilities and customs clearance arranged. Alternatively, we can deliver to a bonded warehouse or your clearing agent's depot in the destination country. Most export customers use a clearing agent for import clearance convenience.

**Q500:** What support do you offer for merchants looking to expand into Africa from a South African base?
**A:** For South African merchants expanding into the region (e.g., a Gauteng merchant supplying projects in Botswana or Mozambique), we provide: export documentation, cross-border freight arrangements, market insights, and introductions to local partners. This allows you to pursue regional opportunities without establishing a physical presence in each country.

---

## Category 21: Stock Range & Selection (Continued)

**Q501:** I'm in a coastal town — what products should I prioritise for the beach-house and holiday rental market?
**A:** For coastal markets like leading adhesive manufacturerslito, Richards Bay, and George, prioritise: wood-look porcelain planks (hardwearing, sand-resistant), large-format light-coloured tiles (reflect natural light), anti-slip outdoor pavers (R11-R12 rating), subway tiles for kitchen splashbacks (easy to clean), and neutral stone-look tiles. Holiday rental owners prioritise durability and low maintenance.

**Q502:** What tile sizes are currently trending for residential bathrooms in 2024?
**A:** The dominant bathroom tile sizes are 600x600mm and 600x1200mm for floors, with 400x800mm and 600x1200mm for walls. Large-format tiles (1200x1200mm and above) are growing in the luxury segment. Mosaic feature strips remain popular for shower niches and vanity backdrops. Rectified edges are preferred for the seamless look.

**Q503:** Should I stock wood-look porcelain planks, and what sizes sell best?
**A:** Yes, wood-look porcelain is one of the strongest-selling categories. Best-selling plank sizes are 200x1200mm and 200x1000mm. Wider planks (250x1500mm) are trending for premium installations. Stock 3-4 popular wood tones: light oak, medium walnut, and dark ebony. Wood-look porcelain works in lounges, bedrooms, and even bathrooms where real wood can't go.

**Q504:** What is the demand for concrete-look and industrial-style tiles in secondary markets?
**A:** Concrete-look porcelain has strong demand in urban Gauteng markets and is growing in secondary cities among younger, design-conscious homeowners. In traditional markets, concrete-look remains a niche. Stock a few concrete-look options (light grey, medium grey) if your customer base includes modern home builders and renovators.

**Q505:** Do you stock tiles for the commercial hospitality market — hotels, restaurants, lodges?
**A:** Yes, our commercial hospitality range includes: heavy-duty porcelain (PEI 4-5), large-format tiles, anti-slip commercial flooring (R11-R13), through-body porcelain, and feature walls. We also supply pool tiles, spa-grade mosaics, and outdoor pavers. Project-specific specifications and technical data are available through our Spec Lab team.

**Q506:** What is a "book-matched" porcelain slab, and should I stock it?
**A:** Book-matched porcelain slabs have a continuous veining pattern that mirrors across adjacent slabs, creating a dramatic symmetrical effect like an open book. They're stunning for feature walls, countertops, and reception desks. Book-matched slabs are premium products (R350-R800/m² wholesale) — stock if you serve the high-end residential or commercial market.

**Q507:** Do you stock tiles specifically designed for feature walls and fireplaces?

**Q508:** What is the demand for "terrazzo" look tiles, and do you stock them?
**A:** Terrazzo has experienced a major revival. We stock terrazzo-look porcelain tiles in various chip sizes and colourways — from classic white/grey to bold pink and green variants. Terrazzo-look tiles are popular in commercial spaces, trendy homes, and hospitality projects. Stock 2-3 colourways to cover the trend.

**Q509:** Should I stock tiles for the DIY market, or focus only on professional installers?
**A:** The DIY market is significant in South Africa, particularly for bathroom renovations, kitchen splashbacks, and patio projects. Stock DIY-friendly products: standard ceramic wall tiles, easy-to-cut porcelain, self-adhesive mosaic sheets, and user-friendly grouts. Package these with installation guides and promote them to DIY customers.

**Q510:** What pool tiles do you stock, and is this a profitable category?
**A:** Our pool tile range includes: mosaic pool tiles (glass and porcelain), anti-slip pool surround tiles, coping tiles (bullnose and square edge), and pool feature tiles. Pool tiles are a profitable niche — pool renovations are common in affluent areas, and the average pool project value is high. Stock basic blues plus 2-3 premium options.

**Q511:** Do you stock tiles for braai areas and outdoor entertainment spaces?

**Q512:** What is the demand for "subway" or "metro" tiles in South Africa?
**A:** Subway tiles (typically 100x200mm or 100x300mm) remain extremely popular for kitchen splashbacks, bathroom walls, and feature installations. They're affordable, versatile, and suit both modern and traditional designs. Stock white gloss as your volume line, plus 3-4 colours (grey, black, navy, sage) for design variety.

**Q513:** Do you stock tiles with textured or 3D surfaces for feature walls?
**A:** Yes, our 3D/decorative tile range includes: wave-pattern tiles, geometric relief tiles, linear textured tiles, woven-look tiles, and sculptural 3D panels. These are typically used on single feature walls in bathrooms, bedrooms, or reception areas. 3D tiles are higher-margin products that differentiate your showroom.

**Q514:** What products should I stock for the retirement home and accessibility market?
**A:** For the growing retirement and accessibility market, stock: anti-slip floor tiles (R10-R11), large-format tiles with minimal grout joints (easier cleaning, tripping hazard reduction), contrasting colour tiles for visual impairment navigation, grab bar-compatible wall tiles, and walk-in shower-compatible products. This market is growing rapidly in South Africa.

**Q515:** Do you stock "mini" or "metro" format tiles for traditional or heritage-style homes?
**A:** Yes, smaller format tiles for heritage-style installations include: 100x100mm and 150x150mm floor tiles, 75x150mm and 100x200mm wall tiles, penny-round mosaics, and traditional border/trim pieces. These suit homes in heritage areas like Stellenbosch, older parts of Durban and Cape Town, and period renovations.

**Q516:** What is the demand for metallic and glass mosaic tiles in the residential market?
**A:** Metallic and glass mosaic feature strips and sheets remain popular for: bathroom feature walls, kitchen splashbacks, and bar/entertainment area backdrops. They're typically used as accents rather than full-room coverage. Stock a selection of gold, silver, and copper metallic mosaics, plus iridescent glass options for luxury projects.

**Q517:** Should I stock tile adhesives, grouts, and sealers, or refer customers elsewhere?
**A:** While we don't stock these directly, we have partnerships with leading adhesive manufacturers, leading adhesive manufacturers, and Cemcrete and can introduce you to their distribution network. Some merchants prefer to stock a basic range of adhesives and grouts as add-on sales. Alternatively, refer customers to approved adhesive stockists. Either way, recommending the right adhesive is part of good service.

**Q518:** What is the market for heated towel rails and underfloor heating as add-ons to tile sales?
**A:** Underfloor heating and heated towel rails are excellent add-on sales for bathroom renovations. We can introduce you to underfloor heating suppliers (Devil, Schluter, Warmup). These products increase average transaction value by R3,000-R15,000 per bathroom and position you as a complete bathroom solutions provider.

**Q519:** Do you stock tiles in the warm "KZN colours" — terracotta, beige, brown tones?
**A:** Yes, warm tones remain popular in KZN, Mpumalanga, and Limpopo. We stock: terracotta-look porcelain tiles, warm beige ceramics, brown-toned wood-look planks, and cream/ivory-look tiles. Your account manager can provide a curated warm-tones selection aligned to your regional preferences.

**Q520:** How do I select products for a new branch in an area I'm not familiar with?
**A:** For new market entries, we provide: demographic analysis of the area, regional sales data from nearby markets, competitor product analysis, a recommended opening stock package, and a 90-day review to adjust based on early sales patterns. Your account manager will visit the area with you to assess the market before finalising your range.

**Q521:** What products are most profitable per square metre that I should prioritise?

**Q522:** Do you stock tiles suitable for high-rise apartment buildings with weight restrictions?
**A:** Yes, for high-rise applications we recommend: thinner porcelain tiles (8-9mm rather than 10-12mm), large-format lightweight tiles, and LVT flooring (very light). Always check the building's floor loading specifications. Our technical team can advise on weight calculations for specific tile choices in multi-storey buildings.

**Q523:** What is the demand for "zellige" or handmade-look tiles in the SA market?
**A:** Zellige (Moroccan handmade-look) tiles are a growing niche in the premium market, particularly in Cape Town and coastal areas. They offer an artisanal, imperfect aesthetic that appeals to design-conscious customers. We stock zellige-look tiles in various colours. Stock if your market includes architects, interior designers, and luxury homeowners.

**Q524:** Do you stock tiles that look like wallpaper or fabric?
**A:** Yes, our decorative range includes tiles with wallpaper-like patterns (floral, geometric, tropical) and fabric-look textures (linen, silk). These are statement pieces for feature walls in bedrooms, boutique hotels, and restaurants. They're niche products with high margins — stock 1-2 designs as showroom conversation pieces.

**Q525:** How do I decide when to adopt a new trend vs sticking with proven sellers?
**A:** The 70/20/10 rule works well: 70% of your range should be proven, consistent sellers; 20% should be growing trends with demonstrated demand; 10% can be speculative new products or niche items. Test new trends with small quantities first, and expand only when sales justify. Your account manager provides trend strength analysis to guide decisions.


---

## Category 22: Commercial & Project Supply

**Q526:** How does Tiletoria support wholesale merchants bidding on commercial projects?
**A:** We provide comprehensive commercial support including: technical specifications and data sheets, product certifications (SANS, NHBRC), project-specific pricing for large orders, phased delivery scheduling, and showroom access for your clients at our Spec Lab. Contact us early in the tender process for maximum support.

**Q527:** What is project-specific pricing, and how do I qualify?
**A:** Project pricing is negotiated per job for significant commercial orders (typically R100,000+ wholesale value). Pricing considers total volume, product mix, delivery schedule, and payment terms. To qualify, submit your project requirements including bill of quantities, timeline, and delivery schedule to your account manager.

**Q528:** Do you supply products that meet NHBRC and SANS requirements for new builds?
**A:** Yes, all our products meet applicable SANS standards. We provide test certificates and compliance documentation for NHBRC projects, including slip resistance certificates, water absorption data, and breaking strength reports. These documents are essential for project handover packages and can be requested from your account manager.

**Q529:** Can you supply large-format tiles for commercial lobby and reception areas?
**A:** Yes, our commercial large-format range includes 1200x1200mm, 1200x2600mm, and custom slab sizes in porcelain and sintered stone. These are suitable for hotel lobbies, corporate receptions, retail flagship stores, and upscale residential developments. Technical support for installation of large formats is available.

**Q530:** How do you handle phased deliveries for large construction projects?
**A:** Phased deliveries are standard for commercial projects. We work with your project schedule to deliver stock as needed — typically monthly or bi-monthly drops aligned to construction phases. This reduces on-site storage requirements and improves cash flow. A project delivery plan is agreed upfront and reviewed regularly.

**Q531:** Do you stock anti-slip commercial tiles for restaurants, hotels, and hospitals?
**A:** Yes, our commercial anti-slip range includes tiles rated R10 through R13 for various applications. R11 is standard for commercial kitchens and hotel pool surrounds. R12-R13 is used for industrial and heavy commercial wet areas. We also supply ramp-specific tiles with enhanced grip profiles.

**Q532:** Can I get a dedicated project manager for very large commercial jobs?
**A:** For major commercial projects (R500,000+ wholesale value), a dedicated project coordinator from our Spec Lab team is assigned. They manage: stock allocation, phased deliveries, technical queries, site visits (where feasible), and liaison with architects and contractors. This ensures seamless project execution.

**Q533:** What is the lead time for commercial-grade porcelain in large quantities?
**A:** For stock items, immediate delivery is available.
