Tile-in drains require specific installation techniques and fall calculations. We can recommend compatible drain systems and provide installation guidance.

---

## Category 41: Final Quick-Reference Q&As

**Q901:** How quickly can you deliver to Polokwane?
**A:** Delivery to Polokwane from our Northriding branch typically takes 1-2 business days for stock items. Minimum order values apply for delivery — contact your account manager for specific rates and scheduling.

**Q902:** Can I get a same-day collection from your Durban warehouse?
**A:** Yes, same-day collection is available from our Durban warehouse if you place your order by 10:00 AM. Orders placed after 10:00 AM are ready for collection the next business day. Always confirm collection time with your account manager.

**Q903:** What is the cheapest tile you stock at wholesale?
**A:** Entry-level ceramic wall tiles start from approximately R45-R65/m² at wholesale. These are suitable for rental properties, budget renovations, and high-volume projects. Your price list details all price bands.

**Q904:** Do you stock black tiles, and are they popular?
**A:** Yes, we stock black tiles in various finishes — polished, matte, and textured. Black is popular for feature walls, bathrooms, and modern kitchens. It works well as a contrast with white or as a dramatic statement.

**Q905:** What is your best-selling floor tile colour right now?
**A:** Grey in various shades (light, medium, and dark) remains the best-selling floor tile colour, followed by beige/greige and wood-look brown tones. Your account manager can provide the latest bestseller data for your region.

**Q906:** Can I get a product sample sent to me by courier?
**A:** Yes, samples can be sent by courier for a nominal delivery fee. Contact your account manager with the product codes you need. Up to 10 sample tiles per month are free for active wholesale accounts; you pay only the courier cost.

**Q907:** Do you have a clearance or specials list I can access?
**A:** Yes, clearance and special offers are communicated monthly via email and WhatsApp to all active wholesale accounts. Clearance stock is typically 20-40% below standard wholesale prices. Quantities are limited and sold on a first-come, first-served basis.

**Q908:** What is the difference between your Johannesburg and Cape Town pricing?
**A:** Ex-branch wholesale pricing is consistent nationwide. However, delivered pricing varies by region due to freight costs. Cape Town customers may have different landed costs on certain imports due to port of entry differences, but our pricing system normalises these variations.

**Q909:** Can I pay with a credit card?
**A:** No, we do not accept credit card payments for wholesale accounts. Payment must be by EFT, debit order, or direct deposit. This policy applies to all wholesale accounts regardless of size.

**Q910:** How do I update my email address for statements?
**A:** Email your updated contact details to accounts@tiletoria.co.za with your account number and the new email address for statements. Changes are processed within 24 hours.

**Q911:** Do you stock tile grout in colours, or just white and grey?
**A:** We stock grout in a wide range of colours including white, grey (various shades), beige, black, and colour-matched options for popular tile colours. Grout colour cards are available for customer selection. Epoxy grout is available in a more limited colour range.

**Q912:** What is the shelf life of tile adhesive?
**A:** Unopened bagged adhesive typically has a 6-12 month shelf life when stored in a dry environment. Opened bags should be used within a few weeks. Always check the manufacturing date on the packaging and store in a dry, elevated area. We can introduce you to adhesive suppliers for detailed shelf-life information.

**Q913:** Can I return unopened boxes of tiles after 30 days?
**A:** Returns after 30 days are considered on a case-by-case basis. Stock must be in original packaging, undamaged, and from our current range. A restocking fee of 15-20% typically applies for returns beyond 30 days. Contact your account manager to discuss.

**Q914:** Do you stock "tile paint" for customers who want to change tile colour?
**A:** Tile paint and tile refinishing products are not part of our standard range. These are typically sold through paint retailers. We focus on replacement tiles rather than surface coatings, as paint solutions generally have limited durability compared to new tiles.

**Q915:** What is the weight of a standard pallet of 600x600mm tiles?
**A:** A standard pallet of 600x600mm porcelain tiles weighs approximately 1,000-1,200kg including packaging. Ceramic wall tiles of the same size are lighter — approximately 800-1,000kg per pallet. Always confirm weights for transport planning.

**Q916:** Can I get a product data sheet in Afrikaans?
**A:** Most technical documentation is available in English. Some product brochures and marketing materials may be available in Afrikaans on request. Contact your account manager for specific language requirements.

**Q917:** Do you stock tiles for braai and fireplace surrounds?

**Q918:** What is your policy on price matching?
**A:** We evaluate price match requests on a case-by-case basis. Provide your account manager with the competitor's written quote for an identical or directly comparable product. We'll review our pricing and respond within 24 hours. Price matching is at our discretion.

**Q919:** Can I get a dedicated phone line for my account?
**A:** While we don't provide dedicated phone lines for individual accounts, all wholesale accounts have direct access to their account manager's mobile number for urgent matters. A-tier accounts also have access to a priority service desk.

**Q920:** How do I complain if I'm unhappy with service?
**A:** Raise your concern with your account manager first. If unresolved, escalate to the branch wholesale manager, then the regional wholesale director. For serious concerns, email wholesale@tiletoria.co.za with details. We take all complaints seriously and aim to resolve them within 5 business days.

**Q921:** Do you have loading facilities at your warehouses?
**A:** Yes, all our warehouses have loading docks and forklift facilities for efficient loading of customer collections. Self-loading with your own vehicle is also accommodated. Loading assistance is provided free of charge.

**Q922:** What is the maximum discount you offer for very large orders?
**A:** For orders exceeding R500,000 wholesale value, project-specific pricing can be negotiated. The exact discount depends on product mix, payment terms, and delivery requirements. Contact your account manager or regional wholesale manager to discuss.

**Q923:** Can I get a copy of your terms and conditions?
**A:** Yes, our Wholesale Trading Terms and Conditions are provided with your account application pack and are available on request from your account manager. Review them carefully before signing.

**Q924:** Do you offer any apprenticeships or learnerships for the industry?
**A:** We don't directly offer apprenticeships, but we support industry training initiatives through MERSETA and other SETAs. We can connect you with training providers for tiling, flooring installation, and retail learnerships. Some of our partners offer bursaries for design and architecture students.

**Q925:** What is the most expensive tile you stock?

**Q926:** Do you stock tiles for children's rooms or play areas?
**A:** We don't stock specifically "children's" themed tiles, but many of our colourful and patterned tiles work well in children's spaces. For play areas, we recommend: soft-coloured large-format tiles (fewer joints), anti-slip flooring for safety, and durable porcelain that withstands heavy play. our exclusive mosaic range range includes fun mosaic options.

**Q927:** Can I get a VAT invoice for all my purchases?
**A:** Yes, all invoices include a full VAT breakdown and our VAT registration number. Monthly statements also summarise VAT. If you need a consolidated VAT report for a specific period, contact our accounts department.

**Q928:** What happens if I go on holiday — who manages my account?
**A:** Notify your account manager of your absence and provide alternative contact details. Your account manager will continue to process orders as agreed. If you've arranged for a staff member to handle orders in your absence, ensure they're listed as an authorised contact on your account.

**Q929:** Do you stock tiles suitable for balcony and patio flooring?
**A:** Yes, 20mm outdoor porcelain pavers and anti-slip floor tiles (R11-R12) are ideal for balconies and patios. These tiles withstand weather exposure, are fade-resistant, and provide safe footing. Ensure proper waterproofing and drainage for balcony applications.

**Q930:** What is the difference between "gloss" and "polished" tiles?
**A:** "Gloss" typically refers to a glazed surface with a shiny finish (applies to ceramic and glazed porcelain). "Polished" refers to mechanically polished porcelain where the tile body itself is polished to a high shine. Gloss is a surface coating; polish is a physical process on the tile body.

**Q931:** Do you have a list of recommended tilers I can share with my customers?
**A:** We don't maintain an official list of recommended tilers, but we can connect you with tilers' associations in your area. Many merchants build their own database of reliable tilers they recommend to customers. Ensure any tilers you recommend are qualified and insured — your reputation is linked to their workmanship.

**Q932:** Can I get a quote for a specific project without placing an order immediately?
**A:** Yes, detailed project quotations are provided free of charge. Submit your bill of quantities or product list to your account manager. Quotes are typically valid for 30 days and don't obligate you to place an order. This is standard service for all wholesale accounts.

**Q933:** What is your busiest time of year, and how does it affect service levels?
**A:** Our busiest periods are September-November (pre-summer building season) and February-April (post-holiday building resumption). During peak periods, order processing may take an extra day, and delivery slots fill up faster. Planning your orders in advance ensures priority service during busy times.

**Q934:** Do you stock carpet-look tiles?
**A:** Yes, carpet-look porcelain tiles are available — they offer the warmth and texture of carpet with the durability and easy maintenance of porcelain. These are popular for bedrooms and hotel rooms. They work well with underfloor heating systems.

**Q935:** What is a "tile screed" and do you supply it?
**A:** A screed is a thin layer of cementitious material applied over a concrete slab to create a level, smooth surface for tiling. We don't supply screed materials directly, but we can introduce you to suppliers through our industry network. Proper screeding is essential for a successful tile installation.

**Q936:** Can I get an exclusive range for my store only in my town?
**A:** Exclusive distribution of specific ranges can be discussed for accounts with significant volume commitments and dedicated display investment. This is negotiated on a case-by-case basis with our regional wholesale manager. No exclusivity is guaranteed without a formal agreement and performance obligations.

**Q937:** Do you stock LED-lit mirrors for bathrooms?
**A:** Yes, LED-lit bathroom mirrors are available as part of our bathroom accessories range. These are popular add-on sales for bathroom renovations. Various sizes and styles are available, including anti-fog and touch-control options. Contact your account manager for the current range.

**Q938:** How do I know if my business is performing well compared to industry benchmarks?
**A:** Industry benchmarks for tile retailers: gross margin 30-40%, stock turn 4-6x annually, debtor days 35-45 days, rent as % of turnover 5-8%, wages as % of turnover 12-18%, and net profit 5-12%. Your account manager can discuss how your performance compares to these benchmarks during quarterly reviews.

**Q939:** What is your policy on price list confidentiality?
**A:** Wholesale price lists are strictly confidential and may not be shared with third parties, including end customers, competitors, or non-authorised persons. Price list confidentiality is a term of your wholesale trading agreement. Breaches may result in account review or termination.

**Q940:** Do you offer any sustainability or recycling programmes for old tiles?
**A:** While we don't operate a consumer recycling programme, construction waste recycling companies can process old ceramic and porcelain tiles into aggregate. We can provide information on waste management companies that accept tile waste. We're also exploring expanded sustainability initiatives for the future.

**Q941:** Can I get help with a commercial tender submission?
**A:** Yes, our Spec Lab team assists wholesale partners with commercial tender submissions by providing: technical data sheets, product specifications, compliance certificates, and project-specific pricing. Contact your account manager early in the tender process for maximum support. Complex submissions may require 1-2 weeks' notice.

**Q942:** What is the maximum size tile you stock?
**A:** Our largest standard stock tiles are 1200x1200mm and 1200x2600mm gauged porcelain slabs. Custom sizes up to 1600x3200mm can be imported on special order with extended lead times. Large-format slabs require specialised handling and cutting equipment.

**Q943:** Do you stock tile nosing for stairs?
**A:** Yes, stair nosing profiles are available in various materials (aluminium, brass, PVC) and finishes to match or complement your tile installations. These provide a safe, finished edge for tiled stairs. Bullnose tiles (factory-rounded edge) are also available for selected ranges.

**Q944:** What is your policy on supplying a merchant in the same town as me?
**A:** We evaluate new account applications based on market size, growth potential, and the applicant's business model. In growing markets, multiple accounts can coexist. We don't guarantee territorial exclusivity but respect established customer relationships. Your competitive advantage comes from service, range, and relationships.

**Q945:** How do I make a suggestion for improving your wholesale service?
**A:** We welcome suggestions via: your account manager, our quarterly wholesale survey, the annual customer forum, or email to wholesale@tiletoria.co.za. Suggestions that lead to implemented improvements are recognised. Your feedback directly shapes our service evolution.

**Q946:** Do you have any partnerships with building societies or banks for customer finance?
**A:** We don't directly offer consumer finance, but some merchants partner with building societies or furniture finance providers to offer instalment plans for larger tile purchases. This can increase sales by making high-value renovations more accessible. Explore partnerships with local finance providers if this aligns with your customer base.

**Q947:** What is the most common mistake new tile retailers make?
**A:** The most common mistakes: overstocking on slow-moving products, underpricing to compete with chains, neglecting contractor relationships, poor stock management, inadequate staff training, and insufficient working capital. Success comes from focusing on fast-moving products, building loyalty, managing cash flow, and delivering excellent service.

**Q948:** Can I get a press release or announcement if I open a new store?
**A:** We're happy to support your store opening with a mention in our trade communications and social media. For significant openings, we can provide a congratulatory message or press quote. We don't issue formal press releases on behalf of partners, but we celebrate your success through our channels.

**Q949:** How do I transition from a purely retail business to serving contractors?
**A:** Transition steps: extend your trading hours for contractors (early opening), create a trade counter area, offer trade pricing tiers, extend credit terms gradually, stock the products contractors need (not just what retail customers want), build personal relationships with local tilers, and offer reliable delivery. Contractors are the backbone of wholesale tile distribution.

**Q950:** Do you have any statistics on tile market size in South Africa?
**A:** The South African tile market is valued at approximately R8-12 billion annually at retail level. The market includes domestic consumption and export to neighbouring countries. Per capita tile consumption in South Africa is approximately 1.5-2m² per person per year. The residential renovation segment accounts for the largest share of demand.

**Q951:** What is the difference between "domestic" and "commercial" grade products?
**A:** Domestic grade products are designed for residential use with moderate foot traffic. Commercial grade products have higher durability ratings (PEI 4-5), greater breaking strength, and are designed for heavy foot traffic, frequent cleaning, and commercial maintenance regimes. Commercial grade products cost more but last significantly longer in demanding environments.

**Q952:** How do I handle a product that has been specified by an architect but I can't source?
**A:** If the specified product isn't in our range, contact our Spec Lab team immediately. We can often suggest an equivalent or superior alternative with comparable technical specifications. Early engagement with the architect allows for specification substitution before procurement begins. We regularly achieve successful substitutions.

**Q953:** Can I get your products featured in a home improvement TV show or magazine?
**A:** We periodically participate in home improvement media features and can include our wholesale partners where appropriate. If you have a media opportunity, contact marketing@tiletoria.co.za with details. We evaluate media requests based on audience reach, brand alignment, and timing.

**Q954:** What should I do if a customer claims a tile is defective but it's actually correct?
**A:** Politely explain the product characteristics with reference to the product specification sheet and industry standards. Show the declared shade variation rating (V1-V4) and explain what that means. Offer to have our technical team assess if the customer remains unsatisfied. Documentation and education are your best defences against unfounded claims.

**Q955:** Do you offer any tools to help me calculate adhesive and grout quantities?
**A:** We can provide coverage rate tables for common adhesives and grouts. These tables show approximate coverage per bag based on trowel size and tile format. Your account manager can supply these reference tables, or our technical team can calculate specific quantities for larger projects.

**Q956:** How do I handle bulk orders for property developers building multiple units?
**A:** For bulk developer orders: negotiate project pricing with your account manager, agree on consistent product across all units, set up phased deliveries aligned to construction schedules, provide bulk-packaging options where possible, and offer volume rebates for total project commitment. Property developers are valuable repeat customers.

**Q957:** What is your policy on supplying products for government housing projects?
**A:** We supply products for government housing and RDP projects through our wholesale partners. These projects require competitive pricing on entry-level products that meet minimum SANS standards. Pricing is negotiated at tender stage. BEE compliance and CSD registration are typically required for government project participation.

**Q958:** Can I get product training for my staff via video call?
**A:** Yes, video call training sessions (Zoom, Microsoft Teams) are available for product introductions, new range briefings, and refresher training. This is particularly useful for outlying accounts or when in-person visits aren't feasible. Contact your account manager to schedule a video training session.

**Q959:** Do you have any recommended practices for showroom lighting?
**A:** Good showroom lighting is essential: use 5000K-6000K daylight temperature LED lighting to show true tile colours, avoid yellow-toned lighting that distorts colours, provide adjustable spotlights on feature displays, ensure consistent lighting across the showroom, and include some natural light simulation. Poor lighting is the fastest way to lose a sale.

**Q960:** What is the typical profit margin for an independent tile retailer in South Africa?
**A:** Successful independent tile retailers typically achieve: gross margin 30-45%, operating expenses 20-30% of turnover, and net profit 5-15% of turnover. Top-performing merchants achieve 15%+ net profit through excellent stock management, strong contractor relationships, and premium product mix. Margins vary significantly by location and business model.

**Q961:** How do I handle a customer who wants to install tiles themselves (DIY)?
**A:** Support DIY customers by: recommending easier-to-install products (standard ceramics, smaller formats), providing installation guides, selling the correct tools and materials, advising on substrate preparation, recommending online tutorial videos, and being available for phone support. DIY customers who succeed become your best word-of-mouth marketers.

**Q962:** Do you stock any South African-manufactured tiles?
**A:** Yes, we stock tiles from major South African manufacturers including Johnson Tiles and Ceramic Industries. Local tiles offer shorter lead times, competitive pricing, and support for the local economy. They're ideal for volume sales and projects requiring rapid turnaround. Your price list identifies SA-manufactured products.

**Q963:** What is the best way to display large-format tiles in a small showroom?
**A:** In small showrooms: use vertical display boards (tiles mounted on walls take no floor space), install a large-format tile on the floor in a prominent position, use digital screens to show installed large-format rooms, create one spectacular feature wall, and keep loose samples for customers to handle. One stunning large-format display is better than many small ones.

**Q964:** How do I handle a customer who wants a tile that you've discontinued?
**A:** Check your sample archive for a piece of the discontinued tile. If available, use it to recommend the closest current alternative. Show the customer the alternative in person — they may prefer it. If exact matching is critical (e.g., for repairs), check our remaining stock or special order options. Be honest about discontinuation.

**Q965:** What is your policy on supplying products for export outside Africa?
**A:** While our primary export focus is Africa, we can supply other international markets on request. Export outside Africa requires specific documentation, customs procedures, and potentially different product certifications. Contact our export division for non-African export enquiries — we'll assess feasibility on a case-by-case basis.

**Q966:** Do you offer any insurance products or warranties I can resell to my customers?
**A:** We don't offer insurance products for resale. However, the manufacturer warranties on our products are passed through to your end customers. You may explore third-party extended warranty or home improvement insurance products through insurance providers as an additional service offering.

**Q967:** How do I create an effective window display for my tile showroom?
**A:** Effective window displays: change them monthly to reflect seasons or promotions, use lifestyle scenes (not just tile stacks), include props (towels, plants, accessories) to create context, use good lighting, keep windows clean, and include clear pricing or promotional messaging. Your window is your billboard — make it count.

**Q968:** What is the role of "impulse buy" products in a tile showroom?
**A:** Impulse products near the checkout increase average transaction value: tile cleaning products, grout sealers, small tools, silicone sealant, and accessories. While not major revenue drivers, these items have high margins and require minimal selling effort. A well-stocked impulse area adds 2-5% to total sales.

**Q969:** Can I get a letter confirming my account is in good standing?
**A:** Yes, letters of good standing confirming your account status and payment record are available on request from our accounts department. Allow 2-3 business days for processing. These letters are useful for tender submissions, lease applications, and credit applications with other suppliers.

**Q970:** What should I do if I receive duplicate invoices?
**A:** Contact our accounts department immediately with the invoice numbers. Duplicate invoicing is rare but can occur during system updates. We'll investigate and cancel the duplicate invoice. Never pay a duplicate — always verify against your purchase records before making payment.

**Q971:** Do you offer any pre-opening support for new store launches?
**A:** For significant new store openings by existing wholesale partners, we offer: opening stock package planning, display support, staff training before opening, marketing material supply, and potentially a visit from your account manager for launch support. Contact your regional wholesale manager to discuss pre-opening support.

**Q972:** How do I handle a customer who wants to match tiles from a photo?
**A:** Ask the customer to bring a physical sample piece if possible — photos can be misleading due to lighting and camera quality. If only a photo is available, compare it against our range in person. Similar products from our range can usually be identified. Manage expectations — an exact match from a photo is difficult.

**Q973:** What is the most effective promotional strategy for a tile retailer?
**A:** The most effective promotion is a targeted, time-limited offer on a specific product range (e.g., "20% off all wood-look porcelain this month"). This creates urgency, focuses marketing, and moves specific stock. Avoid generic store-wide sales that erode margins across your entire range. Bundle promotions (tile + adhesive + grout) also work well.

**Q974:** Can I get a detailed product specification sheet for a specific tile?
**A:** Yes, detailed specification sheets are available for all products on request. These include: dimensions, thickness, weight, water absorption, breaking strength, chemical resistance, slip rating, PEI rating, frost resistance, and installation recommendations. Your account manager or the online portal provides these sheets.

**Q975:** How do you handle a situation where your warehouse runs out of a product I've ordered?
**A:** If a stock-out occurs on an ordered product, we immediately: check other branches for transfer availability, check the import pipeline for incoming stock, offer the closest alternative from our range, and provide an estimated restock date. Your account manager will contact you with options within 24 hours of identifying the shortage.

**Q976:** What should I include in my contractor welcome pack?
**A:** A contractor welcome pack should include: your product catalogue and price list, credit application form, delivery information and areas covered, your contact details (including after-hours), a summary of your terms and conditions, any loyalty programme details, and a small gift (branded cap, tape measure, or coffee mug). First impressions matter.

**Q977:** How do I handle a delivery that arrives when my store is closed?
**A:** Arrange delivery during your business hours, or designate a staff member to receive after-hours deliveries. Some merchants provide a secure delivery area with a lockable cage. Never leave expensive stock unsecured. Coordinate delivery times with your account manager to ensure someone is available to receive.

**Q978:** What is your busiest day of the week for orders?
**A:** Tuesdays and Wednesdays are typically our busiest order days, with Friday being popular for contractors placing orders for Monday delivery. Mondays are quieter as customers process weekend enquiries. Understanding order patterns helps you plan staffing and customer service levels throughout the week.

**Q979:** Do you have a minimum annual purchase requirement to maintain wholesale status?
**A:** We don't enforce a strict minimum annual purchase requirement. However, accounts with no activity for 6+ months may be reviewed for continued wholesale status. Active accounts receive the full benefit of our wholesale services, pricing, and support. We're looking for engaged partners, not passive accounts.

**Q980:** Can I get a product that is listed in your catalogue but not in your SA stock?
**A:** Products in our catalogue that aren't currently held in SA stock can often be brought in through our import pipeline. Check with your account manager — the product may be on an incoming container, or we can add it to our next order from the factory. Lead times apply for non-stock items.

**Q981:** How do I recommend the right tile format for a small bathroom?
**A:** For small bathrooms: recommend larger format tiles (600x600mm or 600x1200mm) with fewer grout joints — this creates a more spacious appearance. Light colours enhance the sense of space. Rectified tiles with narrow grout joints maximise the seamless effect. Avoid small mosaic tiles on floors (too many grout lines make the space feel busy).

**Q982:** What is your policy on providing free samples to my customers?
**A:** As a wholesale partner, you manage your own sample policy for end customers. We provide sample tiles to you at no cost (within reasonable limits) for your showroom and customer sampling. Most merchants offer free small samples and charge a refundable deposit for larger sample boards. Your sample cost is a marketing investment.

**Q983:** Can I arrange for a Tiletoria representative to present at my contractor open day?
**A:** Yes, we regularly participate in contractor events hosted by our wholesale partners. A product specialist or account manager can present new products, provide technical training, or simply be available to answer questions. Book at least 3-4 weeks in advance to ensure availability. We bring samples and promotional materials.

**Q984:** What is the best way to handle end-of-range or clearance stock in my store?
**A:** Clearance strategies: create a dedicated clearance area with clear signage, price aggressively (at or near cost) to move quickly, bundle clearance items with full-price products, offer to contractors at special prices, list on online classifieds, and donate unsold stock for tax benefits after a reasonable period. The goal is to recover cash, not maximise margin.

**Q985:** How do I stay motivated during slow business periods?
**A:** Slow periods are opportunities: refresh your showroom, train your staff, review and optimise your range, visit contractor customers to strengthen relationships, plan marketing campaigns for the busy season, catch up on admin, research industry trends, and set goals for the coming quarter. Use downtime productively — the busy season will return.

**Q986:** What is the most important factor in building a successful tile retail business?
**A:** Relationships — with suppliers, contractors, customers, and staff. A tile business built on strong relationships survives market downturns, competitive pressure, and operational challenges. Products and prices can be matched; relationships cannot. Invest in people, deliver on promises, and be present in your market. Everything else follows.

**Q987:** Can I get access to Tiletoria's historical sales data for market research?
**A:** Aggregate, anonymised sales trend data is available to wholesale partners for market planning purposes. This data shows category trends, price band performance, and regional patterns without revealing individual account information. Contact your account manager for access to relevant trend reports for your planning.

**Q988:** How do I handle a customer who wants a rush order for a weekend installation?
**A:** If you have stock, accommodate rush orders where possible — this builds immense loyalty. Charge a small rush fee if it requires special effort. If you don't have stock, check with your account manager for emergency stock transfer from another branch. Rush orders are a service differentiator that chains often can't match.

**Q989:** What role does community involvement play in building my business?
**A:** Community involvement builds brand awareness and goodwill: sponsor local sports teams, support charity events, participate in community festivals, offer trade discounts to local schools and churches, and engage with local business associations. In smaller towns, community standing directly influences customer loyalty. Be a visible, positive presence.

**Q990:** Do you have any preferred packaging or labelling requirements for orders I deliver to my customers?
**A:** We don't dictate your packaging to end customers. However, we recommend: keeping tiles in original packaging until installation (protects from damage and identifies batch numbers), providing product information with delivery, and including your business card or sticker on deliveries. Branded packaging tape on re-wrapped pallets is a simple brand touchpoint.

**Q991:** How do I handle a situation where my customer's tiler criticises the tiles I sold?
**A:** Listen to the specific criticism — sometimes tilers have valid concerns, sometimes they're protecting a relationship with another supplier. If the criticism is unfounded, provide factual information about the product's quality and compliance. If there's a genuine issue, address it proactively. Never publicly criticise a customer's tiler — it creates defensiveness.

**Q992:** What is the future outlook for independent tile merchants in South Africa?
**A:** The outlook is positive for well-run independents who: differentiate through service and exclusive products, build strong contractor relationships, embrace digital marketing, offer design expertise, and maintain efficient operations. The market is large enough for independents and chains to coexist. Those who adapt to changing consumer expectations will thrive.

**Q993:** Can I get a backup contact for emergencies when my account manager is unavailable?
**A:** Yes, your welcome pack includes your account manager's direct number and the branch wholesale desk number. For genuine emergencies outside business hours, your account manager's mobile number is provided. We ensure coverage during leave periods through designated backup colleagues. You're never without support.

**Q994:** How do I create a winning proposal for a large commercial project?
**A:** A winning proposal includes: competitive pricing on specified products (or equivalent alternatives), evidence of stock availability, phased delivery capability, technical support commitment, references from similar projects, BEE credentials, professional presentation, and a clear timeline. Engage our Spec Lab team early for technical support on complex proposals.

**Q995:** What is the single biggest mistake you see wholesale customers make?
**A:** The single biggest mistake is poor cash flow management — overstocking on slow-moving products while running out of fast-movers, extending too much credit to slow-paying contractors, and not maintaining adequate cash reserves. Cash flow kills more businesses than lack of sales. Manage your cash as carefully as you manage your sales.

**Q996:** Do you offer any benchmarking data so I can compare my performance to similar merchants?
**A:** During quarterly business reviews, we share anonymised benchmark data comparing your performance to similar-sized merchants in non-competing regions. This covers: stock turn, margin achievement, category mix, and growth rates. Benchmarking helps you identify strengths and areas for improvement relative to peers.

**Q997:** How do I handle a product recall or safety alert if one is issued?
**A:** In the unlikely event of a product recall: stop selling immediately, quarantine affected stock, identify all customers who purchased the product from your sales records, contact each customer with recall details and instructions, and document all actions. Tiletoria coordinates the recall process and provides replacement or credit for affected stock.

**Q998:** What are your plans for expanding the wholesale product range in the next 2-3 years?

**Q999:** Can I get a testimonial or case study from another successful merchant in your network?
**A:** With permission, we can connect you with successful wholesale partners in non-competing regions who are willing to share their experiences. These conversations are invaluable for learning best practices and avoiding common pitfalls. Contact your regional wholesale manager to arrange an introduction.

**Q1000:** What is the best piece of advice you can give a new wholesale customer?
**A:** Start with a focused range of proven bestsellers, build strong relationships with 10-20 reliable contractors, manage your cash flow meticulously, never compromise on customer service, and reinvest profits into growing the business. Success in this industry comes from consistency, relationships, and operational discipline — not from any single magic formula.

**Q1001:** How do I handle a stock discrepancy between what I ordered and what was delivered?
**A:** Check the delivery note against your purchase order immediately upon delivery. Note any discrepancies on the delivery note before signing. Take photos if stock is damaged. Report shortages or wrong products to your account manager within 24 hours with the delivery note reference. We'll resolve discrepancies promptly — replacement stock or credit will be issued.

**Q1002:** Can I set up automatic reorder points for my bestselling products?
**A:** Our online portal allows you to set stock alerts for specific SKUs. When stock at our warehouse drops below your alert threshold, you receive a notification. For sophisticated inventory management, integrate our stock feed with your ERP system to trigger automatic reorder suggestions based on your sales velocity and desired cover levels.

**Q1003:** What should I do if a customer slips and falls in my showroom?
**A:** First, ensure the customer receives appropriate medical attention if injured. Document the incident with photos of the area, witness statements, and an incident report. Notify your public liability insurance broker immediately. Review your showroom floor safety — ensure tiles aren't slippery when wet and that spillages are cleaned promptly. Prevention is better than cure.

**Q1004:** How do I create a memorable unboxing experience for delivered orders?
**A:** While tiles aren't typically "unboxed" like luxury goods, attention to delivery detail matters: ensure tiles are clean and undamaged, include a delivery note with your branding, add a thank-you card or sticker, provide care instructions, and follow up after delivery. Small touches show professionalism and care — contractors notice and remember.

**Q1005:** What is the most effective way to introduce a new product range to my market?
**A:** Launch strategy: create a prominent display in your showroom, run a limited-time introductory offer, email your contractor and customer database, post on social media with lifestyle imagery, host a launch event or open day, offer samples to key contractors, and train your staff thoroughly before launch. A well-executed launch generates buzz and early sales momentum.

---

*End of Q&A Knowledge Base — Persona 9: Smaller Flooring Merchants (Wholesale)*
